chapters.title: Chapter 1598 Sect Leaders Of The Three Grand Sects
chapters.body:
Chapter 1598 Sect Leaders Of The Three Grand Sects

Chapter 1598 Sect Leaders of the Three Grand Sects

A whole week has passed since Yuan was transported to the Primal Expanse. During this time, Xi Meili remained at the Endless Beast Den, anxiously waiting for Yuan to return.

Kelan went to speak with Senior Bai and explained the situation to him. Unsurprisingly, Senior Bai was extremely upset to hear this news. “Forgive me, Senior Bai. I have failed my mission.” Kelan lowered his head and apologized.

Senior Bai didn’t respond for a long time before sighing, “You’re not at fault. I doubt I would be able to prevent it even if I were right beside him. Also, nobody could’ve expected a rift to appear. Follow me.”

“Are we going to…?” Kelan swallowed nervously and asked.

Senior Bai nodded, confirming his suspicions, “Yes, we’re going to speak with the Celestial Overlord.”

Sometime later, Senior Bai and Kelan stood before Xu Jiaqi, who was sitting behind her desk that was stacked with mountains of papers.

“Didn’t you just leave?” Xu Jiaqi spoke without even looking at them.

Senior Bai responded, “Sorry, but this is urgent.”

“I’ve been hearing that word a lot lately. What is it this time?”

Senior Bai turned to look at Kelan, his gaze gesturing to Kelan to do the speaking for obvious reasons.

Kelan took a deep breath before falling to his knees and kowtowing.

“Due to my incompetence, Yuan, who I had been tasked to watch over, was swallowed by a rift!” he spoke in a shouting voice.

However, Xu Jiaqi merely raised an eyebrow and muttered, “A rift? What rift?”

“The ones that rarely appear when an Infinite Beast Spawn is destroyed…” Kelan clarified.

“What?! How did that happen?!” Xu Jiaqi exclaimed.

“This is what happened…” Kelan proceeded to recall everything that led to Yuan being swallowed by the rift.

Xu Jiaqi wore a deep frown on her face throughout the entire thing.

“And that’s how Yuan entered the rift…”

Once Kelan was done, a profound silence filled the room.

After a long moment of silence, Xu Jiaqi stood up and began walking to the exit.

“Where are you going?” Senior Bai inquired.

“I’m going to speak with an expert. Then, I am going to head down to the Fourth Heaven,” she said.

“Don’t take this the wrong way, but even if we go down there, what can we do? Nobody has ever returned from the other side.” Senior Bai shook his head.

“…” Xu Jiaqi halted her movement after hearing his words.

The most uptodat𝓮 n𝒐vels are published on freёwebnovel.com.

A moment later, she walked out of the room without responding.

Senior Bai looked at Kelan, who appeared to be on the verge of tears from fear, and said, “Let’s go.”

“Where…?” he asked.

“The Fourth Heaven.”

While Xu Jiaqi disappeared somewhere, Senior Bai and Kelan made their way to the Fourth Heaven. However, due to Senior Bai’s identity and position within the Celestial Overlords, more work and effort were required before he could be permitted to enter the Fourth Heaven.

Upon arriving at the Endless Beast Den, Senior Bai was surprised to see many experts there.

“The Three Grand Sects, huh? How are they involved?” Senior Bai asked Kelan.

Kelan didn’t go into detail and only recalled the important parts when he explained the situation, so Senior Bai wasn’t aware the Three Grand Sects were involved.

“Actually, they were the ones who were fighting with Yuan,” he explained.

“Huh? The Three Grand Sects? Why?”

Kelan explained how Yuan thought it was silly to monetize hunting wild magical beasts, which started the whole fight.

Senior Bai rubbed his eyes in a stressed manner and sighed, “What a headache.”

They descended shortly after.

A few days before Senior Bai and Kelan arrived at the Endless Beast Den, the Sect Leaders of the Three Grand Sects gathered there after being informed of the situation.

“E-excuse me…” The Sect Leader of the Grand Mystic Sect approached Xi Meili, who hadn’t moved from her spot since Kelan left.

“…”

Xi Meili didn’t respond.

Despite being ignored, the Sect Leader wasn’t offended, or more precisely, didn’t dare to be offended.

He continued, “I am Du Daomi, the Sect Leader of the Grand Mystic Sect. I would like to apologize for what happened to your friend—”

“Save your breath.” Xi Meili suddenly interrupted. “We both know you don’t care.”

The Sect Leader immediately became speechless. After a moment of silence, he returned to the other two Sect Leaders.

“It’s pointless. We might actually make things worse if we try to appease her,” said Du Daomi.

“So what do we do? Wait for the people from Celestial Overlord to show up and kill us?” The Sect Leader of the Grand Harmony Sect said.

“Don’t say that… the Celestial Overlord is a righteous faction. They wouldn’t kill us over something like this… right?” The Sect Leader of the Grand Defiance Sect said.

In addition to the Sect Leaders of the Three Grand Sects, those directly involved with the incident, such as the Grand Elders and Elder Hao, were also present.

“Fuck… this is all your fault, Elder Hao. Why did you have to bother that young man?” Grand Elder Du cursed, blaming him for everything.

“Don’t push your blame on me, Grand Elder! I was only doing my job! You were the one who initiated the fight!” Elder Hao exclaimed.

“So what?! I wasn’t the one who destroyed the Endless Beast Den!” Grand Elder Du turned to look at Grand Elder Tang and Grand Elder Lu, whose complexions were as pale as sheets.

“I-I only wanted to spectate! Grand Elder Tang was the one who pressured me into attacking him!” Grand Elder Lu pointed her fingers at him.

“Shut up! I was only avenging Grand Elder Du and trying to save face for the Three Grand Sects! If you weren’t so weak, Grand Elder Du, we wouldn’t be here right now!” Grand Elder Tang redirected the blame to Grand Elder Du again, going in circles.

They continued to blame each other for the next several days until Senior Bai and Kelan appeared before them.

chapters.title: Chapter 1599 Infinite Beast Spawn Rift
chapters.body:
Chapter 1599 Infinite Beast Spawn Rift

Chapter 1599 Infinite Beast Spawn Rift

When Grand Elder Lu and Grand Elder Tang noticed Kelan approaching them, they immediately alerted their Sect Leaders.

“S-Sect Leader! It’s the Celestial Overlord! They’re here!”

“What?!”

The Sect Leaders immediately froze after hearing this. They were too busy pondering how they could fix their situation to notice Senior Bai and Kelan’s presence.

‘Fuck! We messed up!’ They cried inwardly.

However, they had no time to think and immediately greeted Senior Bai and Kelan.

They clasped their hands and lowered their body in a respectful manner, “We greet the seniors from Celestial Overlord.”

Kelan responded, “If you’re here to apologize or make excuses, you can save your breath. We’re not here for you.”

“R-really…? You’re not going to punish us? Even if it might cost me my life, I have to admit that we’re partially responsible for what happened.” Du Daomi lifted his head and said.

“While you’ve admitted to being responsible, are you going to take responsibility? How are you going to do that?” Senior Bai stepped forward and asked. freёweɓnovel.com

“T-that’s… we will no longer monetize the Endless Beast Den…” Du Daomi said.

Of course, this decision was made on the spot without the other two Sect Leader’s approval. However, neither of them objected to it.

Although no magical beasts are currently spawning, it was due to the appearance of a rift. Once a rift appears, no more magical beasts would spawn for at least ten years.

Senior Bai sneered at their approach to accountability. Despite what happened to Yuan, he could not bring himself to place the blame squarely on the Three Grand Sects.

If the rift hadn’t appeared, what they did would only qualify as a skirmish since they wouldn’t have been able to hurt Yuan, much less kill him.

The Celestial Overlords were a righteous faction, and causing trouble for the Three Grand Sects over this unfortunate incident would only tarnish their reputation.

The next moment, Senior Bai walked past them and approached Xi Meili.

“How are you doing? I am Bai Linyu, a friend of Yuan.”

‘Bai Linyu?! The Sect Master of the Gates of Heaven?!’

Those from the Three Great Sects were greatly shocked and bewildered after learning Senior Bai’s identity.

This content is taken from freёwebnovel.com.

Senior Bai was a prominent figure not only in the upper heavens but also revered in the lower heavens for his virtue. To ascend the ranks of the Celestial Overlords, one must accumulate good karma through acts of righteousness, so one could imagine how much Senior Bai had done to reach his rank of Overlord.

Furthermore, Senior Bai was the Sect Leader of the Gates of Heaven, a top-tier sect that spanned across the Fifth Heaven, Seventh Heaven, and Supreme Heaven.

Not many sects from the upper heavens establish branches in the lower heavens due to resource constraints, but those that do are considered the elite of the elite.

“Bai…? Are you the owner of the Myriad of Techniques?” Xi Meili asked, as she recalled Yuan mentioning him to her before.

“Yes, you’re correct.” Senior Bai nodded with a friendly smile on his face.

“I see…”

After a moment of silence, Xi Meili continued, “Please tell me you know a way to save Yuan…”

The smile on Senior Bai’s face immediately disappeared, replaced with a perplexed expression.

“Unfortunately, nobody has ever returned after entering the rift. However, my superior is currently seeking an expert’s help, so there might still be hope. For now, we can only wait for my superior to show up.”

Those from the Three Grand Sects felt their heart skip a beat after hearing this information.

Senior Bai was an Overlord of Celestial Overlords, so only a Celestial Overlord would qualify to be his supervisor.

“S-Senior… does this mean a Celestial Overlord will make an appearance here…?” The Sect Leader of Grand Harmony Sect asked Kelan with a nervous look on his face.

Kelan looked at him and said, “Not just any Celestial Overlord, either. Lady Xu will make an appearance herself.”

“C-Celestial Overlord Xu Jiaqi?!?!” Du Daomi fell on his buttocks after hearing her name.

Among the few Celestial Overlords in their faction, Xu Jiaqi stood out as the most prominent and influential figure. She also had a reputation for being incredibly ruthless and merciless. Just her name alone would scare even the most vicious Immortals out there.

“Xu Jiaqi?! The Celestial Goddess?!” Grand Elder Du cried out loud.

“No… we’re doomed…” Elder Hao fell to his knees with a despaired look on his face.

“…” Kelan understood their emotions very well. In the Celestial Overlords, there was nobody more fearsome than Xu Jiaqi, and her involvement was reserved only for the most significant of tasks.

“Since we’re going to die anyway, I might as well ask. Who was that young man? And what’s his relationship with the Celestial Overlords? To make the Celestial Goddess come down to the Fourth Heaven, he must be very important to her…” Du Daomi asked.

“I don’t know about his relationship with Celestial Overlord Xu, but he’s a good friend of Senior Bai and also a General within the Celestial Overlords.”

“A General?! But he looked very young!” Grand Elder Du exclaimed in disbelief.

“That’s just how talented he was.” Kelan shook his head, his voice filled with disappointment.

Grand Elder Du swallowed nervously as he recalled Yuan effortlessly defeating him despite having a much lower cultivation. This sort of talent was almost unheard of. Unfortunately, Yuan had gone inside the rift, and the chances of him returning were pretty much zero, so he was essentially already dead.

Meanwhile, Xu Jiaqi was engaged in a conversation with the expert she had sought out with some difficulty.

“Is there a way to bring back someone who entered an Infinite Beast Spawn Rift?”

This expert, a tall, beautiful woman with brown hair, tanned skin, and green eyes, was surrounded by many powerful magical beasts as she spoke, “You should already know the answer to that question, so why waste your time coming here to ask me?”

Xu Jiaqi silently clenched her jaw and narrowed her eyes, fixing the woman with a cold, piercing glare.

chapters.title: Chapter 1600 Yuan’S Message
chapters.body:
Chapter 1600 Yuan’S Message

Chapter 1600 Yuan’s Message

The beasts surrounding the woman instantly sensed the threat in Xu Jiaqi’s gaze toward their master and bared their teeth in a defensive warning display.

In response, Xu Jiaqi’s body emitted killing intent.

“I don’t have the time to joke with you today, Huyao.” Xu Jiaqi uttered the next moment in a cold voice.

Sensing her killing intent, the beasts began to howl and roar, their cries echoing with aggression.

Seeing this, the woman named Huyao sighed, “Stop it, you idiots. You know I won’t be able to avenge you guys if you fight her and perish, right?”

“…”

The magical beasts immediately calmed down after hearing her words. Huyao continued a moment later, “To answer your question—no, there’s no way to bring back someone who’d entered such a rift. Why are you asking? Did someone you know enter a rift? It’s been a long time since I have heard of someone entering one of those.”

However, Xu Jiaqi didn’t respond to her inquires and was even walking away.

“Leaving already? What’s the rush?”

“If you don’t have any helpful insights, I have no reason to stay here any longer.” Xu Jiaqi said.

Huyao showed a mysterious smile and said, “Actually, now that I think about it, there might be a way.”

Xu Jiaqi instantly halted her movement and turned around to look at Huyao, who continued speaking, “If someone as powerful and as capable as you enters a rift, perhaps you might find a way to bring them back.”

The atmosphere in the room suddenly changed drastically as an overwhelming pressure was exuded by Xu Jiaqi.

“I thought I said I didn’t come here to joke around?” Xu Jiaqi stared daggers at Huyao.

“That’s none of your business.”

“You made it my business by coming to me for advice.”

“Who said I was joking? Most of the people that went to explore the rift were run-of-the-mill Immortals. If someone like you were to explore the rift, there’s a good chance you might make it back.” Huyao explained with a brave smile.

“…”

Visit freёwebnovel.com for the 𝑏est n𝘰vel reading experience.

Xu Jiaqi remained silent, her expression contemplative, almost as if she were seriously considering the possibility presented to her. freёwebnoѵel.com

Huyao’s eyes widened at this, and she said, “Don’t tell me you’re actually thinking about it. Now I am really interested in this individual you’re trying to save.”

“That’s none of your business.”

“You made it my business by coming to me for advice.”

“You think you’re giving me advice? You’ve only given me nonsense.”

Huyao shrugged and said, “Then let me tell you this. Even if you were to enter the rift to rescue this individual, you’d need to find a rift to enter. A rift only remains open for a few moments before disappearing, so you’d need to find a new one, which is only possible by destroying Infinite Beast Spawns, which has a very low chance of spawning.”

“Once an Infinite Beast Spawn is destroyed, you will need to wait at least a decade before you get another chance, and the last time a rift opened up was several thousand years ago.”

“I have no intention of entering the rift, but thanks for nothing.” Xu Jiaqi left shortly after.

“Never easy to please that woman…” Huyao shook her head with a smile.

A few days later, Xu Jiaqi arrived at the Endless Beast Den. However, given her prominent status, she discreetly made her way into the Fourth Heaven to avoid drawing unnecessary attention.

This time, the Sect Leaders of the Three Grand Sects made sure to pay attention to their surroundings and noticed Xu Jiaqi’s arrival almost instantly.

“Who are they?” Xu Jiaqi pointed to those from the Three Grand Sects with her gaze.

Senior Bai gave her a quick explanation of their involvement.

After learning of the full situation, Xu Jiaqi’s gaze remained on them for a few moments, her thoughts unknown.

The Sect Leaders were so nervous that none of them dared to utter a word without Xu Jiaqi’s permission, and even their greeting was done in silence.

In the end, Xu Jiaqi decided to ignore them and asked Senior Bai, “Any news on Yuan or the rift?”

He shook his head and said, “Unfortunately not. What about your end?”

“Nothing useful,” she sighed.

“So there’s nothing we can do besides wait for Yuan to return by himself?” Xi Meili asked.

“…”

Xu Jiaqi silently nodded.

Overwhelmed by emotion, Xi Meili’s composure shattered, and she began to weep, releasing a torrent of tears she had restrained for more than a week.

Seeing this, the urge to go after Yuan grew stronger inside Xu Jiaqi’s heart.

However, just as they pondered how to comfort Xi Meili, they noticed several individuals approaching them from a distance. Four individuals, to be precise.

“Hm? Those ladies are…” Senior Bai recognized them, as he’d seen them at the Myriad of Techniques before.

They were Meixiu, Chu Liuxiang, Li Jinxi, and Lan Yingying.

“W-what are you guys doing here?” Xi Meili wiped her tears and forced herself to stop crying to ask them.

“Yuan told us to come here,” Meixiu said.

“What? Yuan? How…?” Xi Meili wore a puzzled look on her face, as did everyone else there.

“Um… let’s just say I have a method to contact Yuan even when we’re far away from each other. Anyway, he wanted me to tell you not to worry about him.”

“W-wait a minute. Let’s slow down a little. Who are you guys? And where is Yuan now?” Xu Jiaqi stepped forward and asked.

“They’re Yuan’s friends. I have seen them with him at the Myriad of Techniques on several occasions.” Senior Bai quickly explained.

Meixiu nodded and said, “But who are you…? We were only expecting Xi Meili.”

She logged out of Cultivation Online several days after Yuan wrote the note. After reading it, she gathered the others and rushed to the Endless Beast Den to explain the situation to Xi Meili. However, only Xi Meili was mentioned in Yuan’s note, and they weren’t acquainted with anyone else there.

“This may be our first time meeting like this, but I have seen you guys before with Yuan at the Myriad of Techniques, which I own.” Senior Bai said.

“They’re from his faction, and they came here after what happened to Yuan.” Xi Meili further explained.

“I see…” “Anyway, hurry up and tell us about Yuan. Is he okay? What’s his situation like?” Xu Jiaqi inquired in an impatient manner.

chapters.title: Chapter 1601 A Forgotten Rumor
chapters.body:
Chapter 1601 A Forgotten Rumor

Chapter 1601 A Forgotten Rumor

“According to Yuan, he was taken to a place called the Primal Expanse after going through the rift,” said Meixiu after Xu Jiaqi’s impatient inquiry into Yuan’s situation. “Primal Expanse? Where is that? I have never heard of this place before.” Senior Bai mumbled out loud.

Xu Jiaqi looked at Kelan with a questioning gaze. Seeing this, Kelan shook his head and said, “No, I haven’t heard of this place, either.”

“Primal Expanse…?”

However, Xi Meili’s face was ponderous, almost as if she was trying to recall something.

Xu Jiaqi noticed this and quickly asked, “You have heard of it?” “I think I have heard of it, but I don’t remember where or when…” she sighed.

Meixiu then spoke, “This is what Yuan said—the Primal Expanse appears to be its own world that exists outside the Nine Heavens, and there are no humans here, only Magical Beasts they call Primals and Predators.”

Upon hearing this, a spark of recognition flickered in Xi Meili’s mind, prompting her to exclaim, “I remember now! I recall reading about it in an ancient scroll owned by my father when I was very young. It claimed that Magical Beasts and Divine Beasts didn’t simply come into existence but actually traveled to the Nine Heavens from another world called Primal Expanse!”

“When I asked my father about it, he said it was a rumor that was once popular during the Primordial Era but has since faded into obscurity and forgotten by most people.”

Xu Jiaqi wore a serious look on her face as she digested this information.

She turned to look at Meixiu and asked, “Did Yuan say anything else about this place?”

“No, but he said that he’d give me an update once he explores the world a little longer. Ah, he also said he’s safe and to not worry about him and that he will definitely return to the Nine Heavens as soon as possible.”

“I’m relieved to hear that he’s safe for now, but he still needs to find a way back to the Nine Heavens. Moreover, the existence of this Primal Expanse has serious implications now that we’ve confirmed its existence,” Senior Bai said.

“Even if we know about its existence, what can we do about it?” Kelan shrugged. “It exists outside the Nine Heavens, and those who have gone there never returned. It might as well not exist.”

Xu Jiaqi suddenly glared at him with a dangerous gaze. Kelan trembled when he realized that he’d screwed up by essentially saying that Yuan would never return from the Primal Expanse.

“I believe in Yuan. He’ll definitely return to us.” Lan Yingying suddenly said.

Sometime later, Senior Bai asked Xu Jiaqi, “What are you going to do now?”

“I’m going to see if I can learn more about the Primal Expanse, so I’ll leave now. Make sure to update me whenever Yuan gives an update.” “You heard it yourself, Kelan. If I am not available, you can directly notify the Celestial Overlord.” Senior Bai said to Kelan.

“Yes…” Kelan acknowledged. He never thought he would work as a mere messenger, given his position in the faction, but he did not dare to complain and considered this as punishment for his failure to protect Yuan.

Senior Bai and Xu Jiaqi left the scene and returned to the upper heavens shortly later.

“What now?” Chu Liuxiang asked.

“Well… there’s nothing we can really do until Yuan updates us, so I guess we can return to training,” Meixiu said.

Upon hearing this, Kelan said, “If you need training, how about I train you guys? I don’t mean to brag, but nobody trains as hard as me in the faction. I have nothing else to do, and I need to stay by your side to contact Yuan.”

They looked at him with doubtful gazes.

“W-what’s with those looks?! Do you not believe me?! How about this? If even one of you doesn’t see any significant improvements after training with me for a month, I’ll give each of you an Empyrean-grade treasure!”

Kelan suddenly realized something and quickly said, “Ah, let me introduce myself first. My name’s Kelan, and I was assigned to be Yuan’s bodyguard. We’re from the same faction, and I am his senior by one rank.”

Meixiu and the others began introducing themselves afterward.

‘This bastard is surrounded by so many beautiful women. Are they his lovers?’ Kelan wondered inwardly.

Once the introductions were over, Kelan turned to look at those from the Three Grand Sects and said to them, “Hey, I need a favor.”

“O-of course! Whatever you need!” Du Daomi immediately responded.

“We’re going to borrow the training facilities in your sect for a little. Don’t worry, I will compensate you for the troubles.”

“Please, you don’t need to worry about any compensation. You can use our sects as much as you want for free. We’ll even give you your own living quarters.”

Sometime later, Kelan and the group of ladies followed Du Daomi to the Grand Mystic Sect.

Meanwhile, in the Primal Expanse, after spending several days running because Xiao Hua had to reveal her scent, Yuan finally came to a halt.

Without even looking back, Yuan had a feeling that Yingzi was behind him.

He slowly turned around to look behind him, and sure enough, Yingzi was standing there, wearing Xiao Hua’s clothes. However, it was evident that Yingzi had never worn clothes before, given the disheveled way she had managed to put them on.

“Umm… you’re wearing them incorrectly. Let me help you.” Yuan said to her.

Yingzi silently nodded.

Yuan approached her and had to completely strip her naked before he could even begin fixing her clothes.

After helping her with the clothes, Yuan said, “Since I don’t know anything about this world, do you mind giving me a tour?”

“Where would you like to go?” she asked.

“Do you have cities in this world?”

“I don’t know what cities are,” she said.

Follow current novels on freewebnσvel.cѳm.

“A city is an area in which a large number of people—or Primals and Predators, in your case— live close together. It’s a community of sorts.” Yuan explained.

chapters.title: Chapter 1602 Sword Head Serpent’S Territory
chapters.body:
Chapter 1602 Sword Head Serpent’S Territory

Chapter 1602 Sword Head Serpent’s Territory

“A place where Primals and Predators live together? We call those ‘territories’ here. For example, we’re currently in the Sword Head Serpent’s territory.” Yingzi said.

Yuan surveyed their surroundings with a raised eyebrow and further clarified, “There’s been a slight misunderstanding. Cities also have buildings for dwelling and marketplaces where you can buy or sell things.”

“There’s no place like that in the Primal Expanse. While the idea of buying and selling exists, it’s rarely practiced. Most Predators engage in trade, but for everyone else, acquisition typically involves taking directly from others.” Yingzi said.

In the Primal Expanse, the saying ‘the strong prey on the weak’ was not just a principle but a stark reality. Here, if someone desired something, they simply took it by force. And since trading was the norm, there was no need for currencies in this world.

Yuan pondered for a moment before asking, “Then, do you know a way to leave this place?”

“I don’t understand.”

“I am trying to return to my own world,” he clarified.

“I am not sure that’s even possible.” Yingzi shook her head.

“There has to be a way. In my world, we have these specific areas where Magical Beasts—Primals spawn endlessly, and I believe they came from this world. The fact that I managed to travel to this world proves that our worlds are connected in some way.”

“Even if you say that, I have no clue. However, I may know someone who does.” “Can you take me to them?”

“In exchange for something.”

“What do you want?”

“Something from your world.”

“Do you have anything specific in mind?” Yuan asked.

“No, not really.”

Yuan pondered for a moment before retrieving several things from his spatial ring.

The first item was a spirit stone. He didn’t know if they existed in the Primal Expanse and wanted to find out. He also took out several Divine-grade treasures and some random pills. Lastly, he took out some food—mostly meat skewers that he had saved up for occasions.

“Is there anything you want?” Yingzi inspected all of the items with great interest. Her eyes were essentially sparkling with excitement.

“What is this?” Yingzi pointed to the spirit stone and asked.

“It’s a spirit stone. We, humans, use it to cultivate by absorbing the spiritual energy contained inside. We also use it as a currency to buy things.”

“What about these things? They smell very nice.” Yingzi pointed to the meat skewers next.

“They’re meat skewers. This one is made from beef. This one is from chicken. This one is made from lamb.”

Yingzi’s gaze danced back and forth between the spirit stone and meat skewers without giving the treasures even a single glance. After a moment of pondering, she pointed to the meat skewers and said, “I want these.”

Yuan nodded and handed her twelve sticks, four of each flavor.

Yingzi accepted the skewers with a smile on her face. This was Yuan’s first time seeing any real emotion on her face.

However, the unique and rich aroma of the skewers quickly permeated the area, alerting the Primals nearby.

Since skewers were unknown in the Primal Expanse, their enticing aroma was as novel and irresistible as the scent of humans, sending the Primals into a frenzy.

When Yuan noticed this, he immediately said to Yingzi, “Um… can you quickly finish eating before the others arrive?”

However, Yingzi shook her head and said, “No, I will slowly savor this food at my own pace. If anyone dares to steal from me, I will kill them.” Yingzi’s presence was still nonexistent, so the nearby Primals could only smell Yuan and the skewers.

Dragons were at the top of the food chain in the Primal Expanse, so Primals instinctively avoided them. Thus, no one dared to approach Yuan despite his relatively low cultivation level. However, this caution was abandoned once they caught the enticing scent of the meat skewers.

Yuan wanted to run away, but he also wanted to see Yingzi’s prowess. In the end, he decided to stay with her.

A few moments later, the first Primal to appear before them resembled a colossal elephant as large as a mountain, its brown skin adorned with red spots across its body.

Just its size alone was menacing enough, but its strength was also at the Divine Sovereign level.

Yet, when its gaze landed on Yingzi, who wasn’t even looking at it, the massive creature’s body trembled, and a deep sense of fear flickered in its eyes.

After a loud trumpet-like roar, the elephant turned around and stomped away faster than it approached them.

Read latest chapters at freёweɓnovel.com Only.

“What the…?” Yuan was surprised by the Primal’s reaction.

The elephant-like Primal’s reaction to Yingzi was puzzling, as she wasn’t exuding any presence or aura of danger that could explain why such a formidable creature would flee from her. This hinted at unseen powers within Yingzi that Yuan did not yet comprehend.

The next several Primals that appeared reacted similarly to the first. Each time their gaze landed on Yingzi, they displayed an intense reaction of fear, quickly turning and fleeing as if their lives depended on it. As for Yingzi, she didn’t even bother looking at the Primals that showed up and continued to enjoy the skewers in her grasp at her own place.

Many minutes later, when Yingzi reached her last skewer, Yuan suddenly sensed an unfathomable presence appear, and it was quickly approaching them.

‘What a powerful presence! This Primal must be at a level that rivals an Immortal!’ Yuan cried inwardly.

When he turned to investigate the presence, he saw a massive black snake, even larger than the elephant, with a horn-shaped like a sword protruding from its head.

“Is that the Sword Head Serpent?” Yuan asked Yingzi.

“Looks like it,” she calmly confirmed.

“Will you be fine against it?”

“…”

Yingzi didn’t respond, as she was busy chewing the meat in her mouth.

The Sword Head Serpent arrived shortly later. However, unlike the other Primals, it did not immediately run away after seeing Yingzi. Although it still showed fear in its gaze, it refused to run away for some reason.

chapters.title: Chapter 1603 Chaotic Essence
chapters.body:
Chapter 1603 Chaotic Essence

Chapter 1603 Chaotic Essence

After silently staring at Yingzi for several moments, the Sword Head Serpent spoke, “If you value your life, leave behind that meat and get out of my territory!”

Having a territory named after oneself signifies dominion over that area, and entering another’s domain without permission typically invites conflict. Should the Sword Head Serpent flee from an unwelcome guest in its own territory, it would risk becoming a laughingstock, vulnerable to attacks from others who might perceive it as weak.

This was why the Sword Head Serpent refused to flee despite its fear of Yingzi. Driven by the need to protect its territory and maintain its pride and honor, it was prepared to face even death rather than show weakness and risk losing the territory that it had ruled for many decades. free𝑤ebnovel.com

In response to the Sword Head Serpent’s threat, Yingzi simply glanced at it. Her look alone sent an unfathomable wave of fear coursing through the Sword Head Serpent, paralyzing it with dread.

However, the Sword Head Serpent somehow managed to shake off the fear and moved to strike Yingzi.

Seeing the Sword Head Serpent approach, Yingzi pointed at it with her empty skewer sticks. In the next instant, the serpent froze completely as a massive black spike emerged from the ground—or, more precisely, from its own shadow, impaling its body with lethal precision.

The life in the Sword Head Serpent’s eyes vanished instantly, and its lifeless body thudded heavily to the ground, sending a resounding echo through the area.

Yuan was left speechless by how effortlessly Yingzi had disposed of the Sword Head Serpent, which would rival the strength of an Immortal in the Nine Heavens.

‘She’s much more fearsome than I’d expected…’ he swallowed inwardly.

Once she finished all of the skewers, Yingzi said, “Okay, I’ll bring you to that individual now. Follow me.”

Yingzi suddenly disappeared into thin air.

“Huh?”

Yuan’s eyes widened with shock. He wasn’t even able to follow her movement, much less follow her in person.

Yingzi returned a few moments later and asked him, “What’s wrong? Why aren’t you following me?” “Umm… you’re too fast for me. I couldn’t even see your movements, let alone follow you,” he sighed.

“Can you slow down a little?”

“…”

“If we travel at your pace, it’ll take centries to reach our destination,” she said.

“Centries?! How massive is this place?” Yuan was dumbfounded to hear this.

“Very.”

Yuan sighed, “Is there no other way? Asking me to travel at your speed is unreasonable and, quite frankly, impossible.” “I can help you, but I want that shiny object you call spirit stone,” Yingzi said.

“Deal.” Yuan immediately retrieved the biggest and shiniest spirit stone in his possession before handing it to her.

Yingzi played around with the spirit stone for a few minutes before tossing it in inside her shadow.

‘The ability to manipulate shadow, huh?’ Yuan thought to himself.

“Okay, let’s go.”

Yingzi suddenly enveloped Yuan with a mysterious energy before bringing him with her. The method she used was similar to using Qi Manipulation to move something or someone. They were moving so fast that Yuan only saw a blurred background as they traveled.

“Yingzi, what’s this weird energy you’re using? It feels like spiritual energy, but it’s clearly not.” Yuan asked. “You mean Chaotic Essence? It’s what we use to cultivate,” she explained.

“There are two types of essence in this world. Chaotic Essence and Pure Essence. There isn’t much Pure Essence, but that doesn’t matter since we can only cultivate Chaotic Essence.”

Yuan wondered if this was the reason why Yu Ning and Xiao Hua claimed they couldn’t cultivate properly.

‘Pure Essence must be Spiritual Energy. However, this world lacks Spiritual Energy, and humans cannot absorb Chaotic Essence. It’s almost like the Nine Heavens but inverted. In that case, Chaotic Essence might be something like impurity for us.’

It didn’t take long for Yuan to understand the power sources of Primal Expanse.

“By the way, how long will it take to arrive at our destination now?” he asked a moment later.

“About half a year,” she calmly responded.

“Half a year?!” Yuan cried out loud.

He had never anticipated that reaching their destination would take so long. At this rate, he might be stuck in the Primal Expanse for much longer than he had desired. Yet, there was nothing he could do. If it weren’t for Yingzi, he might actually be stuck in this world forever. “When the Divine Heaven used to be one world, it would take Immortals hundreds of years to travel around the world without teleports.” Yu Ning suddenly recalled.

“At the speed we’re currently traveling, I wouldn’t be surprised if this little girl already surpasses the fastest flying treasure in existence,” she continued.

“Do you know what kind of bloodline she has? She appears to have the ability to manipulate shadows.” Yuan asked.

Updat𝓮d from frёewebnoѵēl.com.

“I know of several bloodlines that can control shadows to a certain extent, and from what I’ve seen so far, it doesn’t rule out any of them. I’ll let you know once I lower the candidates.”

“Alright. What about Chaotic Essence? Do you know anything similar to that in the Nine Heavens?”

Yu Ning pondered for a moment before responding, “There are several areas in the Divine Heaven where cultivators are unable to cultivate due to the chaotic spiritual energy, such as the Primordial Chaos. I have never been to these places myself, so I cannot confirm firsthand, but according to what I’ve heard from those who have experienced it, it’s similar to what I am experiencing in this world.”

‘How come I am able to absorb this Chaotic Essence?’ he wondered inwardly.

Yuan tried to absorb the spiritual energy there again, and sure enough, his body absorbed everything without any issue.

As for the quality of the energy, it was incomparably better than the Fourth Heaven. ‘Half a year, huh… What should I do in the meantime?’ Yuan began pondering how he should spend the next half a year.

chapters.title: Chapter 1604 Training With Kelan
chapters.body:
Chapter 1604 Training With Kelan

Chapter 1604 Training With Kelan

While Yuan journeyed through the Primal Expanse with Yingzi, Xu Jiaqi was traversing the Nine Heavens, gathering more information about the Primal Expanse. Since this topic was related to magical beasts, she focused on meeting the beast clans, such as the Dragon Clan and the Phoenix Clan.

At one of the most ancient Dragon Clans in the Supreme Heaven, Xu Jiaqi requested to meet with their historian.

“The Primal Expanse, huh? It’s been a very long time since someone has brought this place up.”

The Dragon Clan’s historian, Long Qi, spoke with interest as he combed his white beard.

He retrieved a large, ancient scroll and opened it, revealing all sorts of information on the Primal Expanse.

“Don’t let the amount of information here fool you, Lady Xu. They’re all just speculations and rumors. In truth, nobody has been to the Primal Expanse.”

“Then where did the rumor even come from? Who started it?” Xu Jiaqi asked.

“Do you know how some people claim they have memories of their past life? Well, some beasts vaguely recall a place called the Primal Expanse, where they lived in their past life. According to these individuals, the Primal Expanse is strictly ruled by beasts, so no humans exist there.” freewёbn૦νeɭ.com

“…”

‘It’s exactly like how Yuan described that place…’ Xu Jiaqi thought to herself.

“Do you mind if I borrow this scroll?” she asked Long Qi a moment later.

“Of course, but why are you suddenly interested in the Primal Expanse—if you don’t mind me asking?” Xu Jiaqi pondered whether she should reveal the fact that the Primal Expanse was not just a rumor but existed somewhere outside the Nine Heavens.

“I heard someone mention it, and it piqued my interest. That’s all.”

In the end, Xu Jiaqi decided not to reveal this information yet, fearing it might make the world a little chaotic.

‘I need more information about the Primal Expanse before I reveal this information… if I even should…’ she thought to herself.

After leaving the Dragon Clan, Xu Jiaqi visited the Phoenix Clan and asked for information on the Primal Expanse. She would visit multiple clans over the next several weeks, gathering as much information as she could.

Meanwhile, Meixiu and the others began their training with Kelan after a quick assessment. It only took a single five-minute spar with each of them for Kelan to understand their prowess and how to train them.

“I will spend a day training each of you, starting from the one I think is the weakest currently.” Kelan’s gaze fell on Meixiu as he spoke.

“So you’ll be first.”

“Okay,” she nodded.

‘I already knew I was quite weak compared to the others, but it still hurts.’ Meixiu sighed inwardly.

Although she had been rigorously trained in combat by her mother from a young age to serve the Yu Family, she couldn’t match Chu Liuxiang, who had the advantage of training with a cultivator since childhood.

Kelan shifted his gaze to Chu Liuxiang next, “You’ll be training with me after her.”

Then he looked at Lan Yingying and said, “You’re third.”

“On the fourth day, you’ll be training with me.” Kelan looked at Xi Meili, who wore a surprised expression on her face.

This content is taken from fгeewebnovёl.com.

“Huh? Me?” she pointed at herself, seemingly in disbelief.

“Yes, you,” Kelan confirmed.

Xi Meili turned to look at Li Jinxi for a moment before looking at Kelan again.

“You think she’s stronger than me? Surely, you are mistaken.”

Xi Meili couldn’t believe that she would be weaker than Li Jinxi, who had a much lower cultivation.

Kelan shook his head and explained, “While your cultivation is indeed superior, when it comes to experience and execution in combat, she is superior.”

“How is that possible?! There’s no way she has more experience than me! I have lived for over 10 thousand years!” Xi Meili refused to believe that someone as young as Li Jinxi would have more combat experience than her.

“You don’t have to believe me. The two of you can spar with each other—without any cultivation.” Kelan shrugged.

Xi Meili looked at Li Jinxi, who calmly nodded and said, “I can do it.”

Xi Meili then said, “Please don’t take this the wrong way. I just have a hard time believing that you have more experience than me.”

“Don’t worry, I won’t.” Li Jinxi said.

They entered one of the arenas available at the Grand Mystic Sect’s training ground sometime later.

“For this spar, you are not allowed to use your cultivation or techniques, so you’ll be limited to just your weapon and the basics. Ah, movement techniques are allowed.”

“Good luck.” Xi Meili said to her.

“You too.”

While Xi Meili and Li Jinxi fought each other, Yuan asked Yingzi to stop for a little so he could write a note for Meixiu.

“Where did you go just now? You never gave me an answer.” Yingzi questioned him.

“Uhh… it’s a little complicated.” Yuan wasn’t sure how to explain Cultivation Online in a way that she would understand.

He pointed at his body and said, “You see this body? It’s actually not my real body but a part of my soul. My real body is somewhere far, far away, and I have the ability to return to my real body whenever I want. Whenever I return to my real body, this one will disappear until I return. Do you understand?”

Yingzi took a moment to understand his words. Eventually, she nodded and said, “I understand. Where is your real body? Is it in the Nine Heavens?”

“No, it’s actually outside the Nine Heavens, sort of like the Primal Expanse. However, that world is much smaller.”

“If this isn’t your real body, why do you need to find a way to leave the Primal Expanse? Can’t you just create another body?” Yingzi then asked.

“Even if this isn’t my real body, it’s still part of my soul, which is arguably even more valuable than my physical body, so I can’t just abandon it,” Yuan said.

“Is that so…”

They resumed their journey shortly after.

chapters.title: Chapter 1605 Meixiu’S Training
chapters.body:
Chapter 1605 Meixiu’S Training

Chapter 1605 Meixiu’s Training

‘This is ridiculous!’ Xi Meili cried inwardly as she desperately defended herself against Li Jinxi’s endless assault.

It’d only been several minutes since they started their spar, yet she was already pushed into a corner. Xi Meili had never anticipated that someone other than Yuan could possess such outrageous talents.

“Wow… Li Jinxi’s growth has been tremendous lately. What kind of training did she undergo in seclusion?” Chu Liuxiang muttered, her voice filled with awe. Despite having trained for the same amount of time, Li Jinxi’s progress had far outstripped her own.

“Her experience doesn’t match her age, but I can say the same thing about Yuan and many other geniuses out there,” Kelan said. “In this world, there are three types of talent. Those who are gifted just enough to be above the average, those who stand above the majority as true geniuses, and then we have monsters that defy logic.”

“Li Jinxi’s talent would enter the monster category. As for Yuan… I believe he is a separate kind of monster—the type that dominates over other monsters.”

Many minutes later, Li Jinxi barely secured her victory against Xi Meili.

“You’re very strong. Other than two other people, you’re the third person ever to push me this far…” Li Jinxi said afterward, her body soaked in sweat.

“Besides Yuan, who is the other individual?” Xi Meili asked as she lay on the arena with a dazed look on her face.

“She’s my—our mentor.” Li Jinxi said as she turned to look at Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang.

“…”

After a moment of silence, Xi Meili released a deep sigh and said, “I admit defeat. You’re definitely better than me when it comes to fighting experience. I apologize for looking down on you.”

Li Jinxi extended her hand and smiled, “It’s okay.”

Xi Meili accepted the hand and was pulled back to her feet.

“We will begin our training tomorrow,” Kelan said to them before dismissing them a moment later.

Everyone returned to their own living quarter, which was provided by the Grand Mystic Sect.

Meixiu logged off Cultivation Online after returning to her room.

The first thing she did was look for Yuan’s note, and sure enough, there was one by the bed.

Her eyes widened with surprise after reading it. frёewebnoѵēl.com

“Half a year just to visit someone who may or may not know how to leave the Primal Expanse?” Meixiu knew it would take a while, but she hadn’t anticipated it would be to this extent.

Once she finished reading, Meixiu began writing her own note. She mentioned their meeting with Senior Bai and Xu Jiaqi and their training sessions with Kelan and suggested scheduling a meeting to discuss their experiences in more detail in a few weeks during the Eternal Lotuses boot camp.

The following day, Meixiu began her training with Kelan.

“I see, so he won’t be back for at least half a year. That will be more than enough time to transform you guys.”

“Now… in order to wield a bow properly, you must first build a foundation and strengthen your body, so we’ll begin with tempering your body.”

Kelan retrieved a spatial pouch and handed it to her.

“You will use these when you’re not training with me to temper your body. It will be very painful, but it’s the fastest and most effective way to temper your body.”

“I also grabbed a few body-tempering techniques for you. You can choose the one you like the most. Since you’re not trying to become a Body Refiner like myself, you don’t need any crazy body-tempering technique.”

Meixiu spent the next hour reading the techniques before choosing one.

“Good. Then, we’ll focus on your technique today. We will begin your training next week.”

Kelan spent the entire day helping Meixiu comprehend the technique. At the end of the day, Meixiu returned to her living quarters and began her body-tempering process.

Inside the spatial pouch provided by Kelan contained all sorts of medicine that could only be found in the upper heavens. While they were not priceless treasures, it wasn’t something that could be obtained easily.

This wasn’t Meixiu’s first time tempering her body since she would occasionally temper her real body. In fact, every member of the Demon Sealing Faction tempered their body to a certain extent. Not only did it strengthen their bodies, but it also helped them with Liya’s spartan-like training.

After following Kelan’s instructions inside a jade slip, Meixiu tossed a specific medicine into the bathtub.

After waiting for half an hour, she entered the hot water.

The moment the tip of her toes touched the water, intense pain coursed through her entire body. “Ah!?” Meixiu exclaimed, immediately jumping back and staring at the bathtub with wide eyes. She had endured many painful moments in the past—from her first body-tempering experience to training with Liya—but none compared to the excruciating pain she had just experienced.

However, she could already feel her body strengthening just from that slight touch.

Meixiu silently stood there, staring at the bathtub with a nervous gaze.

Eventually, she clenched her teeth and entered the bathtub again, sticking an entire foot inside the water.

“Mmh!?!?” Meixiu nearly lost consciousness from the pain but managed to endure it.

Updat𝓮d from frёewebnoѵēl.com.

Many minutes later, once she felt like she could endure more, she submerged her other foot into the water.

An hour later, she submerged the rest of her body in the water, which increased the pain substantially.

Meixiu couldn’t handle the pain for long and jumped out of the bath a minute later to catch a breath.

Over the next several hours, Meixiu repeated this process, slowly increasing the time she could remain submerged in the water.

The medicine was so potent that even after an entire day, the water retained its full strength, as effective as when it was first prepared.

As for Meixiu’s condition, she felt better than ever before, her body brimming with energy.

Although the experience was incredibly painful, the rewards were significant enough to motivate her to continue.

chapters.title: Chapter 1606 Qi Resistance Ball
chapters.body:
Chapter 1606 Qi Resistance Ball

Chapter 1606 Qi Resistance Ball

The day after Meixiu’s training, Chu Liuxiang met Kelan at the training grounds.

“As someone who specializes in spiritual attacks, you need to focus on increasing your spiritual energy, spiritual strength, and your control. While increasing your spiritual energy will come naturally through cultivation, in order to increase your spiritual strength and control, you need specific techniques and rigorous training.

Kelan retrieved a black, round metal ball from his spatial ring. At first glance, this metal ball appeared quite ordinary, but it gave out a peculiar aura that made Chu Liuxiang feel anxious.

“This is a Qi Resistance Ball. As its name suggests, it has very high resistance against spiritual energy, even outright repelling it if it’s weak enough. Your training will consist of levitating this Qi Resistance Ball until you can hover it above your head and keep it there for an hour. Give it a try.”

Chu Liuxiang nodded and proceeded to wrap her spiritual energy around the ball. However, the moment her spiritual energy touched the ball, she immediately lost control, causing it to disperse into the air.

“What?”

Chu Liuxiang’s eyes widened with surprise, and she quickly tried again, only to find that the results were the same.

“Not only is your spiritual energy too weak, but your control is also atrocious. The spiritual energy was too flimsy. Watch how I do it.” Kelan said before wrapping his spiritual energy around the ball, lifting it into the air with seemingly no effort.

“Can you see the difference?” he asked her.

“Yes… While my spiritual energy is spread apart like a cloud of mist, yours are tightly compressed and wrap around the ball like sheets,” Chu Liuxiang explained, highlighting the difference between the way they manipulated their spiritual energy.

Kelan nodded in approval and took out a technique before handing it to her, “This will teach you how to properly control your spiritual energy.”

For the next two hours, Chu Liuxiang studied the technique. Once she had memorized it, she tried to pick up the Qi Resistance Ball again.

However, while her control over her spiritual energy had improved, it was still nowhere near being able to touch the ball, much less pick it up.

Chu Liuxiang returned to studying the technique again and Kelan would give her advice here and there.

By the end of the day, while Chu Liuxiang still failed to pick up the ball, she was able to cover it with her spiritual energy without it dispersing.

“You can keep the Qi Resistance Ball to practice on your own time. I want significant improvements by our next session,” said Kelan.

It was Lan Yingying’s turn the next day.

“Before we begin, I must admit that out of the entire group, it’s going to be the trickiest to train you, who specializes in Sacred Fire,” Kelan said to her.

“I can’t use Sacred Fire, and I am unfamiliar with such techniques. However, I know someone who is, and I have already contacted them for assistance. Until they respond, I will have you focus on strengthening your spiritual energy like Chu Liuxiang.”

When she first started, Lan Yingying was unable to touch the ball, just like Chu Liuxiang. However, after a day of training with the Qi Resistance Ball, Lan Yingying managed to levitate the ball an inch off the ground for three seconds.

The next day, it was Xi Meili’s turn to train. freewebnσvel.cѳm

“Since your lack of experience is your biggest weakness, that’s what we’ll be focusing on,” Kelan said to her.

The fastest way to gain experience was through practice, so they immediately began sparring with each other.

However, even after sparring for an entire day without any breaks, Xi Meili was not even close to landing a hit on Kelan. It felt like she was fighting a ghost—one that could anticipate all of her movements and perfectly avoid them.

“After spending an entire day analyzing your style, I understand why you’re lacking in experience despite having ten thousand years of experience. Your fighting style is outdated.” Kelan said at the end of their training.

“O-outdated? How is that even possible?” Xi Meili said.

Typically, in the cultivation world, the older something is, the more valued it tends to be. This is why ancient treasures and techniques are highly sought after.

Updat𝓮d fr𝙤m ƒгeeweɓn૦vel.com.

“Not everything improves with age. In some areas, modern knowledge surpasses the old—your fighting style being one of them.”

“…”

Xi Meili was left speechless, unable to refute Kelan’s point. The style she had learned was indeed ancient, dating back millions of years to when the Xi Family first entered seclusion. And within such isolation, advancements tend to occur at a much slower pace.

“It’s not going to be easy to adjust your fighting style, considering you’ve spent thousands of years honing it and engraving its movements into your muscle memory. But once you manage to do so, you’ll see a significant increase in performance,” Kelan explained.

“I will find you a new style that resembles your current style in a few days. We can start your real training next week.”

Li Jinxi showed up the next day for her training.

“Frankly, your techniques are impeccable, and I have no criticisms there. However, it’s clear that your body is struggling to keep up with your movements,” Kelan said.

He continued, “In order to narrow the gap between your abilities and your body’s limits, you will focus on tempering your body, much like Meixiu. However, you will need to push even further than her—perhaps two steps further.”

“It’s going to be painful, but once you’ve achieved a physique that can handle your abilities, your prowess will skyrocket to unimaginable levels.” “Will it be enough to beat Yuan?” Li Jinxi suddenly asked an unexpected question.

“That’s…” Kelan was caught off guard by such a question and didn’t know how to respond for a moment.

“I’ll be honest with you. The chances of catching up to him are near zero, much less defeating him. However, nothing is impossible in this world. With enough luck and dedication, even a cripple can even surpass a god.”

“That’s good enough for me.” Li Jinxi said with an eager smile.

Sometime later, Kelan spent several hours sparring with her before sending her home with a bunch of body-tempering medicine, but they were several times more potent than the ones he gave Meixiu.

chapters.title: Chapter 1607 The Real Boot Camp
chapters.body:
Chapter 1607 The Real Boot Camp

Chapter 1607 The Real Boot Camp

In the blink of an eye, several weeks have passed since Yuan entered the Primal Expanse, and his group started training with Kelan.

“We’ll return in three days.” Meixiu notified Kelan of their absence due to Eternal Lotuses’ boot camp.

“That’s fine. Make sure to pass on my message to Yuan.” Kelan nodded in acknowledgment.

“Of course.”

After logging off together, Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang were greeted by the sound of heavenly music coming from their balcony. When they looked at the balcony, they could see Yuan playing the zither.

They joined him on the balcony and waited for him to finish. Sometime later, they talked about their training with Kelan and conveyed his message.

“How’s your training with Kelan? I haven’t known him for long, but he’s definitely one of the strongest people I’ve met.” Yuan asked.

“It’s only been a few weeks, and my prowess has already increased significantly. While Manager Liya trains us through brute force and teaches us how to fight, Kelan trains us individually and focuses on our weaknesses and foundation.” Chu Liuxiang said.

Meixiu nodded, agreeing with her, and said, “I never thought having a proper teacher would have such drastic differences. I wouldn’t be surprised if he has many disciples under him.”

“I guess I’ll have to thank him once I return.” Yuan smiled.

“Speaking of your situation, how is it?” Meixiu asked.

“The same as usual. We’ve been traveling at a speed that even I cannot comprehend without any rest, but we’re still many months away from our destination. The Primal Expanse is unfathomably massive.”

“Kelan has a message for you. He said that Lady Xu is currently researching the Primal Expanse and would you like to learn as much about it as you can.”

He nodded, “I was already planning on doing that. Although the Primal Expanse exists outside the Nine Heavens, it is still connected to them in some way.”

The sourc𝗲 of this content is freēwēbηovel.c૦m.

Sometime later, the Demon Sealing Faction made their way to the Eternal Lotuses’ residence.

When they arrived, around 90 percent of their members were standing in a formation, waiting for their arrival.

“There’s more people here than I expected.” Wang Ming commented after he saw this. He’d only expected half of them after what they experienced last time.

The Eternal Lotuses members trembled after seeing Wang Ming and the others smile.

“Even if the majority of you quit after today, I applaud you for showing up and giving it a chance despite what you experienced during the trial.” Shi Lang said to them.

“What you will experience in the next three days will be much harsher than the trial, but you will definitely become much stronger.” Wang Bingbing said.

The group swallowed nervously at such words, and many of them had regretful expressions on their faces.

After the introductions, they were split into smaller groups again to make training more effective.

However, Yuan did not participate and only watched as a spectator. Though, he would occasionally give advice to the instructor, as well as the participants.

The boot camp was gruesome and spartan-like, with the participants training until they threw up or fainted. Ten percent of the participants gave up within an hour, and fifty percent gave up in just the first half of the day.

By the end of the day, only thirty percent remained.

“Good job enduring today. Now, you just need to endure the next two.” Wang Ming said, reminding them that the boot camp was a three-day event.

One of the participants then asked out loud, “That’s physically impossible! We’re all completely drained! We won’t have enough energy tomorrow, much less for the remaining two days!”

Yuan stepped forward and said, “Don’t worry. We have something that will not only help you with your recovery but also strengthen your body simultaneously.”

“Really? What is it?”

“A medicine bath,” he said. “There’s a public hot spring available on the Dragon Spiral Mountain. I have already reserved the whole place, too.”

“A hot spring?”

The participants were excited upon hearing this.

After giving them some time to recover enough energy to walk, they descended the mountain until they reached the lower area, where the hot spring was located.

An old lady was waiting for them outside when they arrived.

“Welcome to the Dragon Hot Spring, Young Master Yuan.” The old lady bowed to him respectfully.

The hot spring was divided into two sections: one for males and another for females, each spacious enough to comfortably accommodate a thousand people. As the exhausted participants submerged themselves in the medicinally enhanced waters, they felt the exhaustion swiftly drain from their bodies.

“Wow! What kind of magical water is this?! I have never felt anything like this before!”

“All of my exhaustion went away like smoke! How is that possible?!”

The participants couldn’t believe the hot spring’s potent effect. “If I had known there was a place like this here, I would’ve come here every day!” However, Wang Bingbing said, “Actually, this is just a normal hot spring, so you wouldn’t get to experience this if you come here at another time. This medicine bath was specifically prepared for you by Yuan.”

Meanwhile, at the male’s side, Wang Ming asked, “What kind of medicine did you put in here, Yuan? I would like to bathe in this once in a while.”

“Medicine? I didn’t put any medicine in here,” he calmly said.

“Huh? Then what did you put in here?”

“I fused my spiritual energy with the water, so you’re bathing in my spiritual energy,” he revealed.

“What…?”

Everyone there looked at him with wide eyes.

“You can do such a thing with spiritual energy? I didn’t know that.” Shi Lang muttered.

“The concept is similar to transferring your spiritual energy to another individual to replenish their energy. However, in this case, since there isn’t any method to contain the spiritual energy, you will need to constantly fuse your spiritual energy with the water, or it’ll quickly lose effect.”

“In other words, only you can do something like this.” Wu Zao said.

“Once you increase your cultivation, you can also do it. However, unless someone does it for you, it wouldn’t benefit you.” Yuan explained.

chapters.title: Chapter 1608 The Real Boot Camp(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1608 The Real Boot Camp(2)

Chapter 1608 The Real Boot Camp(2)

The members of Eternal Lotuses soaked in the hot spring for two hours. By the time they emerged, not only had all traces of their exhaustion vanished, but they also felt stronger than they had at the start of the day.

When they returned to their residence, those who had quit or didn’t participate in the boot camp were absolutely bewildered to see them so energetic.

“What the hell? Why do you all look so energetic? You spent the entire day training at their boot camp, right? I was only there for four hours, but I am still exhausted!”

“Of course, we were exhausted. However, they had us bathe in a medicine bath at the Dragon Hot Spring, relieving us of all exhaustion in just two hours!”

“It not only made our exhaustion disappear, it even strengthened our bodies and boosted our cultivation!” “Dragon Hot Spring? There’s a hot spring here?”

“What?! Such a place exists?! I’m going to go bathe there right now!” Since the members that bathed in Yuan’s spiritual energy weren’t aware of the hot spring’s true nature, they didn’t stop the other members from rushing to the Dragon Hot Spring.

When these members arrived at the hot spring and soaked in the water, they were baffled when they were still exhausted and nothing was happening.

“Were they messing with us because we didn’t participate in the boot camp? This is just a normal hot spring!”

“Surely, they can’t be that petty.”

“They called it a medicine bath, right? Perhaps they’d already absorbed all of the medicine.” Someone there made a sound judgment.

“I guess the medicine bath is only a privilege for those who endured that hellish training.”

These members quickly left the hot spring after realizing that it had lost its healing effects, and most of them contemplated on giving the boot camp another chance.

Later that night, the Demon Sealing Faction had dinner together.

“Have you guys done any Faction Wars since our last one?” Yuan asked them.

“Who would dare to send us a challenge after what you did to the Fiery Garden of Hell?” Wang Ming shook his head.

“The Faction War scene has exploded in popularity and participation because of the formation, though,” Meifeng commented.

“Any movements from the Ten Great Families or the government?” Yuan then asked.

“No, they have been awfully silent. However, I was told by Manager Liya that there have been many people watching the Dragon Spiral Mountain.”

“She’s not doing anything about it?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“She said that as long as they don’t enter the Dragon Spiral Mountain or cause trouble, they have no reason to act.”

“Sounds fair.” Yuan nodded.

“Actually… I have something to say.” Wang Xiuying suddenly raised her hand. “What’s the matter?”

“I don’t know if this is related, but there’s been many people trying to contact me through my grandfather.”

“Doctor Wang?” Yuan frowned slightly at this news.

“I wouldn’t think too deeply into it. After all, our exposure exploded after Yan Xiaoxiao’s interview was released.” Xu Murong commented.

“Murong’s right. In fact, it would be weird if nobody tried to reach out to you.” Hong Xiuquan said.

“I guess you’re right…” Wang Xiuying nodded.

“It’s never bad to be cautious,” Yuan said.

He continued, “Speaking of Doctor Wang, how is he doing lately?”

“The same as usual, I guess. Ah, he started cultivating recently.”

“Oh? Then I will need to send him some support.” Yuan said.

Although it was a job, Doctor Wang looked out for him since childhood. If it weren’t for Doctor Wang, he might’ve died before Cultivation Online came out.

“It’s okay. I have already sent him some pills and resources to help with his cultivation.” Wang Xiuying said.

“Still, I should—”

“Trust me, it’s fine.” Wang Xiuying interrupted. “I bet you’re going to give him valuable resources, correct? His cultivation is too low to even use them. You can help him once he increases his cultivation a little more, okay?”

“If you say so…” Yuan nodded.

Later that night, Yuan and Meixiu spent the night together in Meixiu’s old room while Chu Liuxiang slept alone.

After spending several hours passionately embracing each other, Yuan commented on her physique at the end, “I can tell that you’ve been training really hard. Your body is incomparably sturdier than before.”

“Huh?! Is my body that hard?!” Meixiu began panicking, worried that she’d tempered her body too much.

Yuan chuckled and said, “I didn’t mean literally. Your body is still very soft and pleasing to touch, so there’s no need to worry.”

Meixiu blushed at such words.

The next day, when the Demon Sealing Faction arrived for the second day of boot camp, they were pleasantly surprised to find that many of the participants who had quit the previous day had returned, ready to continue their training.

“What happened? What made you change your heart?” Shi Lang asked them.

These returning participants explained that they were motivated to rejoin after seeing the significant improvements their peers had made after just one day of training. “I’m glad to hear that you’ll give it another chance, but actions speak louder than words. With that said, no matter how many times you quit, we won’t stop you from returning.” Wang Ming said.

They started training shortly after, with the second day proving even more intense than the first. Many of those who had quit on the first day found themselves unable to withstand the increased rigor, while those who had endured the initial day’s challenges barely managed to hold on through the entire session.

Surprisingly, by the end of the second day, a higher number of participants completed the training compared to the first day. Although the increase was modest, it was still an impressive achievement, given the heightened intensity of the day’s challenges.

Soon, they were rewarded by the spiritual hot spring.

“Wow! This is even better than I anticipated! I’m so glad I held on!” One of the participants who had quit on the first day exclaimed after experiencing the hot spring.

“Instructor Wang, only those who have endured the training can enjoy this luxury, correct?” Another asked.

“That’s right. Those who quit won’t be allowed in here.” Wang Bingbing confirmed.

chapters.title: Chapter 1609 Half A Year Later
chapters.body:
Chapter 1609 Half A Year Later

Chapter 1609 Half a Year Later

After spending two hours at the Dragon Hot Spring, everyone returned to their living quarters. frёewebnoѵel.ƈo๓

Only two days have passed since they started training, but the members of Eternal Lotuses who didn’t quit were already experiencing tremendous growth. All had at least two breakthroughs, while some even had four breakthroughs. As for those who had stopped training on the first day but returned on the second day, they had at least one breakthrough. The rest of the Eternal Lotuses were envious of their exponential growth and contemplated returning to training.

Later that night, Yuan spent the rest of the night with Chu Liuxiang.

“Is the Primal Expanse a dangerous place?” Chu Liuxiang asked at the end of their passionate session, which lasted for hours.

“Yes, it’s a gruesome place where only the strong survive.”

“Doesn’t that apply to our world, as well?”

“It’s much more so over there, where humans don’t exist. When humans want something, the normal ones will negotiate for it. However, according to what I was told, they forcefully acquire everything in the Primal Expanse. It’s a place where laws don’t exist and the strong rules over the weak.”

“The law of the jungle, huh? I guess that makes sense since it’s a world of beasts. Be safe over there.”

Yuan smiled and said, “Unless my Immortal Monarch’s Dominion doesn’t work on the beasts there, I should be relatively safe no matter what I am up against. I also have an expert guiding me.”

He had been curious whether the Immortal Monarch’s Dominion would affect the beasts in the Primal Expanse, but he hadn’t yet had the opportunity to test it.

The following day, even more participants showed up at the boot camp than on the second day. However, as many had expected, the third day of boot camp was even more torturous than the previous two days combined. Every single participant had either puked or lost consciousness at least once.

Fortunately for them, the training ended earlier than anticipated, and they were rewarded with a four-hour spiritual bath.

“Good work, everyone. We’ll come back next month for the next boot camp session!”

The next day, everyone from the Demon Sealing Faction except Meifeng and Yuan went to the mountain’s peak to train with Liya.

“Where have the three of you been lately? You haven’t been showing up to training.” Liya asked Meixiu, Chu Liuxiang, and Li Jinxi, who had spent most of their time training with Kelan in Cultivation Online.

Meixiu explained the situation to Liya, who said afterward, “I understand. Let me see how much you’ve improved through your new trainer. If it’s good enough, you won’t need to attend my training any longer.”

The following hour, Meixiu and the other two had a sparring session with Liya, who was quickly surprised by their drastic improvements.

“Heavens! Who’s training them?! They’ve improved so much that they’re almost unrecognizable!” Wang Ming exclaimed.

In just a month, Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang, who had consistently ranked at the bottom, experienced a dramatic rise, propelling them to the top of their power ranking. Meanwhile, Li Jinxi, who was already at the pinnacle of the rankings, found her lead expanding even further as the gap between her and the others became more pronounced.

“Who’s your teacher?” Liya asked them afterward.

“He’s Yuan’s bodyguard. We’re being trained by him while Yuan is away,” Chu Liuxiang explained.

“Yuan has a bodyguard?” Liya raised her eyebrows at this information. She never expected someone as monstrous as him would need protection.

“It’s a little complicated…”

“Whatever, I don’t really care. Anyway, the three of you no longer need to attend my training since that person is much more capable than me.” Liya said a moment later.

“Yes! Thank you for everything so far!” They bowed to her.

“Shit… we really need to ascend to the Fourth Heaven and train with them now…” Wang Ming muttered out loud.

Hearing this, Liya turned to him and said, “That’s actually a great idea. Not only would you improve faster with that person, but I would no longer need to waste my time training you.”

Although it may seem like Liya was upset, she genuinely wanted the best for them. Meanwhile, Yuan continued his journey with Yingzi.

“My body is feeling sore.” Yingzi suddenly said as she slowed down.

Seeing this, Yuan retrieved a meat skewer and handed it to her without uttering a single word.

Yingzi accepted his offering and immediately continued their journey, acting as though the soreness magically disappeared.

This was Yingzi’s way of asking for more food, and she would do it once a week, so it wasn’t anything new to Yuan.

‘Fortunately, I have an immense stock of meat skewers due to Xi Meili’s eating habits…’ Yuan thought to himself.

A month passed in the blink of an eye, and it was already time for the Eternal Lotuses’ second boot camp.

During this month, Yuan spent most of his time doing mental training since it was pointless for him to improve his cultivation normally.

New novel 𝓬hapters are published on ƒreewebɳovel.com.

Meixiu and the others had become celebrities within the Grand Mystic Sect, recognized by the disciples as the Five Ethereal Fairies. Whenever they trained with Kelan on the training grounds, a crowd of spectators would gather, cheering them on enthusiastically.

Their beauty and diligence had captivated the hearts of many young disciples, drawing attention from even the top disciples within the sect. However, no matter who approached them, they consistently shut down any advances.

Due to disturbances caused by a few troublemakers and the increasingly rowdy crowd at the training ground, Sect Leader Du Daomi took decisive action. He restricted access to the training ground and forbade any disciple from disturbing the group, threatening expulsion for those who defied the order.

In the blink of an eye, another month had passed, and it was time for the second boot camp.

The Demon Sealing Faction spent three days training the members of Eternal Lotuses and returned to their daily routine.

One month… two months… three months…

Half a year has passed since Yuan entered the Primal Expanse.

“We’re almost there.” Yingzi notified Yuan as they reached the end of their six-month-long journey.

chapters.title: Chapter 1610 Supreme Ox’S Territory
chapters.body:
Chapter 1610 Supreme Ox’S Territory

Chapter 1610 Supreme Ox’s Territory

After traveling for another two weeks, Yingzi suddenly stopped moving and said, “We’re at the Supreme Ox’s territory.”

“Finally, after almost seven months.” Yuan stretched his body. Since he had to attend boot camp three days every month, it’d extended their trip slightly. “…”

Yingzi remained still even many moments later, prompting Yuan to ask, “What’s the matter?”

“What do you mean?” she asked.

“Why aren’t you moving?” he clarified.

R𝑒ad lat𝒆st chapt𝒆rs at free𝑤ebnovel.com Only.

“I do not have a good relationship with the Supreme Ox, so my presence there will only make things difficult. You will have to meet him yourself,” she explained.

“How strong is this Supreme Ox?”

“His cultivation is at Perfect Chaotic Immortal.”

“Sorry, but I am not familiar with your cultivation system.”

Yuan proceeded to explain Nine Heaven’s cultivation system to her.

After taking a few minutes to comprehend Nine Heaven’s cultivation system and comparing it to the Primal Expanse’s cultivation system, she began explaining it to Yuan.

The Primal Expanse had ten major cultivation realms, each divided into four minor realms: Early, Intermediate, Advanced, and Perfect.

The major realm consisted of Chaotic Establishment, Chaotic Adept, Chaotic Master, Chaotic Commander, Chaotic King, Chaotic Emperor, Chaotic Overlord, Chaotic Immortal, Chaotic Supreme, and Chaotic God.

The strength of a Perfect Chaotic Immortal was akin to a peak-level Golden Immortal, but that was only pure speculation. Unless two fights with such cultivation collided, there was really no method of finding out.

‘Equal to a peak-level Golden Immortal, huh? I won’t be able to defeat them if we were to fight for whatever reason suddenly, and I don’t want to expose my trump card so soon…’

Although Yuan trusted Yingzi to a certain extent, he couldn’t completely trust her at this moment, and he was confident that she would be watching him, so he didn’t want to use Immortal Monarch’s Dominion and reveal it to her just in case he needed it for her.

He turned to look at Yingzi and spoke, “Yingzi, as you can probably tell, my cultivation is a little lacking. If this Supreme Ox were to attack me for whatever reason, I’d die for sure. If that happens, can I count on you to protect me? Of course, I will compensate you.”

Yingzi pondered for a moment before sticking out her hand, with three fingers raised.

“What is that?” Yuan asked.

“Three hundred meat skewers. If you can provide me with that, I will guarantee your safety,” she calmly said.

“It’s a deal,” he immediately agreed.

He’d be willing to exchange 3 million meat skewers for his life, much less three hundred.

To further improve their relationship, Yuan gave her ten skewers as a downpayment.

“Why are you giving me them?” she asked.

“I’ll give you ten now and the rest if you protect me. We call this a downpayment in our world.”

“What if I don’t need to protect you? Will I have to give them back to you?” Yingzi asked.

“You can keep the ten skewers even if nothing happens to me.”

Yingzi nodded and immediately began eating the meat skewers since she didn’t have to return them.

Meanwhile, Yuan ventured into the Supreme Ox’s territory. This domain was a vast, rocky plain, offering a stark contrast to the environment of the Sword Head Serpent’s territory. Furthermore, the gravity in this region was significantly altered, being ten times heavier than usual, adding an extra layer of challenge to navigating the area.

The Supreme Ox’s territory spanned ten thousand miles, a vast expanse that could easily bewilder anyone from Earth. However, Yuan was guided by Yingzi’s directions through divine sense, ensuring that he wouldn’t lose his way.

As Yuan traveled across the rocky plains of the Supreme Ox’s territory, he observed many Primals roaming the area. Most of these creatures were around the level of a Divine Warrior, equivalent to what would be called an Intermediate Chaotic Master in their world.

Two days into his journey, Yuan spotted a massive mountain in the distance. In the Primal Expanse, the sky was positioned much higher than in the Nine Heavens, towering several times higher, yet this colossal mountain nearly grazed these lofty heights. Moreover, the side of this mountain bore an impressive engraving of an axe-shaped symbol.

Once Yuan approached a certain proximity, a domineering voice resonated through the air, carrying an authoritative echo.

“Halt, intruder.” Yuan halted immediately, and he could see a silhouette descending towards him from the direction of the mountain.

This individual possessed a bulky physique similar to that of a human, with two arms and two legs, but its head was distinctly that of an ox.

The ox raised an eyebrow after examining Yuan closely. freēwēbηovel.c૦m

“Are you really a dragon? You smell like one, but your cultivation is…”

“What does my cultivation have to do with my bloodline? Who are you, anyway?” Yuan said.

“I am the Captain of the Supreme Ox’s Axe Squad. Why have you entered our territory?”

“I’d like to speak with the Supreme Ox.”

“…”

The Captain narrowed his eyes at Yuan, seemingly pondering something.
