After a moment of silence, he said, “I understand. I will bring you to the Supreme Ox now.”

‘That was too easy…’ Yuan thought inwardly. The ease with which they permitted him to meet their leader seemed suspiciously straightforward, prompting him to question if there were any ulterior motives behind their accommodating behavior.

‘Is it because I am a dragon?’

Regardless of what they were up to, with Yingzi protecting him, Yuan had no need to worry and followed the Captain.

Sometime later, they reached the peak of the mountain, where a large throne made of bones was situated at the center, commanding a view of the surrounding landscape.

However, sitting on the throne was an ugly middle-aged man with unnatural facial features so unsettling they could scare even ghosts.

Yuan’s eyebrows twitched upon seeing this middle-aged man, who was easily the most unattractive individual he had ever encountered.

“Supreme Ox, you have a visitor.” The Captain kneeled before the Supreme Ox and said.

The middle-aged man, who had been staring at Yuan long before they even arrived at the mountain, spoke with a mysterious smile on his face, “What brought you to my territory, young dragon?”

chapters.title: Chapter 1611 Appearances
chapters.body:
Chapter 1611 Appearances

Chapter 1611 Appearances

The Supreme Ox’s gaze felt unsettling to Yuan. It carried a mixture of craving and shock, leaving Yuan puzzled about the reason behind such an intense stare. However, he decided to ignore it for now and answered the Supreme Ox’s question.

“I am looking for a method to leave this world, and I was told you might know the method.” freewēbnoveℓ.com

“Huh?” The Supreme Ox’s eyebrows raised in a puzzled manner.

“Putting your request aside, who told you that I would know such a thing?”

Knowing that Yingzi and the Supreme Ox weren’t on good terms, Yuan refrained from mentioning her before him.

“Some Predator I met by chance. I never got to learn his identity, unfortunately.”

Supreme Ox shook his head and said, “I don’t have the slightest clue to what you’re talking about. However, I may know someone who might.”

“…”

Yuan was left speechless. After spending half a year traveling to this location, he discovered that the Supreme Ox knew nothing significant, and now, the Supreme Ox was echoing the same information Yingzi had given him.

‘Am I being scammed here?’ he couldn’t help but wonder inwardly.

“Who might this individual be?” Yuan eventually asked.

“Slow down, young dragon. You may carry the bloodline of dragons, but that doesn’t mean you can make free requests in my territory. I will provide you with the information you seek if you’re willing to do me a favor.”

“What is this favor?”

“It’s quite simple. Allow me to buy—no, let me borrow your appearance for ten thousand years.”

“Huh?” Yuan was bewildered by such an odd favor. “Since you’re a dragon, I’m sure you have no lack of beautiful appearances like the one you’re currently wearing, so lend it to me for a bit. Don’t be stingy; it’s only ten thousand years.”

Yuan’s eyes widened at the Supreme Ox’s weird wording.

‘No lack of appearances? Like the one I’m wearing? What nonsense is this?’ “Why do you want my appearance?” Yuan decided to ask.

“Are you seriously asking me that? Look at me! I’m heinous compared to you!”

“…” Yuan was speechless.

At this moment, Yingzi’s voice resounded inside his head, ‘In the Primal Expanse, due to a lack of humans and our understanding of them, Primals like trading appearances.’

‘How does that even work? Are you asking me to take off my skin like we do with clothes? We can’t do that.’

Foll𝑜w current novℯls on ƒrēewebnoѵёl.cσm.

‘It’s not like that. If you agree to let the Supreme Ox borrow your appearance, he will simply copy your appearance and use it for himself. Also, you won’t be allowed to use your appearance for ten thousand years if you agree.’

‘Why would they choose to borrow appearances? Can’t they simply craft their own, tailored to their liking?’ He struggled to grasp the rationale behind adopting another’s visage when the possibility of creating a unique one existed.

‘That’s easier said than done. With a lack of humans, there are only so many authentic appearances to go around. Also, it is an unspoken rule to never copy another’s appearance without permission, even if you change some of the features.’

Once Yuan thought about it, Yingzi was right. Even humans would have difficulties if told to create a human appearance without any reference since it cannot resemble another’s appearance too much, much less the beasts within Primal Expanse, where humans don’t exist.

‘How valuable are appearances in this world?’ Yuan continued asking.

‘It greatly varies depending on the available features. Of course, the good-looking ones will be worth more. I have never seen anyone with your quality of appearance, so it should be worth a fortune.’ Yingzi said.

‘Thanks for the information.’

“What’s wrong, young dragon? Why aren’t you saying anything?” Supreme Ox asked with a nervous smile on his face.

Seeing this, Yuan smiled coldly and said, “You want to borrow my appearance in exchange for some information that may not even be reliable? Are you looking down on me, Supreme Ox?”

The Supreme Ox sucked his teeth after hearing Yuan’s words. He was clearly trying to take advantage of Yuan due to his seemingly young age and low cultivation base.

“Fine! I won’t ask to borrow it, but allow me to use your appearance as a reference, at the very least!”

“You’re still asking for too much.” Yuan shook his head.

Supreme Ox frowned and muttered in a cold voice, “Don’t try to push your luck too far, young dragon. The other Predators may be afraid of the dragon bloodline, but we don’t fear you.”

The atmosphere suddenly stiffened, and Yuan could sense many powerful entities in the vicinity announcing their presence, almost as if they were threatening him.

Yuan remained calm and said, “Are you sure you want to threaten me? You might have to spend the rest of your life in that appearance.”

Before the Supreme Ox could speak, Yuan continued, “I was going to say that while my appearance isn’t for sale or rent, I have plenty of high-quality appearances available, but if you don’t want them, that’s fine.”

As someone with countless encounters with humans, he had an unlimited supply of human appearances to bargain with in this Primal Expanse. “W-wait!” Supreme Ox quickly said.

He raised his hand and made a gesture. In the following moment, all of the surrounding presences disappeared.

“I apologize for my subordinates’ unruly behavior. They are too easily agitated. Anyway, what other appearances do you have? May I see them?”

Yuan retrieved an appearance-altering pill and swallowed it, transforming himself into a middle-aged man with somewhat handsome features.

Supreme Ox’s face brightened with excitement the moment he saw Yuan’s disguise, even standing up from his seat.

“That one! I want that one!” he hastily said.

“Ten thousand—no, seven thousand years! I’ll give you the information if you will lend it to me for seven thousand years!”

Yuan calmly nodded, “Deal.”

The Supreme Ox didn’t waste any time and immediately copied every little detail of Yuan’s new appearance, taking it for himself.

chapters.title: Chapter 1612 Snowy Fox Mountain
chapters.body:
Chapter 1612 Snowy Fox Mountain

Chapter 1612 Snowy Fox Mountain

After the transaction, Yuan returned to his original appearance and asked, “So, who is this individual who might have the information I want?”

The Supreme Ox, now with a pleasing-to-the-eyes appearance, spoke with a satisfied smile, “The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy is known to have knowledge that surpasses every Predator in the Primal Expanse. If she doesn’t have the information you need, I’m afraid that nobody else in the Primal Expanse does.”

“Where can I find this Divine Fox?”

“The Mystic Valley, but unless the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy wants to see you, it’s almost impossible to find her.”

“Sounds a bit troublesome, but I’ll figure it out when I get there. Thank you for the information.”

“By the way, which dragon tribe are you from?” The Supreme Ox suddenly asked.

“That’s a secret.”

“Is that so? Then keep your secrets.”

A moment later, a small figure appeared before Yuan like a ghost.

“W-who the hell are you?!” The Supreme Ox was startled by Yingzi’s sudden appearance, looking like he couldn’t believe he didn’t notice her presence until she stood right before him.

‘No… even with my eyes directly on her, I cannot sense her presence! This ghost-like presence… she must be—’

“Shadow Dweller! What are you doing in my territory?! And that appearance… where did you acquire such a high-quality appearance?!”

“Shadow Dweller?” Yuan raised an eyebrow at this familiar name.

He turned to look at Yingzi, who turned out to have the same bloodline as one of the Nine Divine Supremes!

“Are you trying to start another bloodbath in my territory?!” The Supreme Ox roared, and the presences that appeared to threaten Yuan returned, but there were significantly more this time.

“Don’t mind me. I am only here as that guy’s travel guide.” Yingzi remained calm despite the many threatening presences surrounding them and pointed at Yuan with her gaze.

“T-travel guide? You?” The Supreme Ox couldn’t believe what he was hearing.

How could an entity like her become a guide for someone else, even if that individual was a dragon?

“Are you ready to go to the Mystic Valley?” she asked Yuan the next moment.

“How far away is the Mystic Valley from this place?”

Yingzi pondered for a moment before responding, “Fifty years, give or take.”

“…”

‘You can’t be serious…’

Yuan was left absolutely gobsmacked by the ridiculous distance. He thought half a year was already bad enough, but fifty years?

“That’s too long!” he cried out loud.

Seeing an opportunity to earn more appearances, the Supreme Ox said, “I know of a way to get to the Mystic Valley in six months.”

“Oh?” Yuan turned to look at him with a hopeful gaze.

“There’s a natural space tunnel that will take you to the Snowy Fox Mountain, which is around six months away from the Mystic Valley.”

“I can get you there in two months,” Yingzi then said.

“As for the location of this space tunnel…” The Supreme Ox spoke with a suggestive smile.

Yuan immediately realized what the Supreme Ox was thinking, smiled back, and said, “Not just seven thousand years. I’ll let you keep that appearance permanently.”

“DEAL! IT’S A DEAL!” The Supreme Ox excitedly agreed.

The Supreme Ox proceeded to retrieve a medallion made from a piece of bone and tossed it to Yuan.

“Since the space tunnel is within my territory, you will need to show that to the guards there.”

He turned to look at one of his minions and said, “Bring them to the space tunnel.”

“As you command, Supreme Ox.” The minion acknowledged with its head lowered.

Yuan left the mountain to follow the guide shortly later. They arrived at their location three days later. The space tunnel appeared to be an ordinary cave at a glance, but Yuan’s divine sense told a different story.

There were two ox guards standing outside this cave, and they glared at Yuan and Yingzi as they approached.

“The Supreme Ox has given them permission to use the space tunnel.” The ox minion explained.

Yuan showed the bone medallion to the guards, who took it and examined it.

“Very well, you may enter.”

Yuan and Yingzi entered the cave the next moment while the ox minion stayed behind.

“Just keep walking until you exit the other side.” The ox guard said.

The cave was dimly illuminated by the mysterious purple rocks sticking out of the walls around them.

There was also a weird energy that filled the cave. This energy felt similar to the energy from teleport formations that Yuan was familiar with, but it was slightly different, and it only got more intense as they walked deeper into the cave.

After walking for several days straight, Yuan could finally see the end of the cave.

His steps hastened as they approached the glowing white light.

When they finally exited the cave, Yuan was greeted by a white mountain range and snow so deep it reached his waist. As for Yingzi, her entire body was submerged in snow, leaving only her head visible.

“Fifty meat skewers.” Yingzi suddenly said. “Traveling in this terrain is a pain, so it will cost you more.”

“Sure. Do you want them now or later? The skewers will quickly get cold in this weather.”

If he didn’t protect his body with spiritual energy, the intense cold would easily freeze it within seconds, but even with his body protected, it was still freezing cold.

‘I can’t recall the last time I felt this cold…’

This content is taken from freёwebnovel.com.

Cultivators can regulate their body temperature using spiritual energy, ensuring comfort even if they stand naked in a snowstorm. However, this ability can be challenged under extreme conditions that are too severe for their energy to suppress.

Moreover, the snow in this area seemed to possess a unique trait that drained spiritual energy, making it even more challenging for Yuan to maintain a steady body temperature.

“You can give them to me later,” she said.

“Alright.”

Yingzi picked up Yuan and flew toward the Mystic Valley shortly later.

chapters.title: Chapter 1613 Shadow Dweller
chapters.body:
Chapter 1613 Shadow Dweller

Chapter 1613 Shadow Dweller

As they ventured deeper into Snowy Fox Mountain, a fierce blizzard raged around them. The harsh conditions didn’t affect Yuan, but he could sense the relentless blizzard gradually chipping away Yingzi’s energy that protected them from the blizzard. Fortunately, Yingzi was able to replenish her energy faster than it was being depleted, averting what could have been a perilous situation.

“Yingzi, if you don’t mind me asking, what kind of Predator are Shadow Dwellers?” Yuan suddenly asked this question that had been on his mind since it was revealed to him that she was one.

This content is taken from freёwebnovel.com.

“What do you mean? Can you be more specific?”

“You know how the Sword Head Serpent is a snake, and the Supreme Ox is an ox? What about Shadow Dwellers?”

“If you’re asking about my shape, we don’t really have one. We can become any shape or size, just like shadows.”

‘A shapeshifting beast?’ Yuan tried to imagine her true form, but the closest image he could conjure was that of the shadow monsters he’d seen on television—an entity devoid of any distinct features, merely a shadowy figure resembling a silhouette.

“We dwell in the shadow, hunt with shadows, and traverse through shadows. We are the pinnacle of hunters—at least, that’s what the others say.”

“Sounds like a species that prefers living in isolation,” Yuan commented.

“It’s true. Shadow Dwellers rarely show themselves. I, however, am an exception. I prefer traversing the world and experiencing new things. That’s probably why I am considered an outcast,” Yingzi casually remarked.

Despite calling herself an outcast, Yingzi didn’t sound dispirited or saddened, almost as if she was completely fine with it.

“Tell me about your world—this Nine Heavens.” Yingzi suddenly said.

Yuan nodded and recalled some of his experiences in the Nine Heavens with her.

A month passed in the blink of an eye.

Halfway through their journey, in the midst of Yuan’s mental meditation, a voice suddenly rippled loudly throughout the Snowy Fox Mountain.

This voice carried a profound power that had the ability to stop the blizzard.

Yingzi stopped moving after hearing this voice, and she warned Yuan, “The owner of this territory will be here shortly.”

The moment Yingzi finished her sentence, and before Yuan could even respond, a white-haired woman wearing white robes appeared before them.

“You may be a dragon, but how dare you trespass into my territory without permission? Are you looking for death?” The woman spoke in a bold tone.

At a glance, the aura exuded by this woman was slightly stronger than the Supreme Ox. “Huh? There are two of you?” The woman’s eyes widened with surprise after realizing that she’d failed to sense Yingzi’s presence.

“I apologize for entering your territory, but we don’t have any business with you or this place. We’re just trying to reach the Mystic Valley.” Yuan explained the situation to her.

“I don’t care what you’re doing or where you’re going. It doesn’t change the fact that you’re trespassing into my territory,” she sneered.

‘Are entering another’s territory usually such a big deal?’ Yuan asked Yingzi through divine sense.

‘It varies from individual to individual, but it’s usually considered rude to enter another’s territory without permission. This only applies to Predators, though. Primals have no intelligence, so we can’t really blame them for trespassing. They’re also considered a source of food and power for Predators, so they’re more than welcome to trespass.’

After taking a quick moment to ponder a way out of this, Yuan spoke, “Is there anything we can do to make it up to you? Would a high-quality appearance be good enough?”

“Y-you… are you bribing me?” The woman wore a bewildered expression on her ordinary face. freёwebnovel.com

Killing intent surged from her the following moment. “I don’t care who you are or what you have to offer! Nobody bribes me!”

Noticing the woman was preparing to strike, Yuan quickly swallowed an appearance-altering pill and transformed himself into a beautiful woman. He also ensured the appearance had white hair, reflecting the woman’s current appearance.

“…”

The woman, previously trembling with anger, suddenly froze. Her enraged expression shifted to one of bewilderment, as if she had just witnessed a miracle.

“Y-you’re willing to give me that appearance…?” she asked in a low voice.

Yuan nodded and said, “If you’re willing to overlook our trespass and let us continue our journey without any further issues, I’ll let you keep it.”

The woman lowered her head to hide her face while she pondered whether she should accept his offer.

‘If I accept his offer, my pride will… Forget it! I must get my hands on that appearance, no matter the cost!’

It didn’t take long for the woman to make up her mind.

“It’s a deal!”

She swiftly mimicked Yuan’s appearance and then flew away immediately afterward, not uttering another word.

They continued their journey shortly later, and the blizzard never returned.

After another month, they finally arrived at their destination.

Before reaching the Mystic Valley, they had to cross an ocean whose surface suddenly began to glow, casting a shimmering light across the expansive waters.

“So this is the Mystic Valley, huh? It’s a beautiful place.” Yuan commented after seeing the glamorous scenery before him.

The Mystic Valley was draped in blue and purple trees, lending the area an exotic ambiance. Additionally, these trees bore glowing fruits that illuminated the entire valley, casting a light reminiscent of street stalls glowing softly in the night.

Moreover, a long river that flowed into the glowing ocean bisected the valley, contributing to its mystical atmosphere. The water in this river also glowed, further enhancing the otherworldly aura of the place.

“Do you know where we can find the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy?” Yuan asked Yingzi after he finished admiring the place.

Yingzi shook her head and replied, “No, but the Mystic Valley is a relatively small place, and one could fully explore it in just a few months. If we search every nook and cranny, we should eventually find her.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1614 Mystic Valley
chapters.body:
Chapter 1614 Mystic Valley

Chapter 1614 Mystic Valley

Foll𝑜w current novℯls on ƒrēewebnoѵёl.cσm.

Shortly after their arrival, Yuan and Yingzi started looking around the Mystic Valley, using their eyes and divine sense to search for the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy.

“I want five meat skewers now.” Yingzi extended her hand and said.

Yuan acknowledged her request and retrieved five skewers before giving them to her.

The moment the meat skewers emerged from his spatial ring, releasing their unique aroma into the air, all of the Primals within a hundred-mile radius detected the scent and charged in their direction.

Sensing the incoming danger, Yingzi released a burst of her aura. The instant the Primals detected her powerful presence, they abruptly turned and fled in the opposite direction, not halting their frantic escape even hundreds of miles later.

This was Yuan’s first time experiencing Yingzi’s ‘presence.’ It was chaotic and unlike anything he had previously encountered. There was something fundamentally different about the beasts in the Primal Expanse compared to those in the Nine Heavens.

‘Is this due to the Chaotic Essence that they absorb?’

Yuan wondered how it would affect his body if he cultivated Chaotic Essence, but he was reluctant to try, as it could harm his body.

Over the following weeks, Yuan and Yingzi methodically traversed the Mystic Valley. Throughout this journey, they encountered numerous species of Primals, none of which Yuan recognized.

Most of the Primals’ strength ranged between Spirit King and Divine Ancestor, with a few having the strength of an Immortal. However, regardless of their strength, not many approached them, primarily due to Yuan’s dragon scent. The few who dared to approach them were instantly scared away when they saw Yingzi, so their journey was relatively peaceful.

One day, Yuan halted abruptly when a unique glow caught the corner of his eye. Turning his attention to the source, he discovered it was a glowing purple mushroom nestled among the branches of a nearby tree. Intrigued by its luminous appearance, he moved closer for a better look.

“Do you know what this is?” Yuan asked Yingzi, who stared at it from a distance, seemingly unwilling to get closer to it for whatever reason.

“That is a Primal Bane. It contains a tremendous amount of Chaotic Essence, but it’s also extremely poisonous. Just its scent alone could kill,” she explained.

“Is that why I don’t sense any Primals in this area? Are you okay?” he asked her.

Yingzi replied, “I am not weak enough to be killed by its scent, but I dislike its smell.” “More importantly… how are you fine?”

Yuan shrugged and said, “I’m not sure, but I don’t think it’s poisonous for me. After all, I can feel my cultivation rising just by being near it.”

He proceeded to pick the mushroom, which was the size of an apple.

“Are all humans as reckless as you?” Yingzi asked with raised eyebrows.

Yuan chuckled and said, “I can’t speak for all humans, but I am definitely not as reckless as you think.”

Yuan examined the Primal Bane in finer detail once it was in his hands.

After staring at it for several minutes, Yuan suddenly opened his mouth and tossed the glowing mushroom inside.

Yingzi was taken aback by Yuan’s actions. However, her immediate concern wasn’t for his safety but rather the prospect of not receiving any more meat skewers if something were to happen to him.

As Yuan bit into the glowing mushroom, it released a sweet nectar that tingled his taste buds and offered a surprising burst of flavor.

<You have consumed Primal Bane>

<You have gained Supreme Poison Resistance>

<You have absorbed enough Qi for a breakthrough!>

<You have reached Second Level Spirit Emperor>

<You have absorbed enough Qi for a breakthrough!>

<You have reached Third Level Spirit Emperor>

<You have absorbed enough Qi for a breakthrough!>

<You have reached Fourth Level Spirit Emperor>

Not only did Yuan gain poison resistance from the mushroom, but he also experienced three consecutive breakthroughs in his cultivation despite the Celestial Emperor’s curse, which was a pleasant surprise.

Yingzi’s eyes widened slightly as she witnessed these events, leading her to ponder whether Yuan’s human nature made him unique, or if there was something inherently special about him.

“This Primal Bane is great. Do you see any more around here?” Yuan turned to Yingzi and asked.

Although she was speechless, Yingzi still used her divine sense to search for more Primal Banes.

A few moments later, she said, “There’s another one about two days away.”

“Great! Let me eat that one, too!”

“…”

Two days later, Yuan tossed the second Primal Bane into his mouth.

<You have absorbed enough Qi for a breakthrough!>

<You have reached Fifth Level Spirit Emperor>

He only managed to breakthrough once this time around, but he still felt it was worth it.

As they continued to look for the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy, Yuan also kept an eye out for more Primal Banes or anything that seemed like it would strengthen him.

A week later, Yingzi suddenly descended toward the glowing river. With a swift hand gesture, she raised an azure-colored flower from the water and hovered it before Yuan.

“This is a Luminous Aquatic Lily. It’s poisonous enough to instantaneously kill a Chaotic Overlord. I’ll give it to you for one hundred meat skewers,” she said.

Yuan stared at the beautiful flower that glowed even brighter than the Primal Bane and swallowed nervously.

‘Young Master! That’s the Sacred Ocean Lily!’ Yu Ning’s shocked voice suddenly resounded.

‘Oh? You recognize this treasure?’ he inquired.

‘Of course. It’s an extremely rare treasure that can only be found in the Azure Dragon Clan’s territory, deep within the Azure Ocean. It’s said to grant one immunity to poison!’ Yu Ning explained.

‘Immunity to poison, huh?’

“Alright, It’s a deal,” Yuan said to Yingzi the next moment.

‘This is safe to consume, right?’ Yuan asked for confirmation from Yu Ning.

‘You’re asking that now, after you ate that weird mushroom, even though you knew it was poisonous?’ she said in a dumbfounded voice.

‘Anyway, yes, it’s poisonous and can only be safely consumed if taken with Saint Dew, which neutralizes the poison when consumed together.’

chapters.title: Chapter 1615 Luminous Aquatic Lily
chapters.body:
Chapter 1615 Luminous Aquatic Lily

Chapter 1615 Luminous Aquatic Lily

With a pondering face, Yuan stared at the Luminous Aquatic Lily, also known as the Sacred Ocean Lily in the Nine Heavens. Despite Yu Ning’s confirmation that it was poisonous, he had Supreme Poison Resistance, and he managed to consume the Primal Bane without any resistance.

‘I cannot rely on Yingzi forever since I only had a limited amount of skewers left to use on her. Moreover, I don’t feel any sense of dread from it…’

He considered whether to consume the Luminous Aquatic Lily immediately or wait until he had obtained the Saint Dew to safely neutralize its poisonous effects.

“Aren’t you going to eat it?” Yingzi suddenly asked him.

After hearing Yingzi’s words, Yuan quickly made up his mind. Without any hesitation, he tossed the entire flower into his mouth the very next second.

“Young Master?!” Yu Ning exclaimed in a shocked voice after seeing his actions. She’d just told him that it was poisonous, so why would he do something so foolish?

“!!!”

Upon consuming the Luminous Aquatic Lily, Yuan felt an immense burst of energy surge through his body, so powerful that it threatened to cause his body to explode. Recognizing the urgency, he quickly found a place to sit down and focus on cultivating to control the overwhelming energy.

Since the Luminous Aquatic Lily had thrived in an environment rich with Chaotic Essence, consuming it began to alter Yuan’s aura. Gradually, his aura grew more chaotic, beginning to resemble that of the residents of the Primal Expanse, marking a significant transformation in his energy.

Although the Primal Bane also contained a lot of Chaotic Essence, it was not enough to affect Yuan. The Luminous Aquatic Lily, however, was at least fifty times more potent than the Primal Bane.

Yuan’s cultivation soared in the following moments.

<You have absorbed enough Qi for a breakthrough!>

<You have reached Fifth Level Spirit Emperor>

<You have absorbed enough Qi for a breakthrough!>

Read 𝓁at𝙚st chapters at ƒrēewebnovel.com Only.

<You have reached Sixth Level Spirit Emperor>

<You have absorbed enough Qi for a breakthrough!>

<You have reached Seventh Level Spirit Emperor>

<You have absorbed enough Qi for a breakthrough!>

<You have reached Eighth Level Spirit Emperor>

<You have absorbed enough Qi for a breakthrough!>

<You have reached Ninth Level Spirit Emperor>

In the blink of an eye, Yuan’s cultivation base surged to the peak of the Spirit Emperor level, then began to stabilize. Over the following hours, his cultivation slowly advanced, inching closer to the threshold of Spirit Sovereign.

A day after Yuan consumed the Luminous Aquatic Lily, his aura, which had been tranquil for some time, suddenly skyrocketed.

<You have absorbed enough Qi for a breakthrough!>

<You have reached First Level Spirit Sovereign>

<Sacred Ocean Lily has been completely refined>

<Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has strengthened the effects of Sacred Ocean Lily>

<Your skill ‘Supreme Poison Resistance’ has developed into ‘Hundred Poison Immunity’>

<Your skill ‘Hundred Poison Immunity’ has developed into ‘Ten Thousand Poison Immunity’>

<Due to a large consumption of Chaotic Essence, you have begun to develop a Chaotic Heart>

Ding!

<You have received a Quest>

[Quest: Chaotic Heart]

[Difficulty: Hard]

[Quest Description: Consume Chaotic Essence and complete your Chaotic Heart]

[Progress: 10%]

[Reward: Chaotic Heart]

Yuan stared at the quest with wide eyes.

“Congratulations on your breakthrough, Young Master…” Yu Ning’s dumbfounded voice snapped him out of his daze.

He turned to look at Yingzi and asked, “What’s a Chaotic Heart?”

“That’s what we have and need to cultivate,” she calmly responded.

Her eyes suddenly narrowed as she fixed an intense stare at him.

“I can sense a Chaotic Heart, one that didn’t exist before, within you… However, it doesn’t seem complete… How is that possible?” she muttered in a dazed voice.

The residents of the Primal Expanse are naturally born with a Chaotic Heart, so nobody knew it could be created—in a human, much less.

“What did you do?” Yingzi asked him a moment later.

“Besides consuming the Luminous Aquatic Lily, nothing really. I think it’s because I consumed a large amount of Chaotic Essence, which I am not used to,” he said.

“…”

Yingzi wore a pondering expression on her face, but she didn’t say anything else.

“Give me a few more hours. I want to try something.”

Yuan closed his eyes again and began cultivating.

Six hours later, his progress increased by a single percent.

‘At this rate, I will need to cultivate for 23 days to complete the Chaotic Heart.’

“I need 23 days to complete my Chaotic Heart. Can you wait a little for me?” Yuan asked Yingzi, who calmly nodded.

Once Yuan returned to his cultivation, Yingzi sat beside him and watched him slowly form his Chaotic Heart.

However, Yuan emerged from his cultivation only a week later.

‘This won’t work. My progress slowed down significantly after reaching 20 percent. I will need to consume treasures if I want any decent progress.’

He should have gained 28 percent in the last week, but he only managed to achieve an increase of 9 percent. He reached 20 percent overall progress on the second day but failed to make any further progress in the subsequent days.

“What’s wrong?” Yingzi asked when she noticed he’d suddenly stopped cultivating.

“There were some errors in my calculations. It seems the requirements increase as I get closer to completing the Chaotic Heart,” Yuan sighed out loud.

“At this rate, I don’t know how long it will take me to finish it. I will need to consume more treasures containing Chaotic Essence if I want to complete my Chaotic Heart anytime soon.”

“Treasures, right? I can find them for you, but in exchange…”

“You want meat skewers, right?”

She nodded.

“Alright.”

They returned to looking for the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy shortly after.

As they continued to search the Mystic Valley, Yingzi would occasionally bring Yuan treasures rich with Chaotic Essence. While these treasures were not as potent as the Primal Bane, much less the Luminous Aquatic Lily, it was still enough to progress his Chaotic Heart.

chapters.title: Chapter 1616 Divine Fox Of Grand Prophecy
chapters.body:
Chapter 1616 Divine Fox Of Grand Prophecy

Chapter 1616 Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy

A month has passed since Yuan consumed the Luminous Aquatic Lily. During this time, he’d consumed numerous treasures while searching for the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy.

His progress with the Chaotic Heart had reached fifty percent, but his cultivation hadn’t increased even slightly due to the Chaotic Heart absorbing all of the Chaotic Essence.

Most of the treasures were acquired by Yingzi, and in exchange for them, Yuan had to give her several hundred meat skewers.

‘Will my supply last until I can create my Chaotic Heart…?’ Yuan wondered inwardly as he checked his swiftly declining stock of meat skewers.

He had only about five thousand meat skewers left, which might seem plentiful, but given Yingzi’s rapid consumption rate, his supply could be depleted within a year.

“We’ve searched the entire Mystic Valley.” Yingzi suddenly announced.

“Is it possible the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy isn’t in her own territory?” Yuan asked.

“There is a chance she isn’t home, but we won’t know for sure unless we burn down this whole place to draw her out.”

Yuan looked at Yingzi with wide eyes, clearly surprised by her savage suggestion.

“It won’t take much effort or time,” Yingzi said, misunderstanding his feelings.

Before Yuan could respond, a new voice echoed through the air, “I knew my Mystic Valley would be receiving some special visitors, but alas, what a disappointment. How dare you even speak of destroying my beautiful territory?” The tone was stern, filled with a mixture of anger and disappointment.

A formidable presence suddenly enveloped the area, and Yuan caught a glimpse of a silhouette in the corner of his eye, prompting him to swiftly turn his head toward the source.

Standing atop an enormous boulder in the distance was a majestic azure-colored fox with golden eyes and purple patterns across its body. Its four tails fluttered gracefully behind it, adding to its ethereal presence.

This golden fox possessed a cultivation level that was at least two to three tiers higher than both the Supreme Ox and the woman Yuan had encountered in the Snowy Fox Mountain, and it exuded a fierce holy aura, almost as if it was a divine being from heaven.

“Are you the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy?” Yuan asked despite knowing the answer.

“That’s right. Who’s asking?” she responded, her voice tinged with a mix of pride and arrogance.

“Hello. My name is Yuan, and I came here for your help.”

“My help? That’s a good joke, considering you were just talking about destroying my territory to lure me out,” she coldly sneered.

“What? I’m sure she was just joking out of frustration since we couldn’t find you even after searching the whole Mystic Valley.”

The Divine Fox of Grand Propechy narrowed her eyes and sneered, “Yes, I saw…how you stole and consumed all of the treasures in my territory!”

Yuan’s eyes widened at her accusation. It had never occurred to him that gathering natural treasures in the wild could be considered stealing.

Unbeknownst to him, in the Primal Expanse, once an individual claimed a territory, everything within it was considered their possession. Thus, taking anything from that territory was viewed as stealing.

“To tell you the truth, I’m not from around here, so I don’t know your rules. I apologize if I offended you, and if there’s any way I can compensate you, don’t hesitate to let me know.”

The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy fell silent after hearing his words. Eventually, she said, “If you wish to compensate me, then give me those meat sticks you have been giving that Shadow Dweller.”

“Of course. How many do you want?”

“All of it,” she responded without hesitation.

“That’s…”

However, before Yuan could even formulate a response, immense killing intent suddenly surged from Yingzi’s small frame, enveloping the entire Mystic Valley in an instant.

“Don’t push your luck too far, Divine Fox,” she growled in a shivering tone.

Despite the intense atmosphere, the Divine Fox neither fled nor showed any signs of fear, standing firm and composed. In stark contrast, the nearby Primals scrambled away in terror, visibly shaken to their core, even pissing themselves while they fled.

“What? You wanna go at it? I’m not afraid of you.” The Divine Fox sneered, her posture oozing with confidence for some reason.

“We didn’t come here to fight you. Also, I would give you all of my meat skewers if I could, but as you can see, it will only lead to an even bigger problem, so I will refrain from doing so.” Yuan sighed.

“How about five hundred meat skewers?”

“…”

After a moment of silence, the Divine Fox nodded, “Fine. I’ll forgive you for five hundred of those meat sticks.”

Yuan immediately retrieved 500 meat skewers and offered them to the Divine Fox, who started eating them the moment it was in her grasp.

“Oh my! What a unique flavor! I have never tasted anything like this before! What kind of Primal meat is this?!” exclaimed the Divine Fox, her entire body trembling with joy as she savored the meat skewers, clearly delighted by the unfamiliar and exquisite taste.

“I can tell you if you answer one of my questions,” Yuan saw this opportunity and said.

Visit fгee𝑤ebɳoveɭ.cøm for the best novel reading experi𝒆nce.

“There is almost nothing I don’t know. What do you want to know?”

“A method of leaving this world,” he said.

“…”

The Divine Fox stopped chewing on the meat skewers and stared at him in silence, her thoughts unknown.

Sometime later, she quickly chewed her food and swallowed before speaking in a calm voice, “As I thought, you’re not from this world.”

“How can you tell?” Yuan asked out of curiosity.

“How can I not after watching you for the last several weeks? Not only do you lack common sense, but you also lack a Chaotic Heart. Are you even a dragon or a human disguised as one? It’s been a few million years since we’ve had a visitor from the other side.”

Yuan smiled inwardly after observing the Divine Fox’s knowledgeable demeanor, hopeful that she might possess the information he needed to return to the Nine Heavens.

chapters.title: Chapter 1617 Primal Monarch
chapters.body:
Chapter 1617 Primal Monarch

Chapter 1617 Primal Monarch

“You’re right. I am not from this world originally. I came to the Primal Expanse by accident, and I am trying to look for a way back to my own world.” Yuan said to the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy after she correctly guessed his identity.

“And you think I know this method? What made you come here, anyway?” The Divine Fox asked.

“That’s what the Supreme Ox said. Don’t tell me you don’t…” Yuan swallowed nervously.

“I do know how to leave this world.”

“Really?!”

Yuan’s expression brightened after hearing this.

“Yes, but I do not have the power to do so. If you want to leave this world, you will need to find someone else.”

“Not this again…” Yuan muttered in disbelief.

He had already spent a year in the Primal Expanse, with most of that time devoted to searching for someone who could help him escape this realm.

This was now the third time he was being directed to find another individual who ‘might’ be able to assist him. The repeated dead ends were becoming increasingly frustrating, but there was nothing he could do but continue following these leads.

Seeing Yuan’s disappointed face, the Divine Fox elaborated, “You probably don’t know this, but the Primal Expanse has a master—one true ruler that governs every Primal and Predator in existence.”

“Not only does he rule over the entire Primal Expanse, but he also has the power to connect this world to the other world. If you want to leave this world, you’ll need to find him.”

“Who is this individual?” Yuan asked.

“Some call him the One True Monarch while others call him the Primal Monarch or Monarch of Primals.”

“Primal Monarch…?” Yuan repeated in a mumbling voice.

Although this should have been his first time hearing of this individual, Yuan couldn’t shake a sense of familiarity, as if he somehow already knew or was connected to them in some way.

“Where can I find him?” Yuan then asked.

The Divine Fox pointed the skewers in her hand at him and said, “So, what kind of meat is this?”

Clearly, she had no intention of answering all of his questions for free.

The Divine Fox pointed the skewers in her hand at him and said, “So, what kind of meat is this?”

Clearly, she had no intention of answering all of his questions for free.

“There are three types of meat used for those meat skewers. They come from animals we call chicken, lamb, and cow.” “Are animals in your world what we call Primals here?” she asked.

“No. Animals don’t have spiritual energy. They’re essentially powerless Primals.”

“I see… Which meat is this?” Divine Fox asked as she showed him one of the skewers.

“That white meat is from chicken. The small, chunky ones are lamb meat. The brown slices are beef—cow meat.” Yuan explained.

“And what are these tiny specks of dust on it? I can tell that’s where most of the flavors come from.” The Divine Fox continued to inquire.

“We call those spices, which are commonly used to give or enhance flavors in our food. Without them, the meat will taste quite bland.”

“I see… how intriguing…” The Divine Fox stared at the meat skewers.

“I happen to have some spices on me. If you want, I can give you some.” Yuan suddenly retrieved several bottles of spices, mainly salt, sugar, garlic, and crushed pepper.

“Oh?!” The Divine Fox’s gaze suddenly brightened.

“Of course, it’s not for free—”

“If you want to find the Primal Monarch, it’s impossible. He’s not an individual you can just seek out. Like me, he will only appear before you if he chooses to do so. If he doesn’t want you to find him, you will spend the rest of your life trying.”

“What?! Then how am I—”

The Divine Fox interrupted, “Of course, I can help you find—give you a better chance of finding him. With my divination, I can locate his proximity. Whether or not you will be able to find him is entirely up to your luck and fate.”

“However, for my abilities to work on the Primal Monarch, I will need to use a tremendous amount of Chaotic Essence—more than what I currently have, so you’ll need to supply me with the rest. This is also why I can only give you his proximity, as I lack the power to do anything more.”

Yuan tossed the spices to the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy after receiving her answer.

“So, what would it cost to have you help me find him?” Yuan asked the following moment.

“I’ll be severely weakened after using my ability on the Primal Monarch, so it’ll cost you more than just some spices or food.” The Divine Fox said.

“I have an annoying neighbor who’s always been a pain in my butt and consistently trying to scheme against me. I want you to get rid of them for me,” she said.

“If this individual is so strong that even you cannot get rid of them, what hope do I have?” Yuan asked.

“They’re not particularly strong. I would have no issue dealing with someone at their level in ordinary circumstances, but this one has a special background, so I am unable to harm them without putting myself at risk. With that being said, they still have the strength of a Perfect Chaotic King, way above what you can handle at your current level.”

“Perfect Chaotic King… so around Divine Sovereign, huh? I think I can manage.” Yuan said.

“Huh? You’re barely a Perfect Chaotic Adept. There’s no way you can defeat someone three major realms stronger than yourself. In the first place, I never expected you to do the work, but your little friend here.” The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy’s gaze stared at Yingzi as she spoke.

Updat𝓮d from freewēbnoveℓ.com.

Yuan smiled and said, “I have my ways.”

“Whatever. I don’t care how you deal with that bastard as long as you get rid of him. Once you do that, I’ll divine the Primal Monarch’s location for you. Also, don’t forget about the extra Chaotic Essence. I need a treasure that will temporarily boost my strength, or it won’t work, and coincidentally, that annoying bastard has something like that in his possession.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1618 Starfire Dragon
chapters.body:
Chapter 1618 Starfire Dragon

Chapter 1618 Starfire Dragon

“So, who is this Primal that you want to get rid of?” Yuan asked.

“Not a Primal. He’s a Predator.” The Divine Fox corrected him. “He has a dragon bloodline—a real one.”

“Dragon bloodline, huh? Is that why you cannot attack him?”

“He’s from the Divine Dragon Sanctuary, one of the top powers in the Primal Expanse. If I attack him and offend them, they could easily destroy me and my territory. However, you’re a different story. Even if you kill him, it won’t affect you once you leave this place.”

Yuan raised an eyebrow and said, “But you were expecting Yingzi to kill him, and she is a resident of this world.”

Divine Fox of Grand Propechy looked at Yingzi and sneered, “You know nothing about Shadow Dwellers. With their ability to hide their presence completely, she can traverse the Primal Expanse without worry, even with the entire world hunting her.”

“Even if she’s completely invisible, it doesn’t make it okay to use her like that. Also, I don’t have to kill your neighbor, only get rid of him, right?”

The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy became silent to ponder.

A moment later, she spoke, “I would prefer it if you killed him, but if you can’t, just make sure he never returns.”

“Sounds good. Then I’ll go visit him now.” Yuan said.

“What about me?” Yingzi asked.

“I don’t think I’ll need your help this time, but you can come with me just in case.”

Yingzi nodded and decided to follow him, motivated by the opportunity to possibly acquire more meat skewers.

Yuan began traveling to the dragon’s territory after acquiring more information from the Divine Fox.

According to the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy, Yuan’s next target was a Starfire Dragon, who resided within the Flaming Peak—a fiery land perpetually engulfed in flames.

The Flaming Peak was roughly ten days away from Mystic Valley, even when traveling at Yingzi’s swift pace. As they neared their destination, the temperature soared to extreme levels, reaching intensities that could melt even Spirit Kings if they ventured too close.

‘If it weren’t for my physique, I would probably be a puddle right now…’ Yuan thought to himself as he tried to cool himself.

He looked at Yingzi, who didn’t have a single drop of sweat on her, seemingly unaffected by the intense heat.

The heat only worsened as they approached the Flaming Peak. However, despite his situation, Yuan took this opportunity to temper his body.

<You have gained Weak Fire Resistance>

<You have gained Powerful Fire Resistance>

As Yuan bolstered his fire resistance, the oppressive heat began to affect him less. Gradually, he adjusted until the once scorching temperatures felt merely warm, akin to that of lukewarm water.

Upon arriving at Flaming Peak, Yuan was met with a fiery spectacle. The place itself was an island featuring a solitary mountain at its center. The entire island was engulfed in flames, and the mountain was an active volcano, further intensifying the blazing landscape.

Upon arriving at Flaming Peak, Yuan was met with a fiery spectacle. The place itself was an island featuring a solitary mountain at its center. The entire island was engulfed in flames, and the mountain was an active volcano, further intensifying the blazing landscape.

Resting within the volcano, submerged in lava, was a solitary silhouette. Upon closer examination with his divine sense, Yuan discovered it was a handsome young man with short red hair.

When the handsome young man sensed Yuan’s divine sense probing, his eyes snapped wide open, revealing their striking golden hue.

“Who dares to spy on this Young Lord?!”

The Starfire Dragon roared as he leaped into the air before staring directly at Yuan’s direction.

“Hm? A dragon?” The Starfire Dragon narrowed his eyes after realizing this.

“Which clan do you belong to? And what business do you have here in my territory—with a Shadow Dweller, no less?” The Starfire Dragon immediately began interrogating Yuan.

“How dare a lowly Starfire Dragon question me?” Yuan roared in an arrogant voice.

Given that the Primal Expanse was a dog-eat-dog world, Yuan decided that it would be in his best interest to project strength, adopting an overbearing demeanor to show no signs of weakness.

“L-lowly Starfire Dragon…?!” The handsome young man trembled in anger and disbelief.

While it was true that the Starfire Dragon’s bloodline wasn’t top-tier—ranking only in the lower middle in terms of quality and purity—the Starfire Dragon had never before been humiliated or looked down upon in such a manner.

“HOW DARE YOU!”

As the Starfire Dragon’s anger surged, so did its aura, sending powerful ripples across the entire island, causing the flames to dance violently.

“I don’t care who you are or where the fuck you came from, but you will not be leaving here alive today!”

The Starfire Dragon soared toward Yuan like a flaming meteor.

Seeing this, Yingzi spoke, “Do you want me to suppress him for you?”

“No, it’s fine. I can handle this.”

Yingzi puffed her cheeks at his refusal, seemingly disappointed that she couldn’t earn some meat skewers.

Once the Starfire Dragon was close enough, Yuan suddenly activated Dragon’s Gaze.

“Wha—?!”

The Starfire Dragon halted upon being struck by Dragon’s Gaze.

“T-this power is—! Dragon’s Influence! You’re royalty?!” The Starfire Dragon cried out loud, his anger dissipating instantly after realizing he was baring his fangs against royalty.

Even if he had the Divine Dragon Santuary’s backing, they would not hesitate to disown him if they knew he’d offended a dragon of a royal bloodline.

‘Why the hell is a royal dragon causing trouble for me, a nobody?!’

Although the Starfire Dragon found it weird that he didn’t recognize Yuan even though he memorized every member of the royal family, not to mention Yuan’s low cultivation base, he was in no position to think properly.

“Your Highness! This is an misunderstanding! Please tell me why you are here!” The Starfire Dragon lowered his head and said.

“Not only do you dare to question me, but you even dare to give me orders? Who do you think you are, huh?!” Yuan roared, sending chills down the Starfire Dragon’s spine.

‘This overbearing demeanor! The strength of his Dragon’s Influence! He’s definitely royalty—a high-ranking one, at that!’ he cried inwardly.

chapters.title: Chapter 1619 Starfire Dragon(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1619 Starfire Dragon(2)

Chapter 1619 Starfire Dragon(2)

“M-my apologies, Your Highness! It wasn’t my intention! I was wrong!” Starfire Dragon quickly lowered his head.

“If you know you’re wrong, why are you still hovering above me and looking down on me like you’re above me? Get on your knees! Or are you waiting for me to help you with my authority!? A lowly dragon like you?!” Yuan shouted, increasing the intensity of Dragon’s Gaze.

‘This fucker is really too overbearing! But there’s nothing I can do!’ The Starfire Dragon cried inwardly before reluctantly descending to the ground and kneeling before Yuan.

“…”

Yingzi stared at Yuan with slightly widened eyes, clearly surprised by his domineering demeanor.

Once the Starfire Dragon was forced to kneel on the ground amidst the flames, Yuan fell silent. He spent the next several moments staring intently at the subdued dragon, his silence serving to further humiliate the proud creature.

Eventually, Yuan spoke, “So, what are you doing here, on this island that is obviously too small for you?”

“T-this place may be small, but the yang energy here is very beneficial for me, so I am training here.”

The Starfire Dragon didn’t dare to reveal the truth—that he was actually there to court the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy, fearing that it might make him look weak and pathetic. Moreover, he was afraid that Yuan might take an interest in the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy and steal her away from him.

However, Yuan suddenly trembled, his face filled with anger.

“Y-Your Highness…?” The Starfire Dragon had a bad feeling after seeing this.

“Do you think I am stupid? That I am easily fooled? How dare you, a lowly dragon, mock me?!” Yuan roared, his body surging with immense killing intent.

“?!?!”

The Starfire Dragon was left in utter shock and fear after sensing Yuan’s immense killing intent, which manifested as a red miasma enveloping the entire island.

Even Yingzi, who had encountered countless bloodthirsty Primals and Predators throughout her life, was taken aback by the intensity of Yuan’s killing intent. Despite her extensive experience with formidable beings, she had never felt anything as overwhelming as the aura Yuan now emitted.

“I will give you one last chance to tell me what you are doing on this island!” Yuan roared.

“I…I…I am truly sorry, Your Highness! Training here was just an excuse! I am actually here for the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy, whose territory is close by! I have been trying to court her for many years now, and living close to her makes it more convenient!”

“If you don’t provide me a good reason why you lied to me the first time, I’ll ensure you experience a fate worse than death!” Yuan said.

“I truly don’t know why I lied to you, Your Highness! Please forgive me!”

“No, that’s not good enough.” Yuan shook his head with a profound glint in his eyes.

“But I’ll give you a chance to redeem yourself.”

“Anything!”

“Let’s have a spar. If you can defeat me within half an hour, I will pretend you never lied. However, if I win, you will leave this place and never approach the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy.”

“?!?!”

The Starfire Dragon couldn’t believe his ears.

‘He wants to spar with me? As far as I can tell, he’s only at Perfect Chaotic Adept! He won’t last a second against me, much less half an hour!’

Yuan narrowed his eyes at the Starfire Dragon and muttered in a cold voice, “Are you looking down on me right now?”

“H-huh? O-of course not! I wouldn’t dare!” The Starfire Dragon vigorously shook his head. ‘He’s probably hiding his cultivation. There’s no way someone with a royal bloodline would have such a low cultivation!’ The Starfire Dragon convinced himself.

A few moments later, Yuan descended to the fiery ground. Despite his entire body being engulfed in flames, he did not burn and only felt mildly uncomfortable due to his enhanced resistance.

“Do you need some time to prepare?” Yuan asked.

“No, I can start whenever.”

He nodded and said, “Don’t hold back just because of my status. I don’t want any excuses later.”

“As you command…”

The Starfire Dragon transformed entirely the following moment, becoming a giant, red dragon.

He had expected Yuan to shift into his true form for the battle, as fighting in human form would be a disadvantage. So, when Yuan remained in his human guise, he couldn’t help but ask, “You’re going to fight me in that appearance?”

“You’re not even worth it. In fact, if you can make me fully transform, I’ll consider it my defeat,” Yuan said as he partially transformed.

‘This arrogant bastard!’ The Starfire Dragon clenched his teeth, cursing inwardly.

“Then here I come.”

Without using any weapons, Yuan made the first move and approached the Starfire Dragon. He wanted to see his current limit after so many breakthroughs.

They clashed shortly after, with the Starfire Dragon unleashing devastating flames with every movement.

Using nothing but his fists, Yuan struck the Starfire Dragon’s body, each strike as heavy as mountains.

‘Fuck! How does he exude so much power with that cultivation?! It doesn’t make any sense!’

However, as strong as it seemed, Yuan’s punches were only strong enough to inflict superficial damage on the Starfire Dragon.

Not only were the differences in their cultivation too vast, but the Starfire Dragon’s body was unfathomably durable.

‘Does he really intend to fight me while only partially transformed with that level of cultivation?!’ The Starfire Dragon was in disbelief, but he was also filled with anger.

‘Royalty or not, I will teach you a lesson for underestimating me!’

The Starfire Dragon opened its mouth and unleashed a massive ball of flames, its size rivaling that of the entire island itself. The air around it shimmered with intense heat as the colossal fireball gathered energy.

‘Let’s see if you still refuse to fully transform after this!’

“Starfire Meteor!”

The Starfire Dragon released the attack shortly after, hurling the massive ball of flames at Yuan.

chapters.title: Chapter 1620 Starfire Dragon(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1620 Starfire Dragon(3)

Chapter 1620 Starfire Dragon(3)

Long before the Starfire Dragon’s fiery attack even reached him, Yuan could feel his skin being scorched by the intense heat radiating from the massive ball of flames.

Despite the dangerous situation, he refused to move away, seeming intent on blocking the attack with his bare body.

“H-he can’t be serious… right?” The Starfire Dragon muttered in a dazed voice.

After the Starfire Dragon unleashed his attack, he began to fear the possibility of actually killing Yuan. Unsure, he glanced at Yingzi, searching for any sign of concern.

Noting her nonchalant demeanor, he sighed in relief, thinking, ‘I doubt she would actually let me kill him. I need to push him to fully transform, no matter the cost!’ With this resolution, he continued his fiery assault.

As the Starfire Meteor neared, about to make an impact, Yuan wrapped his arm in Enhanced Sword Aura and flicked it toward the massive ball of flames. The heat was intense enough to incinerate his arm completely, yet he sliced the meteor in half, and his burned arm fully recovered a few seconds later.

“What is that sharp energy?” Yingzi’s eyes widened with interest after seeing Sword Aura for the first time.

The Starfire Dragon was just as shocked and puzzled as Yingzi, as he’d never heard of anything like that before.

‘What is that weird energy? And what is with that ridiculous regenerative ability? My flames have a unique property that greatly reduces healing! Why isn’t it working on him?!’

Unbeknownst to the Starfire Dragon, his flames actually reduced Yuan’s healing ability. Otherwise, it wouldn’t have taken him a few seconds to heal but instantly.

<Your resistance to fire has increased>

“Not bad.” Yuan examined his arm, which appeared completely healed on the surface. Despite this, a lingering burning sensation remained.

‘What kind of dragon bloodline does he have? I don’t know of any bloodlines with such a powerful healing ability, not to mention that weird energy he used to split my fire!’

“L-let’s see if you can cut this!” The Starfire Dragon soared into the air, spreading his wings wide. As he did so, tens of thousands of small fireballs materialized, raining down upon Yuan like a fiery meteor shower. In response, Yuan cloaked his entire body in Enhanced Sword Aura, transforming himself into a blur as he charged directly toward the Starfire Dragon, undeterred by the onslaught of flames.

As Yuan charged forward, his Enhanced Sword Aura sliced through the barrage of fireballs, annihilating anything it touched. However, the intense heat from the fireballs penetrated his aura, inflicting direct damage on his body. Despite this, Yuan’s form, now completely charred, continued to advance resolutely towards the Starfire Dragon.

‘What the fuck?!’

The Starfire Dragon was left terrified by Yuan’s unrelenting will and persistence. He always thought dragons with royal bloodline were mentally weak and disliked pain, but Yuan appeared to be the complete opposite of the rumors.

In the midst of Starfire Dragon’s shock, Yuan arrived before him and swung his arm, unleashing a powerful sword light.

The Starfire Dragon hastily snapped out of his daze to avoid the sword light, but he failed and ended up having one of his wings severed.

He unleashed a thunderous roar, echoing through the air with such ferocity that he seemed to have entered a state of frenzy.

“I don’t care anymore!”

The Starfire Dragon suddenly curled his body into a tight ball, and a red, ominous light began radiating from him, signaling the buildup of a potentially devastating attack.

“Need my help? You might die from this.” Yingzi suddenly said.

Yuan shook his head with a smile, “I’ll be fine.”

Not wanting to get caught up in the blast, Yingzi distanced herself from the battlefield.

“STARFIRE…”

The light suddenly intensified, and a fiery aura enveloped the Starfire Dragon.

“BURST!”

The Starfire Dragon erupted in a colossal explosion, his flames rapidly expanding to envelop the entire island in a mere blink of an eye, turning the surroundings into a sea of fire, including the ocean.

The ferocious fire from the Starfire Dragon’s explosion obliterated everything it touched, leaving nothing behind. Yuan was completely engulfed by the flames. The explosion maintained its intensity, with a massive ball of fire resembling the sun hanging in the air for several moments before it gradually began to dissipate.

The Starfire Dragon, completely exhausted of Chaotic Essence, fell directly into the ocean afterward.

Yingzi looked around for Yuan, but he was nowhere to be seen, almost as if he’d been erased from existence.

<Your fire resistance has increased significantly>

Suddenly, as if he’d materialized out of thin air, Yuan’s body quickly recovered until there was not a single burn left on his body.

“…”

Seeing this, Yingzi began doubting whether Yuan was a human or a monster.

“What kind of dragon are you…?” Starfire Dragon muttered in a low voice as he stared at Yuan with a defeated look on his face.

“I have never seen or heard of an indestructible dragon before… Even the Shadow Dragons, rumored to be as indestructible as Shadow Dwellers, would’ve perished just now…”

“That’s none of your business. Anyway, it’s your defeat, so you’ll never show your face before the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy again as punishment. If I find out that you’ve broken our agreement, I will slaughter your entire bloodline. Do you understand?”

“Of course, Your Highness. I swear upon my bloodline that I will never approach the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy again…”

Yuan nodded and suddenly retrieved a pill before tossing it into the dragon’s mouth.

“W-what did you just feed me?” The Starfire Dragon was instantly alarmed.

“Relax. That will recover your injuries.” Yuan said.

Sure enough, the Starfire Dragon’s exhaustion was quickly lifted, and within minutes, he was full of energy, almost as if they had never fought. “W-what a miracle treasure! Thank you, Your Highness!” The Starfire Dragon returned to his human appearance and bowed to Yuan.

“I’m tired of seeing your face. Scram.” Yuan coldly sneered.

“Yes!”

The Starfire Dragon didn’t dare to linger and immediately flew away as if his life depended on it.

chapters.title: Chapter 1621 Ranked Territories
chapters.body:
Chapter 1621 Ranked Territories

Chapter 1621 Ranked Territories

“It feels like I am forgetting something…” Yuan muttered to himself shortly after the Starfire Dragon left the scene.

“Whatever. It’s too late to worry about that now.”

Sometime later, Yingzi brought Yuan with her back to the Mystic Valley.

“Do all humans have regenerative abilities like that?” Yingzi asked him as they traveled.

“No, they don’t. I have an ability that allows me to regenerate that quickly.”

“Can this ability be taught?”

“I don’t think so since it requires you to eat something dangerous.”

While humans are incapable of consuming Demon Cores, Yuan wondered if beasts in the Primal Expanse have such capability since the Chaotic Essence in their world was similar to the energy found within Demon Cores.

Once they returned to the Mystic Valley, the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy appeared before them with a weird expression on her face.

She had watched their interaction with the Starfire Dragon and was left speechless by the results.

“Are you really human?” she suddenly asked Yuan with a doubtful face.

“Of course. What makes you think otherwise?”

The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy explained, “Because humans aren’t capable of using the power of our world. Moreover, you managed to create an incomplete Chaotic Heart. Are you perhaps half human and half Primal?”

The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy had always suspected Yuan’s identity since the very beginning, but due to so many confusing factors, it was nearly impossible to confirm anything about him.

“No, I am full human. I am also unsure why I can cultivate Chaotic Essence, but I think it might have something to do with my physique.”

The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy pondered for a moment before responding, “If I recall correctly, some humans are born with special bodies and bloodlines, just like Primals, but they’re incredibly rare.”

“What about your healing abilities? Is that also due to your unique physique?”

“No, that’s something else entirely.”

“Is that so? Well, I won’t pry further. Anyway, I believe you may have forgotten about something.”

“Oh? I also had the same feeling, but I don’t remember what exactly.”

“You were supposed to acquire a treasure that will allow me to use more Chaotic Essence than I currently contain, and the Starfire Dragon had something like that, but you let him go without it.”

“Ah, right. I remember now. With that said, I was only going to do that if I killed him, which I didn’t. Also, he would become suspicious of my identity if I suddenly asked for his treasure. I’m supposed to be royalty, after all.”

“Dragon’s Influence, huh? I was extremely shocked when you used it. Does your world have dragons, too?”

“Of course. In fact, I think the beasts in my world originally came from the Primal Expanse. The only difference is that they cultivate different resources.”

“It’s possible, but there is only one individual who can confirm that—the Primal Monarch.” The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy said.

“Anyway, now that you’ve dealt with my annoying neighbor, you’ll need to find me a treasure that can temporarily boost my Chaotic Essence. The closest place with something like that would be the Phoenixes’ territory. They’re about three months away from here.”

Yuan narrowed his eyes at this information.

“Phoenixes’ territory?”

He wondered if they could help accelerate Feng Yuxiang’s recovery. Despite his efforts in constantly feeding her his blood, there were still no signs that she would awaken anytime soon.

“Yes, but there is a slight problem. The phoenixes in this world aren’t kind to dragons. In fact, they detest each other to the point where they will attack each other on sight. I’m afraid you won’t get near them, much less acquire their treasure.

“You call that a slight problem?” Yuan said with a raised eyebrow. freewёbn૦νeɭ.com

The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy shrugged and said, “Then you’ll have to visit the Divine Dragon Sanctuary. They’re pretty far from here, but there is a space tunnel nearby that will shorten the trip to two years… from two hundred years.”

Yuan shook his head and said, “No, I’ll visit the phoenixes’ territory. I have some business with them, regardless of the treasure.”

“Are you sure? Even the Shadow Dweller won’t be able to protect you there.” The Divine Fox said as she shifted her gaze to Yingzi.

While Yingzi was undoubtedly powerful, she was not among the most powerful beings in the Primal Expanse. As a matter of fact, she was far from it. Yuan looked at Yingzi and asked her, “I never asked you, but how strong are you?”

Due to her unique existence, it was impossible to determine Yingzi’s cultivation base unless she disclosed it herself.

“Early Chaotic Emperor,” she calmly revealed.

“Only Early Chaotic Emperor?” Yuan was a little surprised to hear that she was only slightly stronger than the Starfire Dragon in terms of cultivation, as she had been incredibly confident throughout their journey.

The Starfire Dragon had a cultivation equivalent of Divine Sovereign in the Nine Heavens, so Yingzi would only be as strong as a Divine Ancestor.

“You shouldn’t underestimate a Shadow Dweller because, just like you, they’re far more powerful in reality due to their unique abilities to control shadows.” The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy said.

“At her level, she can probably defeat even a Chaotic Overlord, much less a Perfect Chaotic Emperor, and the difference between a Chaotic Emperor and a Chaotic Overlord is immense, sort of like the difference between being a mortal and an immortal in your world.”

“However, the Phoenix territory you’ll be heading to is a rank two territory, with rank one being the strongest and rank three being the weakest. In a rank two territory, there will be multiple Chaotic Immortals and a single one has the capability to erase you from existence with their breath alone.”

“What rank is the Mystic Valley?” Yuan asked out of curiosity.

The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy laughed out loud, “Hahaha! My territory doesn’t even qualify to be ranked! Only large communities with many powerful Predators are qualified. This place is a small house on an island in comparison.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1622 Feng Yuxiang’S Spatial Ring
chapters.body:
Chapter 1622 Feng Yuxiang’S Spatial Ring

Chapter 1622 Feng Yuxiang’s Spatial Ring

In order for a territory to be ranked, it must first have a community with at least a hundred Predators. Once that requirement was fulfilled, the overall prowess of that territory determined the rank of the territory.

The Phoenix territory that Yuan planned on going to was a rank-two territory, with over ten Chaotic Immortals, each as strong as a Bronze Immortal, at the very least.

Even though the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy warned him of the dangers and the Phoenixes’ hatred toward Dragons, Yuan had to go there not just to find the Primal Monarch but also for Feng Yuxiang.

“If you’re resolute on going to the Phoenixes’ territory, I won’t stop you.” The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy pointed in a certain direction and continued, “If you go straight, you’ll arrive at your destination in about three months, and I have already taken into consideration the Shadow Dweller’s speed.”

“Thank you. I’ll return once I acquire the treasure.”

“Don’t forget this time, and it’s called the True Ember.” “I won’t.” Yuan smiled.

Yuan and Yingzi left the Mystic Valley and began their journey to the Phoenixes’ territory shortly after.

“Yingzi, I don’t expect you to protect me inside the Phoenixes’ territory. If you sense danger, just run.”

However, Yingzi said, “While I won’t be able to defeat the Phoenixes, I can ensure you keep your life, at the very least, but it’ll cost you.”

“Of course.” He nodded.

While they traveled, Yuan pondered how he should communicate with the Phoenixes once they arrived.

‘According to the Divine Fox, they will attack Dragons on sight, so I probably won’t be able to have a proper conversation with them. I should try to acquire their attention as soon as possible, but how can I achieve that?’

Yuan pondered whether it would be a good idea to reveal Feng Yuxiang’s egg to them.

‘No, I can’t do that until I am certain they won’t attack me. I cannot risk it, especially when Feng Feng is in that condition.’

‘Should I just use Immortal Monarch’s Dominion on them? That will definitely get their attention, but there’s a chance it might not work on them, and there’s a good chance it might anger them even further.’

Unable to think of anything, Yuan asked Xiao Hua and Yu Ning for advice.

“Sorry, but I can’t think of anything.” Yu Ning sighed.

“Brother Yuan, why don’t you look inside that dumb pigeon’s spatial ring and see if there’s anything useful in there.” Xiao Hua suddenly suggested.

Feng Yuxiang had left behind her spatial ring when she entered her slumber. Normally, one wouldn’t be able to access another person’s spatial ring while the owner was still alive, but Feng Yuxiang was Yuan’s servant and had previously granted him permission to use it.

However, Yuan never even tried to look inside her spatial ring, as he respected her privacy and refused to invade it.

“I guess I have no choice. I’ll apologize to her later.”

Yuan retrieved Feng Yuxiang’s spatial ring and opened it for the first time.

“?!?!?”

Yuan’s eyes widened with shock after seeing the treasures inside.

He’d always known that Feng Yuxiang had a large collection of rare treasures because of her store in the Lower Heavens, but he’d vastly underestimated her wealth.

After seeing just how truly wealthy Feng Yuxiang was, Yuan couldn’t help but bow to her inside his head.

Not only did Feng Yuxiang’s spatial ring have a larger storage space than his own Dragon Spatial Ring, but there were all sorts of treasures that could only be found in the Upper Heavens. What she’d shown in her store at the Lower Heavens barely scratched the surface of her vast collection.

There were mountains of treasures in the spatial ring for Yuan to go through, and luckily for him, he had nothing but time for the next three months.

After spending two and a half months going through all of the items, Yuan found several that might help him in the Phoenixes’ territory.

[Burning Phoenix Pill] [Tier 7] [Purity: 94%]

[Effects: Permanently increases fire resistance and grants the power of a Phoenix for nine sunrises]

[Ancient Phoenix Robes]

[Grade: Mythic]

[Quality: Peak]

[Description: An ancient robe, once worn by the most noble of Phoenixes, symbolizing their supreme status and power. Grants fire immunity. It can only be worn by those with a Phoenix bloodline.]

[Warning: Those without a Phoenix bloodline will be punished with Phoenix’s Wrath if they try to wear this item!]

[Primordial Phoenix Blood Essence]

[Effects: ???]

[Description: The Blood Essence of a Primordial Phoenix]

When Yuan saw the Primordial Phoenix Blood Essence, it reminded him of the Dragon Ancestor’s Blood Essence, and knowing how valuable it was, he didn’t dare to use it without Feng Yuxiang’s permission. However, it made him wonder how Feng Yuxiang acquired such a precious treasure in the first place.

‘Phoenix’s Wrath… how bad could it be?’

After taking some time to mentally prepare himself, Yuan asked Yingzi to stop traveling temporarily.

“Give me a moment to try something out,” he said to her before gaining some distance from her.

Once he felt there was enough distance between them, Yuan retrieved the Ancient Phoenix Robes and wore it on him.

Ding!

<The Ancient Phoenix Robes burns with rage!>

<You have been afflicted with Phoenix’s Wrath!>

[Phoenix’s Wrath]

[Effects: Inflicts immense pain upon the targeted.]

Yuan’s body suddenly became engulfed in flames that were several times stronger than the Starfire Dragon’s most powerful attack, enveloping him in a blazing aura of unprecedented pain. Yet, despite the immense pain and being enveloped in flames far more intense than those of the Starfire Dragon, Yuan’s body resisted immediate disintegration, and only his skin was burnt away. The purpose of Phoenix’s Wrath was to inflict pain so excruciating that death would seem a preferable alternative.

Yuan attempted to withstand the pain, but even with his exceptional physique and high pain tolerance, he could only bear it for a few seconds. Upon removing the Ancient Phoenix Robes, the flames of Phoenix’s Wrath dissipated, yet the agonizing pain lingered in his body for a little longer, enough to nearly make him lose consciousness.

<Your resistance to fire has increased tremendously>

<You have gained Super Fire Resistance>

chapters.title: Chapter 1623 Phoenix Ascendancy Plateau
chapters.body:
Chapter 1623 Phoenix Ascendancy Plateau

Chapter 1623 Phoenix Ascendancy Plateau

“Fuck, that was fucking painful!” Yuan cursed out loud after taking off the Ancient Phoenix Robes.

He’d worn it out of curiosity and expected some pain, but he never anticipated it to be that painful. ‘Even though it increased my fire resistance by an entire stage after wearing it for a few seconds, I don’t want to experience that level of pain again… It’s not worth it…’ Yuan quickly put the Ancient Phoenix Robes back into Feng Yuxiang’s spatial ring before retrieving another treasure.

‘I need to mask my dragon scent first and foremost since I cannot undo my transformation, or everyone will know that I am a human.’

The item he retrieved was a small red pouch. ƒreewebηoveℓ.com

[Phoenix Powder]

[Description: Made from Phoenix Feathers. It has incredible healing properties and produces the scent of a Phoenix when mixed with water.]

‘This is exactly what I am looking for. Since it’s only made from feathers, I doubt it’s too valuable to use,’ Yuan smiled at this find.

Sometime later, Yuan filled a bottle with water and added the Phoenix Powder to it, creating a light red liquid that resembled blood diluted in water. The mixture emitted a unique scent that tingled his nose with each whiff.

“Yingzi, does this smell like a Phoenix to you?” Yuan asked her.

Yingzi took a deep breath and pondered for a moment before responding, “It’s similar, but it’s a little weak. I don’t think it’ll be able to mask your dragon scent, either.”

“Is that so?”

Upon hearing Yingzi’s criticism, Yuan added more Phoenix Powder to the liquid, intensifying the smell.

“That should be good enough,” Yingzi said after taking another deep breath.

“Okay.”

Yuan proceeded to rub the liquid all over his body until Yingzi could no longer smell the scent of dragons on him.

Not only did the liquid mask his Dragon scent and make him smell like a Phoenix, but he could also feel his body becoming more energetic.

‘I should save the Burning Phoenix Pill for when I really need it since it only lasts for nine days…’

Now that Yuan had found a way to mask his Dragon scent, he needed to make his identity more convincing, a challenging task given that he wasn’t originally from their world, and crafting a believable persona required careful attention to the cultural and social nuances of this new environment.

“Can you tell me what you know about Phoenixes in the Primal Expanse?” Yuan asked Yingzi as they continued their journey.

“Hmm…” Yingzi had to really think about it since she hadn’t had much interaction with Phoenixes, given their rarity.

“I’m not too familiar with them, but from what I’ve heard, they’re all incredibly selfish and unreasonably prideful creatures. They’re arrogant, have bad tempers, and are generally unpleasant to be around if you’re not a fellow Phoenix.”

“…”

Yingzi’s description of Phoenixes left Yuan speechless, but he didn’t doubt it.

A week later, Yuan had to reapply the liquid on his body to maintain his Phoenix scent.

As they approached the Phoenixes’ territory, Yingzi suddenly warned Yuan, “A Phoenix is coming.”

Moments later, a large, fiery Phoenix could be seen soaring in their direction. This Phoenix stopped once it was before Yuan.

‘Divine Emperor, huh? A little weaker than I was expecting, but this is good for us.’ Yuan quickly gauged the Phoenix’s strength.

“I don’t recognize you. Which bloodline do you belong to? And what are you trying to achieve in our Phoenix Ascendancy Plateau? ” The Phoenix asked him.

“I am from the Ancient Phoenix Bloodline,” Yuan said, using Feng Yuxiang’s bloodline as his own.

“A-Ancient Phoenix?!” The Phoenix exclaimed in a shocked voice after hearing this.

“That’s impossible! There are very few Ancient Phoenixes in the Primal Expanse, and I know all of them! How come I don’t recognize you?”
