“Can’t you tell by my cultivation that I was only recently born?” Yuan shook his head in a disappointed manner.

“Indeed, you do smell a little young… Who are your parents, if you don’t mind me asking?”

Yuan frowned and responded in an irritated tone, “I do mind. Who the hell are you to question me? I may be young, but I still have the Ancient Phoenix Bloodline. Watch your manners!”

He deduced that the Ancient Phoenix Bloodline likely held a high standing among the Phoenix clans, so he decided to embrace this identity and play along.

The Phoenix trembled after hearing Yuan and quickly said, “M-my apologies!”

“Forget it. Anyway, let’s talk about my reason for being here. I am looking for a treasure called the True Ember, and I was told that I could find it here.”

“The True Ember? While it’s true that we have it, it’s not something we can give away…”

“I am willing to trade for it. Is there anything you want for it?”

“I apologize, but I do not have the authority to speak on this.”

“Then bring me to someone who does.”

“I understand. Please, follow me.”

Sometime later, they reached the heart of the territory, where a community of Phoenixes resided. However, within the Phoenix Ascendancy Plateau, most of the Phoenixes possessed low-grade bloodlines, and the majority of its population consisted not of true Phoenix bloodlines but rather of subspecies related to Phoenixes, such as the Divine Yang Bird or the Vermillion Sky Hawk.

“Please wait here while I notify our leader of your presence.” The Phoenix that led Yuan said to him before disappearing somewhere.

Yuan used this opportunity to closely observe the Phoenix Ascendancy Plateau. Despite being referred to as a community, there were no buildings; instead, most of the beings resided within caves and outdoor nests. This setting gave Yuan the impression of being in a zoo, one predominantly populated by birds and Phoenixes, a stark contrast to the urban or structured habitats he might have expected.

The temperature within the Phoenix Ascendancy Plateau was also hot and dry, but it wasn’t as bad as the Starfire Dragon’s territory.

<Your fire resistance has increased slightly>

Still, it was hot enough to temper his body.

chapters.title: Chapter 1624 Phoenix Ascendancy Plateau(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1624 Phoenix Ascendancy Plateau(2)

Chapter 1624 Phoenix Ascendancy Plateau(2)

“Leader, we have a situation.” The Phoenix that met with Yuan bowed before a handsome young man.

This young man’s hair blazed red with strands of bright orange, mirroring the fiery aura he carried about him. Clad in a robe of red and gold, the colors of flame and royalty, he exuded the powerful presence of a Golden Immortal, attracting attention and awe from those around him.

“Is this about the guest you brought back?” The young man asked.

“Yes. He claims to have the Ancient Phoenix Bloodline. It’s a little suspicious, but I dare not verify his identity.” The Phoenix said.

Even if there was a ninety-nine percent chance that Yuan might be a fake, they couldn’t risk offending an Ancient Phoenix. “He is also traveling with a Shadow Dweller.”

“A Phoenix and a Shadow Dweller traveling together? What an odd pair.”

A young lady with features reminiscent of the young man sat beside him, her expression marked by curiosity.

“The Ancient Phoenix Bloodline is an Imperial-grade bloodline, and it ranks in the top 3 amongst all Phoenix bloodlines. They’re essentially royalty. Meanwhile, my Scarlet Phoenix Bloodline is only ranked 9th. If he turns out to be real, we must assist him to the best of our capabilities. However, if he is a fake… I’ll make him suffer a fate worse than death.” The young man said.

“What does he want, anyway?”

“He wishes to trade for our True Ember.”

“The True Ember, huh? While it’s not a priceless artifact, it’s quite valuable. If he’s an Ancient Phoenix, he is likely to have come from the rank one Phoenix territory, Eternal Phoenix Domain, and they have plenty of artifacts that surpass our True Ember, so why would he come all the way here?”

“Forget it. There’s no point guessing their motives. Bring them to me, and I’ll ask them myself.”

“Yes!”

The Phoenix departed the next moment, returning to Yuan and Yingzi.

“Nobody is dumb or bold enough to imitate an Ancient Phoenix, right?” The young lady suddenly muttered out loud.

The young man sighed, “The Primal Expanse is immense beyond comprehension. There are bound to be some fearless individuals out there.”

Sometime later, the Phoenix returned to Yuan’s side.

“Esteemed guest, our leader would like to meet you. Please, follow me.”

The Phoenix led Yuan and Yingzi to the summit of the tallest mountain within the territory. Upon reaching the peak, Yuan was greeted by a surprisingly familiar sight—a pagoda stood prominently on the mountaintop.

‘So they do have buildings in this world… I guess they’re just incredibly rare.’ Yuan thought to himself.

However, the pagoda was striking in its simplicity, almost to a fault. It appeared that the builder, while earnest in their efforts, had clearly lacked experience and imagination in its design, resulting in a structure that was functional but unadorned and stark. It was almost as if the builder had to work on it with nothing but a brief explanation of its outer appearance.

Once the Phoenix brought Yuan and Yingzi inside the pagoda, it became even more apparent that the builder had no idea what they were building. Although the pagoda looked like it had four floors from the outside, inside, there was only one floor and room. The other floors were just an empty space.

Inside, at the far end of the modestly decorated room, sat two individuals: a handsome young man and a beautiful young woman.

Their appearance was top quality, surpassing even the appearance Yuan had given the Supreme Ox. However, they would still only be considered slightly above average when compared to those truly beautiful in the Nine Heavens.

The young man narrowed his eyes at Yuan the instant they entered the room, his gaze as sharp as swords.

Before Yuan could even open his mouth, the young man spoke with a cold expression, “Bastard, what are you trying to pull here?”

“What do you mean?” Yuan frowned.

“The Ancient Phoenix Bloodline ranks the third purest amongst all Phoenix bloodlines, and all inferior bloodlines would naturally feel submissive toward them. You may smell like a Phoenix, but I don’t feel anything from you. I have seen Phoenixes with royal bloodlines before, and your aura is nothing like theirs. You may be able to mimic our smell, but you will never be able to mimic our royal bloodline. You should’ve disguised as an ordinary Phoenix instead, you bastard.”

Even though his disguise was instantly exposed, Yuan remained calm and began to think of a way out of his predicament.

“Are you willing to bet your life—your territory and all its residents on those accusations?” Yuan asked.

“Your threat is as weak and empty as your attempt to fool me. I can easily erase someone at your cultivation with a sneeze. As for the Shadow Dweller, even if she has the ability to wipe us out, the Eternal Phoenix Domain will avenge us.”

“Kneel before me!” The young man suddenly roared, and his body exuded a profound aura that instantly filled the room.

“…”

A few moments of silence went by without anything happening, leaving Yuan puzzled.

The young man frowned and muttered, “If my Phoenix’s Influence failed to work on you, then you must not be a Phoenix at all… What are you?”

Yuan swiftly noticed an opportunity to turn the situation around and showed a cold smile on his face.

“You dare order me to kneel? A lowly Phoenix like you?”

The young man’s face twisted with anger as he stood up.

“How dare—” freeweɓnovel.cѳm

“Who permitted you to talk back? Shut up!”

Upon hearing Yuan’s voice, imbued with an unfathomable power, the young man felt his own voice falter, leaving him unable to utter another word.

“?!?!” The young lady turned to look at him with wide eyes.

“F-father?! Please tell me you’re only pretending to—”

Yuan shifted his gaze to her and shouted, “Shut up!”

The young lady immediately felt as though someone had suddenly seized her throat, sealing her ability to speak.

“?!?!”

“Kneel!”

Thud!

Unable to resist his command, the young man and young lady suddenly fell to their knees, their gaze filled with shock and horror as they stared at Yuan.

chapters.title: Chapter 1625 True Ember
chapters.body:
Chapter 1625 True Ember

Chapter 1625 True Ember

‘This power… Phoenix’s Influence?! Impossible! He should be a fake! Why does he wield such power?!’ The young man exclaimed inwardly as his entire being was forced to obey Yuan’s commands.

‘He was a genuine Ancient Phoenix?! We’re screwed! Our entire territory is screwed!’ The young lady cried inwardly.

Due to her lower cultivation, the Immortal Monarch’s Dominion was even more potent on her.

As for the young man who was as strong as a Golden Immortal, even he was powerless before the Immortal Monarch’s Dominion.

The last time Yuan used Immortal Monarch’s Dominion on an immortal-level entity, it could only affect them for a few seconds. However, he was still only a Spirit King back then.

After his breakthrough, Yuan’s control and prowess of Immortal Monarch’s Dominion reached unprecedented levels. Moreover, he had been strengthening his Soul Strength nonstop for the past year during his travel around the Primal Expanse.

At his current prowess, he could easily command even a Golden Immortal for an entire minute.

‘For whatever reason, Immortal Monarch’s Dominion feels stronger in the Primal Expanse…’ Yuan thought to himself after noticing this phenomenon.

Yuan spoke in the following moment, “I will ask you again. Are you willing to bet your life and your territory on your claims?”

Yuan released Immortal Monarch’s Dominion on the young man so he could speak.

“…”

However, the young man didn’t respond.

Seeing this, Yuan turned his focus to the young lady next to the young man and spoke in a commanding tone, “Who gave you the permission to breathe?”

“!?!”

The young lady instantly lost her ability to breathe and fell to the ground with her hands grasping at her throat.

The young man instantly snapped out of his daze and shouted, “I-I was wrong, Your Highness! Please forgive me and my daughter for this offense! I will even give you the True Ember as an apology!” frёewebηovel.cѳm

“…”

Yuan had a calm look on his face, but he was inwardly surprised that his small experiment actually worked. It made him wonder if he commanded someone to die, would their heart instantly stop?

“Your Highness! Please!” The young man pleaded again when Yuan refused to release the young lady.

“I’ll forgive you this once.”

The young lady immediately began gasping for air after Yuan deactivated the Immortal Monarch’s Dominion.

“The faster you hand over the True Ember, the faster I will be out of your feathers,” he added.

“Get the True Ember for His Highness!” The young man quickly ordered the young lady, giving her an excuse to leave the room.

“Right away!” The young lady hastily left.

Once they were alone, Yuan said, “Is there anything you want for the True Ember? Although you said it’ll be for free, I don’t want any rumors of me bullying you to spread.”

“I-I don’t dare ask for anything.” The young man said.

Yuan silently pondered for a moment before speaking, “You seem to have a thing for human appearances. I can upgrade your appearance and your daughters in exchange for the True Ember.”

The young man looked at him with wide eyes and said, “Really?”

He nodded and said, “Give me a brief description of your ideal appearance. Young, middle-aged, or old? Hair color and length? Body shape?”

“Umm… I would prefer something a little older than my current appearance. I like my current hair color and style. As for my body…”

After receiving the description, Yuan consumed an appearance-altering pill and transformed into the young man’s ideal appearance.

The young man’s eyes widened with surprise after seeing how high-quality it was.

“What do you think?”

“Can I really have such a high-quality appearance?”

“Are you doubting me?” Yuan narrowed his eyes.

“N-no!” “Then hurry up and take it.”

The young man quickly changed his appearance, becoming more handsome, a little taller, and a few years older.

“Thank you, Your Highness, for this appearance!” The young man stood up and bowed to Yuan. “If there’s anything else you need, please don’t hesitate to ask!”

Yuan smiled and quickly said, “Then let me ask you something.”

He retrieved Feng Yuxiang’s egg and continued, “Do you know how I can speed up my friend’s recovery progress?”

“T-that’s—!” The young man’s face became filled with shock after seeing the egg.

“What’s wrong?” Yuan asked him with a slight frown.

“W-who is inside that shell?! I have seen this state countless times before, but I have never seen such a complex and powerful shell! Not to mention…”

Yet, what shocked him most wasn’t the shell itself but the overwhelming sensation it evoked within him. Bloodlines of a lower grade instinctively revere those of higher lineage, and he felt a powerful compulsion to kneel and kowtow before the entity within the shell.

‘There’s only one bloodline that could make my blood react in such a fashion! To think such a powerful entity would be in this sorry state… what happened?’ he wondered inwardly.

“I’m sorry, but there’s nothing we can do here to help you or the one inside that shell since our bloodlines are too weak. If you want to revive the Phoenix within that shell, you’ll need to visit the Eternal Phoenix Domain. There, you’ll be able to find the Primordial Phoenix.”

‘How far away is the Eternal Phoenix Domain?’ Yuan asked Yingzi since he would have exposed himself if he had asked the Phoenix before him.

‘It’s on the other side of the world, so it’ll take several hundred years if we travel normally,’ she said.

‘That’s too far… I guess we’ll have to put this on hold for now.’ Yuan sighed inwardly.

Sometime later, the young lady returned to the room with the True Ember, but she was astonished by her father’s new appearance.

“His Highness said he’d upgrade our appearances for the True Ember,” he explained.

Once he received the description, Yuan gave the young lady her new appearance.

“Thank you, Your Highness! Here is the True Ember!”

The young lady handed Yuan a small black rock that emitted intense heat. “Thank you.” Yuan quickly tossed the True Ember inside his spatial ring.

chapters.title: Chapter 1626 Ancient Canyon
chapters.body:
Chapter 1626 Ancient Canyon

Chapter 1626 Ancient Canyon

After putting away the True Ember, Yuan quickly made his way toward the exit, unwilling to remain in the Phoenixes’ territory any longer.

Neither the young man nor the young lady tried to stop him. In fact, they also wanted him to leave as soon as possible.

Once they left, Yuan and Yingzi began flying back to the Mystic Valley.

“Fuck, this is why I hate royalty. They do as they please and treat us like dirt.” The young man sneered in disdain once they were alone.

“We did call him a fake first…” The young lady sighed out loud.

“I don’t understand what went wrong. As far as I can tell, he definitely didn’t have the bloodline of an Ancient Phoenix. Yet, he was able to use Phoenix’s Influence on me despite his lack of cultivation.”

“Haa… forget it. It’s already in the past.”

Three months later, Yuan and Yingzi made it back to the Mystic Valley in one piece.

“Oh? You’re back already? That was faster than I anticipated.” The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy greeted them the moment they entered her territory.

“I got the True Ember,” Yuan said.

“Really? How did you deal with the Phoenixes as a dragon? Didn’t they attack you on sight?”

Yuan shook his head with a smile, “I have my methods.” “Anyway, here is the True Ember.”

He retrieved the treasure and tossed it to her.

“Okay, give me a week to prepare.”

While the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy began her preparation to divine the Primal Monarch’s location, Yuan spent that time with Meixiu and the others.

While Yuan was in the Primal Expanse, Meixiu, Chu Liuxiang, and Li Jinxi continued their training with Kelan. Meanwhile, the rest of the Demon Sealing Faction had graduated from their sects and traveled to the Fourth Heaven, joining Kelan’s training three months ago.

“How’s your progress? Have you figured out a way to get out of the Primal Expanse yet?” Wang Ming asked him during dinner.

“No, but I think I am getting close.”

“Take your time so the rest of us can catch up.” Shi Lang chuckled.

“The Primal Expanse is so vast that most of my time is spent traveling. I have been pretty lucky so far with the distance I need to travel, but I’m worried that the next place I need to go might take too long,” Yuan sighed. He could imagine the Primal Monarch being in a location that was several decades or even centuries away. “Have you tried to kill yourself? Who knows, you might respawn back in the last city you visited.” Wang Bingbing suddenly said.

“That’s quite a dangerous suggestion. It’d be disastrous if he becomes a cripple because of that.” Wang Ming said.

Yuan nodded and said, “I have thought about it, and as Wang Ming said, it’s quite risky. That said, if nothing else works, I can only try that as a last resort.”

“By the way, the Lord wanted to speak with you as soon as possible.” Wang Xiuying suddenly said.

“I’ll go meet him after this.”

After dinner, Yuan went to speak with the Lord.

“What’s up?” Yuan greeted the Lord casually.

“We have been contacted by the Qi Family. Do you remember them?” The Lord said.

“Of course. Since you’re contacting me, it must be related to demons, right?” Yuan could easily guess the reason behind the Qi Family’s contact. The Qi Family was the Earth’s version of the Demon Sealing Clan, and Yuan had personally trained them before.

“You’re correct. They have recently stumbled upon another sealed demon. According to them, this demon is unfathomably strong and surpasses the 11 demons you dealt with in the past.”

“Where is this demon? I’ll deal with it as soon as possible.”

“They haven’t disclosed the location to us yet. I’ll notify them of your arrival if you want to visit them directly.”

“You can do that. I’ll visit them first thing tomorrow morning.”

“I understand.”

After leaving behind the Starry Abyss so that Ji Ran could heal the Lord, Yuan returned to his residence.

The next morning, Liya was already waiting at Yuan’s front door, ready to escort him to the Qi Family, just as she had done previously.

“Welcome back to the Qi Family, Manager Liya. Yuan.”

The Qi Family was ready to greet them long before their arrival.

“It’s been a while.” Yuan smiled.

“Thank you again for your assistance,” Qi Man said with his hands clasped.

“Don’t mention it. Anyway, where’s the demon?”

“It’s located at the Ancient Canyon.”

“Is it close to breaking out of its seal?” “No, and some people are watching over it even as we speak. They would notify us if there were any changes.”

“Even if it’s not close to breaking out of its seal, I am going to take care of it immediately.”

Qi Man then said, “Please don’t be too hasty. We are aware of your prowess, but this demon is different. We believe it’s at least a Superior Demon with a tiny chance of it being a Demon General.”

“A Demon General, huh?” Yuan muttered with an intrigued face.

Suddenly, someone could be heard shouting, “Elder Qi! We have just received a message from the expedition team! It’s an emergency! Cracks have started appearing on the demon!” “What?!” Qi Man exclaimed.

“Looks like we can’t take things slowly anymore,” Yuan said. “Bring me to the demon.”

“Y-yes! I will bring you there myself!” Qi Man quickly responded.

Yuan and Liya followed Qi Man to the Qi Family’s private jet and quickly left.

A day later, they arrived at their destination, the Ancient Canyon.

The Ancient Canyon is a 1,000-mile-long plateau composed of horizontal layers of lava and rock, featuring a range of landscapes, including arid deserts and barren stretches. This area is rarely explored due to its harsh climate and extreme heat, which can easily reach up to 170 degrees Fahrenheit, making it nearly impossible to explore without proper equipment. “We’ve arrived.” Qi Man notified them after the jet landed.

chapters.title: Chapter 1627 A Different Person
chapters.body:
Chapter 1627 A Different Person

Chapter 1627 A Different Person

After disembarking from the jet, Qi Man led Yuan and Liya to the sealed demon, where a dozen members of the Qi Family were stationed to keep watch over it.

“Yuan?” Qi Fang, one of the people stationed there, was a little surprised to see Yuan.

“Hey, it’s been a while.”

“You came here alone?” she asked.

“Yes, the others are busy with their training.”

Yuan’s attention quickly shifted to the sealed demon in the distance.

“Just one? How disappointing.”

“Actually, we found another sealed demon just a few hours ago,” Qi Fang suddenly revealed.

“What?! How powerful is it?!” Qi Man exclaimed.

“It’s hard to tell, but it shouldn’t be any stronger than this one.” Qi Fang’s gaze landed on the sealed demon several meters away.

Yuan approached the demon and examined it with his divine sense.

“This demon…is a Demon General,” he muttered a moment later.

“What?! Are you certain?!” Qi Fang exclaimed in a shocked voice.

He nodded in confirmation and continued speaking, “Not only is it a Demon General, but it’s as powerful as a Spirit Sovereign.”

Qi Fang and everyone who heard his evaluation fell to their knees, their faces etched with despair.

“S-Spirit Sovereign? How the hell are we supposed to kill it?! This demon could destroy the world by itself!” Qi Man cried out loud as he fell back and collapsed on the ground.

“E-Elder Qi!”

“Grandfather!”

Qi Fang rushed to his side.

Seeing this, Yuan said, “Calm down. It’s only a Spirit Sovereign. I’ll take care of it.”

Qi Fang frowned at his words and said, “This isn’t the time to be arrogant or get carried away, Yuan. I know you’re powerful, but you’re only at the peak of Spirit Grandmaster. There’s no way you can fight a demon that is as strong as a Spirit Sovereign, much less kill it! The only thing we can do is strengthen the seal until we’re capable of dealing with it!”

“True, my cultivation is only at the peak of Spirit Grandmaster, but it won’t take long for me to increase it.”

Yuan suddenly retrieved a treasure he’d acquired from the Primal Expanse. After swallowing it, he could immediately feel his cultivation soaring to the next realm. A second later, Yuan had a breakthrough and reached the first level of Spirit Lord.

However, his surge of spiritual energy didn’t slow down; it continued relentlessly until he broke through to the second level of Spirit Lord. It then pushed him into the third level before finally settling at the fourth level of Spirit Lord.

If it wasn’t for the Celestial Emperor’s curse, he could’ve easily entered the Spirit Sovereign realm with that treasure.

“Alright, let’s get this over with.”

While everyone was still in a daze after witnessing Yuan breakthrough with the ease of breathing, he swiftly unsealed the demon before any of them could even react.

“Ah! What have you done?!” Qi Fang cried out loud.

“Get away from here if you don’t want to get caught up in our battle,” Yuan said to them.

“R-run!” Qi Fang shouted, snapping the others out of their daze. freёweɓnovel.com

Once they realized their situation, they immediately scrambled to safety—to Liya’s side.

Moments later, the demon was fully released from its seal and calmly began to survey its surroundings and assess the situation.

“Hahaha! I am finally free!” It suddenly burst out laughing, its voice loud and powerful enough to create shockwaves.

Yuan didn’t interrupt the demon’s celebration and patiently waited for it to notice him. Sometime later, the demon turned to look at Yuan and spoke, “There’s even a meal prepared for me? This must be Demon God’s work!”

The demon suddenly lunged at Yuan, its claw-like hands reaching for his neck.

However, just as it was able to reach Yuan, it suddenly felt something grasping onto its neck, holding it back from advancing any further.

The demon was stunned to realize that Yuan had seized its neck before it could grab his.

“When did you—?!”

“Shhh…” Yuan hushed the demon by tightening his grasp on its neck. If it weren’t for the demon’s regenerative abilities, it would have already died from having its neck crushed.

“You just escaped your seal, so I’m sure you haven’t fully recovered your strength. Don’t worry, I’ll give you some time to recover so it isn’t too boring.”

Yuan flicked his arm, flinging the demon away like a ragdoll.

“Yuan! What the hell are you doing?!” Qi Fang yelled at him after seeing his actions.

“Calm down. It’s just a mere Demon General. I can deal with trash like this with my eyes closed.” Yuan laughed out loud.

The demon trembled in anger after being humiliated by Yuan in such a fashion. “How dare a mere livestock mock me… a great Demon General?!”

Veins appeared all over the demon’s face and body.

“UNFORGIVABLE!”

The demon’s aura erupted with intense bloodlust.

Feeling the demon’s overwhelming bloodlust and the terrifying pressure that accompanied it, Qi Fang and the others trembled uncontrollably. They were so paralyzed with fear that even if given the chance, they wouldn’t have been able to run away.

‘What the hell are you doing?’ Even Liya couldn’t help but question Yuan’s decision to anger the demon, as it would only make things more difficult for him.

“DIE, YOU DAMN LIVESTOCK!”

The demon lunged at Yuan for the second time.

“Too slow!”

Yuan lunged forward as well, flying straight toward the demon. Using nothing but his raw strength, he began pummeling the demon. Each of Yuan’s punches was powerful enough to blow a hole through the demon’s body.

“Hahaha! What’s the matter, you fucking trash?! Is this all your anger amounts to?! You’re too weak! No wonder why you were sealed by the Demon Sealing Clan!” Yuan relentlessly provoked the demon during its beatdown.

Qi Fang and the others were left speechless by Yuan’s sudden change in behavior, feeling as though he had become a completely different person.

“AAAAAH!” The demon let out a crazed roar, its entire being radiating with unfathomable anger as its body suddenly began to transform.

chapters.title: Chapter 1628 Second Sealed Demon
chapters.body:
Chapter 1628 Second Sealed Demon

Chapter 1628 Second Sealed Demon

“Damn livestock! You will regret mocking me!”

The Demon General suddenly grew in size until it was three times its original height, its muscles bulging as if they were on the brink of bursting, and two bat-like wings sprouted from its back.

“So you grew a little bigger and a lot uglier. I don’t see how that will save you.” Yuan commented on the demon’s transformation.

Sure enough, even after its transformation, the Demon General couldn’t touch a single hair on Yuan’s body, much less defeat him.

‘This bastard has grown so much since our last spar, and it’s only been a year…’ Liya narrowed her eyes at Yuan. She had always been proud of her own talents and strength, but before Yuan, she felt as insignificant as an ant.

‘Is this the prowess of the Immortal Monarch? I guess he didn’t command the Nine Divine Supremes through sheer luck…’

“Impossible… How is he overwhelming a Demon General with the strength of a Spirit Sovereign at his cultivation? It doesn’t make any sense!” Qi Fang muttered with a dazed expression.

“He’s not even using any Demon Sealing Aura, either! Are you telling me that he’s overwhelming the demon with brute force?! No wonder why he’s so confident!” Qi Man cried out loud.

“Even if he’s confident and everything seems fine for now, why is he messing around? Demons aren’t playthings, and things can go wrong in the blink of an eye!” Qi Fang anxiously clenched her fist.

“Fuck! Fuck! Fuck! Where did a human like you come from?!” The Demon General no longer wanted to fight Yuan and began preparing to run away.

“Even if the entire world knew my name, I guess it’s not possible for everyone to recognize my face, huh?” Yuan muttered in a low voice.

While there was not a single demon who didn’t know of the Divine Paragon, not all of them had the misfortune of encountering him, so it wasn’t shocking that this Demon General didn’t recognize Yuan.

“I guess it’s about time we finish this.”

Yuan suddenly surged with Demon Sealing Aura. The moment the Demon General sensed this aura, its entire being trembled with fear. Although demons typically don’t fear Demon Sealing Aura, even though it is their greatest weakness, Yuan’s Demon Sealing Aura was on an entirely different level—one that made the demon feel completely powerless, as if surrounded by the abyss.

“No matter how many times I see it, his Demon Sealing Aura always humbles me…” Qi Man muttered, completely in awe.

The Demon General didn’t dare to confront Yuan’s Demon Sealing Aura and instantly ran away.

“Where the hell do you think you’re going?”

Yuan caught up with the demon in no time and shoved his hand into the demon’s body before pulling out of its Demon Core.

“T-this technique! You can’t be the Divine—”

The Demon General perished before it could even finish its sentence.

Yuan quickly tossed the Demon Core into his pocket and turned to look at the Qi Family.

“Bring me to the next demon,” he said to them.

Once they snapped out of their daze, the Qi Family escorted Yuan to the next sealed demon several hours away.

Yuan frowned as he looked at the sealed demon. Unlike the usual human-shaped statue, this one had no particular shape and resembled a small mountain.

“We mistook this as an ordinary boulder at first, but we examined it a little closer since it seemed out of place, and sure enough, we could sense a sealed demon inside.” Qi Fang explained.

“Yuan…?” Qi Fang raised an eyebrow upon noticing a surprised expression on Yuan’s face. “This isn’t an ordinary demon…” he spoke slowly.

“This is a special demon called an Abomination! They’re essentially mutants even within the Demon Clan.”

“Abomination? We have never heard of them before. How strong are they?” Qi Man asked.

“They’re not only much harder to kill but also stronger than the average demon, and judging by its energy… it’s as strong as a Divine Warrior.”

“What?! Divine Warrior?! That an entirely different realm!” Qi Fang exclaimed in a shocked tone.

“There’s no way we can defeat something like that even if we’re given a thousand years!” Qi Man said.

Yuan shook his head and said, “I can still deal with it, but I will need some time to prepare.”

‘Yu Ning, can you set up a barrier around this area so I don’t completely destroy this place?’ Yuan asked.

“Of course, Young Master.”

Once Yuan transferred control to Yu Ning, his cultivation temporarily soared to the peak of Spirit Enlightenment.

‘I guess the peak of Spirit Enlightenment is my new limit, huh?’ Yuan thought to himself.

Sometime later, Yu Ning returned the control to Yuan after setting up the formation.

“I am finished, Young Master. As long as they’re not stronger than a Divine Grandmaster, no attacks will be able to break this formation.”

“Thanks.”

He turned to look at the Qi Family and said, “You guys should stand further back.”

“Are you sure you want to unseal it now? Why don’t you come back a few years later—when your cultivation is much higher? The seal is strong enough to keep that demon sealed for another decade, so you have plenty of time.” Qi Man suggested.

Yuan looked at him and said, “You should know by now that one’s cultivation isn’t the most important factor when dealing with demons. It’s our Demon Sealing Aura that matters, and it doesn’t require spiritual energy to be strengthened.”

He stopped paying attention to the Qi Family and focused on the sealed Abomination.

Once he was prepared, Yuan broke the seal and waited for the Abomination to emerge.

The earth trembled as the Abomination began breaking free from its seal. Cracks rapidly spread across the boulder before it suddenly exploded a few seconds later, revealing the creature’s ugly and menacing form.

“T-that is an Abomination?!” Qi Fang and the others wore expressions of fear and disgust upon seeing its grotesque appearance. Even though Yuan had warned them about its hideous appearance, none of them could have anticipated just how horrifying it would be.

chapters.title: Chapter 1629 The Divine Fox Of Grand Prophecy’S Divination
chapters.body:
Chapter 1629 The Divine Fox Of Grand Prophecy’S Divination

Chapter 1629 The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy’s Divination

The second the Abomination broke out of its seal, Yuan activated Swords of Eternal Torment. Once the Abomination’s massive body was covered in swords, immobilizing it, Yuan instantly transformed. His hair and eyes turned a majestic gold, and his aura erupted like a volcano, radiating immense power.

“T-that’s?!” Liya’s eyes widened with shock after seeing Yuan’s dragon transformation for the first time.

‘Not only does he know Dragon’s Gaze, but he can even transform?!’ she stared at Yuan’s handsome face with a dazed expression.

Meanwhile, after his transformation, Yuan retrieved Number One Under Heaven and prepared to launch Heaven Splitting Sword Strike.

He had to control its power so that it didn’t exceed the strength of a Divine Grandmaster and accidentally destroy the world, but he also had to ensure it was powerful enough to completely obliterate the Abomination in one strike.

After taking a moment to adjust and gather spiritual energy, Yuan swung his sword, launching a beam of sword light that enveloped the Abomination’s figure.

Fortunately for them, the Abomination wasn’t as massive as the one Yuan had faced during the Elite Demon Sealer Examination.

The moment Yuan’s Heaven Splitting Sword Strike hit the formation, it triggered a massive earthquake powerful enough to be felt across the entire world.

Cracks spread across the formation, yet it somehow managed to hold together, clinging to a sliver of strength.

When the dust settled and the Qi Family could finally see the situation, they were bewildered to find that Yuan was standing alone and the Abomination had vanished entirely.

“Heavens! He killed a demon with the strength of a Divine Warrior while only being a Spirit Lord!” Qi Man cried out loud, his entire being trembling in awe.

“How on earth is he doing that? Ignoring his Demon Sealing Aura, his strength doesn’t match his cultivation! Is he hiding his true cultivation? It doesn’t make any sense!” Qi Fang shook her head.

Once the Abomination was defeated, it left behind its Demon Core.

Yuan’s eyes widened with surprise when he saw how different it was from an ordinary Demon Core. It wasn’t just the size of a watermelon but also pitch black in color.

“This is a Demon Core…?” Yuan mumbled as he picked up the core.

He was also a bit disappointed, realizing it would be impossible for him to consume a Demon Core of this size, as it wouldn’t even fit in his mouth.

The Qi Family approached him once they determined it was safe to do so.

“W-what the heck is that?” Qi Fang asked as she pointed at the Demon Core.

“The Abomination’s Demon Core. This is also my first time seeing something like this,” he said.

Qi Man shivered at its sight and said, “It feels very ominous… What are you going to do with it?”

“I don’t know if we can even do anything with it.”

Qi Man then said, “In that case, would you mind allowing our family to research it? Of course, we’re not asking you to give it to us. It’ll still be your property.”

Yuan nodded, “Sure. Let me know if you learn anything.”

He handed the Demon Core to Qi Man.

“S-so heavy!” Qi Man nearly dropped the Demon Core once it was in his grasp.

The Demon Core was deceptively heavy and weighed several tons.

“Oh, sorry. It was almost weightless for me, so I forgot to tell you.” Yuan said with an apologetic smile.

“It’s okay…” Qi Man quickly moved the Demon Core back to their private jet.

Sometime later, they returned to the Qi Family to celebrate the demon’s extermination with a big feast.

“Yuan, can I bother you to check on our disciples’ progress before you leave? It’s been some time since their lecture with you, after all.” Qi Huan asked him.

“Of course.”

During the feast, the disciples put on a performance to showcase their Demon Sealing Aura.

“What do you think, Yuan? Have they improved a little?”

He nodded, “Yes, they’ve improved, but there’s still a lot of work to be done. I would give them another lecture, but I have to return to Cultivation Online by the end of the day. Next time, surely.”

“We’ll be waiting for your return.” After the feast, Liya escorted Yuan back to the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

During their travel, she questioned him, “How did you use dragon transformation? Only those with a dragon bloodline could possibly do such a thing.”

“I was able to do that after performing the Dragon Cleansing Ceremony.” “The Dragon Cleansing Ceremony?!” Liya was deeply shocked to learn this, finding it unbelievable that dragons, who were famously strict about their traditions and bloodlines, would permit a human to partake in one of their most sacred rituals, especially those of royal bloodlines.

‘Which royal bloodline would be foolish enough to do such a thing?’ she sighed inwardly.

Once they returned to the Dragon Spiral Mountain, Yuan spoke with the Lord, recounting everything that had transpired.

“An Abomination, huh… That thing would’ve spelled the end of this world if you weren’t here,” he sighed.

“As long as I am here, no demon will cause havoc on Earth ever again. Notify me again if they find any more demons.”

“Of course.”

At the end of the day, Yuan returned to Cultivation Online. “Welcome back. You’re just in time for my divination.” The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy had just finished her preparation.

“Now, witness my divination!”

The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy was suddenly enveloped in a golden aura that expanded outward until it covered the entire Mystic Valley.

“Grand Prophecy, show me the location of the Primal Monarch!” she roared.

A complex and majestic magic circle materialized beneath the Divine Fox, radiating an unfathomable aura. The Divine Fox’s eyes glowed with a golden light that shone like a flashlight, and intricate symbols began to form within her eyes.

The Divine Fox remained in this state for several moments until she suddenly coughed up a mouthful of blood.

“T-that is impossible…” she muttered in disbelief.

“What happened? Did you fail?” Yuan asked with a slight frown, wondering if something went wrong with the divination.

“No… the divination succeeded, and I have the Primal Monarch’s location. But the Grand Prophecy revealed that its location is… here! In the Mystic Valley!” she exclaimed, her voice filled with terror.

“What?” Yuan’s eyes widened with shock upon hearing this.

chapters.title: Chapter 1630 Primal Monarch
chapters.body:
Chapter 1630 Primal Monarch

Chapter 1630 Primal Monarch

“What did you say? The Primal Monarch is located somewhere in the Mystic Valley? Are you sure you divined properly?” Yuan looked at the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy.

“Y-you! Are you mocking me?! I have never failed a divination! I am sure of it! The Primal Monarch is in the Mystic Valley!” The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy said as she frantically looked at her surroundings with a nervous and fearful expression.

The Primal Monarch was an entity that stood at the very pinnacle of the Primal Expanse. If there were Primals and Predators within the Primal Expanse, the Primal Monarch would be the Apex Predator, existing in a league of its own.

Knowing that the Primal Monarch was in her territory, the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy didn’t dare to make any significant movements, remaining as still as a statue.

Yuan looked at Yingzi and noticed that she, too, appeared quite nervous. It was the first time he had ever seen such emotions from her.

‘This can’t be a coincidence…’ Yuan thought to himself.

He recalled something the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy said.

“He will only appear before you if he chooses to do so, huh?” he mumbled.

Several moments passed in silence, with nothing happening.

After waiting a little longer without any changes, Yuan decided to make the first move.

“Primal Monarch! You can hear me, right?! In fact, I bet you’re looking at me right now!” he shouted with the support of his spiritual energy, allowing his voice to ripple throughout the entire Mystic Valley.

The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy and Yingzi turned to at him with terrified expressions on their faces.

“W-what the hell are you doing?! Do you have a death wish?!” The Divine Fox shouted at him.

Yuan ignored her and continued, “I doubt you’re here by chance! Are you here for me? If so, why don’t you show yourself already?”

“Yuan…” Yingzi called out to him in a nervous tone. If the Primal Monarch wanted to kill Yuan, even she was powerless to do anything about it.

“Don’t tell me you want to play hide-and-seek with me?! I don’t have the time for that!”

As Yuan’s voice faded and silence returned, a faint rustling noise suddenly broke the stillness.

Yuan and the others immediately turned their heads toward the source of the noise, and a small silhouette slowly emerged from the tall bushes.

“You are…?” Yuan’s eyes widened with shock when he saw the appearance—the face of this individual. freewёbnoνel.com

Once the silhouette fully emerged from the bushes, it became clear that it was a child—a young boy. He had short black hair and innocent eyes that held a sharp and powerful gaze. Like Yingzi, he had an uncanny absence of presence, despite standing right in front of them.

Yuan was in disbelief, not because this individual had the appearance of a child, but because the child’s face was identical to his own when he was younger. It was as if he were staring at a mirror image of his past self.

The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy and Yingzi also noticed this obvious detail. If they hadn’t known any better, they would’ve mistaken this child for Yuan’s son.

“You… you are the Primal Monarch?” Yuan asked.

After a moment of silence, instead of answering his question, the child said, “Who are you? And why do you have that appearance?”

The atmosphere in the Mystic Valley shifted abruptly. Sensing this change, both the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy and Yingzi began to sweat profusely.

“What do you mean? I have always looked like this. In fact, I should be the one asking you that. Why do you look like my childhood self?” Yuan responded.

The child narrowed his eyes.

“What is your name? And how are you related to Tian Yi?”

Yuan’s eyes widened with surprise after hearing this familiar name.

‘He knows the Immortal Monarch?!’

“I am Yuan. As for my relationship with Tian Yi, why are you asking? Who is he to you?”

“I am the one asking asking questions here, brat.”

A domineering pressure suddenly surrounded Yuan, forcing him to his knees.

“!!!”

The force was so powerful that Yuan couldn’t fight it even for a split second.

“You kneel!” Yuan immediately activated Immortal Monarch’s Dominion on the child.

“…”

However, the child remained standing, seemingly unaffected by the Immortal Monarch’s Dominion.

Yuan’s eyes widened after seeing the results—or the lack of. He was certain that the child before him was a beast, so this made it the first time his Immortal Monarch’s Dominion failed on a beast.

“As I thought, you were the one who used that power earlier.” The child muttered, clearly unfazed by it.

The pressure on Yuan suddenly disappeared.

“Are you that brat’s descendant? Is Tian Yi still alive?” The child asked.

“What…?” Yuan muttered, utterly confused by the situation.

Despite the strange situation, Yuan couldn’t sense any killing intent from the child. Furthermore, the child’s tone when speaking of Tian Yi was akin to that of a father speaking to his child.

‘I guess it’s safe to assume that he’s not my enemy.’

“I’m not Tian Yi’s descendant,” Yuan spoke as he got back to his feet.

“I am—was Tian Yi in my past life.”

“What?” The child’s eyes widened with surprise after hearing this.

“Bullshit. Then why don’t you recognize me?” He shrugged, “I reincarnated not long ago and am still recovering my memories. Unfortunately, my memories of Tian Yi’s childhood are still missing.”

“Anyway, you’re the Primal Monarch, right? Since you know Tian Yi, I must have traveled to this place in the past.”

“So you really don’t remember anything, huh?”

After sighing, the child spoke, “Indeed, I am the Primal Monarch.”

“You asked about my relationship with Tian Yi—you, right? I am your father—.”

“You’re my father?!” Yuan cried out loud at this revelation. “So I wasn’t even human?!”

“Let me finish, you brat. I am not your real father. You somehow wandered into our world when you were still a child, and I took you in.”

“Is that so…?” Yuan swallowed nervously.

chapters.title: Chapter 1631 Retrieving Tian Yi’S Memories
chapters.body:
Chapter 1631 Retrieving Tian Yi’S Memories

Chapter 1631 Retrieving Tian Yi’s Memories

‘W-what the hell is going on here? The Primal Monarch is that human’s adoptive father?’ The Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy was bewildered by this information. She had countless questions, but she didn’t dare to ask them.

“Since you don’t remember me, I guess it’s safe to assume you don’t remember our promise. Did you wander to the Primal Expanse again by accident, too?” The Primal Monarch looked at Yuan.

He nodded, “Yes, I came here by accident. I wanted to look for you because I was told you could take me out of this place. How long have you been watching me?”

The Primal Monarch sighed, “Since you used my power at the Phoenixes’ territory.”

“Your power…? You mean Immortal Monarch’s Dominion?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“If that’s what you call it, sure. However, that power—the ability to command Primals and Predators—was originally mine, to begin with.” The Primal Monarch revealed.

Yuan wasn’t too surprised to learn that the Immortal Monarch had inherited his power from another source, as it seemed unlikely for a human to naturally possess such control over beasts in the first place.

The Primal Monarch suddenly gestured to Yuan and said, “Let me see the kind of life you’ve lived after leaving the Primal Expanse.”

“Huh? You want to see my memories? But I don’t have all of them.”

“That doesn’t mean anything to me. While you don’t remember it now, they’re definitely somewhere in there, so all I need to do is take it out from hiding.”

“You can do that…?” Yuan didn’t think of this until it was mentioned.

“Does it have any side effects?”

He was worried that it might affect his fate.

‘No… my appearance in the Primal Expanse isn’t a coincidence or an accident. It was fate that brought me here, so the Primal Monarch retrieving my memories should also be considered fate.’

“If I was an inexperienced brat like yourself, you might lose all of your memories and become a vegetable.” The Primal Monarch calmly said.

“But you’re not… right?” Yuan swallowed nervously.

“You really don’t change, huh?” A subtle smile appeared on the Primal Monarch’s face.

The next moment, he pointed two fingers at Yuan’s forehead before shooting a warm beam of light directly into his head.

Primal Monarch raised an eyebrow the following moment as he thought inwardly, ‘This brat reincarnated more than once…’

Despite his interest and curiosity, the Primal Monarch didn’t touch the memories that didn’t belong to Tian Yi.

After retrieving Tian Yi’s memories, the Primal Monarch observed his entire life after leaving the Primal Expanse.

Sometime later, the Primal Monarch lowered his hand and said, “I understand the situation. Although I was able to retrieve most of Tian Yi’s memories, I was unable to retrieve the final moments of his life due to some interference. How do you feel?”

“I remember now…” Yuan muttered in a somewhat dazed voice.

After the Primal Monarch pulled out Tian Yi’s memories, he remembered everything from his childhood.

Tian Yi was born into a wealthy and influential family. However, he lacked the talents necessary to survive there. In fact, he didn’t have any cultivation talents, which was a complete surprise to Yuan since the Immortal Monarch was the fourth reincarnation, so he should’ve been a genius like his previous incarnations.

Due to his lack of talent, Tian Yi was abandoned by his parents and exiled from the family at the age of six, leaving him to wander the streets as a foundling. However, that life didn’t last long, as his family didn’t want him to stay in the same city, so they forced him out into the wilderness, leaving him to fend for himself.

Shortly after Tian Yi entered the wilderness, he encountered a powerful magical beast that chased him to a nearby Endless Beast Den. At that moment, two cultivators were locked in a fierce battle, causing the destruction of the Endless Beast Den.

The destruction of the Endless Beast Den created a rift, and in his desperation, Tian Yi entered it, not caring where it might lead him.

Once he came out on the other end, Tian Yi found himself in an unfamiliar world that reeked of wild beasts and blood.

“Where am I…?” Tian Yi muttered as he frantically looked around.

Suddenly, a rough voice resounded, “I came here as soon as I noticed the scent of a human, but what is this? A human this small… You won’t even be able to fill the gap between my teeth!”

Tian Yi turned to the source of the voice and was immediately scared shitless when he saw a massive black dragon hovering in the sky.

“This human is mine!” Another voice suddenly resounded, followed by the appearance of a Phoenix with fiery purple flames.

“You dare covet my prey?! I’ll tear you to shreds!” The black Dragon roared.

“Not before I roast you for dinner!” The purple Phoenix laughed arrogantly.

Their aura fiercely crashed with each other. Unable to bear the pressure, Tian Yi fainted instantly.

Tian Yi didn’t know how long he had been asleep for, but by the time he woke up, neither the Phoenix nor Dragon could be seen, almost as if they’d vanished magically.

“This is the first time I’ve seen a human like you.”

An unfamiliar but calm voice suddenly resounded.

Jumpscared by the voice, Tian Yi jumped to his feet and began running away without looking at the source of the voice.

“Where do you think you are going?”

The voice that was far away from Tian Yi a moment ago suddenly sounded like it was right behind him.

Tian Yi swiftly realized that running wasn’t an option and surrendered to his fate.

He stopped running and fell to his knees.

“Go ahead and eat me. I don’t care anymore.”

“Why would I eat you? Even a thousand of you wouldn’t fill half my stomach. Eating you would only arouse more hunger.” The voice laughed.

At this moment, Tian Yi turned around to face the voice, and to his bewilderment, the voice belonged to a child who didn’t look any older than himself.

As a matter of fact, this child didn’t simply look to be the same age. He looked exactly like Tian Yi, as if he was a perfect clone.

chapters.title: Chapter 1632 Tempering In Blood
chapters.body:
Chapter 1632 Tempering In Blood

Chapter 1632 Tempering in Blood

“W-who are you?! Why do you look like me?!” Tian Yi was terrified when he realized that the face before him was actually his own face.

“I like it, so I decided to use it.”

“You decided to use it…? You’re not human, are you?”

“Humans don’t exist in the Primal Expanse. Other than you, who came here from another world, everyone here is what you call beasts.”

“Another world?” Tian Yi couldn’t comprehend this information and became dazed.

Seeing his dumbfounded expression, the entity with his face said, “I am the Primal Monarch, and you are currently in my world, the Primal Expanse.”

He continued, “What are you going to do now? Humans cannot survive in this world alone, and you’re not even a cultivator. The moment I leave you alone, you’ll become someone’s food or toy until you die.”

Tian Yi fell silent.

A moment later, he spoke in a defeated tone, “My family abandoned me because I was born without talents, and I somehow ended up in this dangerous place. I think this is Heaven’s way of telling me to just die. I am just a powerless mortal. Who am I to go against Heaven’s will? No matter what happens to me, I will accept it.”

The Primal Monarch smiled and said, “You’re an interesting human. Despite your young age, you’re surprisingly clear-headed and intelligent.”

“This Heaven you speak of doesn’t exist here. No, to be more precise, I am Heaven here!” The Primal Monarch boldly declared.

“I don’t care how mighty your Heaven is; it won’t influence my territory. So if you want to survive, I can make that a reality.”

Tian Yi stared at the Primal Monarch with wide eyes and spoke in a dazed manner, “Even if you say that, how am I supposed to survive in this place?”

The Primal Monarch showed a wide smile and said, “Humans have never managed to pique my interest, but I think it’ll be fun to have you around for some time.”

“You want me to follow you?” Tian Yi asked.

“You don’t want to?”

“I’ll do it!” Tian Yi responded without any hint of fear in his voice. This was the first time he felt accepted and needed, so he had no reason to refuse, even if that individual was not human.

“Good.”

After the Primal Monarch accepted Tian Yi as his follower, he escorted Tian Yi back to his home, which existed in a different dimension. There, the Primal Monarch taught Tian Yi everything he needed to know about the Primal Expanse and the entities living there.

‘This brat truly has a bright mind. His ability to learn and comprehend things is almost frightening. Even Predators who have lived for tens of thousands of years wouldn’t be able to match him.’ The Primal Monarch quickly realized that the notion of Tian Yi being talentless was completely false.

‘To throw away such talents, humans are such foolish creatures.’

In the blink of an eye, two years had passed since Tian Yi began studying under the Primal Monarch.

“Listen here, you brat. If you want to live more than a hundred years, you’ll need to start cultivating. However, it’s impossible for humans to cultivate properly in the Primal Expanse since your bodies cannot adapt to our environment. If they forcefully cultivate our Chaotic Essence, they will turn into mindless beasts.”

“In that case, isn’t it hopeless for me?” Tian Yi spoke calmly, almost as if it didn’t matter to him whether he could cultivate or not.

“A hundred years is already a long time—enough to live a fulfilled life.”

“That’s what someone who’s never truly experienced life would say. Anyway, the reason humans are unable to cultivate Chaotic Essence is because they don’t have a Chaotic Heart, something all beasts in the Primal Expanse are born with.”

“In that case, what will happen if we give them a Chaotic Heart?” The Primal Monarch spoke with a profound smile as he glared at Tian Yi, who swallowed nervously.

“Is it even possible for a human to acquire a Chaotic Heart?” Tian Yi raised an eyebrow.

“We’ll find out soon enough.”

“I guess so.”

Tian Yi understood that he was the subject of an experiment that had never been done before, fully aware that things could go disastrously wrong, yet he remained indifferent.

The Primal Monarch went to the Primal Expanse to prepare for Tian Yi’s experiment.

He returned a few hours later and prepared a bath filled to the brim with the blood of various beasts.

“First and foremost, we need to strengthen your frail body. This will be incredibly painful, unlike anything you’ve ever experienced before, so brace yourself.”

However, to the Primal Monarch’s surprise, Tian Yi neither screamed nor showed any signs of pain during the tempering from the beginning to the very end, lasting for six hours straight.

“How do you feel?” The Primal Monarch asked Tian Yi as he emerged from the bath.

“Feels like my body is surging with strength,” he said.

“Did you not experience any pain during the tempering?”

“I did, and it hurt like hell.” Tian Yi calmly replied.

“…”

The Primal Monarch gazed at the blood bath and thought to himself, “I never could have imagined he’d endure six hours on his first tempering. How interesting!”

He began to feel that he had made the right choice in taking Tian Yi under his wing. Over the next four years, Tian Yi continued to bathe in the blood that the Primal Monarch provided. The pain he endured grew more intense as the quality and potency of the blood increased.

“Your body should be strong enough to grow a Chaotic Heart now.”

The Primal Monarch retrieved a mountain of treasures and continued, “Although we are born with a Chaotic Heart, it isn’t complete at birth and requires tremendous effort to complete it. Once completed, Predators can further grow a second or even third Chaotic Heart by consuming large amounts of treasures. Let’s see if you can grow a Chaotic Heart by consuming treasures.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1633 Primal Monarch’S Bloodline
chapters.body:
Chapter 1633 Primal Monarch’S Bloodline

Chapter 1633 Primal Monarch’s Bloodline

After his body tempering period ended, Tian Yi began consuming an enormous amount of treasures provided by the Primal Monarch to cultivate a Chaotic Heart. He was akin to a pig, but instead of eating pig food, he was being fed priceless treasures.

However, despite the vast number of treasures he consumed, there were no signs of his Chaotic Heart forming.

Despite this, Tian Yi’s cultivation soared, reaching the level of Divine Ancestor in just four years solely by consuming treasures. He achieved this without needing to cultivate or learn any cultivation techniques, as the Primal Monarch assisted him in absorbing and containing all that energy.

“Hmmm… your Chaotic Heart isn’t forming… We might be missing something.” The Primal Monarch eventually halted feeding him treasures.

“Maybe it’s because I lack a proper bloodline,” Tian Yi casually guessed.

“Bloodline, huh? That might be it…” The Primal Monarch fell silent for a few moments, a contemplative expression on his face.

“I have never done or even thought about doing this before, but I can give you some of my Blood Essence, which has a chance of giving you my bloodline.” The Primal Monarch muttered with a curious face.

Even in the Divine Heaven, humans can acquire bloodlines through the Blood Essence of Magical Beasts or Divine Beasts. However, since the creation of Blood Essence requires tremendous effort and even involves sacrificing one’s longevity, they are only created for special occasions and are incredibly rare, even in the world of beasts.

“Once you accept my Blood Essence, we’ll essentially be family. What do you think? Are you willing to accept the bloodline of a beast?” The Primal Monarch asked Tian Yi.

Tian Yi smiled and said, “I already see you as a family and a father, so nothing’s changed.”

The Primal Monarch laughed out loud, finding his answer extremely entertaining.

“Very well! Then, give me some time to form my Blood Essence. Since this is my first time doing something like this, it will take a few years to complete.”

“A few years? What should I do in the meantime?” Tian Yi asked.

“I’ll teach you a few of my techniques, so you can use this time to train them.”

The Primal Monarch transferred several of his own techniques to Tian Yi directly through his mind, allowing him to understand them instantly. However, he still needed to train to deepen his understanding and strengthen the techniques.

Afterward, the Primal Monarch entered seclusion to form his Blood Essence, leaving Tian Yi alone for the next decade.

In these ten years, Tian Yi focused on training the techniques bestowed by the Primal Monarch, occasionally tempering his body and consuming the leftover treasures. His cultivation reached the peak of Immortal Ascension without any issues.

When the Primal Monarch returned with his Blood Essence, he didn’t immediately feed it to Tian Yi, as he feared that Tian Yi was too weak to absorb his Blood Essence.

“Let’s increase your cultivation a bit more to be safe.”

Thus, he fed Tian Yi even more treasures.

After a few more years, Tian Yi broke through Immortal Ascension and became a true Immortal at the Bronze Immortal realm.

Normally, a cultivator would need to face a Heavenly Tribulation before they can ascend past Immortal Ascension and become an Immortal, but since Tian Yi was located in Primal Expanse, where the ‘Heavens’ had no influence, he was able to break through that barrier without facing one.

“So, I just consume this like water?” Tian Yi inquired with the Primal Monarch’s Blood Essence in his grasp.

“Yes, pour it onto your tongue and let it be absorbed.”

Tian Yi followed the Primal Monarch’s instructions and placed a single drop of Blood Essence onto his tongue.

The Blood Essence melted into his tongue the instant it touched. At first, he felt no change, but after a few moments, his entire body began to burn up, as if his blood were boiling.

The Primal Monarch watched with great focus, paying attention to every little detail and change occurring within Tian Yi’s body.

Veins bulged across Tian Yi’s body, as if they were on the verge of bursting through his skin. Soon, they began to glow with a golden hue, as if liquid gold were coursing through them.

“AHHHHH!”

Tian Yi let out a beastly roar that shook the entire world. Moments later, he began to calm down, and his body quickly started to recover. ƒreewebɳovel.com

“How do you feel now?” The Primal Monarch asked him.

Even before Tian Yi’s response, the Primal Monarch could tell from Tian Yi’s aura that he had successfully absorbed the Blood Essence.

“I feel… the same?” Tian Yi muttered in a somewhat puzzled voice.

Although he didn’t realize it, his voice carried an unfathomable power capable of commanding all beasts if there were any near him. Of course, it wouldn’t work on the Primal Monarch, who gave him the power.

“You may not feel it now, but you’ll realize it sooner or later,” said the Primal Monarch.

“As long as you learn to control that power, you’ll be invincible within the Primal Expanse, even as a mortal.”

“However, great power comes with great responsibilities, and I trust that you won’t abuse this power.” Tian Yi smiled and said, “I won’t betray that trust, but if I ever do, you can kill me.”

“Kill you? Hmph! I’ll make you suffer a fate worse than death.” The Primal Monarch sneered.

After acquiring his new powers, Tian Yi spent several more years studying and practicing to master control over them.

During this time, Tian Yi also successfully formed his Chaotic Heart, albeit an incomplete one.

Once the Primal Monarch was confident Tian Yi could survive by himself, he was released into the Primal Expanse for experience.

“What should I do in the Primal Expanse besides completing my Chaotic Heart?” Tian Yi asked the Primal Monarch before leaving for the Primal Expanse.

“How should I know? This is meant to expand your experience, so live however you want for the next hundred years. Also, you will be hunted everywhere you go because you’re a human, and your scent will drive everyone mad, so try to stay alive. I don’t want all of my efforts to go to waste.”

“I will try not to disappoint you.” Tian Yi left for the Primal Expanse with an excited smile on his face.

chapters.title: Chapter 1634 Tian Yi’S Growth
chapters.body:
Chapter 1634 Tian Yi’S Growth

Chapter 1634 Tian Yi’s Growth

After Tian Yi left home to gain experience in the Primal Expanse, the Primal Monarch secretly followed him, having nothing better to do. Of course, since this was Tian Yi’s first time exploring alone, he also felt a little worried for him.

However, the Primal Monarch’s worries were quickly relieved once he saw how quickly Tian Yi adapted to the Primal Expanse.

While his cultivation as a Bronze Immortal was respectable in the Primal Expanse, it was far from sufficient for him to be reckless and careless. Tian Yi didn’t have any big ambitions and wanted to complete his Chaotic Heart as soon as possible, which he set as his primary goal.

He traversed the Primal Expanse in search of treasures, dominating nearly every territory he encountered.

Tian Yi’s presence quickly spread throughout the Primal Expanse like wildfire, and within a few years, nearly every powerhouse there had heard of the human causing havoc in their territories.

“A human who can cultivate and use his powers? Bullshit! There’s no way such a human exists!”

“What? This human has a Chaotic Heart? Are you sure he’s really human and not just a Predator in disguise?”

Most of the powerhouses refused to believe that a human could create such chaos in their world, especially when every human before him had been unable to adapt to their Chaotic Essence.

However, some had their interest piqued and left their territories to seek out this human they had been hearing so much about.

After another decade, it was all but confirmed that Tian Yi was real, and he was growing at such a tremendous rate that he could pose a threat to even the powerhouses in the Primal Expanse if given a few more decades.

Tian Yi quickly became a presence that could not be ignored, prompting Predators across the Primal Expanse to attempt to either tame or kill him. However, every failed attempt only made Tian Yi stronger. Eventually, Tian Yi became so powerful that it took several powerhouses just to face him on equal footing.

“Fuck! Where’s the Primal Monarch?! Why isn’t he doing anything about this human when his world is being flipped upside down?!”

The natives of the Primal Expanse were puzzled by the Primal Monarch’s silence. Normally, whenever a situation threatened the balance of the world or someone caused excessive chaos, the Primal Monarch would intervene.

Yet, he was nowhere to be seen. This made some wonder if something had happened to the Primal Monarch. However, after a hundred years had passed since Tian Yi first appeared in the Primal Expanse, he vanished as suddenly as he had arrived.

Unbeknownst to the natives, Tian Yi had returned to the Primal Monarch’s home.

“How was your experience in the outside world?” the Primal Monarch asked upon Tian Yi’s return, pretending as if he hadn’t been watching him the entire time.

“It was a little scary at first with the amount of beasts trying to kill me, but once I got used to it, it was pretty fun.” Tian Yi laughed out loud.

“Is that so? Let’s see how many Chaotic Hearts you’ve managed to form in the last 100 years…”

After a quick examination, the Primal Monarch was surprised to sense four Chaotic Hearts within Tian Yi’s body.

“Four Chaotic Hearts in a hundred years, huh? Not bad, considering that even the strongest Predators in the Primal Expanse only have six after millions of years.” Not only did Tian Yi acquire 4 Chaotic Hearts, but his cultivation even reached Golden Immortal in a hundred years.

“What should I do now? I have done pretty much everything there is to do in this world—not that there is much to do in the first place.”

“Well, do you have any interest in owning your own territory?” The Primal Monarch asked.

“Not really. I just don’t see the appeal of owning a territory.”

“Unfortunately, there isn’t much for you to do in this world. In the first place, the Primal Expanse wasn’t created for humans. As for the rest of us, we live to increase our strength and expand our territories. We thrive off growth and preying on the weak.”

“If there’s nothing to do, I can simply create my own entertainment. For example, I can bring human entertainment into this world.” Tian Yi suggested.

However, the Primal Monarch shook his head and said, “That won’t work. What you humans find entertaining is meaningless to us. Just as owning territories holds no appeal for you, we find human culture dull and uninteresting.”

Tian Yi sighed, “This is why I didn’t mind remaining as a mortal and living only a hundred years.”

The Primal Monarch fell silent for several moments before asking, “Have you ever thought about returning to your home—the human world?”

Tian Yi looked at the sky and responded in an oddly calm tone, “I have. Many times, in fact. However, even if I were to go back, I wouldn’t know what to do. Furthermore…”

He looked at the Primal Monarch directly in the eyes and continued, “Although I was born in the human world, I grew up in this world, and my family is also here, so this world is my home.”

The Primal Monarch laughed out loud and said, “That’s heartwarming and all, but you don’t belong here. Now that you’re all grown up and have the power to survive without my help, I think you should go back to the human world.”

“You seem eager to send me away. Why?” Tian Yi noticed this and inquired with a deep frown, feeling like he was being abandoned by his family for a second time.

The Primal Monarch shook his head and said, “This world is more complex than it appears. Also, there’s nothing left for me to teach you, and unless you want to die of boredom in this world or spend the rest of your life fighting the natives of this world, it only makes sense for you to return to your original world.”

“In the first place, I never expected you to remain in this world by my side forever. I took you in and helped you grow so that you could one day return to your world and become living proof that this ‘Heaven’ isn’t omnipotent and its will could be defied.”

“…”

Tian Yi was speechless, unsure of how to respond.

chapters.title: Chapter 1635 Primal Monarch’S Blood Essence
chapters.body:
Chapter 1635 Primal Monarch’S Blood Essence

Chapter 1635 Primal Monarch’s Blood Essence

“Do I… do I really have to leave and return to the human world?” Tian Yi stared at the Primal Monarch with a dispirited expression.

The Primal Monarch smiled and said, “The Primal Expanse isn’t your final stop. You are fated to achieve great things in the human world. I’ll give you a few years to prepare, but you must leave within ten years.”

Tian Yi sighed, “Fine. If you so desperately want me to leave, I won’t argue any further. However, how do I even return to the human world? According to what I’ve heard, it’s impossible to leave the Primal Expanse.”

“There is only one way to leave the Primal Expanse, and that is through me.” The Primal Monarch said.

“Whenever you’re prepared to leave, just let me know.”

Tian Yi nodded.

In the years that followed, Tian Yi sought to learn all he could from the Primal Monarch, yet he soon realized there was nothing more the Primal Monarch could teach him. Despite this, Tian Yi chose not to waste their remaining time together.

In the blink of an eye, nine years passed.

“I am ready to leave.” On a certain day, Tian Yi approached the Primal Monarch to declare his departure.

The Primal Monarch nodded and said, “Before you leave, promise me one thing.”

“What?”

“That you will never submit to anyone, especially to Heaven’s will, ever again and become a being worthy of the Monarch’s bloodline. Also, don’t come back to the Primal Expanse unless you have fully awakened my bloodline.”

“You never told me how to awaken it even though I have asked many times,” Tian Yi frowned.

“Because you didn’t need such knowledge back then.” The Primal Monarch shook his head, and he continued speaking, “Anyway, to fully awaken the Monarch’s bloodline, you will need to absorb the Blood Essence of nine different beasts with a Supreme-grade bloodline.”

“Supreme-grade Blood Essence?! And nine of them?! That’ll take an eternity!” Tian Yi exclaimed.

There are barely a dozen Predators in the entire Primal Expanse who possess a Supreme-grade bloodline, and asking even one of them for their Blood Essence was nearly impossible—let alone nine.

Moreover, he had to accomplish this task in the human world, where the likelihood of finding even nine entities with a Supreme-grade bloodline was slim, if not nonexistent.

“You’re asking for too much… You might as well tell me never to return.” Tian Yi sighed out loud.

“Are you going to give up without even trying? I didn’t know I’d raised such a useless quitter.” The Primal Monarch sneered in disdain.

Tian Yi clenched his fists and gnashed his jaw, “Just wait, you old booger! Not only will I awaken my bloodline, but I’ll even take your position as the Primal Monarch and force you into retirement the next time I come back, so prepare yourself!”

“Hahaha! You’ll take my position? It’s a promise! I cannot wait, you snotty little brat!” Shortly after, the Primal Monarch opened a rift to the Divine Heaven, and Tian Yi stepped into it without looking back.

After digesting Tian Yi’s memories, Yuan looked at the Primal Monarch with emotional eyes.

“Sorry, but I don’t think I can fulfill my promise to you right now…” Yuan spoke with a bittersweet smile.

“Well, this won’t be the first time you’ve disappointed me.” The Primal Monarch shrugged.

He continued, “Not only did you fail to awaken your bloodline, but you’ve managed to weaken it to its weakest state.”

“I’ll awaken the bloodline this time for sure,” Yuan said with a resolute expression on his face.

However, the Primal Monarch shook his head and said, “Even if you consume nine Blood Essence right now, you won’t be able to awaken your Monarch bloodline because it is still in its dormant state.”

“Huh? What do you mean?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“You’ve managed to activate your bloodline, but it’s only Royal-grade, so you’re not receiving any actual benefits from it.”

“Even your Immortal Monarch’s Dominion is just a technique, not your bloodline’s ability.”

“How do I awaken my bloodline then?”

The Primal Monarch suddenly opened a rift and said, “Follow me.”

“Wait a moment.”

Yuan turned to look at Yingzi, “Thank you for everything.”

He retrieved a thousand meat skewers and handed them to Yingzi, who accepted them in a daze.

“You too.” Yuan gave some skewers to the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy before entering the rift with the Primal Monarch.

On the other side of the rift lay the Primal Monarch’s own dimension, the place where Tian Yi had once lived. The world within this dimension was as vast as the Primal Expanse, but only Tian Yi and the Primal Monarch inhabited it at this moment.

Yuan surveyed his surroundings, feeling a sense of nostalgia.

Sometime later, the Primal Monarch spoke, “I knew you would return one day, so I prepared this for you, but I never anticipated you would return in such a state.”

He showed Yuan his palm, where a small bottle rested.

“This is…” Yuan swallowed nervously after seeing the drop of blood inside.

“You made a second Blood Essence?”

Most beings don’t even dare to create Blood Essence, fearing the harm it could cause them. Those brave enough to attempt it would only produce a single drop in their lifetime, as that was their limit. Yet, the Primal Monarch had created two drops.

“If you consume this Blood Essence, your Monarch’s bloodline will awaken its true prowess.”

Yuan accepted the Primal Monarch’s Blood Essence.

“Thank you…”

Yuan tossed the bottle into his spatial ring, causing the Primal Monarch to raise an eyebrow.

“What are you doing? Saving it for later? Why?”

“But I am only a Spirit Sovereign now, and I was an Immortal the last time I consumed your Blood Essence.”

“That’s true, but you also didn’t have the Monarch’s bloodline back then. You already have the Monarch’s bloodline this time, so the requirements won’t be as harsh.”

“Is that so…? Then I’ll consume it now…”

Yuan retrieved the Blood Essence.

chapters.title: Chapter 1636 Awakening Immortal Monarch’S Bloodline
chapters.body:
Chapter 1636 Awakening Immortal Monarch’S Bloodline

Chapter 1636 Awakening Immortal Monarch’s Bloodline

Yuan stared at the Blood Essence in his grasp with a nervous gaze. Despite the Primal Monarch’s assurance that it was safe to consume at his cultivation level, he couldn’t help but feel uneasy.

After all, the Blood Essence belonged to an entity likely far stronger than even the Dragon Ancestor, whose Blood Essence he also possessed and has yet to consume due to his lack of cultivation.

“Are you really scared of a single drop of blood? When did you become such a coward?” The Primal Monarch casually mocked.

“A single drop of blood? Is that how little you view your own Blood Essence?” Yuan shook his head as he opened the bottle.

After taking a deep breath, Yuan tilted his head backward and poured the Blood Essence onto his tongue.

The Blood Essence immediately melted into his tongue.

<You have consumed ‘???’ Blood Essence>

<Immortal Monarch’s Bloodline is reacting to ‘???’ Blood Essence!>

Yuan immediately sat down and began cultivating in silence. He could feel his body burning, almost as if his blood was boiling, but it wasn’t as intense as his first experience as Tian Yi.

<Immortal Monarch’s Bloodline has improved>

<Immortal Monarch’s Bloodline has reached Imperial-grade>

<Immortal Monarch’s Bloodline has improved>

<Immortal Monarch’s Bloodline has reached Divine-grade>

A few days later, Yuan’s bloodline had ascended by two grades. Unlike treasures and techniques, bloodlines had only four known grades, with the vast majority being Royal-grade.

After Royal-grade came Imperial-grade. In the human world, those with Imperial-grade bloodlines were regarded as nobles, hailing from the most elite bloodlines. In the beast world, they were considered royalty.

Above Imperial-grade was Divine-grade. Those with a Divine-grade bloodline were destined to rise above all others, standing as Emperors among their kind. If one searched the entire Nine Heavens, there would be less than a hundred entities with a Divine-grade bloodline.

The highest-known grade of bloodline was the Supreme-grade bloodline. Only the most powerful entities, such as Dragon Goddess Yeyou, the Primordial Phoenix, and the Dragon Ancestor, possessed such bloodlines. They are so rare that one could count all of them with two hands.

“Only Divine-grade? I was expecting it to reach Supreme-grade.” Yuan mused afterward.

“The Monarch’s bloodline is, without doubt, Supreme-grade at the very least. However, you’ve only partially awakened your bloodline. If you want your bloodline to reach Supreme-grade, you’ll need to consume Supreme-grade Blood Essences to truly awaken your bloodline’s full potential.” The Primal Monarch said to him.

“I need to consume nine different Supreme-grade Blood Essence, correct? Can I assume that your Blood Essence doesn’t count?”

The Primal Monarch nodded, “My Blood Essence won’t count, so you still need nine more.”

‘The Dragon Ancestor should have a Supreme-grade bloodline, so that’s one down. If I count the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence in Feng Feng’s possession, that’s two…’

“What are your plans now?” The Primal Monarch suddenly asked him. “Will you be returning to the human world now?”

“No, there’s still something I must do here… unless you can help me.”

Yuan retrieved Feng Yuxiang’s egg and showed it to him.

“Can you do something about this?”

“What do you want me to do, exactly? If you want me to awaken the Phoenix inside forcefully, I can do that, but it’ll injure the Phoenix.”

“Can you speed up her recovery?”

The Primal Monarch glared at the egg and said, “As far as I can tell, the Phoenix inside is already fully recovered.”

“Really? Then why isn’t she coming out? I was told that she would come out once she fully recovers.”

“I’m afraid I don’t have an answer to your question. If you want to know, you should visit the Phoenixes’ territory.”
