Yuan nodded, “That was my plan from the start, but it’s a little far, and I can’t spend too much time in this place. Do you think you can send me there?”

“The Eternal Phoenix Domain, right?”

“Yes, that’s right.”

“I can send you there. What about your friend?”

“My friend?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“That Shadow Dweller you’ve been traveling with. She’s still at the Mystic Valley.”

“I thought she’d left by now…” Yuan muttered in a low voice.

After taking some time to ponder, Yuan said, “Let me talk to her for a bit.”

The Primal Monarch flicked his arm, casually creating a rift that led him directly back to the spot where he had left from.

When Yuan returned to the Mystic Valley, he noticed that Yingzi hadn’t moved an inch from her spot. As for the Divine Fox of Grand Prophecy, she had disappeared somewhere, presumably to recover after exhausting herself to divine the Primal Monarch’s location.

“Don’t tell me that you’ve been waiting for me.” Yuan looked at Yingzi with a raised eyebrow.

She nodded and said, “Well, I still don’t understand what it means to be a human.”

“What if I never returned?” he asked.

“If you hadn’t returned by the time I finished all of the meat skewers, I would’ve left,” she calmly said.

Yuan glanced at the number of meat skewers Yingzi had left. He had been gone for five days, yet she had only eaten a little over 100 skewers. It was clear to him that she had purposely slowed down, waiting for his return.

“Well, I am going to visit the Eternal Phoenix Domain now. Want to come along?”

“I won’t be able to protect you, though,” she said.

“That’s fine. I can protect myself now.”

“Are you going to be visiting the Phoenixes’ territory in that guise? They’ll attack you before you can even get close.” The Primal Monarch suddenly said to him.

“Hmm… you’re right, and pretending to be a Phoenix is too risky. If I cannot be a Dragon or a Phoenix, I might as well go there as a human.”

Yuan deactivated his transformation for the first time since he came to the Primal Expanse, and his human scent immediately started to fill the air.

The Primals within the Mystic Valley quickly picked up his scent and immediately began rushing toward his location.

Despite the approaching threat, Yuan remained calm. He took a deep breath and spoke in a commanding voice, “I don’t have time to play with you lot. Scram.” freёwebnovel.com

Though his voice was barely louder than usual, it sent a powerful ripple through the entire Mystic Valley.

The moment the Primals heard his voice, which resounded inside their heads like a thunderclap, they immediately turned and fled in the opposite direction.

chapters.title: Chapter 1637 Forbidden Technique
chapters.body:
Chapter 1637 Forbidden Technique

Chapter 1637 Forbidden Technique

“I’m ready to visit the Eternal Phoenix Domain,” Yuan said to the Primal Monarch after chasing away the Primals.

The Primal Monarch nodded and said, “Don’t expect me to protect you if you get in trouble there.”

He smiled, “I’ll be fine. Thanks for worrying.”

“Who the hell is worried?” The Primal Monarch sneered before opening a rift that led directly to the Eternal Phoenix Domain.

“Can you hold these for me with your treasure?” Yingzi handed him the meat skewers she didn’t eat.

Yuan nodded and stored the skewers in his spatial ring before stepping into the rift, with Yingzi following closely behind. Upon emerging from the rift, Yuan found himself standing before a vast land that resembled the Phoenix Ascendancy Plateau.

His human scent instantly spread and attracted the attention of nearby Primals.

‘This is the perfect opportunity for me to test out my new powers while I wait for the Phoenixes to show up…’ Yuan thought to himself and patiently waited for the Primals to show up.

A few moments later, a massive purple-feathered bird, its wings large enough to cover an entire mountain, came diving toward Yuan’s location.

This bird exuded an aura equal to that of a Divine Emperor cultivator.

Yuan calmly gazed at the incoming bird. Once it was close enough, Yuan muttered in a nonchalant tone, “Die.”

In the next moment, the bird’s movements suddenly slowed, and then, without warning, it began to fall lifelessly, plummeting straight into the ground.

<You have killed Fleeting Lightning Bird>

“…”

Yingzi’s eyes widened in shock as she witnessed Yuan kill the Primal with nothing more than a simple voice command.

“So it actually works…” Yuan mumbled to himself.

He had never used the Immortal Monarch’s Dominion in such a manner in his previous life, so this was something new to him.

More Primals appeared shortly after, but they also dropped like flies as Yuan commanded them to die with nothing more than his voice.

Although this power initially seemed limitless, Yuan began to feel fatigued after killing several more Primals in this manner, and it was mental fatigue that couldn’t be healed even with his powerful soul cultivation technique. It was also apparent that the stronger his target, the more it drained his energy and exhausted him.

‘Let’s try something else now…’

Yuan suddenly made a grasping motion toward the corpses scattered on the ground and muttered, “Condense!”

In response, the corpses began to disintegrate into their purest form—spiritual energy—before condensing and forming what is known as a monster core.

In the Primal Expanse, entities do not leave behind a monster core because they cultivate through their Chaotic Heart. Yet, Yuan had managed to transform their corpses—their very Chaotic Hearts—into monster cores through his unique power.

<You have learned Absolute Core Creation>

[Absolute Core Creation]

[Rank: Celestial]

[Mastery Level: Max]

[Description: Allows the user to create perfect monster cores from beast corpses.]

“What is that?” Yingzi asked as she stared at the monster cores with a curious gaze. This was her first time seeing a monster core.

“The Primals and Predators in the human world don’t have Chaotic Hearts. Instead, they have a monster core, which you’re looking at right now.” Yuan explained.

“They don’t have Chaotic Hearts…?” Yingzi muttered in disbelief, unable to fathom such a possibility, as it was impossible for them to survive without a Chaotic Heart.

“Do you want it? Here you go.” Yuan tossed the monster cores to her.

Once the monster cores were in her grasp, Yingzi inspected them with great interest. Meanwhile, Yuan shifted his focus to the approaching Primals.

“Although I can’t turn them into monster cores while they are still alive, I can do this instead…”

When his next target was close enough, Yuan pointed two fingers at it and muttered, “Extract!”

The targeted Primal suddenly sensed an overwhelming dread as it felt its longevity rapidly depleting, its life force draining away uncontrollably.

However, its longevity wasn’t disappearing into thin air. Instead, it was being used to form a Blood Essence.

Sure enough, just moments later, Yuan successfully extracted the Primal’s Blood Essence.

<You have gained Fiery Giant Eagle’s Blood Essence>

<You have learned Forbidden Blood Extraction>

[Forbidden Blood Extraction]

[Rank: Celestial]

[Mastery Level: Max]

[Description: Forcefully extracts Blood Essence from your target. This forbidden technique is greatly frowned upon by the Heavens. Using this technique may invoke Heaven’s Tribulation!]

Yuan swallowed nervously at the description.

‘A technique that could invoke Heaven’s Tribulation just by using it? Luckily, I am still in the Primal Expanse, where Heaven has no influence…’ Yuan thought to himself.

“…”

When she realized what Yuan had just done, Yingzi wasn’t merely shocked—she was absolutely terrified. The ability to forcefully extract another’s Blood Essence was a terrifying technique, one that could plunge the entire Primal Expanse into chaos and turmoil.

Seeing Yingzi’s fearful expression, Yuan smiled apologetically and said, “I’m sorry if that scared you. I was just testing it out. I won’t do that again.”

Yingzi silently acknowledged his words, but the fear in her heart couldn’t be dispelled so quickly.

Meanwhile, several powerhouses had also witnessed Yuan’s terrifying powers from afar and were left in shock.

“That human just used the Primal Monarch’s powers!”

“Who is that human?! Where did he come from?!”

“Did he come to our territory to cause trouble, or is he here for something else?”

Despite their status as powerhouses, these individuals didn’t dare to show themselves before Yuan, fearing that he might forcefully extract their Blood Essences.

“Let’s just stand by and observe him a little longer before deciding what to do with him.”

“Yeah, let’s do that.”

Meanwhile, Yuan continued killing the Primals who approached him because of his scent. However, he refrained from using the Monarch’s power unless necessary.

‘What’s taking them so long to show up? They should’ve been aware of my presence the moment I showed up in their territory…’ Yuan wondered inwardly, unaware that they were purposefully avoiding him.

Sometime later, a human-shaped figure approached Yuan, but this person was not one of the powerhouses.

chapters.title: Chapter 1638 One Condition
chapters.body:
Chapter 1638 One Condition

Chapter 1638 One Condition

“Hey, you with the weird scent!”

A voice suddenly called out to Yuan.

Yuan turned toward the source of the voice, spotting a young lady with long crimson hair, amber-colored eyes, and fierce facial features.

“About time someone shows up,” Yuan mumbled after seeing this young Phoenix.

“Who the hell are you? And why are you causing a scene in our territory?” the young lady questioned, seemingly unaware that Yuan was a human.

It was well known that humans couldn’t use their powers in the Primal Expanse, and the fact that no human had appeared in their world for millions of years left the young lady unaware of Yuan’s true identity.

When the powerhouses realized who this young lady was, they began panicking.

“What the hell is she doing over there?!”

“We need to intervene before that human steals her Blood Essence!”

Meanwhile, Yuan faced the young lady and spoke, “I have some business with the Primordial Phoenix.”

The young lady frowned and said, “The Primordial Phoenix isn’t someone you can meet just because.”

“Then how can I meet them?”

The young lady narrowed her eyes and said, “First and foremost, you must identify yourself. What bloodline do you have? I am not familiar with your scent. Also, we need to know your reason for seeking out the Primordial Phoenix.”

“I’m Yuan, a human, and I need the Primordial Phoenix’s help with something.”

“Huh? A human?” The young lady’s eyes widened with surprise after hearing such an unexpected answer.

“To be messing with me… you obviously don’t care about meeting the Primordial Phoenix,” she sneered in disdain.

“Why would I be messing with you? Can’t you tell by my scent? There’s a reason the Primals have been swarming me.”

“…”

The young lady was speechless.

At that moment, two more figures appeared: a middle-aged man and a woman, both exuding an aura equivalent to a cultivator at the God Ascension realm. As soon as they arrived, they positioned themselves in front of the young lady, as if shielding her with their bodies.

“Seniors? Why are you here?” The young lady was surprised by the appearance of these two powerhouses.

“We should be asking you that! Why did you come here when we told everyone to remain in their home?!”

“You did? I didn’t get the word.” The young lady shrugged.

“Anyway, I came here because I noticed a weird scent coming from this guy. He claims to be a human, but I don’t believe him.”

The two powerhouses turned to look at Yuan with deep frowns on their faces.

“What is your relationship with the Primal Monarch?” The woman asked in a nervous tone.

“What? The Primal Monarch?” The young lady’s eyes widened.

“He’s my adoptive father.” Yuan calmly responded.

“?!?!”

“The Primal Monarch adopted a human?!” The middle-aged man exclaimed, his face filled with disbelief.

“Then are you here on behalf of the Primal Monarch?” The woman continued to ask.

He shook his head, “No, I came here because I personally have business with the Primordial Phoenix.”

“What sort of business?” Yuan retrieved Feng Yuxiang’s egg and said, “I was told that they would be able to help my friend here.”

The Phoenixes before him trembled, their faces filled with shock when they saw the egg in Yuan’s grasp.

“T-that is…! Where the hell did you get that?!” The young lady cried out loud.

“That shell! It can’t be…!” The middle-aged man swallowed nervously.

“W-we must notify Her Majesty immediately!” The woman exclaimed.

Yuan raised an eyebrow at their reaction and asked, “Does that mean I get to meet the Primordial Phoenix?”

However, before the Phoenixes could respond, Yuan suddenly felt a strong, overwhelming pressure appear out of nowhere.

Yuan tilted his head upward to see a massive, majestic Phoenix hovering above them.

When the Phoenixes noticed this newcomer, they immediately lowered their heads and greeted it, “We greet Your Majesty!”

“The Primordial Phoenix…?” Yuan recognized the Primordial Phoenix’s appearance since she looked exactly like Feng Yuming in her beast form.

In the next moment, the Primordial Phoenix began to shrink, transforming into the form of a human.

The Primordial Phoenix took the form of a stunning woman, tall and slender, with long, fiery red hair cascading down her back, nearly reaching her feet. Her presence radiated a majestic aura, one that commanded respect, like that of an empress.

‘She looks just like Feng Yuming…’

Yuan was startled by how closely this Primordial Phoenix resembled Feng Yuming. Yet, it was clear that she wasn’t Feng Yuming, but another distinct Primordial Phoenix.

The Primordial Phoenix descended gracefully, halting just before Yuan, her sharp gaze locking onto the egg in his hands. After a brief, tense silence, she shifted her eyes to Yuan and asked in a calm, yet commanding tone, “Who are you?”

“I’m Yuan, a human from the human world.”

“What is your relationship with the one inside this shell?”

“We’re companions.”

“Then you’re not much of a companion, letting her end up in such a state,” the Primordial Phoenix remarked, her tone laced with disdain.

Yuan narrowed his eyes and said, “You don’t even know what happened.”

“That’s irrelevant.”

“Whatever. I’m not here to argue but to see if you can heal her.”

The Primordial Phoenix looked at the egg and said, “Of course, I can heal her. However, why should I do that for you? Did you think I would be willing to heal her just because she’s a Phoenix?”

“I was hopeful, but if you’re unwilling to heal her, just let me know so I can stop wasting my time here.”

“How dare you speak to Her Majesty in such a tone! Even the Primal Monarch addresses her with respect!” the middle-aged man shouted, his voice brimming with anger.

The Primordial Phoenix narrowed her eyes at him.

After a brief silence, she spoke, her voice calm yet firm, “I am willing to heal her… but only under one condition: you must leave her with us in the Eternal Phoenix Domain.”

Yuan frowned after hearing about her condition. “Are you serious?” “Did it sound like I was joking?” the Primordial Phoenix replied, her voice cold and unwavering.

chapters.title: Chapter 1639 Primordial Phoenix’S Outburst
chapters.body:
Chapter 1639 Primordial Phoenix’S Outburst

Chapter 1639 Primordial Phoenix’s Outburst

“If I heal her and let her continue traveling with you, she will undoubtedly be hurt again, perhaps even face death,” the Primordial Phoenix explained the reason behind her condition, her tone resolute. “In that case, I would rather she stay here with us, where she will be close to her own bloodline and, more importantly, where she’ll be safe.”

Hearing this, Yuan calmly replied, “You’re right. It is dangerous to be with me, and there’s a high chance she’ll get hurt again in the future. However, it’s her decision whether she wants to continue traveling with me. Neither you nor I have the right to decide her path for her.”

“Moreover, she doesn’t even belong in this world. It’s unreasonable to ask her to stay.”

“You speak so confidently despite knowing nothing.” The Primordial Phoenix sneered.

“Why don’t you enlighten me, then?”

“You’re not worth it.”

Yuan shrugged, “Then I guess we have nothing left to talk about.”

He put Feng Yuxiang away and prepared to leave.

“Where do you think you’re going? This isn’t a place where you can come and go as you please,” the Primordial Phoenix said sharply, her voice echoing with authority. “Leave that Phoenix behind, and only then will I allow you to leave.” With those words, she unleashed a powerful pressure, instantly restricting Yuan’s movements.

The Primordial Phoenix was undoubtedly one of the strongest beings Yuan had encountered so far, second only to the Primal Monarch. If compared to a cultivator, her power would rival that of a Cultivation God—an entity that stood at the very peak of the cultivation world.

Sensing the Primordial Phoenix’s overwhelming presence, Yuan instantly realized that he was powerless against her—and that not even the Monarch’s power could save him.

However, Yuan remained calm, confident that the Primal Monarch was watching everything unfold and wouldn’t allow him to die.

“If you think the Primal Monarch will save you, think again,” the Primordial Phoenix said, her eyes gleaming with certainty. “He and I have an agreement—that he won’t interfere with anything that happens within my territory, no matter what.”

She had guessed the obvious reason behind his calm demeanor.

“If he does show up to save you, I am willing to fight him to death.”

“…”

Yuan was speechless. He couldn’t understand why the Primordial Phoenix was so determined to keep Feng Yuxiang in the Eternal Phoenix Domain with them.

Before Yuan could respond, she continued, “Or perhaps you believe I can’t kill you because that isn’t your real body? Unfortunately for you, my flames can burn anything—even a body crafted from your soul.” Her voice carried a cold certainty that sent a chill through the air.

Yuan’s eyebrows twitched at her words.

‘She can tell that this isn’t my real body?!’ he cried inwardly.

“As a curtsy to the Primal Monarch, I will give you one last warning. Leave behind that Phoenix. Otherwise, I completely erase you from existence!”

Suddenly, flames erupted around them, encircling Yuan and trapping him within a blazing sphere of fire. The intense heat pressed in from all sides, leaving him no escape.

Seeing this, Yuan spoke out loud, “I know you’re seeing this, you old booger! Are you really going to let me die?!”

“It’s useless. The Primal Monarch won’t interfere.” The Primordial Phoenix sneered at his futile attempt.

However, a voice suddenly resounded, “You were quite confident that you’d be fine right before I sent you off, so what is this, you little brat?”

The Primordial Phoenix frowned when she realized it was the Primal Monarch’s voice.

“Primal Monarch, I won’t hurt him as long as he leaves behind the Phoenix!”

The Primal Monarch then spoke, but not to the Primordial Phoenix, “You heard her, brat. It would save me a lot of headaches if you leave behind that Phoenix.”

“Hmph! I’d rather die!” Yuan sneered.

“It’s just a Phoenix. I’m sure you can easily acquire more when you return to your world.”

“Just a Phoenix? She’s much more than that to me! If you’re not going to help, just leave! You useless old booger!”

“…”

After a brief silence, a deep sigh echoed through the fiery enclosure. Suddenly, a rift tore open beside Yuan, and the Primal Monarch emerged from it.

“What do you think you are doing, Primal Monarch? We had an agreement! Don’t tell me you plan on breaking it!” The Primordial Phoenix roared in anger.

“I know we had an agreement,” the Primal Monarch responded, his voice calm yet firm. “But I cannot allow my one and only son, since the beginning of time, to die here.”

He paused, his gaze steady on the Primordial Phoenix. “Why are you so determined to take his companion from him, anyway? Even if she dies by his side, what does that have to do with you? It’s not like she’s the only Phoenix out there.”

The Primordial Phoenix trembled at his words, her composure faltering. After a moment, she responded with a fierce intensity, “It has everything to do with me! You’re not the only one trying to save your child here, Primal Monarch! That Phoenix is my daughter—my oldest!”

“What?! Your daughter?!” Yuan’s eyes widened with shock after hearing the Primordial Phoenix’s unexpected claim.

‘Feng Feng is her daughter?! How is that even possible?!’ he cried inwardly.

“And unlike you, who merely adopted that human, she’s my biological daughter!” the Primordial Phoenix continued, her voice trembling with emotion, revealing the feelings she had been holding back all this time.

Yuan stared at the Primordial Phoenix, his jaw slack in disbelief. He could sense the truth in her words—her emotions were raw and genuine, leaving no room for doubt.

However, Yuan couldn’t fathom how Feng Yuxiang could be her child. Had Feng Yuxiang somehow traveled to the Nine Heavens, much like he had accidentally found himself in the Primal Expanse?

“So she was your child, huh? That explains a lot.” The Primal Monarch mused.

The Primordial Phoenix pointed at Yuan, her voice rising with fury as she shouted, “I won’t allow my daughter to stay with someone who let her nearly die, even if you are the Primal Monarch’s son!”

Her fierce gaze burned with such intensity that Yuan felt as if his entire body was engulfed in flames. Coupled with her harsh words, he felt a deep sense of guilt, as though he was in the wrong.

chapters.title: Chapter 1640 A Vicious Curse
chapters.body:
Chapter 1640 A Vicious Curse

Chapter 1640 A Vicious Curse

“Since you’re her mother, this changes everything. However, that doesn’t mean I’m willing to let you take her away from me.” Yuan said to the Primordial Phoenix after a moment of silence.

He continued, “How about this? Why don’t you help your daughter recover first, then we can discuss her future together? I promise that I won’t leave before the two of you have a conversation. I really don’t want to fight you.”

“Hmph! You don’t want to fight me? You aren’t even capable of doing so!” The Primordial Phoenix sneered.

She turned to look at the Primal Monarch and continued, “Although you haven’t done anything yet, I am still going to consider this a breach of our agreement, Primal Monarch!”

“Does this mean you have given up on trying to kill my son?”

“That will depend on my daughter’s testimony. If I find out that he’s mistreated her even slightly, I will not hesitate to kill him even if it’s the last thing I do,” she responded in a cold voice.

The Primordial Phoenix removed the flames shortly after and said to Yuan, “Follow me.”

She created a rift and entered it.

“Go ahead. I won’t be coming along.” The Primal Monarch said.

“Alright.” Yuan nodded and followed the Primordial Phoenix into the rift.

Yingzi, who had been watching in the background, silently followed him. As for the three Phoenixes…

“So the Phoenix inside that egg was my eldest sister, huh? No wonder I felt an odd sense of familiarity with her…” the young lady mused, her voice softening as the realization dawned on her.

“I can’t believe that the First Princess has returned after so long…” The woman muttered in a dazed voice.

“Things are going to get chaotic here very soon. I need to notify everyone of the situation as soon as possible.” The middle-aged man sighed.

Once Yuan stepped through the rift, he found himself in a vast, breathtaking garden that seemed to stretch endlessly in every direction. The air was thick with the fragrance of medicinal plants, all flourishing with vibrant yang energy.

“Put my daughter on that altar.” The Primordial Phoenix pointed to the altar covered with complex array symbols in the distance.

Yuan nodded and approached the altar before retrieving Feng Yuxiang’s egg and placing it in the center of the altar.

“Get back,” said the Primordial Phoenix as she stood before the altar.

Yuan stepped back and waited for her next move.

However, the Primordial Phoenix didn’t do anything besides silently staring at the egg with a motherly gaze.

A few moments later, she suddenly frowned and roared, “Which bastard placed such a vicious curse on her?!”

“That happened to her before we met, so I don’t know the details.” Yuan quickly shook his head when she turned to look at him for answers.

“Just so you know, I’ve been working to get that curse removed since we met,” he added.

“Hmph! The reason why she hasn’t emerged from her shell is because of this damned curse.” The Primordial Phoenix revealed with a cold sneer.

“Unfortunately, I cannot do anything until we completely remove the curse, or I risk injuring her, even killing her.”

“Do you have a way to remove the curse?” Yuan asked.

“We can remove the curse with the help of a treasure. However, we don’t have that treasure at our territory.”

“But you know where it is, right?”

“The treasure is called the Sacred Chalice of Purification, and it is in the possession of those annoying dragons at the Divine Dragon Sanctuary,” she said.

“The Divine Dragon Sanctuary?” Yuan didn’t expect to hear this name again.

“It’s a rank one territory, one that rivals our Eternal Phoenix Domain, controlled by the dragon race,” the Primordial Phoenix sighed, her frustration evident. “I would retrieve the treasure myself, but doing so would undoubtedly spark an all-out war between our territories.”

“I can get the treasure.” Yuan suddenly volunteered.

She looked at him and sneered, “You? What can you possibly achieve with that level of cultivation? Your Monarch’s power is useless against those whose strength far exceeds your own.” Her tone was sharp, dripping with condescension.

“Must everything be solved with violence and force?” Yuan shook his head.

He proceeded to transform, revealing his dragon form to her.

“You…” The Primordial Phoenix’s frown deepened as she sensed the unmistakable scent of a dragon exuding from his body. This was the first time such a scent had breached her sacred territory—her private sanctuary. Her displeasure was evident, her gaze hardening with irritation.

“I’ll go there as a fellow dragon,” Yuan said with a calm smile, ignoring her displeased glares. “Even if you go there as a dragon, it won’t do you any good,” the Primordial Phoenix scoffed. “The Sacred Chalice of Purification isn’t just any treasure—it’s one of their most valuable, revered as sacred. It has never left their territory before, and they guard it like their life depends on it.”

“That does sound troublesome…” Yuan mumbled to himself after hearing this.

After a moment of silence, he shrugged, “Well, I’ll figure something out—probably.”

“You… why would my daughter follow someone as foolish as you…?” The Primordial Phoenix sighed.

“Then do you have a better suggestion?” Yuan inquired.

The Primordial Phoenix remained silent.

Eventually, she said, “Even the Primal Monarch might not be able to acquire the treasure from them without a fight, so I really have no idea how you’re going to do it. However, it’s still better than suddenly going to war with them—not that I am afraid of them or anything like that.”

“We’ve been trying to conquer or eliminate each other since the beginning of time,” the Primordial Phoenix continued coldly. “We’ve gone to war more than once, even for reasons far more insignificant than this. All we need is time to prepare, and that’s exactly what we’ll be doing while you do your thing. So don’t worry. Even if you fail to secure the treasure, we’ll strike when the time is right.”

Yuan swallowed nervously.

The Primordial Phoenix’s expression was deadly serious as she spoke of going to war with the dragons, all for the sake of reviving her daughter. Her resolve was unwavering, showing just how far she was willing to go for her child’s life.

chapters.title: Chapter 1641 Human From Back Then
chapters.body:
Chapter 1641 Human From Back Then

Chapter 1641 Human From Back Then

“I’ll do whatever it takes to acquire the treasure. Just don’t do anything reckless and foolish before I return.” Yuan shook his head at the Primordial Phoenix’s eagerness to go to war with the dragons.

“Who the hell are you to tell me what to do?” The Primordial Phoenix sneered.

She continued, “Ten years. If you haven’t returned with the treasure by then, we will go there and get it ourselves.”

‘Ten years?’ Yuan smiled inwardly after hearing this generous number. ‘I only plan to stay in this world for another year at most. As if I need ten years!’

“Alright. Ten years.” He agreed to her terms.

“Anyway, I will need some time to prepare before I head to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary. If I go there in my current state—with an incomplete Chaotic Heart, they will definitely suspect my identity.”

The Primordial Phoenix narrowed her gaze on him, seemingly pondering something.

“Since you brought my daughter back to us, we will assist you in that regard,” she suddenly said.

“Eh? Really?” Yuan wasn’t expecting this at all and was pleasantly surprised.

“It’s only a single Chaotic Heart, and it’s your first, so it won’t take much effort.”

“Then I will take up on your offer,” Yuan said.

“…”

“By the way, I have been meaning to ask you this, but how did Feng Feng—your daughter end up in the human world?”

“Are you seriously asking me that? Do you even know anything about the Primal Expanse? Are you really the Primal Monarch’s son?” The Primordial Phoenix suddenly bombarded him with questions.

“Now that you mentioned it, I don’t really know much about the Primal Expanse…” Yuan suddenly came to this realization. freewёbnoνel.com

Despite spending over a hundred years in the Primal Expanse, Yuan hadn’t taken the time to learn much about its intricacies. Instead, he had devoted nearly all his focus to training.

‘I never anticipated that I would be leaving the Primal Expanse so soon back then, so I never really paid attention to the world, and that old booger never told me much about the world, either.’ Yuan sighed inwardly.

“Did the Primal Monarch really not tell you anything about our world? How long have you been living in the Primal Expanse?” The Primordial Phoenix asked.

“I came to the Primal Expanse by accident a little over a year ago,” he said.

“Only a year ago? And you’d become the Primal Monarch’s son in that little time? Something isn’t adding up here.” The Primordial Phoenix frowned.

“It’s a little complicated, but this isn’t my first time to the Primal Expanse.”

“Elaborate.”

‘Since she’s Feng Feng’s mother, I guess telling her the truth is fine. She’d find out eventually, anyway.’

With that in mind, Yuan explained his situation to her.

“I lived in the Primal Expanse for a little bit in my previous life,” he calmly explained.

The Primordial Phoenix’s eyes widened after hearing this.

“Your previous life…? Reincarnation? Wait a minute…”

The Primordial Phoenix suddenly began approaching him, her movements graceful yet deliberate. When she stood directly in front of him, she leaned in, bringing her face so close to his that their lips were just a breath apart, almost touching.

Her gaze was fierce, searing into his face with such intensity that it felt as though her eyes alone could burn him. The heat of her presence was almost overwhelming, like a fire just beneath the surface.

After a tense moment of silence, she abruptly stepped back, her eyes widening in shock as she cried out, “Y-you! No wonder you felt so familiar! You’re that human from back then! The one who appeared out of nowhere and caused chaos in the Primal Expanse for a hundred years before vanishing just as suddenly!” Her voice was filled with both recognition and disbelief.

“Hm? You know me—Tian Yi?” Yuan asked, his brow furrowing as he tried to recall the Primordial Phoenix from Tian Yi’s memories. However, he had fought so many phoenixes during that time that it was impossible to pinpoint her for certain.

“Who in the Primal Expanse hasn’t heard of you back then?” the Primordial Phoenix sneered. “Though I never met you personally, I heard all about your so-called ‘feats’—how you went from territory to territory, beating up the owners individually.” Her voice was laced with a mixture of disbelief and frustration as she recalled the stories of his chaotic past.

“As a matter of fact, you also made a mess in the Eternal Phoenix Domain back then!” the Primordial Phoenix continued, her voice rising with frustration. “Unfortunately, I was away when you invaded our territory. By the time I returned, you had already disappeared from the Primal Expanse, so I wasn’t able to exact revenge!” Her eyes burned with fury as she recalled the memory of returning home, only to find her people beaten to a pulp and their treasures plundered. The humiliation and rage she had felt at that moment resurfaced, igniting her anger once more.

“Oh, that was just my training,” Yuan said as he reminisced the past.

“Then why did you take our treasures?!”

“It requires a lot of resources to form Chaotic Hearts, you know? Anyway,” Yuan continued calmly, “while I did take your treasures, I didn’t kill any of the Phoenixes. Besides, you guys were the aggressors. I was simply passing through your territory when your people attacked me first, so you can’t really blame me for defending myself.”

The Primordial Phoenix trembled, but she eventually stopped.

“That explains why the Primal Monarch never showed up to stop you…”

“…”

“Whatever,” the Primordial Phoenix suddenly scoffed, waving her hand dismissively. “That’s all in the past, and I’m not petty enough to hold a grudge over something that happened millions of years ago.” Her tone softened slightly, though the tension in the air lingered.

“Thanks. Now, can you tell me about your daughter’s situation?” Yuan returned to the topic.

“Before that, you should learn more about the Primal Expanse,” she said.

“In that case, allow me to explain.” A third voice suddenly resounded.

“I knew you hadn’t left yet…” the Primordial Phoenix sneered, her voice dripping with disdain. “But to snoop around in my private world… I didn’t realize you were this shameless, Primal Monarch.”

Sure enough, a moment later, the Primal Monarch appeared before them, stepping through an entrance he had created himself with a faint smile on his face.

chapters.title: Chapter 1642 The Connection Between Two Worlds
chapters.body:
Chapter 1642 The Connection Between Two Worlds

Chapter 1642 The Connection Between Two Worlds

“Shameless? I am just trying to ensure my son’s safety. That’s all. I have no interest in you or your world.” The Primal Monarch shrugged.

“Hah! Do you think I am the type to go back on my words? I am nowhere as shameless as you, Primal Monarch!”

“Anyway, you had plenty of chances to explain to him before, so why didn’t you do so?” “Hmm… good question…”

The Primal Monarch began pondering with a serious face.

A few moments later, he had a look of realization and said, “I remember now! I forgot to tell him!” “Are you serious…?” The Primordial Phoenix frowned.

He shrugged, “My mind was occupied with other things back then, and teaching him about our world wasn’t a priority, so I never gave it any thought.” “Unbelievable…” Yuan muttered.

“Anyway, let me make it up by explaining our world to you now.” The Primal Monarch looked at Yuan and went on to explain the connection between the Primal Expanse and the Nine Heavens.

“As you already know by now, the ‘beasts’ of your world are originally from the Primal Expanse. Of course, not all of them came from the Primal Expanse, but a good portion of them are.”

“Now the question is, how does that work? How did they enter your world?” freewёbnoνel.com

“But before I answer that question, I have one for you. What happens when a human dies in your world?” The Primal Monarch faced Yuan with a profound smile.

“Well, as long as their soul isn’t destroyed, they will eventually be reincarnated. Without the memories of their past lives, obviously.”

The Primal Monarch nodded and continued asking, “Then what do you think happens to beasts after their death?”

Yuan pondered for a moment before responding, “Unlike humans, beasts don’t reincarnate. Unless they have a unique bloodline, they simply return to nature—being part of the world as spiritual energy.”

“You’re partially correct, and you’re missing a few pieces.” The Primal Monarch spoke.

“Just like humans, beasts also reincarnate,” he proceeded to explain. “However, their process is vastly different. When a beast dies, as long as its Chaotic Heart—or ‘core’—isn’t destroyed or consumed, it returns to nature as Chaotic Essence in the Primal Expanse and spiritual energy in your world. Over time, that energy will give birth to a new beast, whether by natural birth or through another beast.”

“Unlike humans, who rely on their own bodily fluids to breed and procreate, beasts only need spiritual energy to do so. There are two ways they acquire this energy—by receiving it from another being or by absorbing the natural energy around them. Most prefer to use nature’s energy, as it is both the most convenient and abundant source. However, those who wish to maintain or strengthen their bloodline’s purity and strength typically acquire it from another individual.”

Yuan absorbed the information with a serious expression, carefully processing every word. When the Primal Monarch mentioned the beasts’ method of breeding through spiritual energy, Yuan’s thoughts drifted to Lan Yingying and how she had requested his spiritual energy to conceive her future child.

While it wasn’t unheard of for beasts to conceive using a human’s spiritual energy, it was incredibly rare due to the unpredictability of the results. The fusion of human and beast energy often led to unforeseen complications, making such occurrences highly unusual and risky.

However, there are special races that can conceive with a human’s spiritual energy without any complications, and Lan Yingying belongs to that category.

“Now then, let’s finally get to the main topic,” the Primal Monarch said suddenly. “In the Primal Expanse, for reasons still unknown, when beings here perish, they don’t always return to nature as Chaotic Essence. Instead, they sometimes transform into spiritual energy and drift into your world, where they are reborn.”

“Huh?” Yuan’s eyes widened at this information.

He looked at the Primordial Phoenix and muttered, “Does that mean your daughter died in the Primal Expanse and was reincarnated in the human world?”

She nodded, “Yes, but due to our unique bloodline, our reincarnation process is somewhat identical to that of humans. For instance, when a human reincarnates, they will always return as humans. If I were to die right now, I would eventually come back as a Primordial Phoenix. However, if there are no interferences, a dragon could reincarnate as a snake or even a pig in their next life. In simpler terms, we will naturally retain our core identity and bloodline no matter how many times we are reborn, as long as there are no interferences.”

“Therefore, no matter how many times she dies, my daughters will always be my daughter.”

“I see…” Yuan muttered.

“By the way, if you didn’t know, the Primal Expanse existed long before the human world even came into the picture.” The Primal Monarch suddenly said.

“I have another question. If a beast dies in the human world, is there a chance that they could return to the Primal Expanse?” Yuan asked.

“No. Once one leaves the Primal Expanse, they will never return—at least not in that fashion. Of course, they can still return the same way you did.”

“How often does that happen?”

“As often as humans show up. Also, if a beast without a Chaotic Heart visits the Primal Expanse, it will be as powerless as a human. After all, only those with a Chaotic Heart or the Monarch’s bloodline could cultivate and utilize Chaotic Essence.”

“That explains why I am able to use my powers even without a Chaotic Heart…” Yuan mused to himself.

“That said, a beast born in the human world can still form a Chaotic Heart, so they’re not completely hopeless.” The Primal Monarch added.

Sometime later, Yuan said, “I understand how the Primal Expanse and the Nine Heavens are connected, but I still don’t know why.”

“Unfortunately, even I do not know why.” The Primal Monarch shook his head.

“Hmm… what about your bloodline? Just what exactly is the Monarch’s bloodline?”

The Primordial Phoenix stared at the Primal Monarch with intrigue. Although the beings in the Primal Expanse understand and have accepted the Primal Monarch’s powers, nobody knows how or why such a bloodline exists.

chapters.title: Chapter 1643 Six In Total
chapters.body:
Chapter 1643 Six In Total

Chapter 1643 Six in Total

“What is the Monarch’s bloodline, huh?” The Primal Monarch looked at Yuan with a profound smile on his face.

After a moment of silence, he responded with a casual shrug, “No clue.”

“Huh?” Yuan’s face became dumbfounded.

“I am the first of my kind, and it has been that way since the beginning of time. I do not know why I was born or my purpose. I merely exist.”

The Primordial Phoenix stared at the Primal Monarch with a pondering expression. As he had claimed, he had existed since the beginning of time before any other race was born. In fact, there are speculations that he had been created alongside the Primal Expanse itself, an ancient being intertwined with the very fabric of the world.

No one truly knows the full extent of the Primal Monarch’s power, as he has never revealed it. Throughout the ages, he has remained detached from worldly affairs, rarely interacting with its residents or intervening in their matters. He was a silent ruler, observing the world from a position so high that others could hardly comprehend it—his presence akin to that of a god.

He also possessed an unrivaled ability that allowed him to command all bloodlines as if they were his servants, even those of the so-called purest and most powerful bloodlines.

Due to his terrifying abilities, few dared to challenge him, allowing him to remain at the peak of the world since the beginning of time.

“You never questioned it or tried to find an answer?” Yuan asked, his curiosity piqued.

The Primal Monarch remained silent for a moment before shaking his head.

“I was curious during my earliest years,” he admitted, “but once I realized that knowledge wouldn’t change anything, I have since stopped caring about it.”

‘Just what is so special about this human for the Primal Monarch to be so attached to him?’ The Primordial Phoenix wondered inwardly.

The Primal Monarch was a godlike existence, one so far beyond the reach of most that even speaking to him seemed unimaginable. Yet, somehow, a mere human had managed to pique his interest enough to not only be adopted as his own but also be granted his immense powers. It was simply incomprehensible even for someone as experienced and knowledgeable as the Primordial Phoenix.

Sometime later, after their conversation ended, the Primordial Phoenix opened a rift, leading Yuan to the Eternal Phoenix Domain’s primary residence, where most of the phoenixes dwelled. “There will be someone to assist you with your Chaotic Heart.” The Primordial Phoenix said to him after opening the rift.

“You’re not coming?”

“I am going to examine her curse a little more.”

“I understand.”

The moment Yuan emerged from the rift, he was immediately greeted by a familiar face—the young lady who was the first to approach him. And before them, multiple islands floated majestically in the sky, each one occupied by a powerful phoenix, their fiery auras radiating through the air.

“We meet again. I was given instructions by my mother to assist you.” “Your mother…? You’re also the Primordial Phoenix’s daughter?” Yuan asked.

She nodded, “I am the youngest. You can call me Feng Yao.”

“Nice to meet you. By the way, how many siblings do you have?” “I have five siblings, but only three of them still exist in the Primal Expanse. The last two drifted into the other world, one of them being the eldest, whom you brought back.”

“So there are six of you in total, huh? Are you all Primordial Phoenixes?” “Of course not. The Primordial Phoenix bloodline is the purest and highest-grade Phoenix bloodline. Besides the eldest, we all have a different bloodline. However, while our bloodline isn’t as pure as the eldest, we’re still at the top of our bloodline.”

‘So Feng Feng is a Primordial Phoenix like her mother? But her character status shows a different bloodline. Is this also due to the curse?’

Yuan recalled that Xiao Hua’s character status changed when she released her seal, so Feng Yuxiang might be in a similar situation.

“Anyways, enough about me. What’s your relationship with Eldest Sister?” Feng Yao stared at him with glowing eyes that were filled with curiosity.

“We’re traveling companions,” Yuan said with a smile, purposefully avoiding the fact that they were in a master-servant relationship.

‘They’d probably skin me alive if they find out about our real relationship…’ Yuan could imagine the Primordial Phoenix’s fury if she found out, sending shivers throughout his body.

“How long have you known the eldest?” Feng Yao continued asking as they walked to their destination.

“We’ve only known each other for a few years.”

“Huh? That little time? You’re practically strangers!”

Yuan looked at her with raised eyebrows, but he refrained from commenting on her logic.

“What was she like when she still lived in the Primal Expanse?” Yuan suddenly asked.

Feng Yao shrugged and said, “I was born long after she was gone, so I don’t have the slightest clue. However, the others might know. You can try asking them later.”

“I’ll just ask the Primordial Phoenix later,” he said.

“…You’re really something, you know?” Feng Yao suddenly sighed.

“What do you mean?”

“Despite being her daughter, I rarely see my mother, let alone speak with her,” Feng Yao remarked with a hint of bitterness. “I think you’ve spent more time with her than I have in my entire life so far.”

“Seriously? Even though you all live in the same territory?”

“What do you know? Mother spends most of her time inside her own world.”

“Doing what? Increase her cultivation, which could already rival even the strongest cultivators in my world?”

“No, she spends most of her time conceiving another child. It takes hundreds of millions of years for someone at her level to conceive a single child, after all. This doesn’t include the time it takes her to absorb enough Chaotic Essence to begin the process, either.”

Yuan was left speechless by this revelation.

‘Hundreds of millions of years for a single child? I suppose it would disrupt the balance of the world if such a powerful being could conceive too quickly,’ Yuan thought to himself.

The stronger the being, the more time is required to conceive, a principle that applies even to humans.

‘Compared to the Primordial Phoenix, Yingying’s one thousand years is almost laughably short…’

“Alright, we’re here.” Feng Yao said out loud once they reached their destination.

chapters.title: Chapter 1644 Nine Levels Of Hell
chapters.body:
Chapter 1644 Nine Levels Of Hell

Chapter 1644 Nine Levels of Hell

“Where exactly are we?” Yuan inquired as they stood at the peak of a massive volcano. He thought they were going to feed him treasures to complete his Chaotic Heart.

“We’re at the Nine Levels of Hell Peak.”

“What a terrifying name. Why did you bring me here? The Primordial Phoenix said you’d help me complete my Chaotic Heart.”

“Were you expecting us to feed you treasures or something? You’ll be forming your Chaotic Heart here.” Feng Yao pointed at the raging flames inside the volcano.

“How does that work?” Yuan asked with raised eyebrows.

“There are nine floors in the Nine Levels of Hell, each floor engulfed in my mother—the Primordial Phoenix’s flames,” Feng Yao explained. “If you temper your body within these flames, you’ll eventually complete your Chaotic Heart. The deeper you go, the more intense the flames become, but the rewards will also be greater.”

Of course, they could have simply fed Yuan the necessary treasures to complete his Chaotic Heart. However, since the Primordial Phoenix couldn’t kill him, she saw this as an opportunity to inflict some pain on him instead. Thus, this grueling method of tempering his body in the flames was chosen as a way to achieve his goal while ensuring he endured some suffering in the process.

‘Not petty enough to hold a grudge, huh?’ Yuan recalled the Primordial Phoenix’s words and shook his head inwardly.

“Anyway, you’re only a human, and your cultivation is also pathetically low, so even the first level might be unbearable for you.” Feng Yao continued, “That’s why I’ll give you this.”

She handed him a bottle that contained a single drop of blood inside.

“Phoenix blood?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“My blood, to be precise. If you consume it, you’ll have enhanced fire resistance for a short time, enough to endure the second level at most.”

It was only ordinary phoenix blood, but Feng Yao had a powerful bloodline, so even a single drop would be priceless in the Nine Heavens.

“Your blood is only enough for the second level? Just how powerful are these flames?” Yuan asked.

“What do you know? My Mother is the most powerful phoenix in existence. Her flames can burn even someone with fire immunity.”

“Is that so? Have you heard of Phoenix’s Wrath?” Yuan suddenly asked.

“Phoenix’s Wrath? Of course, I have. Why?”

“How does it compare to the flames here?”

Feng Yao pondered for a moment before responding, “The Phoenix’s Wrath would only be as strong as the flames in the fourth level.”

‘The fourth level only? This place is no joke…’ Yuan swallowed nervously.

If the pain he experienced from the Phoenix’s Wrath was only as strong as the fourth level, he couldn’t imagine how much pain he’d experience in the ninth level.

“Alright, I’m going in,” Yuan said a moment later.

“Don’t bite more than you can chew.” Feng Yao said.

Yuan didn’t say anything and jumped inside the entrance, diving straight toward the second floor.

“Wha—?! I just warned him! And he didn’t even consume my blood! Are all humans this reckless?!” Feng Yao was shocked when Yuan jumped straight to the second level.

As Yuan passed through the first level, a wave of intense heat washed over him, making him feel as though he had stepped into an oven. The air was thick with scorching energy, but to his surprise, it wasn’t painful—just overwhelmingly hot, like standing too close to a blazing fire.

The moment Yuan stepped into the second level, the intensity of the heat skyrocketed. It was fierce enough to scorch his skin in an instant, but despite the flames’ strength, they couldn’t burn him faster than his body could regenerate. His healing abilities kicked in immediately, restoring his skin as quickly as it was damaged, creating a relentless cycle of burning and regeneration.

<Your fire resistance has increased!>

<Your fire resistance has increased!>

<Your fire resistance has increased!>

‘This is a great place to temper my body and increase my fire resistance!’ Yuan thought, his excitement growing as the flames continued their relentless assault. ‘Perhaps I could even achieve fire immunity in this place!’

The burning sensation was excruciating, but it wasn’t unbearable. Filled with determination, he sat down and began cultivating on the second level.

“Wow, he’s actually enduring the second level right off the bat. I guess I underestimated him a little.” Feng Yao mused, her interest piqued.

Not only did the flames temper Yuan’s body, but they also contained rich Chaotic Essence that gradually advanced his Chaotic Heart. However, the process was slower than he anticipated.

‘It’ll take years to finish my Chaotic Heart at this rate. I’ll head to the third level once I improve my fire resistance to the next level.’

With that in mind, Yuan continued to temper in the flames.

Sometime later, a figure appeared at the Nine Levels of Hell Peak and landed right beside Feng Yao.

“Elder Brother Mu.” Feng Yao greeted this tall and handsome man with a respectful smile.

“When did you return?”

“A few hours ago,” he responded, his gaze staring intently at Yuan’s figure.

Feng Mu was the second youngest in the family, but he was still a billion years older than Feng Yao.

“I came back immediately after hearing that the eldest has returned. Is that him? The human who brought back the eldest from the human world?” Feng Mu asked.

“Yes, he’s the one.”

“He’s less impressive than I anticipated—even disappointing,” Feng Mu remarked, shaking his head in dissatisfaction. “I heard he was related to the Primal Monarch, too. Must be a lie.”

“No, it’s true. The Primal Monarch even showed up to protect him when Mother was about to kill him.” Feng Yao said.

“Seriously? How is someone so weak related to the Primal Monarch? I can burn him to ashes with a single breath.”

Feng Mu quickly lost interest in Yuan and asked about Feng Yuxiang.

“Have you spoken to the eldest yet? What was she like?”

“That’s… Haven’t you heard? She came back as a ‘shell’.”

“What?” Feng Mu immediately became dumbfounded.

chapters.title: Chapter 1645 Fire Immunity
chapters.body:
Chapter 1645 Fire Immunity

Chapter 1645 Fire Immunity

“What the hell are you talking about? The eldest came back in such a state?” Feng Mu almost couldn’t believe what he was hearing.

“Unfortunately. I don’t know the situation, but Mother’s going to take care of it. In the meantime, I am tasked with helping him complete his Chaotic Heart.” Feng Yao pointed at Yuan.

Feng Mu shifted his gaze to Yuan again and sneered, “What a waste of time and effort.”

“Is that really the case?”

“Huh? Don’t tell me you think differently.”

“I don’t know humans enough to tell if he’s talented or not, but he was chosen by the Primal Monarch. I think that says enough.” Feng Yao shrugged.

At this moment, Yuan suddenly opened his eyes and stood up.

<You have gained Supreme Fire Resistance>

After Yuan’s fire resistance reached a new threshold, he wasted no time and immediately descended to the third level. The flames on the third level were no longer the familiar orange hue of the first two levels. Instead, the third level was bathed in intense blue flames, radiating an even more potent and refined heat.

Even with his Supreme Fire Resistance and powerful body, he experienced pain that was several times worse than the second level.

At this point, Yuan’s entire figure was completely engulfed by flames, so much so that even his silhouette was no longer visible. The blue flames enveloped him entirely, making it seem as though he had become one with the fire itself. Despite the overwhelming intensity, Yuan remained focused, letting the flames temper his body further and push his limits.

“Wow, he’s actually cultivating in the third level,” Feng Yao muttered, covering her mouth in surprise, her eyes wide with awe. She had only expected him to reach the second level at most, but in just a few days, he had surpassed her expectations.

Feng Yao turned to look at Yingzi, who had been silently blending in with the background, and spoke to her, “How long have you been following this human?”

“Since the moment he arrived at the Primal Expanse, I guess.”

“Why are you following him? I thought Shadow Dwellers are introverts.” “I wanted to know what it means to be human.”

“Oh? That does sound interesting. What have you learned so far?” A new voice suddenly interrupted.

Yingzi turned toward the voice and saw a beautiful woman with short red hair and purple eyes hovering gracefully above the Nine Levels of Hell Peak. Her presence was commanding, and the air around her seemed to shimmer with an ethereal glow as she watched the scene below with calm intensity.

“Elder Sister Liqiu.” Both Feng Yao and Feng Mu bowed to her in a respectful manner the moment they noticed her presence.

This newcomer was Feng Liqiu, the second eldest in their family.

After snapping out of her daze, Yingzi responded, “Not much, but from what I’ve seen, humans are beings that grow exponentially fast.”

“From my understanding, human growth varies from person to person.” Feng Liqui said. “Although I don’t know how he compares with the rest of the humans in his world, he’s definitely a lot better than the humans I’ve encountered in the Primal Expanse.”

Being the second oldest in the family, Feng Liqui has lived for billions of years in the Primal Expanse, so she has encountered plenty of humans in the past.

“Have you spoken with Mother yet, Elder Sister Liqui?” Feng Mu asked.

“Yes, but it was brief.”

“Elder Sister Liqui, you’ve met the Eldest before, correct? What was she like?” Feng Yao suddenly asked after recalling Yuan asking her the same question.

“Elder Sister, huh?” Feng Liqui closed her eyes and recalled her memories of Feng Yuxiang, which were billions of years old.

“We never really got along and constantly competed against each other to impress Mother. As for what she was like… I don’t remember.”

“Anyway, she’s probably reincarnated many times by now. I doubt she’s the same person I remember.”

Feng Liqui’s gaze returned to looking at Yuan.

“I’m more interested in this human with the Primal Monarch’s bloodline…” she muttered with a slight smile.

“What’s so interesting about that weakling?” Feng Mu shook his head, unable to comprehend it.

“Are you pretending to be stupid, or are you actually stupid?” Feng Liqiu narrowed her gaze at him.

“W-what did you say?! Did you just call me stupid?!” Feng Mu exclaimed, clearly surprised by her sudden mockery.

“Amongst the thousands of phoenixes in our territory, how many of them are capable of cultivating in the third level? Most of them can’t even endure the second level! Yet, this human is able to achieve that with his pitiful cultivation!” “T-that’s…” Feng Mu became speechless.

“A human capable of withstanding Mother’s flames… I am very intrigued!” A profound smile appeared on Feng Liqiu’s face as she returned to focusing on Yuan.

“Hmph. Whatever.” Feng Mu didn’t linger and left the scene shortly after. However, he didn’t leave the Eternal Phoenix Domain.

“Are you going to stay here? Elder Sister?” Feng Yao asked Feng Liqiu.

“I don’t have anything else to do, so I might as well. I also want to see his limit.”

“Huh? You think he’ll be able to go higher than the third level? That’s impossible! He’s already at his limit on the third level!” Feng Yao refused to believe that he could go any further.

“Is that what you think? I think he still has some tricks tucked under his sleeves.”

“I mean, I did give him a drop of my blood, which he hasn’t consumed yet. However, that won’t help him in the next level.”

“Oh? You gave him your blood? Did Mother tell you to do that?” Feng Liqiu looked at her with a suspicious smile.

“No, but don’t misunderstand. This is my way of showing gratitude. He brought back the Eldest, after all.”

Two weeks later, a significant change occurred in the flames surrounding Yuan’s body. The once ferocious flames, which had relentlessly burned him, began to shift, their intensity softening as they seemed to merge with his aura. The fire no longer consumed him but appeared to dance in harmony with his energy, as though he had mastered its power and become one with the flames.

<You have acquired Fire Immunity>

<You have acquired the title ‘One With the Flames’>

chapters.title: Chapter 1646 Completed Chaotic Heart
chapters.body:
Chapter 1646 Completed Chaotic Heart

Chapter 1646 Completed Chaotic Heart

“That human! He’s achieved fire immunity!” Feng Yao exclaimed, her expression filled with astonishment. “I didn’t know humans could do that!”

She had always believed that only beasts with unique bloodlines, like phoenixes, were capable of acquiring such immunity. The fact that a human had done it defied her expectations and understanding.

Even in the Nine Heavens, achieving fire immunity was an exceptionally rare accomplishment. Those who managed it typically relied on special techniques, priceless treasures, and years of dedicated cultivation to reach that level. There were also those born with unique physiques that granted them fire immunity naturally, but that was a different story since they hadn’t earned it through effort but were simply born with the gift.

The fact that Yuan had attained this without the aid of a technique made his accomplishment all the more extraordinary.

However…

“No… this isn’t something as simple as fire immunity!” Feng Liqiu’s eyes widened as she came to this realization.

[Fire Immunity]

[Rank: Divine]

[Mastery: Level 1]

[Description: Grants the user immunity to most fire]

<Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has activated>

<Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has begun fusing with Fire Immunity>

<Progress: 1%>

‘What? It’s fusing with my Fire Immunity?’ Yuan raised an eyebrow at this notification. ‘Is this similar to how my two physiques fused with each other?’

<Progress: 5%>

<Progress: 9%>

Unlike fusing two physiques together, the progress this time was much quicker.

“He’s absorbing the flames!” Feng Yao exclaimed when she noticed this. “Fire Immunity only makes one impervious to most fire, but it doesn’t have the ability to absorb flames!”

Feng Liqiu narrowed her eyes and spoke, her voice calm but filled with passion. “Fire Immunity may be the end of the journey for most, but for us phoenixes, that is just the beginning.”

“Above Fire Immunity lies True Fire Immunity, where one becomes truly immune to all flames,” Feng Liqiu continued, “And beyond that is the realm of Fire Harmony. Once one reaches Fire Harmony, not only will they be immune to fire, but they’ll also be able to absorb it, transforming flames into their own power—the apex of fire control.”

“Are you telling me that a human has managed to achieve Fire Harmony when even I haven’t been able to do so?!” Feng Yao exclaimed, her voice filled with disbelief and frustration. The idea that a mere human could reach a level of mastery that had eluded her was almost inconceivable.

Feng Liqiu shook her head and said, “No, that can’t be possible. He’s just achieved Fire Immunity, so it doesn’t make sense for him to achieve Fire Harmony so quickly. There must be something else going on inside that weird body of his.”

Suddenly, Yuan stood up and jumped even deeper into the Nine Levels of Hell, reaching the fourth level.

The fourth level’s flames were as powerful as the Phoenix’s Wrath, a force Yuan couldn’t withstand for more than a few seconds before. However, after achieving Fire Immunity, the once unbearable flames became barely endurable.

<Progress: 51%>

The progress of his physique, which had stalled at 50 percent before Yuan entered the fourth level, began to rise once more.

As for his Chaotic Heart, it was already on the verge of completion.

“This is something different… It’s similar to Fire Harmony, but it’s not the same…” Feng Liqiu mused to herself as she spent more time watching Yuan.

Several hours later, Yuan completed his Chaotic Heart.

<You have completely formed your Chaotic Heart>

Ding!

<Your character status has been updated>

[Yuan]

[Cultivation(Dantian): First Level Spirit Sovereign]

[Cultivation(Chaotic Heart): N/A]

Yuan wasn’t surprised to see a second cultivation because Tian Yi also achieved the same thing after returning to the Divine Heavens and cultivating his Dantian.

<Due to having a Chaotic Heart, your Heaven Refining Immortal Physique will no longer refine Chaotic Essence into Qi!>

<You have absorbed Chaotic Essence>

<You have absorbed enough Chaotic Essence for a breakthrough!>

<You have reached Early Chaotic Establishment>

<You have reached Intermediate Chaotic Establishment>

<You have reached Advanced Chaotic Establishment>

<You have reached Perfect Chaotic Establishment>

<You have absorbed enough Chaotic Essence for a breakthrough!> freeweɓnovēl.coɱ

<You have reached Early Chaotic Adept>

<…>

<You have reached Perfect Chaotic Adept>

<You have reached Early Chaotic Master>

Yuan’s second cultivation soared rapidly, experiencing breakthroughs every few minutes. In just a few short hours, Yuan’s second cultivation reached Early Chaotic Master, akin to a 9th-level Spirit Enlightenment in human cultivation, surpassing his original cultivation.

“What am I seeing right now…? I can sense two distinct energies coming from him! Is this what happens when a human forms a Chaotic Heart? I have never seen or heard of anything like this before!” Feng Yao exclaimed.

Feng Liqiu narrowed her eyes at Yuan and said, “This isn’t my first time seeing a human with a fully formed Chaotic Heart. Do you recall the human who made a mess in our territory several million years ago? However, he didn’t have the other energy at that time, so this is also my first time seeing someone with both at once. I wonder how it affects him.” “I have heard about him, but that happened before I was born.” Feng Yao nodded.

“Now that he’s formed his Chaotic Heart, he should be leaving soon.”

However, Feng Yao would soon realize that Yuan had no intention of leaving so soon. He may have formed his Chaotic Heart, but he still had something else to complete.

‘Chaotic Essence is essentially the same as spiritual energy, but it’s more chaotic, giving it far greater destructive power,’ Yuan thought, recalling Tian Yi’s experience. ‘Unfortunately, it can’t be used with human techniques. However, I can fuse it with my spiritual energy to enhance my techniques and make them more powerful. Or, I can directly enhance my body with it.’

When the fusing progress halted at 70 percent, Yuan looked to the fifth level.

After a moment of stillness, his body suddenly surged with Chaotic Essence. Once he’d strengthened his entire body with Chaotic Essence, Yuan jumped straight to the fifth level, where the flames were bright green and five times more powerful than the fourth level.

chapters.title: Chapter 1647 Fifth Level Of Hell
chapters.body:
Chapter 1647 Fifth Level Of Hell

Chapter 1647 Fifth Level of Hell

The instant Yuan entered the fifth level of Nine Levels of Hell, his progress shot up from 70 percent to 80 percent.

However, the pain he was experiencing was excruciating and would break the minds of even the toughest cultivators out there. The Nine Levels of Hell weren’t created for humans but for phoenixes, beasts with a natural affinity for fire.

Even a newly born phoenix with the lowest bloodline would have supreme fire resistance, and it wouldn’t take long for them to achieve fire immunity.

In fact, even a Golden Immortal would perish inside the fifth level, much less a Spirit Sovereign like Yuan.

“How much more is this human going to surprise me? Even I, who has almost achieved True Fire Immunity, can barely endure the fifth level’s flames!” Feng Yao exclaimed.

“…” Feng Liqiu didn’t make any comments and merely watched him in silence.

Yuan remained in the fifth level for a week straight, and his progress reached 89 percent.

Once he realized he wouldn’t advance any further, Yuan looked at the sixth level, where purple-colored flames raged.

After a moment of silence, he released a resolute roar before transforming into his dragon form.

“That’s—?!” Feng Yao and Feng Liqiu were startled by Yuan’s transformation.

While they took a moment to comprehend the situation, Yuan dove into the sixth level.

“This scent… does he have dragon blood flowing through his veins?!” Feng Yao cried out loud.

“Does mother know about this?” Feng Liqiu looked at her with a slight frown.

Dragons were forbidden from entering the Eternal Phoenix Domain. It didn’t matter if they were real dragons or only had a single drop of dragon blood in their whole body. Rules were rules, and not a single dragon managed to remain in their territory for more than a day, much less cultivate inside the Nine Levels of Hell Peak, a sacred place for phoenixes.

“I-I don’t know!” Feng Yao vigorously shook her head. “I was only told to bring him to this place!”

Once the nearby phoenixes noticed Yuan’s scent, they immediately rushed over to the Nine Levels of Hell Peak, thinking that a dragon had invaded their sanctuary.

“A dragon?! In the center of the Eternal Phoenix Domain?! Impossible!”

“How did a dragon manage to invade so deeply inside our territory without revealing its presence until now?!”

“This dragon has a weak presence, but it still doesn’t explain how it evaded us until now!”

“The scent is coming from the Nine Levels of Hell Peak! Don’t tell me they’re trying to sabotage our sacred place!”

Hundreds of phoenixes gathered at the Nine Levels of Hell Peak within minutes, surrounding the place.

Not a single one of them wasn’t baffled at the moment of their arrival when they saw the situation.

“W-what the fuck is going on…? What the fuck am I looking at?!”

“Are my eyes deceiving me, or is there a dragon inside the Nine Levels of Hell?! At the sixth level, no less!!!”

None of the newcomers dared to believe their eyes.

Sometime later, they snapped out of their daze and began shouting at each other.

“Hey! Someone go fish that dragon bastard out!”

“Why don’t you do it?!”

Although nobody there wanted to admit it, none of them could enter the sixth level, much less drag Yuan out.

“Lady Liqiu! You’re the only one here who can endure the flames on the sixth level!”

Eventually, they all turned to the only individual there who had any hopes of surviving the sixth level.

Feng Liqiu remained silent for a moment before responding to their hopeful gazes, “Huh? Why should I?”

Her words instantly shattered their hopes and left them dumbfounded.

“W-what do you mean, why?! There’s a dragon in our territory—inside the Nine Levels of Hell!”

She shrugged, “I’m exhausted right now.”

“Exhausted? From what?!!”

“From rushing back home,” she explained.

“…”

An awkward silence filled the atmosphere.

Suddenly, a loud and authoritative voice resounded, “What the hell is going on here?!”

“Master Mu!” These phoenixes became hopeful again after seeing Feng Mu’s appearance.

These phoenixes pointed to the sixth level of the Nine Levels of Hell without uttering a word.

When Feng Mu followed their gesture and saw Yuan cultivating in the sixth level, his eyes widened with shock and disbelief.

“The sixth level?! Impossible!”

He immediately looked at Feng Liqiu and questioned her, “What did you do to him?”

He assumed Feng Liqiu was responsible for Yuan’s achievements since she was the only one there capable of entering the sixth level.

“And why the hell does he smell like a dragon? Is Mother aware of this?”

“No clue what you’re talking about.” Feng Liqiu casually responded

Feng Mu looked at Yuan and frowned.

“Whatever. If you’re not going to take him out, I will do it myself. I won’t allow him to dirty this place with his presence any longer.”

“Halt.”

Right as Feng Mu prepared to dive into the volcano, a calm and clear voice rippled through the air, halting everyone’s movement.

The second the phoenixes recognized this voice, they lowered their heads and loudly greeted her.

“Empress Feng!”

“We greet Her Majesty!”

The Primordial Phoenix emerged from a rift, her figure hovering majestically above the Nine Levels of Hell. Her gaze was fixed intently on Yuan, observing him with a mixture of surprise and curiosity.

“Nobody is allowed to disturb him. Leave.”

The phoenixes bowed to her and left the scene without uttering a single word. The only ones who remained were her children.

Once most of the spectators were gone, the Primordial Phoenix created a barrier around the peak that prevented Yuan’s scent from spreading.

“Mother, did you let him enter knowing that he has dragon blood flowing through his veins?” Feng Mu asked.

“Yes, I am aware.”

“…”

Once the Primordial Phoenix confirmed this fact, Feng Mu no longer had any say in this matter. He gave Yuan one final glance before disappearing from the scene.

“I know you’re watching this, Primal Monarch.” The Primordial Phoenix suddenly spoke out loud.

Sure enough, another rift appeared above the volcano, and the Primal Monarch emerged from it.

“I don’t remember the last time I made so many public appearances in such a short time. In fact, this might be a first,” he spoke as he appeared.

chapters.title: Chapter 1648 Impervious To Fire
chapters.body:
Chapter 1648 Impervious To Fire

Chapter 1648 Impervious to Fire

“Primal Monarch!” Feng Yao and Feng Liqiu lowered their head after snapping out of their daze.

The Primal Monarch acknowledged them with a slight nod before looking at the Primordial Phoenix and asking, “So, why did you call for me?”

The Primordial Phoenix looked at Yuan and spoke, “You know, I have secretly been to the human world before, and I’d even spent a few thousand years there.”

“What? When did you do such a thing? And why?” Feng Liqiu expressed shock after learning this information for the first time despite being the second eldest.

“After giving birth to my fourth child, I suddenly had the urge to see how my first child was doing, so I traveled to the human world to satisfy my curiosity. Just going there took several million years of traveling nonstop. I was only able to achieve this because she had the same bloodline as me, and I could sense someone of the same bloodline no matter the distance. If it weren’t for that, I wouldn’t have been able to find the human world, much less see my child.”

“After arriving at the human world and confirming with my eyes that my first child was there, I spent several thousand years exploring the world. During my time there, I studied the humans, learning their customs and whatnot.”

“Unfortunately, what stood out the most about humans was their insignificant strength. Even the strongest cultivators back then were laughably weak.” The Primordial Phoenix recalled her experience in the Divine Heavens long before it was split.

“No matter how much potential they had, it didn’t impress me even in the slightest. That’s why I left their world so quickly. It was simply too boring, and I have already reunited with my first child—without her knowledge.”

“However, this human cultivating inside my flames right now, he’s different, and I want to know if you had anything to do with it, Primal Monarch.”

The Primal Monarch smiled and said, “In essence, you want to know if I have anything to do with his abilities, correct?”

The Primordial Phoenix nodded.

The Primal Monarch looked at Yuan and smiled, “He may have my bloodline, but what he’s displaying right now has nothing to do with it. It’s something entirely different.”

“I see…”

“Why do you even care?” The Primal Monarch asked.

“Just curious,” she responded in a nonchalant manner.

A few days later, Yuan’s aura suddenly began to transform.

The Primordial Phoenix and the others watched with anticipation.

<Progress: 99%>

Yuan stared at the progress for several hours straight, hoping it would be completed without needing him to enter the seventh level.

Realizing he needed stronger flames to complete his progress, Yuan turned his gaze toward the seventh level, where black flames roared ominously. These flames exuded a dangerous and oppressive energy, far more intense than anything he had encountered before.

‘I will only be able to last a few seconds in the seventh level…’

Even with his godlike physique, fire immunity, and incredible regeneration, Yuan knew he would perish in the seventh level if he stayed longer than a few seconds. The sheer power of the black flames was beyond anything he had faced before, and even this estimate felt like a dangerous gamble. But despite the risk, Yuan was determined to finish the progress.

“He’s looking toward the seventh level… don’t tell me he plans on going deeper? Does he have a death wish?” Feng Yao swallowed nervously.

Feng Liqiu narrowed her eyes, recalling her first time entering the seventh level.

When she first attempted the seventh level, she’d just achieved Fire Harmony, but even that was not enough to withstand the black flames, and she nearly lost her life just entering it.

‘If Mother didn’t save me at the last second, I would’ve perished on that day…’ Feng Liqiu sighed inwardly.

After preparing himself, Yuan took one last deep breath before jumping into the black flames.

‘The flames are much more powerful than I anticipated! I won’t last more than a second in this place!’ Yuan immediately realized that he’d underestimated the prowess of the black flames, but it was already too late to go back.

More than half of his body was burned away the instant he entered the seventh level. However, at the same time, his progress also advanced.

<Progress: 100%>

<Congratulation, your Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has successfully merged with Fire Immunity>

<Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has evolved to Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique(Incomplete)>

The moment Yuan’s physique evolved, the black flames ceased their relentless assault on his body. Instead of burning him, the flames began to restore his body, even strengthening it.

Once his body was fully restored, the flames began filling his Chaotic Heart with Chaotic Essence, causing his cultivation to soar once again.

<You have absorbed Chaotic Essence>

<You have absorbed enough Chaotic Essence for a breakthrough!>

<You have reached Intermediate Chaotic Master>

<You have reached Advanced Chaotic Master>

<You have reached Perfect Chaotic Master>

His cultivation halted at Perfect Chaotic Master, rivaling someone at the peak of Divine Master.

Yuan inspected his body, marveling at the transformation. The black flames, which had once pushed him to the brink of collapse with unbearable pain, now felt soothing and comforting, as if he were basking in the perfect sunlight.

‘Am I completely impervious to fire now?’ Yuan wondered inwardly.

He turned his gaze toward the eighth level, where beautiful white flames raged with an ethereal intensity. Strangely, despite their brilliance and power, he couldn’t sense any threat from them. It was as though the flames no longer held the capacity to harm him. “W-what just happened?! His body was on the verge of destruction just seconds ago! Now it seems as if the flames don’t even affect him!” Feng Liqiu exclaimed, her voice filled with shock and disbelief.

“Not only is he unaffected by it, he’s absorbing the flames and turning them into his own strength…” The Primordial Phoenix muttered, her eyes narrowing as she observed Yuan with a thoughtful expression.

chapters.title: Chapter 1649 Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique
chapters.body:
Chapter 1649 Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique

Chapter 1649 Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique

“Turning the flames into his own strength… that is a realm above Fire Harmony…” Feng Liqiu muttered in a dazed voice.

While the phoenixes remained stunned by Yuan’s remarkable achievements, he decided to push the boundaries of his newfound power. Without hesitation, he leaped straight into the eighth level, where the brilliant white flames roared fiercely.

‘Sure enough, I don’t feel any difference… no… this is even more comfortable than the black flames!’ Yuan thought, closing his eyes as the white flames enveloped him.

“This is… outrageous…” Feng Liqiu muttered, her body trembling as she stared at Yuan’s figure. The white flames, powerful enough to seriously injure even her—despite having pushed her Fire Harmony to its limits—seemed to have no effect on him.
