Just days ago, he had struggled on the second level, yet now, this mere human was enduring the flames of the eighth level with ease. The sight left her speechless and shaken.

“I don’t get it.” The Primordial Phoenix suddenly muttered out loud.

“Even if he has the ability to absorb my flames, there should be a limit to how much he could absorb without blowing up. His cultivation isn’t increasing, and I refuse to believe that his single Chaotic Heart could contain so much Chaotic Essence! Where are all of the Chaotic Essence going?”

The Primordial Phoenix’s question was a valid one. Just like a cup, no vessel—no matter how powerful—could hold an infinite amount. If one kept pouring water into a cup, it would eventually overflow. Similarly, there had to be a limit to how much power Yuan could absorb before reaching a breaking point. Yet, this breaking point was nowhere to be seen with Yuan.

Sure enough, Yuan’s Chaotic Heart could not contain an infinite amount of Chaotic Essence, and once it was completely filled to the brim, the Chaotic Essence was sent elsewhere.

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has activated>

<You have learned ‘Primordial Fire’>

[Primordial Fire]

[Rank: Celestial]

[Mastery Level: 1]

[Description: The highest level of fire mastery, attainable only by a few bloodlines. Primordial Fire transcends all other flames, bypassing even the strongest fire resistance and immunity. It is the ultimate expression of fire, a flame unrivaled in destructive capability and feared by even phoenixes.]

‘So I can turn the flames I absorbed into my own power…’ Yuan swallowed nervously at the implications.

‘If I can merge my physique with the fire immunity, I should be able to do the same with other immunities. Perhaps that’s how I complete my new physique…’

Sometime later, Yuan turned his gaze to the ninth and final level of the Nine Levels of Hell. Unlike the previous eight levels, there were no visible flames in the ninth. However, despite the absence of fire, Yuan could clearly sense something powerful and ominous lingering within.

‘It looks like I am not completely impervious to all fire yet…’ Yuan sighed inwardly. Though he had endured the eighth level without any issues, he could instinctively sense that the ninth level was different—deadly, even for him. ‘I guess it’s about time I leave this place.’

In just a few days, he’d completed his Chaotic Heart, gained fire immunity, evolved his physique, and even acquired a Celestial-rank skill.

Feeling satisfied by the results, Yuan left the Nine Levels of Hell.

The moment Yuan emerged from the volcano, he instinctively sensed something hurtling toward him. Turning around, his eyes widened as he spotted a massive ball of black flames flying directly at him.

Yuan instinctively raised his arm, opening his palm to block the incoming flames with his bare hand. The moment the massive ball of flames made contact with his palm, it was absorbed instantly. The flames, which were strong enough to kill even immortals, vanished into his hand, leaving Yuan unscathed. “What was that about?” Yuan looked at the Primordial Phoenix, who was responsible for the surprise attack. freewebnσvel.cøm

“I wanted to confirm your abilities.”

“What if that had killed me?”

“But it didn’t.”

“Unbelievable…”

“Why are you complaining? You should be thanking me for letting you use the Nine Levels of Hell.”

“I would’ve if you didn’t send me here because of your pettiness.” Yuan shrugged.

“I have no clue what you’re talking about.” The Primordial Phoenix shook her head.

“Whatever. I’m going to head to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary now that I have completed my Chaotic Heart.” “W-wait a minute!” Feng Liqiu suddenly spoke up.

“Hm? Who are you?” Yuan asked.

“I’m Feng Liqiu, the second eldest in the family.”

“Oh, hello. Do you need something from me?”

“Can you tell me how you are absorbing the flames?”

“It’s mostly thanks to my unique physique, that’s all.”

“Were you born with that physique?”

“Yes.”

Feng Liqiu suddenly became silent with a ponderous expression on her face.

“If there’s nothing else—”

“Wait, I have one more thing—a request.”

“What kind?”

Feng Liqiu narrowed her eyes and spoke, “Give me some of your blood and Chaotic Essence.”

“What?” Yuan raised an eyebrow, unable to comprehend her request at first.

However, Feng Yao and the Primordial Phoenix reacted with shock, their eyes wide in disbelief. The request clearly struck them, while the Primal Monarch, observing the scene, wore a slight smile, as if amused.

“Hm? Wait a second…” Yuan suddenly had a sense of Deja Vu.

Then, he recalled a certain conversation he had with Lan Yingying.

He swallowed nervously before asking, “That request… you want to conceive with my energy?” She nodded without hesitation.

“What the hell are you thinking?” The Primordial Phoenix suddenly interjected.

“I have been wanting a child for a while now, and I think this is the perfect time.”

“But with a human…? I have never heard of anything like that before! Is that even possible?”

“I guess we’ll find out soon enough!” Feng Liqiu smiled.

“Ahem.” Yuan cleared his throat and said, “It’s definitely possible for beasts to conceive with humans since it’s been done before in my world, but I have no intention of doing something like that with someone I don’t know.”

“But I already told you who I am.” Feng Liqiu said.

“You know what I mean…” Yuan shook his head.

chapters.title: Chapter 1650 Divine Dragon Sanctuary
chapters.body:
Chapter 1650 Divine Dragon Sanctuary

Chapter 1650 Divine Dragon Sanctuary

“Honestly, I don’t really understand.” Feng Liqiu shook her head, and she continued, “Most would be delighted, even honored, if I requested for their Chaotic Essence to conceive my child. Excluding dragons, I cannot imagine anyone refusing.”

Feng Liqiu’s status as the daughter of the Primordial Phoenix alone granted her immense prestige in the Primal Expanse. Her lineage was revered, making her among the most respected in the realm, with many viewing her as nearly untouchable.

Moreover, she was a powerhouse in her own right, standing as one of the strongest Predators in the Primal Expanse. In the entire Eternal Phoenix Domain, her strength was second only to her mother, the Primordial Phoenix. Countless powerful beings had willingly offered their Chaotic Essence to Feng Liqiu over the years without her even needing to ask them, but not a single one of them managed to receive her approval.

Just as human men court women out of love, in the Primal Expanse, beings offer their Chaotic Essence to those they admire to express their desire for a deeper bond and in hopes of conceiving a child with them.

“If I’m going to conceive a child with someone, I’d at least want to get to know them first,” Yuan said.

“Is that a human thing?” Feng Liqiu asked.

“I guess.”

Hearing this, she no longer continued the topic, but she didn’t appear to have given up yet.

“Can you bring me to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary?” Yuan looked at the Primal Monarch.

“Do you have a plan to acquire the treasure from them yet?” The Primordial Phoenix suddenly asked.

He shook his head, “No, I don’t. I’ll figure it out as things progress.”

The Primal Monarch didn’t ask any questions and created a rift that directly led to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary for him.

“The rift will bring you to a place two days away from the Divine Dragon Sanctuary.”

“Okay.” Yuan looked at Yingzi and asked, “Want to come along?”

She immediately nodded. With the Primordial Phoenix and the Primal Monarch’s presence there, the atmosphere was a little too suffocating for someone like her.

“Then I’ll see you again once I acquire the treasure.” Yuan entered the rift the next moment, followed by Yingzi.

The Primal Monarch left shortly after Yuan left, disappearing into thin air.

“Sister, were you serious just now?” Feng Yao looked at Feng Liqiu and asked.

“Of course.”

“But why…?”

“I am curious,” she said with a smile. “What kind of child would be born from a phoenix and a human like him? I want to find out.”

“What do you think, Mother?” Feng Liqiu looked at the Primordial Phoenix to get her opinion on the matter.

“I think that’s a ridiculous reason to conceive your first child with a human.” The Primordial Phoenix shook her head, but she didn’t reject the idea for some reason.

Feng Liqiu noticed this and chuckled, “So you’re also curious, huh? Now I wonder what would happen if a Primordial Phoenix like Mother conceived with a human.”

Hearing this, the Primordial Phoenix smacked Feng Liqiu in the back of the head and sneered, “Don’t get ahead of yourself.”

After emerging from the rift, Yingzi guided Yuan toward the Divine Dragon Sanctuary. Even before they began their journey, Yuan’s eyes were drawn to a towering, pillar-like structure in the distance. The massive tower seemed to stretch endlessly into the heavens, dominating the horizon with its grandeur and presence.

“That must be the Dragon Ascending Mountain. I wanted to visit this place before, but it was too far away.” Yuan recalled his experience as Tian Yi.

Starting from the Eternal Phoenix Domain, the Divine Dragon Sanctuary was located on the far opposite side of the Primal Expanse. Without special treasures, the journey would take an unfathomable amount of time. Even with such treasures, it would have taken Tian Yi over a hundred years to reach the Divine Dragon Sanctuary on his own, which was why he had chosen to ignore it in the past.

“Do you know anything about the Divine Dragon Sanctuary?” Yuan asked Yingzi as they traveled.

“I have never been to this part of the Primal Expanse, but the Divine Dragon Sanctuary is one of the most famous territories in this world.”

“Although it has ‘Dragon’ in its name and many dragons gather there, other races are also allowed to stay in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary,” Yingzi continued. “It’s quite populated and diverse, unlike the Eternal Phoenix Domain, which is more exclusive.”

“Is that so…”

Over the next two days, Yuan pondered how he should go about acquiring the Sacred Chalice of Purification. Unfortunately, with so little information on the Divine Dragon Sanctuary, formulating any reliable plans was pretty much impossible.

In the end, Yuan decided to join the Divine Dragon Sanctuary to learn more about them first before planinng anything.

Two days later, Yuan and Yingzi arrived at the Divine Dragon Sanctuary. Unlike the Eternal Phoenix Domain, where entry was closely guarded, no one approached or attempted to stop them from entering the territory. In fact, Yuan noticed a constant flow of other presences arriving at the territory at the same time, giving the place a more open and welcoming atmosphere. However, Yuan didn’t know where to start.

In order to become affiliated with a territory, one typically required the approval of the territory’s master. However, the Divine Dragon Sanctuary, being one of the largest and most powerful territories in the Primal Expanse, operated differently. It was impractical for one of the strongest beings in the world to handle such insignificant matters, so a dedicated group of individuals was appointed specifically for this purpose.

“Yingzi, can you sense any dragons nearby?” Yuan asked her.

“I sense a few, but none of them have the scent of a true dragon. They probably belong to an inferior bloodline.”

“Can you bring us to the one who seems to be the most reliable?”

“How can I tell if they’re reliable?”

“Uhh… forget it. Just bring us to the closest one.”

“Okay.”

Sometime later, Yuan and Yingzi approached a tall man with striking blue hair. The moment he sensed Yuan’s scent and powerful aura, the man’s eyes widened in surprise and awe.

“W-who are you?”

chapters.title: Chapter 1651 Dragon God Festival
chapters.body:
Chapter 1651 Dragon God Festival

Chapter 1651 Dragon God Festival

“I am Yuan, and I traveled half the world just to come to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary.” Yuan introduced himself.

The blue-haired man swallowed nervously. He could tell just from Yuan’s scent alone that he was in the presence of a true dragon with a powerful bloodline.

“I cannot imagine the effort and time it took you to come here, but I would like to welcome you on behalf of the Divine Dragon Council.” The man said.

“It wasn’t too bad.” Yuan smiled. “Anyway, I came here because—”

Before Yuan could finish his sentence, the man interrupted, “The Dragon God Festival, correct?” “The what?” Yuan raised an eyebrow after hearing this unfamiliar name.

“The Dragon God Festival,” he repeated before asking, “You didn’t come here for the festival? Pretty much every single visitor these days are here for the festival.”

“Sorry, but I came here to visit the Divine Dragon Sanctuary because of its widespread fame,” Yuan shook his head.

“Please, there’s no need for you to apologize. I should be the one apologizing for making such assumptions in the first place. If you have any questions, don’t hesitate to ask me.”

“In that case, can you tell me more about this Dragon God Festival?”

The man nodded.

“The Dragon God Festival is a tradition in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary that happens only once every twenty-five million years. During the festival, the participants compete against each other for the title of Dragon God Successor. Naturally, since this is related to the dragon race, only dragons are allowed to participate. However, there’s some leniency. Even those with inferior or incomplete dragon blood are permitted to join.”

“Every 25 million years…?” Yuan wore a dumbfounded expression on his face after hearing this information.

He then asked, “What kind of competitions occur during the festival?”

“I’m sorry, but I don’t know,” the blue-haired man replied, shaking his head. “The trials are decided by the Dragon God, and they change with every festival. We only learn the details of the trials when the festival is about to begin.” “When does the festival begin?”

“Exactly two years from now, so very soon.”

“…” Yuan was speechless.

‘Since the festival takes place every 25 million years, I guess two years is nothing in their eyes…’ he sighed inwardly.

“Is there anything to be gained from this festival?” Yuan asked a moment later.

“Of course! Not only will you become the Dragon God’s Successor if you win the festival, but the Dragon God will even grant you any one wish!” The man replied, his voice filled with excitement.

“Any wish…?” Yuan repeated as he mused to himself.

The man nodded, “That’s right. The Dragon God is one of the most powerful beings in the Primal Expanse. He can fulfill almost any wish.”

‘I must be fated to participate in this festival…’ Yuan sighed inwardly.

If he won the festival, he could wish for the Sacred Chalice of Purification.

“Can I participate in the festival?” he asked a moment later.

“Of course. As long as you are related to the dragon race, you can sign up for the festival.”

“Are there any other requirements? For example, do I have to be affiliated with the Divine Dragon Sanctuary?”

“No, the festival is open to everyone as long as they meet the requirements.”

“That’s great. Where do I sign up?”

The man pointed to the Dragon Ascending Mountain and said, “Do you see that tower? You can sign up there.” “Thank you very much.” Yuan and Yingzi made their way to the tower shortly after.

‘Now that I’ve decided to participate in the festival, I have to wait two years for it to begin…’

Yuan pondered what he should do in the meantime while they approached the Dragon Ascending Mountain.

Sometime later, Yuan and Yingzi reached Dragon Ascending Mountain, but by the time they got there, tens of thousands of beings were already lined up.

Once they joined the queue, Yuan took a moment to inspect the towering structure ahead. The Dragon Ascending Mountain was, in reality, just an incredibly tall pillar stretching into the sky. There was an entrance at the base of the tower, but Yuan couldn’t quite imagine what lay inside or what purpose it served. He tried looking inside using his divine sense, but an invisible force prevented him from peeking inside.

“Hey.” Amid his thoughts, Yuan suddenly felt someone tugging at his clothes.

“Hm?”

He turned to look at Yingzi, who was staring at him.

“I want to eat the rest of my meat skewers,” she said.

“Alright.”

Yuan pulled out a handful of meat skewers and handed them to Yingzi. The rich, savory scent of the skewers immediately filled the air, catching the attention of everyone in line and even those nearby. Heads turned, and whispers spread as the aroma quickly became the center of attention.

“What is that smell? My mouth became watery after a single whiff!” “It’s coming from the back of the queue!”

“Look at those meat on sticks in that little girl’s hands! What kind of meat is that?! I’ve never smelled anything so delicious before!” someone exclaimed, their voice filled with astonishment.

The crowd murmured in agreement, their eyes fixed on the food in Yingzi’s grasp.

“Hey! What the fuck are you trying to pull here?!”

An irritated voice suddenly roared, cutting through the chatter. Yuan shifted his gaze to the man standing directly in front of them in the queue. The man’s expression was tense, clearly agitated by something, and his eyes flicked toward Yuan and Yingzi with clear frustration.

“What do you mean?” Yuan calmly inquired.

“You know what I mean! Why would you pull out something like that without considering your place and situation?! Or are you trying to start something on purpose?!”

“It’s just food,” Yuan said with a seemingly innocent expression on his face.

“Well, it’s causing a commotion! Unless you share it with the rest of us, get it out of our faces!”

“What kind of logic is that? I don’t see why we need to share it with you.” Yuan shook his head and said, “You’re the one causing a commotion here. Anyway, if it bothers you that much, feel free to take the meat skewers away from her. I won’t stop you.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1652 Dragon Princess Meihui
chapters.body:
Chapter 1652 Dragon Princess Meihui

Chapter 1652 Dragon Princess Meihui

The man glanced at Yingzi, only to be met with a fierce, piercing glare that sent chills through his entire body. Instinctively, he knew that if he dared to make a move for the meat skewers in her hands, it would lead to a swift and terrible demise. ‘Her lack of presence… is she a Shadow Dweller?’ The onlookers pondered Yingzi’s identity.

After chewing and swallowing the meat in her mouth, Yingzi spoke out loud, “I’m willing to exchange my food.”

“Really?” The man was pleasantly surprised, and his opinions of Shadow Dwellers slightly improved.

However, Yingzi continued, “In exchange for one skewer, I will have your life.”

“…”

Not just the man before her but even Yuan and the other onlookers reacted with bafflement.

“Y-you crazy little bitch! I’m gonna fucking kill you!” The man roared with an enraged face, and his aura surged with bloodlust. However, right as the man raised his hand, another voice boomed.

“What is going on here?! Who dares to cause a commotion right before the Dragon God Festival?! I’ll disqualify you on the spot!”

Everyone there turned to look at the approaching figure. She was a tall beauty with a strict face and an authoritative air around her.

“Dragon Princess Meihui!”

The people there lowered their heads when they recognized her identity.

Yuan looked at her with interest.

‘In terms of prowess, she seems to be on par with Feng Liqiu.’

“So, what is the problem here?” Dragon Princess Meihui glared at Yuan and the man who exuded bloodlust with a deep frown on her face.

However, if one looked closely, one would see her eyes glancing at the meat skewers in Yingzi’s grasp every few seconds.

Dragon Princess Meihui would never admit it, but she couldn’t care less about their insignificant drama. What prompted her to show up in the first place was in Yingzi’s grasp.

“Dragon Princess, we were quietly waiting in line when this unreasonable fellow in front of us suddenly became aggressive.” Yuan was the first to speak up, and he directly placed all blame on the man.

“D-don’t give me that bullshit! You were the ones who provoked us with your food!” The man hastily refuted.

Yuan shook his head and said, “How did we provoke you with our food? I don’t recall dangling them in front of your nose and provoking you. Or were you so enticed by the scent that you became offended?”

“Either way, my friend was minding her business when you started yapping about sharing our food without providing any logical reason, and then you got angry when she refused.”

Yuan turned to look at the Dragon Princess with an apologetic smile, “Although it wasn’t our intention, it is true that our food was the main cause for the commotion. I hope you can forgive us.”

Before the Dragon Princess could even open her mouth to respond, Yuan retrieved a handful of meat skewers and shoved them toward her.

“Please accept our apology.”

Silence filled the atmosphere while the onlookers were shocked by the implications behind Yuan’s actions.

‘That bastard has the audacity to bribe the Dragon Princess so openly?!’

The Dragon Princess stared at the meat skewers with a somewhat dazed face.

When she snapped out of it a moment later, she cleared her throat and spoke, “Is that so? Don’t worry about it. Since eating here is not a crime, you are not responsible for what happened.”

She turned to look at the man with narrowed eyes and continued, “I will only give you a warning. If you continue to make a scene, I will disqualify you from the festival.”

“I understand! I will not disappoint the Princess a second time!” The man didn’t dare to argue back and responded with his head lowered.

“Then I will take my leave.”

Just as the Dragon Princess turned to leave, Yuan spoke again, his tone calm but firm. “You haven’t accepted my apology yet, Princess.”

He was referring to the meat skewers that were still in his grasp.

The Dragon Princess instantly halted her movements, but her eyebrows were twitching.

“If you really don’t want it, I’ll take them back—”

As Yuan began pulling his arms back, she turned back to Yuan and swiftly accepted the meat skewers.

“If you insist, I will accept it,” she said in a somewhat bashful voice.

After accepting the skewers, the Dragon Princess wasted no time and immediately took a bite. The moment the flavors hit her tongue, her eyes widened in surprise and delight, clearly caught off guard by how delicious the food was.

“Oh my, this is so delicious! What kind of meat is this?” The Dragon Princess inquired after swallowing.

“Chicken.”

“What is that?”

The Dragon Princess raised an eyebrow in puzzlement as she moved on to the next skewer.

“It’s a kind of bird.”

“Like phoenixes?”

“No, but I guess they’re pretty similar.”

“I have eaten phoenixes before, but they don’t taste anything like this.” The Dragon Princess shook her head.

Before she realized it, the Dragon Princess had finished all the meat skewers, and a wave of disappointment washed over her. She hadn’t expected to enjoy them so much, and now that they were gone, she found herself wishing for more. Her expression softened slightly, though she tried to maintain her composure.

She turned to look at Yingzi, who was slowly enjoying the skewers, and sighed inwardly, ‘I should’ve slowly savored every bite, but alas…’

Then she shifted her gaze to Yuan’s handsome face.

After a moment of silence, she cleared her throat and said, “Thank you for the food. It was amazing. Can I get your name?”

“I am Yuan.”

“Then, Yuan, I’ll give you this as a thank you.”

She suddenly gestured toward the people working at the front of the line. Noticing her signal, one of them quickly rushed over, holding a small token in his hands.

“This token verifies that you are a participant in the Dragon God Festival,” Dragon Princess Meihui grabbed the token and handed it to Yuan, “With this, you won’t need to wait in line anymore.”

Yuan gladly accepted the token.

“Thank you!”

chapters.title: Chapter 1653 Divine Dragon Council
chapters.body:
Chapter 1653 Divine Dragon Council

Chapter 1653 Divine Dragon Council

“Then I’ll be on my way now.”

After Yuan had been given the token, Dragon Princess Meihui quickly left the scene.

There were two major reasons why Dragon Princess Meihui gave Yuan the token, allowing him to skip the assessment entirely. The first reason was simple: she could tell by Yuan’s dragon scent that he was more than qualified to participate, making the assessment process unnecessary and a waste of time.

The second reason was because of the meat skewers. If the others in line were forced to endure the enticing aroma for too long, it would likely lead to another commotion. By allowing Yuan to leave early, they would avoid any further disruptions caused by the irresistible scent of the skewers.

With the token in his grasp, Yuan and Yingzi left the scene, disappearing into the distance.

“I guess I don’t need to worry about being affiliated with the Divine Dragon Sanctuary for now.” Yuan tossed the token into his spatial ring.

“What are you going to do for the next two years?” Yingzi asked him.

“Hmm…”

Yuan pondered for several minutes before responding, “If the Divine Dragon Sanctuary is as powerful as the Eternal Phoenix Domain, they should have training areas like the Nine Levels of Hell, right? I should take this time to strengthen myself further and prepare for the festival.”

“But will they allow someone who isn’t affiliated with their territory to use these training spots?” Yingzi mused.

“Let’s go ask that blue-haired dragon.”

Yingzi and Yuan made their way back to the blue-haired dragon, who’d barely moved from his spot since they left.

“Hm? You’re back already? Did something happen?” The blue-haired man asked them with a puzzled face since it’d been less than two hours since they left.

Yuan shook his head and explained, “No, I have the participation token in my possession. I returned because I have some more questions.”

“Already? That place is usually crowded, and it normally takes several weeks to acquire a token unless you skipped the queue,” the man remarked, his expression filled with surprise.

“One thing led to another, and I somehow managed to skip the line.” Yuan smiled.

“So something did happen…”

“By the way, why are you still here?”

“Oh, I am something like a guide. It’s my job to assist visitors like you. There are many first-time visitors because of the Dragon God Festival, after all.”

“I see…”

“Anyway, you have some questions, right? I’ll try my best to answer them.”

“I’m looking for something to do while I wait for the Dragon God Festival. Are there any training areas available in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary?” Yuan asked.

“Training grounds, huh? There are several, but they are only accessible by those affiliated with the Divine Dragon Sanctuary.”

“How does one become affiliated?”

“You must visit the Divine Dragon Council and pass several assessments. Once you become an affiliate, you will essentially become part of the Divine Dragon Sanctuary’s prowess. For example, if our territory is ever threatened, you are obligated to aid us.”

“Can I ask about the assessments? What do they ask for?”

“The Divine Dragon Council will ask about your background to determine whether you are qualified to become an affiliate.”

“So there’s a chance I won’t qualify, huh?”

“Yes, but I have no reason to believe why you wouldn’t qualify. In fact, you’re pretty much guaranteed to qualify because you belong to the dragon race.”

“I see. Where is the Divine Dragon Council?” In response to Yuan’s question, the blue-haired man retrieved a white stone and said, “This will bring you directly to the Divine Dragon Council.”

“This…?” Yuan accepted the white stone and inspected it with interest.

“You’ve never seen a teleport stone before?” The man inquired, sounding somewhat surprised.

“Uhh, no. They don’t exist where I am from.”

“I guess that’s not too surprising since they’re unique within the Divine Dragon Sancutary. These teleport stones are created by the Void Dragon, who has the ability to teleport. If you shatter that stone, you will create a rift that will directly bring you to the Divine Dragon Council.”

Sometime later, Yuan shattered the teleport stone, releasing the energy stored within. The energy surged out, swirling in the air before forming a rift in front of him.

“The rift will disappear after ten seconds.” The man quickly warned him.

“Thanks for your help again.” Yuan said to him before jumping into the rift, followed by Yingzi.

Upon emerging from the rift, Yuan found himself standing before a majestic pagoda, its intricate design far surpassing the one he had seen at Phoenix Ascendancy Plateau. The towering structure was adorned with elaborate carvings and shimmering details, exuding an air of ancient power and significance. Its grandeur made it clear that this was a place of great importance.

Just like the Dragon Ascending Mountain, there was a huge line waiting to go inside the pagoda.

‘Fortunately, the line isn’t as long… Moreover, it’s moving much faster,’ Yuan thought to himself as he entered the back of the line.

As they waited in line, Yuan listened to the conversations around them. “Hopefully I qualify as a Divine Dragon Warrior. That would make life much easier in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary.”

“Unless you are a dragon or have a Master-level bloodline, it’s near impossible to become a Divine Dragon Warrior.” ƒreewebɳovel.com

“I’ll be satisfied with just being affiliated with the Divine Dragon Sanctuary since having a higher rank also means having more responsibility.”

“So what if I have more responsibility? Only Divine Dragon Warriors are allowed to use their training grounds, and that is my sole reason for coming here!”

After overhearing the nearby conversation, Yuan began to wonder if being affiliated with the Divine Dragon Sanctuary was similar to joining a faction in the Nine Heavens since there appeared to be ranks amongst those affiliated.

‘I need to become at least a Divine Dragon Warrior to use their training grounds, huh?’ Yuan mused inwardly.

Sometime later, he looked at Yingzi and asked, “Are you also going to become an affiliate?”

chapters.title: Chapter 1654 Divine Dragon Council(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1654 Divine Dragon Council(2)

Chapter 1654 Divine Dragon Council(2)

“I don’t really have a reason to join the Divine Dragon Sanctuary.” Yingzi shook her head.

Most people join the Divine Dragon Sanctuary for one of two reasons. Some sought access to the powerful training grounds available within the territory, hoping to grow stronger. Others join to use the Divine Dragon Sanctuary’s prestigious name as a shield, gaining protection and influence under its banner. “I do not care about their training grounds, and I don’t need their protection, so I don’t really benefit from joining them. However, if you were to remain in the Primal Expanse forever, I wouldn’t mind joining them with you.”

Yuan smiled at her words and said, “Unfortunately, I must return to my home soon.”

“I know. That’s why I won’t join them. Also, I am what most consider free-spirited. I don’t wish to be constrained by their rules. It won’t matter to you who will be leaving soon, but it’s a big deal for those living in the Primal Expanse.”

“I see…”

After a moment of silence, Yuan spoke again, “This just crossed my mind, but do Shadow Dwellers also have their own territory?”

She nodded, “We do. It’s a small and remote territory somewhere in the Hollowed Expanse, where sunlight doesn’t exist.”

Yuan’s eyes widened at this information, and he said, “I know of the Hollowed Expanse! But I never got the chance to see it for myself for the same reasons I didn’t visit the Divine Dragon Sanctuary.”

The Primal Expanse was composed of many smaller regions, each with its own distinct environment, power structures, and unique inhabitants. These regions varied greatly, from fiery domains to shadowy realms,

One such region was the Hollowed Expanse, a world shrouded in eternal darkness, where no sunlight ever touched the land. The creatures and powers that thrived there were adapted to the shadows, such as the Shadow Dwellers.

Since Shadow Dwellers are born from shadows, most of them originated from the Hollowed Expanse. In this realm of eternal darkness, the lack of sunlight and the pervasive gloom created the perfect conditions for these beings to come into existence. “What kind of place is the Hallowed Expanse?” Yuan asked a moment later. freeweɓnøvel.com

Yingzi recalled her memories of the Hollowed Expanse and said, “It’s a gloomy place, with hardly any living trees and plants. The land is mostly sand and dirt, and not many Primals venture there. If we want food, we have to leave the region.” Based on Yingzi’s description, Yuan imagined the Hollowed Expanse as a vast desert, barren and desolate, where it was always nighttime.

“That’s why I left the Hallowed Expanse in the first place, even if it means weakening myself,” Yingzi continued.

Since shadow and darkness were the sources of Shadow Dwellers’ power, they preferred to stay in the darkest places, where their abilities were at their peak. For Yingzi, leaving the Hollowed Expanse, the darkest place in the Primal Expanse, significantly weakened her. The line moved quickly as they chatted, and before Yuan realized it, they had reached the front, now standing directly in front of the towering pagoda. Whenever someone exited the pagoda, it was immediately clear whether they had succeeded or not. Those who had a badge pinned to their chest wore expressions of pride or relief, while those without a badge left empty-handed, often looking disappointed or frustrated. Eventually, it was Yuan’s turn.

Noticing the woman standing by the entrance beckoning toward him, Yuan stepped forward and approached the pagoda.

“You can wait for me out here,” Yuan said to Yingzi as he left.

Sometime later, Yuan entered the first floor of the pagoda. The interior was modestly decorated, with minimal furnishings. At the far end of the room, a young man sat behind a desk, his posture relaxed.

When the young man noticed Yuan’s scent, he raised an eyebrow and asked in a surprised tone, “What dragon bloodline do you have?”

‘My dragon bloodline…?’ Yuan swallowed nervously. Fortunately, he’d prepared for this question.

“I’m an Azure Dragon.”

When Yuan considered his identity as a dragon, his first thought was to claim he hailed from the Xi Family’s heritage. However, he quickly realized that he had never actually learned the name of their bloodline. In the end, he used the Azure Dragon’s name. “An Azure Dragon? That can’t be right,” the young man replied, his brow furrowing in doubt. “I can tell by your scent alone that you come from a much more prestigious bloodline. Are you trying to hide your identity? Why? If you want to become affiliated with our Divine Dragon Sanctuary, you must tell us the truth. We don’t accept those with ulterior motives.”

‘I didn’t think he would see through me so easily…’ Yuan sighed inwardly.

He responded a moment later, “Actually, I don’t know my race. I was abandoned when I was young, and the ones who accepted and raised me were Azure Dragons.”

The young man narrowed his gaze at Yuan.

“Where are you from?”

“I’m from a remote and insignificant territory located on the other side of the Primal Expanse,” Yuan revealed, his tone calm and sincere.

“What is your reason for wanting to join our Divine Dragon Sanctuary?”

“My parents have always spoken highly of the Divine Dragon Sanctuary even though they’ve never been here,” Yuan swiftly responded. “They also recommended that I come here once they are no longer in this world.”

‘What a load of bullshit…’ the young man sneered inwardly at Yuan’s excuses.

However, even though he knew that Yuan wasn’t being truthful, he couldn’t ignore Yuan’s powerful bloodline.

‘I don’t know what he’s planning, but we can figure out his true identity through the assessment.’

Sometime later, the young man smiled at Yuan and said, “Alright, you pass. Moreover, I believe you have the potential to become someone greater than a Divine Dragon Warrior, so I’m going to give you a chance.”

“Head to the second floor and take the assessment there. If you pass, you’ll become a Divine Dragon Commander instead of a mere Divine Dragon Warrior. Be proud. You’re only the fourth to be sent to the second floor in the last thousand years.”

Join Privilege for VIP Chaps!

chapters.title: Chapter 1655 Divine Dragon Council(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1655 Divine Dragon Council(3)

Chapter 1655 Divine Dragon Council(3)

‘Huh? That’s it?’ Yuan thought, surprised by how easy the process had been. He had expected the assessment to be much more difficult and strict, but in reality, it had turned out to be far simpler than he had anticipated.

“Then I’ll head to the second floor now.” Yuan gave the young man a slight bow before approaching the spiraling staircase by the wall.

Several moments later, Yuan stepped onto the second floor. The layout and decorations were almost identical to those on the first floor. At the other end of the room, a middle-aged man was slumped over a desk, seemingly asleep. His posture was relaxed, and his soft, rhythmic breathing filled the otherwise quiet room.

Yuan didn’t say anything and approached the desk.

Suddenly, without opening his eyes, the middle-aged man spoke, “It’s been two hundred years since someone was sent up here.”

A moment later, the man slowly opened his eyes and sat up, his calm gaze immediately locking onto Yuan. “Hmm. I see why you were sent up here. You have a strong bloodline.”

“Thank you.”

The middle-aged man then spoke, “In order to qualify as a Divine Dragon Commander, you must demonstrate a certain level of prowess, and your bloodline must be at least Commander quality.” In the Primal Expanse, bloodlines are ranked in the following order: Primal, Master, Commander, Emperor, and Monarch. Each rank signifies a major increase in potential and influence. Monarch-quality bloodlines sit at the very pinnacle, and only supreme beings like the Dragon God, Primordial Phoenix, and Primal Monarch possess such powerful bloodlines. The middle-aged man retrieved a finger-sized needle and tossed it to Yuan.

“Feed it your blood,” he instructed.

Yuan didn’t act right away. Instead, he stared at the needle in silence for a moment, as there was a good chance this would expose his identity.

The next moment, Yuan pricked his finger with the needle, and it immediately absorbed his blood.

09:54

“What are you thinking so deeply about?” The middle-aged man suddenly inquired when he noticed Yuan’s hesitancy.

“Nothing important. It’s just that I’ve never seen anything like this before and was wondering what it does.” Yuan said with an innocent smile.

“Where did you come from to have never seen that before? It tests the quality of your bloodline.” The middle-aged man briefly explained.

“I see…”

The next moment, Yuan pricked his finger with the needle, and it immediately absorbed his blood.

Once the needle had consumed enough of Yuan’s blood, it began to glow faintly with a green light. In a matter of moments, the color shifted rapidly from green to blue, then to red, before finally settling into a glistening silver. The middle-aged man’s eyes widened with shock.

“Q-quickly! Let me see it!” He hastily urged.

Yuan nodded and gave the needle to him, who inspected it with a serious expression on his face.

“My eyes are not deceiving me! You actually have an Emperor-quality bloodline!?” The middle-aged man stood up and shifted his gaze from the needle to Yuan, his eyes burning with excitement.

“What sort of bloodline do you have?!”

Yuan was inwardly relieved that the needle only revealed the quality of his bloodline and nothing more.

“Uhh… I don’t know,” he responded a moment later.

“What do you mean by that?” “I grew up without my parents.”

“Is that so?” the middle-aged man muttered, his tone laced with skepticism as he eyed the glowing needle. While he clearly doubted Yuan’s background, he didn’t outright deny it either. After all, given how chaotic the Primal Expanse was, Yuan’s claim was entirely possible—even common. In a realm where powerful bloodlines often mingled and mysterious origins were not unheard of, the idea of someone like Yuan emerging from obscurity wasn’t out of the question.

“Alright, you pass this assessment. Congratulations, you’re the second to pass the second floor in the last thousand years.”

“What about assessing my prowess?” Yuan asked.

“You’ll be doing that on the next floor. Although your cultivation is lacking, I don’t doubt you have immense potential as someone with an Emperor-quality bloodline, so there’s a very good chance you’ll qualify as a Divine Dragon Commander even if you fail the next assessment.” “Alright. Then I’ll head to the next floor now.” Shortly after Yuan left the second floor, the middle-aged man noticed the needle was still glowing.

“That’s strange. Why is it still active?”

Right as he muttered these words, the needle abruptly changed colors again.

“T-this is—?!” he gasped, his face pale with shock. He dropped the needle and stumbled backward, falling onto his buttocks.

The silver needle was now glowing a brilliant golden color.

“A golden glow…” he whispered in disbelief, fully aware of the significance of such a sight.

“No… this can’t be possible!”

The middle-aged man shook his head in disbelief before staring at the golden needle for a moment longer. Then, he pulled out something that worked like a communication jade slip and started speaking into it.

Meanwhile, Yuan had just reached the third floor, only to find it completely empty except for two words painted on the wall that read, “Be patient.”

After seeing the words, Yuan patiently waited for the interviewer to show up.

‘There’s a formation in the center of the room.’ Yu Ning suddenly notified him.

She continued after examining it, ‘It appears to be a teleport formation.’

Yuan smiled as he sat on the floor and patiently waited for the interviewer to show up. Unbeknownst to Yuan, the Divine Dragon Sanctuary was in a commotion, stirred by a piece of specific information that spread like wildfire amongst the upper ranks.

“What? A new descendant of the Dragon God showed up?”

“How is that even possible? The Dragon God should only have three descendants!”

“Perhaps he conceived another one in secret.”

“That’s highly unlikely.”

“Where’s this supposed fourth descendant?”

“According to Councillor Luo, he’s currently on the third floor of the Divine Dragon Hall.”

“How long has it been since someone made it to the third floor?”

“Who’s going to do the assessment?”

“I heard the interviewer has already been decided.”

About fifteen minutes had passed since Yuan began waiting for his interviewer to show up when the teleportation formation suddenly flickered to life, and a handsome young man with a dignified and commanding presence emerged from it.

chapters.title: Chapter 1656 Long Yejun
chapters.body:
Chapter 1656 Long Yejun

Chapter 1656 Long Yejun

After appearing on the third floor, the handsome young man approached Yuan with a calm smile, “Hello. I hope you didn’t wait too long.”

Yuan quickly stood up and said, “No, I didn’t.”

The young man then introduced himself, “I am Long Yejun, and I will be your interviewer. Before we begin, can you tell me about yourself?”

“I’m Yuan, and I traveled here from the other side of the Primal Expanse. I grew up in a small territory with two Azure Dragons, who I consider my parents. Unfortunately, I don’t know my real parents.”

“I see…” Long Yejun mumbled with a ponderous face.

Sometime later, he looked at Yuan with a serious expression and said, “To be honest, based on the assessment from the second floor, we believe you may have a connection to the Dragon God.”

“What?” Yuan’s eyes widened with surprise after hearing this unexpected news.

“When you say Dragon God, do you mean the master of the Divine Dragon Sanctuary—that Dragon God?”

Long Yejun nodded, “Indeed.”

Yuan wore a dazed look on his face as he pondered how such a thing was possible inwardly.

‘I may be related to the Dragon God? That’s ridiculous. I don’t recall ever meeting him even as Tian Yi, so how is it possible that we’re related?’

Long Yejun stared intently at Yuan’s face, carefully analyzing every detail as if searching for something hidden beneath the surface.

‘He’s genuinely surprised by the information. Maybe he really has no idea that he has the Dragon God’s blood within him, and it was pure coincidence that he came to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary…’ he wondered inwardly.

After a brief moment of silence, Yuan’s expression shifted, as though a sudden realization had dawned on him.

‘The Blood Cleansing Ceremony!’

He gained the ability to transform after the Blood Cleansing Ceremony at the Ancient Dragon City, and the Xi Family claimed they were unaware of the origin of the blood he absorbed. However, the system had confirmed that the blood belonged to the Dragon God.

“That expression… did you remember something?” Long Yejun inquired with a slight smile on his face.

Before Yuan could respond, he continued, “Anyway, I am not here to question your bloodline. We can do that after the assessment.”

“For this assessment, I will be evaluating your combat prowess. The most effective way to do that is through a sparring match with you.”

Long Yejun suddenly suppressed his cultivation to Perfect Chaotic Master, matching Yuan’s cultivation.

“You will pass this assessment if you can injure me even slightly,” he said afterward.

“Are you sure?” Yuan asked. “Yes. And take as much time as you need to prepare.”

“…”

Yuan didn’t say anything else and became silent.

‘How should I go about this?’ he wondered inwardly.

He could easily injure this confident interviewer with Number One Under Heaven, but he was pretending to be a dragon, and very few beings in the Primal Expanse used weapons. Moreover, he wanted to test out something.

Sometime later, Yuan said, “Alright, I am ready.”

Long Yejun beckoned with a provocative gesture, saying, “Show me what you’ve got.”

In the next instant, Yuan sprang from the ground, hurtling toward Long Yejun with incredible speed.

[Relentless Dragon Overwhelms the Nine Heavens!]

As Yuan activated his fist technique, he channeled his Chaotic Essence into it, amplifying its power several times over.

Long Yejun’s eyes widened as Yuan’s aura suddenly erupted with overwhelming force. Instinctively, he leaped back, attempting to dodge the oncoming strike.

However, Yuan suddenly activated Dragon’s Gaze, catching Long Yejun completely off guard. The technique paralyzed him for a split second, leaving him vulnerable.

“F—”

Long Yejun couldn’t even utter a word before Yuan’s fist, engulfed in a black aura that resembled flames, slammed into his gut, launching him across the room. His body crashed into the wall with such force that the entire building trembled. If not for the defensive formations reinforcing the structure, his impact would have shattered through the wall entirely.

Despite the brutal blow, Long Yejun somehow managed to land on his feet, his expression grim but composed as he steadied himself.

He took a deep breath, then forcefully blew his nose, expelling a fair amount of blood.

Long Yejun stared at his own blood on the ground, then chuckled with excitement. “When was the last time I saw my own blood…? Hahaha!” he laughed, a wild glint in his eyes.

“Does this mean I pass the assessment?” Yuan asked.

Long Yejun stopped laughing to look at him with a solemn gaze.

“No,” he responded.

“But you—”

“That wasn’t enough to injure me.”

Yuan didn’t say anything and merely looked at the blood on the floor.

“Ahem!” Long Yejun awkwardly cleared his throat and explained, “I was training before I came here, and that blood was accumulated then.”

Yuan shook his head inwardly and responded with a bittersweet smile, “I understand. Let’s continue, then.”

A satisfied smile appeared on Long Yejun’s face as he settled into a combative stance, his eyes gleaming with anticipation.

After a brief moment of stillness, Long Yejun made the first move, using a movement technique to instantly get in front of Yuan.

Yuan reacted swiftly, but as he raised his arms to defend, Long Yejun’s eyes gleamed with a golden light.

‘Dragon’s Gaze?!’

Yuan was caught off guard by Long Yejun’s Dragon’s Gaze and became momentarily stunned.

“Payback time!” Long Yejun laughed as he drove his fist into Yuan’s stomach, precisely where he had been struck moments earlier.

Yuan was instantly sent flying, crashing into the wall with even greater force than when he had struck Long Yejun. The impact reverberated through the room, shaking the entire building.

Yet, it was Long Yejun who bore the brunt of the damage from the exchange. His hand was bruised and slightly bleeding, while Yuan emerged seemingly unscathed. “Fuck! What is your body made of?!” Long Yejun cried out, shaking his hand in an attempt to dispel the pain, disbelief written across his face.

chapters.title: Chapter 1657 Endless Arena
chapters.body:
Chapter 1657 Endless Arena

Chapter 1657 Endless Arena

Although Yuan appeared unscathed on the outside, he hadn’t emerged from the exchange completely uninjured. Beneath his calm exterior, the impact had taken a slight toll on his body, but his regenerative ability swiftly healed the damage, allowing him to recover almost instantly.

‘I cannot underestimate this man. Not only did he penetrate my defenses with his bare fists, but he’s even restricted his cultivation to my level.’ Yuan stared at Long Yejun with a sharp gaze.

Unbeknownst to him, an excited smile crept onto his face, giving him a fierce and almost vicious appearance.

When Long Yejun saw Yuan’s expression, he also grinned excitedly, “I see! So you’re just like me! This is going to be fun! Hahaha!”

“However, this place is too small and restrictive for us to really go all out.”

Long Yejun tossed a teleport stone to Yuan and said, “Let’s change our location to somewhere more suitable.”

Before Yuan could even respond, Long Yejun crushed his own teleport stone and disappeared from the room.

Yuan didn’t think too much about it and also crushed the teleport stone the next moment.

After being teleported, Yuan found himself in a vast land that seemed to have been artificially flattened. The ground was smooth and level, resembling the surface of a grand arena prepared for battle, and it stretched endlessly toward the horizon, its sheer size almost overwhelming. freēwebnovel.com

“Welcome to the Endless Arena,” Long Yejun said, gesturing to the vast expanse around them. “This is the largest arena in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary, spanning over 100,000 miles. We won’t have to worry about slamming into walls and whatnot here.”

“Whenever you’re ready.” Long Yejun gestured to Yuan a moment later.

After a moment of stillness, Yuan made the first move. However, this time, Long Yejun had no intention of letting Yuan land a hit so easily and swiftly countered with a move of his own.

When they clashed, a powerful ripple surged through the arena, as if two colossal mountains had collided, sending shockwaves across the vast expanse and shaking the ground beneath them.

Each of their punches was so powerful that it generated a fierce gust of wind, while every kick unleashed small windstorms, the sheer force of their battle reshaping the air around them.

In addition to their fists and legs, they also unleashed Dragon’s Gaze on each other whenever possible. However, now that they were both fully aware of each other’s capabilities, neither of them was caught off guard, skillfully countering the other’s Dragon’s Gaze with their own.

‘How is he canceling out my Dragon’s Authority?!’ Long Yejun thought in disbelief. ‘Even though I’ve suppressed my cultivation, my soul strength should still be vastly superior!’

Meanwhile, Yuan was focusing all his efforts on breaking through Long Yejun’s mental defenses, but no matter how hard he tried, he found himself unable to penetrate them.

‘We’re pretty evenly matched at this moment, but neither of us is truly going all out…’

Since it was rare for Yuan to encounter an opponent equal in strength, he took this moment to savor the fight. In fact, he had already forgotten about the assessment entirely, lost in the thrill of the battle.

Long Yejun felt the same way. Even if he suppressed his cultivation to match his opponent, only a handful of people in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary could even land a hit on him, let alone rival him in strength.

“What was your name again?” Long Yejun suddenly asked.

“Yuan.”

“Allow me to introduce myself again! I am Long Yejun, a descendant of the Dragon God! Since you also have the Dragon God’s blood in you, that makes you my younger brother!”

“How many children does the Dragon God have?” Yuan asked as they continued fighting.

“We always thought there were only three of us, but with your sudden appearance, there are now four of us. I am the second eldest.”

“The second eldest, huh? No wonder why you’re so strong!” Yuan laughed out loud.

“If I am strong, when what are you, the youngest? Your cultivation may be low, but your prowess does not match your cultivation in the slightest, being several realms stronger! Just to fight with you on an equal footing, I had to increase my cultivation to Intermediate Chaotic Commander!” Long Yejun admitted that he’d increased his cultivation just to match Yuan’s prowess.

As Yuan and Long Yejun continued their intense battle, nearby beings began to take notice. Given the vast size of the Endless Arena, it was common for multiple individuals to use it simultaneously, typically without paying attention to others. However, the sheer ferocity of Yuan and Long Yejun’s fight was impossible to ignore, compelling onlookers to steal glances and witness the spectacle unfolding before them.

“Huh? Isn’t that Prince Yejun? Who is his opponent? I don’t recognize him.”

“I don’t recognize him, but he’s fighting with Prince Yejun on equal footing while having a lower cultivation! What in Dragon God’s name is going on?!” One of the onlookers exclaimed in disbelief, unable to comprehend how someone could rival Long Yejun, a descendant of the Dragon God.

“Wait a second. I heard rumors about a fourth descendant showing up very recently. Is that him?”

“The Dragon God’s fourth descendant looks extremely promising! He might even rival the eldest—that tyrant!”

“Impossible. Nobody can rival that tyrant, who is the closest to reaching the Dragon God’s prowess.”

“Anyway, Prince Yejun is obviously holding back. If he became serious, the Fourth won’t be able to fight back.”

“I wouldn’t be so sure about that… It seems to me the Fourth is also holding back.”

“Time will tell.”

More and more people began showing up at the Endless Arena to watch Yuan and Long Yejun’s fight once word of it spread.

As the fight progressed, both Yuan and Long Yejun grew more serious, gradually revealing more of their true strength. “That should be enough for a warm-up. Let’s get serious now.” Long Yejun suddenly stopped fighting and said. “Fine by me.” Yuan nodded, agreeing with him.

chapters.title: Chapter 1658 Dragon God’S Descendant
chapters.body:
Chapter 1658 Dragon God’S Descendant

Chapter 1658 Dragon God’s Descendant

Yuan and Long Yejun silently stared at each other, seeming waiting for the other to act first.

“If you’re not going to move first, then allow me to!” Long Yejun suddenly began transforming. His aura erupted like a volcano, and scales began appearing all over his body.

Furthermore, a golden mask that was shaped like a dragon’s face materialized on Long Yejun’s face, giving him a fearsome presence.

Seeing this, Yuan used God of War’s Astral Arts, but he restricted the avatar’s size to be only slightly larger than his own body.

“What is that…?” Long Yejun murmured, staring at the avatar with awe and intrigue, as he’d never seen a technique like that before.

It was not just Long Yejun. The onlookers were also baffled by God of War’s Astral Arts, a unique technique unlike anything they’ve seen before.

“Why are you just standing there? Let’s go!” Yuan made the first move a moment later when Long Yejun only stood there in silence.

Long Yejun quickly snapped out of his daze to block Yuan’s strike before countering with his own.

Long Yejun’s speed and power had significantly increased since his transformation. As for Yuan, not only did he become stronger, but he also grew another pair of arms, allowing him to attack more frequently.

After their power-up, each of their punches and kicks would create powerful windstorms and cause violent earthquakes.

“What is going on here?” A new onlooker showed up after being attracted by the commotion.

“Princess Meihui!” The others bowed to her after recognizing her.

“The Dragon Prince is currently sparring with the fourth Dragon God Descendant.” Someone filled her in on the situation.

“Huh? My brother? And what do you mean fourth Dragon God Descendant?” Dragon Princess Meihui raised her eyebrows in a puzzled manner. This was her first time hearing this news.

“You haven’t heard? This person managed to reach the second floor of the Divine Dragon Council, and when his bloodline was tested, it was revealed he had the Dragon God’s blood within him. It’s been spreading around like crazy.”

“This is my first time hearing about it.” Princess Meihui shook her head.

She narrowed her focus at the figure fighting with her brother, Long Yejun. When she saw Yuan’s face and recognized him, her eyes widened with shock.

“He’s the fourth Dragon God Descendant?! No way!” she exclaimed.

“What? You know him?” “We’ve met and even spoken very recently. However, I can’t say that I know him. To think he’d have Dragon God’s blood in him… what kind of coincidence is this?” Princess Meihui sighed out loud.

“It’s just a rumor for now since they haven’t confirmed if he is truly the Dragon God’s fourth descendant.”

Princess Meihui shook her head and said, “It’s likely true since I felt a strange connection with him when we first met. Although it was subtle, it was still there. I didn’t think much of it back then and even ignored it.”

“No way… so he might really be the fourth Dragon God’s Descendant?” The others were speechless.

In the Divine Dragon Sanctuary, those who were affiliated followed a strict ranking structure. This hierarchy determined not only status but also privileges and responsibilities, with higher-ranked individuals holding greater authority and access to powerful resources, while the lower ranks strove to prove themselves worthy of advancement.

The majority of affiliates within the Divine Dragon Sanctuary do not possess a rank, making even the lowest rank a position of immense authority.

The lowest rank within the Divine Dragon Sanctuary was the Divine Dragon Warrior, entrusted with maintaining order within the territory. They also served as the first line of defense and enforcers of the territory’s peace.

Above them were the Divine Dragon Commanders, responsible for leading and directing the Divine Dragon Warriors. They were akin to generals in the military. Only a hundred Divine Dragon Commanders existed in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary, while hundreds of thousands of Divine Dragon Warriors exist.

The next and highest attainable rank was the Divine Dragon Council. Comprised of fewer than a dozen individuals, the members of the council wielded the greatest authority within the Divine Dragon Sanctuary, second only to the Dragon God and his descendants. They were entrusted with making crucial decisions and overseeing the sanctuary’s most important matters.

Although the Divine Dragon Council was the highest rank, there was a rank even higher than them, reserved only for the Dragon God’s offspring—the Dragon God’s Descendants.

“Has the Dragon God commented on the situation?” Princess Meihui asked the ones around her.

“No, but the Dragon God should have been notified already.”

Meihui looked toward the sky and mumbled, “Father…”

Yuan and Long Yejun continued their battle for two days straight without a moment of rest. Both had long forgotten about the assessment, fully absorbed in the thrill of the fight, pushing each other to their limits as they exchanged relentless blows. Neither seemed willing to back down, their stamina and determination seemingly boundless.

“You’re really something, Yuan! I haven’t been entertained to this extent in a very long time! However, I can tell that you’re still holding back! Show me everything you’ve got!” “Are you sure? Don’t complain if you get hurt!”

“Hahaha! Of course! I won’t complain even if you kill me by accident!” Long Yejun laughed out loud.

“Then allow me to show you my true strength!”

Yuan’s aura suddenly surged, transforming into something more refined and razor-sharp, resembling the cutting edge of a sword.

Long Yejun halted his movement and even subconsciously backed away from Yuan after sensing his Enhanced Sword Aura.

“What is that power…?” Long Yejun muttered in astonishment

Everyone watching was equally bewildered by Yuan’s Sword Aura. The sharp, otherworldly energy left them in awe, unable to comprehend its origin or nature.

“I am not done yet.”

After releasing his Enhanced Sword Aura, Yuan imbued it with his Chaotic Essence.

BOOM!

The Sword Aura instantly intensified several times, almost reaching the realm of Supreme Sword Aura in terms of prowess.

chapters.title: Chapter 1659 Chaotic Sword Aura
chapters.body:
Chapter 1659 Chaotic Sword Aura

Chapter 1659 Chaotic Sword Aura

“Oh, this is not bad—much better than I anticipated,” Yuan nodded in satisfaction, impressed by the result of fusing his Chaotic Essence with his Enhanced Sword Aura.

“What is that power?” Long Yejun asked again.

Yuan pondered for a moment before responding, “Chaotic Sword Aura.”

He decided to give his Sword Aura a new name.

“Chaotic Sword Aura… what a fearsome technique!” Long Yejun swallowed nervously.

Despite the distance between them, it felt as though his body was being assaulted by countless invisible blades. Had his physique been any weaker, Yuan’s aura alone would have been enough to kill him on the spot.

In fact, a sizeable 500-meter-long crater had formed around Yuan. His Chaotic Sword Aura was so sharp and overwhelming that his mere presence had obliterated everything around him.

“Here I come,” Yuan warned Long Yejun before he swung his arm in a chopping motion.

Sha!

A black-colored sword light was created from the motion and flew at Long Yejun.

“?!”

Long Yejun was barely able to react and narrowly dodged it—or so he thought.

After sensing what felt like a drop of water flowing down his face, Long Yejun awkwardly reached for his face.

“This is…”

When he looked at his fingers, they were stained by his own blood.

Long Yejun trembled at the sight of his own blood. It had been so long since someone had managed to penetrate his scales that he couldn’t remember the last time it happened.

“Ahh… this sensation… this thrill that I am feeling…”

A crazed expression appeared on Long Yejun’s face as he licked his blood-stained fingers.

“HAHAHAHA!”

Long Yejun’s aura suddenly skyrocketed, piercing straight through the heavens.

“Let me enjoy this some more, Yu—Brother Yuan!”

Long Yejun charged at Yuan.

Yuan also wore a wide smile on his face as he faced Long Yejun head-on.

BOOM!

When they clashed, Yuan’s Chaotic Sword Aura scattered in every direction, carving deep sword cuts into the surrounding area. Long Yejun, who stood directly in front of Yuan, bore the brunt of the scattered Sword Aura with his bare body.

Sword cuts appeared all over Long Yejun’s body, quickly drenching him in blood. However, despite the severity of the sight, the wounds were superficial—no more than paper cuts. Within moments, his naturally strong body healed all the sword cuts.

“My turn!”

Long Yejun opened his mouth, and a bright orb of light suddenly began to form inside, glowing with intense energy as it rapidly grew in size and brilliance.

Yuan’s instincts warned him of the impending danger, but he found himself unable to move when he tried to dodge as Long Yejun held tightly onto his wrists. Long Yejun had deliberately closed the distance between them, enduring Yuan’s previous attack directly with his body, all in order to capture him and prevent his escape.

“Dragon’s Breath!”

Once he gathered enough energy, Long Yejun unleashed it from his mouth, shooting a beam of light resembling Heaven Splitting Sword Strike, even rivaling its destructiveness.

Yuan’s entire body was swallowed by the massive beam of light, his silhouette disappearing completely within its blinding brilliance.

Long Yejun kept his mouth open, continuously releasing the Dragon’s Breath until every last ounce of energy had been expended.

It took several long seconds before Long Yejun finally closed his mouth. When the Dragon’s Breath attack was complete, it left a path of utter devastation in its wake, and Yuan was nowhere to be seen.

“S-shit… Did I accidentally kill him?” Long Yejun muttered in a dazed manner after snapping out of his frenzied state.

A tsunami of worry and guilt washed over Long Yejun as he realized what he’d just done. He frantically scanned the devastated area, his heart pounding anxiously, dreading the worst outcome.

“Wow, that was scary. I almost died there.”

A familiar voice suddenly resounded, prompting Long Yejun to turn around.

Yuan was standing right behind him, seemingly unscathed. However, he was naked since all of his clothes and equipment were destroyed by the attack.

“Thank the Dragon God you’re alive!” Long Yejun exclaimed, feeling a surge of relief wash over him when he saw Yuan had survived his brutal attack. He let out a shaky breath, realizing just how close he’d come to a tragic mistake. He wouldn’t have known what to do if he had accidentally killed Yuan.

“Do you still want to continue?” Yuan asked, seemingly eager to continue fighting despite his near-death experience.

Long Yejun shook his head with a bittersweet smile and said, “No, let’s stop here.”

He continued, “It’s embarrassing considering my status and experience, but I tend to lose control when I get too excited in a fight. Anyway, I’m glad you’re safe. This is my loss.” Yuan smiled and dismissively waved his hand, “Don’t worry about it. I know how you feel since it also happens to me.”

“But how did you survive? I am certain my Dragon’s Breath landed on you since I could still feel the sensation of your wrist after I released it.” Long Yejun suddenly asked.

“It did hit me, and it even destroyed most of my body.”

“What…? But you look perfectly fine…”

“I’m quite confident in my regenerative abilities.” Yuan smiled. Although Long Yejun was too preoccupied to see it, the onlookers had witnessed Yuan reconstructing his entire body from a single cell, which left them all astonished, even terrified.

Sometime later, Yuan retrieved the Golden Dragon Robe and wore it.

‘Thank the heavens I didn’t wear this during the battle, or it would’ve been destroyed…’ Yuan sighed inwardly.

Unfortunately, the rest of Yuan’s equipment had been utterly obliterated by Long Yejun’s Dragon’s Breath. Among the losses were the treasured armor and boots that had once belonged to the Evil God, which he had only recently acquired.

These two treasures had endured thousands of years of abuse and even survived one of the biggest wars in the Divine Heaven. Yet, they were somehow destroyed so soon after returning to Yuan’s possession.

“Stygian Vest and Stygian Boots… What a pity…” Yuan muttered in a gloomy tone.

Realizing that he’d destroyed Yuan’s treasures, Long Yejun said, “Sorry, I also destroyed your valuables.”

“It’s fine. At least they were destroyed in a battle. They were also quite old and worn out, anyway.”

“No, it’s not fine. I will compensate you fully. I promise.” Long Yejun said with a heartfelt face.

Yuan nodded, acknowledging his sincerity.

chapters.title: Chapter 1660 Ancient Azure Dragon
chapters.body:
Chapter 1660 Ancient Azure Dragon

Chapter 1660 Ancient Azure Dragon

Sometime after their fight ended, Long Yejun said to Yuan, “Although you have passed the assessment, it never mattered since you’re one of the Dragon God’s descendants, so you will automatically be ranked above the Divine Dragon Council.”

“Oh…”

Yuan wondered if he should follow the flow and pretend to be a Dragon God’s Descendant.

‘If the Dragon God is as powerful as the Primordial Phoenix, there’s a good chance he will be able to see through me, so the longer I continue to impersonate his offspring, the worse things will get for me…’ fɾeeweɓnѳveɭ.com

‘However, if I reveal myself now, who knows how Long Yejun and the others will react…’

After some serious pondering, Yuan decided to continue acting as one of the Dragon God’s Descendants to take advantage of their training grounds for as long as possible.

Even if his identity were exposed later by the Dragon God, Yuan was confident that the Primal Monarch wouldn’t allow any real harm to come to him.

“Hey, we meet again.”

A familiar voice suddenly resounded, prompting Yuan to turn his head.

“Princess Meihui.” Yuan acknowledged her with a nod.

“There’s no need for such formality between us siblings. Allow me to introduce myself again. I am Long Meihui, the youngest of the Dragon God’s Descendant.” Long Meihui said with a beautiful smile on her face.

“I’m Yuan.”

“I’ll call you Brother Yuan from now on, so you should call me Sister Meihui. Elder Sister is fine, too.” Long Meihui chuckled blissfully.

Although she was the youngest, she was still billions of years old, so one could imagine her happiness in finally becoming an elder sister.

“I am finally an Elder Sister! I am no longer the youngest! Ahaha! I thought this day would never come!” Long Meihui laughed out loud. She was so happy that she began crying a moment later.

“By the way,” Yuan suddenly spoke, “Can I still participate in the Dragon God Festival as the Dragon God’s Descendant?” “Of course. We’ll also be participating.” Long Yejun nodded.

“I see. That’s great.”

“Are you participating because there’s something you want to accomplish? Can you tell us? Maybe we can help you right now.” Long Meihui suddenly said.

As one of the Dragon God’s Descendant, there was very little they couldn’t achieve in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary.

Yuan nodded and said, “A close friend of mine is cursed, and I need the power of the Sacred Chalice of Purification to cure him.”

“The Sacred Chalice of Purification, huh? If you can bring your friend to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary, we can help him.” Long Yejun said.

Yuan shook his head and sighed, “Unfortunately, my friend cannot leave his territory, so I have to bring the treasure to him.”

“Hmm… that would be difficult to do since the Sacred Chalice of Purification hasn’t left the Divine Dragon Sanctuary since it was brought here.” Long Meihui said.

“But if you win the Dragon God Festival, you can make that request to the Dragon God.” Long Yejun continued.

“That’s what I plan on doing,” Yuan said.

“Good luck, Brother Yuan. Although we’ve participated in nearly every Dragon God Festival since its creation many billion years ago, we’ve only won a few times.” Long Meihui said.

“What kind of trials are in the festival?” Yuan asked.

“All kinds, from trials that test your talent to nonsensical ones like racing each other across the Primal Expanse.” Long Yejun said.

“Racing across the Primal Expanse…? How long did that take to finish?” Yuan asked nervously.

“A few hundred years.”

“Oh…” Yuan immediately became worried after hearing that the festival may potentially last for hundreds of years.

“What’s the shortest time a festival has ever lasted?” he asked a moment later.

“The shortest festival happened 750 million years ago. I believe that one ended in just a few minutes.” Long Yejun said after pondering for a few moments.

“Ah, I remember that one. It was a ridiculous trial that required us to catch a Primal prepared by the Dragon God. The Eldest caught it in less than three minutes.” Long Meihui sighed as she recalled the events of that day.

“Anyway, Brother Yuan, are you free now? I want to compensate you for the valuables that I destroyed.” Long Yejun suddenly said.

“I am free now.” Yuan nodded.

“Great. Take this.” Long Yejun tossed another teleport stone to him.

“I’ll see you later.” Long Meihui said to him before flying away.

Sometime later, Yuan crushed the teleport stone and entered the rift, followed by Long Yejun.

When they emerged from the rift, Yuan found himself standing before a beautiful pavilion with a pond built around it.

“Where are we?” he asked.

Long Yejun smiled and said, “The Silver Pavilion.”

When Long Yejun approached the pavilion, another portal appeared.

“This is just the entrance to my real home.”

Once they entered the portal, Yuan found himself surrounded by an endless, shimmering sea that was completely motionless, like water in a still cup. He stood on a long silver bridge that stretched out toward a distant island, where a large house was situated.

“What do you think of my world?” Long Yejun asked for his impressions.

Yuan looked around for a moment before responding, “I can see that you like water.”

Long Yejun laughed at his response.

He then said, “I’m actually a water dragon—an Ancient Azure Dragon.”

“A water dragon? I thought you were a land dragon with how well you fought on land.” Yuan was surprised to learn this.

Long Yejun smiled and said, “If we had fought near the ocean—even near a small pond, I wouldn’t have lost to you.”

Water dragons could only unleash their true potential when fighting near or within water, so Long Yejun had been severely handicapped during his battle with Yuan.

“I wouldn’t be so sure about that,” Yuan said provocatively.

“Do you want to find out?” Long Yejun’s aura suddenly surged, causing the still water around them to tremble with excitement.

However, before Yuan could even respond, Long Yejun retracted his aura and chuckled, “I’m just joking with you. Follow me. I’ll bring you to my living quarters.”

Yuan silently nodded, maintaining a composed expression, though inwardly, he was startled by Long Yejun’s aura just now, which was incomparably stronger than when they had fought. ‘If we really fought here, I have only about a 20 percent chance of winning…’ he sighed inwardly.

chapters.title: Chapter 1661 Long Yejun’S Treasures
chapters.body:
Chapter 1661 Long Yejun’S Treasures

Chapter 1661 Long Yejun’s Treasures

After walking for several minutes, they finally arrived at the island.

“Welcome to my humble home.” Long Yejun said to Yuan as he opened the door.

“Though, this is just my secondary home that I only use when I am in my human form. My real home is the water outside.”

The interior of the house was incredibly simple. There was only one large room with a couch, a square table, four chairs, and some wall decorations. “What do you typically do here?” Yuan asked out of curiosity.

“This.”

Long Yejun suddenly sprawled out on the couch, his entire body stretched across it with one foot casually hanging off the edge.

“What am I looking at…?” Yuan didn’t want to make any assumptions and asked.

“You can’t tell? I am relaxing.”

“…”

Yuan was speechless.

“Although the human form is very restricting and weak, it has its advantages. The biggest one is their ease of movement and small size. I wouldn’t be able to do this if I were in my true form.” Long Yejun said.

“I see…”

“Speaking of human form…” Long Yejun sat up and stared at Yuan with a serious face.

“Your appearance is top-notch. Did you acquire it from the Human Face Artisan?”

“Human Face Artisan?” Yuan wore a puzzled look on his face.

“You don’t know the Human Face Artisan? He’s one of the top appearance makers in this world. Although his products are extremely expensive, they’re all top-quality. In fact, most of the top-quality appearances are created by him, mine included.”

“Is that so… Anyway, to answer your question, I didn’t acquire my face from him. I made it myself.” “What?! You made such a high-quality appearance by yourself?! You should start a business with that talent! The Human Face Artisan will finally have some real competition!”

“I’ll think about it,” Yuan said with a stiff smile.

Sometime later, Long Yejun got off the couch and walked toward the steel door on the other end of the room. When he opened the door, a descending staircase was revealed.

“Come with me.” Yuan nodded and followed Long Yejun down the staircase.

The staircase led to a tunnel made of transparent materials, allowing them to see the surrounding water. It reminded Yuan of an aquarium, with the serene, vast expanse of water pressing in from all sides as they walked through the passage. However, there was no aquatic life, so there was nothing to see.

After walking for several minutes, they arrived at another room where numerous objects, each radiating a vast amount of Chaotic Essence, were displayed like a prized collection.

“Welcome to my treasure room, where I store most of my valuables. Unfortunately, I don’t have any valuables that could replace your destroyed clothes and shoes.”

Since humans don’t exist in the Primal Expanse and Predators only take human form as a hobby, armor-type treasures meant for human anatomy are extremely rare.

“Since I destroyed two of your valuables, you can pick any two treasures from here.” Long Yejun continued.

“Huh? Any? Are you sure?” Yuan was pleasantly surprised by his generosity.

“Yes, I am sure.”

“Then I’ll look around.”

There were about a dozen treasures in the room, and Yuan approached the one that was closest to him.

However, when he inspected the item, he was shocked by the notifications.

<Item could not be analyzed>

[???]

[Grade: ???]

[Quality: ???]

[Description: ???]

‘The system failed to analyze the treasure?’

This was the first time the system couldn’t identify a treasure at all.

‘Is it because the treasures were created in the Primal Expanse?’ he wondered.

A few moments later, Yuan looked at Long Yejun and said, “Do you mind giving me a brief description of these treasures so I know what they do?”

“Oh, of course.”

Long Yejun walked beside him and continued, “This treasure is called Dragon’s Avarice. If you feed it enough Chaotic Essence, it will unleash a devastating attack that rivals even a Dragon’s Breath.”

“Is that the technique you used on me?” Yuan asked.

“Yes.”

“Let’s move on to the next one. I’ll make a decision after I hear about every treasure.”

“Sure.”

The next treasure was a large golden scale that was the size of a watermelon.

“This is a scale directly from the Dragon God. It doesn’t really have any particular uses, but it’s extremely durable—near indestructible. I wouldn’t be able to put a scratch on it even if I used my full power.” Long Yejun said.

“The Dragon God’s scale? How did you acquire this? And why is it so small?”

“It was given to me by the Dragon God—our father. Our siblings also have one, so he’ll probably give you one later. It’s small because I made Father shrink it since its actual size is a pain in the ass the carry around. Its real size could cover the entire Endless Arena.”

“I see…”

The next treasure was an elegant earring, crafted from gleaming silver and adorned with a brilliant sapphire gem. “Oh, I crafted this one myself. It greatly increases the wearer’s ability to control water.
