I chose this shape after meeting a human a few hundred million years ago, and he was wearing something like this on his ears.” Long Yejun explained,.

“What happened to that human?” Yuan asked out of curiosity.

“He took his own life when he realized that he couldn’t return home and couldn’t cultivate in this world. He was also my first and last human friend.” A sorrowful expression appeared on Long Yejun’s face as he recalled this tragic event.

“Sorry if I made you uncomfortable…” “It’s fine. It happened a long time ago.”

The next treasure appeared to be nothing more than an ordinary sheet of black leather at first glance, but Yuan could sense an ominous aura emanating from it.

“This is dragon skin that belongs to the Dragon of Destruction, who was said to be the Dragon God’s strongest rival billions of years ago. After his defeat, he was skinned alive and eaten by the Dragon God. This is all that remains of the Dragon of Destruction today.”

Yuan stared at the black leather with great interest.

‘I can make new armor out of this…’ he thought.

chapters.title: Chapter 1662 Two Treasures Of His Choice
chapters.body:
Chapter 1662 Two Treasures Of His Choice

Chapter 1662 Two Treasures of His Choice

Long Yejun noticed Yuan’s intrigued expression and smiled, “You seem interested. Do you want it?”

He nodded, “A little, but I will decide after looking at the other treasures.”

“Then let’s move on to the next one.”

The next treasure displayed was a beautiful golden marble resembling a dragon’s eyes.

“This is the Dragon’s True Eye. If you consume it, you’ll significantly strengthen all of your senses and even learn Dragon’s Authority. Since you already know Dragon’s Authority, this treasure won’t benefit you too much, but for an ordinary dragon, it essentially turns them into royalty.”

The next treasure appeared to be some kind of stone. It didn’t have any distinctive shape, and its Chaotic Essence was also the weakest in the room. ƒгeewёbnovel.com

“This is a small piece of the Dragon God’s claw. I acquired it from the first Dragon God’s Descendant, who managed to chip it off the Dragon God during a spar.”

“If you know how to make your own treasures, this will definitely create a very powerful treasure.”

“Hmm…”

Yuan stared at the Dragon God’s claw with interest. Because he had recovered Tian Qiyuan’s memories as the Exalted Blacksmith, these materials were more enticing than already completed treasures because he could easily customize the treasure to his own preferences.

‘This material will be the most effective if used to create a weapon, but I already have enough weapons. Perhaps I can use it to upgrade the Abyssal Dagger.’

All sorts of ideas appeared inside Yuan’s head.

Sometime later, Long Yejun continued introducing the treasures to him.

Since there were only a dozen treasures in the room, it didn’t take long for the tour to be finished.

“So, have you decided on which two treasures you want?” Ling Yejun asked him afterward.

Yuan nodded and walked to the Dragon of Destruction’s skin.

“I want this for my first choice.”

“I had a feeling you would pick that. If needed, I can get someone to craft a treasure for you with that skin.”

Yuan shook his head and said, “It’s fine. I plan on doing it myself.”

“What? This material isn’t your ordinary skin but the Dragon of Destruction’s skin. You’ll need to be a master of crafting to deal with it.”

“I know. I just need the proper equipment and place to craft the treasure.” Yuan showed a confident smile.

“Unbelievable… How many talents do you have?” Long Yejun muttered with a dumbfounded face.

After snapping out of it, he continued, “Alright, I’ll arrange the perfect place for you to craft the treasure. It might take a few months, though.”

“As long as it can be done before the Dragon God Festival,” Yuan said.

Long Yejun retrieved the Dragon of Destruction’s skin from the display and handed it over to Yuan.

“There’s a lot more than I anticipated,” Yuan mumbled after unfolding the skin to examine it properly.

At a glance, there didn’t appear to be much skin when it was neatly folded inside its display, but unexpectedly, after it was unfolded, its full size was revealed to be large enough to use as a ship’s sail.

‘With this much material, I can create more than just a piece of armor!’ Yuan smiled excitedly after realizing this.

After storing the skin inside his spatial ring, Yuan approached his second choice.

Long Yejun raised his eyebrows in a puzzled manner after seeing Yuan’s second choice.

“Why did you pick this treasure?” he asked.

“I’m not sure myself. It’s a gut feeling, I guess.” Yuan said with a genuine face.

“Are you sure? I only placed it here because of its mysterious origins.”

The treasure Yuan had picked for his second choice had an unknown origin and effects. Long Yejun had examined and tried to figure out its secrets since he acquired it but to no avail.

“I am sure.”

“If you say so…” Long Yejun didn’t say anything else and handed the treasure to Yuan, who placed it inside his spatial ring without examining it.

Seeing how confidently Yuan handled the treasure, Long Yejun had a feeling that Yuan knew the origin of the treasure, but he decided not to inquire about it for now.

Once Yuan had his two treasures, they left the treasure room and returned to the main room.

“What are you going to do until the Dragon God Festival in two years?” Long Yejun asked.

“I’m going to use the training grounds to strengthen myself.”

“Which one?”

“I’m not sure yet since this is my first time at the Divine Dragon Sanctuary.” “I see. Wait a moment.”

Long Yejun suddenly tapped his forehead, retrieving an orb of light.

After extracting the orb of light, he made a flicking motion with his fingers, shooting the orb of light directly into Yuan’s forehead.

Once he absorbed the light, information on the Divine Dragon Sanctuary began filling his head, from its training grounds to other miscellaneous places. He was essentially given a map of the entire Divine Dragon Sanctuary.

Sometime later, Long Yejun asked, “Have you found a training ground yet?”

Yuan nodded and said, “The Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp.” Long Yejun’s eyes widened, and he hastily said, “W-wait a second. That’s one of the most dangerous training grounds available! Unless you’re a poison dragon, it’s essentially suicide to train there! Even I don’t dare to go there.”

“I’ll be fine. My poison resistance is quite strong.” Yuan said.

“I never asked, but what kind of dragon are you?”

“I’ll keep it a secret for now. If you can guess it, I’ll do you a favor.”

“Alright, I’ll accept your challenge.”

“Before I head to the baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp, can you take me back to the Divine Dragon Hall? I came to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary with a friend.”

“Sure thing.”

Long Yejun shattered a teleport stone to the Divine Dragon Hall a moment later.

“Sorry for making you wait so long.” Yuan approached Yingzi, who was waiting beside the entrance.

“Hm? Where did you come from?” Yingzi was puzzled since she hadn’t noticed him leaving the Divine Dragon Hall even though she had been watching it without looking away the entire time.

Yuan immediately explained the situation to her.

chapters.title: Chapter 1663 Baneful Dragon’S Poisonous Swamp
chapters.body:
Chapter 1663 Baneful Dragon’S Poisonous Swamp

Chapter 1663 Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp

“I see…?” Despite Yuan explaining the situation to her, Yingzi only became more puzzled afterward. She couldn’t fathom how Yuan, who was obviously human, became the Dragon God’s Descendant. Moreover, he should have the Primal Monarch’s bloodline. However, she quickly stopped thinking about it, realizing it was pointless to overthink something she wasn’t meant to understand.

“Your friend is a Shadow Dweller, huh? It’s been over a million years since I last encountered their race.” Long Yejun commented as he stared at Yingzi, as it was incredibly rare for a Shadow Dweller to be so far away from their natural habitat, the Hollowed Expanse.

“Oh, let me introduce myself. I am the second Dragon God’s Descendant, Long Yejun.”

“Yingzi.”

“Let’s head to the Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp now, shall we?” Yuan said a moment later.

“Sure.”

Since their destination was only three days away from the Divine Dragon Hall, they decided to fly there instead of wasting a teleport stone.

While they traveled, Long Yejun gave an introduction to the Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp.

“Baneful Dragons are the strongest poison dragons and some of the most feared creatures in the Primal Expanse due to their dreadful poisons. Their poisons are so deadly that even entities with poison immunities could perish from them. They’re also so incredibly annoying to deal with that even the Dragon God avoids fighting them as much as possible.”

“How are they annoying to deal with?” Yuan asked curiously.

“Their bodies are essentially made of poison, so they release poison from any wounds inflicted upon them. The only way to fight them is from a safe distance, and you must defeat them swiftly, or else their poison will spread. Unfortunately, that is easier said than done because Baneful Dragons are annoyingly durable.”

“There’s a reason poison dragons are considered one of the most powerful and dangerous dragon bloodlines.”

“What bloodline does the Dragon God have?” Yuan asked.

“I would love to tell you, but unfortunately, even I do not have the answer.”

“What? Even though you’re his offspring?”

Long Yejun nodded and sighed, “We’ve asked him many times, but he said he’d only tell us if we can defeat him, which is essentially impossible. Even the Eldest, who is recognized as the most talented dragon since the Dragon God—the Unrivaled Tyrant—hasn’t defeated him after many billions of years.”

“I’ve heard about the first Dragon God’s Descendant multiple times now, but I still don’t have a clue what they’re like.” Yuan suddenly said.

“There’s only one thing you need to be aware of about the Eldest—” Long Yejun paused to look around nervously.

Once he was sure nobody was eavesdropping on them, he continued speaking through divine sense, “That bitch is fucking crazy! No matter what, don’t speak with her! As a matter of fact, don’t even approach her! If you ever see her, run the other direction!” ‘The Eldest is a woman, huh?’ Yuan thought to himself.

“What does she look like so I know it’s her?” Yuan then asked.

“She has multiple appearances and changes quite often, so it’s impossible to recognize her from appearance alone. However, you’ll still recognize her at a glance.”

“I don’t get it.”

“You’ll understand when you meet her.”

“Not even a hint?”

“Nope.” Long Yejun refused with a smile.

“Forget it then.”

Three days later, they arrived at the Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp.

Just as its name implied, the place was literally a swamp but with poisonous water and plants everywhere.

“There are three different zones in the Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp: Outer, Inner, and Center. Every zone has many layers of poisonous water, each containing a unique poison. We’re currently in the first zone on the very outskirts of the place.” Long Yejun explained.

“As you go deeper and approach the center of the swamp, the poison will become stronger. Do not underestimate this place, or you’ll die. I have the antidote for all poisons up to the inner zone, so you don’t have to worry about dying. However, once you enter the inner zone, I cannot save you, and only the Baneful Dragon could save you.”

Yuan nodded, “Thank you for the warning.” freewebnσvel.cѳm

After taking a deep breath, Yuan entered the swamp.

‘The swamp is much shallower than I anticipated…’ Yuan thought to himself as the water in the swamp only reached his knees.

<You have been poisoned by ‘Phantom Poison’>

<Ten Thousand Poison Immunity has nullified the effects of ‘Phantom Poison’>

Once he confirmed that his poison immunity was working, Yuan began walking deeper.

Several steps later, he was poisoned by a different type of poison.

<You have been poisoned by ‘Whispering Death’>

<Ten Thousand Poison Immunity has nullified the effects of ‘Whispering Death’>

He continued walking deeper into the swamp.

<You have been poisoned by ‘Blood Corruption Poison’>

<Ten Thousand Poison Immunity has nullified the effects of ‘Blood Corruption Poison’>

<You have been poisoned by ‘Death’s Extract’>

<Ten Thousand Poison Immunity has nullified the effects of ‘Death’s Extract’>

After walking three hundred steps and being exposed to over 20 different types of deadly poison, Yuan had only managed to complete a little under 10 percent of the first zone, meaning he was still over two thousand steps away from reaching the second zone.

‘My poison resistance isn’t increasing. I guess I have to be poisoned by something that my Ten Thousand Poison Immunity cannot handle…’

With that in mind, Yuan continued walking to the next zone.

Sometime later, Yuan finished 90 percent of the first zone after experiencing over 200 different types of poison.

When he was poisoned for the 207th time, a change occurred in his notifications.

<You have been poisoned by ‘Eternal Agony’>

<Ten Thousand Poison Immunity has failed to nullify ‘Eternal Agony’>

Ding!

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has activated>

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has begun refining ‘Eternal Agony’>

<Your poison resistance has increased>

<Your poison resistance has increased>

<Your poison resistance has increased>

Seeing that his physique had finally activated, Yuan sat in the poisonous water and began cultivating. Although his physique was refining the poison, it didn’t mean he wasn’t experiencing any pain. In fact, Yuan was currently experiencing excruciating pain from every inch of his body.

chapters.title: Chapter 1664 Hundred Thousand Poison Immunity
chapters.body:
Chapter 1664 Hundred Thousand Poison Immunity

Chapter 1664 Hundred Thousand Poison Immunity

“Wow, he’s doing much better than I expected…” Long Yejun muttered with a surprised look after seeing how far Yuan had progressed in the Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp.

He turned to look at Yingzi, who was standing beside him, and asked, “How long have you been traveling with him?”

“A little over two years.”

“So only recently, huh? How did you guys meet?”

“I was the one who approached him. He appeared out of nowhere and piqued my interest.”

“Is that so…”

Sometime later, Yuan stood up after receiving a notification.

<Your poison resistance has stregnthened>

<Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has begun fusing with Ten Thousand Poison Immunity>

[Progress: 10%]

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has nullified Eternal Agony>

<You have been poisoned by ‘Plague Poison’>

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has activated>

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has begun refining ‘Plague Poison’>

<Your poison resistance has increased>

Yuan stopped to cultivate again.

A few minutes later, Yuan walked to the next layer. He continued this until he reached the last layer of the first zone.

<Your skill ‘Ten Thousand Poison Immunity’ has developed into ‘Hundred Thousand Poison Immunity’>

[Progress: 25%]

It was only when his poison immunity advanced to the next realm that he noticed an increase in his progress. This realization led Yuan to believe that he would need to continue elevating his poison immunity to even higher levels in order to complete his progress.

Sometime later, Yuan walked forward, stepping into the second zone of the swamp.

Long Yejun swallowed nervously when he saw this. The second zone was incomparably more challenging than the first, and even poison dragons had trouble adapting to the poison there.

“He’s only been here for a few hours, and he’s already entering the second zone…” Long Yejun sighed out loud as he recalled taking several hundred million years to reach that point.

“His insane speed of adapting to poison… he’s definitely a poison dragon!” Long Yejun was already convinced that Yuan was a poison dragon. Otherwise, it would make no sense.

“Hm?”

Long Yejun suddenly noticed a powerful presence behind him, prompting him to turn around.

A tall and handsome man with purple, blue, and green hair was walking toward them with a slight smile on his face.

“It’s been a while since I’ve seen you at my playground, Prince Yejun.” This man greeted Long Yejun with respect.

“Baneful Dragon. Are you here to watch my little brother, too?”

Sure enough, this newcomer was the Baneful Dragon—the creator of the Poisonous Swamp.

“Your little brother?” The Baneful Dragon looked at Yuan and smiled, “So that’s the rumored fourth Dragon God’s Descendant. And I came here because I noticed something odd was happening at my playground.”

“Something odd?” Long Yejun raised his eyebrows in a questioning manner.

“My Poisonous Swamp started losing its strength rapidly a few hours ago, almost as if someone was purposefully detoxifying the poison.”

The Baneful Dragon narrowed his gaze at Yuan and continued, “Turns out he has been absorbing my poison and using it to enhance his own body—and at an incredible rate at that.”

“What? Isn’t that something only someone who has attained Poison Harmony could achieve?” Long Yejun’s eyes widened with shock. “Although Brother Yuan has poison immunity, he definitely hasn’t reached the level of Poison Harmony yet!”

Yingzi looked at Yuan with a weird expression on her face.

‘Isn’t this exactly what happened at the Eternal Phoenix Domain? So he can do more than just absorb fire, huh?’ Yingzi, who had witnessed Yuan’s feats at the Nine Levels of Hell, immediately realized what was happening.

However, she said nothing to Long Yejun and the Baneful Dragon since it would reveal Yuan’s true identity, leaving them to figure everything out themselves.

When Yuan entered the second zone and was poisoned, his Hundred Thousand Poison Immunity immediately nullified the poison effects, so he continued walking deeper until he could no longer continue at the 20% mark.

“If he’s stopping there, then his poison immunity should’ve only reached the level of Hundred Thousand Poison Immunity.” The Baneful Dragon remarked.

“That’s where I am now, and that took me hundreds of millions of years to achieve…” Long Yejun sighed.

The Baneful Dragon looked at him and sneered, “You make it sound like you’ve been training for hundreds of millions of years straight when, in reality, you’ve only really trained for a few thousand years.”

It took Yuan a little longer to adapt to the poison in the second zone and a little over a week to advance his poison immunity to the next stage.

<Your skill ‘Hundred Thousand Poison Immunity’ has developed into ‘Myriad Poison Immunity’>

[Progress: 75%]

[Myriad Poison Immunity]

[Description: Grants the user immunity to over a million poisons, including Immortal-grade poisons.]

When Yuan attained Myriad of Poison Immunity, he’d just traversed a little over 75 percent of the second zone. However, even with his improved poison immunity, he was only able to continue all the way up to 90 percent before needing to halt again.

“I have never seen anyone adapt to poison so quickly…” The Baneful Dragon spoke with a shocked expression on his face.

“That’s my little brother for you! Hahaha!” Long Yejun laughed with prideful expression, as if he was the one who was being praised.

“By the way, can you tell what kind of poison dragon he is?”

The Baneful Dragon shook his head and said, “Everything about him tells me that he’s a poison dragon, but for some reason, I cannot see him being a poison dragon. My gut tells me he isn’t one.” “What? He isn’t a poison dragon? How is that possible? There’s no way anything other than a poison dragon could adapt to poison so quickly!”

“I am well aware of that. If I have to be honest, even I cannot compare to him in that category.”

“No way! You have the strongest bloodline and highest poison affinity as a poison dragon! How could you lose to someone who isn’t even a poison dragon? That doesn’t make any sense!” Long Yejun exclaimed.

The Baneful Dragon remained silent, almost as if he had no response to such remarks.

chapters.title: Chapter 1665 Heaven’S Bane
chapters.body:
Chapter 1665 Heaven’S Bane

Chapter 1665 Heaven’s Bane

Another two weeks have passed since Yuan attained Myriad Poison Immunity.

<Your skill ‘Myriad Poison Immunity’ has developed into ‘True Poison Immunity’>

[True Poison Immunity]

[Description: Grants the user immunity to most poisons.]

[Progress: 90%]

‘Looks like I will have to advance my poison immunity by one more stage if I want to complete the fusion…’ Yuan thought to himself as he took his first step into the third zone, where the poisons are strong enough to kill even immortals within seconds.

“I would’ve stopped him from advancing further if you weren’t here, Baneful Dragon.” Long Yejun said. freewёbnoνel.com

However, the Baneful Dragon shook his head and said, “Even with me here, there’s nothing I can do to save him in the third zone because the poison there are strong enough to kill him before I can even attempt to save him.”

“My Poisonous Swamp isn’t the most dangerous training ground in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary without reason. It has the most casualties, and even other Baneful Dragons have perished here because they underestimated my poison.”

“What? Then I need to stop him immediately! Before it’s too late!” Long Yejun hastily said.

“He’ll be fine.” Yingzi suddenly spoke for the first time since the Baneful Dragon’s appearance.

“Where is your confidence coming from?” The Baneful Dragon inquired, his gaze filled with intrigue.

“From experience, I guess. I have seen what he’s capable of.”

“But you’ve only been with him for around two years!” Long Yejun exclaimed.

“And in those two years, I have witnessed enough to be confident of him.” Yingzi shrugged.

Long Yejun shifted his focus to Yuan again and swallowed nervously.

“Even that Unrivaled Tyrant hasn’t stepped foot inside the third zone…” he mumbled in a dazed voice.

The third zone of the Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp was much smaller than the previous two, requiring only a hundred steps to reach its very center. However, each step introduced a new, more potent layer of poison. These toxins were so deadly that even those at the God Ascension would succumb to them within minutes.

Even with True Poison Immunity, Yuan couldn’t take more than ten steps before he had to sit down to cultivate.

The pain he experienced was excruciating, and if it weren’t for his regeneration ability that constantly created healthy blood cells and restored his organs, the poison would’ve melted his body within seconds.

“Oh god… what is his body made of?” The Baneful Dragon was shocked by what he was witnessing, and he even began to doubt the effectiveness of his poison, something he had been immensely proud of for countless years.

“That regenerative ability… if he’s not a poison dragon, he’s a dragon with extremely high recovery rates…” Long Yejun immediately started thinking of the possibilities.

“Only an Undying Dragon would be able to rival that recovery speed of his… but they are especially weak to poison, so that can’t be right…”

“The Dragon God’s blood within him might have something to do with that.”

“No… the Dragon God’s blood only enhances our existing abilities to extreme lengths. None of the Dragon God’s Descendants have abilities beyond what they’re capable of—not even the Unrivaled Tyrant.”

For example, Long Yejun’s ability to control water reached levels other Ancient Azure Dragons could never hope to achieve because of the Dragon God’s blood within him. However, he would not suddenly acquire the ability to control the void or fire.

Four hours later, Yuan stood up and took another step before sitting back down again to cultivate. He would continue to spend four hours for each step until he reached the 69th step.

Ding!

<Your skill ‘True Poison Immunity’ has developed into ‘Perfect Poison Immunity’!>

[Progress: 100%]

<Congratulation, your Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has successfully merged with Perfect Poison Immunity>

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique(Incomplete) has improved>

<You have acquired the title ‘Harmonious Poison Body’>

The Baneful Dragon narrowed his eyes, sensing a drastic change in Yuan’s body and aura as his physique merged properly.

“What a ridiculous monster. Even the Unrivaled Tyrant is nothing in front of his poison affinity!” The Baneful Dragon praised loudly. “Don’t let her hear that, or she’ll kill you.” Long Yejun said as he nervously looked around to make sure nobody there heard it.

“Relax. This place is usually empty.” The Baneful Dragon laughed.

“I wonder why.” Long Yejun shook his head.

Meanwhile, Yuan continued his march toward the very center of the poisonous swamp. Unlike before, when each step took him hours to complete, this time he moved forward without pause, not stopping for even a second.

In the blink of an eye, he reached the very center of the Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp.

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has activated>

<You have learned ‘Heaven’s Bane’>

[Heaven’s Bane]

[Rank: Celestial]

[Mastery Level: 1]

[Description: The strongest poison—the bane of all existence. A single drop of Heaven’s Bane could poison even Heaven itself.]

Despite the powerful new technique he’d just acquired, Yuan was paying attention to something else—something inside the Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp.

He crouched down and reached into the water, carefully retrieving a purple crystal as large as his entire head. The crystal gleamed with an otherworldly glow and exuded a deadly presence.

[???]

“What is this?” he muttered.

“Congratulations, fourth Dragon God’s Descendant!” The Baneful Dragon suddenly appeared before Yuan with a wide smile on his face.

“Huh? Who are you?”

“I am the Baneful Dragon—the owner and creator of this wonder playground! And you’ve just become the first entity to defeat all of my poison by reaching the very center of the swamp!”

“That object in your hand is a Baneful Crystal—a treasure I have been nurturing for over a billion years in this playground. It can be used to create poison or as a material to craft another treasure. I’ll give it to you to celebrate this occasion.” “Thank you, Baneful Dragon. I will put this treasure into good use,” Yuan said, already thinking about how he should use it.

chapters.title: Chapter 1666 The Next Training Ground
chapters.body:
Chapter 1666 The Next Training Ground

Chapter 1666 The Next Training Ground

“I can’t believe you have managed to conquer the Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp in such a short time, and it’s even your first visit to this place.” Long Yejun praised Yuan when he returned.

He said, “Are you a Baneful Dragon, too? Only a Baneful Dragon with Dragon God’s blood could possibly achieve this feat.”

Yuan shook his head with a smile and said, “Sorry, you’re wrong. I am not a Baneful Dragon.”

“I want to believe you, but alas…”

“Anyway, let’s head to the next training ground.” Yuan then said, leaving them speechless.

“Already?”

“There’s less than two years until the Dragon God Festival, so I am short on time. I am going to experience most of the training ground in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary by then.”

“Hahaha! You’re really something, little brother! Good! I’ll also stay with you until then!” Long Yejun declared, as he had a feeling that he might be witnessing history being made.

“What about the location where I can craft my treasure?” Yuan then asked.

“Don’t worry, I’ve already notified my contact about it. I’ll let you know when it’s available.”

“Thank you.” Yuan nodded.

“Where are you going next?” The Baneful Dragon suddenly inquired.

Yuan took a moment to look at the map inside his head before responding, “The nearest training ground from here is the Sky Splitting Dragon’s Tribulation, so I’ll train there next.”

“The Sky Splitting Dragon’s Tribulation, huh?” Long Yejum mumbled.

“It’s been a while since I went there, so I’m going to tag along. I also want to see how far you’ll go in a different environment.” The Baneful Dragon said.

Long Yejun shattered a teleport stone that brought them near their destination shortly after.

‘If I can become immune to lightning, I’ll no longer have to worry about Heavenly Tribulations back in the Nine Heavens…’ Yuan thought to himself as they entered the rift.

In the Nine Heavens, when an individual attains immunity to a particular element, their unique physique is given a specific name. For instance, those immune to poison are known to possess the ‘Perfect Poison Body,’ while those immune to fire are referred to as ‘One With the Flames.’

For those who achieve immunity to lightning, they possess what is known as a ‘Heavenly Physique,’ a physique that directly defies Heavenly Tribulations. This unique trait grants them the power to withstand the wrath of the heavens itself, making it one of the most coveted and sought-after physiques for cultivators.

Most cultivators perish during Heavenly Tribulations when attempting to ascend to the next major realm. Thus, one can only imagine the immense value of the Heavenly Physique that enables its bearer to completely ignore these tribulations and ascend unchallenged.

A few days later, Yuan and his group arrived at the Sky Splitting Dragon’s Tribulation. The training ground stretched across a vast, barren landscape, where lightning strikes continuously poured down like raindrops in a storm, illuminating the area with their relentless fury.

With countless lightning strikes crashing down incessantly, the training ground was filled with a deafening noise. The clamor was so overwhelming that it was impossible to stay there without sealing one’s hearing.

Long Yejun snapped his fingers, covering their group in a soundproof barrier.

“What a chaotic place and scenery, but it’s also oddly beautiful…” Yuan muttered.

Unlike the Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp, which was devoid of any presence, the Sky Splitting Dragon’s Tribulation was bustling with beings.

“Hey, look over there! Two of the seven Dragon Monarchs are together!” It didn’t take long for the beings there to notice Yuan’s group.

“Wow! The Water Dragon Monarch and the Poison Dragon Monarch? What a rare sight!”

In the Divine Dragon Sanctuary, there exists a prestigious title separate from the regular rankings, bestowed only upon the strongest dragons of their respective elements. Both the Baneful Dragon and Long Yejun held this revered title as the Poison Dragon Monarch and the Water Dragon Monarch.

The Spy Splitting Dragon was also one of the seven Dragon Monarchs as the Lightning Dragon Monarch.

“What are they doing together at this training ground? And who are those two unfamiliar faces with them?”

“I recognize the young man! He’s the fourth Dragon God’s Descendant!”

“So he’s the one…”

While the onlookers chatted, Long Yejun explained the training ground to Yuan.

“There are nine levels of difficulty in this training ground,” Long Yejun explained. “You basically temper your body at the level you can endure until you’re ready to move on to the next. Quite simple, right?”

“For reference, my limit is level 3,” Long Yejun continued.

“Only level 3?” Yuan remarked with raised eyebrows, surprised that the limit seemed relatively low for someone of Long Yejun’s stature.

“As a water dragon, I am especially weak to lightning, so it’s much harder for me to train here than others.”

“By the way, my limit is level 5.” The Baneful Dragon said.

“What about the Unrivaled Tyrant?” Yuan asked out of curiosity.

“She’s conquered all nine levels a few million years ago.” Long Yejun said. “But it took her 300 million years of straight training.”

“300 million years…?” Yuan swallowed hard at the sheer enormity of that number, unable to hide his awe and disbelief.

‘Hopefully, it won’t take me anywhere as long…’

After taking a moment to prepare himself, Yuan entered the training ground.

“Then I’ll see you later.”

“Good luck.” They said to him.

“Look! The fourth Dragon God’s Descendant has entered the training ground!” The onlookers started shifting their attention to him.

After entering the training ground, Yuan was immediately bombarded with lightning strikes that could easily kill even a Divine Grandmaster. However, it merely tickled him, who had attained the Golden Immortal Physique. Yet, he didn’t continue to the second level and sat down to cultivate in the first level.

‘Unlike the poisonous swamp, where I had some poison immunity since the beginning, I don’t have any resistance to lightning, so I must build it up from scratch,’ he thought inwardly.

chapters.title: Chapter 1667 Sky Splitting Dragon’S Tribulation
chapters.body:
Chapter 1667 Sky Splitting Dragon’S Tribulation

Chapter 1667 Sky Splitting Dragon’s Tribulation

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has activated>

<Your lightning resistance has increased slightly>

<Your lightning resistance has increased slightly>

<Your lightning resistance has increased slightly>

An entire day had passed since Yuan began his training at the Sky Splitting Dragon’s Tribulation, yet he had still not managed to develop any resistance to lightning.

Lightning resistance was not only one of the most sought-after immunities but also one of the most difficult to train. Among all the immunities, the Heavenly Physique was one of the rarest, even though it had the greatest number of pursuers. Seeing that he was only progressing slightly, Yuan was tempted to enter the second level directly.

After some contemplation, Yuan moved to the border between the first and second levels. In the second level, the lightning strikes crackled with a vivid purple hue, a stark contrast to the blue lightning of the first level.

A few moments later, Yuan entered the second level with just one of his arm to test the waters.

DONG!

As if his arm had become a lightning rod, the purple lightning strikes that had been raining down aimlessly suddenly converged, targeting him with unerring precision.

“Whoa!”

Yuan cried out loud when the lightning strikes completely obliterated his arm in the blink of an eye, and he hastily took several steps back afterward.

“The difficulty between each level is no joke…” he swallowed nervously.

Unfortunately, there was nothing he could do but continue training in the first level until he gained some resistance to lightning.

“Is he bold or reckless? I have never seen anyone try to advance to the next level so quickly.” The onlookers laughed after witnessing Yuan’s blunder.

“He was probably influenced by his success in my playground and underestimated this place.” The Baneful Dragon smiled. “Fortunately, he was smart enough to only stick an arm in there, or that would’ve been the end of him.”

Unbeknownst to Yuan, the lightning strikes in the second level were several times more powerful than the Heavenly Tribulation Feng Yuxiang had faced and nearly lost her life to.

A few days later, Yuan finally acquired some lightning resistance.

<You have gained Weak Lightning Resistance>

After gaining Weak Lightning Resistance, Yuan immediately attempted to enter the second level once more.

Yet, nothing had changed; his arm was still instantly obliterated by the purple lightning.

“…”

However, not all hope was lost, as he was able to strengthen his lightning resistance this time, while his previous attempt did not give him any experience.

<Your lightning resistance has increased moderately>

<Your lightning resistance has increased slightly>

<Your lightning resistance has increased moderately>

Thus, Yuan began cultivating with only half of his body exposed to the second level, straddling the border and experiencing the effects of both levels simultaneously. This way, he could push his limits while still maintaining some degree of safety.

“What the hell am I looking at?”

The onlookers were in disbelief after seeing Yuan’s ridiculous training method that took advantage of his insane regenerative ability.

“Does that really work?”

“Only if you have his speed of recovery…” freewēbnoveℓ.com

Meanwhile, Long Yejun was laughing.

“Hahaha! What a brutal yet efficient method of training! Only someone like him could possibly do something like this!” “He did that with the poison at my playground, too…” The Baneful Dragon shook his head with a defeated smile.

Word of Yuan’s training at the Sky Splitting Dragon’s Tribulation spread like wildfire, drawing an increasing number of onlookers. Some were captivated by the rumors of the fourth Dragon God’s Descendant, eager to glimpse his identity, while others came to witness his potential firsthand.

Two weeks later, Yuan advanced his lightning resistance to the next level.

<Your skill ‘Weak Lightning Resistance’ has developed into ‘Powerful Lightning Resistance’>

After attaining Powerful Lightning Resistance, the purple lightning in the second level no longer obliterated his arm. Instead, it merely tickled him, while the lightning from the first level had become so weak he could no longer feel it at all.

Seeing this, Yuan confidently strolled into the second level. However, he didn’t stop and continued moving until he was right before the third level.

Then, just like before, he tested the water with his arm.

BOOM!

The red lightning strikes in the third level instantly obliterated his arm, and his lightning resistance didn’t increase even slightly.

Meanwhile, the purple lightning no longer increased his resistance moderately. Now, it only increased his resistance slightly.

<Your lightning resistance has increased slightly>

After seeing the results, Yuan no longer bothered with the third level and focused on cultivating in the second level.

“My god, he’s managed to progress from the first level to the second level in just three weeks! What monstrous speed!” The onlookers exclaimed with shock.

“Even the Unrivaled Tyrant took several years! Does this mean the fourth Dragon’s God Descendant is potentially even more talented than the Unrivaled Tyrant?”

“No, this just means he’s more talented than her in this one aspect. The Unrivaled Tyrant has conquered most of the training grounds in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary, after all.” “Also, it is still too early to say anything. Even if his progress is faster than others, that won’t mean anything if he reaches his limit too quickly.” A month later.

<Your skill ‘Powerful Lightning Resistance’ has developed into ‘Super Lightning Resistance’>

After advancing his resistance, Yuan repeated what he had done at the first level and cultivated in the third level with only half of his body.

<Your lightning resistance has increased moderately>

<Your lightning resistance has increased moderately>

During this time, news of Yuan’s intense training session continued to spread across the territory, drawing in a steady stream of new onlookers. Curiosity and excitement filled the air as more people gathered, eager to witness his remarkable progress.

“Elder Brother, I knew you’d be here.” Long Meihui approached Long Yejun after noticing him at the training ground. “Oh, you’re also here, Baneful Dragon.”

“It’s been a while, Princess Meihui.” The Baneful Dragon greeted her with a handsome smile.

“How long have you been watching?” Long Yejun asked her.

“I just arrived,” she said, turning to gaze at Yuan with a look of awe in her eyes.

chapters.title: Chapter 1668 Courting Death
chapters.body:
Chapter 1668 Courting Death

Chapter 1668 Courting Death

“How long has he been training here?” Long Meihui asked.

“It’ll be two months tomorrow.” Long Yejun said.

“Did he start from level two?” “No, he started from level one.”

“What?! He went from level one to level three in two months?! That’s impossible!” Long Meihui exclaimed, her face filled with disbelief.

The Baneful Dragon smiled and said, “Then you’ll definitely not believe this, but he came here right after conquering my Poisonous Swamp in just a month.”

“WHAT?!”

Long Meihui snapped her head to look at the Baneful Dragon with eyes as wide as saucers.

“That’s literally impossible! Even Elder Sister hasn’t conquered the Poisonous Swamp! And you’re telling me he’s managed to do just that in a month?!”

Long Yejun chuckled at her reaction, “It’s true. I was there from the beginning.”

“That’s…” Long Meihui fell silent with a dazed expression on her face.

In the blink of an eye, two more months passed. <Your skill ‘Super Lightning Resistance’ has developed into ‘Supreme Lightning Resistance’>

After advancing his lightning resistance, Yuan entered the third level with his entire body and walked straight to the border of level three and level four.

However, when he tried sticking an arm in level four, Yuan was pleasantly surprised to see his lightning resistance increase, and his arm hadn’t been obliterated, only burnt.

<Your lightning resistance has increased significantly>

Of course, it was unfathomably painful. Even though it was only his arm getting struck, he felt the pain throughout his entire body.

‘No pain, no gain!’

After mentally preparing himself, Yuan walked into the fourth level, shocking the onlookers.

“He’s going straight into level four this time!”

“How bold!”

“No matter how many times I see it, his regenerative ability still shocks me… Is he an Undying Dragon?”

“Most likely.”

Time continued to swiftly pass, and another three months have passed. <Your skill ‘Supreme Lightning Resistance’ has developed into ‘Incomplete Heavenly Physique’>

By now, Yuan has spent seven months in the Sky Splitting Dragon’s Tribulation. If the cultivators in the Nine Heavens knew that he’d managed to start creating his Heavenly Physique in such little time, not even the heavens could predict their reaction.

Even the most talented cultivators required centuries, if not millenniums, of rigorous training to attain the Incomplete Heavenly Physique, but Yuan had managed to achieve it in a little over half a year.

However, the Incomplete Heavenly Physique was just the beginning, as he still had to advance it a few more times to achieve the completed Heavenly Physique.

<Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has begun fusing with Incomplete Heavenly Physique>

[Progress: 25%] freeweɓnovēl.coɱ

Yuan went to stick an arm into the fifth level shortly after.

<Your lightning resistance has increased significantly>

When he confirmed that he was able to survive the lightning strikes, he entered the fifth level with his entire body.

“Level five in seven months! I don’t think anyone is going to beat this record!” The onlookers could feel their bodies trembling in excitement.

“If he continues at this pace, he’ll reach level nine before the Dragon God Festival!”

However, to the onlooker’s shock, Yuan increased his pace, and just a month later, he began walking toward the sixth level.

<Your skill ‘Incomplete Heavenly Physique’ has developed into ‘Imperfect Heavenly Physique’>

[Progress: 50%]

When he entered the sixth level and was bombarded with silver-colored lightning strikes, Yuan was pleasantly surprised to find out that he was enduring the lightning strikes with relative ease.

Thus, he made a bold move and went into the seventh level after testing the waters with an arm.

“He’s already at level seven?!”

The seventh level unleashed torrents of black-colored lightning strikes, each bolt as thick as a tree trunk, crashing down with immense power and fury.

<Your lightning resistance has increased significantly>

<Your lightning resistance has increased significantly>

<Your lightning resistance has increased significantly>

Although he was able to endure the seventh level, each lightning strike was powerful enough to almost completely erase his existence.

Ding!

<You have acquired the title ‘Courting Death’>

[Courting Death]

[Description: The holder of this title has faced the brink of death countless times. All sources of healing will be 1,000% more effective.]

After receiving his new title, Yuan’s regeneration became even more powerful.

“Is it just me, or is he regenerating even faster now?”

“You noticed it, too?”

The onlookers did not miss this detail.

By now, the entire Sky Splitting Dragon’s Tribulation was filled to the brim with onlookers, all of them watching with anticipation for the moment Yuan conquers the training ground.

In fact, even the Sky Splitting Dragon himself was present to witness history being made.

“Took you long enough to show up, Lightning Dragon Monarch.” Long Yejun said as he looked at the handsome, bulky man approaching their group.

“I had to confirm the rumors—that someone had reached the seventh level from the first level in under a year. Is it true?”

“It is very much true.” Long Meihui said.

The Sky Splitting Dragon looked around and asked, “Where’s the Unrivaled Tyrant? I thought she’d be here as well.”

“I haven’t seen her since she entered the Void Vortex two million years ago.” Long Yejun said.

“But she should be returning soon for the Dragon God Festival.” Long Meihui continued.

The Baneful Dragon shuddered at the thought and sighed, “If only she never returned…”

Time continued to pass, and it has been ten months since Yuan entered the Sky Splitting Dragon’s Tribulation.

<Your skill ‘Imperfect Heavenly Physique’ has developed into ‘Heavenly Physique’>

Ding!

<You have acquired the title ‘Heavenly Physique’>

Yuan had finally acquired the Heavenly Physique after ten gruesome months of training. However, even after acquiring the highly coveted Heavenly Physique, his progress only went up to 90 percent.

[Progress: 90%]

‘I guess there’s one more stage beyond the Heavenly Physique…’

With that thought in mind, Yuan began approaching the eighth level, where golden-colored lightning strikes poured heavier than rain in a storm.

“He’s heading for level eight!” The onlookers swallowed nervously as they watched Yuan step into the eighth level after making sure he could endure it.

chapters.title: Chapter 1669 Heavenly Physique
chapters.body:
Chapter 1669 Heavenly Physique

Chapter 1669 Heavenly Physique

<Your lightning resistance has increased significantly>

After reaching the eighth level, Yuan didn’t pause and continued toward the ninth level. However, just as he was about to extend his arm into the ninth level, he suddenly stopped. His instincts had kicked in, warning him that he wouldn’t escape with just a destroyed arm if he underestimated the ninth level.

‘Let’s ignore the ninth level for now…’

Yuan took several steps back and sat down to cultivate in the eighth level.

“I guess he’s not just bold and reckless.” Long Yejun laughed out loud after seeing Yuan’s actions.

“He has good instincts.” The Sky Splitting Dragon smiled.

“You guys want to play a game? Let’s bet on how long he’ll take to reach the ninth level and conquer the training ground.” The Baneful Dragon suddenly suggested. “I’ll start with five months.”

“Sounds fun. I’ll bet a year.” The Sky Splitting Dragon confidently said.

“Then I’ll go with six months.” Long Meihui said.

Long Yejun pondered for a moment before speaking, “I’ll bet he’ll conquer this place within three months.”

“What are we betting, anyway?” Yingzi asked.

“Good question. How about something we consider valuable? Even if it’s only slightly.”

“Sure.”

“Then I’ll bet a hundred meat skewers he’ll finish in a month,” Yingzi said.

“Meat skewers? What are those?” They looked at her with raised eyebrows.

The only person who knew what the meat skewers were, aside from Yingzi, was Long Meihui, who had experienced their deliciousness before.

“It’s food,” she calmly said.

“Food…? How’s that valuable?” The Baneful Dragon asked.

Long Meihui responded in Yingzi’s stead, “I can vouch for their value. They’re unlike anything you’ve ever seen or tasted before. I can still clearly taste them even though it’s been a year since I had them.”

“Now I’m interested.” Long Yejun said with an intrigued smile.

“But I thought they belonged to Brother Yuan.” Long Meihui then inquired while looking at Yingzi.

“They do, but some of them belong to me,” she said.

“I see…”

A month and twenty days later.

<Your skill ‘Heavenly Physique’ has developed into ‘Flawless Heavenly Physique’>

[Progress: 100%]

<Congratulation, your Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has successfully merged with Flawless Heavenly Physique>

“Finally…” Yuan stood up and immediately began walking towards the ninth level.

This time, his instincts told him that he had no problem withstanding the rainbow-colored lightning strikes in the ninth level. “No way… is he already ready to conquer the training ground?” The Sky Splitting Dragon exclaimed in a shocked voice after seeing Yuan’s actions.

“Slightly under two months… If he manages to conquer this place, it’ll be the Shadow Dweller’s win since she has the closest guess.” Long Yejun said.

After taking a deep breath, Yuan stepped into the ninth level. As the lightning strikes rained down on him, they rapidly changed colors, giving an odd scenery.

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has activated>

<You have learned ‘Heaven’s Final Judgement’>

[Heaven’s Final Judgement]

[Rank: Celestial]

[Description: The pinnacle of lightning mastery, a level no human has ever attained. A single strike shall split the heavens and obliterate stars!]

“He’s done it! The fourth Dragon God’s Descendant has actually managed to conquer the Sky Splitting Dragon’s Tribulation in less than a year!”

The onlookers cheered loudly after witnessing history being made, causing the place to tremble.

“Unbelievable…” Long Meihui muttered in a dazed voice.

“Here.” The Baneful Dragon handed a treasure he deemed valuable to Yingzi after losing the bet.

The Sky Splitting Dragon and the others followed.

“Thank you.”

Yingzi tossed the treasures into her shadow without even inspecting them.

Sometime later, Yuan reunited with Long Yejun and the others. “Congratulations, Brother Yuan! I can’t believe you actually conquered this training ground in just a year!” Long Meihui congratulated him.

“Huh?” However, Yuan did not react in the manner they expected.

“H-how long have I been training…?” he asked in a shaky voice, his face pale with a look of terror as he sought confirmation.

“Well, it hasn’t been a full year, but it’s close.” The Baneful Dragon said.

“11 months and 20 days, to be exact.” Long Yejun confirmed.

“Fuck! I had been training for that long?!”

Yuan cried out in surprise, suddenly realizing that he had completely lost track of time.

“I’ll be right back!”

Without explaining further, Yuan suddenly disappeared into thin air, dumbfounding everyone there.

“What the? Where did he go?” The Sky Splitting Dragon muttered with a dazed expression.

“I don’t know, but he’ll be back soon,” Yingzi said.

Meanwhile, in the Nine Heavens, Xi Meili approached Meixiu and asked with a worried face, “Still no news from Yuan?”

Meixiu shook her head in a dispirited manner.

“It’s been a whole year since he stopped all communication… What if something happened to him?” Xi Meili sighed out loud.

Yuan had been so absorbed in his training that he’d forgotten to log off, missing his monthly update to the others for an entire year.

“Don’t worry, I’m sure he’s fine,” Meixiu reassured Xi Meili. “The last time we spoke a year ago, he mentioned that he would stay in the Primal Expanse for at least another two years to revive Feng Feng. He’s probably just lost track of time while training. This isn’t the first time it’s happened, though it is definitely one of his longest absences.”

“I really hope that’s the case…”

At that very moment, Chu Liuxiang appeared before them like a ghost, a wide smile spreading across her face as she announced, “Yuan’s back!”

“What?! Really?!” Xi Meili’s eyes flickered with excitement and relief after receiving this news.

“Yes,” she reassured.

“What happened to him?” Meixiu inquired.

Chu Liuxiang chuckled and said, “He said he was training and lost track of time. He also wanted to say he’s sorry for making us worry.”

“As long as he’s safe…” Xi Meili said.

Chu Liuxiang continued, “Anyway, he returned to Cultivation Online immediately after telling me that. However, he said he’ll return as soon as possible to update us once he’s done with his business in the Primal Expanse.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1670 Something Fun
chapters.body:
Chapter 1670 Something Fun

Chapter 1670 Something Fun

“Where did you go just now?” Long Yejun asked Yuan after he returned to the Primal Expanse a few minutes later.

“That’s a secret,” Yuan said with a smile.

It was common for powerful beings in the Primal Expanse to disappear and appear suddenly, so the others weren’t too baffled by Yuan’s sudden disappearance.

Of course, they were curious, but seeing how Yuan didn’t want to talk about it, they didn’t question it.

“Are you going to the next training ground now?” The Baneful Dragon suddenly inquired.

Yuan shook his head and said, “I am going to take a short break. Is there any place where I can have some privacy for a few days?”

“You can stay at my abode.” Long Yejun said.

“Thanks.” Yuan nodded.

“W-wait! Before you go, can I shamelessly ask for a few more of those skewers?” Long Meihui suddenly said.

Yuan smiled, “Sure.”

He retrieved ten meat skewers and handed them to her the next moment.

Long Yejun and the others stared at meat skewers as they were being handed to her, their eyes wide with surprise.

“What a unique aroma! Do you think I can get some, too?” Long Yejun asked with a watery mouth.

“Of course.” After giving a handful of skewers to Long Yejun, Yuan turned to look at the others and asked, “Do you also want some?”

“Please!” The Baneful Dragon and the Sky Splitting Dragon eagerly nodded.

“Thanks!”

“By the way, I am the creator of this place, the Sky Splitting Dragon.” The Sky Splitting Dragon introduced himself after receiving the skewers.

“Oh, it’s nice to meet you. This is a great training ground.” Yuan said.

“You have no idea how much it hurts my pride when you conquer it so quickly.” “Let us know when you head to the next training ground. I want to be there when you conquer another one.” Long Meihui said a moment later.

“Sure.” Yuan nodded.

Sometime later, Long Yejun shattered a teleport stone, teleporting Yuan and Yingzi to his living quarters.

“You can go without me. I received the notification that your crafting room was prepared a month ago. I’ll go verify it myself first. Don’t worry, there’s no rush. You can take your time.” Long Yejun said to them.

“Thanks for everything so far.”

“No, thank you for entertaining me.” Long Yejun laughed before disappearing.

Once they were inside Long Yejun’s home, Yuan handed Yingzi the rest of her meat skewers and said, “I’ll be gone for about a week or two.” “Okay.”

Yuan logged off shortly after.

When he returned to his real body, Chu Liuxiang and Meixiu were already awake and waiting for him.

“Welcome back,” Meixiu said.

“I’m so sorry!”

Yuan wasted no time lowering his head and apologizing to her.

“I was caught up in training and lost track of the time!”

“I know. Lulu already told me.”

“Still, I wanted to apologize to you myself.”

“It’s fine. Honestly, it didn’t feel that long. I guess the higher our cultivation, the more we’re prone to losing track of time.” Meixiu chuckled.

“Right? I have also been a victim of this.” Chu Liuxiang said.

Yuan then inquired about the Lord, “Anyway, how’s the Lord doing? I haven’t replenished his energy in a year, after all.”

“He’s doing fine for now. I visited him last week.” Chu Liuxiang said.

“That’s good. I’ll go replenish his energy right now.”

Yuan left from their balcony and flew straight to the peak of the mountain.

The Lord emerged from his cave after sensing Yuan’s presence approaching his place.

“It’s been a while.”

“Sorry, I lost track of time during my training. I hope you’re doing okay.”

“I have endured many centuries without any help. A single year won’t kill me.” The Lord laughed out loud. “Still… Anyway, I’ll call Ji Ran now.”

Yuan summoned the Starry Abyss shortly after.

However, after being summoned, Ji Ran said, “Sorry, but I barely recovered any spiritual energy because the Primal Expanse lacks spiritual energy, so I won’t be able to help.”

“Now that you mention it…” Yuan rubbed his eyes after realizing this blunder. freewёbnoνel.com

“I’m sorry, but you’ll have to use my spiritual energy until I leave the Primal Expanse,” he then said.

“I don’t mind waiting until you leave the Primal Expanse. After all, even if you give me all your spiritual energy, it will only give me relief for a brief moment.” The Lord said. He continued, “Moreover, the amount of spiritual energy I have received so far is enough to last another decade.”

“Alright. Then, I will return once I leave the Primal Expanse. It shouldn’t take too long. Probably another year or two at most.”

Yuan returned to his living quarters afterward.

“How’s training with the Eternal Lotuses lately? It must’ve been rough without my spiritual bath.” Yuan asked.

“Yes, it was definitely rough on the trainees at first since they suddenly stopped recovering overnight, but they’ve adapted. Moreover, every single member of their faction is training with us now.” Meixiu said.

“That’s good to hear.”

“The Eternal Lotuses have also advanced their rank back to the top 10.” Chu Liuxiang then said.

“What about us? Have you guys participated in any Faction War since the last one?”

“Unfortunately, they’re still afraid to challenge us. And since we’ve been inactive for a whole year, we’re back to being unranked.” Chu Liuxiang sighed.

“The ranking doesn’t have any meaning to us, anyway.” Yuan shrugged nonchalantly.

He then asked, “Any news on the government or the Ten Great Families?”

“Well, we know they’re watching us from outside the Dragon Spiral Mountain, but that’s about it,” Meixiu said.

“That’s good.”

“By the way, when are you going to return to Cultivation Online?” Chu Liuxiang suddenly asked.

“I intend to stay here for a week or two before I return.”

“Do you have something to do on Earth?”

He shook his head and said, “No, I just want to spend some time with you guys since I’ve been away for a whole year.”

“Hmm… then do you want to go out and have some fun? It’s been a while since we’ve done anything fun together.” Chu Liuxiang suggested.

Yuan nodded, “Let’s do that.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1671 Ready Or Not
chapters.body:
Chapter 1671 Ready Or Not

Chapter 1671 Ready or Not

“Do you have any suggestions?” Chu Liuxiang asked.

Yuan pondered for a good moment before responding, “There is this place that I have always wanted to go to as a child but never got the chance to for obvious reasons. Nine Flags Amusement Park.”

“An amusement park, huh? I have also never been to one before. Let’s go!” Chu Liuxiang immediately agreed, even appearing quite excited about it.

“What about the others?” Meixiu asked.

“We can invite everyone. I’ll let Yu Rou know later.” Yuan said.

Sometime later, Chu Liuxiang logged into Cultivation Online to invite the Demon Sealing Faction.

Meanwhile, Yuan and Meixiu went to visit the Eternal Lotuses.

“Brother! You’re finally back!” Yu Rou was nearly brought to tears after seeing Yuan’s face for the first time in over a year.

“Sorry…” Yuan apologized and explained the situation to her.

“I thought we would see each other more often after becoming your neighbor… how wrong I was…” she sighed.

Yuan smiled and said, “This is the nature of the cultivation world. Anyway, you’ve grown taller and even more pretty since I last saw you.”

“Trying to change the subject? Well, I’ll forgive you this time…” Yu Rou showed a bashful smile after hearing his praises.

“Then, changing the subject again, I’m here to give you an invitation. We’ve decided to visit the Nine Flags Amusement Park. Want to tag along?”

“I want to go!” Yu Rou responded immediately and without any hesitation.

She continued, “Oh, but can I invite Sister Jingyi and Sister Lianhua?”

“Of course.”

“Great! I’ll go let her know now!”

While Yu Rou went to speak with Xia Jingyi, Yuan visited Bai Lianhua.

“Hm? Who is it?” Bai Lianhua stopped her paperwork after hearing her door being knocked on.

“It’s me, Yuan.”

“Yuan?!”

Surprised by his voice, Bai Lianhua swiftly stood up and rushed to open the door.

“It’s really you! Are you okay?”

Yuan nodded and explained the situation.

“Thank god it’s only that. I was worried that something bad might’ve happened to you. I’m glad that wasn’t the case.” Bai Lianhua sighed in relief after learning of the truth.

“Sorry for making you worry.”

“It’s fine.”

“Anyway, did you need something from me?”

“Yes, I’d like to invite you to the amusement park.”

“A-amusement park?!” Bai Lianhua took a step back with a bewildered expression on her face.

‘Is he asking me out on a date?!’ she cried inwardly as her heart began racing like a sports car.

“If you’re too busy, I understand,” Yuan said as his gaze shifted to the piles of paperwork on her desk.

“I’ll come!” Bai Lianhua hastily responded. “I need a breath of fresh air once in a while.”

“Great. I’ll let you know when once I receive the other’s answers.” “Huh? The others?” Bai Lianhua wore a dumbfounded look on her face.

“Yes, I also invited the rest of the Demon Sealing Faction. Yu Rou and her friend will be coming, as well.”

“Is that so… I cannot wait.”

Bai Lianhua’s face flushed with redness after she realized her blunder.

‘Why did I think we’d be alone?!’ she cried inwardly.

Later that day, Yuan confirmed that everyone would be going to the amusement park.

“When do you want to go? I’ll schedule it for everyone.” Meifeng asked him during dinner.

“Since this is quite sudden, how does the day after tomorrow sound?” Yuan asked the others, as everyone invited was present at the dining table.

Once everyone agreed to go in two days, Meifeng went to schedule the event.

“An amusement park, huh? The closest thing we have to one at the Jaded Garden would be the playgrounds…” Wang Ming mused.

“I cannot wait!” Wang Bingbing said.

After dinner, Li Jinxi approached Yuan to make an appointment.

“Let’s have a spar tomorrow morning. I want to see how much stronger you’ve gotten in a year.”

“Sure. I’ll meet you at the training ground tomorrow.”

That night, Yuan went to the spare room with Meixiu, and the two of them embraced each other for the entire night.

“How’s your training with Kelan?” Yuan asked as they took a bath together.

“Even though we’ve trained with him for a year, his training sessions don’t get any easier. No matter how much we improve, he always has something even more difficult prepared for us. Because of that, we’re unable to relax. However, as a result, we improve consistently at a tremendous rate.”

“What about you? What kind of training made you lose track of time?”

Yuan gave her a detailed description of the training grounds in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary and the training he’d done so far.

“Your training is at an entirely different level… but why am I not surprised? Meanwhile, we’re still learning our basics and strengthening our foundations.” Meixiu sighed.

“The basics are just as important as whatever I am doing. Remember, I have experience from my past lives, so it’s only natural for me to be significantly ahead. My starting line is different from everyone else, so there’s no need to compare yourself to me.”

“I know, but it’s human nature to compare ourselves with others.”

After coming out of the bathroom, Yuan headed straight to the training ground, where Li Jinxi stood, the Golden Empress already firmly in her grasp.

Although it had been a year since they last sparred, and it was natural for people to change in that much time, Yuan could sense that there was something fundamentally different about the Li Jinxi standing before him now.

“Did something happen?” Yuan inquired with a curious gaze.

“Why would you ask me that?” Li Jinxi asked.

“Just felt like it, I guess.”

“I’ll tell you if you can beat me.”

Yuan smiled and said, “You should only utter such words if you’ve never lost to your opponent before.”

“Well, ‘I’ haven’t lost to the current you yet,'” Li Jinxi said in a weird tone.

“What?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

Li Jinxi couldn’t wait any longer and got into her fighting position.

“That stance…” Yuan’s eyes widened.

“Ready or not, here I come, Yuan! Or should I call you Tian Xin!?”

A wide smile spread across Li Jinxi’s face as she flew at Yuan, who looked stunned and was completely caught off guard by the way she addressed him.

“Jin Xi?!” he exclaimed.

chapters.title: Chapter 1672 Amusement Park
chapters.body:
Chapter 1672 Amusement Park

Chapter 1672 Amusement Park

ƒrēewebnovel.com

Yuan hastily retrieved his Abyssal Dagger to block Li Jinxi’s attack.

“So you finally remembered your past life?” Yuan inquired as they clashed.

“I won’t say anything until you beat me!” Li Jinxi repeated.

“But you’ve already said a lot…” Yuan smiled.

However, instead of going all out to defeat her quickly, Yuan was curious to see how strong Li Jinxi had become after a year of training with Kelan and regaining Jin Xi’s memories.

Their clashes were so intense that, if not for the formation surrounding the training ground, each strike would have caused the entire mountain to tremble violently.

“I know this is not all you’ve got!” Li Jinxi said to him a few minutes later when neither of them were gaining an advantage.

“I can say the same to you. You seem weaker compared to our last spar a year ago.” Yuan smiled.

“Hmph. If I truly went all out, this mountain would perish in the blink of an eye!”

“Only the mountain? This world would perish if I went all out.” Yuan shrugged.

“You will be returning to the Nine Heavens soon, right? Let’s postpone our match until then. We can only go all out in the Nine Heavens, anyway,” she suggested.

“I can’t give an exact date of my return, but it won’t be too long. I also agree that we won’t be able to fight seriously unless it’s within the Nine Heavens, so I don’t mind postponing our match until I return.”

After a slight pause, Yuan continued with a provocative smile, “It’ll give you more time to mentally prepare for your inevitable defeat, too.”

“Then it’s settled.” Li Jinxi said as she stowed away her weapon.

“Are you really not going to say anything until then?” Yuan then asked.

“That’s what I said, no?”

Without saying anything else, Li Jinxi left the training ground, leaving Yuan standing there with a dumbfounded face.

After leaving, Li Jinxi returned to her room and stood before a mirror with a perplexed expression on her face.

“So it’s real…” she muttered in a dazed voice.

Early in the morning the following day, everyone from the Demon Sealing Faction and three individuals from Eternal Lotuses gathered at the gates of the Dragon Spiral Mountain, where a long limousine picked them up and drove them to the Dragon Spiral Mountain’s private airport.

“It’s Yuan and the rest of the Demon Sealing Faction! They’re finally leaving the Dragon Spiral Mountain!” The people hired by the government to watch over the Dragon Spiral Mountain were shocked to see Yuan and the others leave the mountain, and they quickly notified their superiors.

“When did you buy a private jet?” Yuan asked Meifeng when they arrived at the airport to see a luxurious private jet with the name of their faction printed on its body.

“A few months ago,” she said. “We have plenty of money, so you don’t have to worry about our finances.”

“I am not worried at all. I was just curious.” Yuan said with a smile. Even if they were completely out of money, he could easily sell some treasures and gain a fortune.

With the amount of treasures in his possession, as well as what he could acquire, it wouldn’t be an exaggeration to say the Demon Sealing Faction was one of the wealthiest in the entire world.

Once they boarded the jet, Meifeng drove it herself. The flight was only an hour long.

When they landed, the group entered another transport vehicle that brought them directly to the amusement park.

“Huh? Why is it so empty? Is the amusement park closed?” Yu Rou muttered out loud after seeing the empty parking lot.

Yuan used his divine sense to peer inside the amusement park and spotted several workers going about their tasks. However, not a single customer was present within the park’s grounds.

Meifeng then spoke, “Oh, that’s because I reserved the whole amusement park for today, so we’re the only ones here.”

“Seriously…?” Yu Rou stared at her with wide eyes.

“The experience will be different if we’re alone…” Chu Liuxiang sighed.

Meifeng shrugged and explained herself, “In case you forgot, you’re all celebrities. If I hadn’t done this, we would’ve been swarmed by people.”

Yuan then said, “Although the experience will be different than what I had anticipated, I understand why it was done. I don’t really mind it, either. Also, this means we don’t need to wait in line for the rides.”

“I actually prefer that we’re alone since we don’t need to deal with anyone,” Wang Bingbing said.

Sometime later, they entered the amusement park.

“What should we do first?” Yu Rou asked.

“We have plenty of time to try everything, so why don’t we explore the park clockwise?” Yuan suggested.

“Sounds like a plan.”

Thus, the group began exploring the park, experiencing every attraction one at a time.

However, several attractions later, Wang Ming sighed out loud, “This is more boring than I’d anticipated. Is it because this park was built for mortals? I don’t feel any excitement from the rollercoasters.”

As cultivators, their senses had been significantly enhanced, and things like rollercoasters felt like they were moving at a snail’s pace. As for the height, it wasn’t even worth mentioning.

“It’s a little disappointing, but there’s nothing we can do about it. After all, this place wasn’t made for cultivators.” Yuan said.

They continued to explore the amusement park until they experienced all of the attractions.

“Wow, that turned out to be a chore.” Shi Lang laughed out loud as they left the amusement park.

“Although there wasn’t any blood-pumping and heartstopping excitement, I still had fun. It’s like a walk in the park—one that is more elaborate, I guess.” Yu Rou said.

“I agree. It wasn’t that bad.” Chu Liuxiang said.

After leaving the amusement park, they returned to the Dragon Spiral Mountain to have a feast. Since Meifeng’s cooking was better than most restaurants out there, they decided to save a trip and dine at home.

“Brother, do you have anything else planned?” Yu Rou asked Yuan as they feasted. Yuan shook his head and said, “Not really, but if there’s anything you want to do, don’t hesitate to let me know.”

“Okay. Let me think about it.” she nodded.

chapters.title: Chapter 1673 Returning To The Primal Expanse
chapters.body:
Chapter 1673 Returning To The Primal Expanse

Chapter 1673 Returning to the Primal Expanse

At the end of the feast, Yu Rou said, “Brother, if you don’t have anything to do, why don’t you give us a concert? It’s been a while since I have listened to your music.”

Yuan nodded, “Sure. How does tomorrow noon sound? I can do it in our training ground.”

“Then it’s decided! I’ll let the rest of my faction know, so make sure you have enough seats!” she eagerly said.

“Okay.”

Later that night, Yuan cuddled with Meixiu. He’d cuddled with Chu Liuxiang yesterday after his short spar with Li Jinxi ended, and the two of them didn’t leave the room until the very next day until it was time for them to head to the amusement park.

The following day, after embracing Meixiu for an entire night and morning, Yuan held a private concert in his training ground with the Demon Sealing Faction and Eternal Lotuses as his audience.

During the concert, Yuan played all sorts of instruments, from the zither to the piano to obscure instruments most of them had never seen before.

Meanwhile, government agencies around the world were in the midst of a meeting. The subject of the matter was, of course, about Yuan.

“Yesterday at 0600, Yuan and the entire Demon Sealing Faction left the Dragon Spiral Mountain for the first time in over a year.”

“His sister, Yu Rou, her close friend, Xia Jingyi, and the leader of the Eternal Lotuses, Bai Lianhua, was also with them.”

“They flew from the Dragon Spiral Mountain’s private airport to Jade City’s airport, and from there, they went to the Nine Flags Amusement Park.”

“The Demon Sealing Faction’s manager, Meifeng, who used to work for the Yu Family, had reserved the entire amusement park for the whole day. They spent six hours and thirty-two minutes in the amusement park and returned straight to the Dragon Spiral Mountain.”

Every detail of the Demon Sealing Faction’s movement was reported to the people in the meeting.

“He went radio silent and holed up inside the Dragon Spiral Mountain for over a year, then came out just to visit the amusement park? What do you guys think of this?”

“Bastard doesn’t even put us in his eyes. How dare he have fun at an amusement park after threatening us? He’s acting like we don’t even exist!”

“Honestly, I’d rather he ignore us than pay attention to us.”

“That’s what a coward would say! How can you let a single man who’d just reached adulthood scare you with some threat?!”

“You say that, but I know you’re also scared of him! This man has the power to destroy the world! We should ensure he doesn’t see us as a threat rather than make an enemy out of him!” “Let’s change the topic for a moment. Did we manage to find anyone close to him who’s willing to spy on him for us?”

“We’re still working on it, but we’re making progress. We’re currently in contact with several members of Eternal Lotuses trying to make a deal.”

“Increase the rewards if they’re still hesitant—enough to blow their hesitancy away in an instant!”

In the end, they decided to continue monitoring Yuan from a distance—at least until they found a way to deal with him.

Meanwhile, Yuan ended the concert after four hours. Then, for the rest of the day, Yuan sparred with the members of Eternal Lotuses to see their growth.

“Since it’s been a while, let’s end today with a spiritual water bath,” Yuan said, and the members cheered with excitement. freёwebnovel.com

After the bath, everyone returned home.

For the next week, Yuan devoted all his time to Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang, making up for the past year he had been away, with most of that time spent making love.

“I’ll be returning to the Primal Expanse today,” Yuan announced after spending 13 days on Earth.

“Try not to disappear on us for too long again, okay?” Chu Liuxiang teased.

Yuan nodded with a bittersweet smile.

After saying his goodbyes, Yuan returned to the Primal Expanse.

“Welcome back.” Yingzi was there to greet him upon his return, and all of the skewers she had before he left were gone.

“Did Long Yejun come by while I was gone?” he asked her.

“Yes, he came by two days ago and left this behind.”

Yingzi handed him a teleport stone before continuing to speak. “He said everything’s been prepared, and you can use this teleport stone whenever you’re ready.”

“Alright.”

After leaving Long Yejun’s home and returning to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary, Yuan shattered the teleport stone and entered the rift with Yingzi.

When he emerged from the rift, Yuan found himself on a remote island that had an active volcano that was larger than any mountain he’d seen before.

“Where is this?” Yuan muttered out loud, as this place wasn’t part of the Divine Dragon Sanctuary.

Suddenly, a powerful presence filled the entire island.

“Are you the one Prince Yejun said will be coming?” A stern voice rippled.

“Yes, I am Yuan.”

A half-naked, tall, bulky man with a bald head appeared before them a moment later.

“Let’s make this clear. I only agreed to lend you my precious workshop because I owe Prince Yejun a favor. I don’t care if you’re the fourth Dragon God’s Descendant. If you make a mess of my workshop, I will kill you.”

Yuan nodded with a confident smile and said, “Don’t worry, I’ll be out of your hair—your sight before you even know it.”

“By the way, is Long Yejun not here?”

“No, but he said he’ll be back soon.”

“Do you mind if I wait until he returns?”

“I don’t care, but I only agreed to lend you my workshop for ten years—not even a second more. Oh, one more thing. The timer started when I notified you a month ago.”

Yuan silently nodded.

‘It’s been a while since I created something, so I will be a little rusty, but it shouldn’t take more than a few months to finish my treasures.’ He thought to himself.

chapters.title: Chapter 1674 Metal Dragon Monarch
chapters.body:
Chapter 1674 Metal Dragon Monarch

Chapter 1674 Metal Dragon Monarch

“Can you show me the workshop for now?” Yuan asked the bald man. “By the way, how should I address you?”

The man lifted his head in a prideful manner and introduced himself, “I am the Metal Dragon Monarch, Refined Obsidian Dragon! I am also one of the few beings in the Primal Expanse capable of creating treasures! As a matter of fact, nobody dares to claim they are number one as long as I am still alive!”

Creating treasures was unheard of in the Primal Expanse until a human skilled in treasure crafting appeared hundreds of millions of years ago. Since then, they have acquired the ability and knowledge to craft treasures.

However, despite its potential, crafting treasures remained a niche practice in the Primal Expanse, as the knowledge and techniques were originally designed solely for humans.

While some, like the Refined Obsidian Dragon, have refined and further developed their techniques to new heights that allowed them to craft treasures for Primals and Predators, most lacked the talent to achieve such mastery and remained mediocre in their abilities.

The lack of treasures for Primals and Predators also contributed to why the beings of this world preferred using human appearances, as that allowed them to wield treasures. That said, only the truly powerful are capable of acquiring treasures, which is why only the top beings in the Primal Expanse take the form of humans.

Sometime later, the Refined Obsidian Dragon led Yuan to a massive cave located at the base of the volcano.

The workshop was so massive that Yuan couldn’t help but think it had been built for giants. He glanced at the Refined Obsidian Dragon, whose human form was also significantly larger than normal, resembling that of a miniature giant.

All sorts of tools hung on the wall, but the most eye-catching was the elegant black hammer that hung prominently above the furnace, its surface gleaming with a mysterious aura.

Yingzi also looked around with interest. This was her first time being in this kind of environment, and it piqued her interest.

The Refined Obsidian Dragon took a seat near the entrance, his gaze fixed on Yuan and Yingzi. It was as if he were a vigilant guard, ensuring they wouldn’t cause any trouble in his workshop.

Yuan also sat down and closed his eyes to cultivate.

Two days later, Yuan opened his eyes when he sensed a familiar presence appearing on the remote island.

Long Yejun showed up at the workshop shortly after.

“Were you waiting for me?” he asked Yuan.

“Yes. I figured you wanted to watch.”

“I sure do! Also, this is for you.” Long Yejun retrieved a fist-sized crystal that crackled with electric energy, tiny arcs of lightning dancing across its surface.

“What is this?” Yuan asked.

“The Sky Splitting Dragon wanted me to give you this as your reward for conquering his training ground in record time.”

Yuan accepted the crystal and inspected it with interest.
