“T-that is!” The Refined Obsidian Dragon’s eyes widened with shock after seeing the crystal.

He hastily rushed over to get a closer view of it.

“What a wonderful material! I could craft something even better than my previous masterpiece if I had this!” He glanced at Yuan and frowned, “I doubt you have the ability to handle this material yourself. Even if you can somehow handle it, you’ll definitely waste its potential! Instead of wasting such a precious material, you should give it to me. In fact, I am probably the only one in this world who is qualified enough to use it!”

“Refined Obsidian Dragon, even if Brother Yuan is willing to give you the material, the Sky Splitting Dragon won’t approve of it since you didn’t earn it.” Long Yejun shook his head.

Yuan smiled and said, “Sorry, but I have no intention of giving it to you.”

“You ignorant bastard! Do you even know what you’re holding?! Do you even know how to use it?!” The Refined Obsidian Dragon exclaimed angrily.

“I have never seen this material before, but I will understand it once I spend some time with it. Also, who said that I wouldn’t be able to use it properly? I certainly didn’t say anything like that.” Yuan shrugged.

The Refined Obsidian Dragon then spoke, “You seem confident in your abilities. In that case, let’s put you to the test. If you can craft something that satisfies me and prove you can handle this material, I’ll no longer attempt to take it from you.”

“However,” he continued, his eyes narrowing, “if you prove incapable of handling this type of material, you’ll have to hand it over to me. No, I’ll take it from you!”

Yuan frowned and said, “I don’t mind you wanting to test me, but why is there no benefit for me, regardless of the outcome? If I prove my abilities, nothing changes, but if I fail, you’ll take my material. Does that sound fair to you?”

“As the one suggesting this test, you should, at the very least, put something on the line! How else will you atone for belittling me?”

The Refined Obsidian Dragon gritted his teeth, struggling to suppress his irritation. Yet, he couldn’t find any flaw in Yuan’s logic. After all, even he had to admit that he was the aggressor in this situation.

“Very well. If you can prove yourself, I will give you the most valuable material in my possession as an apology!”

Yuan nodded with a smile, “That’s more like it.”

“Give me a few hours to prepare the materials for your test.” The Refined Obsidian Dragon said before leaving the workshop.

“I’m sorry about this, Brother Yuan,” Long Yejun said, his tone apologetic. “The Refined Obsidian Dragon may come off as an asshole, but it’s only because he’s incredibly passionate about his craft.”

“In fact, even if you were the Dragon God himself, he would still try to take the material from you if he deemed you unqualified.”

Yuan shook his head and said, “You don’t need to tell me. I understand where he’s coming from, nor do I blame him.”

“Thank you.” Long Yejun smiled.

chapters.title: Chapter 1675 Crafting His First Treasure
chapters.body:
Chapter 1675 Crafting His First Treasure

Chapter 1675 Crafting His First Treasure

The Refined Obsidian Dragon returned to the workshop several hours later with a leather pouch filled with materials.

He tossed the bag before Yuan and said, “If you can craft a treasure while using half of the materials in there, I’ll consider you worthy.”

Yuan grabbed the bag and poured all of the materials out. There were twenty-one materials in total, and he didn’t recognize any of them. freēwebnovel.com

However, instead of panicking, Yuan smiled and muttered, “Interesting. This will be fun.”

He suddenly sat before the materials, grabbed one of them, and started inspecting it in silence.

“…”

The Refined Obsidian Dragon’s eyebrows twitched at Yuan’s actions, and he sneered inwardly, ‘At least he isn’t completely incompetent.’

After spending several hours examining every single material there in great detail, Yuan closed his eyes and began doing mental simulations. “What’s he doing?” Long Yejun asked the Refined Obsidian Dragon. Despite being a blacksmith himself, he didn’t understand Yuan’s actions.

The Refined Obsidian Dragon narrowed his gaze at Yuan and pondered for a moment before responding, “I believe he’s doing mental stimulations. He’s basically crafting the treasure inside his head right now.”

“What? You can do something like that? I have never even heard of this until today.” Long Yejun couldn’t fathom crafting treasures inside his mind.

“It’s possible, but it’s extremely difficult to achieve since you need to know the materials perfectly, and you also need to be incredibly knowledgeable and experienced in blacksmithing.” The Refined Obsidian Dragon clenched his fists in frustration as he spoke.

‘Mental simulations? Hahaha! Even I cannot do mental simulations! There’s no way he’s doing it properly! This is just a waste of time!’ he mocked inwardly.

A few hours later, Yuan stood up and said, “Okay, I am ready to craft the treasure. Do you have a hammer I can borrow?”

The Refined Obsidian Dragon grabbed an old hammer and tossed it to him.

Yuan looked at the worn hammer with a slight smile and said, “It’ll only make you look worse if I pass the test with this half-broken tool.”

“Here, you can use mine.” Long Yejun retrieved his personal hammer and offered it to Yuan.

However, he shook his head, refusing to accept it.

“Thanks, but I don’t need it.”

He walked to the anvil and controlled the materials from afar with his Qi Manipulation, throwing all of them into the furnace. “What the…?” Long Yejun’s eyes widened at his bizarre actions.

“Hahaha! What the fuck is he doing?! How can he throw all of the materials in her at once?!” The Refined Obsidian Dragon burst out laughing.

They were taught to deal with one material at a time, so Yuan’s actions made him look like a complete fool who knew nothing about smithing.

“I can’t believe I actually thought he was competent even for a second! He had me fooled in the beginning!”

Yuan completely ignored the Refined Obsidian Dragon’s loud mockery and focused on the materials inside the furnace.

A few days later, Yuan retrieved a single piece of material from the furnace. This material had no impurities and was perfectly refined.

When the Refined Obsidian Dragon saw this, his eyes nearly popped out of their sockets.

“What the fuck?! That’s impossible!” he cried out loud.

“He actually managed to refine that many materials at once, even though each one needed to be handled differently and required a different refining time?” Long Yejun muttered in disbelief, struggling to comprehend the feat.

The Refined Obsidian Dragon felt an intense urge to rush over and inspect the refined material up close, but he held back. It wasn’t out of respect for Yuan but rather because of an unspoken rule among blacksmiths to never interrupt one once they have begun their work.

After inspecting the refined material and being satisfied with it, Yuan placed it on top of the black anvil before him and started hammering away with the damaged tool.

The Refined Obsidian Dragon felt his heart skip a beat with every strike Yuan delivered to the material, his ears captivated by the mythical sound that resonated each time the hammer made contact.

“What is this sound? How is he creating such a beautiful melody with that tool?” Long Yejun muttered, his eyes wide with awe.

Even Yingzi, who knew nothing about smithing, could sense that something extraordinary was unfolding before her. She found herself completely absorbed by the performance, unable to look away.

“The refined material… his hammering techniques… the sound it produces… it’s beyond even my understanding…”

The Refined Obsidian Dragon fell to his knees as the crushing reality of the vast difference in skill between himself and Yuan set in. It was an unfathomable gap, leaving him overwhelmed and humbled.

“It turns out I was the ignorant one all along…”

Tears flowed from his eyes.

After striking the anvil for several hours without pause, Yuan finally completed the treasure, which took the form of a sword. It radiated a powerful aura that would easily be graded as a peak-quality Empyrean-grade treasure in the Nine Heavens.

<Congratulations! You have crafted your first treasure!>

<Congratulations! You are the first player to ever craft an Empyrean-grade treasure!>

<You have acquired the title ‘Divine Blacksmith’!>

Yuan walked to the Refined Obsidian Dragon, who was still on his knees and placed the treasure before him.

“I’m done. Please examine it.”

“…”

“…”

“You’re done…? Already?”

The Refined Obsidian Dragon muttered in a dazed voice.

“It’s a bit slower than usual, but it can’t be helped since I am still rusty, so thank you for giving me this chance to warm up.”

The Refined Obsidian Dragon slowly reached for the sword before him, but he stopped himself centimeters away and withdrew his hand.

“I don’t deserve to touch it, let alone examine it. I am also not qualified to test you. I apologize for my ignorance and disrespectful behavior earlier. I hope you’ll forgive me, Master Yuan.” The Refined Obsidian Dragon apologized in a sincere tone as he kowtowed respectfully before Yuan.

Long Yejun stared at the scene, his face filled with bewilderment. He could never have imagined witnessing the Refined Obsidian Dragon behave in such a manner.

“He’s showing Brother Yuan more respect than he does to the Dragon God…” he sighed with a bittersweet smile.

chapters.title: Chapter 1676 The Refined Obsidian Dragon’S Sincerity
chapters.body:
Chapter 1676 The Refined Obsidian Dragon’S Sincerity

Chapter 1676 The Refined Obsidian Dragon’s Sincerity

“I don’t mind forgiving you for your unruly behavior, but I will need you to prove your sincerity first,” Yuan said to the Refined Obsidian Dragon.

“How can I show my sincerity? I will do anything!” The Refined Obsidian Dragon shouted.

“Although I can make do with the materials I possess currently, it would be great if I had more. Do you understand what I mean?” Yuan spoke with a calm expression on his face.

It took a second, but the Refined Obsidian Dragon responded with great enthusiasm, “Of course, I understand! It would be an honor if Master Yuan used my materials for his creations! Please wait here while I gather my finest materials!”

The Refined Obsidian Dragon rushed out of the workshop the very next moment.

“Brother Yuan… how many more surprises are under your wings? Where in god’s name did you learn how to craft treasures like that?!” Long Yejun asked him with shining eyes.

“I am mostly self-taught,” Yuan said with a calm smile.

“Self-taught?! Surely, you are teasing me.” Long Yejun didn’t believe him even for a second.

“I am serious.”

“…”

“How long did it take for you to achieve that level of mastery? It must’ve taken billions of years. I am beginning to wonder if you’re actually older than me…” Long Yejun continued to inquire.

“I am definitely nowhere as old as you, and it took me several thousand years to achieve my current level.”

“…”

Long Yejun was speechless.

‘It only took him several thousand years to achieve his level of mastery?! My nap lasts longer than that!’ he exclaimed inwardly.

However, after thinking about it, Yuan had managed to conquer the Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp and the Sky Splitting Dragon’s Tribulation in a little over a year.

‘If I see it in that manner, I guess a few thousand years is actually a very long time…’

“By the way…” Yuan suddenly grabbed the sword he’d just created off the ground and offered it to Long Yejun, “You can have this.”

“What? Are you sure?” Long Yejun stared at him with wide eyes.

He nodded, “Take it. You’ve been extremely helpful to me without expecting anything in return, so consider this my way of thanking you.”

Long Yejun nodded and accepted the sword. When he held the treasure in his grasp, and its aura seeped into his skin, his entire body trembled.

“What a masterpiece! The treasure I crafted is trash in comparison!”

“Don’t undersell your own creation. That earring is also a piece of art.” Yuan smiled.

“I am only speaking the truth, Brother Yuan. I don’t know how to explain this, but I have never held a treasure like this before. Compared to the other treasures, there’s just something fundamentally different about this treasure.” Sometime later, the Refined Obsidian Dragon returned to the workshop. This time, he brought back with him an even larger sack.

“Here you go, Master Yuan! You can use these materials as you please!” The Refined Obsidian Dragon poured the materials on the ground, forming a small mountain.

Yuan’s eyebrows twitched as he took in the sheer quantity of treasures laid out before him. In his past life, he had encountered all sorts of treasures, including some of the most valuable and powerful materials. Yet, every single material before him now was entirely unknown.

‘If I brought these treasures back to the Nine Heavens, they’d be priceless as one-of-a-kind treasures!’ Yuan thought to himself.

However, the Refined Obsidian Dragon was not finished and continued, “Of course, I didn’t forget about the other thing.”

He retrieved a black rock and offered it to Yuan. freewebnσvel.cøm

“This is my most valuable material—the Infinite Obsidian. I found this rock floating in the Infinite Expanse six hundred million years ago, and I have been trying to refine it since then. Unfortunately, I lack the skills and experience to refine it, much less turn it into a treasure. However, I believe you, Master Yuan, will be able to achieve what I could not.”

Yuan accepted the rock and inspected it with immense interest. His eyes flickered as he examined every little detail.

‘What a mythical material…’

Although he didn’t know what kind of treasure it was, Yuan could tell it was as valuable as the materials he used as the core of Number One Under Heaven.

‘No, this thing might be even more powerful… If I use this to enhance Number One Under Heaven, who knows what will happen!’ Just the mere thought of enhancing his masterpiece sent chills through Yuan’s entire being. He had always believed that Number One Under Heaven was already a perfect treasure, beyond any further improvement. Yet, the appearance of this unknown material had turned his understanding on its head, igniting a surge of excitement within him.

‘Unfortunately… my cultivation is lacking. I will need to improve my cultivation before I can even attempt to refine this material.’

“Thank you. I will put this treasure to good use. You have also proved your sincerity, so I’ll forgive you and let bygones be bygones,” he said to the Refined Obsidian Dragon after putting the Infinite Obsidian inside his spatial ring.

“Your words mean more than you can fathom, Master Yuan!”

He nodded and said, “I am going to start crafting my treasure now.”

Hearing this, the Refined Obsidian Dragon made a swift grasping motion toward the massive hammer hanging on the wall. As it flew through the air, the hammer began to shrink, transforming until it was small enough to be held comfortably, even by an ordinary human.

“Please use this hammer, Master Yuan!”

Yuan accepted the hammer and smiled, “It’s a nice hammer.”

“Thank you very much!” The Refined Obsidian Dragon looked as if he was about to cry.

‘This guy has gone crazy…’ Long Yejun looked at the Refined Obsidian Dragon while shaking his head inwardly.

“Master Yuan, do you mind if I watch you craft? I swear I won’t disturb you.” The Refined Obsidian Dragon then asked with begging eyes.

Yuan nodded, “Sure.”

“Thank you very much!!!” The Refined Obsidian Dragon actually shed tears this time.

chapters.title: Chapter 1677 Robes Of Destruction
chapters.body:
Chapter 1677 Robes Of Destruction

Chapter 1677 Robes of Destruction

Yuan sat before the mountain of materials and proceeded to inspect each one for several days.

After completing his inspection a week later, Yuan closed his eyes and began doing simulations inside his head.

Several more days later, he stood up, picked the needed materials from the pile, and tossed them directly into the furnace.

“It’s finally time!” the Refined Obsidian Dragon exclaimed with excitement, his eyes gleaming like a child about to enter his favorite ride in an amusement park after waiting in line for hours.

While the materials were being refined, Yuan retrieved the Dragon of Destruction’s skin and Number One Under Heaven, using it to cut the tough skin.

“What is that treasure?!?!?!” The Refined Obsidian Dragon’s eyes popped out of their sockets after seeing Number One Under Heaven.

Long Yejun swallowed nervously and mumbled in a dazed voice, “No wonder why he didn’t even bat an eyelid when giving me that sword… That’s a true masterpiece…”

By the time Yuan finished cutting up the skin, the materials inside the furnace just finished their refinement.

After retrieving the materials, he immediately began combining the Dragon of Destruction’s skin and the refined materials.

Clang! Clang! Clang!

Beautiful sounds filled the workshop.

“His movement and techniques are even better than before!” The Refined Obsidian Dragon’s jaw dropped.

“So he wasn’t joking when he said he was rusty…” Long Yejun swallowed nervously.

“It’s too vast…” The Refined Obsidian Dragon suddenly mumbled in a dazed voice.

“What is?” Long Yejun asked. freewebnσvel.cѳm

“Our difference. I knew he was much better than me, but I could’ve never imagined it was to such an extent. I am no different than a baby in his eyes.”

“Don’t punch yourself down too much… You’re still one of the best blacksmiths in the Primal Expanse.” Long Yejun sighed.

“Pei!” Refined Obsidian Dragon suddenly spat on the ground. “One of the best? Don’t you ever say that to me again! I feel nothing but shame when I recall how prideful I was before Master Yuan opened my eyes! I was just a frog in a well, unaware of how truly vast the sky is!”

A few days later.

<Congratulations! You are the first player to ever craft a Celestial-grade treasure!>

<You have acquired the title ‘True Divine Blacksmith’>

[True Divine Blacksmith]

[Description: All of your creations below Celestial-grade will always result in peak quality!]

Yuan looked at the result of his work with a satisfied smile.

[Robes of Destruction]

[Grade: Celestial]

[Quality: Peak]

[Description: A piece of art crafted by Player Yuan. It exudes a menacing aura. When worn, the wearer will be able to use the technique ‘Dragon of Destruction’s Ultimate Chaos’ once every 72 hours. Increases the wearer’s Physical Defense by 100,000%. ‘Dragon of Destruction’s Scales’ will automatically activate when the wearer’s life is threatened, negating all damage for 10 seconds(30 days cooldown). This treasure is indestructible.] [Dragon of Destruction’s Ultimate Chaos]

[Description: Absorbs a portion of all damage taken, which can then be released with up to 1,000% increased power.]

Yuan immediately wore the Robes of Destruction, a black and red outer garment that appeared simple yet elegant.

“I am speechless, Master Yuan! I thought I couldn’t respect you any more than I already was, but you’ve proven me wrong!” The Refined Obsidian Dragon fell to his knees and started kowtowing to Yuan. “You’re exaggerating.” Yuan smiled.

“Please! Can this humble dragon take a closer look at the completed treasure?!” he begged.

“Sure.”

“I will forever be in your debt!”

The Refined Obsidian Dragon jumped to his feet and rushed over to inspect the Robes of Destruction.

“Does it have a name yet?” he asked.

Yuan nodded, “Robes of Destruction.”

“A powerful name, befitting of someone of your exalted stature!” Refined Obsidian Dragon nodded.

“…”

Yuan could’ve never expected the Refined Obsidian Dragon to be so good at kissing ass.

“Are you going to make more treasures?” Long Yejun suddenly asked him.

“Yes, but not from scratch. I am going to upgrade an existing treasure next,” he revealed.

“Upgrading an existing treasure? You can do something like that?!” Refined Obsidian Dragon exclaimed in shock. He always thought that once a treasure was completed, it could no longer be altered.

“I’ll show you how it’s done in a moment,” Yuan said.

Refined Obsidian Dragon nodded and swiftly backed away, returning to his original location.

Yuan retrieved the Starry Abyss and the Baneful Crystal—the treasure he’d acquired at the center of Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp.

“That dagger gives off a similar aura to his sword,” The Refined Obsidian Dragon noticed this detail and mused out loud, unaware they were both Soul Weapons, something that did not exist within the Primal Expanse due to a lack of usage of weapons.

“And that material… it gives me the shivers. What is it?”

“That treasure belonged to the Poison Dragon Monarch, Baneful Dragon.” Long Yejun said.

“I see… But will he be okay handling such a poisonous material? While his smithing abilities are second to none, his cultivation, on the other hand…”

“Oh, he’ll be fine. He acquired it from the very center of the Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp, after all.”

“Seriously? Just how talented is he?”

“You have no idea…” Long Yejun chuckled.

Their conversation stopped at that point when Yuan started working.

‘Ji Ran, it will be a little chaotic for a bit. Please bear with it.’ Yuan warned him before tossing both the Starry Abyss and the Baneful Crystal inside the furnace.

When he retrieved them from the furnace a few hours later, only the Starry Abyss remained, glowing purple and blue.

“He fused the completed treasure with the material while they were inside the furnace… I have tried doing that before, but it would always completely mess up the treasure, so I figured it was impossible.” Refined Obsidian Dragon sighed.

Sometime later.

<Congratulations! Starry Abyss has evolved and gained new abilities!>

Yuan gazed at the Starry Abyss with a satisfied smile. The once fully black dagger now shimmered with shades of purple and blue, resembling a vibrant nebula.

chapters.title: Chapter 1678 Starry Abyss Upgrade
chapters.body:
Chapter 1678 Starry Abyss Upgrade

Chapter 1678 Starry Abyss Upgrade

<Starry Abyss has evolved into Baneful Abyss>

<Baneful Abyss has learned the ability ‘Mark of the Baneful’>

[Mark of the Baneful]

[Description: When this weapon inflicts damage, the victim will be inflicted with the Mark of the Baneful. Those affected by Mark of the Baneful will be severely poisoned and take continuous damage until their life is completely extinguished. The poison damage will increase over time. Mark of the Baneful can only be dispelled by the Baneful Abyss.]

<Baneful Abyss has learned the ability ‘Revenge of the Baneful’>

[Revenge of the Baneful]

[Description: Whenever someone inflicted with Mark of the Baneful dies, their body will explode, automatically releasing a poison mist in a 100-meter radius. Those caught in the poison mist will also be inflicted with Mark of the Baneful.]

<Baneful Abyss has learned the ability ‘Poison Acceleration’>

[Poison Acceleration]

[Description: The Baneful Abyss will accelerate the effects of all poison in its vicinity.]

<Baneful Abyss has learned the ability ‘Poison Absorption’>

[Poison Absoption]

[Description: The Baneful Abyss has the ability to absorb all poison to enhance its own strength.]

Even without needing to test its abilities, Yuan could tell that the poison within the Baneful Abyss rivaled even Heaven’s Bane.

‘Revenge of the Baneful… this will allow me to kill a crowds of enemies with extreme ease.’ Yuan thought to himself after reading its second ability.

After its upgrade, the Baneful Abyss has transformed into a terrifying weapon that, if misused, could turn the heavens upside down.

“What an ominous aura that weapon now exudes. Even I don’t dare to approach it.” Refined Obsidian Dragon swallowed nervously after sensing the Baneful Abyss’ sinister aura, even taking a few steps back.

“It gives off the same dangerous aura as the Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp.” Long Yejun commented.

Yuan looked at Refined Obsidian Dragon and showed a teasing smile, “You don’t want to inspect this? I’ll even let you touch it.”

Refined Obsidian Dragon began sweating as he started making excuses, “I don’t dare touch it… It’s too much for a humble blacksmith such as myself. I would only dirty your beautiful creation…”

“It’s fine. Come, give it a touch. But don’t touch the blade. You might die.” Yuan beckoned.

Refined Obsidian Dragon cried inwardly as he reluctantly approached Yuan. However, once he got closer and saw just how beautiful the dagger was, his passion overwhelmed his fear.

“Wow! What a truly unique treasure! I can even feel a soul residing inside!”

“You can tell? This is what’s called a Soul Weapon. They’re different from ordinary treasures, as they give birth to their own consciousness.”

“Such a miraculous treasure exists?!” Long Yejun also approached them to take a closer look.

“A Soul Weapon… how do you make something like this?” he then asked.

“Some treasures have the potential to become a Soul Weapon if you use it long enough,” Yuan explained.

“Does that mean I can turn the sword you gave me into a Soul Weapon?” Long Yejun asked.

“It’s possible.” He acknowledged.

“I guess I should start learning to wield the sword now…” Long Yejun mumbled.

“By the way, Refined Obsidian Dragon, this hammer of yours has already begun forming its consciousness. If you continue to use it, it should become a Soul Weapon in the future.”

“Really?!” Refined Obsidian Dragon was immensely delighted to hear this information.

“Anyway, I’m going to continue crafting some more treasures. I still have plenty of Dragon of Destruction’s skin left.” Yuan suddenly said.

“I also have the Sky Splitting Dragon’s material, too.”

Yuan resumed his mental simulations shortly after.

A few hours later, he tossed the Sky Splitting Dragon’s treasure into the furnace before he began cutting up the Dragon of Destruction’s skin.

“Looks like he’s going to make a new pair of shoes to replace the ones I destroyed next.” Long Yejun muttered.

Sure enough, Yuan wanted to replace the Stygian Boots next.

Sometime later.

<Congratulations! You have successfully crafted a Celestial-grade treasure!>

[Boots of Swift Destruction]

[Grade: Celestial]

[Quality: Peak]

[Description: A treasure crafted by Player Yuan. When worn, the wearer will be able to use the technique ‘Sky Sundering Steps’ and ‘Lightning Strides’. Grants the wearer immunity to most movement restrictions. This equipment can be charged with Lightning Qi to increase the wearer’s speed by up to 1,000%]

[Sky Sundering Steps]

[Description: A movement technique that allows the wearer to turn into lightning and travel at the speed of light.]

[Lightning Strides]

[Description: Traverse the Heavens at unimaginable speed. Instantly move up to 100,000 meters in any direction in the blink of an eye.]

Yuan slipped on the Boots of Swift Destruction, their sleek black design accented with bold red soles, perfectly complementing his Robes of Destruction.

“How much longer before the Dragon God Festival begins?” Yuan asked Long Yejun.

“We still have about ten months. Do you plan on training some more?”

He nodded.

“But first, I’d like to test out some of my newly learned techniques.”

“Then we can go back to the Endless Arena.”

“You’re leaving already?” Refined Obsidian Dragon asked, seemingly reluctant to see him go.

“Yes. I want to prepare myself as much as possible for the Dragon God Festival.”

“Then, if you ever need to craft something in the future, my workshop will always be available for you. As for the remaining materials… you can take them with you.”

“Are you sure?” Yuan asked.

He’d only intended to use some of them.

“I’m sure. Although it was short, I have learned a lot about blacksmithing from watching you, and I don’t want to feel like I’d stolen your techniques.”

“I allowed you to watch because I wanted you to learn from it, but if you’re willing to give me all of these wonderful materials, I won’t refuse.”

With a wave of his sleeve, Yuan gathered all of the materials and tossed them inside his spatial ring.

“Thanks again.” Long Yejun brought Yuan and Yingzi to the Endless Arena shortly later with a teleport stone.

chapters.title: Chapter 1679 Boots Of Swift Destruction
chapters.body:
Chapter 1679 Boots Of Swift Destruction

Chapter 1679 Boots of Swift Destruction

frёewebnoѵēl.com

After returning to the Endless Arena, Yuan immediately started testing his new equipment and techniques.

‘First up, let’s try out my new equipment.’

Yuan looked at Long Yejun and said, “Throw some attacks my way.”

“How many?”

“As many as you want.”

Long Yejun nodded and started attacking Yuan with techniques weaker than his Dragon’s Breath.

Since these attacks weren’t threatening enough, Yuan just allowed them to land on his body.

The Robes of Destruction increased his Physical Defense by 100,000%, and what would’ve severely damaged him before barely hurt him now.

“I’m going to attack now,” Yuan suddenly warned.

Once Yuan had accumulated enough damage, he activated Dragon of Destruction’s Ultimate Chaos, unleashing a ball of concentrated power at Long Yejun.

Long Yejun’s eyes widened slightly when a ball of mass destruction that was ten times more powerful than all of his attacks combined flew at him.

Of course, even if the Dragon of Destruction’s Ultimate Chaos could strengthen the attacks a thousand times, it would not be enough to harm a hair on Long Yejun, who only attacked Yuan with Divine Lord-level attacks while being as powerful as a God Ascension cultivator.

“Was that your robes ability?” Long Yejun asked afterward. “That attack resembled one of the Dragon of Destruction’s techniques.”

Yuan nodded, confirming his suspicions.

“So you can draw out the power of the Dragon of Destruction in such a manner… how interesting…” Long Yejun muttered as he learned something new.

“Okay, now attack me with the intent to kill me.” Yuan suddenly said. “Do that Dragon’s Breath attack on me, but make it stronger than last time.”

“Are you serious?” Long Yejun’s eyes widened with surprise.

“Don’t worry, I won’t die.”

“If you say so. Don’t blame me if anything happens!”

Long Yejun immediately began preparing for a Dragon’s Breath.

A few moments later, he released it.

A Dragon’s Breath that was twice as powerful as the last one flew at Yuan, who merely stood there with a calm smile on his face.

<Robes of Destruction has detected a life-threatening attack>

<Dragon of Destruction’s Scale has activated>

A black barrier enveloped Yuan’s entire body. When the Dragon’s Breath connected with the barrier, it was immediately absorbed by the barrier, like a black hole.

Seeing this, Long Yejun continued to release Dragon’s Breath.

Long Yejun closed his mouth after seven full seconds.

“Wow… you really escaped that unscathed. Is that also the robe’s ability?”

Yuan nodded. “But I can only use it once a month.”

“That’s amazing. You basically have an extra life every month!” Yuan tested the Boots of Swift Destruction next.

He tested Sky Sundering Steps first. When he activated the technique, Yuan transformed into a streak of golden lightning as he traversed around the arena at the speed of light. However, his spiritual energy was also being drained at lightning speed.

Even with how rapidly he could restore his spiritual energy, Yuan could only maintain Sky Sundering Steps for a full minute before he was completely exhausted.

‘Even if it’s only for a minute for now, I can travel an immense distance in that minute, and if I use it wisely, I can maintain it for much longer.’

After recovering enough spiritual energy, Yuan began testing Lightning Strides.

Every time he activated Lightning Strides, he would instantly travel tens of thousands of meters, up to 100,000 meters.

‘It’s basically short-distance teleporting, and it doesn’t consume that much spiritual energy either.’

He looked at Long Yejun and asked, “Do you have any technique that restricts movement?”

“I do. Want me to use it on you?”

He nodded.

“Alright.”

Long Yejun made a grasping motion at Yuan the next moment.

“Water Prison.”

A barrier of water enveloped Yuan. It didn’t directly restrict his movements, so the movement immunity from the Boots of Swift Destruction didn’t activate, but it did trap him inside.

Once he was fully imprisoned, Yuan activated Lightning Strikes again.

Sha!

He turned into a streak of lightning that bypassed the water barrier, appearing outside it without any issues.

“It’s not completely teleportation, but it’s close.” Long Yejun muttered after seeing this.

Sometime later, Yuan said to Long Yejun, “Alright, I’m going to attack you with a lightning attack next.”

“Do your worst.” Long Yejun replied with a confident pose and smile.

Yuan pointed at him with two fingers and activated—

[Heaven’s Final Judgement!]

The sky above Long Yejun suddenly darkened before swiftly releasing a thick lightning bolt, striking him.

At the same time, Yuan felt all of his spiritual energy deplete almost instantly.

Meanwhile, Long Yejun casually swatted the lightning bolt away.

“It’s definitely powerful—even more powerful than your robe’s ability, but it didn’t completely activate.” Long Yejun commented as his gaze landed on Yuan. “Because you lack the cultivation to activate it.”

Yuan nodded and said, “Yes, my cultivation is not enough to use it, but I want to try again.”

He sat down and cultivated until he fully restored his cultivation.

“I’m going to do it again now.”

Once he was prepared, Yuan activated the technique again. However, instead of just using his spiritual energy, he also used the chaotic essence within his Chaotic Heart.

The sky above Long Yejun darkened again, but it was much more ominous this time.

Yuan could feel his spiritual energy and chaotic essence deplete instantly, but miraculously, he managed to retain a sliver of chaotic essence.

[Heaven’s Final Judgement!]

BANG!

A massive streak of golden lightning emerged from the black clouds, striking Long Yejun.

Long Yejun tried to flick it away like last time, but when the lightning struck his arm, it immediately exploded, releasing countless smaller lightning energy.

Once the explosion subdued, Long Yejun looked at his arm. It wasn’t bleeding, but he wasn’t completely unscathed either, as there was a slight burnt spot where the lightning struck.

“For you to damage me without my cultivation suppressed, even if it’s only to this degree… As impressive as ever, Brother Yuan!” Long Yejun laughed out loud.

chapters.title: Chapter 1680 Infinite Spiraling Abyss
chapters.body:
Chapter 1680 Infinite Spiraling Abyss

Chapter 1680 Infinite Spiraling Abyss

“With that said, it is clear that you still lack the cultivation to truly utilize its true power.” Long Yejun said as the wound on his arm quickly healed.

“Yes, that feels like a fraction of its true potential. Unfortunately, that was the best I can do with my cultivation.”

Yuan sighed, but he wasn’t discouraged. If anything, he was excited for the future when his cultivation was sufficient enough to call down the true Heaven’s Final Judgement.

‘I guess the technique isn’t Celetial-grade for no reason.’ He smiled inwardly.

Yuan wanted to test Heaven’s Bane and the Baneful Abyss, but he felt that it was too dangerous. While his techniques may not affect Long Yejun, poison was a different story, especially when it’s one of the strongest poison in the world.

‘There is a good chance that the poison won’t affect him, but there’s no reason to risk it just to satisfy my curiosity. I can test it later on people who really deserve it. As for Primordial Fire… let’s not reveal too much just in case.’

With that decided, Yuan ended his testing there.

“Alright, I am done here,” he said a moment later.

“So, which training ground will you attempt to conquer next?”

Yuan began pondering.

He’d already conquered the three biggest and most useful elements out there: fire, lightning, and poison.

His lightning immunity made him unaffected by even heavenly tribulations. His poison immunity made it impossible to assassinate or poison him. As for his fire immunity, the fire element was one of the most widely used elements in the cultivation world.

After pondering for some time, Yuan smiled and said, “I want to try my hands at the Infinite Spiraling Abyss.”

Long Yejun’s eyes widened as he hastily exclaimed, “No, you don’t!”

“The Infinite Spiraling Abyss is arguably the most dangerous training ground in the Divine Dragon—in the entire Primal Expanse!”

Yuan was surprised by his reaction and asked, “For you to react like this… Is this place really that dangerous?”

“You have no idea! Only those who have a good understanding of the power of the void should think of training there. Even if you’re an expert, a single mistake and you’ll instantly be consumed by the Infinite Spiraling Abyss and never return!”

The power of the void, simply put, was the power to control space itself. If one could master this ability, one would have the freedom to traverse anywhere in the world.

This power was incredibly potent and filled with infinite possibilities, but it was also the most difficult and dangerous to cultivate, with countless casualties just trying to understand the surface of it. Hence, only a handful of cultivators dare to pursue it, and most of them do so after becoming powerful Immortals. However, even to this day, not a single cultivator has ever mastered the power of the void.

‘I am well aware of how dangerous the power of the void is…’ Yuan sighed inwardly. ‘After all, not a single of my previous incarnations had any understanding of it despite being peak figures in their era—at least as far as I am aware.’

However, to be fair, none of them had tried to understand the power of the void, as the very concept itself was nearly nonexistent, and they were busy pursuing other paths.

“Don’t worry, I understand how dangerous the power of the void is, and I am not going to directly challenge the Infinite Spiraling Abyss. I am going to study it from the outside and try to understand it first.”

“I see… if you’re only going to study it from the outside, I guess it’ll be fine.” Long Yejun mumbled.

A few days later, they arrived at the Infinite Spiraling Abyss, located inside a massive crack in the ground, its depths disappearing into darkness.

Unlike the Sky Splitting Dragon’s Tribulation, this place was completely empty.

“So this is the Infinite Spiraling Abyss…” Despite being miles away, Yuan could feel a vacuuming force emanating from the crack, as if it were trying to pull him into its endless depths.

“Be careful, or you’ll get sucked inside.” Long Yejun warned him.

“I’ll be right back.”

Before Yuan started his training, he logged off and wrote a note to the others in case he lost track of the time again.

Since the Eternal Lotuses no longer needed his help with the spiritual bath, Yuan could spend more time in Cultivation Online without logging off.

Once he returned to the Primal Expanse, Yuan immediately began trying to understand the power of the void.

As Yuan became focused, Long Yejun looked at him and wondered, ‘Will he actually understand the power of the void like this?’

He didn’t mention it to Yuan because he wanted to see what Yuan was capable of, but there was another training ground in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary for those who wanted to understand the power of the void, and it was a much easier place specifically for beginners like Yuan.

The Infinite Spiraling Abyss was meant for experts—those who already have a good understanding of the power of the void.

‘The others told me to contact them if Brother Yuan visits another training ground, but this place is dangerous, and the fewer distractions there are, the better.’

Trying to understand the power of the void was no joke, even for powerful beings like dragons.

In the entire Primal Expanse, the number of beings who had fully grasped the understanding of the power of the void was fewer than ten—and this referred solely to the comprehension of its nature, not the ability to wield the power itself.

‘Besides the Primal Monarch, Dragon God, and Primordial Phoenix, I cannot imagine another being capable of wielding the power of the void. Not even the Abyssal Dragon, who is known as the Void Dragon Monarch, could wield such power to its full potential…’

As Yuan stared into the Infinite Spiraling Abyss, he gradually fell into a trance, much like the state he experienced when he first comprehended Dragon’s Gaze.

chapters.title: Chapter 1681 Gathering Of The Seven Dragon Monarchs
chapters.body:
Chapter 1681 Gathering Of The Seven Dragon Monarchs

Chapter 1681 Gathering of the Seven Dragon Monarchs

‘Power of the void… what a profound and mysterious concept…’ Yuan pondered as he gazed into the Infinite Spiraling Abyss. ‘It’s like a branch that endlessly spreads out, extending into countless possibilities…’

The more Yuan tried to comprehend the power of the void, the more overwhelming it felt, almost as if he’d been tossed inside the void itself.

Long Yejun and Yingzi silently watched him from a distance while ensuring that he didn’t accidentally fall into the Infinite Spiraling Abyss.

‘What is that profound and mysterious feeling that surrounds him? I have never felt anything like that before.’ Long Yejun wondered to himself as Yuan’s aura gradually began to change.

After a few days, Yuan began showing signs of entering enlightenment. However, the concept of enlightenment did not exist within the Primal Expanse. As a matter of fact, beings in the Primal Expanse are incapable of entering enlightenment. The reason remains unknown because nobody knew of its existence in the first place.

“What a mysterious aura…” Yingzi was immediately drawn to the aura Yuan was emitting due to his enlightenment, and she subconsciously approached him.

However, Long Yejun snapped her out of it and said, “You can’t disturb him now. I don’t know why, but I am getting this feeling that it’d be dangerous to disturb him now.”

Yingzi nodded and moved further away from Yuan so she couldn’t disturb him.

As time passed, the profound aura surrounding Yuan grew stronger and stronger until it reached the point where even other beings from far away could vaguely sense it.

When Long Yejun noticed several presences approaching their direction, he immediately set up a barrier around Yuan and the Infinite Spiraling Abyss.

Then, using sound transmission, he commanded the approaching presence to leave.

“As the second Dragon God’s Descendant, I, Long Yejun, command you to leave and stay away from this place for the rest of the year!”

When those approaching heard Long Yejun’s voice and command, they instantly halted, silently bowed in their direction, and turned around, swiftly leaving.

However, Yuan’s aura of enlightenment continued to grow stronger—much stronger than ever.

‘My concealment barrier isn’t able to stop his aura from spreading even though it could completely hide our presence? What an extraordinary aura!’ Long Yejun was inwardly shocked, but he didn’t have much time to think about such things.

‘I will be busy asking people to leave,’ he smiled inwardly.

‘Now that I think about it, I’ve done things for Brother Yuan I wouldn’t bother for my other two siblings. What’s so different about him?’ Long Yejun realized that he had been helping Yuan to the best of his abilities without expecting anything in return, something quite unlike his usual self. Yet, despite this change, it felt as natural as breathing for him to help Yuan.

‘He’s humble and pleasant to have around, unlike the other two, who are always trying to get on my nerves. This… is this what it feels like to have a younger brother?’ <Your understanding of the Power of the Void has improved slightly>

<Your understanding of the Power of the Void has improved moderately>

<Your understanding of the Power of the Void has improved significantly>

As Yuan’s enlightenment deepened, so did his progress in comprehending the power of the void.

A few days later, Yuan’s aura of enlightenment grew so intense that it spread throughout the entire Divine Dragon Sanctuary. Fortunately, word of Long Yejun’s order forbidding anyone from approaching had also circulated, so few dared to get too close. Most onlookers observed from a distance, respecting the boundary set around Yuan.

Of course, a few individuals still approached—mainly the Dragon Monarches and the third Dragon God’s Descendant, Long Meihui.

“You’re so mean, Elder Brother! You said you’d contact us the moment Junior Brother Yuan visits another training ground!” Long Meihui stared at him with her cheeks puffed angrily.

“I didn’t say anything because I didn’t want any distractions near Brother Yuan. I hope you understand,” he explained.

“The Infinite Spiraling Abyss… Even I could’ve never imagined he’d come here after conquering my playground…” The Sky Splitting Dragon said.

“Hahaha! I’d expect nothing less from the monster who conquered my poison swamp!” Baneful Dragon laughed out loud.

“You’ve changed, Prince Yejun. I never thought you’d be this caring toward another being.” A new face said, his body naturally exuding intense heat.

“What do you know, Hellfire Dragon?” Long Yejun sneered.

The Hellfire Dragon, also known as the Fire Dragon Monarch, was a beautiful woman with long black hair and fiery eyes.

“As expected of Master Yuan, he’s as brilliant as ever.” Refined Obsidian Dragon sighed out loud, his gaze filled with admiration. ƒree𝑤ebnσvel.com

“The fourth Dragon God’s Descendant… he’s just a beginninger. Why did you allow him to cultivate here?” Another new face said. He was a middle-aged man with long, wavy black hair and pitch-black eyes.

“You’re here too, Abyssal Dragon?”

The Abyssal Dragon was the Void Dragon Monarch.

“Who wouldn’t want to investigate that weird aura? I came here as soon as I sensed it,” said another new face, the last of the Seven Dragon Monarchs, the Ice Dragon Monarch, Eternal Frost Dragon. She was a young lady with short white and light blue hair and silver eyes.

“Well, Elder Sister isn’t here.” Long Meihui said.

“How odd. There’s no way she wouldn’t make an appearance during such a commotion. In fact, I’d expected her to be the first one to show up, too.” Abyssal Dragon said.

“She’s probably too busy training to notice.” Eternal Frost Dragon shrugged.

“No way. Even if she’s deep asleep, this weird aura would’ve instantly woken her up.” Long Yejun shook his head.

Meanwhile, the onlookers were abuzz with excitement as they witnessed all seven of the Seven Dragon Monarchs gathered in one place, a sight so rare that it left everyone in awe.

“Maybe the Unrivaled Tyrant died without anyone knowing?” The Baneful Dragon suddenly suggested.

“Hahaha! Great joke! Even if everyone were to suddenly die in the Primal Expanse, she’d still be alive and kicking!” Sky Splitting Dragon laughed out loud.

“Yeah, no way she’d die. What could possibly kill her when even the Dragon God failed to do so?” Hellfire Dragon shook her head.

chapters.title: Chapter 1682 Basic Void Understanding
chapters.body:
Chapter 1682 Basic Void Understanding

Chapter 1682 Basic Void Understanding

One month… two months…

Three months passed in the blink of an eye. During this time, Yuan remained fixated on the Infinite Spiraling Abyss, his eyes staring into the darkness in a dazed manner.

<Your understanding of Power of the Void has increased significantly>

Ding!

<Congratulations! You have comprehended the basics of Power of the Void!>

<Due to your understanding of Power of the Void, you have acquired a new title!>

<You have acquired the title ‘Basic Void Understanding’>

[Basic Void Understanding]

[Description: You grasp the fundamentals of the Power of the Void, allowing you to manipulate the void to a minor extent.]

<You have learned the technique ‘Void Manipulation’>

[Void Manipulation]

[Grade: Mythic]

[Mastery Level: 1]

[Description: Allows you to manipulate the void to a certain extent.]

After acquiring the most basic understanding of the void, the Infinite Spiraling Abyss looked entirely different in Yuan’s eyes. It no longer seemed out of reach and incomprehensible.

Once Yuan was familiar with the power of the void within the Infinite Spiraling Abyss, his progress skyrocketed, and his aura of enlightenment became even stronger.

However, despite Yuan’s ever-growing understanding, he couldn’t shake the feeling that he wasn’t getting any closer to the next level. It was as if he had encountered an invisible bottleneck, and this feeling lingered for an entire month.

Suddenly, as if possessed by a ghost, Yuan suddenly began flying toward the Infinite Spiraling Abyss, shocking everyone there.

“Brother Yuan! What are you doing?!” Long Yejun exclaimed with a bewildered expression.

However, Yuan had moved with such speed that by the time Long Yejun processed the situation, he’d already flown into the Infinite Spiraling Abyss.

“My god! Why did he do that?!” Long Meihui cried out loud.

The Abyssal Dragon shook his head and sighed, “How foolish. He’d only just reached a basic understanding, yet he dared to challenge the Infinite Spiraling Abyss meant for those who have fully comprehended the power of the void. I had high hopes for him, too.” “Right? How boring. I also had high hopes for him after hearing of his previous feats. I guess he’d only conquered the last two training grounds with luck.” Hellfire Dragon casually shrugged.

“Master Yuan isn’t someone who’d take on something he cannot handle!” Refined Obsidian Dragon roared.

“Since when did you become the type to have faith in others, Metal Dragon Monarch?” The Eternal Frost Dragon asked him.

“Hmph! If you knew Master Yuan’s true capabilities, you’d also be revering him!”

“Will he be okay?” Yingzi asked Long Yejun with a worried look on her face. Since their first meeting, this was the only time she didn’t have any confidence in Yuan’s actions.

Long Yejun clenched his fists, and he responded with a deep frown on his face, “Unfortunately, even if it’s Brother Yuan, I don’t think he can escape the Infinite Spiraling Abyss. If anyone can save him, it’s the Dragon God and possibly my Elder Sister if he hadn’t gone too deep into the void.”

“You only mentioned that he wouldn’t be able to escape, but what about his chance of survival?” Yingzi asked after noticing this detail.

He sighed, “While the Infinite Spiraling Abyss is indeed very destructive, I believe Brother Yuan can survive it with his current level of regeneration. However, as he descends deeper into the void, the destructiveness will continue to intensify until it eventually surpasses his regenerative abilities.”

“How about the Dragon God? You said he could probably save Yuan. Can we ask for his help?.” Yingzi said.

“Hah! Good luck with that!” Hellfire Dragon sneered. “The Dragon God will only show himself during the Dragon God Festival or when he needs something. Since we’re so close to the Dragon God Festival, he probably won’t appear until the very last second, right before the festival begins.”

“I can try looking for him, but it’ll take so long that it’s better just to wait for him to show up during the Dragon God Festival.” Long Yejun sighed.

“No way…” Yingzi wore a sorrowful look on her face.

“What about Elder Sister?” Long Meihui asked.

“She’s even harder to find than the Dragon God. Unless you’re fated to meet her, you’ll never find her.” Long Yejun sighed.

He continued, “We can only hope Brother Yuan survives long enough for the Dragon God to show up.

Meanwhile, somewhere in the Infinite Expanse, two individuals had been quietly observing the events unfolding at the Infinite Spiraling Abyss since the very beginning, their expressions calm yet alert.

A handsome man with long, beautiful golden hair glanced at the young child hovering beside him and spoke, “Are you sure you don’t want to save him? He’s your son, isn’t he?” “Weren’t you prepared to kill him for faking his identity as your descendant just some time ago? Why are you suddenly worried about his wellbeing?” The Primal Monarch inquired with a nonchalant expression on his face.

The handsome man, who was known as the Dragon God, smiled, “I was—until he showed the qualifications to become a genuine descendant of mine. I can save him right now, but then I’ll take him off your hands.”

“What kind of nonsense is that?” Primal Monarch shook his head and continued, “Also, even if you want him, does he want to be your son? You’re just a slightly stronger dragon than the rest.”

“Hahaha!” The Dragon God suddenly burst out laughing, his laughter powerful enough to shake the void around them.

He stopped a moment later and said, “Did I just receive your permission to save him? Whether he wants to become my son or not… we can deal with that afterward!”

The Primal Monarch sighed, “Do you really think he needs your help?”

“…”

The Dragon God stared at the Infinite Spiraling Abyss and sneered, “Whatever.”

Meanwhile, after entering the Infinite Spiraling Abyss, Yuan found himself in a pitch-black world that seemed to spiral endlessly around him as he continued to fall deeper into the void. His body was also constantly being damaged; while it wasn’t enough to threaten his life, he could feel the intensity of the damage gradually increasing, becoming stronger with every passing moment.

chapters.title: Chapter 1683 Leaving The Infinite Spiraling Abyss
chapters.body:
Chapter 1683 Leaving The Infinite Spiraling Abyss

Chapter 1683 Leaving the Infinite Spiraling Abyss

Although Yuan’s consciousness was awake, he was not fully aware or in control, akin to a state of sleep paralysis, where he was conscious of his surroundings but unable to move or react.

As the void continuously damaged him, his body felt like it was slowly being chipped away by countless ants. It wasn’t too painful, but the gnarly sensation would drive most people mad if they had to endure it for an extended time. freeweɓnovel.cøm

Time felt incredibly slow inside the Infinite Spiraling Abyss—almost as if it were frozen. However, what felt like minutes to Yuan was actually days outside the void, and two full months had passed since he entered the Infinite Spiraling Abyss.

During this time, Yuan’s comprehension of the power of the void continued to progress. Despite the constant pain throughout his body that grew more intensive as time passed, it wasn’t able to disturb Yuan from his enlightenment. If anything, it sped up his cultivation, as every time the void inflicted damage on his body, he would absorb some of that damage, refining it into his own power.

It has been six months since Yuan started training at the Infinite Spiraling Abyss. As the Dragon God Festival drew near, more and more guests showed up at the Divine Dragon Sanctuary to participate or cheer on the participants.

However, these new guests were puzzled to find the Divine Dragon Sanctuary nearly empty. Typically, the sanctuary would be bustling with activity, filled with countless beings, but this time it was eerily quiet.

“Apparently, everyone went to the Infinite Spiraling Abyss.”

“What is going on there?”

“No idea.”

“I heard the Seven Dragon Monarchs are also there.”

“Seriously?! Let’s check it out!”

At the Infinite Spiraling Abyss, Long Yejun sighed, “There’s less than half a year before the Dragon God Festival begins… Hang in there, Brother Yuan…”

Another two months have passed since Yuan entered the Infinite Spiraling Abyss. Inside, Yuan was still half concious, and the void’s strength has become so much that his regeneration could no longer keep up with it. However, it was just slightly below the rate his body was being destroyed, so his body was slowly being chipped away.

As of this moment, Yuan’s body was a mess. His limbs were missing, and only his head and upper body remained.

Yu Ning tried to wake him up, but no matter how much she called for him, there was no response.

After another month, only Yuan’s head remained.

‘How long has it been? Am I going to die here? If I die here and return to the Nine Heavens, what about Feng Feng? Would I be leaving her behind?’

<Your understanding of the Power of the Void has increased signfiicantly>

<Your understanding of the Power of the Void has reached the next realm>

<You have acquired the title ‘Moderate Void Understanding’>

Unfortunately, even after his Void Understanding had reached the next level, Yuan’s condition remained unchanged, as he’d fallen too deep inside the void.

However, Yuan suddenly sensed another presence nearby. ‘What a powerful, almost tyrannical presence… Is that old booger finally here to save me?’ Yuan wondered as the presence approached him.

“My, my, what do we have here? A human inside the Infinite Spiraling Abyss?”

A small silhouette stopped before Yuan, her gaze staring at Yuan’s handsome face with interest.

Yuan’s transformation had been undone shortly after he entered the void, so he was in his human form at this moment.

“You’ve only just achieved Moderate Void Understanding, yet you dare to enter the Infinite Spiraling Abyss. I have never seen a being as reckless as you. But what is this weird aura you’re emitting?”

However, Yuan couldn’t respond.

“…”

Seeing Yuan was unresponsive, the silhouette tapped his forehead with a finger.

The next moment, the void ceased to attack him, and his body instantly regenerated fully.

His connection with the void had been severed, blocked by the barrier the silhouette had cast around him, and his enlightenment also ended shortly after.

Once he’d regained control over his body, Yuan looked at the silhouette and said, “Thank you for saving me. You must be the first Dragon God’s Descendant, the Unrivaled Tyrant.”

“Oh? You recognize me?”

Yuan nodded, “Long Yejun spoke of you before. He said I’d instantly recognize you the moment I saw you. Truly, your presence is something else.”

The Unrivaled Tyrant’s presence was unfathomably profound and chaotic, so much so that even the void around them was being affected by it.

Yuan examined the Unrivaled Tyrant’s appearance. She had a petite figure and a somewhat skinny body, with short black hair that ended at her shoulders, a refined face, and sharp eyebrows that gave her a naturally fierce expression. At a glance, she looked like an ordinary, pretty little girl, but once her dark red eyes locked onto you, it felt like you were being preyed on by a bloodthirsty monster.

“Um… My name is Yuan.” Yuan introduced himself.

“I’m Long Wu Qing, eldest of the Dragon God’s Descendants. How long have you been living in the Primal Expanse?”

“Not long. I’ve only been here for about three years.”

Long Wu Qing narrowed her eyes and said, “Are you lying to me?”

“Why would I do that?” Yuan quickly refuted.

“You’ve just arrived in the Primal Expanse, yet you already have a completed Chaotic Heart? How do you explain that? Also, I didn’t know humans were capable of having Chaotic Hearts.”

“I guess you can say I’m a special case. My father is the Primal Monarch, after all.”

Long Wi Qing’s eyes widened after hearing his words.

“The Primal Monarch is your father…?”

“Well, he’s not my real father. I was adopted by him.”

“I see… In any case, I have a lot to ask you, but we can continue our chat later. The Dragon God Festival is about to begin, so I will take my leave now. Do you wish to come with me or continue training here?”

“Honestly, I’m trapped in here, so I’d appreciate it if you can take me with you.”

Long Wu Qing nodded and brought him along as she traversed the void.

‘She’s a pretty nice person. I wonder why Long Yejun made her seem like some unreasonable monster.’ Yuan thought to himself.

“Ah. I cannot wait to get back and have a feast. I have been training here for the last hundred million years.” Long Wu Qing suddenly muttered out loud.

Yuan looked at her and said, “I happen to have some food with me. Do you want some?”

“I am a very picky eater,” she said.

Yuan didn’t say another word and directly retrieved several meat skewers for her.

Long Wu Qing’s body trembled the moment she noticed their savory aroma.

“Let me see those!” She hastily said in a demanding tone.

Yuan handed them to her.

Once the skewers were in her hand, Long Wu Qing examined them for several minutes before putting them in her mouth.

“Mmm… I have traversed nearly the entirety of Primal Expanse and savored all kinds of beings, but nothing comes close to this!”

After her fist bite, Long Wu Qing wouldn’t stop until all of the meat skewers were gone—the skewers included.

“Did you bring that food from the human world?” She asked afterward.

“That’s right.”

“Do you have any more?”

“Of course.” Yuan didn’t hesitate to grab another handful of meat skewers for her.

‘She did save my life, after all.’ He smiled inwardly.

“Do you have more?” Long Wu Qing asked again a few minutes later.

“Here you go.” Yuan continued taking out meat skewers for her.

“You’re very different from the other humans I have met,” she suddenly said. “They would all cower in fear and soil themselves the moment they see me. Even my siblings are no different.”

“Have you met a lot of humans?” He asked.

“A few hundred of them, I suppose.”

“Wow, that’s a lot.” Yuan was surprised by the number.

“I have lived a long time, after all. However, humans have stopped coming to our world for some time now. Do you know why?”

“Because they’re afraid. After all, none of those who have come to this world have ever returned, so people just stopped coming.”

“That makes sense. What about you? Are you sad that you can no longer return to the human world?”

“Not at all. I can return whenever I want with the Primal Monarch’s help, but I am staying here for reasons.”

“Once I win the Dragon God Festival, I will return.”

“Hahaha!” Long Wu Qing suddenly burst out laughing.

“You actually dare to claim you’ll win the Dragon God Festival right before me? How bold! So, you think you can defeat me?” She looked at him with an excited face, but her aura was oozing with bloodlust.

Despite her threatening aura, Yuan faced her and spoke calmly, “Depending on the conditions, yes, I can.”

Long Wi Qing stared at him in silence for a moment before turning away, but she didn’t say anything.

A few more minutes of awkward silence later, they emerged from the Infinite Spiraling Abyss, shocking everyone there.

“Brother Yuan! He’s actually managed to escape!” Long Yejun exclaimed in an excited voice.

Then he noticed Yuan had emerged with a different appearance, and even his scent had changed.

However, before he could even think about it, his entire body trembled as Long Wu Qing’s tyrannical aura permeated throughout the entire place.

“The Unrivaled Tyrant?!” The Dragon Monarchs and onlookers exclaimed with shocked faces after noticing her presence, their very souls trembling in immense fear.

chapters.title: Chapter 1684 Unrivaled Tyrant
chapters.body:
Chapter 1684 Unrivaled Tyrant

Chapter 1684 Unrivaled Tyrant

“My, what’s going on here? I have never had such a massive crowd to welcome me back before.” Long Wu Qing spoke with a slight smile on her face when she noticed the Seven Dragon Monarchs and the onlookers outside the Infinite Spiraling Abyss.

“I-it’s the Unrivaled Tyrant! Run for your lives!” The onlookers began scrambling in fear, resembling a group of startled cats, their escape messy and swift.

The only ones who didn’t attempt to escape were the Seven Dragon Monarchs, primarily because fleeing would make them appear weak, a vulnerability that would only invite further bullying and challenges to their authority.

“What is this weird scent coming from the fourth Dragon God’s Descendant?” Eternal Frost Dragon suddenly mused out loud.

Since he was in his human form, Yuan’s human scent swiftly permeated the area. It was so intense that it even halted some of the onlookers running away. However, once they realized the scent was coming from the Unrivaled Tyrant’s direction, they began running again.

“This scent… is the scent of a human!” Long Yejun’s eyes widened as he stared at Yuan, who now had black hair.

“No way… why does Brother Yuan have the scent of a human on him? What happened inside the Infinite Spiraling Abyss?” He didn’t immediately entertain the possibility that Yuan had been human all along; instead, he assumed that something within the Infinite Spiraling Abyss had triggered this puzzling transformation. After all, there was simply no way a human could have the Dragon God’s blood.

Yingzi wore a nervous look on her face after realizing that Yuan’s identity had been exposed, but there was nothing she could do there. Even if she wanted to take Yuan and escape from the Divine Dragon Sanctuary, she knew it was impossible to escape with the Seven Dragon Monarchs so close.

Moreover, with Long Wu Qing beside Yuan, she would kill Yingzi before she could even reach him.

‘The only thing I can do now is hope for the Primal Monarch to help us,’ she sighed inwardly. freёweɓnovel.com

“Thank you for your help, Senior Long,” Yuan said to Long Wu Qing after coming out of the Infinite Spiraling Abyss.

“You can just call me Wu Qing. I permit you to do so,” she casually said. “Anyway, I need to stretch myself. I’ll talk to you again afterward.”

When the Seven Dragon Monarchs heard her words, their faces darkened, and they simultaneously released their cultivation, shocking Yuan.

“What are they—?”

Before he could even finish his sentence, Long Wu Qing turned to look at them with a murderous smile on her face.

“Let me see how much you’ve all grown!” she laughed as she flew at them.

“This crazy bitch! The Dragon God Festival is right around the corner, and she wants to fight now?!” Hellfire Dragon exclaimed.

“Elder Sister! Please wait a moment!” Long Yejun shouted with a begging expression.

“Damn it! Why is she always like this?!” Sky Splitting Dragon cried out loud.

“I shouldn’t have come here!” Refined Obsidian Dragon sighed.

“Have mercy on me…” Long Meihui shed a tear. She wasn’t one of the Seven Dragon Monarchs, but she was still a target.

The Seven Dragon Monarchs attacked the incoming Long Wu Qing with the intent to kill her.

BOOM!

A powerful ripple swept the Divine Dragon Sanctuary when Long Wu Qing collided with the Seven Dragon Monarchs.

Yingzi had instinctively escaped the moment Long Wu Qing looked in her direction, and she barely made it in time, or she would’ve been caught up in their confrontation.

Yuan was swept away like a pebble during a storm at first, but he quickly stabilized himself.

A massive cloud of smoke hovered in the air, concealing the figures of the Seven Dragon Monarchs and Long Wu Qing within its dense, swirling mist.

Moments later, several silhouettes could be seen coming out of the smoke. To be more precise, they appeared to have been tossed out, and their appearance was a mess with blood all over their bodies.

Amongst these silhouettes were the Refined Obsidian Dragon, Eternal Frost Dragon, Long Meihui, and the Baneful Dragon.

After a few more moments, the cloud of smoke was forcefully removed when another powerful blast occurred, revealing Long Yejun, the Abyssal Dragon, Sky Splitting Dragon, and Hellfire Dragon’s figure. However, they were all beaten up and dripping with blood, looking as if they had been run through by several trucks.

Long Wu Qing was also there, but she didn’t have a single scratch on her body.

As Long Wu Qing hovered in the air, exuding an aura of confidence and superiority, she sighed and remarked, “What have you lot been doing for the last hundred million years? Lying on your backs all day?”

She looked at Long Yejun and continued, “I am especially disappointed in you, little brother. I thought you would be able to last a little longer this time, but alas, you’ve barely improved since we last fought.”

“T-that’s nonsense! I have improved a lot! You’re just a damn monster who outpaces all of us significantly, so no matter how much we train, you’ll always be several steps ahead of us!” Long Yejun refuted, his voice full of frustration.

“Huh? What did you just call me?” Long Wu Qing’s eyebrows twitched.

Long Yejun’s body shivered, realizing that he’d misspoke.

Long Wu Qing moved again, but she only targetted Long Yejun this time, beating him left and right.

Long Yejun tried to fight back, but their disparity was simply too vast.

“Wu Qing, can you stop beating him?” A voice suddenly spoke, halting her movements.

Long Wu Qing turned to look at Yuan, who continued speaking, “He’s been nothing but a good friend to me ever since I arrived, and I cannot bear to see him in such a state. If you want to stretch, how about doing it with me? Ah, I would appreciate it if you can suppress your cultivation to match mine, or I’ll die from a single punch from you.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1685 Sworn Brothers
chapters.body:
Chapter 1685 Sworn Brothers

Chapter 1685 Sworn Brothers

A deafening silence filled the area for a good moment after Yuan spoke, with the Seven Dragon Monarchs staring at him with wide eyes filled with disbelief.

“I like your enthusiasm, Yuan, but I don’t want to injure you before the Dragon God Festival. After all, I don’t want to hear any excuses when you lose to me,” said Long Wu Qing.

Her words shocked the others more than Yuan’s words.

‘That crazy bitch is declining a fight?! I didn’t know she was even capable of that!’ Hellfire Dragon cried inwardly.

“But I can’t bear to watch you beat my friend any longer.” Yuan sighed.

Long Wu Qing glanced at Long Yejun for a brief moment before releasing him.

“Consider yourself lucky, little brother.”

She approached Yuan and continued, “Don’t think we won’t fight. We’ll do it the moment the Dragon God Festival is over.”

“I am looking forward to it.”

Long Wu Qing left the scene shortly after.

“I didn’t want to show you such a pathetic sight, Brother Yuan.” Long Yejun approached him a moment later.

Despite his resilient body, the wounds caused by Long Wu Qing didn’t recover normally, as if there was something preventing him from healing.

The same could be said for the others, too.

“Don’t say that.” Yuan shook his head.

“I can’t believe you came out of the Infinite Spiraling Abyss with her. My heart stopped for a second back there.”

“She saved my life.” “How unexpected…” Long Yejun mumbled.

“By the way, what happened to you inside the Infinite Spiraling Abyss? Why do you smell like a human now?”

“Oh…”

It was at this moment that Yuan realized he forgot to transform back to his dragon form. However, considering that his true identity has been revealed to Long Wu Qing, it would only be a matter of time before it was revealed to everyone else. “Actually, I am human.” Yuan decided to reveal his true identity.

“What…?” Long Yejun’s eyes widened with immense shock.

“I apologize for deceiving you until now, but I am not one of Dragon God’s descendants.”

“W-why would you…?”

“I have my reasons…” he sighed.

Long Yejun lowered his head and fell silent, seemingly in deep thoughts.

“I see… so you were human from the start…”

When Long Yejun lifted his head and showed his expression again, it was filled with excitement and admiration.

“I can’t believe a human has conquered the Baneful Dragon’s Poisonous Swamp, the Sky Splitting Dragon’s Tribulation, and even survived the Infinite Spiraling Abyss! Hahaha!” He laughed so hard tears began forming in his eyes.

“Hey, although we’re not real brothers, can I still call you Brother? I’ve grown accustomed to it, and it wouldn’t feel right if I changed it now.”

Yuan nodded, “Of course.”

The realization that Yuan was a human began to set in for the others, their faces filled with shock, some with disdain.

“Master Yuan is a human…? That explains his godlike smithing techniques! The art of smithing was originally humans to begin with!” Refined Obsidian Dragon muttered to himself.

“A human? A human conquered my playground?” The Baneful Dragon wore a look of disbelief on his face.

The Sky Splitting Dragon had a similar reaction.

Long Meihui’s eyes were sparkling with admiration.

The Hellfire Dragon and Eternal Frost Dragon glared at Yuan with disdain in their eyes.

The Abyssal Dragon remained silent.

“Though, I must admit that I am disappointed.” Long Yejun suddenly continued with a weak smile, “You have taught and given me the experience of what it’s like to have a little brother. Now I have to face the reality that I might never have a little brother.”

“Although I cannot be your real brother, we can still be brothers. In the human world, we have the concept known as sworn brothers. While they are not real brothers, they treat each other as one.”

“What a wonderful concept! Let’s be sworn brothers!” Yuan nodded with a smile.

However, a voice filled with power and authority abruptly resounded, “Who said you can’t be real brothers?”

Yuan suddenly sensed a powerful presence behind one, one that surpassed even Long Wu Qing. However, it wasn’t tyrannical and oppressive as hers.

Yuan turned around to see a handsome man with long golden hair and golden eyes hovering behind him.

“Dragon God!” Long Yejun hastily lowered his head after recognizing the man.

The others followed suit.

“Dragon God?” Yuan muttered.

“Indeed. I am your father—or so you claimed.” The Dragon God spoke with a smile that appeared threatening to Yuan for some reason. freeweɓnovel.cøm

Perhaps it was because of his guilt for taking on the identity as one of the Dragon God’s descendants.

“F-father! Please wait! There’s a good reason that Brother Yuan—” Long Yejun hastily tried to come up with an excuse, but the Dragon God sealed his lips with a single glance.

“Brother Yuan, huh? It sure has a nice ring to it.” The Dragon God chuckled.

“Relax, I am not cruel enough to punish you for pretending to be my descendant,” he continued.

“Oh, really?”

Suddenly, another figure appeared beside the Dragon God.

None of those present, except for Yuan and Yingzi, recognized him. However, they were bewildered that this young man could stand beside the Dragon God as an equal.

‘No, he is standing even above the Dragon God!’ Long Yejun noticed this.

“You’re not going to tell him that if I hadn’t stopped you, you would’ve killed him?”

“You dwell too much on the insignificant details, Primal Monarch.” The Dragon God shook his head.

“The Primal Monarch?!”

Long Yejun and the others exclaimed in shock.

“W-we greet the Primal Monarch!” They hastily greeted him in a messy fashion.

“You’ve finally decided to show yourself, you old booger.”

Long Yejun and the others stared at Yuan with terrified expressions.

“B-Brother Yuan! T-that’s…” Long Yejun tried to warn him, but Yuan interrupted with a smile, “Don’t worry, he’s my old man—my father.”

“Y-your father?” Long Yejun looked speechless.

“Yes.” Yuan confirmed.

chapters.title: Chapter 1686 A Feast Before The Festival
chapters.body:
Chapter 1686 A Feast Before The Festival

Chapter 1686 A Feast Before the Festival

“Did you want me to save you sooner? I knew there was someone inside that was capable of saving you, so why would I waste my energy?” The Primal Monarch shrugged with a smile on his face after hearing Yuan’s words.

“What if she didn’t save me?” he asked.

“But that didn’t happen, so why dwell on it?” Yuan sighed and no longer tried to argue with him.

“Ahem.” The Dragon God cleared his throat. “As I was saying. If you want to become my descendant, I will make it possible.”

“You were serious?” Yuan looked at him with wide eyes.

“Of course. Do you want me to show you how serious I am?”

“Hey, why are you trying to poach him when you didn’t even save him?” Primal Monarch suddenly asked.

“My daughter saved him, which is the same thing.” Dragon God shrugged.

“I can’t believe such shameless words are coming out of that mouth. Did you hit your head too hard recently?”

Long Yejun and the others were bewildered by the Dragon God and Primal Monarch’s childish bickering, as they had never seen such behavior from these godlike existences.

‘Since when were they on such good terms with each other?’ Long Yejun wondered inwardly.

Once it seemed like their conversation had ended, Yuan spoke, “I don’t know how you’ll make me a descendant, and I appreciate the offer, but I will have to decline. After all, I already have a father here.”

Primal Monarch snickered after hearing this. The Dragon God’s expression didn’t change much, and he appeared calm, but he was definitely disappointed inwardly.

“What a pity. With your talents, you definitely qualify to be my descendant. But if you don’t want it, I can’t force you.”

“The Dragon God Festival will begin soon, so I will take my leave now. Until then.” The Dragon God disappeared into thin air the very next moment.

“I’ll also take my leave for now.” The Primal Monarch left before Yuan could say anything to him.

The place remained dead silent even many moments later.

Eventually, Long Yejun broke the silence and spoke in a trembling voice, “I can’t believe you’re the Primal Monarch’s descendant! That’s actually even more impressive than being the Dragon God’s descendant!”

“Well, I am not really his blood-related descendant. I was adopted by him, and he gave his Blood Essence to me.” Yuan explained.

“The Primal Monarch’s Blood Essence?! That’s even more impressive!” Long Yejun cried out loud.

He continued, “Anyway, what are you going to do now? Since we still have a little time left before the Dragon God Festival, want to have a feast?”

Yuan nodded and said, “Sure, but I will be away for a little.”

“No worries. We need a few days to prepare the feast, anyway.” Long Yejun nodded.

Yuan looked at Yingzi and said to her, “I’ll be gone for a bit again.”

“Okay.”

Yuan logged off at that moment.

“Huh? Where did he go?” The Baneful Dragon muttered in a dazed voice.

“I can’t sense his presence anymore, almost as if he ceased to exist. Was that the power of the void?” Long Meihui inquired out loud, her face filled with wonder. fгee𝑤ebɳoveɭ.cøm

“The Primal Monarch probably took him somewhere,” Eternal Frost Dragon deducted.

Meanwhile, Yuan returned to Earth again.

He looked at the two sleeping beauties beside him and sighed, “I’d just promised them that I’d not lose track of time, but alas, it’s almost been a year since my last return…”

Although he didn’t technically lose track of time but was trapped inside the Infinite Spiraling Abyss, it was basically the same thing.

Yuan waited for either Meixiu or Chu Liuxiang to wake up.

The following day, Meixiu was the first to wake up. She and Chu Liuxiang had been taking turns logging off every other day to see if Yuan had returned.

“Hm? You’re back?” Meixiu looked at him with a pleasantly surprised face.

“Yes…”

Upon seeing his apologetic smile, Meixiu instantly knew he was about to apologize for his absence again, so she swiftly placed a finger on his lips and said, “You don’t need to apologize. We were prepared for it when you left a note this time.”

Yuan nodded and said, “Alright, I won’t apologize, but I want to tell you what happened this time.”

“Of course.”

Yuan proceeded to explain about being trapped inside the Infinite Spiraling Abyss and how time flowed differently inside.

“It felt like I had only been there for several minutes, but by the time I came out, several months had passed,” he said.

“Anyway, the Dragon God Festival is right around the corner. Once I finish it and hopefully win it, I’ll immediately return to the Nine Heavens.”

“What will happen if you don’t win?” Meixiu entertained this possibility and inquired.
