“Then I’ll have to ask the Primal Monarch for help. If he can’t help Feng Feng, I’ll just have to postpone her revival and return to the Nine Heavens. Though, I doubt the Primordial Phoenix will accept that outcome.”

“She said that she’d wage war with the Divine Dragon Sanctuary for the treasure if I failed, and I cannot allow that to happen no matter what. I don’t know how serious she was being when she said it, but I have a feeling she was being dead serious,” Yuan sighed.

“If I have no other option, I will see if the Dragon God would be willing to lend me the treasure, and in exchange, I will become his descendant.”

“His descendant?” Meixiu wore a puzzled look on her face.

“Apparently, the Dragon God wants me to be his descendant. I don’t know what went through his mind that led him to such a decision, though.”

After chatting for some time, Yuan and Meixiu spent the rest of the day together. He did the same thing with Chu Liuxiang the following day. Then, he visited Yu Rou and the Eternal Lotuses.

Once he caught up with everyone, Yuan returned to the Primal Expanse for the feast.

chapters.title: Chapter 1687 Seasoning
chapters.body:
Chapter 1687 Seasoning

Chapter 1687 Seasoning

When Yuan returned to the Primal Expanse, Yingzi was there to greet him with a teleport stone given to her by Long Yejun.

Without uttering a word, Yingzi handed the teleport stone to him.

After attaining Moderate Void Understanding, Yuan could see the more intricate details within the teleport stone, even able to tell the direction it would take him. He shattered the teleport stone the next moment and entered the rift created by it.

When they emerged from the rift, Yuan and Yingzi appeared at the Endless Arena, but it had a vastly different atmosphere and was no longer as empty as their previous visits. The once-empty platform is now filled with human figures and beasts, with mountains of food in every direction.

Since Yuan was still in his human form, his scent quickly spread, attracting the attention of those around him.

They stared at him in silence for a few moments before someone shouted, “It’s the Primal Monarch’s descendant!” News of Yuan’s true identity had long spread throughout the Divine Dragon Sanctuary. Some were upset, even angry, that they had been deceived by a human, but the majority of them didn’t mind it and became more impressed by his feats in the past two years.

The beings near Yuan respectfully greeted him, regardless of their feelings toward him, as he was the Primal Monarch’s descendant, which ranked even above the Dragon God’s descendants.

“Oh, you’ve arrived.” Long Yejun appeared shortly after their arrival.

“Follow me. I’ll bring you to our spot.”

Yuan and Yingzi followed him to another area where the other Dragon Monarchs were present. However, not all seven of the Dragon Monarchs were present.

Besides the Hellfire Dragon and Eternal Frost Dragon, the rest were there.

“Hello, everyone. Sorry to make you wait.”

“It’s fine. We just finished setting up the place.” Long Yejun said.

“There’s still two weeks left before the Dragon God Festival. Enjoy yourself until then!” Long Yejun’s voice rippled throughout the Endless Arena. Everyone started stuffing their mouths with food the very next moment.

Yuan also started eating the food that had been prepared using beings that only existed in the Primal Expanse.

Although the food had natural flavors, it lacked any seasoning, so it was very lackluster compared to the food in the human world.

Realizing this, Yuan said to them, “You guys like the meat skewers, right? How about I prepare this food in the same way?”

“What? You can do that?” Long Yejun’s eyes widened.

“Please do!” Long Meihui exclaimed, her eyes flickering with excitement.

Yuan nodded and retrieved several bottles of seasoning. “One of the most significant contributors to the excellence of human food is how we apply our seasoning—how we season our food.”

He showed them the bottles and continued, “We call this seasoning. If we apply it to the food, it will give the food a specific taste. If we mix them together, it will create a unique flavor, and different mixtures have different results.”

Yuan sprinkled some salt on his food and handed it to Long Yejun.

“This is one of the most commonly used seasonings. Salt.”

When Long Yejun bit into the meat, his eyes widened with surprise. “What the! This food is now several times tastier!” “L-let me try!” Long Meihui and the others shouted.

Yuan sprinkled some salt onto their food.

“Wow!”

“I can’t believe those tiny sand-like things could enhance the flavors so much!”

“This one is called black pepper, another commonly used seasoning.” Yuan handed them more food.

“What a stark difference!”

“And this is what happens when I mix them together.”

Yuan added salt, black pepper, and garlic powder to the next sample.

Long Yejun and the others trembled with satisfaction when they ate the food.

“I used to underestimate humans, but the fact is, they possess knowledge we could never fathom…” The Baneful Dragon muttered.

“I’ve known that since I learned how to blacksmith from one.” Refined Obsidian Dragon commented.

Yuan then said, “By the way, we typically season our food before cooking it.”

“Speaking of cooking, we also learned that from humans. Before, we would consume our food raw.” Long Yejun said.

Yingzi gently tugged on Yuan’s robes and asked, “How about the flavor of meat skewers? What kind of seasoning do you put on them?”

“Many people have their own special mix, but the majority of them contain cumin, salt, and chili.” Yuan tried to replicate the seasoning of the meat skewers in his possession and handed them to Yingzi.

“It’s missing some flavors, but it’s very close,” she said after tasting it.

“There are hundreds of different seasonings out there, but I only have some of them,” Yuan said.

Yuan took out all of the seasoning in his possession to let the others use them as they pleased.

Long Yejun and the others had a blast testing out every combination, but their peace lasted only for a few minutes.

Suddenly, a rift appeared right next to Yuan, and even before the figure emerged, their tyrannical presence poured through the rift, saturating the surroundings with an overwhelming and oppressive force.

“My, my. How can you have a feast without me?” Long Wu Qing spoke as she walked out of the rift. However, she looked a little different from before. While most of her features remained the same, she was much taller and more mature-looking, resembling that of a peerless woman.

“Because we knew you’d show up regardless.” Long Yejun spoke with a nervous smile.

Long Wu Qing looked at the bottle of salt in his hand and inquired, “What is that white stuff in your hand?”

“Seasoning. Humans use them to enhance the flavor of their food.” Yuan gave her a brief explanation.

“Interesting,” she showed a thrilled smile. “Show me how to use it.”

“Ummm… I don’t mind, but can you do something about your presence? It’s terrifying everyone.” Yuan said.

Everyone at the Endless Arena had instinctively halted their movements since Long Wu Qing’s appearance and hadn’t moved a muscle since then. Some even tried running away, but their bodies were stiff from fear.

chapters.title: Chapter 1688 Dragon God Festival Details
chapters.body:
Chapter 1688 Dragon God Festival Details

Chapter 1688 Dragon God Festival Details

Long Yejun and the others looked at Long Wu Qing with nervous gazes, seemingly waiting for her reaction. Nobody has ever dared to ask her to suppress her presence since she killed the last one who did.

In the Primal Expanse, displaying one’s presence was a way of flaunting their status and prowess. The more powerful the presence, the better. To be told to stop, even if it was a reasonable request, could be interpreted as disrespect. It was akin to telling a wealthy man to stop showing off their jewelry in public because they were reflecting the sunlight and bothering those around him.

“…”

After a long moment of silence, Long Wu Qing’s tyrannical presence unexpectedly began to weaken until it was barely noticeable. Her oddly obedient behavior greatly shocked everyone there, with some even wondering if she was truly the feared Unrivaled Tyrant.

“There. You happy now, little human?” Long Wu Qing glared at Yuan.

He nodded and said, “That’s perfect. Let me show you how to use the seasoning.”

After teaching her the basics, Long Wu Qing began testing other combinations on her own. Sometime later, she’d managed to somewhat replicate the meat skewers’ flavor.

“Hey, do you have any more seasoning? This isn’t enough to recreate the taste of those skewers you gave me,” she said.

“Sorry, but that’s all I have right now.”

“When can you get more?” she inquired.

“When I return to the human world, but once I do that… I won’t be returning.”

“…”

Long Wu Qing fell silent again.

“What a pity,” she muttered a moment later and continued to eat in silence.

‘I can’t believe it! That tyrant is actually have a peaceful meal with everyone!’ Long Yejun swallowed nervously, feeling like he was living a dream.

When he pinched himself and sensed pain, Long Yejun nearly cried tears of joy.

‘As expected of Brother Yuan… If anyone can tame that monster, it’s him!’ he glared at Yuan with a profound gaze.

Yuan’s spine shivered at Long Yejun’s gaze.

‘What is this ominous feeling?’ he wondered inwardly.

For the next few days, Yuan and the others continued to feast until all of the food was devoured, leaving not even the bones. Of course, it was the beasts who consumed all of the bones. As big as his appetite was, Yuan wouldn’t eat bones.

10:46

For the next few days, Yuan and the others continued to feast until all of the food was devoured, leaving not even the bones. Of course, it was the beasts who consumed all of the bones. As big as his appetite was, Yuan wouldn’t eat bones.

Shortly after they finished eating, a powerful presence suddenly appeared in the Endless Arena, alerting everyone there.

“We greet the almighty Dragon God!” The beings there respectfully greeted upon noticing him. freewebnσvel.cѳm

The Dragon God remained silent.

A few moments later, he opened his mouth and spoke, his voice resounding throughout the entire Divine Dragon Sanctuary.

“Heed my words. I will be announcing the details for this year’s Dragon God Festival.”

The entire Divine Sanctuary became dead silent without a single entity making a sound.

“This year’s Dragon God Festival will consist of three separate trials, each requiring the completion of the previous to participate.”

“The first trial shall be a one-on-one deathmatch. In order to make things as fair as possible, your cultivation must be suppressed to Perfect Chaotic Commander. Those of you with a lower cultivation will have to deal with the difference if your opponent has a higher cultivation. Once you participate, you will not be able to forfeit. In other words, half of the participants will die!”

The first trial left everyone speechless. While death was not uncommon during the Dragon God Festival, with many perishing in each event, this trial had been the deadliest to date—and it was only the first of three trials. The sheer brutality of it shocked even the most seasoned participants.

‘Why did the Dragon God make such a trial?’ Long Yejun wondered, his gaze subconsciously shifting toward Yuan.

Yuan was only a Perfect Chaotic Master. If he were unlucky with his opponent, he’d need to fight a Perfect Chaotic Commander in a worst-case scenario.

Although the atmosphere remained quiet, a significant portion of the participants had already made the decision to skip this year’s Dragon God Festival. Whether it was due to insufficient cultivation or simply an unwillingness to risk their lives, many chose to withdraw rather than face the deadly trial ahead.

The Dragon God continued to speak after giving the listeners a moment to think about the first trial.

“Those of you who survived the deathmatch will be qualified to participate in the second trial. In the second trial, you will be given a Blood Reservoir containing my blood. If you are able to absorb and refine more than 90 percent of my blood within 30 days, you will pass the second trial.”

“The Dragon God’s blood?!” Countless beings exclaimed in a shocked voice after hearing the details of the second trial.

Even if it wasn’t his Blood Essence, the Dragon God’s blood was an invaluable treasure, capable of strengthening bloodlines and even transforming a mere snake into a true dragon. Most beings present would willingly start a war over such a precious resource, let alone risk everything in a one-on-one deathmatch.

‘We only need to pass the first trial to absorb the Dragon God’s blood in the second? Even if we only manage to absorb a drop of his blood in the second trial, it’s completely worth it!’

Those who had already decided to skip this year’s Dragon God Festival immediately had a change of heart. They have decided to participate even if they had to risk their lives, including those with a lower cultivation.

‘Why is the Dragon God being so generous for this year’s Dragon God Festival…? No… this is a little too generous. I doubt he’d really let so many people absorb his blood with so little risks.’ Long Yejun and the Seven Dragon Monarchs stared at the Dragon God with suspicious faces.

The Dragon God suddenly laughed, “You lot sure seem eager to absorb my blood. However, did you think it would be so simple?”

‘I knew it!’ Long Yejun cried inwardly after the Dragon God proved his worries to be correct so quickly.

chapters.title: Chapter 1689 Dragon God Festival Details(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1689 Dragon God Festival Details(2)

Chapter 1689 Dragon God Festival Details(2)

The Dragon God continued to speak, “The Blood Reservoir contains exactly ten drops of my blood, so you will need to absorb and refine at least nine drops to pass the trial.”

“However, with that said, the blood you’ll be absorbing is no ordinary blood. Every single drop has been enhanced, and if you’re unable to absorb it, you’ll immediately die a violent and explosive death.”

“For example, let’s say I only have the potential to absorb three drops of blood. If I try to absorb even a single drop more, it will spell the end for me. You may think it’ll be fine as long as you stop at your limit, and while that is true, only some of you will have the capability to tell your own limit. The majority of you will not know your limit and will essentially be walking on a thin rope in the void.”

“I will give you a warning. Whether you heed it or not is none of my problem. Many—and I mean many of you here will not even be able to absorb a single drop of my enhanced blood!” The Dragon God’s voice was grim but also felt oddly provocative, as if he was daring them to participate.

The second trial was arguably even more dangerous and deadly than the first. While half of the participants would perish in the first trial, there was a good chance that most of the participants would perish in the second trial.

As the reality set in for those present, they began to wonder why this year’s Dragon God Festival was so dangerous and different from the rest. It was as though the festival had been tailored and fine-tuned for a particular individual.

Unbeknownst to those present, the Dragon God had changed the Dragon God Festival at the very last minute. While some speculated that was the case, they had no solid proof, and they were not going to question the Dragon God.

Yuan silently stared at the Dragon God. Although the Dragon God wasn’t looking at him, he had a weird feeling the Dragon God was somehow paying attention to him.

“Let’s move on to the last trial.” The Dragon God continued a few moments later.

“If you manage to absorb and refine nine drops of my blood and pass the second trial, you’ll be able to participate in the third and final trial, where you will get the opportunity to refine my Blood Essence!”

“WHAT?!”

Almost everyone there exclaimed in shock, Long Wu Qing included. Long Yejun’s eyes were so wide that his eyeballs were on the verge of falling out of their sockets.

“You did not hear me incorrectly. For the third trial, you’ll need to refine my Blood Essence to become this year’s champion.”

Yuan was speechless. He could’ve never expected the Dragon God to use his own Blood Essence for a mere festival. However, he wasn’t exactly delighted, either.

While he’d managed to refine the Primal Monarch’s Blood Essence, it was mainly because he already had the Immortal Monarch’s Bloodline. The Dragon God’s Blood Essence was an entirely different story and could outright kill him if he attempted to do so.

‘I still don’t dare to consume the Dragon Ancestor’s Blood Essence in my current state, and the Dragon God is most likely even stronger than the Dragon Ancestor. How could I possibly refine his Blood Essence? It sounds like an impossible task…’

The only one there who even had a chance to refine the Dragon God’s Blood Essence would be Long Wu Qing. However, when Yuan looked at her to see her reaction, her face was full of doubt and worry.

‘So even she’s not confident, huh?’ Yuan shook his head inwardly.

“That is all. Due to the unnatural nature of this year’s Dragon God Festival, I will give you all an extra month to think about it. Until then.”

The Dragon God disappeared into thin air afterward. Right before the Dragon God left, Yuan noticed the Dragon God giving him a quick glance.

The entire Divine Dragon Sanctuary remained silent for a few more moments before commotions erupted in every direction.

“Holy fuck! This year’s Dragon God Festival is fucking batshit insane!”

“It’s as dangerous as it is rewarding! Who is going to participate?” freewebnσvel.cøm

“I was… until his warning for the second trial…”

Nearly everyone was having a conversation about the Dragon God Festival at this moment.

“Brother Yuan, what do you think? Are you still going to participate?” Long Yejun turned to ask him.

“The festival turned out to be much more dangerous than I could have ever anticipated. You probably don’t want to hear this, but I don’t want you to participate. It’s too dangerous. This has nothing to do with your talents. Humans are simply unable to absorb the Dragon God’s blood, much less the enhanced version.”

Yuan nodded, “I am aware of its danger. We still have a month to decide, but as of this moment, I haven’t given up yet.”

“It’d be quite bad if one of us gets paired with each other during the first trial…” Sky Splitting Dragon suddenly commented.

“Yes. It’s incredibly risky, and it’ll only become riskier with fewer participants.” Baneful Dragon responded.

“As enticing as the trial seems, I won’t be participating.” Refined Obsidian Dragon had already made up his mind. He was already one of the Seven Dragon Monarchs, and his passion was smithing. He almost has no reason to risk his life at this festival.

“What about you two, the Dragon God’s descendants?” Abyssal Dragon looked at Long Yejun and Long Meihui. He didn’t even bother asking Long Wu Qing because he already knew her answer.

In response, Long Meihui looked at Long Yejun and Long Wu Qing with a worried frown.

“I’m worried that I might need to fight with my own siblings. Will the Dragon God—our father really be fine with us killing each other for this festival?” she wondered out loud.

Long Meihui wasn’t worried that she had to kill her siblings. If she really fought with Long Yejun or Long Wu Qing, Long Meihui had no chance of surviving. She was worried for her own life. It was as simple as that.

chapters.title: Chapter 1690 Yingzi
chapters.body:
Chapter 1690 Yingzi

Chapter 1690 Yingzi

“I…I don’t think I’ll participate in this year’s Dragon God Festival.” Long Yejun spoke after Long Meihui, surprising the others.

“I can’t lie that the rewards aren’t enticing, but as mentioned, there’s a chance I might fight with my siblings or Brother Yuan. I don’t think I’ll be able to kill them if I matched against them.”

Long Yejun could not bear fighting his sworn brother for real, much less kill him. However, since it was a rule in the Dragon God Festival, there was no way to avoid it if he really fought against Yuan.

The Dragon God Festival was more than just a festival. It was a sacred ritual, one that even the Primal Monarch could not interfere with ease.

If Long Yejun and Yuan matched against each other, one of them would need to die, and the Dragon God himself would make sure of it even if it were the last thing he did.

“Hah!” A scornful laugh suddenly resounded.

Everyone there turned to look at Long Wu Qing, who’d just laughed.

She was staring at Long Yujun and said, “Just say you’re afraid of being killed by me. This is your only chance to become truly powerful, but you’re shying away because of some emotional bullshit.”

“Or do you believe the Dragon God will give us another opportunity like this ever again? I don’t think so.”

Long Yejun clenched his teeth, but he couldn’t refute her claims. The chances of a similar Dragon God Festival occurring in the future were extremely slim to none. If he were to let go of this opportunity, he would never close the gap between him and Long Wu Qing.

“If you’re worried about killing me, don’t.” Yuan suddenly chimed in. “If we really matched against each other, I wouldn’t hesitate to kill you. Of course, I will also have no qualms if you kill me.”

“Brother Yuan…?” Long Yejun looked at him with wide eyes.

Yuan smiled and said, “I told you. I need to get my hands on the Sacred Chalice of Purification no matter what. Being sworn brothers doesn’t mean we cannot kill each other. As long as we both agree to it, nothing is off the table.”

“Brother Yejun. I don’t mind if you kill me.” Yuan confirmed with a serious expression on his face.

After a moment of silence, Long Yejun smiled and said, “I also don’t mind if you kill me. Even if I die, it’s not like I will be gone forever. Due to my bloodline, I’ll eventually be reincarnated. However, if I let go of this opportunity, I will never have a chance like this again.” freeweɓnovēl.coɱ

“Then it’s settled.” Yuan nodded with a smile.

Long Yejun turned to look at Long Wu Qing and continued, “Don’t think you’ve won already. We’ll both have our cultivation suppressed, so even if we fought, it’s not like I don’t have any chance at all to defeat you.”

Long Wu Qing laughed out loud, as if she just heard something unbelievable. “What a ridiculous joke. Even if I suppressed my cultivation to Perfect Chaotic Master, you will never defeat me.”

Long Wu Qing shifted her attention to Yuan and said, “You better not run away, little human. You claimed you’d win the Dragon God Festival. If you dare run away, I’ll kill you right here and now.”

Yuan smiled and said, “Don’t worry, I don’t have any plans on running.”

Although he was worried about the second and third trials, he still had to do it for Feng Yuxiang’s sake.

‘No… this is no longer just about Feng Feng. If I lose here, there will be countless casualties from both the Divine Dragon Sanctuary and the Eternal Phoenix Domain,’ he sighed inwardly.

If he fails to acquire the Sacred Chalice of Purification, the Primordial Phoenix will lead the Eternal Phoenix Domain and raid the Divine Dragon Sanctuary, resulting in countless deaths.

“Good, because you seemed hesitant moments ago.” Long Wu Qing smiled.

“I could say the same for you, Wu Qing.”

“Hmph. As if. You were just seeing things. Then I’ll see you next month.”

Long Wu Qing flew away shortly after.

“I’ll probably still skip this year’s Dragon God Festival.” Long Meihui sighed a moment later. “But I’ll wait to see how many will be participating. We have a month, after all.”

After saying that, Long Meihui also left.

“I’ll also leave now.” The others left one after another until it was just Long Yejun, Yuan, and Yingzi.

“What about you, Yingzi? You going to participate?” Yuan asked her.

She immediately shook her head.

“I have no interest in this sort of thing,” she said.

“I see.”

“I’m going to go home and take this time to prepare myself.” Long Yejun said a moment later. “You’re welcome to join me.”

Yuan shook his head and said, “It’s fine. There’s nothing I can do to prepare for the upcoming trials.”

“Then I’ll see you here in a month.”

Long Yejun disappeared afterward.

Now that it was just Yuan and Yingzi, he said to her, “I’m going to leave for a month.”

“Hey, can I come with you?” Yingzi suddenly asked.

“Come with me…? I don’t think that’s possible, unfortunately.”

“You know this isn’t my real body, just a fragment of my soul, right?”

She nodded.

“I am returning to my real body now, and I don’t think there’s a way for you to come with me. Sorry.”

“It’s okay,” Yingzi said.

A split second before Yuan logged off, Yingzi’s figure suddenly flickered and disappeared into thin air.

When Yuan returned to Earth, he removed his helmet and got off the bed to stretch.

“Since there’s nothing I can do to prepare, I might as well use this time to relax,” Yuan mumbled.

“Let’s take a shower first.”

Just as Yuan turned around to face the bathroom, he was shocked to see a petite figure standing right behind him, her face filled with wonder and excitement.

“Y-Yingzi…?” Yuan muttered in a dazed voice, his eyes wide with disbelief.

“Yes?” she calmly responded, confirming that she wasn’t his imagination.

“H-how in the world…?”

chapters.title: Chapter 1691 Yingzi(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1691 Yingzi(2)

Chapter 1691 Yingzi(2)

“Yingzi, how did you follow me?” Yuan asked her with a dumbfounded expression on his face.

“I followed your shadow,” she calmly responded.

“My shadow…?”

“How much do you know about shadows?” she asked him next.

“Honestly, not much.”

Yingzi proceeded to explain, “Most believe that shadows are universal for everyone. While that is somewhat true, it’s different for us, Shadow Dwellers.”

“Just like how everyone has a different soul, their shadow is also unique to them. However, only those born in the shadows like us Shadow Dwellers can see the difference.”

“That means as long as I enter your shadow, I’ll be able to emerge from your shadow, no matter where that may be.”

“With that said, I wasn’t certain I would be able to follow you, and I acted on a whim. But I’m glad it worked. This world may be small, but it has a lot of delicious scents.”

“…”

Yuan stared at Yingzi with a worried gaze. While he was confident that Yingzi wouldn’t cause any trouble for him or Earth, this incident exposed a loophole—a risk that he wasn’t even aware of before.

If Yinzgi was able to follow him from the Primal Expanse through her own abilities, there was a chance the same could happen again. This meant that Earth wasn’t completely safe from unexpected invasions.

‘I will need to be more wary of who I log out around. I should let the others know this too.’ Yuan thought inwardly.

It’d be disastrous if someone dangerous were to follow him back to Earth, after all.

He looked at Yingzi and spoke a moment later, “Um, Yingzi. I trust you, and I don’t mind your presence here, but you’ll need to be extra careful in this world. As you know, this world is very small and fragile. If someone like you were to use your abilities, it would fall apart.”

She nodded, “I am aware. I won’t do anything to your territory.”

“Thank you. By the way, are you feeling okay? This world doesn’t have any Chaotic Essence.”

He figured that if humans were unable to cultivate in the Primal Expanse, those from the Primal Expanse would have the same issue if the roles were reversed.

“I guess it does feel a little uncomfortable since I have never been this deprived of chaotic essence, and I won’t be able to replenish my chaotic essense if I use them, but it doesn’t have any detrimental effects on me.”

“That’s good. Anyway, since you’re here, is there anything you want to do? I’ll show you around.”

Without hesitation, Yingzi replied, “Food.”

“I figured.” Yuan smiled.

“Alright, give me a few minutes to shower first.”

“What is a shower?” she asked.

“It’s when we clean ourselves with water.”

“Why? You don’t look dirty.”

“We can shower even if we’re not dirty.”

“Is it common for humans to shower?”

“Very. Pretty much everyone showers.”

“Okay. I will shower with you,” Yingzi declared.

“If you want.”

Yingzi followed Yuan into the bathroom a moment later.

Once inside, Yuan began taking off his clothes. Yingzi followed.

A certain object on Yuan’s body quickly attracted Yingzi’s attention.

“Hey,” she spoke. “What is that weird thing between your legs?”

When the beings of Primal Expanse first took on the appearance of humans, most got rid of what they deemed useless, such as the object between a man’s legs. In their eyes, it was nothing but a hindrance since they don’t require sexual intercourse to reproduce. Hearing Yingzi’s odd question, Yuan subconsciously looked at the area between her legs. However, what was supposed to be there wasn’t. It was a smooth area without the slit.

“I see,” He muttered as if he understood something.

“Looks like beings in the Primal Expanse only care about the face and overall appearance, so they forgot the most integral part of humans—their private part.”

This made Yuan wonder about their internal organs.

“Anyway, unlike you, we humans reproduce through sexual intercourse, which is done by connecting a woman and man’s private part.”

Even though Yingzi had the appearance of a little girl, her real age could potentially rival even the Lord, so Yuan had no issue teaching her. Most importantly, she wasn’t even human, hence why Yuan could take a shower with her without batting an eye.

“Anyway, let me show you how to shower.”

Yuan quickly changed the subject and pointed to the shower area, which was large enough to fit two or even three people with ease.

“You operate it with this handle.”

Water began pouring from the shower head.

“You can adjust the water’s temperature like this. Turn it this way if you want cold water and the opposite way if you want hot water. Keep it in the middle if you want warm water.”

“Ooooh.” Yingzi stared at the pouring water with interest. “It’s like rain.”

Yuan stepped into the shower once the water temperature was to his liking.

“Once your body is wet, you use body wash to cleanse your body like this. You can also wash your hair with shampoo and finish it with conditioner.”

“Then, you rinse yourself again with water.”

Yuan stepped out a few minutes later and wiped himself with a towel.

“That’s the gist of it. Pretty simple, right?”

Yingzi entered the shower next, and she played around with the water temperature while standing below the shower head.

In the end, she settled with hot water that was raised to the maximum, quickly steaming the place.

Once she was satisfied, she stepped out of the water and started washing her body with body wash and hair with shampoo just like Yuan did.

Afterward, she rinsed herself and stepped out, feeling refreshed.

“We also take baths after showers if you want to relax. It’s when you submerge yourself in water. Since we’re already here, want to try it out?” Yuan asked.

Yingzi immediately nodded.

Yuan quickly prepared the bath.

They entered the large bathtub sometime later and spent the next several minutes in silence.

“I have been meaning to ask, but who are the two female humans outside sleeping next to each other?” Yingzi suddenly asked.

chapters.title: Chapter 1692 Yingzi(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1692 Yingzi(3)

Chapter 1692 Yingzi(3)

“Ah, those two? They’re my partners, Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang.” Yuan explained.

“Partner… so you’re breeding them?” she spoke calmly. The weird phrasing and her calm tone sent shivers down Yuan’s spine.

He cleared his throat and explained, “While some humans enter a relationship with the determination and intent of having children, not everyone wants children. We became partners because we love each other.”

“What is love?”

“It’s an emotion we feel towards someone we want to be with forever—someone you really like. For example, you like to eat food, right? Love is basically several times more intense than that.”

“Hm. I still don’t understand.”

Yuan pondered for a moment before asking her, “How do you feel about me? What emotions do I invoke from you?”

Yingzi stared at his handsome face in silence.

“You pique my interest. You’re always full of surprises. Also, when I look at you, I don’t feel the desire to rip you apart, unlike others,” she said a moment later.

“You’ve followed me for over two years now. Why?”

“At first, I wanted to learn more about humans. But before I realized it, I found myself wanting to learn more about you.”

“Then how do you feel about following me for the rest of your life? Would you be able to do it?”

“I’m not sure what to feel, but I can’t imagine following you for the rest of my life,” she responded with a calm expression.

Yuan smiled and said, “If you can imagine yourself following me—or anyone for that matter—for the rest of your life, you’ll get closer to understanding the feeling of love. With that said, love is a very complex emotion, even for us humans, and there are many different types of love. Of course, it’s also fine if you don’t understand it for the rest of your life. It’s just an emotion, after all. Just like how some people never feel fear, it’s possible some will never experience love.”

“By the way, why do you want to understand humans?” Yuan suddenly asked.

“I don’t really have a reason. I think I am inherently interested in them,” she responded. “Is there a problem with that?”

“No, that’s fine. We humans often do things for no reason, too.”

“I—”

Just as Yingzi opened her mouth, another voice interrupted.

“Welcome back, Yuan! Are you taking a bath? Let me join you!” A cheerful voice came from outside the bathroom.

The very next moment, a naked Chu Liuxiang could be seen strolling into the bathroom with a bright smile on her face.

However, her smile froze when she noticed an unfamiliar little girl resting in the same bathtub as Yuan.

“Eh? Huh? Wait…” Chu Liuxiang’s brain nearly overheated from trying to comprehend the situation.

Chu Liuxiang had no qualms if Yuan found more partners, having already come to terms with and accepted the idea. However, the girl with Yuan appeared so young that it would instantly raise many eyebrows wherever they went, and even someone as accepting as Chu Liuxiang couldn’t help but feel a sense of concern.

Seeing Chu Liuxiang’s panicked and perplexed expression, Yuan said, “Calm down, it’s not what you’re thinking.”

He proceeded to explain the situation in a calm but hasty manner.

“First of all, she’s not as young as she looks. She’s actually from the Primal Expanse, and she somehow followed me back to Earth when I logged out.”

“…”

After taking a long moment to process this information, Chu Liuxiang asked, “But why are you taking a bath together?”

“I am teaching her about humans and our culture. Ah, she’s also not human.”

“So she’s not the ‘third’?” she asked for clarification.

Yuan calmly shook his head.

Chu Liuxiang heaved a deep sigh of relief once her worries were eliminated.

“Oh, I was worried for a second there.”

Chu Liuxiang went to take a quick shower before entering the bath with them.

“Hello, I’m Chu Liuxiang,” she introduced herself to Yingzi.

“I’m Yingzi. We were just talking about you,” Yingzi said.

“Really? About what?” Chu Liuxiang’s eyes flickered with curiosity.

Yuan gave her a brief description of the subject.

Chu Liuxiang chuckled after hearing it.

“Is that so? But Yuan, if you ever want a child, don’t hesitate to ask me.”

Yuan silently nodded with a somewhat stiff smile.

“By the way, how did she follow you from the Primal Expanse?” Chu Liuxiang asked a moment later.

“Her race has the power to control and enter shadows. She entered my shadow right before I logged off and managed to come out through my real body.”

“Such a thing is possible…? That’s ridiculous… and has some serious implications…” Chu Liuxiang muttered in disbelief. “I know what you’re thinking, but if Yingzi is able to come to Earth through her own powers, we cannot dismiss the possibility that others could accomplish the same,” Yuan sighed.

“Isn’t that really dangerous for us?” she asked.

“It is. However, I doubt there are many beings out there who have such capabilities. Still, we need to be careful where and who we log off around from now on in case we do meet someone similar to Yingzi.”

Chu Liuxiang nodded with a serious face.

Sometime later, the three of them left the bathroom.

“By the way, the Dragon God Festival was postponed for a month, and I’ll be staying here for the whole duration. However, I cannot leave Yingzi unattended, so I’ll be accompanying her for a bit.” Yuan said.

Chu Liuxiang nodded, “Alright. Then I won’t bother you for the time being.”

“Can you let the others know the situation for me?”

“Of course.”

After giving Yuan a passionate kiss on the lips, Chu Liuxiang returned to Cultivation Online.

Meanwhile, Yuan went to find Meifeng. However, she was also inside Cultivation Online.

‘I guess Yingzi can experience her food later.’

Yuan brought Yingzi outside.

“I don’t know where to bring you, so let’s just wander until you find something you want to eat,” Yuan proposed with a casual smile.

“Okay!” she nodded eagerly.

chapters.title: Chapter 1693 Yingzi(4)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1693 Yingzi(4)

Chapter 1693 Yingzi(4)

After leaving the Dragon Spiral Mountain, Yuan brought Yingzi to the nearest city for their first destination.

Before leaving, Yuan consumed an appearance-altering pill to change his face so they don’t attract unwanted attention.

“We’re moving too slow. I’ll bring you with me like usual.” Yingzi complained halfway there.

Yuan nodded and said, “Do you see the city in that direction? That’s our destination.”

Yingzi swiftly wrapped her chaotic essence around Yuan and, as if they’d teleported, arrived at the city instantly.

Earth was simply too small for someone like Yingzi. With her cultivation, she could easily circle the world a dozen times before someone could even finish blinking their eyes.

‘Either her cultivation is too high for formation to work on her, or it has no effect on those with a Chaotic Heart…’ Yuan thought inwardly after realizing the cultivation restricting formation had no effect on Yingzi.

After arriving at the city, Yingzi took a moment to savor the unique scenery of tall buildings, cars, and bustling humans.

Although she’d already seen this view through her divine sense, looking at it with her own eyes and being there in person invoked a special feeling that couldn’t be achieved through divine sense.

A look of awe appeared on Yingzi’s face as she realized the complexity of human technology.

“Just how did humans achieve this…?” she mused in a low voice.

“This is what happens when society focuses on technology rather than cultivation. You may find this hard to believe, but the majority of this world learned about cultivation only a few years ago,” Yuan said with a slight smile.

“Is that why everyone’s cultivation is so weak—barely noticeable? Even the weakest beings in the Primal Expanse would be a bigshot here.” “Unfortunately.”

Once she had enough of the scenery, Yingzi walked to the nearest restaurant and pointed at it from outside.

“I want to eat whatever is inside,” she said.

Yuan nodded and approached the waiter by the entrance. “Table for two. Ah, can I also have your biggest table? We’ll be ordering one of everything.”

The waiter looked at Yuan with wide eyes, seemingly in disbelief.

“One of everything? We have 200 different dishes, you know?”

Yuan silently nodded.

“O-okay. Please follow me.”

The waiter led them to one of the biggest tables available and asked again once they were seated, “Just to confirm, one of everything?”

“Yes.”

“Umm… can you pay in advance first?”

Yuan retrieved a black card and placed it on the table.

“T-thank you very much, esteemed guest!” The waiter’s eyes flickered with excitement after seeing the card.

Once the payment was confirmed, the waiter placed their order.

“Why is it taking so long?” Yingzi complained not even five minutes later, her disappointed gaze focused on the empty table before her.

“Besides fast food, they only start cooking once an order is placed. This guarantees the food will come out fresh and hot.” freeωebnovēl.c૦m

After waiting another ten minutes, their first dish finally arrived, with more dishes coming out nearly every minute.

The workers were initially worried they’d need several tables to accommodate all the food, but Yingzi quickly relieved them of their concerns. She devoured each meal within seconds, effortlessly keeping pace with the speed at which dishes were coming out of the kitchen.

Yuan didn’t touch any of the meals and allowed Yingzi to hoard all of the food. Yingzi’s unfathomable ability to consume so much food with her small body left the others shocked. They took out their cellphones and began recording her.

“Heavens! Is she a professional eater?”

“That already exceeds the level of pros!”

“Where does she keep the food? Is there a black hole in her stomach?”

The other customers muttered to each other.

Once Yingzi finished all of the dishes, she stood up and said to Yuan, “Let’s head to the next place immediately.”

Yuan nodded with a smile.

As they walked to their next destination, which was right next door, Yuan asked her, “How does it compare to the meat skewers you’ve been eating?”

With an excited expression on her face, she responded, “The flavors are much more complex, and the textures vary greatly between dishes. While some tasted better than meat skewers, others were less enjoyable. However, overall, even the least impressive dish is far superior to anything I’ve experienced in the Primal Expanse.”

Once they arrived at the restaurant next door, Yuan showed the waiter his black card and said, “I want to order one of everything on the menu. You can charge me in advance.”

The waiter silently stared at him, as it took some time to comprehend his words.

“R-right away!”

When the customers from the previous restaurant realized Yingzi planned on eating more, they followed her to the next restaurant with their phones out, acting like a group of paparazzi.

“My god! They ordered one of everything again!”

“How much does she plan on eating?!”

An hour later, Yingzi finished every dish with ease, and they made their way to the next food establishment, repeating the same thing.

News of a monstrous little eater began spreading across the internet, quickly gaining traction. Videos of Yingzi eating gained millions of views in just a few hours, and every single one of these viewers was shocked by her infinite appetite. With her newfound fame, Yingzi earned the nickname Little Fairy of Gluttony.

However, many were skeptical of her eating ability and claimed it was all fake. Once Yingzi became popular enough, people began traveling to her location just to watch her eat with their own eyes, with some traveling even halfway across the world just to confirm her existence.

“She’s still eating?! This is her 27th food establishment, and she’d been eating almost nonstop for the last ten hours!” The onlookers couldn’t comprehend how Yingzi could eat so much.

When some onlookers attempted to approach her, their advances were immediately halted by Yuan, who was concerned they might annoy Yingzi. He knew that irritating her could potentially lead to disastrous consequences, and he wasn’t willing to take that risk.

A few stubborn individuals tried to force their way past Yuan, only to be sent flying.

‘I knew she’d attract attention, but this is too much…’ he sighed inwardly.

chapters.title: Chapter 1694 Yingzi(5)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1694 Yingzi(5)

Chapter 1694 Yingzi(5)

“Bastard, who the hell do you think you are?! We are just trying to speak with the Little Fairy of Gluttony!” One of the individuals who tried to force his way through and was sent flying returned shortly after to shout at Yuan.

“Little Fairy of Gluttony?” Yuan couldn’t help but crack a chuckle after hearing her nickname.

“Anyway, she’s a friend of mine, and we don’t need any distractions. I don’t mind if you watch from a distance, but stay away from my friend unless you want to die.”

Of course, Yuan had no intention of killing anyone, but Yingzi was a different story. He didn’t know much about her or the limits of her patience, and the last thing he wanted was for things to escalate due to her unpredictable nature.

“Unless you want to die.” The man repeated Yuan’s words in a mocking tone. “Get a load of this bastard!”

Suddenly, another figure stepped forward. He was a skinny young man with depraved facial features, looking like a ghoul.

“Step aside before you get hurt.” The skinny man warned Yuan as he showed off his Spirit Grandmaster cultivation base.

“…”

After a moment of silence, a low sigh resounded.

“Should I just get rid of all of them?” Yingzi stopped eating to ask.

Yuan looked at her and said, “Just focus on your food. I can deal with them myself.”

“Alright.” Yingzi no longer paid them any attention and returned to eating her food.

Yuan released a defeated sigh the next moment. He looked at the skinny man and muttered, “Since we’ve already attracted this much attention, there’s no point in hiding myself any longer.”

Yuan removed his disguise, revealing his true identity to everyone there.

“P-Player Yuan?!” Someone from the crowd instantly recognized him and shouted.

“What?! The same Player Yuan who disappeared after stomping the Fiery Garden of Hell?! He hasn’t shown himself for over three years now!”

Yuan narrowed his eyes at the skinny Spirit Grandmaster and those around him. “I am having trouble breathing with so many people around me. Do you mind giving me some space?”

The skinny man snapped out of his daze and hurriedly left without uttering another word. The others swiftly followed.

Yuan shook his head and returned to his seat. Now that he’d made an appearance for the first time in over three years, the attention on Yingzi skyrocketed to unprecedented levels.

“Player Yuan returns after three years! Unexpectedly, he’s friends with the Little Fairy of Gluttony!”

“What’s Player Yuan’s relationship with Little Fairy of Gluttony?!”

“Player Yuan disguised himself to be with Little Fairy of Gluttony! A secret relationship exposed?!”

Such leadlines swiftly filled the internet.

Despite the growing attention and the increasing size of the crowd around them, nobody dared to approach Yuan or Yingzi. The spectators kept their distance, content to watch from afar, clearly still fearful of Yuan.

Although three years had passed, the majority of people still vividly remembered Yuan’s fight with the Fiery Garden of Hell. The sheer intensity of that battle left an everlasting impression, keeping his reputation fresh in their minds.

After spending a few days visiting literally every single food establishment in the city, Yingzi traveled to the next city and repeated the same thing.

Her seemingly infinite appetite made people wonder if she had a unique physique that refines food into her own energy. Unbeknownst to them, Yingzi truly had an infinite appetite. Shadow Dwellers are made entirely of shadows, so no matter how much Yingzi ate, she would never feel full. However, she doesn’t feel hungry, either.

While unnecessary, Yingzi still enjoyed eating, just like how powerful cultivators could survive without food but still indulge in it once in a while.

In the blink of an eye, two weeks had passed since Yingzi’s visit to Earth. During that time, she had experienced a wide variety of foods and cuisines. However, there was only so much truly unique food to try. Eventually, she began encountering similar dishes, with only minor differences in flavor and quality.

Although she still enjoyed them, Yuan could tell that she was slowly getting bored of them.

“Food can vary greatly depending on our location, with some food only available in specific parts of the world. If you want to experience newer food, we should travel further.” Yuan suggested.

Yingzi nodded and said, “Okay. I’ll let you choose the place next.” After finishing her current meal, Yuan brought Yingzi to the other side of the world to experience their cuisine.

They spent one week eating as much food as they could. Then, for the last week, Yuan bought seasoning for her to use in the Primal Expanse. He bought so much that they had to visit several supermarkets because he emptied all of their stock.

“This month I have spent in this world has been the best of my entire life. I will remember it forever. Thank you,” Yingzi said to Yuan with sincerity as they returned to the Dragon Spiral Mountain.

Yuan nodded with a smile, “You’ve done a lot for me in the Primal Expanse. This is the least I could do for you.”

“Before we return to the Primal Expanse, I would like you to have one last meal.”

“Okay.”

Yuan brought her to the dining room, where a feast prepared by Meifeng was waiting. The Demon Sealing Faction members were also present.

“So that’s the famous Little Fairy of Gluttony, huh?” Wang Ming chuckled after seeing her.

“She resembles Xiao Hua…” Meixiu mumbled after noticing their similarity. After all, Yingzi borrowed most of her features from Xiao Hua.

“Although you’ve eaten a lot of food in the past month, you ate mostly alone. Some say food will taste better when you’re eating with others.” Yuan said to her.

Once the introductions were done, they began feasting.

When Yingzi took her first bite, her eyes widened with surprise.

‘He’s right… there is something different about this food…’ she mused inwardly.

“Hey, Yuan, the Dragon God Festival is tomorrow, right? How confident do you feel about it?” Shi Lang suddenly asked. freeωebnovēl.c૦m

A stiff smile appeared on Yuan’s face as he responded, “Honestly? I don’t feel confident at all.”

“Seriously?” Everyone there turned to look at him while looking surprised.

chapters.title: Chapter 1695 The Dragon God Festival Begins!
chapters.body:
Chapter 1695 The Dragon God Festival Begins!

Chapter 1695 The Dragon God Festival Begins!

Seeing their surprised faces, Yuan explained the details of the Dragon God Festival and why he didn’t feel confident with them.

“The Dragon God is a godlike entity with strength that rivals a Cultivation God. I cannot fathom someone at my level would be able to absorb it, much less refine it.”

While he had a heaven-defying physique that allowed him to refine things one typically could not, even extending the limit of what those around his cultivation could absorb, it did not allow him to refine anything without limit. The training grounds were prime examples. It wasn’t as if he could walk straight into the highest difficulty without any preparation. His body could still be destroyed if its limit was exceeded.

“There’s also one thing I am worried about.”

Yuan wore a serious expression as he spoke, “There is a chance I might not respawn if I die in the Primal Expanse.”

“What? Why not?” Wang Ming inquired.

“Because we don’t know everything about the system. What if the revival system only works within the Nine Heavens? What if I am too far away for it to work? The heavens have no influence in the Primal Expanse, after all.”

After a moment of silence, Yuan asked, “Were there any announcements recently? About me, specifically.”

“No, announcements are quite rare nowadays, and anything related to you would instantly rile up the internet,” Meifeng said. freewёbnoνel.com

“In that case, I’m certain there are limitations to the system. Unless someone has learned Celestial-grade techniques before me, there should’ve been an announcement about it. I even received the notification that I was the first. However, since I was in the Primal Expanse when it happened, something probably prevented the announcement from being made.”

“What if it’s like a cell phone and reception?” Wang Xiuying suddenly said. When the others looked at her, she explained, “You know how cell phones cannot receive messages without reception? What if the Nine Heavens is like a cell phone, and the reason it didn’t make any announcement was because it couldn’t receive Yuan’s data in the Primal Expanse?”

“That does make sense and definitely a possibility…” Yuan nodded with a pondering face.

“In that case, the announcements may show up once you return to the Nine Heavens.”

“I guess we’ll find out when he returns.”

“In any case, the chances of my worries coming true have increased now.” Yuan sighed.

“Having said that, I have no plans on backing down now. I am going to tackle this Dragon God Festival, regardless of the risks,” he swiftly continued with a resolute face.

Although he was concerned, he wasn’t losing his mind over it. After all, he was pretty confident the Primal Monarch wouldn’t let him die needlessly.

After the feast, Yuan and Yingzi prepared to return to the Primal Expanse.

“I have learned a lot about humans this past month,” Yingzi said as she sat on his balcony.

“After seeing so many humans, I think I’ll be able to craft a unique appearance for myself.”

She closed her eyes and began visualizing her ideal appearance.

In the next moment, Yingzi’s appearance began to transform. Her hair grew longer, its color deepening into a much darker shade of black, resembling the void itself. At the same time, her skin became even paler, almost ghostly white, creating a striking contrast against her dark hair.

Her height increased, and her face matured slightly. In just a few moments, Yingzi had transformed from a cute, innocent little girl to a strikingly beautiful girl with a naturally aloof expression, and she even wore a black dress that complimented her appearance.

From behind, she appeared completely human in every aspect. However, once her front was revealed, she left an unforgettable impression, her appearance teetering between human and something ethereal, almost ghostly. It was an otherworldly sight that would linger in one’s mind.

“What do you think?” she asked him afterward.

Yuan smiled, “I think it suits you perfectly.”

Yingzi smiled back and said, “Now the only thing left for me to experience is sexual intercourse. Hey, let’s have sex before we return to the Primal Expanse.”

Her bazaar words instantly shattered whatever atmosphere they just had. However, Yingzi spoke with such clarity that it didn’t sound like a joke at all.

“That is something you should only experience with the one you love, especially if it’s your first time,” Yuan responded with a stiff smile.

“Is that how it works?”

“Normally.”

Yingzi wore a disappointed look on her face.

“Fine.”

Without saying another word, her figure disappeared into Yuan’s shadow.

Yuan returned to the Primal Expanse shortly after.

The moment he returned, something in the sky caught his attention.

There were millions of gold tokens hovering in the sky, and beings could be seen grabbing one from time to time.

As Yuan wondered about their purpose, a familiar presence approached him.

“You’re back.” Long Yejun spoke with a smile.

“Yes. What are those tokens in the sky?” Yuan asked him. “Grab one if you wish to participate in the Dragon God Festival,” he briefly explained, showing the token in his grasp.

Yuan looked around and noticed that nearly everyone around him had a token, and there were tens of thousands of beings in his area alone.

He went to grab one for himself a moment later.

“I’ll leave just in case the Dragon God matches us in groups.” Long Yejun suddenly said.

He wanted to lower his chance of being matched against Yuan as much as possible.

“Then I’ll see you after the first trial. Good luck, Brother Yejun.”

“Best of luck to you too.” Long Yejun disappeared into the distance shortly after.

The very next day, Dragon God’s voice rippled throughout the Divine Dragon Sanctuary.

“This is your last chance to grab a token and participate in the festival.”

“In thirty seconds, everyone with a participation token will be teleported to a secluded arena with their opponent, and you will not be able to leave until one of you dies. If both fighters are still alive after a month passes, the space will collapse and kill both of you.”

The participants were shocked by the last part, which wasn’t mentioned until the last second, causing some of the participants to change their minds by tossing their tokens away.

Once thirty seconds had passed, everyone holding a token disappeared into thin air without any warning.

The Dragon God Festival has begun!

chapters.title: Chapter 1696 Yuan’S Opponent
chapters.body:
Chapter 1696 Yuan’S Opponent

Chapter 1696 Yuan’s Opponent

After being teleported, Yuan found himself surrounded by darkness, almost as if he’d returned inside the Infinite Spiraling Abyss.

However, oddly enough, he was able to see his opponent clearly.

“Hahaha! What luck!” His opponent, a red-headed man, laughed hysterically.

Yuan stared at his opponent with a calm gaze. This wasn’t their first meeting.

“I can’t believe I actually got matched with the Primal Monarch’s son! How lucky!”

“Hellfire Dragon…” Yuan muttered with a slight smile on his face.

Hellfire Dragon was one of the Seven Dragon Monarchs as the Fire Dragon Monarch. He was someone nobody wanted as an opponent, especially for a deathmatch.

However, despite being matched with one of the strongest beings in Divine Dragon Sanctuary, Yuan wasn’t even the least concerned. After all, his opponent specialized in fire.

‘Fortunately, I visited the Eternal Phoenix Domain and acquired fire immunity before coming here.’

“Why are you staying silent? Are you despairing because you realized that you’re doomed?” Hellfire Dragon taunted, appearing very confident.

“On the other hand, you talk too much. They say when one realizes they are screwed, they often become more chatty than usual to cope with their anxiety,” Yuan sneered, wiping the smile off Hellfire Dragon’s now enraged face.

“As I thought, I really don’t like you, human.”

The Hellfire Dragon wasted no time transforming into his true dragon form. Shedding his human appearance, he became a massive red dragon, his scales glowing like molten lava. His aura radiated such intense heat that the air around him twisted and shimmered from the scorching energy.

The Hellfire Dragon’s aura was so powerful that it would have been enough to kill an ordinary Chaotic Commander. However, to Yuan, it was nothing more than a comfortable, lukewarm sensation, barely worth noticing.

“So what if you have godly talents? That shit will amount to nothing if you die an early death.” Hellfire Dragon remarked. “You got greedy, human. If you hadn’t participated in the Dragon God Festival, you would’ve become a powerful figure.”

Yuan’s face remained calm as he responded to his provocation, “Where is your confidence coming from?”

“Where?” Hellfire Dragon sneered.

“Right here!” He abruptly opened his mouth and spat a massive fireball at Yuan.

Despite seeing the ball of fire hurling at him, Yuan didn’t move a muscle and silently stared at it. Once the fireball was close enough to touch him, Yuan extended a finger to poke at it. The moment his finger came into contact with the fireball, its heat and energy were absorbed.

“W-what?” Hellfire Dragon’s eyes widened with shock after witnessing his fire being absorbed.

“Why do you look so flabbergasted? Did you think I only had two types of immunities? Unfortunately for you, I am also immune to fire.”

“Since when?!” Hellfire Dragon roared.

“Since before I came to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary, obviously.” Hellfire Dragon clenched his jaws tightly, his gaze so intense it could engulf an entire world in flames.

“So what if you’re immune to fire? You’re still just a human! I don’t need my fire to kill someone like you!” The Hellfire Dragon started laughing.

“I heard that you almost died to Long Yejun even when he suppressed his cultivation. I am on the same level as Long Yejun if not a little stronger. There’s nothing you can do to defeat me.”

Yuan chuckled and said, “Brother Yejun is indeed very powerful. However, if I wanted to kill him during our spar, it wouldn’t have been too difficult.”

“You’re bluffing!” Hellfire Dragon did not believe Yuan’s claims for a second.

Not only was Yuan a mere Chaotic Master, but he was also human, who were inherently weaker than beasts. “Don’t think you can rely on the Primal Monarch’s power for everything! It won’t work on someone like me!”

“I never intended on using the Primal Monarch’s power, anyway.”

Yuan suddenly retrieved a sword—a majestic sword that emitted an otherworldly aura.

“A sword?” Hellfire Dragon raised his eyebrows, looking surprised. However, it was not because he was worried.

“That tiny thing won’t even scratch my scales, much less—”

In the midst of Hellfire Dragon’s speech, Yuan’s arm suddenly flickered, shooting a powerful sword light made of pure Enhanced Sword Qi at him.

Sha!

The sword light went right through Hellfire Dragon’s right wing, cutting through his scales as if they were tofu.

A moment later, the Hellfire Dragon released a deafening roar, not out of pain, but from the overwhelming shame he felt after realizing one of his wings had been severed. The humiliation of such a blow stung more than any physical injury could.

“I will make you suffer a fate worse than death for this humiliation!” Hellfire Dragon released one last roar before charging at Yuan in his human form.

Meanwhile, their fight was being spectated by the onlookers who didn’t participate in the Dragon God Festival. However, it wasn’t just Yuan’s fight that was being broadcasted. Every single fight was displayed for everyone to see.

Although Yuan’s fight attracted some attention at first, they all went to watch another broadcast, one that had nearly the attention of every single onlooker at the moment.

This specific broadcast displayed Long Wu Qing’s dominating performance as she brutally toyed with her opponent, which wasn’t too surprising considering her unrivaled status.

However, what truly surprised the onlookers and initially captured their attention wasn’t just Long Wu Qing’s presence, but her opponent.

“What’s wrong? Are you already giving up? It hasn’t even been half a day since we started.” Long Wu Qing provoked her opponent with a cold grin on her face.

“Shut up!” her opponent roared, their voice filled with fury. Their entire body was drenched in blood, open wounds covering nearly every inch of their skin. Meanwhile, Long Wu Qing appeared unscathed in comparison.

“What a pitiful sight.” Long Wu Qing shook her head at the fully transformed dragon before her.

“Well, it’s not like you ever had a chance in the first place—just as I said. Right, little brother?” Long Wu Qing remarked, her voice cold and condescending as she stared at Long Yejun with an eerily calm expression, unfazed by his bloodied state.

chapters.title: Chapter 1697 The Sibling’S Deathmatch
chapters.body:
Chapter 1697 The Sibling’S Deathmatch

Chapter 1697 The Sibling’s Deathmatch

After being teleported, Long Yejun found himself inside a secluded dimension that was created solely for the Dragon God Festival. He didn’t waste any time looking at the void and searched for his opponent.

However, he instinctively knew who he had been matched against after sensing her tyrannical presence.

“Elder sister.” Long Yejun muttered in a calm voice after confirming that his opponent was Long Wu Qing.

He had already mentally prepared himself to fight Long Wu Qing, so he didn’t act surprised. As a matter of fact, he had spent the past month preparing to fight Long Wu Qing.

“Why am I not surprised, little brother?” Long Wu Qing suddenly spoke. “Is this fate, or is this father’s desire?”

There was a chance the Dragon God had purposefully matched them together. However, they had no proof.

“Either way, it doesn’t matter. I have been prepared to fight you since I decided to participate in the Dragon God Festival,” said Long Yejun.

“Really? It doesn’t look that way.” A cold smile crept on Long Wu Qing’s face as her fierce eyes glared at Long Yejun’s left arm, which had been trembling nonstop for some time now.

“!!!” Long Yejun was surprised when he looked at his arm. He wasn’t aware that he had been trembling until Long Wu Qing mentioned it.

He hastily grabbed his left arm with his right, forcing it to stop trembling.

“Hahaha!” Long Wu Qing couldn’t help but laugh at his reaction.

In response, Long Yejun released a deafening roar as he transformed into a blue and white dragon.

“What’s the rush, little brother? I have a whole month to kill you, so there’s plenty of time to play. However, if you wish a swift death, I can also make that happen.”

“Screw you!” Long Yejun cursed before launching a quick Dragon’s Breath at her.

However, Long Wu Qing casually flung it away with a swatting motion. “At least you’ve become bolder than before. Or maybe it’s because you know you are going to die soon?” Long Wu Qing treated Long Yejun as a joke.

As infuriated as Long Yejun was, he knew that Long Wu Qing had the power to back up her arrogance.

“Here, I’ll lower my cultivation a little more for you.” Long Wu Qing’s cultivation base suddenly dropped from the peak of Chaotic Command to the peak of Chaotic Master, dropping an entire realm.

Her actions were incredibly arrogant and the ultimate mockery to her opponent. However, Long Yejun wasn’t offended. If anything, he saw this as his chance.

‘I knew you’d do something like this! Your arrogance will be your downfall!’ Long Yejun smiled inwardly as he prepared to launch a full-scale attack on her.

After throwing a few more weak attacks at her, Long Yejun unleashed his most powerful technique, utilizing all of his chaotic essence.

When facing a monster like Long Wu Qing, a prolonged fight would inevitably end in defeat. There was only one way to stand a chance against someone like her—a swift and sudden attack that exploited her weakness: her arrogance. Her tendency to underestimate her opponents could be her downfall if timed correctly.

Unfortunately, Long Yejun miscalculated one thing—the true extent of Long Wu Qing’s overwhelming power.

Even without transforming, Long Wu Qing easily defended against the strongest attack Long Yejun could muster with his suppressed cultivation.

“Did you think I was underestimating you?” Long Wu Qing spoke after seeing the look of disbelief on his face.

“It’s the opposite, little brother. I lowered my cultivation because I know exactly what you’re capable of—that you’d still not be able to defeat me.”

“You fucking monster…” Long Yejun muttered.

Long Wu QIng’s eyebrows twitched, and she scolded, “Just because you’re about to die doesn’t mean I’ll let you say whatever you want.”

For the next few hours, Long Wu Qing would launch an endless assault on Long Yejun, beating him left and right.

Despite Long Yejun’s attempt to fight back, Long Wu Qing was too overpowering for him to do anything.

‘Damn it! If only our cultivation wasn’t restricted this much… No… even if the limit was higher, the results would have remained the same…’ Long Yejun sighed inwardly, accepting the results—his defeat.

“Giving up already? This is why I always mess with you.” Long Wu Qing looked at him with a disdainful gaze after seeing his state, and she increased the intensity of her attacks.

“You give up too quickly. You never push yourself past your limit, and you have a habit of running away from your problems. You’re a disgrace to the Dragon God’s bloodline.”

Long Wu Qing’s words ignited a fire in Long Yejun’s heart, and he released a vicious roar.

“I know I can’t defeat you, but I will survive for a month, and then the both of us will perish together!”

Long Wu Qing merely laughed at his plan, showing no signs of concern. She knew that she could defeat him whenever she wanted, after all.

A few hours later, Long Wu Qing sighed, “Seeing you struggling like this isn’t fun at all. I’d rather go watch that human’s performance than beat a corpse like you any longer. Any last words?”

“…”

Long Yejun didn’t respond. His body hovered in the void like a lifeless corpse, but he wasn’t dead just yet.

“Is that so? I hope you said your goodbyes to your human friend, at the very least.”

“He’s… not a… friend…” Long Yejun suddenly muttered. “He’s my… brother.”

“I’ll consider those your final words.” Without saying another word, Long Wu Qing unleashed a devastating attack at Long Yejun, far more powerful than anything she had displayed so far. “This is your last chance to intervene, father.” Long Wu Qing muttered in a low voice as the attack enveloped Long Yejun’s figure.

However, the Dragon God did not respond, and Long Yejun’s body was obliterated, disintegrating completely under the overwhelming power of Long Wu Qing’s attack.

Long Wu Qing found herself back in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary shortly after.

chapters.title: Chapter 1698 Hellfire Dragon
chapters.body:
Chapter 1698 Hellfire Dragon

Chapter 1698 Hellfire Dragon

After returning to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary, Long Wu Qing ignored the nearby stares being tossed her way and looked for Yuan’s fight.

Once she found it, she disappeared into thin air, appearing at the location it was being broadcasted a moment later.

Her sudden appearance terrified those nearby, causing them to flee as if their lives depended on it.

“I see… so that’s how he fights…” Long Wu Qing muttered with a slight smile on her face as she analyzed Yuan’s fight.

If someone were to look at her right now, they’d never imagine she had killed her own little brother just moments ago based on her calm and nonchalant demeanor.

“Oh, I remember now.” Yuan suddenly spoke as he dodged the Hellfire Dragon’s attack.

“We’ve fought before,” he continued.

“What the fuck are you talking about?” Hellfire Dragon halted his movements to ask. He was certain they had never fought before.

“Well, you didn’t fight the current me, but Tian Yi,” Yuan explained.

“I recall fighting you near the end of my journey. It happened quite long ago, so I don’t blame you if you don’t remember. You had the appearance of a female human at that time, too.”

Although the Hellfire Dragon had the appearance of a human man at this moment, he also had female appearances in his collection. When he first encountered Yuan, he was in his female appearance. The reason Yuan recognized him despite his new appearance was simply because of his unique presence.

Just like how one could identify Long Wu Qing through her tyrannical presence, one could also identify the Hellfire Dragon through his fiery presence.

While beings in the Primal Expanse also had genders, such as male and female, some of them tended to adopt whatever appearance they preferred. The distinction between a female human and a male human was mostly cosmetic in their eyes.

The Hellfire Dragon was a male dragon, but he preferred a female human’s appearance. Hell, even his male appearance was very feminine and could easily be mistaken as a female from afar.

The Hellfire Dragon became silent to ponder Yuan’s words.

‘The last time I fought a human was—’

His eyes widened after realizing the meaning behind Yuan’s words.

“Y-you! You were that bastard human from back then?!” Hellfire Dragon cried out loud as he recalled some bitter memories.

“N-now that I take a good look at your face, you definitely resemble that human! But how is that possible?! You only have a single Chaotic Heart, not to mention your cultivation!”

Yuan merely smiled at his confusion, refusing to explain further.

After learning that Yuan was Tian Yi, the Hellfire Dragon’s eyes flickered with fear. He had been badly beaten in the past by Tian Yi, hence his hatred toward humans. “Are you remembering that day?” Yuan suddenly inquired. “You should’ve just left me alone. If you hadn’t provoked me, I wouldn’t have done that to you.”

Hellfire Dragon’s entire being trembled after Yuan mentioned a certain event.

A few hundred million years ago, the Hellfire Dragon encountered Tian Yi and provoked him, threatening to make him a human pet. Tian Yi became so enraged that he plucked every single one of Hellfire Dragon’s scales, turning him into a scaleless dragon for millions of years until his scales eventually grew back.

That incident had humiliated the Hellfire Dragon to such an extent that it drove him to the brink of insanity. Consumed by the desire for revenge, he trained like a madman until he became a powerhouse. It could be said that Tian Yi was the catalyst who shaped the Hellfire Dragon into who he is today—one of the formidable Seven Dragon Monarchs.

However, Tian Yi left the Primal Expanse shortly after that incident and never appeared again. After hundreds of millions of years, Hellfire Dragon started to forget about that incident.

“After so long, I have finally found you…” Hellfire Dragon muttered in a shivering tone, his voice filled with excitement.

“I have trained myself to the brink of destruction countless times for this moment!”

The Hellfire Dragon resumed his assault, pouncing at Yuan with a ferocity unlike anything he had shown before. It was as if his power was being fueled by the immense rage he had contained within him for the past several hundred million years.

Yuan remained composed. Despite the Hellfire Dragon’s brief boost in power, it was not enough for him to defeat Yuan, who was immune to his fire attacks, and any physical damage dealt would also heal in the blink of an eye.

The Hellfire Dragon had no real means of defeating Yuan, and as the fight dragged on, it became clear he was merely wasting his energy. Each of his attacks, though fierce, lacked the effectiveness needed to overcome Yuan’s defenses.

On the other hand, every time Yuan struck with Enhanced Sword Aura, Hellfire Dragon needed to expend energy to recover from the damage.

It was a fight the Hellfire Dragon was destined to lose, and he realized it. However, instead of accepting it calmly like Long Yejun, he became more and more frenzied—desperate.

Eventually, Yuan had enough and decided to end the fight with Heaven Splitting Sword Strike.

He unleashed a massive beam of light that closely resembled a Dragon’s Breath. In fact, many onlookers mistook Heaven Splitting Sword Strike as Dragon’s Breath.

Hellfire Dragon’s figure was completely engulfed by the light. Once the attack ended, he was nowhere to be seen.

A few moments later, Yuan was transported back to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary.

“Congratulations on your victory, Yuan.” Long Wu Qing approached him shortly after his return.

“Thank—”

“However, if that’s the full extent of your capabilities, I’m afraid you won’t qualify to be my opponent,” she continued.

“…”

Yuan didn’t respond, but it wasn’t due to a lack of words. Instead, his attention was drawn to a certain energy lingering around Long Wu Qing.

“Where’s Brother Yejun?” Yuan suddenly inquired with a serious expression.

“…”

Long Wu Qing refused to respond, merely staring at him in a deafening silence. ƒreeωebnovel.ƈom

“Did you…?” Yuan’s eyes narrowed. He looked at her as if he already knew the answer.

“Yes, I killed him,” Long Wu Qing eventually responded, her voice as calm as it could be.

chapters.title: Chapter 1699 End Of The First Trial
chapters.body:
Chapter 1699 End Of The First Trial

Chapter 1699 End of the First Trial

After hearing Ling Wu Qing’s confirmation that Long Yejun had matched up against her and lost, Yuan tightly clenched his fists.

Long Wu Qing paid attention to every little detail and asked, “Are you angry at me for killing him?”

Yuan closed his eyes for a moment and took a deep breath before responding calmly, “No, I am not.”

“Oh?” Long Wu Qing looked genuinely surprised by his answer.

She knew humans were emotional beings who are often led astray by their emotions, causing them to do unreasonable things.

“My, my. You’re surprisingly calm. Totally not what I expected.”

Yuan raised an eyebrow and responded, “Were you expecting me to lash out at you for doing something I would’ve done myself if I had been matched with him?”

He had prepared to kill Long Yejun if they fought, after all. It would be unreasonable for him to be angry at Long Wu Qing for doing what she has to do.

Everyone who participated in the Dragon God Festival accepted and agreed to such a risk before they grabbed the token so they couldn’t complain about being killed.

“I like you even more now. But that doesn’t mean I will go easy on you later,” Long Wu Qing said with a smile.

Yuan smiled back and said, “I may not blame you, but I am still going to vent my anger on you, so I apologize in advance.”

“I thought you weren’t angry?” she raised an eyebrow.

“Not at you, specifically. Just in general,” he clarified.

“But you’re going to take it out on me?” Long Wu Qing chuckled.

“If anyone could deal with my anger, it’d be you.”

“I guess that’s fine since it’ll make things more entertaining,” she nodded with an understanding expression.

After a few more days, Long Meihui defeated her opponent and returned to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary. When she first heard the news of Long Yejun’s death, she didn’t believe it. However, after she found Long Wu Qing and confirmed Long Yejun’s death, Long Meihui broke down in tears.

“Waaaaah! This is why I didn’t want to participate! Why would the Dragon God do this—let his own descendants die?!” she cried out loud.

“Stop exaggerating. It’s not like he’s gone forever.” Long Wu Qing shook her head.

“As a matter of fact, this isn’t his first time dying,” she suddenly revealed.

“I know that, but it could potentially be millions of years before I see him again!”

“What’s a few million years to beings like us?” Long Wu Qing sighed.

“That’s not the point!” Long Meihui screamed.

“Huh? Who the hell do you think you are raising your voice at?” Long Wu Qing glared at her with narrowed eyes.

“S-sorry…” Long Meihui immediately shrunk her body, looking like a timid rabbit.

“Hmph.” Long Wu Qing sneered.

Long Meihui withdrew to Yuan’s side and asked him, “Are you okay?”

“I am upset about Brother Yejun’s death, but this is the result if you lose.”

Long Meihui sighed, “I hope he reincarnates soon. I don’t think I can handle being alone with my older sister. She’s too scary.”

Yuan pondered for a moment before asking, “How long does it usually take for one to reincarnate?”

“It depends on factors I am unfamiliar with. He could immediately reincarnate, or it could take hundreds of millions of years,” she answered.

“I see. I hope he reincarnates sooner than later.”

After a brief moment of silence, Yuan spoke again, “One more thing. Will he remember memories of his past life?”

“I can’t say for sure. There’s a chance he might reincarnate without his memories, but I’m sure they will still be deep within his consciousness and eventually resurface.”

“Thank you.”

They proceeded to spectate the other ongoing fights for the remainder of the month.

Long Wu Qing approached Yuan again a few days later.

“Hey, do you have any more of those skewers?” she asked him without any shame or hesitation in her voice.

“You still have an appetite after everything that happened?” Yuan looked at her with a look of disbelief.

“Why not?” she responded in an unfazed manner.

Yuan couldn’t help but admire her carefree nature. It was as if nothing in this world could truly bother her.

“So, do you have any or not? Or are you going to refuse because I killed Long Yejun? Don’t be so petty,” she continued.

He sighed, “I do have some, but I am running low on supply, so I cannot give them out so casually.”
