“Sounds like an excuse to me,” she muttered with a displeased look.

However, Yuan wasn’t lying because he was being petty. He was really running low on meat skewers.

He had a chance to replenish his supply when he returned to Earth, but he couldn’t bring them back through the system and had to rely on Yingzi, who was capable of bypassing the system and bringing whatever she wanted back with her.

That said, Yingzi had managed to acquire a tremendous amount of meat skewers. During her food tour, she bought out the entire stock of every single stall she encountered. Of course, it was done using Yuan’s money.

‘Yingzi has plenty of meat skewers, but I doubt she’d be willing to share them.’ Yuan smiled inwardly. He also had no authority to make her share them, even if it was purchased with his money.

“If you give me some, I’ll share Long Yejun’s final words with you,” Long Wu Qing suddenly bargained.

Yuan raised an eyebrow at her words.

“His final words…?” he muttered.

“Alright.”

Yuan retrieved a handful of meat skewers and handed them to Long Wu Qing, who immediately repeated Long Yejun’s final words to him. Word for word.

“I see…” A warm smile appeared on Yuan’s face after hearing it.

As the end of the first trial approached, more and more participants returned to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary. However, there were still plenty of matches that seemed far from being concluded.

Once the time limit had been reached, all of the secluded dimensions were forcefully closed, crushing anyone who remained inside them without any warning.

chapters.title: Chapter 1700 Refining The Dragon God’S Blood
chapters.body:
Chapter 1700 Refining The Dragon God’S Blood

Chapter 1700 Refining the Dragon God’s Blood

“Congratulations to those of you who have survived the first trial.” The Dragon God’s voice rippled throughout the Divine Dragon Sanctuary.

“You may choose to continue to the second trial or give up now. Those of you who will continue, keep the token in your grasp. Otherwise, crush it. You have an hour to decide.”

Once the Dragon God’s voice faded, everyone began mumbling to each other.

“He said that most of us won’t even be able to refine a single drop of blood, correct? Do you think he’s serious, or is he trying to scare us into forfeiting?”

“Knowing the Dragon God, it could go both ways. It’s extremely risky, no doubt. However, the benefit of this trial is worth the risk, so I am going to risk it.”

“Many of us have risked our lives for much less. This is a no-brainer for me.”

Most of the participants shared similar sentiments.

Only a few remained hesitant. However, after thinking about it, they realized if they gave up now, they would have fought to the death for nothing in the previous trial. In the end, everyone who survived the first trial decided to participate in the second trial.

An hour later, the golden token suddenly emitted a golden light, and a small bottle materialized before the participants. Every participant had a single bottle containing ten drops of the Dragon God’s blood.

“You will need to refine at least nine drops of blood to pass the second trial within three months. If you manage to refine all ten drops of blood, I will give you a special reward at the end.” The Dragon God’s voice returned.

“What kind of reward?” The participants mused to themselves.

However, the Dragon God did not respond to their curiosity and disappeared after saying, “The second trial begins now.”

Yuan inspected the bottle of blood in his grasp. He had a quarter of a year to refine nine drops of blood, which seemed plenty of time at a glance.

However, after inspecting the Dragon God’s blood, each drop contained an immense amount of chaotic essence that could take months if not years to refine, but they’re only given three months to refine nine drops, which seemed impossible.

The first participant to act and consume a drop of the Dragon God’s blood was Long Wu Qing. She retrieved a drop and swallowed it without hesitation. She then closed her eyes and began to cultivate in silence.

Many participants followed and consumed their first drop.

“Agh!”

Suddenly, a painful shout pierced the air, drawing everyone’s attention. As heads turned toward the source of the scream, the individual who had cried out exploded into a gruesome, bloody mess before anyone could fully process what had happened.

However, that was just the beginning, and the situation quickly grew more chaotic as more and more participants exploded after failing to refine the Dragon God’s blood.

The gruesome scene terrified the others, but they couldn’t forfeit now and forcefully consumed their first drop of blood.

In half an hour, nearly 70 percent of the participants had perished from just the first drop of blood. There were tens of millions of participants who had passed the first trial, so one could imagine the carnage that occurred within these thirty minutes.

Yuan was one of the last participants to consume the Dragon God’s blood. As soon as the first drop entered his body, a powerful surge of energy began coursing through his veins, filling him with a strength unlike anything he had felt before. freewebnσvel.cѳm

However, that sensation was short-lived. Shortly after his body began surging with energy, something within his body activated and started consuming the energy at a tremendous rate.

<Nine Dragon Symbols have activated!>

<Nine Dragon Symbols has absorbed the Dragon God’s blood!>

The energy from the Dragon God’s blood disappeared within minutes, causing Yuan to raise an eyebrow.

When nothing else happened after waiting for several minutes, Yuan looked at the bottle of blood in his grasp. After some consideration, he retrieved the second drop of blood and consumed it.

His actions greatly bewildered the onlookers.

“Didn’t he just consume a drop of blood a few minutes ago?”

“You’re saying he’s already on the second drop? Impossible! Even the Unrivaled Tyrant is still on her first drop of blood! You definitely saw it wrong, and he’d just consumed his first drop.”

The onlookers quickly dismissed the possibility that Yuan had already refined the first drop of blood, let alone started on the second—everyone, that is, except for Yingzi. Having been watching Yuan closely from the very beginning, she was certain that he had already refined the first drop of the Dragon God’s blood.

“There’s no way he’d just refined a drop of my enhanced blood in just a few minutes. Did you do something to him?” The Dragon God questioned the Primal Monarch after witnessing this.

The Primal Monarch calmly shook his head and refuted, “No, I have nothing to do with this.”

<Nine Dragon Symbols have activated!>

<Nine Dragon Symbols is resonating with the Dragon God’s blood!>

<Nine Dragon Symbols has absorbed the Dragon God’s blood!>

Yuan absorbed the second drop of blood just as fast as the first… No, it was slightly faster.

“No, no, no. There’s just no way!” The Dragon God exclaimed as he watched Yuan consume the third drop of blood, his voice filled with disbelief.

“You must’ve done something to help him! I refuse to believe a human has the capability to refine my blood this quickly!”

The Primal Monarch glared at him and sneered, “Why would I lie to you? If I said I have nothing to do with it, then I didn’t.” His tone was cold, leaving no room for doubt or further questioning.

The Dragon God clenched his fists as he returned to focusing on Yuan, who was already preparing to consume the fourth drop of blood.

A few moments later.

<Nine Dragon Symbols have reached level four!>

chapters.title: Chapter 1701 Dragon God’S Special Reward
chapters.body:
Chapter 1701 Dragon God’S Special Reward

Chapter 1701 Dragon God’s Special Reward

<Nine Dragon Symbols have reached level four!>

<Due to the Nine Dragon Symbols, you have gained the ability to use ‘True Dragon Awakening’>

[True Dragon Awakening]

[Grade: ???]

[Description: Further increases your physical capabilities when transformed. Your base Physical Strength and Physical Defense will be multiplied by 10 times.]

“Oh my god! What are we witnessing?! He’s already on his fourth drop of blood!” The onlookers were bewildered once they confirmed that Yuan had actually managed to refine the Dragon God’s blood in such a short time.

“The Unrivaled Tyrant is still on her first drop of blood! The difference is overwhelming!”

“What’s causing this disparity? How can a human consume the Dragon God’s blood with such ease?”

The onlookers tried their hardest to comprehend the bizarre events occurring before them. Unbeknownst to them, even the Dragon God was baffled by it.

However, when Yuan’s Nine Dragon Symbols reached level four, its existence was revealed for a split second, and the Dragon God did not miss it.

“There’s a mysterious and powerful power hidden deep within his body. To be able to evade even my detection, its origin might surpass even myself.” The Dragon God spoke with a profound frown on his face.

“Primal Monarch, do you know anything about it? He’s your son, after all.” He turned to the Primal Monarch for help.

“Do you know every little thing about your own children?” The Primal Monarch responded with a question.

“If you’re that curious, you can just ask him later.”

The Dragon God sighed inwardly, ‘At this rate, he’ll refine all of my blood.’

Despite offering a special reward for anyone who could refine all of his blood, the Dragon God never truly expected anyone to accomplish it. However, now that someone appeared to have a good chance of clearing the second trial perfectly, he found himself having to seriously consider what the reward would be.

Meanwhile, Yuan continued to refine the Dragon God’s blood at a shocking rate.

<Nine Dragon Symbols have activated!>

<Nine Dragon Symbols is resonating with the Dragon God’s blood!>

<Nine Dragon Symbols has absorbed the Dragon God’s blood!>

In less than an hour, Yuan had cleared the second trial by refining nine drops of the Dragon God’s blood.

‘There’s only one drop left.’ Yuan thought inwardly as he stared at the final drop of blood inside its container.

Since he’d already refined nine drops of blood without any issues, Yuan had no reason to worry about the final drop and tossed it into his mouth without hesitation.

After consuming the blood, Yuan closed his eyes to let the Nine Dragon Symbols do all the work.

A few minutes later, Yuan opened his eyes and took a deep breath.

“That should be the end of it,” he muttered.

However, shortly after he refined all of the Dragon God’s blood, Yuan was forcefully teleported to another location.

“What’s this?” Yuan calmly stared at the Dragon God and asked.

“I’m going to distribute the special reward to you right now,” he said with a profound smile.

“Oh?” The Dragon God teleported him again. He was now standing before a massive pool of blood that was drifting somewhere in the Infinite Expanse.

“Is this what I think it is?” Yuan swallowed nervously.

The Dragon God nodded and said, “Yes, this is a pool of my blood, and it is also your special reward. I will give you until the end of the second trial to refine as much blood as you can from here.”

“Seriously? That means I get to spend three entire months absorbing as much blood as I want?” Yuan did not expect the Dragon God to be so generous.

“That’s exactly what it means. Are you not satisfied?” he asked.

“Of course not! I am more than satisfied!” Not wanting to waste any more time, Yuan immediately jumped into the pool that was large enough to be considered an ocean.

<Nine Dragon Symbols have activated!>

<Nine Dragon Symbols has absorbed the Dragon God’s blood!>

A whirlpool appeared around Yuan when he started to absorb the blood. Although the blood wasn’t enhanced, the quantity more than made up for it.

“What a terrifying scene…” the Dragon God mused to himself, watching in astonishment as Yuan consumed his blood like a black hole.

“At this rate, he might empty the entire place before the three months are up!” the Dragon God swallowed nervously.

A month passed in the blink of an eye.

<Nine Dragon Symbols have reached level five!>

<Due to the Nine Dragon Symbols, you have attained a ‘Dragon Body’>

[Dragon Body]

[Description: Your body now has the natural resilience and raw strength of a real dragon, even without transforming.] freēwēbηovel.c૦m

After acquiring a ‘Dragon Body,’ Yuan’s physique became akin to that of a true dragon while still retaining his human form. His natural strength, resilience, and power had all drastically increased, giving him the might of a dragon in the body of a man.

Dragons stood at the pinnacle of natural strength, born with immense power second to none. Even an infant true dragon could easily kill cultivators in the Divine Realm. In terms of innate prowess, even if Yuan had never acquired the Golden Immortal Physique, he would still be the strongest human with his Dragon Body.

After another two weeks, the Dragon God started to refill the blood in order to prevent it from being sucked dry.

“What a monster…” The Dragon God muttered with a stiff smile.

While Long Wu Qing was undoubtedly stronger than Yuan at the moment, the Dragon God believed that Yuan had the potential to not only catch up to her but eventually surpass her.

Time continued to pass. As the second trial neared its end, Long Wu Qing barely managed to refine the ninth drop of blood with only a week left to spare.

‘I don’t sense his presence anywhere in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary… Did he really die from trying to refine the Dragon God’s blood? What a pity…’ she shook her head in disappointment.

However, she didn’t linger too much on such feelings and immediately prepared for the final trial.

chapters.title: Chapter 1702 The Final Trial
chapters.body:
Chapter 1702 The Final Trial

Chapter 1702 The Final Trial

Long before Long Wu Qing even refined her ninth drop of blood, every other participant had already been disqualified. Some tried to refine more blood than they could handle and perished, while others gave up after refining their first drop of blood.

The vast majority of participants perished in the second trial, meaning tens of millions of participants were killed in just a few months. This level of casualty was unprecedented in the entire Primal Expanse and would definitely go down in history as the most deadly Dragon God Festival.

Of those few thousand participants who survived, about 95 percent of them only refined a single drop of blood. The rest of them only managed to absorb two drops of blood at most, with Long Meihui beating everyone at five drops of blood.

“What? You’re telling me that Yuan, a human, had managed to refine all ten drops of blood in less than a day?! As if I’d believe that, you idiot!” Long Meihui didn’t dare to believe this information despite being told by many witnesses.

“Where is Yuan right now? I want to verify it with him!”

“We don’t know where he went. That human disappeared shortly after refining all ten drops of blood.”

“The Dragon God probably took him away. Understandably so, too.”

Long Meihui scanned the entire Divine Dragon Sanctuary for Yuan’s presence, and sure enough, he had vanished completely. frёewebnoѵēl.com

“T-that doesn’t prove anything!” Long Meihui continued being in denial.

It was simply too hard to believe that a human with such a low cultivation could absorb the Dragon God’s blood, much less refine it.

However, when she recalled Yuan’s unbelievable achievements prior to the Dragon God Festival, his newest achievement became less unbelievable.

“Does my elder sister know about this?” Long Meihui asked.

The onlookers immediately shook their heads.

“Who’d dare be the one to let her know that someone—a mere human—had managed to completely surpass her? She also disappeared somewhere after she completed the trial.”

Not many people dared to speak with Long Wu Qing under normal circumstances, so it was even less likely that someone would approach her with bad news.

“So she doesn’t know… I wonder how she’ll react once she realizes…” Long Meihui muttered.

Meanwhile, somewhere in the Infinite Expanse, Yuan continued absorbing the Dragon God’s blood.

<Nine Dragon Symbols have reached level six!>

<Due to the Nine Dragon Symbols, you have attained a ‘Dragon Core’, increasing your cultivation speed by 100,000%. Your Dragon Core will also passively absorb and refine spiritual energy.>

After acquiring a Dragon Core, Yuan’s body naturally absorbed the surrounding energy with astonishing speed, as if he were actively cultivating even when at rest. This meant that even when sleeping, he’d cultivate faster than someone actively trying their hardest, giving him an unfair advantage during a fight when his enemy cannot replenish spiritual energy as easily.

Suddenly.

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has activated>

<Dragon Core has begun fusing with Chaotic Heart>

Ding!

<Dragon Core has fused with Chaotic Heart>

<You have attained Chaotic Dragon Core>

Yuan’s Dragon Core had unexpectedly fused with his Chaotic Heart. Now, it can absorb both spiritual energy and chaotic essence without any issue. “You have one day left.” The Dragon God notified him when his buffet was nearing its end.

Yuan opened his eyes and asked, “Do you mind if I take some of your blood back to my world?”

The Dragon God raised his eyebrows in a questioning manner and inquired about his reason for needing his blood.

Not hiding anything, Yuan explained that he knew a certain dragon family in the Nine Heavens and accidentally absorbed all of their precious blood during a sacred ceremony, and he promised to help them.

“Your blood is almost identical to the blood I absorbed at that time, so I figured I could use it to refill their supply,” he said.

“Identical…? It can’t be…” The Dragon God muttered with a serious expression on his face.

“I promise I won’t take much,” Yuan added.

The Dragon God nodded and said, “I’ll let you take some, but in return, you’ll have to forfeit your last day here.”

“Deal!” Yuan did not hesitate to sacrifice a day of Dragon God’s blood buffet to repay the Xi Family.

Yuan proceeded to collect the Dragon God’s blood with a large bottle.

“Okay, I’ve collected enough.” Yuan did not get greedy and only collected what he needed to help recover the Xi Family’s blood pond.

“Then I will send you back now.” The Dragon God said.

The next moment, Yuan found himself back in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary.

“The second trial has concluded, with two participants who have passed.” The Dragon God’s voice rippled throughout the Divine Dragon Sanctuary shortly later.

“What? Two participants? Who’s the second?”

The beings who were not aware of Yuan’s feat wondered.

Somewhere in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary, Long Wu Qing had a greatly surprised expression on her face.

“Two…? Is that why the trial didn’t end when I finished? Cause there was someone else in the trial? Who is the other?” she mused to herself.

Yuan did not come to her mind because she thought he’d already died.

“The final trial will begin in ten minutes.”

Ten minutes later, the Dragon God forcefully teleported Yuan and Long Wu Qing to a secluded platform in the Infinite Expanse. “Y-you’re still alive?!” Long Wu Qing’s eyes widened in shock as she saw Yuan standing beside her, alive and completely healthy. “You thought I was dead?” Yuan chuckled.

“But I couldn’t sense your presence! And I searched the entire Divine Dragon Sanctuary!”

“Ah, that’s because I was teleported by the Dragon God after I finished the trial,” he briefly explained.

“What…?” She looked as if she were staring at a ghost, her face frozen in disbelief. In her entire life, she had never experienced such shock.

“That means you had refined nine drops of blood even before me…?” she muttered in a dazed voice.

“Nine drops? No, I refined all ten drops.” Yuan corrected her with a smug look.

“?!?!?!”

chapters.title: Chapter 1703 Consuming The Dragon God’S Blood Essence
chapters.body:
Chapter 1703 Consuming The Dragon God’S Blood Essence

Chapter 1703 Consuming the Dragon God’s Blood Essence

“Ten drops of blood? He refined all ten drops of blood…?” Long Wu Qing muttered to herself with a somewhat blank look on her face, looking like she was experiencing a mental breakdown.

As unbelievable as it sounded, Long Wu Qing did not doubt Yuan’s claim. “How did you do it?” she asked.

“That’s a secret,” Yuan replied with a smile.

However, even Yuan didn’t know the answer. While he knew it was due to the Nine Dragon Symbols, he was unaware of its origins.

Long Wu Qing narrowed her eyes at him, almost as if she were trying to unveil his secrets.

‘His body has become stronger than before, and even his Chaotic Heart is different. It must’ve been the effect of consuming the Dragon God’s blood.’

If Long Wu Qing knew that Yuan had absorbed and refined an ocean of the Dragon God’s blood in the amount of time it took her to refine nine drops of blood, she’d probably go crazy from shock.

“Are you two done flirting with each other yet? If so, I’ll begin the final trial.” The Dragon God suddenly said.

“What nonsense is that…?” Long Wu Qing almost couldn’t believe her ears.

“I am ready. You can start the trial whenever.” Yuan said.

The Dragon God retrieved two drops of his Blood Essence and gave a drop to Yuan and Long Wu Qing.

“I will give you a month to refine my Blood Essence. The first to successfully refine it will be the winner of this Dragon God Festival and have one wish granted by me.” The Dragon God explained the rules.

“…”

After hearing the rules, Long Wu Qing looked at Yuan with a slight frown. If he’d managed to refine all ten drops of the Dragon God’s blood faster than she could refine nine drops, there was a good chance he’d also beat her in the third trial.

‘No… what am I even thinking?’ Long Wu Qing shook her head to wake herself up.

‘I can’t believe I thought I’d lose before the trial began. This is not like me at all.’

“Good luck,” Yuan suddenly said to Long Wu Qing.

“The final trial begins… now.” The Dragon God announced a moment later.

Like the first trial, the trial was broadcast to everyone in the Divine Dragon Sanctuary, and everyone watched as Yuan and Long Wu Qing consumed the Blood Essence.

<You have consumed Dragon God’s Blood Essence>

<Immortal Monarch’s Bloodline is reacting to Dragon God’s Blood Essence>

Ding!

<Due to the immense energy, you are having trouble absorbing the Dragon God’s Blood Essence>

<Nine Dragon Symbols has activated>

<Nine Dragon Symbols has absorbed some of the Dragon God’s Blood Essence>

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has activated>

<Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique has begun refining the Dragon God’s Blood Essence>

For the first time ever, all three of Yuan’s unique traits worked together to tackle a single source of power. However, even with his Immortal Monarch’s Bloodline, Harmonious Heaven Refining Immortal Physique, and Nine Dragon Symbols working together, the Dragon God’s Blood Essence was still too much for someone at Yuan’s level to handle.

The Dragon God narrowed his eyes at Yuan and wondered, ‘As I figured… No matter how much of my blood he absorbs, they’re just ordinary blood. It cannot compare to even a drop of my Blood Essence… Is this his limit?’

Yuan could feel his body swelling. He was on the verge of bursting from the overwhelming pressure building inside him like a balloon with too much air.

‘Ah… is this the end…?’ Yuan sighed inwardly, unable to do anything to reverse or fix the situation.

The Primal Monarch appeared and prepared to save him. However, the Dragon God intervened, saying, “You shouldn’t spoil your child too much. In case you forgot, we’re still in the middle of the Dragon God Festival. I will not allow any intervention, even if it’s from you.”

“Get out of my way.” A vicious glint flickered within the Primal Monarch’s eyes, and his aura emitted killing intent.

For the first time ever, the Primal Monarch displayed bloodlust. fгeewebnovёl.com

The Dragon God trembled, but he was unrelenting, even prepared to fight to death.

Suddenly, a mythical aura appeared, captivating both the Dragon God and the Primal Monarch.

When they followed the aura’s direction, it led them directly to Yuan.

“What the—?” The Dragon God subconsciously muttered. His eyes widened as he witnessed a golden object emerging from Yuan’s chest.

Once fully exposed, it was revealed to be a spear-like object.

When the Dragon God attempted to probe the spear with his divine sense, it was instantly and forcefully canceled, something that could only be done by an entity of equal or stronger prowess. This unexpected resistance left him momentarily taken aback.

Suddenly, the golden spear’s tip aimed directly at Yuan and shot forward. It pierced through the front of his chest and exited out his back, completely penetrating his body in one swift, brutal motion.

Ding!

<Dragon’s Soul has absorbed some of the Dragon God’s Blood Essence>

<Dragon’s Soul is resonating with the Dragon God’s Blood Essence>

<Dragon’s Soul has fully awakened>

<Dragon’s Soul has evolved into ‘Dragon God’s Vengeance’>

[Dragon God’s Vengeance]

[Level: Max]

[Rank: Soul Weapon]

[Growth Rate: ???]

[Requirement: Can only be wielded by Player Yuan]

[Description: A beautiful golden spear with an unknown origin. It is shrouded with a mysterious aura that will render all spiritual energy useless.]

[Unique Ability: Requires level 1 Nine Dragon Symbols to activate. When this weapon is out, it greatly enhances the strength of its wielder’s Dragon Techniques.]

[Unique Ability(2): Requires level 3 Nine Dragon Symbols to activate. If a dragon is killed with this weapon, the effects of this weapon will increase based on the dragon’s strength. This weapon also deals 100,000% increased damage to dragons and 10,000% increased damage to other beings.]

[Unique Ability(3): Requires level 6 Nine Dragon Symbols to activate. Allows the user to use Dragon God’s Vengeance’s special technique, Dragon God’s Suppression. If an enemy is struck by Dragon God’s Suppression, their bloodline will be sealed. This effect can only be removed by the Dragon God’s Vengeance. Only one bloodline can be sealed at a time.]

[Unique Ability(4): Requires level 9 Nine Dragon Symbols to activate. ???]

chapters.title: Chapter 1704 End Of The Dragon God Festival
chapters.body:
Chapter 1704 End Of The Dragon God Festival

Chapter 1704 End of the Dragon God Festival

Once the Dragon God’s Vengeance helped him absorb some of the Dragon God’s Blood Essence, Yuan no longer felt like he was expanding uncontrollably. However, he was still having trouble refining it.

The Dragon God stared at the Dragon God’s Vengeance with a profound expression, his gaze sharp and intense, almost as if he were trying to stare a hole into it.

Although he couldn’t see inside the Dragon God’s Vengeance, he was sure it contained a powerful soul, one strong enough to cancel out his divine sense.

While the Dragon God hadn’t used his full strength in his initial attempt to peer inside the spear, he had no intention of trying again. He didn’t want to risk angering the being within, and he had a strong sense that even with his full power, the outcome would remain unchanged.

Meanwhile, Long Wu Qing also struggled to refine the Dragon God’s Blood Essence. While she was strong enough to endure its overwhelming energy, she quickly realized that she would need far longer than a month to refine the Blood Essence fully. However, she didn’t give up and tried her best.

“Hey… isn’t that considered cheating?” One of the onlookers suddenly commented after witnessing everything.

“You literally just spoke my mind. Using a treasure to assist you is outright cheating.”

“But was there a rule that prohibited them from using treasures?” Someone asked.

“No… and the Dragon God didn’t immediately disqualify him, so it must be within the rules.”

The onlookers began debating with each other on the legality of Yuan using the Dragon God’s Vengeance to assist him in refining the Dragon God’s Blood Essence.

Although it was not explicitly stated, using external treasures during this trial was certainly against the rules. However, the Dragon God did not consider the Dragon God’s Vengeance an external treasure for one simple reason—because it was directly connected to Yuan’s soul. In that sense, it was more a part of his body than a separate item, making it an exception.

Having said that, even if Yuan cheated a little, the Dragon God wouldn’t have minded. After all, it was unreasonable to expect a human at Yuan’s cultivation level to refine his Blood Essence in the first place.

Two weeks later—

<You have refined the Dragon God’s Blood Essence>

<Immortal Monarch’s Blood has begun to evolve>

[Progress: 11%]

<Nine Dragon Symbols has begun to evolve>

[Progress: 50%]

<Nine Dragon Symbols have reached level seven!>

<Due to the Nine Dragon Symbols, you have attained a ‘Dragon Heart’, allowing you to learn and use dragon technique as if you are a true dragon, and your physical capabilities have improved drastically.>

Although the Dragon God’s Blood Essence had been completely refined, Yuan had only managed to refine a portion of it, with the Dragon God’s Vengeance refining the majority of it.

“Congratulations, you have won, becoming this year’s Dragon God Festival winner.” The Dragon God approached him shortly after he finished

Yuan pulled the Dragon God’s Vengeance out of his chest and looked at it with a pondering face before asking, “Doesn’t this count as cheating since I didn’t technically refine your Blood Essence myself?”

The Dragon God smiled, “You’re an honest one, huh? Don’t worry, it’s fine. I doubt anyone else would be able to replicate what you did even with the help of treasures.”

Yuan nodded.

He turned to look at Long Wu Qing the next moment. She was still trying to refine the Blood Essence, and her entire body was bulging with veins.

The Dragon God suddenly turned his gaze toward her, making a swift grasping motion. In an instant, he forcefully ejected his Blood Essence from her body. Even if a Blood Essence had been absorbed, as long as it hadn’t been fully refined, it could still be retrieved.

Afterward, Long Wu Qing immediately began gasping for air, her complexion as pale as a ghost. Despite her alarming appearance, she was merely exhausted and not physically injured.

After catching her breath, she turned to look at Yuan and then the Dragon God.

“I see… so I lost, huh?” she muttered in a bitter voice. freewebnoveℓ.com

Long Wu Qing struggled to get on her feet before limping toward Yuan.

“In one month—No… we will have our match in one week,” she said with a fierce gaze.

Yuan then said, “I don’t mind delaying it a little so you can recover properly.”

“This kind of injury will heal within days, and I want to fight you as soon as possible.”

He nodded, “If you insist, we will fight in a week.”

“Meet me at the Endless Arena exactly a week from now,” she said.

“I understand.”

After receiving Yuan’s confirmation, Long Wu Qing disappeared into thin air.

“As the Dragon God Festival winner, you have earned one wish. Tell me your wish,” said the Dragon God a moment later.

Yuan stared at him in the eyes and spoke in a clear and calm voice, “I want to borrow the Sacred Chalice of Purification. I need to bring it outside the Divine Dragon Sanctuary.”

“…”

The Dragon God didn’t immediately respond, but his eyes were wide with disbelief.

“T-that’s your wish? You can have almost anything, you know?” The Dragon God almost couldn’t believe how simple Yuan’s wish was and had to double-check.

“I don’t need anything else. I just need to borrow the Sacred Chalice of Purification,” Yuan repeated.

“…”

The Dragon God fell silent again.

Sometime later, he sighed, “If that’s your wish, so be it. However, rather than just letting you borrow it, I’ll let you keep it. The Sacred Chalice of Purification is a powerful treasure that can remove almost any curse out there.”

The Dragon God made a swift grasping motion toward a specific direction. Moments later, a beautiful golden chalice materialized in his hand, adorned with two lifelike dragons swimming around the cup.

He handed the treasure to Yuan the next moment.

<You have acquired Sacred Chalice of Purification>

[Sacred Chalice of Purification]

[Grade: Celestial]

[Quality: Peak]

[Description: A treasure of unknown origins. It has the power to remove almost all curses. Feed the chalice a drop of blood from the target to remove their curses.]

“Thank you.” Yuan immediately tossed the chalice inside his spatial ring.

“Now that the Dragon God Festival is over, I would like to have a private word with you. It’s related to the soul within that spear of yours.” The Dragon God suddenly said.

chapters.title: Chapter 1705 Dragon Goddess Yeyou’S Origin
chapters.body:
Chapter 1705 Dragon Goddess Yeyou’S Origin

Chapter 1705 Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s Origin

“You want to talk about the soul inside my spear…?” Yuan looked at the Dragon God’s serious expression and swallowed nervously. freeweɓnøvel.com

Noticing his nervous look, the Dragon God said, “Don’t worry, it’s not what you’re thinking. I just want to confirm something that has been weighing my mind for some time now.”

Yuan nodded, “Alright.”

The Dragon God created a rift between them and stepped inside it. Yuan followed.

The Primal Monarch, who had been silent this entire time, also followed them.’

After emerging from the other side of the rift, Yuan found himself in the Dragon God’s personal world. Strangely, it looked exactly like the Infinite Expanse, with vast stretches of empty space all around. They stood on a floating rock, slowly drifting through the void.

The Dragon God looked at the Primal Monarch and sighed, “What part of private conversation don’t you understand, Primal Monarch? Are you still worried that I might harm him or something?”

“I also want to confirm something. Don’t mind me and just have your conversation,” he calmly said.

Yuan then said, “Before that, I have a question for you, Dragon God.”

“What is it?”

“Did you purposefully match Long Yejun against Long Wu Qing?” he asked with a serious expression on his face.

“…”

The Dragon God smiled and replied, “I had nothing to do with their match. It was truly purely coincidence that they were matched up against each other. Whether you believe me or not is up to you.”

“Since you’re such good friends with Long Yejun, let me tell you something. Long Yejun has already reincarnated.”

“What? Is that true?!” Yuan’s eyes flickered with excitement.

“Yes. Not only that, but he’s even reincarnated in the human world.”

“Huh?” Yuan was taken aback by this information. “You mean he reincarnated in the Nine Heavens? Are you sure?”

“Yes, I am certain. I watched him leave the Primal Expanse after his death.”

Yuan clenched his fists with excitement.

‘Brother Yejun reincarnated in the Nine Heavens… This is great news.’ He decided to find Long Yejun once he returned to the Nine Heavens.

“Now then, let me ask you a few questions.” The Dragon God said.

“That soul living inside your spear… what is their identity?”

“I’m not sure I can reveal her identity without her permission…”

“Then, can you tell me if she is a dragon or not?”

“She’s a dragon.” Yuan nodded.

The Dragon God fell silent for a moment before looking Yuan in the eyes and asking, “Do you think I can have a word with her?”

“You want to speak with her? Why?” Yuan asked with a puzzled expression.

“I think I know her,” he said in a sincere voice.

Yuan began pondering, ‘If Feng Feng originated from the Primal Expanse, it’s likely Dragon Goddess Yeyou also came from this world.’

“Let me ask her.”

Yuan retrieved the Dragon God’s Vengeance and spoke to it, “The Dragon God would like to speak with you. Can you come out for a moment?”

“…”

Silence.

“Sorry, but I don’t think—”

Just as Yuan opened his mouth, the Dragon God’s Vengeance trembled, and Dragon Goddess Yeyou materialized before them.

Yuan immediately noticed that she looked a lot more complete than before.

‘Is this due to the Dragon God’s Blood Essence?’ he wondered inwardly.

“As I thought, it was you…” Dragon God muttered after seeing Dragon Goddess Yeyou with his own eyes.

“Do you remember me?” he asked, his voice emotional.

“I do, but I only regained my memories of the Primal Expanse recently,” she responded in a nonchalant voice.

“Why are you in that state?” Dragon God then asked.

Dragon Goddess Yeyou remained silent.

“I never thought I would see you again… sister,” Dragon God continued speaking.

“What…?” Yuan’s eyes widened with shock after hearing the word ‘sister’ come out of the Dragon God’s mouth.

Dragon God looked at him and smiled, “Yes, that’s right. She’s my sister—my twin sister. However, having said that, we were originally one being.”

“???” Yuan wore a puzzled look on his face.

“We were the Dragon God—a single being. However, we eventually split into two, becoming two different individuals,” he explained.

“How does something like that even work?” Yuan asked.

“We were two souls born into a single body, so we don’t share the same mind,” the Dragon God further explained. “Because of that, we were able to split ourselves into two and have our own bodies. However, in doing so, we also split our talents and prowess in half.”

Yuan was shocked by this information, as this meant that the Dragon God and Dragon Goddess Yeyou only had half of their original strength. Yet, they were still able to stand at the peak of their respective world.

‘How powerful would they become if they returned to being a single being again? Would they surpass even the Primal Monarch?’ Yuan wondered as he glanced at the Primal Monarch.

As if he could read Yuan’s mind, the Primal Monarch said, “No, they won’t. I have defeated them even before they split into two.”

“That was billions of years ago! Things might be different now!” Dragon God rebuked.

He then stopped paying attention to the Primal Monarch and returned to looking at Dragon Goddess Yeyou.

“Now that you’ve returned to the Primal Expanse, will you stay? I can help heal your damaged soul and restore your body,” he asked.

“No, I still have unfulfilled business. Also, I left this place for a reason.”

“I see… at least I got to see you again, as brief as it may be…”

Dragon Goddess Yeyou suddenly turned to look at Yuan, her gaze seemingly demanding something from him.

“How long do you plan on ignoring me? You should have regained most of your memories by now. Are you still going to act like you don’t know why I am upset?” she asked. “…”

Yuan suddenly became silent.

He closed his eyes and took a deep breath.

When he opened his eyes again, they flickered with a profound light, and his entire demeanor shifted. It was as if a deep, ancient soul had awakened within him, transforming his presence entirely.

“I wasn’t pretending, nor am I ignoring you. I just didn’t want to bother you before I figured out how to make it up to you,” Yuan said with a bittersweet smile. “I’m sorry, Yeyou.”

Yuan dropped the formalities, calling her directly by her name.

“Hmph,” she sneered.

chapters.title: Chapter 1706 Their Relationship
chapters.body:
Chapter 1706 Their Relationship

Chapter 1706 Their Relationship

“You… what’s your relationship with my sister?” The Dragon God inquired as he stared at Yuan with raised eyebrows.

“She’s my follower—” Yuan stopped mid-sentence as Dragon Goddess Yeyou shot him a dangerous glare, her eyes warning him not to continue.

“Ahem!” Yuan quickly cleared his throat before correcting himself, “She’s my lover.”

“?!?!” Hearing this unexpected answer, the Dragon God’s eyes nearly popped out of their sockets from shock.

The Dragon God turned to look at Dragon Goddess Yeyou for confirmation. Although Dragon Goddess Yeyou appeared nonchalant at a glance, her eyes flickered with delight.

“No way…” The Dragon God could feel his knees weakening. He had never felt so weak and vulnerable before.

“H-how did it happen?” he asked a moment later.

Dragon Goddess Yeyou frowned and said, “Stop inquiring. It’s none of your business, anyway.”

“How can you say that to your twin brother? What if the two of you produce another Dragon God’s descendant? We may have split, but we’re both still considered ‘Dragon God’.”

Dragon Goddess Yeyou sneered, “Even if we do, it’s still none of your business.”

The Dragon God wore a shocked look on his face before asking in a trembling voice, “D-don’t tell me… you already have a child?”

Dragon Goddess Yeyou’s body trembled in anger. “You’re lucky I don’t have a physical body to beat you up right now…” she growled.

“…”

The Dragon God stopped joking and became serious.

“Speaking of your condition, what happened?”

Dragon Goddess Yeyou ignored him and looked at Yuan instead.

“Do you remember your last moments—how you died?” she asked.

Yuan closed his eyes and responded, “Not everything. It’s still a little fuzzy, but I can make out the silhouette of several inhuman figures, and I was engaged in a fight with them.”

“Then forget it. We can talk about this once you remember.”

“You can’t just tell me?”

“If you want to risk your soul shattering, I don’t mind telling you.”

Yuan’s eyes widened at her words.

“Why would telling me have such a dangerous risk?”

“There exists forbidden knowledge which could trigger something akin to a curse or heavenly law if you reveal them to someone who doesn’t have the authority or capability to hear it,” she explained.

“It’s like teaching a mortal an immortal technique so profound that it overwhelms and collapses their mind.”

“I see…” Yuan muttered with a look of understanding. “Well, I’ll eventually remember.”

“By the way, Yeyou, what are you doing inside this Soul Weapon?” he asked her next. “And do you know what happened to the others?”

“We were with you when you died. Some of us perished alongside you, but some survived. I barely survived with my soul. After escaping, I met with the Dragon Ancestor, who placed me inside this unoccupied Soul Weapon.”

Yuan clenched his fists after hearing that some of the Nine Divine Supremes had perished. “Do you know who died and who survived?” he asked.

Dragon Goddess Yeyou pondered for a moment before responding, “As far as I know, Crimson Terror, Heavenly White Tiger God, Divine Yin Yang Crane, and the Shadow Dweller all perished. Right, that dumb pigeon, Feng Yuming, also died.”

Yuan’s heart felt a squeezing pain after hearing that five of the Nine Divine Supremes had perished.

“Although Feng Yuming died, she’ll eventually reincarnate. As for the others… I’m not so sure. It might be possible if their souls weren’t destroyed during that battle. As for the Shadow Dweller… their race is a little unique, so I don’t know what happened to her.” freёwebnovel.com

“About Xingrui… I know that she is currently drifting somewhere out there in the starry sky, probably starving and consuming stars like before… I need to find her as soon as possible.” Yuan revealed what he knew about the Eater of Star’s situation.

“If you can restore my body, I can look for her,” Dragon Goddess Yeyou said.

Yuan turned to look at the Dragon God and spoke, “You said you can help her restore her body? How?”

“I can create another body from scratch for her, but that will take time, especially if she wants it to be as strong as before,” he said.

“How long, exactly?”

“Hard to say, but it’ll definitely take at least a hundred million years.”

“…”

Yuan fell silent. There was no way he could wait that long.

“Do you have another idea?” Yuan looked at Dragon Goddess Yeyou.

“If you bring me back to my world, I will be able to create another body using the resources there, and it won’t take more than a few years,” she said.

“Where is your world? And why didn’t you ask the Dragon Ancestor to bring you there before?”

“Because he cannot access it. Only you and I have access. However, I am unable to access it in my current condition.”

“Are you sure I’ll be able to access it? I have been reincarnated.”

“I’m sure. The key to my world is the Nine Dragon Symbols within your body. You have it, no?”

Yuan’s eyes widened at her words and muttered, “So you’re the source of the Nine Dragon Symbols?”

Dragon Goddess Yeyou suddenly frowned and angrily said, “You don’t remember? So you have no idea what the Nine Dragon Symbols represent?”

“Sorry… I am still missing bits and pieces of my memories.”

“Unbelievable! Truly unbelievable!” After shouting bewilderedly, Dragon Goddess Yeyou abruptly returned to the Dragon God’s Vengeance.

“W-wait! What about your world? Where can I find it? I don’t remember!” Yuan hastily asked.

“…”

No response. Dragon Goddess Yeyou has returned to ignoring him.

“She’s a handful, am I right? She was like that when we were a single being. I couldn’t handle it, so I suggested we have our own bodies.” The Dragon God chuckled.

Yuan merely showed a bittersweet smile. He didn’t dare to respond to the Dragon God, fearing it’d anger Dragon Goddess Yeyou even more.

“Anyway, it’s about time I leave,” Yuan said a moment later.

“What about your match with my eldest daughter?” he inquired with raised eyebrows.

“I’ll return for it once I deal with my business,” Yuan said.

“Make sure you do. I don’t want to see her going on a rampage when you don’t show up,” he said with a stiff smile.

chapters.title: Chapter 1707 Returning To The Eternal Phoenix Domain
chapters.body:
Chapter 1707 Returning To The Eternal Phoenix Domain

Chapter 1707 Returning to the Eternal Phoenix Domain

“Can you bring me back to the Eternal Phoenix Domain now?” Yuan looked at the Primal Monarch.

“Eternal Phoenix Domain? How are you related to those annoying birds?” Dragon God inquired with a raised eyebrow.

“My companion is waiting for me there,” he said.

“Does it have something to do with your wish?”

“Yes. I need the treasure to get rid of her curse.”

“Ah. Can you bring me back to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary first? I also have a friend waiting for me there.” Yuan quickly said, nearly forgetting about Yingzi.

The Dragon God nodded. While he had problems with the Eternal Phoenix Domain and Primordial Phoenix, he wasn’t unreasonable enough to cause trouble for someone purely because they had connections to the phoenixes there.

Yuan returned to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary shortly after. He was teleported directly to Yingzi’s location.

“Congratulations on winning the Dragon God Festival,” Yingzi said to him.

“Thank you.” freewebnøvel.com

“Look! It’s the Primal Monarch’s descendant!”

The nearby beings quickly noticed him and began gathering around him.

“We’re going to return now,” Yuan said to Yingzi.

A rift appeared before them the next moment. Yuan and Yingzi stepped inside it, disappearing from the Divine Dragon Sanctuary. The others tried to follow, but the rift closed before anyone else could enter it.

Meanwhile, when Yuan emerged from the other side, he was already inside the Primordial Phoenix’s world. The place was initially empty, but a bewildered voice resounded shortly after they appeared, “Seriously? You’re just going to waltz into my personal world like that?”

The Primordial Phoenix appeared before them the next moment with a displeased expression on her face.

“Uhh… That old booger sent me here when I told him I wanted to return. I never specified where, too.” Yuan immediately expressed his innocence.

However, the Primordial Phoenix was silent, and she was staring at him with a somewhat dumbfounded expression.

“You… what have you been doing for the last two years?” The Primordial Phoenix asked, as she was greatly shocked by how much stronger he’d gotten in such a short time.

‘He’s essentially a different person! And he reeks of dragon’s scent even while in his human form!’

“A lot has happened, but I will not get into it. Here, this is what you asked for.” Yuan retrieved the Sacred Chalice of Purification and showed it to her.

“Wow, you actually managed to acquire it? How did you do it?”

“Do you know of the Dragon God Festival?”

“I have heard about it. Something about the winner will have their wish granted by the Dragon God.”

“Well, I won it and wished for the Sacred Chalice of Purification.” He gave her a brief explanation of how he acquired the treasure.

“Is that so…”

The Primordial Phoenix wasn’t familiar with the specifics of the Dragon God Festival, but she had heard of its notorious difficulty.

‘I heard that almost any wish could be granted. To think he’d use such a precious opportunity just to save my daughter…’

Primordial Phoenix looked at Yuan in a different light.

She accepted the Sacred Chalice of Purification and said, “Give me a week—a month at most to remove the curse.”

Yuan nodded and said, “I’ll return to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary in the meantime. I still have some business left there. I’ll be back after a week or so.”

“Do whatever you want.”

Yuan returned to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary shortly after and waited for his appointment with Long Wu Qing to arrive.

As the days passed, beings from all over the Divine Dragon Sanctuary gathered around the Endless Arena to witness Yuan’s fight with Long Wu Qing.

The Dragon God even decided to broadcast it throughout the territory.

“Who do you think will win?”

“Although the human has won the Dragon God Festival, he has almost no chance to defeat the Unrivaled Tyrant in an actual fight.”

“The Unrivaled Tyrant is too overpowered. There’s no way she’ll lose to a human.”

“I don’t know about that… If you watched how he dealt with Hellfire Dragon, you wouldn’t say that with such confidence.”

“Even a hundred Hellfire Dragons cannot compare to a single Unrivaled Tyrant.”

Time passed swiftly, and the moment for Yuan and Long Wu Qing’s highly anticipated match had finally arrived.

Long Wu Qing arrived in her petite human form—the one she saved Yuan in.

“Have you recovered?” Yuan asked her.

“Yes, I have made a complete recovery,” she calmly nodded.

“So, any rules before we begin?” he continued.

“Since this is only a spar, I don’t think we should seriously aim for each other’s throat. Of course, accidents could happen, but that’s different from purposefully aiming for the heart.”

Yuan nodded, “I agree. I don’t wish to turn this into a deathmatch.”

“Next, I will lower my cultivation to match yours.”

Long Wu Qing lowered her cultivation to the Perfect Chaotic Master.

“Do you have anything to add to the rules?” she asked.

Yuan pondered for a moment before speaking, “Let’s set the time limit to seven days. Also, to make this more interesting, we’ll each have three lives.”

“How will that work?” she raised an eyebrow.

“While I won’t die even if you destroy my heart, we’ll still consider that as a death,” he explained.

“I see. I can do that,” Long Wu Qing responded confidently. In an instant, she summoned three thin barriers around her body, each one perfectly conforming to her figure, outlining her form with precision.

“Each barrier will represent one life. If you manage to destroy all three at once, I’ll consider it my loss,” she said.

“Whenever you’re ready.” Long Wu Qing said a moment later.

Yuan retrieved Number One Under Heaven.

“Ready.”

The instant Yuan uttered those words, Long Wu Qing opened her mouth and released a massive Dragon’s Breath that completely engulfed Yuan’s figure. The output and strength of her Dragon’s Breath were at least ten times stronger than the one Long Yejun released while sparring with Yuan with his suppressed cultivation.

“…”

“Guess I wasn’t completely ready,” Yuan said after escaping the Dragon’s Breath—but not before dying once to it.

chapters.title: Chapter 1708 Fighting Long Wu Qing
chapters.body:
Chapter 1708 Fighting Long Wu Qing

Chapter 1708 Fighting Long Wu Qing

“Hey, stop messing around and fight me seriously. You don’t have the leisure to hold back against me. Just come at me with your full strength.” Long Wu Qing said to him with a disappointed frown on her face.

This was the first time she faced an opponent who didn’t immediately go all out against her. It was a weird feeling being on the receiving end since she would often be the one to hold back.

“Sorry, it’s a bad habit of mine to slowly ramp things up as I get used to my opponent’s strength.” Yuan apologized.

He was used to slowly increasing his strength to match his opponent so he wouldn’t end the fight too quickly. However, Long Wu Qing was an opponent who could easily fight someone several realms above her own strength, just like Yuan.

“That said… I can also say the same for you.” Yuan said, and his aura suddenly skyrocketed.

He entered his dragon transformation and abruptly unleashed Heaven Splitting Sword Strike on Long Wu Qing, instantly engulfing her body.

“…”

However, Long Wu Qing didn’t try to escape from his strike and stood still until the very end. Once Heaven Splitting Sword Strike ended, Yuan could see that one of the three barriers around Long Wu Qing was heavily cracked.

“Hm. You’re right. I should probably heed my own advice. As an apology, I’ll give you one of my lives.” Long Wu Qing swung her arm, destroying the cracked barrier.

“You really didn’t need to do that.”

“Well, it’s done now, so there’s no point lingering on it.” Long Wu Qing’s aura suddenly intensified and began surging toward the clouds.

Not only did she release her full presence, but she also transformed, causing iridescent, rainbow-colored scales to appear across her body, shimmering with powerful and otherworldly energy.

“Allow me to introduce myself again. I am Long Wu Qing, a Sublime Elemental Dragon, the eldest of the Dragon God’s descendants.”

“I am Yuan, a human.”

After their greeting, Yuan and Long Wu Qing clashed with each other, their collusions sending powerful shockwaves in every direction.

If the Dragon God hadn’t covered the Endless Arena with a barrier to prevent their destruction from spreading too far, the nearby beings would’ve been affected by the shockwaves of their energy clashing.

Although their cultivation was only at the Perfect Chaotic Master, their prowess far exceeded their cultivation by several realms. In reality, they were displaying the strength of a Perfect Chaotic King, a power comparable to the Divine Ancestor Realm in human cultivation.

[Nine Supreme Swords!]

“Nine Dragons Purgatory!”

[Immortal Rending Sword Strike!]

“Tyrant’s Grand Sunder!”

Yuan and Long Wu Qing continued exchanging techniques for several hours without pause, each move met with equal skill and power, with neither gaining an advantage over the other.

The onlookers were absolutely gobsmacked by the spectacle. None of them had ever seen Long Wu Qing locked in a stalemate before, much less struggling to overpower her opponent.

However, Yuan and Long Wu Qing suddenly stopped moving after half a day of fighting.

“Hey, what do you think?” Long Wu Qing asked him with a suggestive smile on her face.

“I think neither of us will be able to truly go all out because we don’t want to kill each other.”

“That’s obvious. I’m asking what you think about tossing that rule out of the window and we fight each other to the death—without actually fighting to the death?”

“I don’t mind,” he said.

“Then…”

Long Wu Qing removed the barriers around her and transformed again, entering her true dragon form—an iridescent-colored dragon with six beautiful wings on her back.

To match her increased size, Yuan activated God of War’s Astral Arts—enhancing its prowess with chaotic essence.

“This is more like it! Hahaha!” Long Wu Qing laughed out loud as they resumed their fight.

However, Yuan was not done yet, and he retrieved Dragon God’s Vengeance, wielding it in his left hand while using Number One Under Heaven in his right.

Long Wu Qing narrowed her eyes at the Dragon God’s Vengeance, her instincts sensing something unpleasant about it.

Yuan’s eyes flickered viciously, and he suddenly lunged the Dragon God’s Vengeance at Long Wu Qing.

Long Wu Qing’s instincts warned her not to touch the spear, so she avoided it.

Not giving her any time to breathe, Yuan attacked with Number One Under Heaven next.

Long Wu Qing used her claws to block the strike, but even with her durable scales, Yuan was able to leave behind somewhat deep scratches on them. Unfortunately, that was the extent of the damage he could inflict on her, even with Enhanced Sword Aura.

As the fight continued, Long Wu Qing began pushing Yuan back slowly but surely. If he hadn’t received a boost in power from the Dragon God Festival, he wouldn’t have been able to last more than ten minutes against Long Wu Qing.

Suddenly, Yuan’s eyes flickered.

[Dragon’s Gaze!]

However, he didn’t stop there and also used Immortal Monarch’s Dominion at the same time.

“Halt!”

Although Yuan’s efforts didn’t stop Long Wu Qing for even a second, they weren’t in vain. He managed to make her flinch, distracting her for a split second. In that brief moment, he seized the opportunity and controlled the Dragon God’s Vengeance to strike her from behind.

The Dragon God’s Vengeance barely penetrated her scales and drew a drop of blood from her.

<Dragon God’s Suppression has activated!>

<Target ‘Long Wu Qing’ has been sealed!>

Long Wu Qing instantly felt something had changed within her, but she wasn’t sure what.

“What did you do just now?” she inquired.

“It’s nothing much. I just sealed your bloodline,” he answered truthfully.

“Oh, I see.” Long Wu Qing’s bland response caused Yuan to raise an eyebrow.

“That’s all you have to say?”

Long Wu Qing chuckled and said, “Do you even know anything about my bloodline? It only helps my training, allowing me to master all elements with ease. Even if you seal it, it won’t have any effect on my ability to fight, as my prowess does not come from my bloodline.”

“Seriously…?” Yuan was dumbfounded by this revelation.

chapters.title: Chapter 1709 True Dragon Awakening
chapters.body:
Chapter 1709 True Dragon Awakening

Chapter 1709 True Dragon Awakening

“Honestly, even though I’ve suppressed my cultivation to match your cultivation, I still have a major advantage against you. Unlike others, I can only weaken my physique so much, not to mention the vast amount of chaotic essence within my body. Suppressing my cultivation doesn’t make my chaotic essence magically disappear, either.” Long Wu Qing said.

However, Yuan smiled and said, “To be honest, I am still not going all out. It’s hard to get rid of habits, unfortunately.”

As he finished his sentence, immense killing intent began oozing from Yuan’s body, forming two beautiful red and black wings on his back.

Although releasing his killing intent didn’t directly enhance his power, it put him in the right mindset to fight seriously.

“My, my. That’s some impressive bloodlust. How many people did you have to kill to build up such intense killing intent?” Long Wu Qing showed a somewhat nervous smile after sensing Yuan’s killing intent. Despite her experience, she’d never felt such intense killing intent before.

“Who knows? Do you count every single ant you accidentally stepped on?”

Not only did Yuan’s aura shift, but his entire demeanor transformed as well, becoming cold and emotionless, almost resembling that of a ruthless, crazed killer.

“Is that the real you?” Long Wu Qing asked out of curiosity.

“It’s one of me.”

“No matter how much killing intent you show off, it won’t change the outcome,” she said.

A cold smile crept across Yuan’s face as a mask resembling a dragon materialized, similar to the one Long Yejun had worn during their battle. Its fierce design added an even more intimidating edge to his presence.

[True Dragon Awakening!]

Yuan’s presence suddenly became several folds stronger.

Sha!

A powerful black-colored sword light flashed when Yuan suddenly swung Number One Under Heaven.

The sword light struck Long Wu Qing’s leg, cutting through her scales and skin.

“?!?!”

Long Wu Qing’s eyes widened with surprise.

“To think you were still hiding this much power… You sly little bastard,” she muttered in a cold voice.

“The true fight starts here, Wu Qing!” Yuan shouted as he lunged at her.

“Is that so?!”

Long Wu Qing’s aura increased again right before they clashed. Just like Yuan, she hadn’t gone all out until now. However, even when using all of her strength, Long Wu Qing quickly realized that she was still falling behind Yuan, and it was not by a small margin.

‘I am losing?! This can’t be happening!’ Long Wu Qing clenched her jaws in frustration.

Other than the Dragon God, she had never lost to anyone, always dominating her opponents with overwhelming strength. Yet, she now found herself being pushed back by a mere human, something she had never thought was even possible.

[Asura’s Blade of Execution!]

Yuan used his chaotic essence to enhance the technique, causing it to emit a vibrant purple aura that radiated with unfathomably destructive energy.

Long Wu Qing sensed her life being threatened for the first time in a long time when she saw the ominous-looking blade in the sky. However, her pride did not allow her to avoid this attack.

Thus, she confronted it with Dragon’s Breath.

The Asura’s Blade of Execution flew toward Long Wu Qing with terrifying speed, effortlessly slicing through her Dragon’s Breath as it made its way toward her.

Upon realizing she couldn’t stop the attack, Long Wu Qing was forced to toss aside her pride and attempt to dodge the blade. However, it was a little too late. A massive wound opened up near her chest, but no blood emerged. With full control over her body, Long Wu Qing used her chaotic essence to prevent any blood from leaking.

‘What is this feeling? Even though I am losing and getting hurt, it doesn’t feel that bad…’ Long Wu Qing thought to herself as she quickly healed her wound.

“Still want to continue?” Yuan asked her.

“Are you seriously asking me that?” Once her wound healed, Long Wu Qing charged at Yuan again.

They continued fighting nonstop for the next several days. Every time Long Wu Qing suffered damage, the strange sensation within her grew stronger, becoming more pronounced with each blow she endured.

“I can’t believe it… The Unrivaled Tyrant is actually being tossed around by a human…”

“Is it just me, or does she appear to be enjoying it?”

“How is that possible? There’s no way she’d enjoy losing when she’s always been the one bullying others. She must be feeling humiliated.”

“Is that so…?”

Although a few onlookers pointed it out, nobody wanted to believe that Long Wu Qing was enjoying her situation. After all, she was the Unrivaled Tyrant—the uncontrollable monster who has terrorized the Divine Dragon Sanctuary for billions of years.

“More! Harder! I can’t get enough of this feeling!” Long Wu Qing could no longer control herself and shouted loudly.

“Huh?” The onlookers stared at her with bewildered looks.

“Who knew getting hurt would feel so good?! Hurt me some more!” Long Wu Qing exclaimed, her expression a mixture of pleasure and pain.

“…”

Yuan was taken aback by Long Wu Qing’s sudden shift in behavior, which messed with his momentum greatly.

“Although I’ve felt pain before, the pain you inflict is different!” Long Wu Qing continued. “I can feel it down to my very bones every time your sword cuts my flesh!”

Long Wu Qing’s body could be seen visibly trembling.

“More! More! More!” she shouted, chasing after Yuan, seemingly having lost all desire to continue the fight, driven by an insatiable urge instead.

Yuan also lost his desire to continue fighting after seeing Long Wu Qing in this state. He withdrew his weapons and undid his transformation.

“Let’s end it here,” he sighed.

“What? No! Let’s fight a little longer!” she exclaimed, sounding like a spoiled child who didn’t want to leave the amusement park to go home yet.

Yuan rubbed his eyes and said, “We’ve been fighting for nine days, so we already went over the time limit by two whole days. If you want to continue, you can find somebody else. I have other business to attend to.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1710 Long Wu Qing’S Mark
chapters.body:
Chapter 1710 Long Wu Qing’S Mark

Chapter 1710 Long Wu Qing’s Mark

“No. The others aren’t capable of injuring me even if I matched their cultivation, and I am not the type to purposefully let someone beat me.” Long Wu Qing said, responding to his suggestion.

“Come on, let’s fight for another week—even another day will be enough!”

Yuan shook his head and said, “I have already lost all desire to fight. If you want to fight, we can do it another time.”

“You promise?” Yuan nodded, but he wasn’t sure he would be able to fulfill this promise anytime soon. After all, he will be returning to the Nine Heavens soon, and it might be a really long time before he visits the Primal Expanse again. Moreover, there was the possibility that he might never return.

However, he had no intention of letting Long Wu Qing know of this.

‘She’s someone who’s lived for billions of years. I’m sure a few thousand—or even a few million years mean nothing to her.’ Yuan thought.

As if to acknowledge their promise, Long Wu Qing returned to her human form and stopped suppressing her cultivation.

Right after she entered her human form, Long Wu Qing approached Yuan and abruptly grasped his neck with her hand.

“What are you—”

Just as Yuan opened his mouth to speak, Long Wu Qing suddenly pulled him toward her, pressing her lips firmly against his in a bold, unexpected move.

“???”

Yuan didn’t even bother trying to escape and accepted his fate because he knew that he wouldn’t be able to escape Long Wu Qing with her full cultivation even if he had access to Heaven’s Supremacy.

<You have been marked by Long Wu Qing>

‘Marked? What is this?’ Yuan wondered inwardly after seeing this notification.

“I’ve just placed a mark on you, so I will know where you are at all times,” she said after releasing him.

“Why do you want to know where I am at all times?” he asked with raised eyebrows.

“Just in case,” she calmly said.

“In case of what?”

“Who knows,” she shrugged.

“But why a kiss?”

“I thought humans liked that sort of thing.”

“…”

“Don’t think too much about it. The mark won’t harm you or affect you in any way,” she reassured him.

“That’s not why… forget it.”

Yuan realized it was pointless to argue with Long Wu Qing.

“Anyway, I am going to leave now.”

“Where are you going?” she asked out of curiosity.

Yuan didn’t immediately respond as he pondered whether it would be a good idea to tell someone eccentric like Long Wu Qing. He didn’t mind telling the Dragon God because he was confident the Dragon God wouldn’t do anything foolish.

Long Wu Qing, on the other hand, was almost impossible to predict.

Seeing that Yuan remained silent, Long Wu Qing smiled and said, “Are you going to meet with a phoenix?”

Yuan raised an eyebrow and asked, “How can you tell?”

“Before we fought, I noticed the lingering scent of a phoenix from you,” she explained.

After all, Yuan was just speaking with the Primordial Phoenix before he returned to the Divine Dragon Sanctuary.

“I see… Yes, I am going to the Eternal Phoenix Domain,” he admitted, realizing there was no point in trying to hide it any longer.

“You hesitated to tell me at first because what? You thought I would do something to them?”

“No, it’s because I didn’t know what you would do. That is my reason.”

“Makes sense, and I don’t blame you,” she said and continued, “While I have nothing against phoenixes, they definitely hate me. They would attack me on sight, regardless if I provoked them or not.”

“Hm? Why?” Yuan asked with a curious face.

“Because I killed a phoenix whose status is akin to one of the Dragon God’s descendants over there. She was the Primordial Phoenix’s eldest child, who is also a Primordial Phoenix.”

“What?” Yuan’s eyes widened with shock after learning this unexpected information.

He could’ve never expected that it was Long Wu Qing who caused Feng Yuxiang’s death when she was still living in the Primal Expanse.

“She was one of the strongest opponents I’ve ever had. It happened very long ago, and I almost lost to her back then.” Long Wu Qing sighed as she recalled the fight in her mind.

“What led to your fighting?” Yuan asked.

Long Wu Qing had to ponder for a moment before she could respond, “Honestly, I don’t remember, but it was probably over something silly.”

“Is that so…”

Despite the fact that Long Wu Qing had killed Feng Yuxiang in the past, Yuan felt no anger toward her, nor did he hold her accountable. After all, he hadn’t been there when it happened, and for all he knew, it could have been Feng Yuxiang’s fault.

Phoenixes are naturally arrogant creatures and are often the aggressors. This was a fact that Yuan couldn’t deny, as he’d also experienced their troublesome behavior firsthand as Tian Yi.

Sometime later, Yuan bid Long Wu Qing farewell before returning to the Eternal Phoenix Domain with Yingzi.

“If you don’t come find me soon, I’ll come find you.” Long Wu Qing warned him as he stepped into the rift

When Yuan returned to the Eternal Phoenix Domain, he waited for the Primordial Phoenix to show up.

Three days later, another rift opened before Yuan.

“Enter.” The Primordial Phoenix’s voice resounded in his head.

Yuan nodded and stepped into the rift, his heart pounding with excitement, as it has been a long time since he last saw or heard Feng Yuxiang’s voice.

Upon arriving in the Primordial Phoenix’s world, the first thing Yuan noticed was the stunning woman standing beside the Primordial Phoenix. She had a tall, slender figure, silky red hair that flowed like flames, and glistening golden eyes that radiated a powerful yet graceful presence.

However, she also appeared confused and slightly overwhelmed, like a patient who had just woken up from a years-long coma.

Yuan’s expression lit up after seeing her familiar face, and with a delighted smile, he called out, “Feng Feng!”

Hearing his voice, Feng Yuxiang swiftly turned to look at him. Her confused look instantly vanished after seeing his handsome face, and her expression became filled with relief and excitement.

“Young Master~!” she cried as she flew at him with her arms spread wide.

Long Wu Qing had to ponder for a moment before she could respond, “Honestly, I don’t remember, but it was probably over something silly.”

“Is that so…”

Despite the fact that Long Wu Qing had killed Feng Yuxiang in the past, Yuan felt no anger toward her, nor did he hold her accountable. After all, he hadn’t been there when it happened, and for all he knew, it could have been Feng Yuxiang’s fault.

Phoenixes are naturally arrogant creatures and are often the aggressors. This was a fact that Yuan couldn’t deny, as he’d also experienced their troublesome behavior firsthand as Tian Yi.

Sometime later, Yuan bid Long Wu Qing farewell before returning to the Eternal Phoenix Domain with Yingzi.

“If you don’t come find me soon, I’ll come find you.” Long Wu Qing warned him as he stepped into the rift

When Yuan returned to the Eternal Phoenix Domain, he waited for the Primordial Phoenix to show up.

Three days later, another rift opened before Yuan.

“Enter.” The Primordial Phoenix’s voice resounded in his head.

Yuan nodded and stepped into the rift, his heart pounding with excitement, as it has been a long time since he last saw or heard Feng Yuxiang’s voice.

Upon arriving in the Primordial Phoenix’s world, the first thing Yuan noticed was the stunning woman standing beside the Primordial Phoenix. She had a tall, slender figure, silky red hair that flowed like flames, and glistening golden eyes that radiated a powerful yet graceful presence.

However, she also appeared confused and slightly overwhelmed, like a patient who had just woken up from a years-long coma.

Yuan’s expression lit up after seeing her familiar face, and with a delighted smile, he called out, “Feng Feng!”

Hearing his voice, Feng Yuxiang swiftly turned to look at him. Her confused look instantly vanished after seeing his handsome face, and her expression became filled with relief and excitement.

“Young Master~!” she cried as she flew at him with her arms spread wide.

chapters.title: Chapter 1711 Feng Yuxiang'S Return
chapters.body:
Chapter 1711 Feng Yuxiang'S Return

“Waaaah! Young Master!” Feng Yuxiang cried her eyes out as she embraced Yuan. “I thought I’d never see you again!”

The moment her life flashed before her eyes during the heavenly tribulation was still fresh to her, almost as if it’d just happened.

Yuan patted her head and said, “I’ve missed you.”

He continued soothing her until Feng Yuxiang finally calmed down.

“…”

The Primordial Phoenix watched them in silence. She’d also tried calming Feng Yuxiang after her revival but was unsuccessful. Feng Yuxiang only grew more confused and anxious as time passed, so the Primordial Phoenix gave up and decided to let Yuan see her. It was also a test of sorts for Yuan. She wanted to see how Feng Yuxiang reacted to his presence.

As for the results, the Primordial Phoenix wasn’t just surprised. She was absolutely gobsmacked by how different Feng Yuxiang had become—at least compared to the haughty daughter in her memories.

“Young Master, does this mean I am still alive? I thought I woke up in the underworld,” Feng Yuxiang said after calming down.

“Yes, you’re still alive. It’s going to take some time, but I’ll explain the whole situation to you.”

Thus, Yuan began explaining to Feng Yuxiang about the Primal Expanse and how he’d got there.

Once Feng Yuxiang understood the existence of the Primal Expanse, Yuan proceeded to explain the Nine Heavens’ connection to the Primal Expanse and how some of the beasts from the Nine Heavens originated from the Primal Expanse.

Next, he spoke of the Eternal Phoenix Domain and how the Primordial Phoenix before them was her mother.

“My… mother?” Feng Yuxiang looked at the Primordial Phoenix with wide eyes. She’d always thought she came into existence naturally, so the sudden appearance of her mother came as a great shock to her.

“So you don’t have your memories…? When did your last rebirth occur—when were you born?” The Primordial Phoenix asked.

“A little over a hundred thousand years ago, I suppose.”

“So recently…” Primordial Phoenix sighed. Most phoenixes who have the ability to reincarnate tend to regain all of their memories after they reach a million years old when they become adults, if not immediately after their rebirth.

“Give me some of your blood,” the Primordial Phoenix requested a moment later.

Feng Yuxiang turned to look at Yuan, almost as if asking for his permission or approval.

Yuan nodded with a gentle smile.

Feng Yuxiang proceeded to give several drops of her blood to the Primordial Phoenix, who immediately began examining her blood.

“Although we have lifted your curse, it still has some lingering effects due to how long it affected you. However, it should completely resolve itself soon. When that happens, you’ll regain your strength, and your true bloodline will reveal itself.”

“I see… thank you for removing my curse,” Feng Yuxiang bowed to her respectfully. However, the Primordial Phoenix shook her head and said, “I may have removed your curse, but it was mostly with the assistance of this treasure, which was obtained by your human friend there.”

“Young Master?” Feng Yuxiang looked at him with sparkling eyes.

He smiled and said, “I’ll tell you about it later since it’s a long story.”

She nodded and then looked at the Primordial Phoenix.

“By the way, he’s not a friend. He’s my master, and I am his number one loyal servant.”

“…”

The Primordial Phoenix’s eyebrows began twitching uncontrollably after hearing such words.

“A…a servant…?” she muttered in a trembling voice, almost as if she was trying to suppress her anger.

Ultimately, she failed, and after a moment of silence, the Primordial Phoenix erupted, “Unacceptable! No child of mine will be lowered to the status of a mere servant!”

“That’s not for you to decide.” Feng Yuxiang rebuked.

Yuan then spoke, “Are you sure, Feng Feng? Now that your curse has been lifted, you are essentially free to go—as we had initially agreed.”

“I already told you that I want to follow you even after my curse is lifted! My mind hasn’t changed even in the slightest! I will follow you until the end of time!” Feng Yuxiang spoke in a stubborn voice, her face resolute.

“…” The Primordial Phoenix narrowed her eyes at Feng Yuxiang.

“Whatever. You’ll definitely change your mind once you recover your memories, so it’s only a matter of time anyway,” she said a moment later.

Feng Yuxiang didn’t respond to the Primordial Phoenix’s words. Instead, she looked at Yuan and asked, “By the way, how long have I been asleep?”

“It’s been about three years, I guess.” seaʀᴄh thё NôᴠelFirё.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“THREE YEARS?!” Feng Yuxiang cried out loud. “Where are you currently in the Nine Heavens?”

Knowing how fast Yuan progressed, she wouldn’t be surprised if he’d already reached the Seventh Heaven or above.

“We’re only in the Fourth Heaven. I have spent the last three years in the Primal Expanse. While I could’ve left sooner—two years ago, I couldn’t leave knowing you could be healed here.”

“Young Master…” Feng Yuxiang began tearing up again.

“I’m so sorry! Because of my uselessness, I have become a burden and set you back three whole years! I’ve wasted three years of your precious life!” Yuan wiped the tears off her face and smiled, “That’s nonsense. I’d spend ten years here for you, let alone three. Also, none of my time here was wasted. As a matter of fact, I think I have grown more here than I would’ve in the Nine Heavens.”

“There are many beings here that rival the Great One and the Dragon Ancestor, who are probably only found in the Ninth Heaven, and I am still a long way from reaching them.”

Three years may seem like a lot at a glance, but considering how much Yuan has grown in the Primal Expanse, he’d probably need tens, if not hundreds, of years to achieve the same results in the Nine Heavens.

“If you say so…” Feng Yuxiang nodded, feeling much better knowing she didn’t hinder Yuan’s growth.

“By the way, how’s Xi Meili? Did anything happen to her?” she inquired a moment later.
