chapters.title: Chapter 1712 Sleepy Xiao Hua
chapters.body:
Chapter 1712 Sleepy Xiao Hua

“Don’t worry, nothing happened to Xi Meili. She endured her heavenly tribulation well.” Yuan said.

“So I was the only one who ended up in such a pathetic state, huh…” she sighed.

“Don’t put yourself down so much. Your case is special.”

“By the way, I met with Bai Xutao.” Yuan suddenly said.

“Y-you did? You didn’t kill him, did you…?” she asked in a somewhat nervous tone.

“No, but I did punish him a little. I’ll kill him later—when I visit the Heavenly White Tiger Clan.”

“…”

Feng Yuxiang began pondering in silence. A moment later, she spoke, “Young Master, if it’s not too much to ask, can you let me deal with Bai Xutao? I want to be the one to punish him properly.”

Yuan nodded without hesitation, “If that’s what you want. I won’t touch him then.”

“Thank you, Young Master… By the way, didn’t Xiao Hua come with you to the Primal Expanse? Why is she so silent?” Feng Yuxiang suddenly asked.

“Now that you mention it…” Xiao Hua had been oddly quiet for the past two years.

“Xiao Hua, are you okay?” he asked her.

“…”

No response.

Yuan frowned at this and said, “Give me a moment to check on her.”

He closed his eyes and entered his Dantian through his consciousness. Inside, he could see a small figure hugging her knees and curled up in a ball as she floated around aimlessly.

Yuan approached Xiao Hua’s figure, but he didn’t bother her. ‘She’s asleep… There doesn’t seem to be anything wrong with her, but something feels off.’ Yuan thought to himself.

He continued to examine her condition without waking her up. However, a few minutes later, as if she sensed his presence, Xiao Hua slowly began opening her eyes.

“Brother Yuan…?” she looked at him with a dazed face, seemingly still half-asleep.

“Xiao Hua? Are you okay?”

She gave him a slow and weak nod, “I think so… It’s just that I have been feeling extremely sleepy lately… and I cannot fight it… I don’t know why… I have never felt like this before…”

“Can I check your body?” Yuan asked with a worried face.

“Yes…”

Yuan gently pressed his fingers against Xiao Hua’s wrist and used his spiritual energy to probe inside her body. Everything appeared healthy—she wasn’t injured or ill. However, the flow of her qi was unusually slow, a condition typically seen when someone is in a weakened state, and their body is instinctively trying to conserve energy.

‘Something is forcing her to conserve her qi, but what…?’ Yuan was completely dumbfounded by her condition. He tried looking through the Evil God’s memories since he was related to the Asura Clan. However, just like Tian Yi’s life, there were bits and pieces missing.

‘It doesn’t seem to be hurting her now, but that could change in the future. I’ll need to figure out the cause of her condition as soon as possible.’

“Xiao Hua, Feng Feng’s revival was a success, so you can sleep peacefully and not worry about her.” Yuan caressed her head in a gentle motion.

“Who is… worried… about that… dumb… pigeon…” Xiao Hua closed her eyes and fell asleep again.

“…”

Yuan stared at her for a few more moments before leaving her alone and returning to the outside world.

“Something’s wrong with Xiao Hua,” he explained the situation to Feng Yuxiang.

“What? What happened to her?” “I don’t know, but she’s in a state of spiritual energy conservation. Something is affecting her, but there’s no telling what.”

Yuan turned to look at the Primordial Phoenix and asked her, “Can you take a look at my friend?”

The Primordial Phoenix silently nodded.

“Father, I would also like to borrow your knowledge,” Yuan said out loud.

“Y-your father?!” Feng Yuxiang exclaimed, as she wasn’t aware of the Primal Monarch’s existence yet.

“I’ll explain it to you later,” Yuan said.

The Primal Monarch appeared a moment later, and Feng Yuxiang was shocked by how much he resembled Yuan.

‘No… it’s more accurate to say he’s Young Master when he was just a child!’ she cried inwardly. Sёarch* The Novёlƒire.n(e)t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Yuan retrieved Xiao Hua from his Dantian and carried her in his arms as she slept soundly.

“Hm. I have never seen this condition before. I can sense a few curses on her, but they have no relationship with her condition.” The Primordial Phoenix said.

“By the way, you also had those curses, but they were dispelled alongside your other curse,” she looked at Feng Yuxiang and said.

“You must be talking about the Celestial Emperor’s curse. They prohibit those with it to acknowledge certain information.” To prevent the Nine Heavens from questioning the existence of Players from Earth, the Celestial Emperor had placed a curse on those below the Seventh Heaven.

“Huh? Now that I think about it, I never questioned it when the Young Master disappeared into thin air. Looking back, it felt like I was affected by an illusion.” Feng Yuxiang said.

The Sacred Chalice of Purification not only removed the curse on Feng Yuxiang’s bloodline, but it also lifted the Celestial Emperor’s curse on her, allowing her to be conscious of Players as people not from their world.

‘I wonder if it’ll get rid of the Celestial Emperor’s curse on me…’ Yuan thought to himself. However, he was not in a hurry to remove the Celestial Emperor’s curse, as it could attract the Celestial Emperor’s attention. Moreover, the curse had almost no effect on him due to his inability to breakthrough without advancing his physique first.

Therefore, if he got rid of the Celestial Emperor’s curse now, he’d only attract unwanted attention without any real benefits.

“This human girl… she’s fighting with another soul. To be precise, her soul is fighting against itself.” The Primal Monarch suddenly said.

“What?” Yuan looked at him with a questioning face.

The Primal Monarch continued, “Her soul…contains two separate souls within. It’s almost like the Dragon God, who originally had two souls in one body. In her case, two souls inhabit a single soul and body, and they’re subconsciously clashing with each other for control of the body.”

“Two souls in one…?” Yuan quickly recalled the other personality Xiao Hua had revealed before.

chapters.title: Chapter 1713 Leaving The Primal Expanse
chapters.body:
Chapter 1713 Leaving The Primal Expanse

“Now that you pointed it out… she really has two souls in one, and it’s almost impossible to notice without looking into the depth of her soul. Even I missed it, after all.” The Primordial Phoenix muttered in a slightly surprised voice, as she had never seen such a phenomenon before.

Yuan recalled what Xiao Hua revealed to him and said, “She said there was another personality sealed within her. Can a soul be sealed inside another soul?”

“I don’t know too much about humans, so I cannot tell you with certainty.” Primal Monarch said.

The Primordial Phoenix then said, “This little human… I can sense the power of reincarnation from her, but it’s not the natural kind.”

Yuan looked at her with raised eyebrows and asked, “What does that mean?”

“For us phoenixes, we can reincarnate naturally without any external help, and we’ll always remain as phoenixes no matter how many times we reincarnate. While humans also reincarnate naturally when they die, they will not reincarnate with the same identity, so they won’t have the same bloodline or innate talents. However, this could still be achieved with the help of techniques and treasures.”

“As for this little human… her reincarnation was forced, meaning she forcefully went through the process of reincarnation before her soul reached the River of Rebirth, where all souls must enter before they can be reincarnated.”

“Is that a bad thing?” Yuan asked.

“The process itself may be unnatural, but there’s nothing inherently negative about it. However, the same cannot be said about the techniques or treasures involved with her reincarnation. Such powerful treasures and techniques tend to have negative effects that could affect the individual in the future. Of course, I am not saying that this little human experienced something like that. I am just speaking in general.”

“I see… Then, is there a way to stop them from fighting each other?” The Primal Monarch then spoke, “There are two ways. Since both souls are active, you can seal or completely destroy one of them. If there’s only one soul, there won’t be any fighting.”

“No, that’s out of the question.” Yuan instantly refused that proposal.

“Then you can only separate the two souls and replicate what the Dragon God did.”

“That sounds good. Do you know how I can separate their souls?”

“That is that tricky part. Their souls are so intertwined with each other that it’ll be incredibly difficult to separate them perfectly, and any mistakes could lead to the destruction of one or both souls.”

Yuan frowned. “I can’t accept such risks.”

“I have another option. If you find the source of her reincarnation, you might find some clues. If you cannot figure it out alone, you can bring her back here, and I might be able to do something about it.” The Primordial Phoenix said.

“The source of her reincarnation… the Asura Clan… Primordial Realm…” Yuan muttered to himself. seaʀᴄh thё NôᴠelFirё.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

He already planned on visiting the Primordial Realm, but Xiao Hua’s condition made it a little more urgent.

Yuan placed Xiao Hua back inside his Dantian shortly later.

“Even if I want to bring her back here, it might be difficult for me to return.” Yuan then said. “I came to the Primal Expanse by accident, after all.”

“In that case, I might be able to help,” Yingzi suddenly spoke.

“Really?” Yuan looked at her with intrigue.

“As a Shadow Dweller, I have the ability to ‘mark’ shadows, allowing me to move from any shadow to them no matter the distance. However, I can only have one mark active at a time, and I currently have the Hollowed Expanse marked.”

“I see… but in that case, you’ll need to—”

“Yes, I will need to be with you if I want to bring you back to the Primal Expanse.”

In other words, Yingzi needed to follow Yuan to the Nine Heavens in order to bring him back to the Primal Expanse.

“Are you sure?” Yuan asked her.

She nodded, “I can come back whenever I want, and I want to see the human world. Also, I still have lots to learn about humans.”

“That world lacks chaotic essence, so you won’t be able to cultivate or replenish the energy you exhaust.”

“I don’t mind. It’s not like I am going to stay there forever,” she said.

“Is that so… If that’s your decision, I won’t say anything else.” Yuan nodded.

Without saying another word, Yingzi turned into a black silhouette and entered Yuan’s shadow, temporarily joining his team.

“Now that I have a method of returning to the Primal Expanse, I need to visit the Primordial Realm.” Yuan clenched his fists with determination.

“Are you ready to return to your world?” The Primal Monarch asked him.

Yuan looked at Feng Yuxiang, who turned to look at the Primordial Phoenix.

“Let’s have a proper conversation next time… mother.” Feng Yuxiang said to her.

The Primordial Phoenix calmly nodded, but her eyes flickered with joy.

Feng Yuxiang entered Yuan’s body the next moment.

“I am ready to return now,” Yuan said a moment later.

The Primal Monarch nodded and created a rift.

“Before you leave.” The Primal Monarch stopped him as he took a step forward.

“…”

However, the Primal Monarch didn’t continue speaking, seemingly pondering about something.

“What is it?” Yuan asked.

“Never mind. I will tell you when you return next time. It’s still too early for you to know now,” he eventually said.

Yuan narrowed his eyes suspiciously.

“Alright. Then, until next time, Father.”

Yuan stepped into the rift and disappeared from the Primal Expanse.

“What were you trying to tell him?” The Primordial Phoenix asked afterward.

The Primal Monarch merely gave her a cold glance before disappearing without uttering a word.

“Hmph. Finally, back to his usual self, huh?” The Primordial Phoenix shook her head, not minding the Primal Monarch.

After returning to the Nine Heavens, Yuan found himself in an unfamiliar scenery. However, with the abundance of spiritual energy, he was, without doubt, back in the Nine Heavens.

chapters.title: Chapter 1714 Rashara’S System(End Of Primal Expanse Arc)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1714 Rashara’S System(End Of Primal Expanse Arc)

“Where am I?” Yuan mumbled as he surveyed his surroundings. However, before he could really pay much attention to his surroundings, he noticed several announcements appearing in the sky almost simultaneously.

<Congratulations! Player Yuan has become the first Player to learn a Celestial-rank technique!> <Congratulations! Player Yuan has become the first Player to craft an Empyrean-grade treasure!>

<Congratulations! Player Yuan has become the first Player to become a Divine Blacksmith!>

<Congratulations! Player Yuan has become the first Player to craft a Celestial-grade treasure!>

<Congratulations! Player Yuan has become the first Player to become a True Divine Blacksmith!>

The sky was bombarded with announcements related to the achievements Yuan had achieved in the Primal Expanse, just as Wang Xiuying speculated.

Nearly every Player in the Nine Heavens was staring at the sky in shock at this moment, their eyes and mouths wide with disbelief and awe. Over three years have passed since Yuan disappeared from the Nine Heavens, and the players have gotten used to playing Cultivation Online in peace without any commotions. In fact, except for a few players, the presence of Player Yuan had diminished from their minds, becoming something akin to an urban legend.

Due to his lack of presence, some even wondered if Player Yuan had retired from Cultivation Online.

However, this was just the beginning of the players’ shock. Once they finally got past the name ‘Player Yuan’ and began reading the rest of the announcement, their eyes popped out of their sockets, and their jaws fell to the ground.

“Celestial-rank techniques?!”

“Crafting Celestial-grade treasures?! How in the world?! The top players have only just begun crafting Earth-grade treasures!”

“What the fuck has Player Yuan been up to?! He disappears for three years and comes back with this bombshell?!”

“Wait a second! We’ve only progressed to the Fourth Heaven! How did he even acquire an endgame technique, much less craft them?!”

“Why did these announcements show up almost simultaneously? There’s no way he achieved all of them back-to-back in such a short amount of time.”

The players were filled with questions. Some players were so shocked and distracted by these announcements that they died because they were in the middle of a battle when it happened. However, no matter how much they wanted answers, they realized they might never find out. Cultivation Online was a mysterious game with a ghost publisher. Unlike other games, nobody could get any help from the administration, so they were all on their own.

Some players have tried seeking help from powerful cultivators because they believed the admins were disguised as NPCs. Unfortunately, none of their efforts resulted in anything.

Most players have already accepted the phenomenon behind Cultivation Online and stopped questioning it, just like how the people of Nine Heavens don’t question the existence of players.

However, unbeknownst to the players and even Yuan, the announcements only appeared between the First Heaven and Sixth Heaven, so the people in the Seventh Heaven and above were not aware of the players activity.

That being said, a single being above the Seventh Heaven was aware of their activity.

Inside his private quarters, the Celestial Emperor stared at the recently made announcements with a profound expression on his face, and his brows furrowed in a worried manner.

“Player Yuan… I knew there was something different about you. To think you’d reincarnated in that abandoned world… I would’ve never guessed,” he mumbled to himself.

“…”

After a moment of silence, he shook his head and sighed, “No, this isn’t enough proof. There’s a slim chance this player is not related to him—who am I kidding? Not even the most talented blacksmiths in the Nine Heavens could become a True Divine Blacksmith in such a short time. There’s only one person who could achieve such a feat.”

The Celestial Emperor stood from his throne and walked to one of the open windows, staring at the world beneath him.

“You’ve finally returned, Shura’s Apostle—Heaven Refiner and the calamity who will destroy this world! The others may have failed, but I will stop you once and for all! For this is my duty as the Celestial Emperor!” The Celestial Emperor clenched his fists, his eyes filled with rage and determination.

The Celestial Emperor proceeded to summon the system and configure it.

<Block Player Yuan from accessing the system?>

“Confirm.”

<Player Yuan has been blocked from accessing the system>

“If you cannot access Cultivation Online, you will be stuck in that world and won’t be able to cause any trouble for me.” The Celestial Emperor showed a satisfied smile on his face.

“However, just in case, I should send a few people to make sure he’s gone from this world.”

The Celestial Emperor’s figure disappeared the next moment.

Meanwhile, in the Fourth Heaven, Yuan received several notifications that made him feel several emotions in quick succession.

<You have been blocked from the system>

<You no longer have access to Cultivation Online> Searᴄh the Novёlƒire.n(e)t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

<Logging off…>

Ding!

<The Shura’s System has detected unusual activities>

<Shura’s System has been activated>

<You have been granted access to Shura’s System>

<Shura’s System has overridden Rashara’s System>

<Attempting to log off>

<You are unable to log off>

“??????”

Yuan wore a bewildered expression on his face as he tried to comprehend the situation. “My access to the system has been blocked? And Shura’s System…? Isn’t that…” Yuan swiftly comprehended the situation.

The original system—the one used by every other player was called the Rashara’s System.

However, his access to that system had been blocked.

‘It was probably done by the Celestial Emperor because he saw my announcements…’

Noticing this, the Shura’s System, which was obviously related to Shura, the unknown entity who gave him the power of Heaven’s Supremacy, activated, preventing him from losing access to Cultivation Online.

However…

“Log off!” Yuan tried to log off.

<You are unable to log off>

<Shura’s System does not support such function>

“I can’t log off?! You have to be fucking kidding me!” Yuan cried out loud, his voice echoing in every direction.

chapters.title: Chapter 1715 The Ning Family
chapters.body:
Chapter 1715 The Ning Family

Although Yuan could no longer log off Cultivation Online, he quickly calmed himself down, as there were more important issues at hand.

‘If the Celestial Emperor is aware of my presence, should I just remove his curse?’

He was slightly worried that the Celestial Emperor could track him through the curse. However, he didn’t immediately use the Sacred Chalice of Purification because there was an even higher chance the Celestial Emperor would realize he was still inside Cultivation Online if his curse was broken.

‘No… I have never heard of any curses that can be used as a tracker. However, the caster can, more often than not, feel their curse being destroyed and track it back to where it had been destroyed…’

If he destroyed the Celestial Emperor’s curse, there was a good chance the Celestial Emperor would send someone to check on the location and realize he was still in Cultivation Online.

‘As of this moment, I need to move without alerting the Celestial Emperor—at least until I find and activate the Dragon Spiral Mountain in the Nine Heavens and reconnect it with Earth.’

“Buy first… I need to find Meixiu and the others to let them know of my situation…” Sёarᴄh the ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Yuan scanned his surroundings with divine sense to better understand his location. However, he saw thousands of individuals flying toward him urgently, their faces filled with panic. In fact, tens of thousands of people in guard uniforms were already surrounding him.

“…”

Yuan wore his mask and patiently waited for them to approach him since they didn’t appear to be with the Celestial Emperor, as they lacked the cultivation and atmosphere.

A few moments later, one of the guards, who appeared to be their leader, approached him. Although the guard appeared calm and fierce at a glance, his gaze was filled with worry and fear.

“I am the Royal Guard for the Ning Family, surnamed Sau. May I request for your identity?” the guard requested respectfully.

Although Yuan was unaware, his presence right now was akin to Long Wu Qing—absolutely tyrannical and overwhelming for those around him. In these people’s eyes, Yuan was no different than a monster in human skin.

“Xiao. My surname is Xiao.” Yuan said.

“T-then, Senior Xiao, what brought you to the Ning Family’s household? Are you here for the Ning Family? Do you have an appointment with them?” Guard Sau continued to ask.

The Ning Family was one of the most influential families in the Fourth Heaven. In fact, they were essentially royalty. Yuan had invaded their private space without warning, and his earlier shouting had alerted them. However, the guards didn’t dare to do anything rash because of his presence.

‘While his cultivation only appears to be in the Spirit Sovereign realm, his real cultivation is definitely much higher! He must be one of the envoys from the upper heavens!’ the guard swallowed nervously.

“Ning Family? Sorry, but I didn’t mean to intrude. I came here by accident. Do you know where the Grand Mystic Sect is? I’ll quickly be on my way.”

“Ah, so you’re with the Grand Mystic Sect. No wonder you’re emitting such an overwhelming presence, Senior Xiao!”

The guards there sighed in relief after mistaking Yuan to be with the Grand Mystic Sect.

“My presence?” Yuan muttered.

“Young Master, your presence right now is very overwhelming—strong enough to make those guards faint if they get any closer.” Feng Yuxiang revealed his situation to him.

Hearing this, Yuan looked at the guard in front of him, who appeared to be struggling to stay awake for some reason.

“Ah.”‘

He hastily suppressed his presence.

“I didn’t mean to scare you with my presence. I apologize if I caused any trouble.”

“No, it wasn’t any trouble at all! We were just slightly alarmed by an unknown presence! That’s all, Senior Xiao!” Guard Sau bowed.

“Anyways… about the direction of the Grand Mystic Sect…”

“The Grand Mystic Sect is located on the other side of this world. However, if you’re willing to wait a few days, they will be making their way to this location.” Guard Sau said.

“Hm? Why? What’s happening here?”

“The Ning Family hosts a worldwide tournament every seven years to allow the younger generation to show off their talents. Most sects tend to send their most promising disciples, and the Grand Mystic Sect is one of them. Of course, this tournament is open to individuals, so even those without any affiliation may participate. In fact, we have the highest number of participants this year.”

“I see,” Yuan said.

He pondered inwardly, ‘Meixiu and the others should still be training with Kelan at the Grand Mystic Sect, but there’s a chance they might come here to participate. Waiting a few days won’t hurt. I still need some time to study Shura’s System, too.’

“If Senior Xiao would like, you can stay with the Ning Family in the meantime. We’ll show you to our royal guest room if you allow us.”

Yuan nodded, “Alright. I’ll trouble you for a few days.”

“It’s an honor to serve you!”

Guard Sau led Yuan to their most luxurious guest room, usually reserved for the Sect Leaders of the Three Grand Sects.

“Please make yourself at home for now. We’ll be contacting you again shortly after speaking with His Majesty.”

Yuan nodded and waited in the room while Guard Sau notified the Ning Family of the situation.

Meanwhile, at the Grand Mystic Sect, a group of young men and women gathered in front of Kelan like a group of private soldiers.

“The Ning Family will be hosting one of the biggest tournaments in a few days, where most of the top talents in the Fourth Heaven will participate. I want all of you to participate and show me the results of your training for the last three years. Whoever wins the tournament will receive a special reward. Whoever leaves the tournament first will be punished instead.” Kelan said to Meixiu and the others.

He continued, “And if nobody wins the tournament… I will subject all of you to hell! Understand?!”

“Yes!” They all responded with excitement, their eyes filled with fighting spirit.

Search the NovelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

chapters.title: Chapter 1716 Shura'S System
chapters.body:
Chapter 1716 Shura'S System

“Have you guys received any news from Yuan recently?” Kelan asked the Demon Sealing Faction afterward.

Everyone there turned to look at Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang since they were usually the ones in contact with Yuan.

“No, I haven’t heard from him.” Meixiu shook her head.

“Me neither—at least not after his last return.” S~eaʀᴄh the ηovёlFire .net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Did he say how long the Dragon God Festival would last?” Kelan continued asking.

“No, he didn’t. However, it probably shouldn’t take that much longer—at least considering the type of trials he had to partake in.”

Yuan had mentioned the three trials to her before he returned to participate in the Dragon God Festival.

“I understand. Keep me updated. My superiors have been hounding me lately because of the lack of information. They’re getting impatient, especially Celestial Overlord Xu.”

A few days later, Kelan brought the Demon Sealing Faction to the Ning Family’s location with the Grand Mystic Sect.

Meanwhile, the head of the Ning Family requested an audience with Yuan after Guard Sau explained the situation to him.

“My surname is Xiao.” Yuan greeted a middle-aged man wearing a pair of golden robes as he sat on a majestic throne.

“I understand that you came here by accident, but we are delighted to serve someone from the upper heavens, Senior Xiao. If you need assistance during your stay, don’t hesitate to ask.”

“Thank you. I appreciate your hospitality.”

“If you don’t mind me asking, why did you come down to the Fourth Heaven?”

“Ah, I’m supposed to look for someone, but we don’t really have any information on him, so I am just wandering around for now.”

“I see… What of the Grand Mystic Sect? What’s your relationship with them?”

“I have a few friends there. Since I am here, I might as well visit them. It’s been many years, after all.”

Their conversation didn’t last long, and Yuan returned to his room to study Shura’ System.

“Character status.”

[Heaven Refiner]

[Cultivation: First Level Spirit Sovereign]

[Chaotic Dragon Core: Perfect Chaotic Master]

“…”

“That’s it?”

Yuan was left speechless by how simple his new character status was. Moreover, his name had changed to Heaven Refiner for some reason.

He retrieved a random item from his storage ring and inspected it.

[Poison Sac]

[Description: A rare item dropped by Demonic Spiders. It can be used to enchant weapons or used to create poison pills]

The system was able to analyze items properly, which was good enough for Yuan.

“Poison sac, huh? This was one of the first items I’ve gotten, but I never really found a good use for it. Now that I have upgraded the Starry Abyss to Baneful Abyss, this is essentially useless…”

Despite its uselessness, Yuan was reluctant to throw it away. He stored it back into his spatial ring shortly later and continued experimenting with the new system.

As the tournament drew near, more and more people gathered at the Ning Family’s territory. A few hundred miles from the Ning Family’s household, a colosseum-like structure had been built specifically for the tournament, its grand design capable of holding the immense crowd that had arrived to witness the event.

“Senior Xiao, would you like to watch the tournament with the Ning Family? His Majesty said he’d prepared a seat for you just in case.” Guard Sau asked Yuan the day before the tournament.

Yuan used his divine sense to sweep the crowd. A smile appeared on his face when he saw Meixiu and the others.

“Sure, I’ll watch.” He nodded.

On the day of the tournament, Yuan met up with the Ning Family before they headed to the tournament.

The Ning Family consisted of Emperor Ning, his four wives, and their twenty-one children. Emperor Ning made his family introduce themselves to Yuan. Yuan introduced himself afterward.

“Senior Xiao, why are you wearing a mask?” One of the younger children asked.

“Ah, I don’t have any special reason. I just enjoy wearing masks.” Yuan removed his mask to show them his handsome face. Naturally, it was disguised, as he’d consumed an appearance-altering pill before leaving the room.

“You must be very strong since you’re from the upper heavens. How strong are you, Senior Xiao?” Another asked out of curiosity.

“How strong, huh? Good question. I also don’t know how strong I am right now since I have nothing to properly scale to.”

“Can you destroy stars with your fists?!” Another child asked.

“I think I am still quite far away from accomplishing such feats, unfortunately.”

“Okay, enough bothering Senior Xiao. We need to move out now, or we’ll be late.” Emperor Ning eventually stepped in to stop them.

They were escorted to the tournament area shortly later by a group of powerful guards. Most of them were at the peak of the Spirit Enlightenment, with a few in the Divine Realm.

When they arrived, they sat in the private seating area reserved only for the Ning Family. However, they made an exception for Yuan and allowed him to sit with them.

“…”

The rowdy crowd quickly became quiet when the Ning Family arrived, and almost everyone there had their heads lowered toward their seating area.

Kelan was one of the few individuals who didn’t bow his head, as the Ning Family was no different than an ordinary family in his eyes.

He wasn’t even paying attention to the Ning Family at this moment but to the mysterious masked individual sitting with them.

‘That man… is formidable.’

Despite Yuan’s efforts to suppress his presence, Kelan could instinctively sense that Yuan was a powerhouse.

“Hey, do you know the identity of that masked man with the Ning Family?” Kelan turned to ask Du Daomi, the Sect Leader of the Grand Mystic Sect.

“No, I don’t recognize him. However, to be able to sit with the Ning Family, his background must be extraordinary,” Du Daomi remarked thoughtfully.

“Is that so…” Kelan continued to stare at Yuan.

Noticing his gaze, Yuan confronted Kelan’s gaze in a somewhat provocative manner. ‘Interesting…!’ An excited smile appeared on Kelan’s face.

chapters.title: Chapter 1717 Tournament Of Rising Stars
chapters.body:
Chapter 1717 Tournament Of Rising Stars

Emperor Ning stood up and began his speech shortly after their arrival.

“My Ning Family welcomes you all to this year’s Tournament of Rising Stars, where those below the age of 27 may participate, regardless of your background or affiliation!”

“Before I reveal the reward, allow me to explain the rules for this tournament first.” Searᴄh the NôvelFire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“First and foremost, each of you will be assigned an opponent who matches your age. As the tournament progresses and there are fewer participants, the age disparity will naturally widen.”

“There is a protection formation around this arena, so you do not have to worry about accidentally being killed. However, your opponents may still forfeit. Once they forfeit, you are prohibited from attacking. If you damage your opponent after they forfeit, you will also be disqualified.”

“Martial techniques that directly damage the soul are also strictly forbidden. If caught, you could be punished with death.”

Several rules later, Emperor Ning revealed the rewards.

“The winner of this year’s tournament will be given an Innate Miracle Pill.”

The onlookers and the participants gasped in shock after hearing the reward. An Innate Miracle Pill could boost one’s innate talents greatly and could turn an ordinary cultivator into a talented one. However, they are incredibly rare and priceless due to the exceedingly rare materials required to concoct them.

Afterward, Emperor Ning said, “Before we begin the tournament, I would like to take a moment to introduce an esteemed guest to you all today. This is Senior Xiao, an envoy from the upper heavens.”

“An envoy from the upper heavens…?” Kelan muttered to himself.

“Would you like to share a few words before we begin the tournament, Senior Xiao?” Emperor Ning asked him.

Yuan pondered for a moment before responding with a question, “Do you mind if I make this year’s tournament a little more exciting?”

“Oh? What do you have in mind?” Emperor Ning’s eyes flickered, his interest piqued.

“If you would allow me, I would like to give the top participants an opportunity to fight me. If they can defeat me, I’ll give them a separate reward.”

“T-that’s… but with your cultivation…” Emperor Ning hesitated to let someone as powerful as Yuan participate in a tournament meant for the younger generation.

Yuan chuckled and said, “You probably won’t believe me, but I actually qualify to participate in this tournament.”

“What?! You’re that young?!” Emperor Ning’s eyes widened with shock. He’d sensed Yuan’s presence before, and it was absolutely terrifying. It was not something someone so young should have.

‘For this young man to be this talented… he’s most likely from the Ninth Heaven!’ Emperor Ning swallowed nervously.

“Don’t worry, I won’t go too hard on them, and I’ll put several restrictions upon myself,” Yuan assured him.

Emperor Ning pondered for a moment before nodding, “Alright, let’s do it. It sounds entertaining, too.”

After receiving Emperor Ning’s approval, Yuan stepped forward and spoke in a voice loud enough for everyone to hear. Naturally, he disguised his voice so Kelan and the others couldn’t recognize him.

“During this tournament, any participant in the top 20 may challenge me. My cultivation will be suppressed to the peak of Spirit Grandmaster. In fact, I qualify to participate in this tournament since I am under the age of 27. If you can defeat me… I will give you a Supreme-rank technique or an Empyrean-grade treasure.”

“?!?!?!”

A Supreme-rank technique and Empyrean-grade treasures are typically only acquirable within the Seventh Heaven and above, so for it to be given as a reward in the Fourth Heaven was a once-in-a-life opportunity.

Even Emperor Ning was shocked by the generous reward.

However, despite the immense reward, every participant understood the challenge ahead. Simply making it into the top 20 was already an incredibly difficult journey, but defeating someone capable of casually offering such valuable rewards would be exponentially harder, even if that person suppressed his cultivation to the peak of Spirit Grandmaster.

“Hah! Spirit Grandmaster? Underestimating us will be his downfall. That reward will be mine!” Wang Ming rubbed his hands like he had already won.

Seeing this, Kelan glanced at him and sneered, “You’re the one who’s underestimating him. That man is formidable and unfathomably dangerous. If I were to suppress my cultivation to his level, even I don’t dare say I am confident in beating him.”

“What? Seriously?” The Demon Sealing Faction members looked at him with wide eyes. They couldn’t imagine Kelan losing to anyone—not even to Yuan.

“What about his cultivation of Spirit Sovereign? Is it his real cultivation?” Wang Bingbing asked.

“It appears to be his real cultivation, but I am sensing another power inside him, and it’s unlike anything I’ve seen before. It’s chaotic and destructive,” said Kelan.

“I guess there’s always a bigger fish, especially in the upper heavens. Yuan might finally have some competition later.” Shi Lang chuckled.

“Alright, I have decided.” Kelan suddenly said, “All of you will challenge that man from the upper heavens. If any of you win, I will give you a special reward that would make even my own disciples jealous.”

“What about the tournament?” Hong Xiuquan asked.

“Forget about it. I brought you here to evaluate your progress and limitations, but after seeing the participants, I have no doubt you’ll rise to the top with ease. However, that man is a different story. You’ll be able to truly test your limits with him.”

Sometime later, the tournament officially began.

“I’ll be right back,” Meixiu said shortly after the tournament began.

“Checking on Yuan?” Chu Liuxiang asked.

She nodded, “He appeared in the announcements, so I thought he came back. However, he hasn’t logged off yet, so maybe he’s still in the Primal Expanse.”

“The announcements were probably just delayed because he’s so far away.”

“Probably. It won’t take long.” She logged off the next moment.

Once she returned to Earth and saw Yuan sleeping peacefully, she caressed his face and gave him a gentle kiss before returning to Cultivation Online.

“Not yet?” Chu Liuxiang asked.

“No.” Meixiu shook her head.

chapters.title: Chapter 1718 Tournament Of Rising Stars(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1718 Tournament Of Rising Stars(2)

Once the tournament officially began, a hundred pairs of participants entered their individual platform. Since there were tens of thousands of participants, it’d take weeks if not months to finish the tournament if they did one match at a time.

None of the Demon Sealing Faction members were chosen for the first session, so they merely stood in their area and waited for their turn. A few of them were staring at Yuan, trying to analyze him.

Li Jinxi had the most intense gaze, but it was not because she recognized Yuan.

‘Why am I getting this weird feeling in my gut when looking at that person?’ she wondered inwardly. It was an inexplicable feeling she was not familiar with. She wondered if this feeling was a natural desire to battle someone strong or something else entirely different. Sёarᴄh the Nôvelƒire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Burning Lotus Sword Art!”

A handsome young man with black and red hair unleashed a powerful and dazzling sword technique, instantly catching the spectators’ attention.

“That must be Xing Chen, the number one disciple of the Burning Lotus Sword Sect. His sword art is as grand and powerful as they claim! He definitely deserves the title ‘Sword That Burns All!'”

Several platforms away, a beautiful young lady wielded a green fan as she leaped around the platform with graceful movements, resembling a dancer.

“Fairy Ling is as beautiful as ever! I’d climb a thousand mountains just to have a glance of her beauty!”

Although many participants were disciples of sects, the majority hailed from prominent clans and families that spanned across the Fourth Heaven. While these individuals might also be disciples of various sects, they were here primarily representing their families, promoting the strength and pride of their bloodlines.

The rest of the participants were rogue cultivators, and a sizable portion of them stood out from the rest, mostly because of their demeanor and attitude towards the tournament.

“Damn! This event is too difficult! Who could possibly win this?!” A specific individual shouted after losing to his opponent. There were other players besides the Demon Sealing Faction present at the tournament. Whether they were qualified to even participate or not was a different story. Three years may not seem like a significant amount of time to those born in the cultivation world, but for those from Earth, three years was a substantial period. In that time, the entire Cultivation Online player base made remarkable progress in their cultivation, advancing far beyond what even talented people born in the Nine Heavens could achieve.

While not every player had made significant progress, this was largely because not everyone pursued advancement with the same level of effort or determination. However, those who did dedicate themselves to improving had risen far beyond what individuals of the same caliber, born in the Nine Heavens, could typically achieve.

In other words, even those players with average talents were able to surpass the talented natives of the Nine Heavens, thanks to the system’s assistance. The system provided the players with advantages that allowed for faster growth and cultivation progress in ways that natural talent alone could not.

However, in the eyes of the natives of the Nine Heavens, the players simply appeared to be extraordinary geniuses. Even if some sensed that something was unusual, the Celestial Emperor’s curse worked its magic, making the players’ presence and achievements seem completely normal.

As for the participants present at the tournament, they ranged in age significantly. There were competitors as young as ten years old and others as old as twenty-seven, just barely hitting the upper age limit for participation.

That said, none of the younger participants expected to win. They were only there for the experience and to show off their talents so others could pay attention to them in the future.

This is also why there was typically only a single reward for the tournament, and it was usually not as grand as the Innate Miracle Pill. This made Emperor Ning’s announcement all the more shocking to the participants, as such a valuable prize far exceeded their expectations.

“Winner, Xing Chen!” One of the judges there suddenly announced.

Once a match ended, the winner was granted a recovery pill to help them recover their exhausted cultivation for the next fight, but they wouldn’t fight immediately.

Whenever a stage is available, the next pair would immediately be called to it. Since the platforms were protected by restoration formations, any damage done to them would repair itself.

An hour later, Xi Murong was the first member of the Demon Sealing Faction to be called onto the stage.

“I’ll be right back,” he said to them before walking to the stage in calm steps.

Xi Murong’s opponent had arrived on the stage before him.

‘Seventh level Spirit Emperor, huh?’ Xi Murong smiled inwardly.

“I’ll give you a chance to forfeit.” Xi Murong said to his opponent as he released his cultivation at the third level of Spirit Enlightenment.

In just three short years, Xi Murong had progressed all the way to Spirit Enlightenment. It wasn’t just Xi Murong, however. Every member of the Demon Sealing Faction had managed to enter Spirit Enlightenment. As a matter of fact, it didn’t even take them three years. They’d spent the first two years improving their foundations and the last year increasing their cultivation.

Naturally, this wasn’t solely due to the system. Kelan had also provided them with numerous treasures that, while priceless in the Fourth Heaven, were abundant in his realm. This is one of the many reasons people seek favor with those from the upper heavens, as what is considered trash in the higher realms is often regarded as treasure in the lower heavens.

“S-Spirit Enlightenment?! Who could’ve anticipated such a genius would show up in this tournament!”

“That senior from the upper heavens must be kicking himself now! There’s no way he could bridge the gap between Spirit Grandmaster and Spirit Enlightenment, regardless of where he’s from!”

The spectators were shocked by the presence of a Spirit Enlightenment cultivator since the majority of the other participants were below Spirit King, with a few at Spirit Sovereign.

chapters.title: Chapter 1719 Tournament Of Rising Stars(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1719 Tournament Of Rising Stars(3)

“Spirit Enlightenment, huh? They’re grown.” Yuan muttered with a smile behind his mask. The Demon Sealing Faction had not only caught up to his cultivation but even surpassed him.

Naturally, whether they could hold a candle against him in prowess was an entirely different story.

Xi Murong’s opponent swallowed nervously after realizing he was at Spirit Enlightenment.

However, the young man didn’t forfeit and stepped forward, “Hmph. So what if you’re at Spirit Enlightenment? I’d never forfeit!”

“Is that so?” Xi Murong muttered as he retrieved a Spirit-grade dagger.

One of the rules in the tournament forbade the participants from using treasures above Spirit-grade, as that would defeat the purpose of the tournament. They were supposed to show off their talents, not their wealth, after all.

The only reason there was no restriction on their cultivation was simply because one’s cultivation was considered a talent.

Xi Murong’s opponent retrieved a Spirit-grade sword, and they started fighting shortly after.

The spectators expected Xi Murong to completely dominate his opponent right as the fight began, but he diverted their expectations when he started messing around with his opponent. However, that is not to say he was playing around. Instead, he was purposefully matching his opponent’s speed and strength.

“Who is that young man at the Spirit Enlightenment? I don’t recognize him.” Emperor Ning asked Guard Sau, who was guarding their area with several other powerful guards.

Guard Sau retrieved a jade slip that had the information on all participants and said, “His name is Xi Murong. We don’t have much information on him, but he’s been training with a high-ranking member of the Celestial Overlords at the Grand Mystic Sect for the past few years.”

“What?! Someone from the Celestial Overlords is in the Fourth Heaven?! Are they here right now?!” Emperor Ning stood up in shock after hearing this information.

The Celestial Overlords are bigshots in the upper heavens, much less the Fourth Heaven. Even royalty like Emperor Ning have to lower their head when speaking to them.

“Yes, he came here with the Sect Leader of the Grand Mystic Sect.”

“You idiot! Why didn’t anyone notify me?! We should’ve invited them to sit with us the moment we learned of their presence! Do you have any idea how bad this looks on us?!” Emperor Ning shouted angrily, causing heads to turn in their direction.

“I-I also wasn’t aware until I saw the information just now… I’ll go invite him right now!” Guard Sau lowered his head and left the next moment.

Once Guard Sau left, Emperor Ning looked at Yuan and sighed, “I apologize for that, Senior Xiao. Do you know of the Celestial Overlords?”

“Of course, I do. Who hasn’t heard of them in the upper heavens?” he calmly replied.

“Ah, I just realized how foolish my question was.” Emperor Ning rubbed his chin in an embarrassed manner.

Meanwhile, Guard Sau approached the Sect Leader of the Grand Mystic Sect and asked him, “Senior Du, we’ve just received news that a member of the Celestial Overlords came to the tournament with your Grand Mystic Sect. Do you know where this esteemed guest is right now?”

Without uttering a word, Du Daomi shifted his gaze to look at Kelan, who was standing right beside him. Seeing this, Guard Sau immediately understood the situation and recognized Kelan as someone from the Celestial Overlords. While Kelan’s presence was suppressed, there was no way to conceal the immense aura his body naturally exuded. Even an ignorant child could tell he was a powerful expert at a glance.

“Esteemed Guest from the Celestial Overlords! His Majesty, Emperor Ning, respectfully requested your presence at his humble booth with his family—if you don’t mind!”

Kelan looked at the location where the Ning Family was spectating from, but his gaze wasn’t directed at Emperor Ning. Instead, they were staring at Yuan.

“Sure,” he said with an excited smile.

“Thank you! Please follow me.”

Kelan looked at the others and said, “I’ll be gone for a bit.”

He followed Guard Sau to the Ning Family’s booth.

When Kelan arrived, Emperor Ning got back on his feet and immediately apologized, “I would like to extend my deepest apology for the late invitation, Esteemed Guest. I wasn’t notified of your presence until just now.”

“It’s fine.”

“How should we address you, Esteemed Guest?”

“Kelan.”

“K-Kelan?! Commander Kelan?! The God of Physiques?!” Emperor Ning almost couldn’t believe his ears after hearing Kelan’s name and recognizing it.

“That’s right. There’s no need to act so formally. Although I accepted your invitation, it wasn’t because of the Ning Family,” Kelan said as his gaze shifted to Yuan, who was calmly viewing the matches, completely ignoring Kelan.

‘Not a single reaction even after learning of my identity, huh?’ Kelan smiled inwardly.

Emperor Ning instantly realized what Kelan was trying to tell him and didn’t try to prolong the conversation, fearing it might annoy Kelan.

“I understand. Then I won’t bother you. If you need anything, just let me know.”

Kelan grabbed the chair that had just been placed beside Emperor Ning’s seat and moved it directly beside Yuan.

Kelan sat down with a loud thud, and his aura immediately began clashing with Yuan’s intensely. The air between them grew thick with tension, and the atmosphere in the booth turned chilling. Emperor Ning and his family instinctively moved their seats away from Kelan and Yuan. They feared for their lives, but none of them dared to utter any complaints.

The two of them remained silent for several minutes until Kelan broke the silence.

“So, where are you from?” S~eaʀᴄh the ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“A place far away from here,” Yuan responded nonchalantly.

“Do you really qualify for this tournament?” “Of course. Do you think I am lying?”

Kelan turned his head to look at Yuan.

Yuan did the same. Although his eyes were hidden by his mask, their gaze confronted each other with intensity that matched their auras.

Feeling Yuan’s gaze through his mask, Kelan smiled and said, “You wanna spar later? I’ll match your cultivation.”

“Do you always ask strangers to spar with you?” Yuan asked.

“Only if they make the hair on my skin tingle.” Yuan chuckled, “I’ll think about it.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1720 Even More Monstrous Than Yuan
chapters.body:
Chapter 1720 Even More Monstrous Than Yuan

“What’s there to think about? I can tell you’re someone who craves a good battle just as much as I do. Moreover, your physique…” Kelan’s gaze lowered to look at Yuan’s physique.

‘His clothes and boots are both Celestial-grade treasures. Either his family or sect spoiled him, understandably so. This young man is not even 28 years old, and his aura is already this overwhelming. His cultivation is also deceptively low. There’s no doubt he can fight someone several realms above his own. Yuan may be a monster, but this guy is on a whole different level!’

Kelan compared Yuan to the mysterious monster before him. Although Yuan was wearing clothes that covered most of his skin, Kelan could still tell that he had a powerful physique.

“You’re a body refiner like me, aren’t you?” Kelan asked.

“I do temper my body whenever I get the chance, but I cannot say that I am strictly a body refiner.” Yuan shook his head.

“That’s absolute nonsense. Even gifted and hardcore body refiners in the Ninth Heaven will have difficulties achieving your level at your age.” Kelan did not believe Yuan in the slightest.

“My level? What do you think my level is?”

“I can’t say for certain without exchanging a few punches with you, but it’s definitely as extraordinary as that ominous energy inside your body.”

‘Oh? He can sense my chaotic essence even though I hid it to the best of my abilities?’ Yuan was slightly surprised. Sёarch* The Novёlƒire.n(e)t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Although Kelan could sense something ominous inside Yuan, it was just a feeling, and he had no idea what it was. Yuan’s gaze suddenly turned to look at the members of the Demon Sealing Faction and asked, “Those children are yours?”

Kelan shook his head and said, “No, not really. I am just training them. They’re not really my disciple.”

“They’re not your disciples, but you’re still willing to train them? I didn’t know the Celestial Overlords were this generous.”

“It’s complicated, and I don’t know about the others, but I don’t mind training strangers as long as they show promise and determination.”

“Are you confident they’ll be able to reach the top 20?” Yuan asked.

“Of course. I have confidence that they’ll dominate the whole tournament and reach the top 20—unless they start fighting each other. However, one of them will definitely win the tournament.” Kelan spoke in a proud manner, as if he was speaking of his own children.

“I see. Then how about this? If one of them manages to defeat me, I’ll spar with you.”

Kelan didn’t immediately respond because even he wasn’t confident they could beat him while his cultivation was suppressed to Spirit Grandmaster.

‘No… I need to have some trust in them, especially that little monster who might one day rival Yuan. I don’t believe they won’t be able to defeat a Spirit Grandmaster, even if their opponent is a prodigy from the upper heavens!’ Kelan thought to himself, shaking away his doubt.

“Alright,” he said a moment later.

“Very well.”

Yuan didn’t say anything else and returned to watching the tournament.

“I’ll be back,” Kelan said before flying back to the others directly from the booth.

“What did you talk to him about?” Li Jinxi asked, her face filled with curiosity.

“That man is much more powerful than I’d anticipated, but that doesn’t mean you have no chance of winning. He’ll be suppressed to Spirit Grandmaster. If you guys, who are also able to defeat those above your own realm, cannot defeat someone at his level… Ah, forget it.”

“Anyway, you need to defeat him no matter what, you hear me? Otherwise, I’ll force all of you to endure a whole year of hell that would make even the disciples at my sect wish for death—regardless if Yuan has returned or not.”

The members of the Demon Sealing Faction swallowed nervously upon seeing Kelan’s serious expression. Although Kelan hadn’t mentioned anything about his spar with Yuan, they could instinctively sense that something significant was on the line.

“If you compare him to Yuan, how would you—” Xi Meili suddenly asked.

Kelan interrupted before she could even finish her sentence, “Yuan is very talented—probably one of the most talented individuals I know, but this man is on another level. I don’t want to pat my own butt, but I am very confident in my ability to assess people’s abilities with a glance.”

“How big is their disparity, in your opinion?” Li Jinxi asked.

Kelan pondered for a moment before responding, “If Yuan was at the Spirit Sovereign realm, he’d probably be able to defeat someone at the Divine Emperor realm—even the Divine Sovereign realm with some difficulty. However, that man will be able to defeat someone in the Immortal Ascension realm. The difference between Divine Sovereign, Divine Ancestor, and Immortal Ascension is unfathomably vast.”

“Of course, this is just an estimate since I haven’t fought him. For all we know, he could be weaker—or even stronger.” “Man, it’s really hard to believe that someone out there could outclass Yuan to such an extent. Is this what it feels like to be a frog in a well? I never thought I’d see the day, but alas…” Hong Xiuquan sighed.

“Hey, hey! Why are you suddenly acting like Yuan is no longer relevant? We’re still talking about Yuan in the past! Who knows how much he’s improved in the last three years.” Chu Liuxiang suddenly spoke up with a frown on her face.

“Calm down, Sister Chu. We’re not looking down on Yuan. He’s still a monster. We’re just surprised that someone is even more monstrous than him.” Wu Zao said.

“However, what Sister Chu said has merit. We haven’t seen Yuan in three years, so we cannot compare them in a fair manner. However, we can all agree that the Yuan three years ago would lose to this powerhouse, right?”

“…”

“I’ll withhold my opinion until I fight that man.” Li Jinxi said.

“Me too.” Wang Xiuying agreed.

At the same time, Feng Yuxiang asked Yuan about Kelan, “Who was that man? And why is he training Meixiu and the others?”

Yuan smiled and said, “Let me explain. It’s a long story, though. Fortunately, we have plenty of time.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1721 Divine Fist Manifestation
chapters.body:
Chapter 1721 Divine Fist Manifestation

“Heavens… a lot has happened since I went to sleep…” Feng Yuxiang couldn’t believe how much had happened in such a short amount of time.

‘Thank the heavens I woke up this soon. If I had slept any longer…’ Feng Yuxiang swallowed nervously. She could imagine sleeping for a decade and waking up to Yuan already achieving immortality. Just the thought of missing Yuan’s growth gave Feng Yuxiang a sense of dread.

“So, when do you plan on letting them know your true identity?” Feng Yuxiang asked Yuan a few moments later.

“I don’t,” Yuan said with a smile.

“Huh?” Feng Yuxiang muttered in a surprised voice.

“Now that I am hunted by the Celestial Emperor, I need to keep ‘Yuan’ away from them. I could be endangering them just by being near them. Of course, that’s not to say I’ll keep my identity a secret forever. Once the tournament is finished, I’ll find a way to tell them.”

“Then why stay here at all? Wouldn’t it be better if you’d just left and never interact with them?” Feng Yuxiang asked.

“I thought of that, but with so many people from the upper heavens coming down to the lower heavens, my identity won’t raise any suspicions. There’ll also be many witnesses who will have seen us acting like strangers to each other.”

“I see what you’re trying to do. Fooling your own allies to fool your enemy, huh?”

“Yes, but I will also admit that I want to see how much they’ve grown, even if it’s a little risky. They wouldn’t fight me with the same intensity if they knew my identity, after all.”

Sometime later, Kelan returned to the booth.

“By the way, I never got your name.”

“Surnamed Xiao.”

“Surname? You can’t tell me your full name?” Kelan raised an eyebrow.

“My background is a bit unique. I am not allowed to disclose my true identity carelessly.”

“Ah, so you’re from one of those families, huh?” Kelan nodded his head as if he understood.

Hidden families were not uncommon in the Nine Heavens, particularly in the upper heavens. These families were known for keeping their backgrounds, techniques, and talents as secretive as possible, rarely revealing their true strength or lineage to the outside world. Searᴄh the Novelƒire(.)ne*t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Their reasons for doing so also varied from family to family, so they were typically shrouded in mystery.

‘So he’s from a hidden family. No wonder why I have never heard of him.’ Kelan thought to himself, and he wondered how many more monsters like Yuan were hidden from the rest of the world, growing in secrecy.

Sometime later, Xi Murong finally defeated his opponent after messing around for many minutes.

As time passed, more and more members of the Demon Sealing Faction entered the stage. Their unusual background and high cultivation base attracted a lot of attention from the other sects. “Heavens. They have another at the Spirit Enlightenment, and he’s even at the fifth level.” “They are standing with the Grand Mystic Sect. Are they disciples? How come I don’t recognize any of them?”

“Just where in heaven’s name are they finding so many monstrous talents? Finding a single one of them every thousand years would already be considered a miracle, yet there’s a dozen of them together at this moment.”

“Ah, I know about them. I heard this from a disciple of the Grand Mystic Sect, and they’re apparently being trained by someone from the Celestial Overlords. They’re also not disciples of the Grand Mystic Sect.”

“What?! Someone from the Celestial Overlords is here in the Fourth Heavens?!”

“My god! No wonder! This explains everything!”

Information of the Demon Sealing Faction being related to the Celestial Overlords quickly spread throughout the spectators’ area, shocking everyone.

Several hours later, two members of the Demon Sealing Faction had been matched against each other.

“What a coincidence, Sister Wang,” Hong Xiuqian said, pressing his fists together, the steel gauntlets on his hands gleaming as he stared intently at his opponent, Wang Bingbing.

“Do you remember the last time we fought each other in an official competition?” he asked.

“Yes, I do.” Wang Bingbing calmly responded, recalling their fight during the Spirit Competition at the Jaded Garden.

“Then you also remember being defeated by me, right?” “Yes, I remember barely losing to you. However, things are different now.”

“Are they, really? We both trained under the same teacher.”

“Hmph! Enough talking! I will show you the difference right now!” Wang Bingbing raised her sword, enveloping it with a sharp aura.

“Sword Aura! That young lady knows Sword Aura at such a young age?!” The audience gasped in shock.

Hong Xiuqian remained unfazed by her Sword Aura, knowing it wasn’t a secret. He calmly bashed his gauntlets together, and a silver aura appeared around them a moment later, emitting a destructive aura.

“That young man also achieved Divine Fist Manifestation!”

While masters of sharp weapons could achieve an “aura,” such as Sword Aura or Spear Aura, those who rely on their fists can achieve Divine Fist Manifestation. This concept is similar to Sword Aura, but instead of enhancing sharpness, its power lies in its overwhelming destructive force, amplifying the raw strength of every blow.

Not only can Divine Fist Manifestation block weapon auras, but they are also just as difficult to cultivate and train as weapon auras.

Wang Bingbing and Hong Xiuqian clashed with each other a moment later. When their Sword Aura and Divine Fist Manifestation collided, a powerful ripple swept the arena, disturbing the nearby fights.

While every platform had formations in place to prevent projectiles and debris from flying outside and harming the spectators or other participants, these formations did not block the ripples of energy created when two powerful forces clashed.

Wang Bingbing went on the offensive while Hong Xiuqian played defense, repeating their match during the Spirit Competition, almost as if they were trying to prove something to each other.

But compared to their fight previously, they were both incomparably stronger with much more fighting experience.

chapters.title: Chapter 1722 Peak Of Spirit Enlightenment
chapters.body:
Chapter 1722 Peak Of Spirit Enlightenment

“What a fierce battle! It’s like they’re really trying to kill each other!”

The spectators were shocked by how intensely Hong Xiuqian and Wang Bingbing were going at each other’s throats, almost as if they were before their archenemies.

As for the match itself, Hong Xiuqian was successfully blocking all of Wang Bingbing’s sword strikes. Moreover, his fists were still in great condition, unlike their previous match, where his fists would be bruised by the end.

As for Wang Bingbing, each of her sword strikes struck exactly where she wanted with deadly precision, and she had almost no wasteful movement. ‘They’re incomparably stronger than before, and they are still hiding their true strength.’ Yuan smiled inwardly, wondering if they were hiding their true strength because of him.

“Sister Wang, I have a better chance of defeating that man. Stop this pointless struggle, and just let me win.”

“Are you seriously saying that?” Wang Bingbing suddenly became even more aggressive, and her aura soared, clearly angry at Hong Xiuqian’s words.

Hong Xiuqian was slightly taken aback and started getting pushed back.

‘Damn, she’s starting to use her real strength!’ Hong Xiuqian clenched his jaw and started fighting more seriously, too. Searᴄh the ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

While they continued fighting, another member of the Demon Sealing Faction was called to the stage.

A beautiful woman with a stern expression walked onto the stage. Her opponent, who was only a Spirit King, began trembling in fear after sensing her cultivation.

“P-Peak of Spirit Enlightenment!”

Li Jinxi’s overwhelming presence on the stage affected not just her opponent but even those around her, including those in the audience.

“A-are you ready to fight?” The judge for their match asked.

Li Jinxi silently nodded.

It took a moment for her opponent to respond. After swallowing nervously, he said, “R-ready!”

“Then let the fight begin!”

The instant the match began, Li Jinxi’s figure disappeared from her spot.

“Agh!”

Her opponent cried out in pain and was sent flying outside the stage a split second later.

Li Jinxi had defeated her opponent in a single move not even a second after the match began, and she used nothing but her own fists.

“W-winner, Li Jinxi!”

Once the match ended, the judge offered Li Jinxi a recovery pill, but she outright refused it and left the stage.

“Heavens! What a domineering beauty! To be at the Peak of the Spirit Enlightenment at her age, her potential is massive!”

“Hahaha! That young man from the upper heavens must be sweating by now! I cannot imagine a Spirit Grandmaster defeating someone at the peak of Spirit Enlightenment, regardless of their background!”

Sometime later, Meixiu entered the stage with a spirit-grade bow. However, she didn’t have any arrows on her, and her bow also lacked the bowstring that traditional bows have, resembling that of an incomplete bow.

Her opponent was a young woman at the third level of the Spirit Sovereign realm who belonged to one of the Three Grand Sects, the Grand Spring Sect.

“Please go easy on me,” said her opponent.

Meixiu silently nodded.

Once the match began, the Grand Spring Sect disciple raised her sword in a defensive manner while Meixiu’s aura skyrocketed, wrapping itself around the bow in her grasp and creating a bowstring. By the time Meixiu raised her bow, an arrow made entirely of her aura was already prepared to launch from the bow.

“!!!”

The Grand Spring Sect disciple instinctively knew that she was incapable of blocking Meixiu’s attack and immediately switched from defensive to trying to avoid the attack entirely.

The arrow flew from the bow a moment later and barely missed the Grand Spring Sect. However, right as the disciple sighed in relief inwardly, she felt something strike her from the back.

“Wha—?!”

The arrow that had missed her initially had suddenly turned around, and in that instant, she felt as though a massive hole had been torn through her stomach. However, when she looked down, she saw no injury on her body; the formation had activated, sparing her life.

“I-it’s my loss…” The disciple sighed in a disappointed manner as she placed a hand on where Meixiu had struck her.

As a Core Disciple of the Grand Spring Sect, she thought she would, at the very least, achieve the top 10. However, she had gotten incredibly unlucky and got matched with someone an entire realm above her for her first match.

‘I couldn’t even show off a single technique before I was defeated… This is so embarrassing…’ The disciple left the stage with her head lowered.

Chu Liuxiang was next, and she also defeated her opponent in a single attack by blasting them away with an unavoidable ice technique that covered the entire stage. Sometime later, Wang Bingbing and Hong Xiuqian’s match finally concluded.

“Winner—Wang Bingbing!” The judge announced.

Wang Bingbing had managed to defeat Hong Xiuqian after a long and arduous battle where neither of them was able to overpower the other for hours until Hong Xiuqian made a mistake from exhaustion—one that Wang Bingbing took advantage of and came out on top.

The spectators cheered madly, causing the whole place to rumble with excitement.

“This year’s tournament is really exciting.” One of Emperor Ning’s children said as he clapped.

“Father, do you think it’s possible to meet them after the tournament? I would love to have a word with that beauty with the bow.” One of the older children asked, his gaze filled with adoration.

Before Emperor Ning could even speak, Kelan responded, “She already has a partner.”

“That doesn’t mean any—”

Kelan looked at him and interrupted, “I wouldn’t continue that sentence if I were you… unless you wish the downfall of your family.”

Emperor Ning hastily went to slap his own son in the face and shouted, “Apologize to Senior Kelan right now!”

“I-I am sorry, Senior! I didn’t realize you were her partner!”

Kelan frowned and said, “Hey, that’s a crazy misunderstanding. I am not her partner, and don’t you dare utter such words again. If her real partner was here to hear that, he’d probably go on a rampage and kill everyone here.”

Yuan turned to look at Kelan with raised eyebrows.

‘What kind of nonsense is that? Is this how he sees me?’ he shook his head inwardly.

chapters.title: Chapter 1723 The Top 20
chapters.body:
Chapter 1723 The Top 20

“He’d kill everyone here over something like that? He sounds like a villain.” Yuan said, joining the conversation.

“Well… I’m not saying he’ll definitely do it, but he is very much capable. That man is a crazed bastard who’d aim his sword at the heavens without any hesitation if needed.” Kelan said, completely unaware that he was talking to Yuan.

“Sounds like you’re scared of him. Is he more powerful than you?”

Kelan rubbed his nose and said, “He may be a little monster with more potential than I, but he is still a hundred—thousand years too early to defeat me.”

“And who said I am scared of him? His unpredictably does make me nervous at times, but that is different from fear.”

“Is that so?” Yuan smiled inwardly.

Suddenly, another of Emperor Ning’s older sons spoke, “T-then what about that beauty who used that ice technique?”

“Ah, she’s also taken… by the same man.” seaʀᴄh thё NôᴠelFirё.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“…”

“That nonchalant fairy at the peak of Spirit Enlightenment is probably single, right?”

Kelan merely shook his head in silence.

“Seriously…?”

Kelan sighed and said, “You can forget about coveting any of the women in that group. They all belong to the same guy.”

“What?!”

The Ning Family was surprised to hear this. Even Emperor Ning couldn’t help but swallow nervously, his gaze filled with envy. “Is this man…also an Emperor?” Emperor Ning had to ask.

How can a single man have so many talented partners if they weren’t in a position of high power and authority?

“No, but he’s also from the Celestial Overlords, and he is one of the youngest Generals in our faction’s history. He’d also qualify to participate in this tournament if he were here.”

“…”

“Anyway, he’s one hell of a womanizer who is always surrounded by women.” Kelan continued.

Yuan had a strong urge to smack Kelan in the back of the head for his earlier statement about all of the girls in the group belonging to him, but he held back.

However, he decided to punish Kelan in the future for spreading misunderstanding when there is a chance.

Sometime later, once all of the participants had their match, they immediately started the second round.

With half of the participants eliminated, the age gap began widening, but it was only by a year or two at most.

During the second round, Wang Ming and Shi Lang were matched against each other, and Wang Ming ended up barely winning.

For the third round, Wu Zao fought Wang Xiuying, with Wu Zao emerging victorious by a landslide.

Although Wang Xiuying also trained with Kelan, she’d only spent half as much time as everyone else since she still had to focus on her alchemy, which was her primary path.

For the seventh round, Li Jinxi ended up matching with Meixiu.

Li Jinxi obviously won by a landslide, but she was unable to instantly defeat Meixiu unlike her previous matches. In the end, their match ended up lasting a whole minute.

Wang Ming and Wang Bingbing also fought each other during the seventh round, with Wang Ming winning.

In the ninth round, Wu Zao got matched against Xi Murong and lost within half a minute.

By the time there were about twenty or so participants left, only Chu Liuxiang, Li Jinxi, Xi Murong, and Wang Ming were left.

Yuan then spoke, “Congratulations to those of you who have made it this far. Now, you have all acquired the qualifications to challenge me. Of course, you don’t need to challenge me right now.”

“You can challenge me after the tournament ends, or you can challenge—”

Before Yuan could even finish his sentence, one of the people there stepped forward and shouted, “I want to challenge you!”

“Good.”

Without saying another word, Yuan leaped from the booth to one of the empty stages there.

The one who challenged Yuan also entered the stage a moment later.

“What happens if more than one of us defeats you? Will you give out the reward twice?” The young man, number one disciple of the Burning Lotus Sword Sect, asked as he faced Yuan.

Yuan calmly nodded, “Naturally.”

The spectators gasped in shock after learning the reward wasn’t for a single person.

Xing Chen sucked his teeth after realizing he had jumped the gun. He thought the reward was only for the first one to defeat him, hence why he was so eager to fight first. However, after learning the truth, he regretted not waiting a bit more to study Yuan’s prowess first. He turned to look at the other participants, and most of them were smiling, almost as if laughing at him.

‘Whatever. It doesn’t matter if I am first or last since I will defeat him regardless!’ Xing Chen readied his sword.

At this moment, every single pair of eyes were on Yuan, their gaze filled with anticipation.

“I will let you have the first…three strikes. By the way, I have sealed all of my treasures’ abilities, so you don’t have to worry about that.” Yuan said.

“Then I won’t be humble!” Xing Chen roared as his sword became engulfed in flames.

“Burning Lotus Raging Sword Strike!”

Yuan merely stood there as Xing Chen swung his fiery sword at Yuan’s neck.

Seeing his opponent aiming for his neck, Yuan willingly tilted his head the other way, giving Xing Chen full access to his neck.

“Your neck is mine!”

The moment the flaming sword touched Yuan’s neck, its movement halted abruptly, and the flames were instantly and forcefully absorbed into Yuan’s body.

Kelan stood up from his seat and exclaimed, “Fire Harmony?!”

“W-what?” Emperor Ning looked at him with a startled and puzzled expression.

“A level that is even higher than flame immunity. Not only is he impervious to flames but he could also absorb them and turn it into his own energy! The peak of fire mastery!”

Kelan clenched his fists as his body trembled in excitement. Even he has only achieved Super Fire Immunity after tens of thousands of years of rigorous training.

“No… There’s simply no way he could’ve achieved Fire Harmony at that age. It must have been one of his techniques at work…” Kelan quickly shook his head, almost as if he couldn’t believe that he had been fooled by such an obvious fact, even if it was only for a brief moment.

“Fuck, that surprised me…” he mumbled a moment later.

Search the NovelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

chapters.title: Chapter 1724 Fire Harmony
chapters.body:
Chapter 1724 Fire Harmony

“That was your first strike,” Yuan said to Xing Chen after tanking his attack without a single scratch on his neck.

“N-no way…” Xing Chen hastily jumped back.

‘There’s no way he actually defended against such a powerful strike with his naked neck! He must’ve used an illusion or something to make it look like that!’ Xing Chen refused to believe that a Spirit Grandmaster had managed to eat one of his most powerful strikes and come out unscathed.

“What are you waiting for? You have two more strikes. I’m not trying to spend a whole day on a single match.” Yuan beckoned him in a provocative manner.

Xing Chen immediately became infuriated, as he had never been mocked in such a fashion before. He began gathering his spiritual energy for several moments before pointing his sword at Yuan.

“Burning Lotus Heavenly Sword!” A massive blade made of pure fire materialized in the sky directly above Yuan. Once it fully materialized and the blade almost appeared real, it fell on Yuan’s head.

Whosh!

Flames spewed in every direction like a tsunami, engulfing the arena in a sea of flames.

“There’s no… way… he could’ve… avoided that…” Xing Chen became incredibly weakened after releasing the technique, which exhausted nearly all of his spiritual energy.

Yuan’s figure was engulfed in flames, but his silhouette could still be seen by the spectators and Xing Chen.

However, before Xing Chen could celebrate, a powerful gust appeared, swallowing up all of the flames. The flames gathered like bugs to a lantern around Yuan, who began taking a deep breath, as if he was preparing to blow a straw house down.

As for the flames, they were simply consumed by Yuan.

“H-how…?” Xing Chen fell to his knees with a loud thud that resounded throughout the stadium.

The spectators could only gasp in shock at what they’d just witnessed.

“There’s no mistaking it…” Kelan muttered in a dazed voice. “He actually achieved the state of Fire Harmony before the age of 30! This level of talent is almost unheard of, even in the Supreme Heaven! He must have been born with a physique blessed by the fire gods!”

Kelan’s desire to fight Yuan skyrocketed after learning this.

‘You kids better defeat him so I can fight him!’ Kelan clenched his fists in anticipation.

Yuan casually patted his clothes after swallowing the flames and said, “If that was your best, then I’ll consider this fight concluded, as you will not be able to damage me, much less defeat me.”

Xing Chen did not even have the energy to respond anymore and directly collapsed onto the ground. One of the workers there swiftly entered the stage to retrieve Xing Chen, bringing him away.

Yuan looked at the rest of the participants and said, “So, who’s next?”

The participants subconsciously stepped back when they felt Yuan’s overwhelming gaze through his mask.

After a few moments of silence, a beautiful girl entered the stage with a graceful leap.

Once the beauty introduced herself, Yuan nodded and said, “The match will begin the moment you raise your weapon.” Searᴄh the ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

The beauty quickly raised the sword in her grasp and prepared to attack. However, before she could even act, Yuan’s figure flickered and disappeared.

When he appeared again a split second later, his fist was already digging into the beauty’s stomach.

“Ah!”

The beauty let out a dramatic cry of pain, and her face contorted in an exaggerated expression as she was sent flying off the stage.

“W-why…? What happened to three free strikes…?” she complained after struggling to get back on her feet.

“It’d take too long if I let everyone have three free strikes,” he shrugged.
