“N-no way…” The beauty muttered in disbelief before falling unconscious and getting carried off the stage.

“Next.” Yuan ignored her and turned to look at the remaining participants, who swallowed nervously and subconsciously avoided his gaze.

“Since nobody is going next, I will do it.”

A familiar face entered the stage.

“I’m Chu Liuxiang. What’s your name?” ‘Lulu…’ Yuan showed a gentle smile on his face after Chu Liuxiang challenged him.

“My surname is Xiao.”

“You can’t tell me your full name?”

“Unfortunately, I am unable to because of reasons beyond my control.”

Chu Liuxiang narrowed her eyes at him and asked, “Have we met before?”

“Why do you ask?”

“Just a feeling.”

“Well, unless you’ve been to the upper heavens, I highly doubt we’ve met, as this is my first time coming to the lower heavens.”

“I see.”

“…”

“You’re not going to ask why I came down here?” Yuan asked.

“Well, I don’t really care, so I have no reason to ask.”

“I understand. Then let’s start the match. I’ll make my move after you make your first move.” Yuan said a moment later.

Chu Liuxiang nodded, and after several moments of stillness, she suddenly brought her arms parallel to the ground and swiftly activated a technique.

“Ice Shield!”

A beautiful and thick shield made of ice and enforced by spiritual energy appeared in front of Chu Liuxiang, capable of blocking any frontal attacks.

She thought Yuan was going to attack her the moment she used her technique like the last match. However, Yuan merely stood there in silence, almost as if he were analyzing her technique.

Seeing this, Yuan smiled and said, “Don’t worry, I won’t end this match too fast since I ended my last one a bit too fast, so we have some spare time to enjoy ourselves.”

“Sorry, but I’m not interested.”

Chu Liuxiang suddenly placed her palms on the ground.

[Frozen Garden of Impalement!]

Thousands of ice spikes erupted from the ground and reached a dozen meters tall, covering the entire arena and leaving no room for Yuan to escape. She had used this technique in several matches before, and not a single one of her opponents had been able to block or avoid it.

However, Yuan didn’t even try to avoid it and allowed the ice spikes to land on him.

“Wow…” Chu Liuxiang showed a nervous smile after seeing Yuan tank such a powerful technique with ease.

chapters.title: Chapter 1725 Fighting Chu Liuxiang
chapters.body:
Chapter 1725 Fighting Chu Liuxiang

<Cold Resistance has improved>

A simple notification appeared when Yuan took Chu Liuxiang’s ice technique with his bare body.

‘Oh? I can temper my body like this?’ He smiled inwardly.

Yuan looked at Chu Liuxiang, “Is that all you’ve got? Show me some more of your ice techniques since they’re quite rare even in the upper heavens.”

Chu Liuxiang snapped out of her daze and gritted her teeth.

[A thousand Ice Lotus Blooms on the Battlefield!]

Hundreds of beautiful frozen lotuses materialized around Yuan, completely surrounding him.

Yuan curiously touched one of them, causing it to burst and sending ice shrapnels in every direction. Almost instantaneously, a chain reaction occurred, and in the blink of an eye, all of the ice lotuses exploded.

<Cold Resistance has improved>

<Cold Resistance has improved>

<Cold Resistance has improved>

Although the system did not specify, Yuan could feel that his cold resistance was only increasing slightly.

Yuan wanted to get at least Weak Cold Resistance by the end of the match, but he didn’t want to prolong their match for too long without any reason, as it would raise eyebrows.

“You’re that tall man’s disciple, right? Show me what he taught you. I promised to fight him if one of you manages to defeat me, after all.”

“No wonder why he appeared a little desperate for us to win…” Chu Liuxiang sighed.

“Forget it. I know I can’t win, and I don’t feel like fighting you for some reason.” Chu Liuxiang shook her head.

“You don’t? Why not? You fell for me or something?” Yuan teased her, hoping it’d get her riled up.

Chu Liuxiang sneered and said, “I am already with the love of my life, so I have no reason to look at another man.”

“Anyway, I—”

Just as Chu Liuxiang prepared to forfeit, Yuan casually swung his arm, sending a sword light made of pure Sword Aura at her.

Chu Lixiuang hastily created a shield of ice before her, but it lasted only a split second before it was sliced in half. If she hadn’t dodged at the same time she created the shield, the sword light would’ve struck her.

“Were you going to forfeit because you couldn’t harm me with your techniques? I didn’t know Kelan raised such a coward—or did you learn that from your partner?”

Chu Liuxiang’s eyes burned with fighting spirit.

Seeing this, Yuan clapped, “So you finally feel like fighting me?”

“Damn, you’re annoying as hell! I will make you regret provoking me!”

Chu Liuxiang began writing in the air, swiftly creating array symbols.

“Oh? You’re an Array Master?” Yuan was pleasantly surprised that Chu Liuxiang had started using array symbols.

A few seconds later, the array symbols that gathered above the arena came together, creating a massive cloud. Soon, it began snowing in the arena.

When the snowflakes touched Yuan’s body, some of his spiritual energy was eaten away.

“A formation that gradually weakens your opponents, huh? This is at least a level 3 formation. Not bad.”

Just like Wang Xiuying, Chu Liuxiang didn’t spend all of her time training under Kelan. She also studied array symbols under one of the Grand Mystic Sect’s sect elders.

“I can’t believe it’s only been a year since I started teaching her about array symbols.” An old lady sighed in admiration and regret as she stared at Chu Liuxiang’s figure from the spectators’ area.

“In just a short year, she went from barely knowing anything about array symbols to becoming a level three Array Master… If she had a better teacher than this useless one, she would’ve definitely reached higher. Her talents are simply incomprehensible.”

Chu Liuxiang wasn’t finished with just one formation and continued creating more array symbols.

Yuan wanted to see what she had planned and purposely allowed her to finish. However, Chu Liuxiang remained alert the entire time, anticipating him to attack at any moment.

A few moments later, Chu Liuxiang completed her second formation, one that weakened the strength and those within it.

The third one affected Yuan’s movements, making him move slower.

By the time Chu Liuxiang was finished, she’d used up a third of her spiritual energy, but she felt that it was worth it as long as it weakened Yuan enough.

“To cast three formations in just a few moments, what a talented Array Master… not to mention her young age…” The spectators were in awe. “You done?” Yuan asked her.

“No, but you are!”

Chu Liuxiang began bombarding Yuan with ice techniques with her remaining spiritual energy.

However, Yuan would not block any of them, allowing them to bombard his body.

Seeing this, Kelan subconsciously began tapping his foot.

‘He doesn’t have any cold resistance. He’s blocking all of her Spirit Enlightenment attacks with his body! Not only does he have Fire Harmony, but his physique has also been tempered to a ridiculous degree! How in heaven’s name does he have the time to achieve all of this?! Did he train inside a time chamber or something?!’

Kelan couldn’t fathom how Yuan was able to attain his physique and Fire Harmony in less than thirty years. While there are time chambers in the upper heavens where time flows differently inside, they are all incredibly rare and scarce, and the time one could spend inside is severely limited.

‘Just what is his background? Is there really such a powerful and resourceful family hidden away somewhere in this world?’

<Weak Cold Resistance has been attained>

Before Yuan acquired Weak Cold Resistance, Chu Liuxiang’s ice techniques would tickle him. However, after acquiring cold resistance, he wouldn’t even feel it touching his body if it weren’t for his clothes.

Kelan’s eyes widened after noticing this.

“He’d gained cold resistance?! Just like that?! It’s only been a few minutes!” he exclaimed in a shocked tone. S~eaʀᴄh the NôᴠelFirё.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

If one could acquire resistance from being struck by another’s techniques, then everyone in the world would possess some form of resistance. However, reality didn’t work that way. Yet, Yuan had somehow managed to achieve this feat, which explained Kelan’s shock and disbelief.

chapters.title: Chapter 1726 Cold Resistance
chapters.body:
Chapter 1726 Cold Resistance

“N-no… let’s think about this properly… There’s no way anyone could achieve resistance after just a few minutes from that level of attack. He must’ve already been on the verge of attaining cold resistance before fighting her. Yup. This has to be the explanation.” Kelan suddenly convinced himself that this must be the explanation, as he found it impossible to believe that Yuan had started from nothing and attained cold resistance in just a few minutes. In other words, he was in denial, unable to accept the reality of Yuan’s rapid progress.

Meanwhile, on the stage, Chu Liuxiang was sweating buckets after exhausting all of her spiritual energy. Unbeknownst to her, her efforts ended up benefiting Yuan greatly.

“Thank you for the training. It helped me a lot.” Yuan said to her afterward.

“As a thank you, I’ll face you seriously.”

Yuan thought about going easy on Chu Liuxiang because she was his partner. However, after thinking about it, he decided to do the opposite.

‘Although we’ve sparred with each other plenty of times, she never really experienced fighting ‘me’.’ Yuan smiled inwardly.

No matter how intense their sparring became, it was still just a spar in the end. Even if they fought with full seriousness, Chu Liuxiang never felt threatened because, deep down, she knew Yuan would never hurt her.

This made it impossible for Chu Liuxiang and the others close to Yuan understand what it’s truly like fighting a monster like Yuan. ‘Facing someone unfathomably stronger than her… This will be a good experience for her, even if it might scare her a bit.’ Yuan believed that Chu Liuxiang needed this valuable experience since most people tend to freeze from fear, which could be the difference between life and death.

A moment later, Yuan’s aura surged dramatically, shifting from a suppressed state to one that was outright tyrannical and terrifying. However, it wasn’t killing intent; instead, it was a destructive and ominous aura that radiated immense power.

His aura was so overwhelming that Chu Liuxiang instinctively let out a terrified scream as she fell backward onto her buttocks, even raising her arms to shield her face. Searᴄh the NôvelFire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Li Jinxi and the other Demon Sealing Faction members instinctively retrieved their weapons and tiptoed as they tried to jump on the stage to protect Chu Liuxiang, but they managed to control themselves at the last second. As a matter of fact, pretty much everyone at the tournament became defensive after sensing Yuan’s aura—participants or not.

“What’s wrong? I only revealed some of my aura, and you’re already shaking on the ground?” Yuan spoke as he slowly approached her with small and steady steps. “Get up and show me some more of the ice techniques that you’re so proud of.”

Yuan retrieved his weapon—a spirit-grade spear. “Why’s he using a spear when he knows Sword Aura?” Kelan frowned, being more intrigued by his weapon of choice than his aura.

Suddenly, the tip of Yuan’s spear began emitting a sharp aura—that one resembled Sword Aura.

“Spear Aura!” Shi Lang exclaimed after seeing his aura. “He knows Sword Aura and Spear Aura?!”

This was not only Yuan’s first time using Spear Aura. It was actually his first attempt using Spear Aura. Despite this, he didn’t feel unfamiliar with it. After all, the God of War, who had mastered all sorts of weapons, also achieved weapon auras for many of them.

<Spear Aura achieved>

The system only recognized his achievement once he used it even though he had the capability to do so for a long time now.

“Where did your bravery a minute ago disappear to? Stand and fight me.” Yuan provoked her.

However, Chu Liuxiang didn’t respond, almost as if she couldn’t hear him.

“…”

Seeing the petrified expression on Chu Liuxiang’s face and how much she was shaking, Yuan began to wonder if he’d scared her a little too much.

‘She won’t hate me when I reveal my identity to her later, right?’ he swallowed nervously at that thought.

Suddenly, a loud voice resounded, “Stand up and face your opponent properly! Have you forgotten about what I told you?!”

Kelan could be seen shouting from the Ning Family’s booth.

Hearing Kelan’s voice and recalling about the conversation he had with everyone some time ago, Chu Liuxiang gritted her teeth and forced herself to stand up to face Yuan.

“If I’m going to stand beside Yuan, who will face many powerful figures in the future, I need to get used to this feeling of overwhelming difference!” Chu Liuxiang muttered as she faced her opponent.

Seeing this, Yuan smiled behind his mask and said in a genuinely proud voice, “Not bad. Then here’s your reward.”

Yuan’s spear flickered the next moment.

Chu Liuxiang suddenly felt a sharp pain in the area between her eyebrows. Despite being aware of the formation that protected her life, Chu Liuxiang was not in the situation to remember such things, and her life flashed before her eyes.

‘Yuan…’ The last image that appeared in Chu Liuxiang’s flashback was Yuan’s childish face when they were still back at the orphanage before she lost consciousness.

Li Jinxi suddenly appeared on the stage to catch Chu Liuxiang’s falling body, her gaze staring intently at Yuan, almost as if she wanted to tear him apart.

“Are you going to fight me next?” Yuan asked in a provoking tone.

“No, I will.” Xi Murong said as he entered the stage with a dagger in his grasp.

“Sure.” Yuan calmly nodded.

Li Jinxi carried Chu Liuxiang off the stage while Xi Murong approached Yuan.

“You can’t scare me with your aura,” he said.

“Oh, really? I swear I saw you flinch when I first emitted it.” Yuan laughed.

Xi Murong gritted his teeth and eagerly said, “Can we start already?”

“You’re itching to fight me, huh? Are you her boyfriend, by chance? Anyway, whenever you’re rea—”

Before Yuan could even finish his sentence, Xi Murong’s figure disappeared like a ghost.

‘A movement technique, huh.’

Although Yuan couldn’t see Xi Murong, he didn’t need to, as he could sense Xi Murong’s position very clearly with his powerful soul strength and divine sense.

“There you are,” Yuan muttered as he suddenly thrust his spear at a specific spot.

chapters.title: Chapter 1727 Fighting Xi Murong
chapters.body:
Chapter 1727 Fighting Xi Murong

“Che!” Xi Murong sucked his teeth when Yuan could see through his movement technique, but he wasn’t too surprised, considering his opponent was a genius from the upper heavens.

Right as Yuan’s spear reached him, Xi Murong’s speed abruptly increased, and his figure disappeared again.

A split second later, Xi Murong appeared behind Yuan with his dagger diving toward his neck.

[Absolute Penetration]

‘No matter how sturdy your body is, this technique will allow me to bypass all of your defenses!’

Sure enough, Xi Murong managed to cut Yuan’s skin, but only the tip of his blade managed to penetrate his flesh. Still, he’d managed to make Yuan bleed, an astonishing feat that most wouldn’t be able to accomplish.

Yet, Xi Murong wasn’t finished. After drawing Yuan’s blood, he activated another skill that injected poison directly into Yuan’s body through the dagger and his wound.

Xi Murong quickly distanced himself afterward. Realizing that Xi Murong had poisoned him, Yuan decided to play along and pretended to be affected.

“W-what did you do to my body?!”

“I just poisoned you with a deadly poison that will melt your internal organs. You’ll experience excruciating pain for a bit, but you won’t die because of the formation.” Xi Murong announced proudly.

“Ah!” Yuan fell to his knees and began trembling and screaming in pain. seaʀᴄh thё Nôvel(F)ire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Hahaha! This is what happens when you underestimate us! Know your place!” Xi Murong laughed loudly.

The spectators were in shock, and they began to believe that Xi Murong had won the fight.

However, Kelan suddenly shouted, “You fool! You’re the one who is underestimating him! Check his body with divine sense!”

“What?” Xi Murong frowned and probed Yuan with his divine sense.

“M-my poison is gone?! Where did it go?!”

At this point, Yuan stopped pretending to claw at his body from the pain and calmly stood back up.

“Your poison? My poison immunity dealt with it the second it entered my body.” Yuan casually explained.

“Poison immunity?!” Xi Murong and the others exclaimed in shock.

Poison immunity was one of the most tedious and painful immunities to attain, so it came as a great shock that someone so young had managed to attain such immunity.

“He has not only Fire Harmony but also poison immunity at that age…” Kelan’s expression turned solemn, looking like he was pondering something.

Unbeknownst to Kelan, Yuan’s poison immunity had reached the maximum level, just like his fire immunity. Otherwise, his reaction would’ve been a lot more exaggerated.

“Y-you!” Xi Murong was speechless, but above all, he felt embarrassed.

He instinctively pounced at Yuan, as if to combat his embarrassment.

“You were so composed just moments ago, but all it took was a little act to completely break you? You still have lots to work on.” Yuan commented as he casually dodged Xi Murong before thrusting his spear at him.

“Agh!”

Xi Murong made a funny expression when Yuan’s spear dug into his neck. However, Yuan wasn’t done and infused a tiny amount of his chaotic essence into his spear.

The moment Xi Murong sensed Yuan’s chaotic essence, it felt as if he suddenly standing before death’s door.

‘Ah… I’m dead…’

This was all Xi Murong could think of before falling unconscious.

“You’re not going to take him away this time?” Yuan looked at Li Jinxi, who wore a deep frown on her face.

“Too scared to approach me? Fine. Let me help you.” Yuan used his foot to pick up Xi Murong’s body before gently tossing him at Li Jinxi.

“W-what was that energy?! It’s so chaotic!”

“It resembles killing intent, but it also feels different. It’s like a mortal standing before a god-like beast. Is this due to his unique cultivation technique?”

The spectators speculated the origin of Yuan’s chaotic essence, but none of them had ever sensed something so foreign.

Only Kelan could somewhat identify his chaotic essence.

‘It’s similar to the chaotic energy typically found in the Primordial Chaos and other places inhabitable by humans! Don’t tell me his family has managed to create a cultivation technique that allows them to cultivate that chaotic energy?!’ The shock Kelan was experiencing right now was far more than when he realized Yuan had Fire Harmony.

If a cultivation technique that allowed one to cultivate chaotic energy was truly out there, it would change the entire landscape of the cultivation world! It could even usher in a new age of cultivation!

‘This man… I must get him to join the Celestial Overlords at all costs!’ Kelan secretly contacted Senior Bai through a communication jade slip meant for emergencies. When Senior Bai answered, Kelan explained the whole situation to him.

“We must acquire this man at all costs! He will definitely play a massive role in the cultivation world in the future! His potential is even higher than that of Yuan’s!”

“Potential higher than Yuan? That is very hard to believe, but I will take your word and notify Lady Xu.”

Unbeknownst to Kelan, his actions would end up sparking a war within the Celestial Overlords.

After their conversation ended, Senior Bai revealed the details of their conversation to the individuals with unfathomably deep cultivation before him.

Senior Bai was coincidentally in the middle of a meeting with most of the upper echelons of the Celestial Overlords when Kelan called, and everyone there was at least within the rank of Overlord, meaning several Celestial Overlords were present as well.

Knowing Kelan would only use the emergency contact when it involved Yuan or his friends, Xu Jiaqi allowed Senior Bai to accept the call.

“A monster from a hidden family who, while not even at the age of thirty, has cultivated Fire Harmony, poison immunity, and an extreme physique? He might also have a cultivation technique that allows him to cultivate Chaos Qi? Does Kelan really expect us to believe such a mythical being exists? Talk about being delusional! Ahahaha!!” A bulky man laughed out loud after hearing the information.

“Regardless of its believability, we should still send someone to verify this information and check out the individual. If this individual turns out to be as formidable as Kelan claimed, we might have another Celestial Overlord in the future.” Another individual spoke.

“Who should we send?” The Overlords there turned to look at the three Celestial Overlords before them.

chapters.title: Chapter 1728 End Of Tournament Of Rising Stars
chapters.body:
Chapter 1728 End Of Tournament Of Rising Stars

“A heaven-defying talent that would make even the most talented individuals in the Nine Heavens look ordinary. I don’t believe such a being exists, nor am I going to waste my time finding out,” said a handsome man with long black hair tied into a ponytail.

The second Celestial Overlord looked at Xu Jiaqi and said, “Why don’t you go and check him out, Senior Sister Xu? It’s been a while since you last recruited someone with significant talents in the faction.”

“…” Xu Jiaqi remained silent; as did Senior Bai.

The information that a monster like Yuan existed in the Celestial Overlords was strictly restricted due to Xu Jiaqi’s efforts to keep his existence hidden until he was powerful enough to walk amongst them properly with his head held high.

If the members of the Celestial Overlords realized that someone not even in the Divine Realm was among their elite ranks, some might find issues with it and cause trouble for Yuan.

While Celestial Overlords like Xu Jiaqi hold immense authority in the faction, they were not in complete command over every member in the faction due to the existence of more than one Celestial Overlord, each having their own group or sub-faction.

For example, the faction had four members at the rank of Celestial Overlords, and each would hold a certain percentage of control over the faction. That percentage varied based on how many members each of the Celestial Overlords or their members had recruited. The members’ loyalty toward each Celestial Overlord also varied.

As of this moment, Xu Jiaqi had the lowest percentage, but those within her group could be considered the elite of elites, consisting of the most powerful members in the faction.

As for the other Celestial Overlords, they had Xu Jiaqi beat in quantity. Moreover, while numbers play a big role, it wasn’t as if the members of the faction could recruit anybody they saw to inflate their numbers.

“I’ll pass.” Xu Jiaqi said.

“Eh? Why not? I thought you loved gathering those with extreme talents. This guy sounds like he’s right up your alley.”

Xu Jiaqi feared that if she recruited this mysterious monster and the others found out about Yuan’s existence later on, things might get messy due to her monopoly of two monsters.

While she wasn’t afraid of the other Celestial Overlords or their complaining, she feared that it might cause strife and affect the entire faction. ‘I only need Yuan. They can have this guy.’ Xu Jiaqi thought to herself.

She was also aware of how powerful Yuan’s Heaven Refining Physique could become, so she wasn’t worried about his potential being surpassed by some nobody.

“I have enough talented individuals by my side already. If I get another one, you’ll all be complaining, especially if his talent is as amazing as Kelan claims, anyway.” Xu Jiaqi sneered.

“Are you sure? Kelan is from your group, so you have priority over this man, regardless of our feelings.”

“Yes, I am sure.”

Xu Jiaqi’s decision really surprised and puzzled many individuals there. She would typically be the first to jump at the opportunity to recruit someone highly talented, but she didn’t seem interested this time for some reason.

Unbeknownst to them, Xu Jiaqi had already secretly recruited a monster and thought this new monster wasn’t worth unnecessary drama. Only Senior Bai understood Xu Jiaqi’s thoughts.

“In that case, I’ll go take a look at this young man,” said the third Celestial Overlord. She was sitting beside Xu Jiaqi and was the one who addressed her as Senior Sister.

“Don’t come complaining to me if he turns out to be the real deal, okay?”

“Celestial Overlord Qing, are you going to go down yourself, or should one of us go in your stead?” One of the Overlords there asked.

“There’s a reason Kelan didn’t outright recruit him but contacted Overlord Bai instead. Would you send your servant to invite an Emperor to your banquet? No, you’d show up yourself to show your sincerity.” Celestial Overlord Qing explained her reasoning.

Sometime later, Senior Bai notified Kelan that Celestial Overlord Qing would be the one to recruit Yuan. Naturally, Kelan was incredibly upset by this decision.

“Why would she be the one coming?! I notified you because I wanted Celestial Overlord Xu to recruit him!”

Senior Bai explained the skepticism the others had about his information and Xu Jiaqi’s reason for not recruiting another monster.

“They think I am misunderstanding something?! That’s outrageous and slanderous! When have I ever made a bad judgment of talents?! I’m telling you that Celestial Overlord Xu will immensely regret not having this guy in her group! He’s even more monstrous than Yuan!”

“What’s done has been done… Unless you are willing to risk offending Celestial Overlord Qing and recruit him before she arrives, go ahead.”

Kelan swallowed nervously at the thought of offending another Celestial Overlord—one that was not in his group. He’d essentially make an enemy out of everyone under Celestial Overlord Qing.

“Haaa… Why did the Celestial Overlords split up in such a fashion? I heard it wasn’t like this when the founder was still alive.” Kelan sighed.

The Celestial Overlords were in complete unity when the founder of the Celestial Overlords was still in control. There were even more Celestial Overlords at that time than currently. Yet, nobody tried to fight for authority or acquire more control, and they were all loyal to the founder.

“Lady Xu is currently working to return the Celestial Overlords to its former glory. However, these things take a tremendous amount of time and energy.” Senior Bai said. “Also, she believes in Yuan more, even if he may be a little behind now.”

“I also believe in Yuan, but there’s truly something unfathomable about this man surnamed Xiao. Whatever. If Celestial Overlord Xu refuses to recruit him, there’s nothing else I can do. I also don’t want to offend Celestial Overlord Qing, as she’s also very scary in her own right.”

Meanwhile, Yuan faced his next moment, a young man wielding a sword.

“Wang Ming, and I’ll be your next opponent.”

“A sword, huh? Let me change my weapon real quick.” Yuan swapped his spear for a sword.

Their fight began shortly later.

Neither of them really spoke to each other. Instead, they used their swords to do the talking.

Wang Ming’s sword gushed with powerful Sword Aura, but it was still behind Yuan’s ordinary Sword Aura.

Realizing this, Wang Ming used his cultivation to make up for the difference.

Only being weaker than Li Jinxi, Wang Ming was the second strongest member of the Demon Sealing Faction, and his performance only further solidified his position.

His movements were perfectly executed, and his sword techniques were on the verge of perfection. His Sword Aura was also impressive for someone his age, something only seen in the upper heavens. Yet, despite his excellence, he was still several steps behind Yuan… in every aspect.

‘Damn it! This is exactly like fighting Yuan!’ Wang Ming gritted his teeth when he realized he couldn’t gain any advantage or overpower his opponent, and he was having flashbacks of his first encounter with Yuan.

“You’re not bad, but in my eyes, you’re only slightly better than my last opponent.” Yuan provoked him.

Wang Ming’s eyes flickered with fighting spirit, and his aura abruptly soared.

[Sword Energy!]

Wang Ming swung his sword at Yuan, releasing a powerful beam of energy that resembled Yuan’s Heaven Splitting Sword Strike. “Hmph.” Yuan also swung his sword, but instead of using Sword Aura, his sword displayed Enhanced Sword Aura, cutting right through Wang Ming’s technique.

“Enhanced Sword Aura?!” Kelan cried out loud as he witnessed Wang Ming being struck. Sёarch* The ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Enhanced… Sword Aura…?” Wang Ming muttered in disbelief as he felt the sensation of his body being sliced in half.

‘Not just Sword Aura and Spear Aura, but he also knows Enhanced Sword Aura?! There’s no way he’s less than thirty years old!’ Kelan started to question Yuan’s true age, as his achievements seemed far too extraordinary for someone so young.

“No way! He defeated another Spirit Enlightenment cultivator while only being Spirit Grandmaster! How is this making any sense?!”

“That must be Enhanced Sword Aura just now! It’s my first time witnessing it, but it’s so distinct from Sword Aura!”

While the spectators were in an uproar, Yuan looked at Li Jinxi and inquired, “Are you next?”

After a moment of silence, she said, “I want to, but I will deal with you once I finish this tournament.”

“If you say so.”

Yuan looked at the other participants, who all appeared to be stricken with immense fear.

“I guess there will be no more challengers for now.” Yuan returned to the Ning Family’s booth the next moment.

The tournament resumed shortly after, but most of the top 20 participants had lost their desire to fight. In the end, Li Jinxi swept away the rest of the tournament without any effort and became the champion.

“The champion of this year’s Tournament of Rising Stars is Li Jinxi, a rogue cultivator!” The judge announced.

The crowd cheered for her, but the celebration was short-lived since everyone knew there was still one more fight left to be fought.

After winning the tournament, Li Jinxi stepped onto one of the stages and beckoned Yuan with a finger.

Once Yuan entered the stage, he said, “Since this is the last fight, and I can tell you’re different from the others, let’s make use of this entire stadium instead of just a single stage.”

“I am fine with that,” she calmly said.

chapters.title: Chapter 1729 Three To Four Realms
chapters.body:
Chapter 1729 Three To Four Realms

Since not just the arenas but the entire stadium was protected by formations, Li Jinxi and Yuan’s fight would not be deadly—as long as they didn’t go overboard.

“Whenever you are ready.” Yuan stood before Li Jinxi with a calm demeanor, almost as if she was no different than the others.

“Before we fight, how about we make another rule? To allow the usage of treasures above Spirit-grade? I don’t think we’ll be able to have a proper fight with so many limitations.”

“…”

Yuan didn’t immediately respond to her suggestion. While lowering the restrictions will definitely make the fight much more entertaining, it will also make the fight much more dangerous.

“I don’t think I like that idea. I am doing this for fun, not because I want to kill you.” Yuan eventually shook his head, rejecting the idea.

He was also worried that he might accidentally reveal his true identity by fighting seriously.

“You think you can kill me?” Li Jinxi’s aura suddenly soared towards the heavens, and her peak of Spirit Enlightenment cultivation began inching toward the next realm.

In the blink of an eye, Li Jinxi’s cultivation reached the first level of Divine Warrior. However, that wasn’t the end, and her cultivation continued to soar.

The audience all wore expressions of awe and shock on their faces as they watched Li Jinxi’s cultivation through the levels of Divine Warrior and enter Divine Master. Yet, Li Jinxi did not appear to be finished yet.

By the end, Li Jinxi’s cultivation stopped at the fourth level of Divine Grandmaster.

“Heavens! A Divine Grandmaster before even reaching the age of thirty?! Her potential rivals those geniuses in the upper heavens!”

“No! Maybe she came from the upper heavens! If she had managed to reach that level in the lower heavens… I cannot even imagine how much she’d grow in the upper heavens!”

“Divine Grandmaster, huh? What a surprise.” Yuan said in a surprised voice.

“I will warn you now that I can fight someone three—even four realms above my own cultivation. If you don’t want to lose miserably, you should lower some of those limitations to increase your own cultivation.”

Li Jinxi spoke in a scornful tone, almost as if she was looking down on Yuan from a place far above him.

“Three to four realms, huh? I see…” Yuan chuckled.

He pointed at her and continued, “Alright, I’ll lower some of the limitations—but not for me.”

“What?” Li Jinxi frowned.

“You can use treasures above Spirit-grade—up to Celestial-grade treasures. However, I will continue to use my Spirit-grade treasure.”

Li Jinxi wasn’t too happy about being looked down upon. “What kind of nonsense is—”

“Hey.” Yuan suddenly interrupted, and his aura skyrocketed. However, his cultivation remained only at Spirit Grandmaster.

“Who do you think you are? Under this heaven, only I am allowed to look down on others.”

Yuan used True Dragon Awakening, causing his aura to explode.

“?!?!?!”

The entire stadium suddenly felt suffocating, and everyone struggled to breathe as a crushing pressure filled the air. Xi Meili’s body trembled violently, her very bloodline resonating with an overwhelming sense of fear.

Due to the stark difference between Dragon Awakening and True Dragon Awakening, Xi Meili was unable to recognize Yuan, not to mention she was overwhelmed by intense fear.

“W-what kind of bloodline does he have?!” Xi Meili fell to her knees as she stared at Yuan’s silhouette with trembling eyes, her teeth clattering as if she were freezing to death.

The blood within her responded to the Dragon God’s Blood Essence inside Yuan, filling her with an instinctive sense of reverence. It was as though she were standing before her own god, her very being compelled to admire his presence.

Kelan stood up again.

“This presence! He’s not human but a dragon?! This explains a lot!” he almost couldn’t believe it. “You…” Li Jinxi gritted her teeth as she struggled to remain standing before Yuan’s oppressive aura.

However, Yuan’s aura suddenly vanished. Although it was only active for a few seconds, and it wasn’t all of his aura, he’d knocked out the majority of the audience with his aura alone.

“So, are you still underestimating me, Miss Three to Four Realms?”

Li Jinxi clenched her fists in frustration, but she couldn’t respond. After a moment of silence, she silently tossed the Spirit-grade sword in her grasp away before using her own treasure.

“That’s more like it!” Yuan laughed out loud. ‘This son of a bitch is even crazier than Yuan!’ she cried inwardly.

With the Golden Empress in her grasp, Li Jinxi flew at Yuan. Sёarch* The novel(F~)ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

As she swung the Golden Empress, it suddenly became engulfed with a golden flame-like aura.

Seeing this golden flame, Yuan instinctively dodged it despite having fire immunity.

‘So he doesn’t just block anything and everything with his body, huh?’ Li Jinxi sneered inwardly.

The Golden Empress had a special ability that directly ignored all fire resistance, so it would’ve gone through Yuan’s fire resistance and damaged him. Naturally, the damage would’ve only been superficial even if the attack landed, and Yuan could’ve easily healed from the damage afterward, but he didn’t want to reveal his regenerative ability, as it could expose his true identity.

“Is dodging all you can do?! You were barking so damn loud just now, too!” Li Jinxi provoked when Yuan continued dodging her attacks without striking back.

“Was that pressure you displayed also just for show?!”

Yuan blocked her next strike with Enhanced Sword Aura and said, “I just didn’t want to end the fight so soon, but if you insist!”

Yuan began striking back without using any techniques, as it’d easily reveal his identity.

Li Jinxi noticed this detail and wondered if Yuan didn’t know any proper techniques at all since he’d spent all of his time improving other aspects.

‘His overall prowess is ridiculously high, but he lacks techniques! This is why his attacks thus far have been incredibly simple! This is his disadvantage!’ Realizing this, Li Jinxi became more confident in her chances of winning.

chapters.title: Chapter 1730 Reincarnators
chapters.body:
Chapter 1730 Reincarnators

With his True Dragon Awakening, Yuan was able to withstand Li Jinxi’s cultivation at the fourth level of Divine Grandmaster whilst only being a Spirit Grandmaster himself.

His Chaotic Dragon Core also assisted him even though he didn’t utilize any of his chaotic essence.

Li Jinxi’s swordsmanship was perfect, but in terms of combat experience, she was still far behind Yuan, even if her past experience was taken into account.

Li Jinxi was a formidable immortal in her previous life, but Yuan specialized in fighting immortals more than ordinary cultivators.

Li Jinxi, having fought many immortals before, could tell that the man before her was as formidable as one—if not even more powerful.

There was no opening in Yuan’s defense, and every time he strikes, he leaves her no room to counterattack.

Li Jinxi would normally risk attacking him, but knowing that a single mistake could end their match, she didn’t dare to risk it.

After several hundred exchanges, Yuan purposefully gave Li Jinxi the opportunity to attack, but he made sure not to make it too obvious.

Li Jinxi saw the chance and took it.

Her sword shone brightly as it unleashed one of its unique abilities.

[Golden Goddess!]

A golden silhouette suddenly emerged from the Golden Empress, taking the form of a beautiful female avatar who greatly resembled Li Jinxi.

This golden avatar was a manifestation of the Golden Empress’ memories of Jin Xi. This meant that the golden avatar had her fighting experience, techniques, and even habits. Its cultivation was even higher than Li Jinxi, being at the peak of Divine Grandmaster.

“One versus two, huh?” Yuan muttered with a slight smile on his face.

Once Jin Xi’s avatar began fighting alongside Li Jinxi, they were able to push Yuan back by constantly throwing powerful techniques at him.

[Golden Empress’ Sword Dance!]

Li Jinxi began executing a series of swift and powerful sword techniques, each faster and more threatening than the last.

Jin Xi’s avatar used her own technique at the same time, supporting Li Jinxi from the rear with long-distance sword techniques.

“Heavens! What a fight! This isn’t something you’d expect to witness in the Fourth Heaven!”

The audience watched with their mouths agape. Li Jinxi displayed techniques and combat prowess meant for immortals, and Yuan could somehow withstand them while only being at the peak of Spirit Grandmaster.

“Although that man from the upper heavens is a monster, that beauty is also a monster!”

“Two monsters fighting each other! I cannot imagine what sort of beings they’ll become in the future!”

“Their names will definitely spread throughout the Nine Heavens!”

The audience cheered louder and louder as the fight progressed.

“And this isn’t even all he can do!” Kelan’s body trembled with anticipation as he imagined fighting Yuan at his full prowess.

However, he then realized that if Li Jinxi failed to defeat Yuan, he would also lose his own chance to face Yuan in battle.

“Li Jinxi! You better defeat him, or I will not forgive you!” Kelan cried out in a desperate voice.

As their fight reached its climax, Yuan said to her through voice transmission, “You don’t fight like an ordinary genius. Are you, by any chance, a reincarnator?”

“Wha—?!”

Yuan’s sudden remarks took Li Jinxi by surprise, causing her to misstep, nearly falling on her face.

“Judging by your reaction, I guess I’d hit the mark.” Yuan continued to tease her.

“You… you are also a reincarnator?” Li Jinxi glared at him with a serious frown.

“Yes, and if I said that I have reincarnated more than once, would you believe me?”

Li Jinxi swallowed nervously.

‘Reincarnated more than once? That’s just like Yuan, but it explains his abnormal prowess,’ she thought inwardly.

Li Jinxi had suspected there would be more reincarnators like Yuan and herself after realizing it was possible, but she never had any solid proof.

As for Meixiu and Chu Liuxiang, they would only speak about their past lives with Yuan, so Li Jinxi was unaware of their background.

“What happened? Why did they suddenly stop fighting?” The spectators were puzzled when the two of them abruptly stopped their match.

“They appear to be talking with each other through voice transmission. I wonder what they are talking about.”

Nobody there dared to eavesdrop on their conversation, Kelan included, as it would be incredibly rude and offensive if caught.

‘If Li Jinxi has such a serious expression, it must be a fairly important subject.’ Kelan wanted to eavesdrop, but he feared it would mess with their chances to recruit him.

“What do you know about reincarnations?” Yuan asked Li Jinxi.

“Clearly not as much as you,” she said.

“If you defeat me, I’ll tell you. I can keep this up forever, but you are starting to run low on spiritual energy, right? How about unleashing your most powerful technique for your next strike? Otherwise, you’d suffer a boring defeat.”

Li Jinxi could tell that he was telling the truth just by his calm and relaxed composure. Meanwhile, she was sweating bullets and breathing heavily. Summoning Jin Xi’s avatar had exhausted a third of her reserve, and it needed a constant supply of spiritual energy to continue functioning.

‘At this rate, I’ll run out of spiritual energy in a few hours and lose by default…’ Li Jinxi gritted her teeth.

‘I refuse to lose in such a manner to someone who isn’t Yuan!’ Her eyes burned with determination.

Seeing Li Jinxi begin gathering her spiritual energy, Yuan smiled and spoke with enthusiasm, “Don’t disappoint me now!”

A few moments later, Li Jinxi finished gathering every drop of her remaining spiritual energy and unleashed it at Yuan.

[Golden Empress’ Absolute Decree!]

Li Jinxi raised the Golden Empress above her head as it grew until it became even larger than the stadium itself.

“H-heavens! Is she trying to destroy the entire stadium?!” Emperor Ning cried out loud.

“Don’t worry, I will ensure the safety of everyone, including the stadium,” Kelan assured him.

The next moment, Li Jinxi swung the massive sword at Yuan.

As the golden sword began descending, Yuan’s eyes flickered with a vicious glint.

[Sword God’s Apex Sword Art] Sёarch* The ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Wait, that’s—”

Li Jinxi’s eyes widened after seeing Yuan’s unique sword stance, but before she could say anything, her vision blanked, followed by a certain notification.

<You have died>

chapters.title: Chapter 1731 Killing Li Jinx
chapters.body:
Chapter 1731 Killing Li Jinx

The entire stadium became dead silent after Yuan killed Li Jinxi, most of it due to the system’s interference, as Li Jinxi was a player.

<Sword God’s Apex Sword Art learned>

Despite the notification, Yuan didn’t budge an inch, almost as if he’d become a stone statue. However, if one were to look underneath his clothes, one would see his entire body covered in sweat.

‘I accidentally killed her!’ Yuan cried inwardly after his mind slowly comprehended the situation.

The Sword God’s Apex Sword Art was a Celestial-rank technique, but Yuan figured Li Jinxi would be able to survive it even without the formation. Moreover, he’d only used a fraction of its power. Yet, the sword technique was much stronger than he’d anticipated, destroying the formation and killing Li Jinxi instantaneously.

He wanted to log off immediately and see if Li Jinxi was okay, but he was unable to do so. seaʀᴄh thё Nôvel(F)ire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

After snapping out of his daze, Yuan turned to look at Kelan and hastily shouted, “I-it was an accident! I swear I didn’t kill her on purpose!” However, Kelan didn’t react, almost as if he couldn’t hear Yuan’s voice, seemingly shocked about something.

‘That sword technique contained the power of the void! He’s managed to comprehend the power of the void before reaching the age of thirty?! There’s simply no way!’ Kelan cried inwardly.

The power of the void was something humans have been trying to master since its existence was revealed to the cultivation world. However, not a single human has ever fully mastered the power of the void to this very day.

Although Yuan only displayed a slight understanding of such power, it was still more than what Kelan and many immortals could comprehend.

As for Yuan, he’d accidentally used the power of the void when using Sword God’s Apex Sword Art because it felt natural, almost as if the technique was created to be used with the power of the void. Meanwhile, after dying to Yuan in Cultivation Online, Li Jinxi sat up on her bed with a dazed look on her face.

“I… died?” she subconsciously checked her body. A few seconds later, she sighed in relief.

‘I didn’t lose my ability to cultivate.’

Once she confirmed that the death didn’t affect her cultivation, Li Jinxi immediately began thinking about the technique that killed her.

‘That sword stance looked very familiar… I think…’

Her death had happened too fast, and she wasn’t able to get a good look at Yuan’s sword technique before she blacked out.

A few moments later, Li Jinxi could hear several pairs of footsteps running in the hallway, and they were all getting closer to her room.

Soon, her door was being knocked on.

“Sister Jinxi, are you okay?” Wang Bingbing’s voice resounded.

Li Jinxi went to open the door to find Wang Bingbing, Wang Xiuying, and Meixiu standing outside her room.

“Yes, I am fine. The death didn’t affect my cultivation,” Li Jinxi nodded.

“I can’t believe that man actually killed you! Wait until Yuan hears about this!” Wang Xiuying said.

“He said it was an accident, but who knows if he’s telling the truth or not…” Meixiu sighed.

“At least you didn’t lose your ability to cultivate, or that would’ve been disastrous.” Wang Bingbing said.

Li Jinxi clenched her hands tightly into fists, and she muttered, “I didn’t think I’d lose so miserably. I’m sorry for letting you all down.”

“Why are you the one apologizing when everyone lost to him? That man could give even a monster like Yuan a run for his money.” Wang Bingbing said.

“Speaking of Yuan, any news from him?”

Meixiu shook her head and said, “He was still asleep when I left the room just now.”

“I see…”

Sometime later, everyone but Li Jinxi returned to Cultivation Online to see Yuan being confronted by Kelan.

“I boastfully claimed that nobody would die under my watch just a second before you killed Li Jinxi. I am both angry and ashamed—not for you but for myself. However, even if I was aware of your intentions, I would not have been able to stop that attack unless my understanding of the power of the void surpasses your understanding, which it does not.”

“I didn’t intend to kill her. Truly.” Yuan sighed.

“I know, but since you’re from the upper heavens, you should already know that you didn’t really kill her.”

“Still, it could affect her cultivation.”

“Even if that’s the case, there are methods to restore her ability to cultivate.”

“What? Really?” Yuan was pleasantly surprised to hear this and inquired for more information.

“Just like how a crippled Dantian could be healed with treasures, there are methods to heal someone with a damaged soul. It’ll be costly, but it’s possible. In the case she lost her ability to cultivate due to a damaged soul, we—the Celestial Overlords— have the capability to heal her.”

“I see. The Celestial Overlords are truly capable,” Yuan said, relieved to learn that a player losing their ability to cultivate after death wasn’t necessarily permanent.

“By the way, do you belong to a faction?” Kelan suddenly asked.

“…Are you trying to recruit me now?”

Kelan scratched his head in a somewhat awkward manner, “Well… I do, but I won’t—because there will be someone even more amazing than me coming to recruit you soon.”

“What? Can you elaborate?” Yuan asked in a puzzled tone.

Kelan scratched his head a little harder, seemingly perplexed about something.

“Ah! Forget it! Since we’re already at this point, I might as well tell you now. One of the Celestial Overlords—the highest authority in our faction—will be coming down to personally recruit you soon.”

“What…?” Yuan muttered in a dazed voice after hearing this.

“This person… is it Celestial Overlord Xu?” he couldn’t help but ask.

“No, it’s someone else. Why did you mention Celestial Overlord Xu? Do you know her?” Kelan asked with raised eyebrows.

Yuan sighed after realizing the trouble coming their way and said to him through voice transmission, “Hey, let’s go somewhere private. I need to speak with you. Ah, bring the children you brought with you.”

“Huh? Sure.” Kelan casually nodded, not thinking much of Yuan’s request.

chapters.title: Chapter 1732 Revealing His Identity
chapters.body:
Chapter 1732 Revealing His Identity

Sometime later, Yuan and Kelan went over to Emperor Ning to say goodbye.

“Before you leave, can you give this reward to that young lady who won the tournament?” Emperor Ning handed the Innate Miracle Pill to Kelan.

Kelan brought the Demon Sealing Faction and Yuan to a nearby area afterward.

“How much time do we have before the Celestial Overlord arrives to recruit me?” Yuan inquired.

“We have a few more days. Why?”

“Alright. Let’s go to the Myriad of Techniques, then.”

“Why the Myriad of Techniques?” Kelan raised an eyebrow, feeling like Yuan was being oddly specific and familiar with their background.

“That’s Overlord Bai’s territory, no? What I have to tell you guys is incredibly important and cannot be leaked. I need a safe place to reveal this information to you.”

Kelan’s eyes widened. This was their first time meeting, so why does he have something so important to tell them?

“Does this have something to do with Yuan?” Meixiu suddenly asked with an observant face.

He silently nodded.

“Huh? Yuan? You know Yuan?” Everyone there looked at him with wide eyes.

“So you’ve known about us this entire time?!”

“I cannot reveal any more here.”

“…”

“Alright, I’ll bring us to the Myriad of Techniques right now.” Kelan retrieved a talisman and burned it with spiritual energy. The next moment, everyone around him was enveloped by a golden light before they vanished into thin air.

The scenery flickered before Yuan and the others, and before they realized it, they were suddenly in front of the Myriad of Techniques.

The talisman worked similarly to the teleport stones in the Primal Expanse, but they were incredibly valuable and limited in travel distance due to human’s lack of understanding of the power of the void. They entered the Myriad of Techniques and went straight into Senior Bai’s world, as Kelan had been given permission by Senior Bai to do so without his presence in case of emergencies.

Once they were inside Senior Bai’s world and protected from eavesdrops and the Celestial Emperor’s interference, Yuan said to them, “Before I begin, I would like to apologize for messing with you all. I kept my identity hidden because I couldn’t let the public know that I am still in Cultivation Online, and I wanted you all to experience what fighting me without knowing it’s me was like.”

“W-wait… that voice…” Chu Liuxiang muttered in a dazed voice after recognizing Yuan’s voice, as he’d stopped disguising his voice after entering Senior Bai’s world.

Yuan removed his mask, revealing his handsome face to them.

“Sorry, it was me this entire time,” he said with an apologetic smile.

“Y-YUAN?!?!” Kelan screamed, sounding like he’d just witnessed his ancestors crawling out of their graves.

“Y-you… it was you this entire time?!” Shi Lang exclaimed.

“Fucking fuck! I knew it! I knew something was off!” Wang Ming cried out loud.

“Damn! I really thought there was someone more monstrous than you for a moment there!” Xi Murong sighed loudly.

“Waaaah! You really scared me during the fight, Yuan!” Chu Liuxiang went to embrace him.

Xi Meili rubbed her eyes and sighed, “I knew your dragon scent was familiar, but there was something very different about it, and it threw me off.” seaʀᴄh thё nôvelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Yuan… when did you return?” Meixiu asked him.

“A few days before the tournament started. When I left the Primal Expanse, I found myself at the Ning Family’s residence, and upon hearing about the Grand Mystic Sect participating in the tournament, I figured I’d wait to see if you guys would show up.”

“After confirming that you guys were also participating, I wanted to see how much you guys have grown first-hand, hence my interference.”

“Umm… About Sister Jinxi…” Wang Bingbing looked at him with a puzzled expression on her face.

“Ah… yeah… I accidentally killed her. I’d like to personally apologize to her, but I cannot do that.” Yuan shook his head.

“What do you mean by that?” Meixiu asked with a slightly worried tone, sensing something was off.

“Before I say anything, Meili, Yingying, you’re still affected by the Celestial Emperor’s curse, so let’s have that removed first.” Yuan retrieved the Sacred Chalice of Purification.

“W-what kind of treasure is that?” Kelan finally snapped out of his daze and asked him.

“A treasure I acquired in the Primal Expanse. It has the ability to remove curses—including the one the Celestial Emperor placed on every being below the Seventh Heaven. This will allow her to understand our existence as players.”

“Such a powerful treasure exists?! The Celestial Emperor’s curse isn’t an ordinary curse!”

Xi Meili and Lan Yingying fed the chalice some of their blood, and a few moments later, they were no longer forced to ignore the existence of players.

Yuan explained their existence to them shortly after.

“Heavens… To think my way of thinking had been controlled by someone without my knowledge this entire time… how terrifying…” Xi Meili shivered from fear after realizing the truth.

“What a horrible thing to do to someone, and they didn’t even warn us…” Lan Yingying sighed.

“Alright, now that we’re all on the same page… The Celestial Emperor is targeting me for some reason, and my access to the system has been blocked. Basically, I’ve been banned from Cultivation Online.” Yuan revealed.

“W-what the hell? Something like that can happen?” Wang Xiuying muttered.

Yuan nodded and continued, “Once someone has their access to the system blocked, they will no longer be able to access Cultivation Online. In other words, they won’t be able to enter the Nine Heavens.”

“Wait. If that’s the case, how are you still here?” Kelan asked.

“Don’t ask me how or why, but I’ve been given access to another system, allowing me to stay inside Cultivation Online. However, there are a few issues, one of them being that I am no longer allowed to log off and return to Earth.”

“What?!” Chu Liuxiang and the others cried out loud.

“This is why I didn’t notify any of you of my return and why I cannot apologize to Li Jinxi until she returns to Cultivation Online after her death cooldown.”

“If you cannot log out… What are you going to do now?” Meixiu asked once everyone comprehended the situation.

“I will need to reestablish the connection between Earth and the Nine Heavens through the Dragon Spiral Mountain as soon as possible, allowing me to travel to the Nine Heavens with my real body, and I have to do so without alerting the Celestial Emperor,” he said.
