chapters.title: Chapter 1733 Inevitable Involvement
chapters.body:
Chapter 1733 Inevitable Involvement

“So your destination is the Dragon Spiral Mountain in the Seventh Heaven, huh. Do you intend on going straight there?” Kelan asked after hearing his plans.

“That… I am not sure yet. I am afraid that I might reveal my presence if I ascend normally,” Yuan shook his head.

When a player successfully ascends to the next realm, there will always be an announcement, and it paves the way for the other players, allowing everyone to ascend.

“However, if I wait for the other players to ascend for me, it’d take way too long.”

Kelan then asked, “Why do you have to evade the Celestial Emperor, anyway? Did you do something to offend him?”

“I don’t know.” Yuan shook his head.

In reality, he had an idea why the Celestial Emperor was hunting him, but he didn’t want to reveal information about his past lives if he didn’t need to. Besides the Demon Sealing Faction and those following him, nobody else was aware of his past lives.

“The Celestial Overlords could help you, you know? I don’t know about the rest of the faction, but Celestial Overlord Xu wouldn’t hesitate to confront the Celestial Emperor for you. If you reveal your talents to the others, I’m sure they’d also be willing to assist you.” Kelan said.

Being one of the most powerful factions in the Nine Heavens, the Celestial Overlords had more than just fame to their name. If they truly wanted, they could force even someone as powerful as the Celestial Emperor to act.

However, Yuan immediately rejected that idea and said, “No, I cannot involve the faction, especially when the Celestial Emperor is clearly hostile toward me. I don’t want to cause you guys trouble.”

Kelan frowned and said, “Wrong. As you’re already a member of the Celestial Overlords, we are obligated to protect you—unless you committed treason or did something we cannot defend, such as murdering innocent mortals or being a demon worshiper.”

“Did you do anything that would harm our reputation?” Kelan asked him with raised eyebrows, his tone somewhat nervous. He truly wouldn’t be surprised if Yuan had done something he wasn’t supposed to do.

“What? Of course not. I did no such thing in this life.” Yuan said.

Kelan found Yuan’s phrasing a little weird and oddly specific, but he didn’t think too much about it.

“Then there should be no problem with the Celestial Overlords helping you. If the Celestial Emperor is truly hunting you down, we will have to get involved sooner or later.”

“…”

Yuan fell silent. He wasn’t sure if he should get the Celestial Overlords involved.

‘No… Now that I think about it, the Celestial Overlords clashing with the Celestial Emperor might be inevitable since the Celestial Overlords was founded by one of my past lives…’

After a moment of silence, Yuan nodded, “Okay. However, don’t let the Celestial Emperor know that I am still in Cultivation Online—the Nine Heavens, just in case.”

Kelan nodded, “I will let Celestial Overlord Xu know… Hell, you can tell her yourself. I don’t know if I will be able to convince her not to kick down the Celestial Emperor’s door in anger.”

“I can tell her myself. When can I speak with her?”

“Since you’re here, we can ask Overlord Bai to bring her to this world. We also need to resolve the situation with Celestial Overlord Qing, who will be coming to recruit you.”

“You can deal with her. You were the one who caused this mess, after all.” Yuan quickly said.

“What?! How can you put all the blame on me?! If you hadn’t hidden your identity, I wouldn’t have contacted them!”

“We can talk about that later. Right now, let’s focus on how I can ascend to the Seventh Heaven without alerting the Celestial Emperor.” Yuan then said.

Wang Xiuying suddenly spoke, “What if you have someone bring you to the Seventh Heaven, like how I was brought to the Second Heaven after the Mystic Realm?”

Kelan then said, “No, that won’t work. We cannot bring anyone without restriction. There are rules and guidelines we have to follow. Bringing someone to the Second Heaven from the Lower Heaven may have fewer restrictions, but the upper heavens are different. At the end of the day, we won’t be able to bring him to the upper heavens while hiding his identity.”

“Can’t you just sneak him there?” Wu Zao asked.

“There is a barrier that separates every realm in the Nine Heavens, and it will prevent unauthorized beings from passing. We can force our way through it, but it’d instantly alert the Celestial Emperor.” Kelan explained. S~eaʀᴄh the NôᴠeFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“How about we climb the Stairway to Heaven for you?” Meixiu suddenly suggested. “I think we have a good chance of ascending at our current level.”

Kelan looked at her and said, “You’ll probably have no issue ascending to the Fifth Heaven and even the Sixth Heaven, but the Seventh Heaven is a different beast. Besides Yuan and Li Jinxi, I don’t think anyone here will be able to pass it anytime soon.”

However, Yuan shook his head and said, “It’s fine. I think I have another method to ascend without alerting the Celestial Emperor.”

“Oh? What method?” Kelan looked at him with genuine intrigue. “I don’t know, but I know someone who might,” he said.

Yuan continued, “Anyway, now that we understand my situation, let’s talk about you guys.”

“Us?” Wang Ming and the others pointed at themselves.

“Yes, you guys. To avoid the Celestial Emperor coming after you guys for being associated with me, I will have to limit our contact for now. And since you’ve done so well training with Kelan for the last three years, I figured it’d be fine if you continued to follow and train under him.”

Yuan looked at Kelan and asked, “You wouldn’t mind taking care of them for a little longer, right? I can ask Senior Bai if you’re too busy.”

“No, I don’t mind. In fact, I already have plans for them since none of them were able to defeat you during the tournament.” Kelan responded with a cold smile.

The Demon Sealing Faction members trembled after seeing his smile.

“However, it won’t be for free. In exchange for my assistance, I want you to spar with me right here and now.” Kelan looked at Yuan with an excited smile.

chapters.title: Chapter 1734 Kelan'S Plan
chapters.body:
Chapter 1734 Kelan'S Plan

“What’s with you and sparring?” Yuan sighed. “How about this? Instead of sparring with a suppressed cultivation, we will have a real fight when I catch up to your cultivation. You’ll also have more fun that way.”

“What? That’ll take forever! I want to fight you right now!” Kelan said, sounding like a spoiled brat.

Yuan rubbed his eyes and said in a defeated voice, “Fine, I’ll spar with you, but it’ll only be for a little.”

“That’s more like it!” Kelan immediately suppressed his cultivation to match Yuan.

“Are you sure? You can increase your cultivation a little more if you want.” Yuan said. “Yes, I’ll be fine. I won’t complain even if you defeat me in an instant,” Kelan nodded.

Yuan turned to look at the others and said, “Can you give us some space? The further, the better.”

Once Meixiu and the others were far enough, Yuan said to Kelan.

“I will come at you with my full strength. Don’t blame me if I kill you by accident.”

Yuan activated True Dragon Awakening and unleashed his aura in the next instant. An overwhelming pressure, several times more intense than what he had displayed during the tournament, permeated throughout Senior Bai’s world, causing those who sensed it to tremble uncontrollably.

“H-heavens! What he showed us during the tournament was only a fraction of his true power?!” Xi Murong cried out loud.

Kelan wore a nervous smile on his face after sensing Yuan’s aura, and he began regretting not taking Yuan’s offer moments ago.

Seeing his expression, Yuan smiled and said, “It’s not too late to increase your cultivation.”

“N-n-no… I am fine…” Kelan responded, stuttering at the beginning.

“Suit yourself.” Yuan retrieved Number One Under Heaven and activated God of War’s Astral Arts next.

BOOM!

Yuan’s aura continued to skyrocket, and just his presence alone was enough to cause instability in Senior Bai’s world.

“Wait a second! This is just a spar! Are you really trying to kill me?!” Kelan exclaimed after sensing real killing intent from Yuan’s gaze.

“What are you talking about? You’re the one who asked for this.” Yuan further strengthened his aura with his chaotic essence, causing cracks to appear in the world.

‘Fuck! He’ll kill all of us at this rate!’ Kelan cried inwardly.

He raised his hand and sighed the next moment, “Alright, you win. We can postpone our match until we have a proper stage to fight on. Since Overlord Bai isn’t here, the world is much weaker, and it will definitely collapse if we fight here.”

Yuan smiled and said, “If you say so.”

His overwhelming presence instantly vanished, and the world began repairing itself shortly after. Sёarᴄh the nôvelFire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Once everything returned to normal, Kelan said, “I plan on bringing your friends to the upper heavens—to my sect, where I will be able to train them properly.”

“Can you bring so many people with you at once?” Yuan inquired.

“Of course. It’s going to be a little expensive, but I can manage. I can even bring you along—if you don’t mind your identity being revealed, that is.” “Thanks for the offer, but I should be fine.”

“Where are you going to take us, exactly?” Chu Liuxiang asked.

“The Seventh Heaven.”

“!!!”

“So we won’t be able to see each other until Yuan reaches the Seventh Heaven…? I don’t know if I like the sound of that…” Chu Liuxiang muttered with a perplexed expression on her face.

She wouldn’t have minded if Yuan could log off, as they would still be able to meet on Earth. However, with Yuan unable to log off, they were essentially separated for an unknown period of time.

“It could be years before we see each other again!”

Meixiu and the others also became dejected after realizing this.

“It’s just a few years. I promise I will try to reach the Seventh Heaven as soon as possible. Also, it’s not like we won’t be able to see each other. Look at where we are now. We can still meet in the Myriad of Techniques since it can be accessed from every realm.” Yuan said.

“Now that you mention it…” Chu Liuxiang couldn’t believe that she’d forgotten about the Myriad of Techniques despite being there currently. “If we can still see each other here, I guess I won’t mind going to the Seventh Heaven…”

Yuan nodded with a smile.

However, someone there suddenly said, “Sorry, but I won’t be going to the Seventh Heaven with you guys.”

“Me too.” Another voice followed.

Everyone there turned to look at the two individuals who had just spoken. It was Lan Yingying and Xi Meili.

“As Yuan’s servant, I am obligated to follow him.” Lan Yingying said.

Xi Meili then spoke, “I left the Ancient Dragon City to explore the world with Yuan. Although my prowess has improved significantly during the last three years, and I would surely become even stronger if I continued training in the Seventh Heaven, that is not my priority right now.”

“Thank you for training me, but I will be staying with Yuan.”

Lan Yingying and Xi Meili bowed to Kelan, who nodded with an understanding smile.

“Since you’re both divine beasts, what I can teach you is very limited, and you’re probably better off traversing the world with Yuan,” he said.

Kelan turned to look at the other and said, “We’ll set off to the Seventh Heaven in a few weeks. I’ll leave for a bit to contact Overlord Bai and Celestial Overlord Xu now.”

Kelan disappeared shortly after.

Once they were alone, Yuan said to them, “By the way, I have one more surprise for you guys.”

“Feng Feng, you can come out now.”

“What?” Xi Meili’s eyes widened after hearing her name.

Feng Yuxiang appeared the next moment with a bright smile on her face.

“It’s been a while, everyone.”

“Feng Feng! You’re back!”

Xi Meili almost cried tears of happiness after seeing that Feng Yuxiang had managed to survive.

Feng Yuxiang noticed Xi Meili staring at her and smiled, “I’m glad to see that you’re also fine.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1735 Growing Stronger Without Doing Anything
chapters.body:
Chapter 1735 Growing Stronger Without Doing Anything

“Sorry if I made you guys worry. As you can see, I am perfectly alive and healthy. Moreover, I’ve come back even stronger!” Feng Yuxiang spoke with a prideful manner, her nose pointed toward the sky.

“Now that you mention it, your appearance and presence is different from before… How did you grow stronger while in such a state?” Xi Meili asked with raised eyebrows.

While most beings experience exponential growth after near-death experiences, Feng Yuxiang’s situation was different.

“A lot has happened, but I learned about my past life as a Primordial Phoenix, and now that my curse has been lifted, my sealed cultivation, bloodline, and talents will gradually return to me, especially once I remember my past life’s experience. I will essentially become stronger without needing to do anything.”

“What…? Growing stronger without doing anything? How envious…” Chu Liuxiang sighed. seaʀᴄh thё NôᴠeFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Feng Yuxiang cleared her throat and said, “In case you forgot, I was already a powerful phoenix before my bloodline was sealed, so it’s not like my strength appears from thin air.”

“By the way, where’s Xiao Hua?” Lan Yingying asked when she noticed her absence.

“Oh… she’s…” Yuan began explaining Xiao Hua’s situation to them.

“Two souls… in a single soul? Such a thing is possible?” Everyone was shocked by the information and the perplexing situation.

“At least she’s not in any immediate danger,” Meixiu said.

Yuan nodded, “Yes, she’s doing fine now, but we cannot be certain she’ll remain like this forever.”

“So what are you going to do now?” Wang Xiuying asked him.

“Once you guys leave for the Seventh Heaven, I will figure out how to ascend without alerting the Celestial Emperor. I also need to ask Tian’er some questions, so I’ll visit the Stairway to Heaven.”

‘There’s also Dong Ye. He can traverse between the Nine Heavens without alerting anyone, so there has to be a way.’

The first person that came to mind when he thought about ascending the Nine Heavens in secrecy was Dong Ye, who has been successfully evading the Celestial Emperor since the Evil God’s era.

“Forget about that for now. I have an even more important question,” Chu Liuxiang suddenly said. “How often should we meet each other?”

“That’s hard to say and probably not something we can make a schedule around. However, I will try to meet with you guys whenever I can,” said Yuan.

“Oh, right, I have another surprise for all of you.” “Another one?! How many surprises do you have?!” Wang Ming exclaimed.

Yuan cleared his throat and said, “You can show yourself now, Yingzi.”

“What? Yingzi?” The Demon Sealing Faction instantly recognized this familiar name.

The next moment, a shadowy silhouette appeared from Yuan’s shadow.

“T-that’s Yingzi?! She looks so different!” Wang Bingbing said.

“Ah, she changed her appearance right before we returned to the Primal Expanse,” Yuan explained.

“Hello, everyone. I didn’t think I would be able to see you all again.” Yingzi greeted them with a slight head nod.

“What a striking appearance. You could easily be mistaken as a ghost in the dark.” Chu Liuxiang commented.

“Wait, so you’re following Yuan now?” Shi Lang asked.

She nodded, “For now, because he plans on returning to the Primal Expanse in the future, and I have the ability to take him back. I am also interested in your world.”

“How do you feel now? Will you be fine without chaotic essence?” Yuan asked her.

Yingzi nodded and said, “Just like when I visited your world, I don’t have any issues living here. However, if I use my powers, I won’t be able to replenish them, so I will only grow weaker as I use my powers.”

“What kind of beast are you?” Xi Meili asked in a curious voice.

“I’m a Shadow Dweller,” she said.

“Shadow Dweller…?” Xi Meili found this name familiar for some reason. Then she recalled the Chronicles of the Immortal Monarch, mentioning that one of the Nine Divine Supremes was a Shadow Dweller.

‘A Shadow Dweller, Feng Yuxiang, who is a phoenix, Lan Yingying, who is a serpent, and myself, who is a dragon… What a terrifying coincidence… or is this fate?’ Xi Meili swallowed nervously after noticing that Yuan had a following that was nearly identical to the Immortal Monarch’s Nine Divine Supremes.

After chatting for a little, Yingzi returned to Yuan’s shadow. Feng Yuxiang also returned to Yuan’s Dantian after saying, “Perhaps it’s due to my body trying to adapt to my bloodline’s awakening, but I feel more exhausted than usual despite not doing anything. I’ll go take a nap. See you guys later.”

Lan Yingying stepped forward next and said, “It’s been a while since I have done this…” She proceeded to turn into a snake before wrapping herself around Yuan’s wrist, turning into a bracelet.

‘How envious… I also want to rest inside his body…’ Xi Meili sighed inwardly, and she started contemplating forming a contract with Yuan just so she could rest inside him like the others.

“We have some time before Kelan returns, so why don’t I tell you guys about my adventure inside the Primal Expanse?” Yuan said.

“Please do!” They exclaimed excitedly.

They found a nearby floating pavilion and sat around Yuan like a group of children during story time.

Yuan recalled his experience inside the Primal Expanse from the moment of his arrival.

Meanwhile, Kelan met with Senior Bai and Xu Jiaqi inside the Celestial Overlords’ Headquarters. Since the place was located inside a secluded world within its own dimension, members of the Celestial Overlords were able to access it from anywhere in the Nine Heavens.

“What’s going on, Kelan? Why did you summon us? Celestial Overlord Qing should be arriving in the Fourth Heaven soon.” Senior Bai inquired.

Kelan scratched his head, looking perplexed as he pondered how he should reveal the news to them.

“Just spit it out already.” Xu Jiaqi urged him with a slight frown.

Kelan’s body trembled, and he hastily responded, “Yuan has returned from the Primal Expanse, and he turned out to be the mysterious man from the upper heavens!”

“What…?” Their eyes widened with disbelief.

chapters.title: Chapter 1736 Internal Strife
chapters.body:
Chapter 1736 Internal Strife

“W-wait a second. Repeat that, and speak slower this time.” Senior Bai said, doubting his hearing just now.

Kelan cleared his throat and repeated in a slow and clear voice, “Yuan has returned from the Primal Expanse, and the mysterious monster surnamed Xiao I wanted you to recruit turned out to be Yuan in disguise… I just found this out…”

“Yuan is back from the Primal Expanse…?” Xu Jiaqi muttered in a dazed voice.

“You… you fool! How could you put Yuan’s existence in the spotlight when we’ve been trying to keep him hidden for as long as possible!” Senior Bai began lecturing him after comprehending the situation. “Now, the majority of the upper ranks and even two Celestial Overlords are aware of him!”

“B-but you were the one who revealed this information to them… I only wanted you to speak with Celestial Overlord Xu…” Kelan said.

“…” Senior Bai fell silent. He wanted to rebuke Kelan, but he found himself unable to do so, as he’d realized his own mistake.

“What should we do now, Lady Xu? It’ll only be a matter of time before the rest of the faction is aware of Yuan’s existence. The other groups will definitely try to covet him.” Senior Bai looked at Xu Jiaqi.

“I think we can still keep his existence hidden. After all, nobody but the three of us are aware of the truth. We can just have Yuan continue his disguise for the time being.” Kelan said.

Xu Jiaqi nodded, “That’s a good idea. However, he’s still part of our faction, which poses some risks and problems.”

In fact, Kelan could’ve figured out Yuan’s identity if he’d tried to locate Yuan since he has access to the location of all members with a lower rank. However, since he didn’t know Yuan had left the Primal Expanse, he had no reason to try to locate Yuan or suspect his disguise, something that had to be done manually.

Another way of exposing Yuan would be to use an identifying talisman, which will automatically glow if they are near a member of the faction.

Commanders like Kelan and those of higher rank possess the ability to forcefully reveal a member’s rank. However, since no one would suspect that Yuan is already part of the Celestial Overlords, the likelihood of this happening remains low.

“What if we temporarily remove him from the faction?” Kelan suddenly suggested.

“…”

Senior Bai and Xu Jiaqi frowned at his outrageous suggestion.

“Why are you looking at me like that? You trust him, right? In that case, you don’t really have to worry about him going off to another faction.”

Kelan’s suggestion was logical and would definitely work. However, Xu Jiaqi didn’t like the idea of removing Yuan from the faction, as it’d reset his progress and contributions to the faction. In other words, Yuan would have to start from the lowest rank when he rejoins the faction in the future.

“If you’re worried about his rank… Well, there’s not much we can really do about that. However, it’s probably still better than exposing his existence to the faction, which will definitely stir the already unstable hornet’s nest.”

Xu Jiaqi clenched her fists. She wanted to keep Yuan a secret from the rest of the faction until he was strong enough or she had enough control of the faction.

‘If the others find out about Yuan, they will definitely fight over him, especially that madman. I have to avoid this at all costs since it will, without a doubt, give the Celestial Overlords a good reason to divide the faction finally.’

The Celestial Overlords may seem perfect in the eyes of outsiders, but there was a serious power struggle within the upper ranks, with each Celestial Overlord trying to gain more authority and control over the other.

“While he may lose his progress, we can still give him special promotions.” Senior Bai suddenly said. “He’s only a General right now, and Kelan has the authority to promote an Apprentice to a Warrior, even Captain. I can promote him to General, and if needed, Lady Xu could promote him even further.”

“I thought we cannot give out special promotions willy-nilly?” Kelan said.

“No, but there are ways around that. We can discuss that in the future.” Senior Bai said.

“Alright. Let’s remove Yuan from the faction temporarily for his safety and the stability of our faction.” Xu Jiaqi eventually made a decision.

“There’s another issue, but you will have to hear it from Yuan.” Kelan then said.

“Why can’t you tell us here?” Xu Jiaqi frowned. Sёarᴄh the nôvel_Fire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Because you probably won’t believe me, and Yuan requested this himself.” Kelan shrugged.

“Fine. He’s in the Myriad of Techniques right now, right?” “Indeed.”

“You can return first.” Senior Bai said.

“Yes!”

Kelan bowed to them before leaving.

“I hope he won’t hold a grudge for removing him, even if it’s only temporary…” Xu Jiaqi sighed.

“Yuan isn’t that kind of person. He’ll surely understand why it must be done.” Senior Bai said.

Kelan returned to the Myriad of Techniques sometime later.

“Where’s Senior Bai and Miss Xu?” Yuan asked Kelan when he returned.

“They’ll be coming later. While we wait for them, I need to talk to you for a moment.” Kelan looked at him with a serious expression.

“What happened?”

Kelan proceeded to explain the situation and that they had decided to temporarily remove him from the faction.

“Miss Xu is worried that my existence might start a war in the faction and divide them, so she’d been keeping my existence a secret this entire time?” Yuan was dumbfounded after hearing this information.

Kelan sighed and explained, “You will eventually learn about this, so I might as well tell you now. Our situation isn’t as good as it appears from the outside. Due to the other Celestial Overlords’ ambitions, the faction has been split into four different groups, and we have internal fights almost daily. For the record, I am on Celestial Overlord Xu’s side, and you’re technically also on our side since she personally recruited you.”

“Divided into four groups? Internal fights? Ambitions? What the hell is happening to my faction?” Yuan muttered in a dazed voice.

“Your faction?” Kelan raised an eyebrow at his weird phrasing.

chapters.title: Chapter 1737 Fateful Reincarnation
chapters.body:
Chapter 1737 Fateful Reincarnation

“Hm? Is that what I said?” Yuan casually shrugged it off and continued, “Anyway, I understand the situation, and I don’t blame Miss Xu for her decision.”

“You can tell her that yourself when she arrives in a bit. Also, you have to tell her about your situation with the Celestial Emperor,” said Kelan.

Yuan nodded, “I will.”

Sometime later, a rift appeared in the world, and two individuals emerged from it.

It was Senior Bai and Xu Jiaqi.

“…”

Senior Bai and Xu Jiaqi’s eyebrows twitched when they sensed Yuan’s powerful presence despite his efforts to suppress it.

‘Just what did he experience in the Primal Expanse to grow so much in just three years?’ Senior Bai swallowed nervously. Meanwhile, Xu Jiaqi was fixated on Yuan’s face the moment she saw it. While she was surprised by his growth, she was even more shocked by something else.

In the three years since Xu Jiaqi had last seen Yuan, he had grown noticeably more mature and handsome. Most striking, however, was that he now bore an uncanny resemblance to the founder of the Celestial Overlords, a similarity that was impossible to ignore.

Although Xu Jiaqi had also noticed this before, she subconsciously ignored it—pretended like it was only her imagination. However, she could no longer ignore their resemblance. “Father…?” Xu Jiaqi muttered in a low voice, her eyes trembling with emotion.

“What?” Senior Bai turned to look at her with raised eyebrows after hearing her mumbling.

“Sorry for making you guys worry, but as you can see, I have returned,” Yuan said to them.

However, Xu Jiaqi acted like she didn’t hear Yuan and approached him in a dazed manner.

She placed a hand on his face and stared at it with intense concentration.

“C-Celestial Overlord Xu…?” Kelan tried calling out to her, but she ignored him.

‘Heaven Refining Physique… your uncanny resemblance to him…’ Xu Jiaqi mumbled inwardly with a profound expression on her face, almost as if she’d realized something.

Without looking away from Yuan, she suddenly said, “Leave us alone for a moment.”

“Yes.”

Noticing her serious tone, Senior Bai and Kelan didn’t question her request and immediately left them alone.

Even after they left, Xu Jiaqi proceeded to envelop Yuan and herself in a concealment formation and a sound-blocking formation.

“What’s the matter, Miss Xu?” Yuan asked her after seeing her actions.

“Do you believe in reincarnation?” Xu Jiaqi suddenly asked with a solemn face.

“Yes, I do.” “What do you know about reincarnation?” she then asked.

“As long as their soul is preserved, everyone who dies will eventually reincarnate, but they won’t retain the memories of their past life.” Yuan gave a brief explanation.

“That is the basics of reincarnation, but did you know that those with a powerful fate could reincarnate into someone who closely resembles their previous life? Some even regain fragments of memories and talents from those lives.” Xu Jiaqi revealed.

“For example, once a Sword Immortal with a powerful fate for the sword perishes, there is a good chance their second life is also fated to walk the path of the sword, becoming a Sword Immortal again. We call this phenomenon Fateful Reincarnation, and the people who have experienced this Fateful Reincarnation are called True Reincarnators.” Sёarch* The NôᴠeFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Fateful Reincarnation…” Yuan mumbled.

“Don’t be surprised to hear what I am about to say, but I believe you are a True Reincarnator who has experienced Fateful Reincarnation, and you were the founder of the Celestial Overlords in your previous life.” Xu Jiaqi suddenly said, unaware that she’d perfectly struck the nail on its head.

Before Yuan could even say anything, she continued, “The founder of the Celestial Overlords also had the Heaven Refining Physique, and your resemblance is uncanny. I’d be willing to wager my life that you’re the founder’s reincarnation.”

“…”

Yuan couldn’t say anything, as he was absolutely bewildered that someone had actually managed to come to this conclusion.

After a moment of silence, Yuan spoke, “Even if what you say is true… What should I do?”

Xu Jiaqi pondered for a moment before responding, “Just continue doing what you normally do. Since you are a True Reincarnator, your future has already been prophesied, and your path has already been determined. While there is a chance that your future might change, it can only be affected by those who are aware of your identity as a True Reincarnator, hence why you must not reveal this information to anyone—not even to Kelan or Overlord Bai. Do you understand me?”

“I understand…” Yuan nodded in a dazed manner.

True Reincarnators are an exceptionally rare existence, and they typically only appear in the Ninth Heaven, where some of the most powerful individuals with powerful fates exist, hence why almost nobody is aware of their existence in the lower heavens.

“Thank the heavens you’ve returned to us in our time of need… I can finally breathe properly for once…” Xu Jiaqi released a deep and profound sigh.

Nobody but Xu Jiaqi herself could understand the waves of emotions affecting her heart at this moment, but she’d managed to control herself and maintain her dignified demeanor. Yet, she was unable to prevent tears from forming in her eyes, causing her sight to become blurry.

Yuan wondered if he should just tell Xu Jiaqi that he’d already begun recalling Tian Yingzhe’s memories but decided to wait until he had enough memories before doing so.

‘She’s already convinced that I am Tian Yingzhe’s reincarnation, so there’s no point telling her. Once I regain enough of Tian Yingzhe’s memories, I’ll have a proper conversation with her.’

Sometime later, once Xu Jiaqi had full control again, she spoke, “I assume Kelan had told you about our situation before we showed up.”

Yuan nodded, “Yes, he told me about the Celestial Overlords’ internal strife and how the faction is essentially divided into four different factions.”

“Indeed. Now that I’ve confirmed you to be our founder’s reincarnation, we definitely cannot allow the others to realize your existence, or things will get messy really fast, and they will definitely hunt you down before you can regain your memories or affect the faction.” Xu Jiaqi spoke in a solemn voice.

“But worry not. I will protect you even at the cost of my life, just as you’d done for me in the past.”

A slight smile appeared on Xu Jiaqi’s face.

chapters.title: Chapter 1738 Removed From The Celestial Overlords
chapters.body:
Chapter 1738 Removed From The Celestial Overlords

“And once you’ve become a True Immortal, I will help you regain your previous position in the Celestial Overlords.” Xu Jiaqi said.

Originally, she planned on uniting the Celestial Overlords by herself, but with Yuan’s Fateful Reincarnation, she tossed all of her initial plans down the drain to help Yuan ascend the Celestial Overlords. She was confident that Yuan had reincarnated to help the Celestial Overlords.

Sometime later, Xu Jiaqi retrieved Yuan’s Celestial Overlords symbol and rank, essentially removing him from the faction.

“Don’t worry, the ranks are meaningless to you, who will ascend beyond the Celestial Overlords in the future.” Xu Jiaqi said to him.

“I am not worried, nor do I blame you for making this decision. As a matter of fact, I was going to ask you to temporarily remove me from the faction because of a certain incident.”

“What incident? Is this related to why Kelan asked me to speak with you?” Xu Jiaqi frowned.

Yuan nodded and said, “You understand the existence of people like me—players, right?”

She nodded, “Of course.”

“Then are you aware that we require the system to visit the Nine Heavens?”

“I don’t have a full understanding of the system since we natives of the Nine Heavens don’t have anything like that, but I know it’s something that assists the people from your world—these players in the Nine Heavens.”

“Yes, that’s the gist of it. Anyway, without the system, we players cannot travel to the Nine Heavens with our souls, and the Celestial Emperor has control of that system.”

After a slight pause, he continued, “The Celestial Emperor has blocked my access to the system, meaning I can no longer enter the Nine Heavens—”

“He what?!” Xu Jiaqi roared as killing intent exploded from her being.

“Let me finish…” Sёarch* The ηovёlFire .net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Yuan hastily continued, “However, due to a miracle, I was able to remain in the Nine Heavens, but I am no longer able to log off—return to my world. I am basically stuck in the Nine Heavens as a piece of my soul until I reach the Seventh Heaven and visit the Dragon Spiral Mountain there.”

“Do you know why the Celestial Emperor acted against you?” Xu Jiaqi asked. “If not, I will go to the Celestial Emperor and demand an answer even if I must be forceful.”

“Please don’t. And it most likely has something to do with my Fateful Reincarnation.”

“Wait…” Xu Jiaqi’s face suddenly became contemplative.

‘Now that I think about it, while Father would occasionally assist the Celestial Emperor with the faction, he never personally assisted the Celestial Emperor. He even refused to be associated with the Celestial Emperor to remain a neutral faction…’

Xu Jiaqi began recalling the finer details of her memories with Tian Yingzhe, who would never smile while in the presence of the Celestial Emperor despite always smiling everywhere else.

‘Did Father have some sort of grievance with the Celestial Emperor…?’ Xu Jiaqi wondered inwardly. ‘Did the Celestial Emperor have something to do with Father’s disappearance—death?’

“If you speak to the Celestial Emperor, he would realize that I am still in the Nine Heavens, and there is also the possibility that he has people in the Celestial Overlords working for him,” Yuan said.

“If you put it like that, I agree we shouldn’t let the Celestial Emperor know. But will you be fine?”

He nodded with a calm smile, “I will be fine. I just need to reach the Seventh Heaven without alerting the Celestial Emperor.”

“In any case, the Celestial Overlords’ situation isn’t good, so you should avoid confronting the Celestial Emperor for the time being.”

“Alright, I will try my best to not affect your fate since I am now aware of your Fateful Reincarnation, but if I sense that you are in danger, I will act, regardless of our opponent’s identity or the costs.” Xu Jiaqi said with a stern look on her face.

“Thank you.” Yuan nodded with a warm smile.

“By the way, how should I deal with the Celestial Overlord coming to recruit me?”

“Do as you wish, but try to refuse without offending her. While she may belong to a different group, her group and my group are quite friendly toward each other, unlike the other two Celestial Overlords.” Xu Jiaqi explained.

“If possible, I would like to get her on our side, which will help us in taking care of the rats.”

Yuan nodded, “I understand. I will try my best.”

Xu Jiaqi suddenly lunged forward and embraced Yuan, pressing her soft breasts against his chest. They were so close that Yuan could feel her heartbeat.

“Miss Xu…”

“Call me Jiaqi when we are alone,” she said firmly, her tone leaving no room for refusal.

“Alright, Jiaqi.” Yuan returned her embrace.

He could feel her body trembling slightly at his touch.

After remaining in that position for several moments, Xu Jiaqi released him and deactivated the formations.

“You guys can come back,” she said out loud.

Kelan and Senior Bai instantly appeared before them.

“I have temporarily removed Yuan from the faction, so nobody in the faction, excluding the three of us, knows of his relationship with us. I shouldn’t need to say this, but if this information is leaked…” Xu Jiaqi glared at Kelan as a warning to him.

While she didn’t think Kelan would betray them, she knew he had a big mouth and would occasionally say things he shouldn’t or didn’t mean to.

“Don’t worry, Celestial Overlord Xu. You can rest assured that I won’t utter about Yuan to anyone.”

“I am really sorry things have escalated to this point, Yuan. I know you don’t blame us, but I still want to offer my sincere apology.” Senior Bai said to him.

“We will definitely help you regain your rank once you return.”

Yuan chuckled and joked, “My rank will mean nothing if the faction goes to shit because of me.”

“What are you going to do now that you’re back in the Nine Heavens?” Senior Bai then asked.

“I will ascend,” he responded with determination.

chapters.title: Chapter 1739 Celestial Overlord Qiang
chapters.body:
Chapter 1739 Celestial Overlord Qiang

“Celestial Overlord Xu, I am going to bring his friends to my sect in the Seventh Heaven, and I am going to train them until Yuan makes his way there,” Kelan said to Xu Jiaqi after Yuan’s matter was settled.

She nodded, “I understand. I will help you with the arrangements.”

Kelan bowed to her, “Thank you!”

With someone powerful like Xu Jiaqi helping him, their process would speed up greatly.

“You will have about a week left with them,” Kelan said to Yuan afterward.

Senior Bai made a gesture with his hands. Moments later, a floating island with a large house approached them from a distance at the speed of sound, stopping right before them.

“You can use this place as you wish until then,” he said.

“Thank you.” Yuan nodded.

Upon closer inspection, there was a beautiful still pond and even a training ground on the island.

“Then I will leave now to deal with your friends’ matters. Don’t forget about what we talked about.” Xu Jiaqi said to Yuan.

He nodded, “I won’t.”

Once Xu Jiaqi and the others left, the Demon Sealing Faction gathered on the island.

There, each member recalled their training experience with Kelan.

Li Jinxi appeared at the Myriad of Techniques once her death cooldown ended.

“You’re really cruel, you know that?” Li Jinxi greeted Yuan with a frown on her face.

Li Jinxi had been told of Yuan’s situation by Meixiu earlier. When she first learned that the man from the upper heavens she’d lost to was actually Yuan, she didn’t know whether to laugh or cry.

“I am truly sorry for killing you. It was an accident.” Yuan apologized to her with a sincere bow.

Li Jinxi sighed, “I know, but that also means I was below your expectations, right?”

Li Jinxi clenched her fist in anger. The thing that frustrated her the most wasn’t her defeat but realizing that despite all of her hard work, she was still miles behind Yuan, and there was a good chance the gap between them had even widened.

‘And to think I thought that I was actually catching up… How laughable,’ she sighed inwardly, feeling dejected.

Seeing her expression, Yuan smiled and said, “You have surpassed my expectations in terms of growth, and you will become stronger at an even faster rate once you train in the upper heavens, where the resources are much more abundant.”

“Let’s postpone our real match with each other until then.”

Li Jinxi nodded with a resolute face.

A few days later, Kelan returned to the Myriad of Techniques and said to Yuan, “Celestial Overlord Qing will arrive in the Fourth Heaven in a few hours. I have prepared a place for the two of you.”

“Alright. Take me there.” Yuan retrieved his mask and wore it before leaving the Myriad of Techniques with Kelan.

Sometime later, as they arrived at their destination, Yuan raised an eyebrow at the unexpected location.

“This is… a wine restaurant?” Yuan muttered as he saw the store.

“Celestial Overlord Qing is a wine enthusiast. She will be able to cope with your rejection much better here,” said Kelan.

“I see.” Yuan didn’t say much and entered the restaurant with Kelan. It was completely empty inside, as Kelan had reserved the entire place for the entire day.

Once inside, they sat at the lone table in the middle of the empty room. There were two chairs, indicating that Kelan had no plans of being there for the recruitment.

“I’ll be waiting outside,” Kelan said.

“So you really have no plan on participating?” Yuan asked.

He shook his head and sighed, “I have no place to participate. Not only am I a mere Commander, but Celestial Overlord Qiang is from a different group. My only job was to make sure you would be present before her arrival.”

“Good luck, and don’t mess it up.”

“How could I mess it up?” Yuan raised an eyebrow.

“How would I know? You’re as unpredictable as fate.” Kelan shrugged before walking outside to wait for Celestial Overlord Qiang’s arrival.

Half an hour later, Kelan noticed a figure descending from the sky, landing before him.

“Commander Kelan greets Celestial Overlord Qiang.” Kelan respectfully bowed to the beautiful young lady standing before him.

Celestial Overlord Qiang was a graceful young lady who appeared to be at the end of her adolescent years. She had an elegant presence, as if brought up in a prestigious and noble family. She was someone who could easily be mistaken as an innocent young lady who had been spoiled her entire life.

Celestial Overlord Qiang acknowledged Kelan with a slight nod before turning to look inside the restaurant. An excited smile appeared on her face as she spoke, “Even though I am still outside, I can feel his tyrannical presence. Looks like you weren’t exaggerating about his existence. If anything, I think you underestimated him!”

Unable to contain her excitement, Celestial Overlord Qiang walked inside the restaurant.

Kelan remained standing outside, acting like a guard.

The moment Yuan laid eyes on Celestial Overlord Qiang, he instinctively wondered if she was lost, as she lacked any noticeable presence.

Celestial Overlord Qiang walked to the table and gave him an elegant bow, “It’s a pleasure to meet you. I am Qiang Qing Yun, one of four Celestial Overlords.”

Yuan stood up and bowed back, “The pleasure is mine, Senior Qiang. My surname is Xiao. Forgive me for not revealing my face or full name due to family circumstances.”

Qiang Qing Yun calmly dismissed his worries with a slow head shake, “It’s okay. I am aware of your situation.” S~eaʀᴄh the Novёlƒire.n(e)t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Please, sit.” She gestured at the chairs.

Yuan nodded and sat back down.

‘So she is Celestial Overlord Qiang, huh? She’s completely different from Xu Jiaqi. If anything, she’s the complete opposite.’ Yuan thought to himself as Qiang Qing Yun sat down before him.

“I’m sure Kelan already mentioned this to you, but I would like to personally invite you to join the Celestial Overlords.” Qiang Qing Yun went straight to the topic after sitting down, leaving no room for small talk.

chapters.title: Chapter 1740 Qiang Qing Yun'S Invitation
chapters.body:
Chapter 1740 Qiang Qing Yun'S Invitation

“This is a little sudden. Why do you wish to recruit me when you don’t even know anything about me?” Yuan asked.

He didn’t want to outright reject Qiang Qing Yun’s invitation without having a conversation, as it would be incredibly rude and somewhat suspicious.

Moreover, he wanted to befriend her if possible, as it would benefit Xu Jiaqi and the Celestial Overlords.

“We can get to know you better as a member. The most important thing to me right now is your potential, and from what I have heard, you’re very gifted—enough to shock Kelan, who has seen all sorts of geniuses.”

“As for myself, I can tell from a glance that you hold extraordinary prowess inside your body, even though you are trying to hide it.” Searᴄh the Novёlƒire.n(e)t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“If I don’t hide it, I won’t be able to go outside without terrifying those around me.” Yuan shrugged.

“I know how you feel. Anyway, what is your answer?” Qiang Qing Yun asked, seemingly impatient for an answer.

“Honestly, while I have heard of the Celestial Overlords, I don’t know much about them. Moreover, due to my family’s circumstances, I will need special permission before I can associate myself with any organization.”

“In that case, do you think I can have a word with your family? I will do my best to convince them to allow you to join our faction.”

“Sorry, but my family prohibits sharing any information about them to outsiders, and that will continue even when I join the faction. If that is a dealbreaker, I’m afraid I will have to reject your invitation.”

“No, that is perfectly fine. As a matter of fact, we have plenty of members who come from hidden families, so we are very familiar with your boundaries.”

“Then what do you expect from me once I join?” Yuan asked.

“Not much, honestly. You are pretty much free to do as you please, and we only ask that you prioritize the faction when we need your help for something.”

“Though, you are required to earn a certain amount of good karma every year. It’s not much and won’t take much of your time unless you wish to ascend the ranks.”

“Furthermore, given your potential, I could even personally train you if you want. If I don’t have what it takes, I know people who do.”

“That’s quite a generous offer, Senior Qiang. I thought it was difficult to get into the Celestial Overlords.”

Qiang Qing Yun chuckled and said, “It is. I am only giving you special attention because of your potential. I also need your power in the future.”

“Oh? Can you elaborate on that?” Yuan’s interest was piqued.

“I shouldn’t say this since you’re not a member yet, but I also don’t want you to feel like I tricked you. In any case, the Celestial Overlords are in a tough situation right now due to internal struggle between members, mostly those at the top of the ranking.”

“Every Celestial Overlord has their own factions and are trying to take control of the entire faction. I need your power to defeat the others.”

“I see. I appreciate you telling such sensitive information to a stranger like me, and I would like to ask—what do you plan on doing if you win?” Yuan asked.

“You won’t be a stranger if you join the faction,” she chuckled before continuing, “Anyway, to answer your question…”

After a slight pause, she spoke with an awkward smile, “Honestly, I haven’t thought that far yet.”

“…” Yuan was speechless.

“You probably think I am unreliable or irresponsible, but I can assure you that I am not like that. Right now, I am merely entirely focused on ensuring the other Celestial Overlords don’t win, as that could spell the end for the faction.”

“The other Celestial Overlords? Why would the faction collapse if they win?”

“They only care about power and authority. People like them will run any organization to the ground when given uncontested authority. I joined the Celestial Overlords because they saved my life in the past, and I will do anything to protect the faction.”

“Is that so? Are the other Celestial Overlords that bad? I never knew.” Yuan went along with her to see if she was telling the truth or just wanted his assistance.

“Hmm… Besides Celestial Overlord Xu, the other two are definitely bad apples. One is hungry for power, and the other is desperate for authority. As for Celestial Overlord Xu… I don’t know about her. She’s a very distant and cold individual. She won’t speak unless necessary. However, that doesn’t necessarily mean she’s a bad person. I don’t get a bad vibe from her. Perhaps that’s why I don’t dislike her.”

Qiang Qing Yun appeared sincere when discussing her feelings for the Celestial Overlords, and her perspective on Xu Jiaqi aligned perfectly with what Xu Jiaqi herself had mentioned.

Realizing this, Yuan’s initial decision to reject her invitation suddenly began to change.

‘If I join her group, I will not only be able to determine whether her feelings are real or fake, but I can even make moves within the Celestial Overlords without affecting Xu Jiaqi and her faction. Moreover, if Qiang Qing Yun turns out to be genuinely concerned for the faction, I can bring her to our side.’

Yuan decided to accept Qiang Qin Yun’s invitation, but he didn’t hastily accept it just yet.

“I understand the Celestial Overlords’ situation, as well as Senior Qiang’s goals, but what can I do to help?” he asked.

“Well, you just need to focus on growing stronger for now since you are still too weak to do anything. If you need help, I, as well as those from my group, will do everything in our power to assist you. After all, it’s been a very long time since we’ve had someone as talented as you join us, and whenever someone highly talented joins, it’s typically Celestial Overlord Xu who recruited them.” Qiang Qing Yun sighed towards the end.

“Alright. Can you give me a few days to contact my family before I give you an answer?” Yuan said.

“Of course! Take as much time as you need!” Qiang Qing Yun’s face brightened after hearing his positive response.

chapters.title: Chapter 1741 The First Players To Ascend To The Seventh Heaven
chapters.body:
Chapter 1741 The First Players To Ascend To The Seventh Heaven

“I shall wait for your response here.” Qiang Qing Yun said a moment later.

“Alright.” Yuan nodded.

Their conversation ended there, and Qiang Qing Yun waved goodbye as Yuan walked out of the restaurant.

When Yuan left the restaurant, Kelan immediately looked at him with a questioning gaze. He wasn’t aware of Yuan’s plans or his conversation with Qiang Qing Yun. However, the conversation ended a little too early for his comfort.

Despite understanding Kelan’s feelings, Yuan ignored him and left. “Commander Kelan.” Qiang Qing Yun’s voice resounded from inside the restaurant shortly after Yuan left.

Kelan hastily entered to see Qiang Qing Yun smiling from ear to ear. This caused Kelan’s eyebrows to involuntarily raise.

‘Why is she so happy when he rejected her invitation?’ he wondered inwardly.

“You’ve done an excellent job scouting such a talented individual. Although I wish to reward you, I don’t want to create any misunderstandings since you belong to Celestial Overlord Xu’s group.”

“It’s fine. And honestly, I intended for Celestial Overlord Xu to recruit him, but she refused, so there’s nothing else I can do.”

“You could’ve recruited him before I arrived. Why didn’t you?” Qiang Qing Yun asked.

Kelan sneered, “And offend you, who has the second largest group in the faction? I’d rather not. There are plenty of talents out there, anyway. We can miss one or two.”

“I wouldn’t be so sure about that. There may be many talented individuals out there, but there are rarely any monsters like him. Even a thousand geniuses wouldn’t be able to compare to him.”

“You think so highly of him despite this being your first meeting with him. Why?”

Qiang Qing Yun smiled and said, “You’re not the only one in the world who recognizes talent when they see it. That man… I have never seen anything like that before.”

‘I must get my hands on him no matter what!’ she clenched her fists, her eyes filled with determination.

While Qiang Qing Yun waited for Yuan’s return, she purchased and drank nearly the entire wine restaurant’s stock.

Yuan pretended to contact his family when, in reality, he was spending his time at the Myriad of Techniques with the others.

“Brother!” A familiar figure greeted Yuan when he returned to the Myriad of Techniques.

“Yu Rou…” A warm smile appeared on Yuan’s face after seeing his little sister.

Meixiu had revealed Yuan’s situation to Yu Rou. When Yu Rou learned of this, she cried like a baby. Fortunately, there were still ways for them to meet inside Cultivation Online.

“Sorry, but I won’t be able to visit you for a while.” Yuan apologized to her.

“It’s okay. It’s not like you have a choice.” Yu Rou shook her head as she embraced him.

“However, I am worried about your body.”

“I should be fine. With my cultivation, I can survive with just spiritual energy alone, and my body naturally absorbs them.”

“Don’t worry. Yu Rou. I will check on Yuan’s condition every week just to make sure his body is healthy.” Wang Xiuying assured her.

“Thank you, Sister Xiuying.”

A few days later, it was time for the Demon Sealing Faction to ascend. However, since Kelan was busy accompanying Qiang Qing Yun, Senior Bai went to the Fourth Heaven to escort them to the Seventh Heaven.

“Until next time, Yuan.”

“Good luck, and don’t slack on your training,” Yuan said to them.

Before they left, Yuan revealed his plan to accept Qiang Qing Yun’s invitation. Senior Bai was baffled at first, but after hearing Yuan’s reasoning, he agreed with Yuan’s decision.

“Acting within the Celestial Overlords without affecting Lady Xu, huh? That’s a great idea. I will let her know later.” Senior Bai said.

Once the Demon Sealing Members left with Senior Bai, only Yuan, and Yu Rou remained in the Myriad of Techniques.

Soon, announcements began appearing for all of the players to see.

<Congratulations! Players Healer Wang, Li Jinxi, Chu Liuxiang, Meixiu, Sword Immortal Ming, Spear God Lang, Shadow Murong, Fluttering Zao, and God of Fists Xiuqian have become the first players to ascend to ‘Fifth Heaven’> Searᴄh the nôᴠel Fire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

<Congratulations! Players Healer Wang, Li Jinxi, Chu Liuxiang, Meixiu, Sword Immortal Ming, Spear God Lang, Shadow Murong, Fluttering Zao, and God of Fists Xiuqian have become the first players to ascend to ‘Sixth Heaven’>

<Congratulations! Players Healer Wang, Li Jinxi, Chu Liuxiang, Meixiu, Sword Immortal Ming, Spear God Lang, Shadow Murong, Fluttering Zao, and God of Fists Xiuqian have become the first players to ascend to ‘Seventh Heaven’>

These consecutive announcements left the entire world shocked and speechless. “W-wait a minute! I recognize their names! They belong to the Demon Sealing Faction!” Some of the players quickly noticed this detail.

“They must’ve found a method to ascend quickly! Does this mean we also get to ascend up to the Seventh Heaven now?”

“No… They didn’t ascend through the Stairway to Heaven. Instead, they were escorted to the upper heavens by experts who already live there, just like when Thunder Emperor and Healer Wang became the first players to ascend to the Spirit Heaven after the Mystic Realm a few years ago.”

“That’s strange… How come Player Yuan didn’t ascend with them? Surely, he wouldn’t want to miss such an opportunity.”

The players were quick to notice Yuan’s name was missing from the announcement.

“Didn’t he also refuse to ascend the last time? He’s probably going to challenge the Stairway to Heaven soon.”

“The sooner the better. I want to see what’s in the Fifth Heaven.”

Yu Rou left shortly after, returning to her own faction.

As for Yuan, he made his way back to Kelan and Qiang Qing Yun. “Wow, what a smell.” Even before Yuan stepped into the restaurant, a strong aroma of wine filled the air, assaulting his nose and eyes.

“Oh, welcome back. Have you spoken with your family?” Qiang Qing Yun placed the bottle of wine in her hand on the table and gestured for him to sit down.

Yuan nodded and responded, “Yes, and they have given me permission to join the Celestial Overlords.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1742 Rejoining The Celestial Overlords
chapters.body:
Chapter 1742 Rejoining The Celestial Overlords

“R-really?! You’ll join the Celestial Overlords?!” Qiang Qing Yun immediately became ecstatic, feeling like she was floating in the heavens from bliss.

She had prepared to fight tooth and nail for Yuan, and she’d even prepared some treasures just in case. However, the recruitment had gone unexpectedly smoothly and without any hiccups.

“Um, before you celebrate, I have some conditions.” Yuan suddenly said, halting her celebration.

Qiang Qing Yun became serious again and responded, “What is it? As long as it’s not unreasonable, we can definitely make it work.”

“First of all, I do not want anyone investigating my background.”

“I can assure you that nobody from my group will investigate your background, but I cannot guarantee you the other groups won’t, as my control over them is limited.”

“As a matter of fact, I can say for sure that they’ll try to investigate you, especially as your reputation increases within the faction.. However, I will still try my best to stop them if I find out.”

Yuan nodded, “That is good enough for me. I understand that it’s impossible to stop people from being nosy, and being investigated is inevitable.”

“My second condition is that you have to restrict access to my location. I am aware that high-ranking members have access to the location of other members.”

Qiang Qing Yun then responded, “I can definitely restrict certain ranks from accessing your location, but I cannot block everyone, such as other Celestial Overlords.”

“As for my final condition… Well, this isn’t really a condition but a request. If other members from opposing groups were to cause trouble for me, I would like the authority to deal with them in an appropriate manner, regardless of their rank.”

Qiang Qing Yun narrowed her eyes with a pondering face.

“Can you elaborate on what you deem as appropriate?” she inquired a few seconds later.

“For example, if someone plots or has the intent to harm or kill me, I will be able to harm or kill them depending on the situation. In other words, an eye for an eye.” Yuan gave a brief but straightforward explanation.

“I see. If a member attacks you without justification, you are already allowed to retaliate without needing my authority. However, please refrain from killing anyone if possible, as that would make things more complicated.”

“That’s good.”

“If that’s all your conditions, I will give you the Celestial Overlords’ symbol now.”

00:52

“If that’s all your conditions, I will give you the Celestial Overlords’ symbol now.” S~eaʀᴄh the nôᴠel Fire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Yuan nodded and extended his arm.

Not wanting to give Yuan another second to contemplate, Qiang Qing Yun swiftly gave Yuan the symbol.

“You are now an official member of the Celestial Overlords. Welcome aboard.” Qiang Qing Yun showed him a bright and satisfied smile.

Yuan looked at the ‘Apprentice’ symbol on his hand and smiled, “Thank you for having me.”

“To celebrate your acceptance, do you have any request? It’s a rare opportunity to receive a Celestial Overlord’s help, especially when they just joined the faction.”

“Can I save it for later?” Yuan asked.

“Of course.”

Qiang Qing Yun continued, “By the way, why did you come down to the lower heavens?”

“I was sent down by my family to hunt down some fugitive as a trial of sorts. Honestly, I don’t really get it.”

“I see. So you’re also here for that individual. But didn’t you hear? The Celestial Emperor has called off the search. Apparently that individual has been dealt with.”

“What? Seriously? When did this happen?” Yuan asked with wide eyes.

“A few days ago. Most have already returned to the upper heavens.”

Yuan laughed inwardly at this information, as this would make it easier for him to traverse the lower heavens without being noticed.

“In that case, I’ll roam around the lower heavens for a bit before returning home. It’s a rare opportunity for me to venture into the outside world, much less the lower heavens.”

Sometime later, Qiang Qing Yun said, “If you don’t have anything for me, I will leave you alone now—”

“Oh, here is a special present from me.”

Qiang Qing Yun stepped forward and tapped Yuan on the forehead.

<Absolute Concealment learned>

“This technique will help you hide your presence better,” she said.

Sure enough, the moment Yuan activated the technique, the rest of the presence he couldn’t hide disappeared magically.

Although it wasn’t as perfect as Yingzi’s complete lack of presence, Yuan could easily be mistaken as an ordinary cultivator. If he hid his presence even further, one could even mistaken him as a mortal.

“Thank you very much, Celestial Overlord Qiang.”

“You can call me Senior Sister Qiang. I’ll give you special permission.”

“I understand, Senior Sister Qiang.”

“Good. Then I will return to the upper heavens now. If you ever need me, you can contact me using this communication jade slip.” Qiang Qing Yun handed Yuan a jade slip.

Under normal circumstances, only Overlords and those closely connected to her would be given a communication jade slip that allows one to contact her directly.

As of this moment, Yuan had direct access to two Celestial Overlords. This was something only a select few Overlords could brag about.

Before leaving, Qiang Qing Yun made sure to grab a few more bottles of wine from the restaurant.

Once Qiang Qing Yun had completely left and her presence could no longer be sensed, Kelan approached Yuan with a deep frown on his face.

“What is the meaning of this? Do you intend on betraying Celestial Overlord Xu?” he inquired.

Yuan sighed, “Are you being serious?”

He proceeded to explain his reasoning for accepting her invitation.

“I have already spoken to Senior Bai about this, and he also agreed to it. I would’ve told you sooner, but you were busy.”

“You’re essentially using Celestial Overlord Qiang as a cover, and you’re acting like a spy. If she realizes this…” Kelan swallowed nervously when he couldn’t even imagine her reaction.

“Then we better make sure she doesn’t find out.” Yuan calmly shrugged, leaving Kelan speechless.

“Fuck… you’re really something else…” Kelan sighed in defeat.
