chapters.title: Chapter 1743 Nine Random Players
chapters.body:
Chapter 1743 Nine Random Players

“Anyway, now that your friends have ascended and your situation with Celestial Overlord Qiang is over, it’s time for me to leave as well. Don’t forget your promise.” Kelan said as he stared at Yuan.

“What promise?” Yuan teased.

“Our fight! We’ll fight once you reach the Seventh Heaven and travel to the Nine Heavens with your real body!”

“Ah, I remember now. I’ll fight you as much as you want if you take care of my friends. If I find out that they had been mistreated in any way, I’ll hold you accountable, understand?”

Kelan swallowed nervously after sensing Yuan’s serious gaze through his mask.

“Relax. They have protection not only from me but also from Overlord Bai and Celestial Overlord Xu. Nobody would be foolish to touch them, especially when they will be spending most of their time inside my sect.”

Yuan nodded, “Then I’ll leave them in your care.”

Kelan left shortly after, returning to the Seventh Heaven.

Meanwhile, Yuan made his way to a secluded location.

“Dong Ye.” Yuan summoned him.

However, there was no response.

After waiting around and trying again a few more times, Yuan decided to give up speaking with Dong Ye and made his way to the Stairway to Heaven instead.

At the Stairway to Heaven, Yuan noticed many players attempting to ascend.

“I shall be the first player besides Player Yuan to ascend!” A player boldly announced as he took his first step on the stairway leading to the gates.

Yet, four steps later, this player was forced to forfeit when he couldn’t even lift his legs under the immense pressure.

There were hundreds of players there, and most of them failed at the sixth step. A few managed to reach the ninth step, with a dozen players managing to reach the door. However, all of these players were tossed out a few minutes after entering the Stairway to Heaven.

Eventually, Yuan decided to participate. Sёarch* The ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Once it was his turn, he jumped onto the staircase and, without stopping, jumped to the next step until he reached all the way to the end.

“Who is that masked person?! Is he a player?!”

The players were shocked by how effortlessly Yuan had climbed the steps.

“There’s no way he’s a player unless he’s Player Yuan!”

“No, he doesn’t feel like a player at all.”

The players were quick to dismiss Yuan as a player, as there was something inherently different about him.

Perhaps it was due to Shura’s system, but the players subconsciously saw him in a negative light, almost as if they were natural enemies.

Upon reaching the door, Yuan opened it and entered it.

Once inside, a small figure emerged from the darkness and lowered their head before Yuan.

“Welcome back, Master.”

“It’s been a while, Tian’er. How have you been?”

“The same as usual.”

“I see. Anyway, I am here to ask you some questions. Is there any way for me to ascend the Stairway to Heaven without alerting the Celestial Emperor? For example, every time I successfully ascend, a notification will be sent throughout the Nine Heavens, and the other players will be able to ascend as well. Is there a way to avoid this?”

Tian’er swiftly responded, “The Celestial Emperor made it so that only players with the Rashara’s System will trigger an announcement when they clear the Stairway to Heaven. As for Master, since Tian’er cannot sense the system within you, there shouldn’t be any announcements when you clear it.”

“Hm? You know about the Rashara’s System?” Yuan didn’t expect this.

“Of course. Such information is necessary for Tian’er to work with it,” she nodded.

“Then do you know about the Shura’s System?”

Tian’er calmly shook her head.

“Then am I still considered a player?” Yuan then asked.

Tian’er stared at Yuan for a moment before nodding her head, “Master is still a player in Tian’er’s eyes.”

“Thanks a lot for the information, Tian’er.” Yuan was relieved to learn that he would be able to ascend without worrying about alerting the Celestial Emperor.

“Then I will be right back.”

Yuan exited the Stairway to Heaven.

“What? He got kicked out already?”

“Hahaha! He lasted even less time than the players!”

As the players laughed at him, Yuan spoke in a loud and clear voice, “I am willing to carry nine players through the Stairway to Heaven. Raise your hand if you wish to come.”

In order to acquire the best rewards in the Stairway to Heaven, Yuan decided to increase the difficulty to the max by bringing as many people as he could.

The entire place fell silent as the players stared at Yuan with wide eyes.

“W-we can do that? Being carried by an NPC, that is…” One of the players suddenly asked out loud.

“I have never heard of this before…”

Despite their suspicions, many players there raised their hands.

Seeing this, Yuan smiled and said, “There are too many people here, so let’s do it like this—the nine highest-paying players will get to ascend with me!”

“What?!”

The players began exchanging looks with each other. It didn’t take long before the players started shouting their offers.

However, even if every single one of these players handed their entire fortune to Yuan, it would still only be pocket change at best. In the end, Yuan ended up choosing nine random players who hadn’t made fun of him to mess with these players.

“Fuck! Are you messing with us?!” “Come down here, and let’s fight!”

Yuan jumped from the door to the ground, where the players were gathered.

“I’m here, now what?” Yuan released a tiny fraction of his aura, which was more than enough to terrify these players.

When nobody dared to move a moment later, Yuan said, “To those nine of you who were chosen, grab a badge and follow me.”

These players hastily went to grab a badge from the dumbfounded individual working there and proceeded to follow Yuan up the staircase.

Once all of the holes were filled, Yuan entered the Stairway to Heaven with the random players.

chapters.title: Chapter 1744 Ascending To The Fifth Heaven
chapters.body:
Chapter 1744 Ascending To The Fifth Heaven

Shortly before Yuan arrived at the Stairway to Heaven, he was stopped by Xi Meili.

“Hey, do you think I can challenge the Stairway to Heaven by myself?” Xi Meili asked him.

“Hm? If that’s what you want, go ahead. I have no real reason to stop you.” Yuan responded.

“Great! I have been wanting to challenge it ever since I heard about it. Hopefully, it won’t be too difficult.”

“With your talents and training with Kelan, I’m sure you’ll be able to pass it with flying colors.”

After hearing that Xi Meili wanted to challenge the Stairway to Heaven, Lan Yingying added, “I would also like to challenge the Stairway to Heaven. Since I chose to stay behind instead of training under Kelan with the others, I need to make the most of every opportunity to train myself.”

“Alright. Then I’ll go first. See you two later.”

“W-wait!” Xi Meili hastily stopped him.

“What if one of us fails to ascend?”

Yuan smiled and said, “Neither of you will fail. However, if you somehow fail to ascend, I still have a method to let you ascend.”

Xi Meili nodded, feeling a little more relieved, knowing they wouldn’t be stuck in the Fourth Heaven.

‘Now that I think about it, what about Yingzi?’

Since Yingzi didn’t originate from the Nine Heavens, would their laws affect her? Yuan decided to figure it out later.

Once Yuan was inside the Stairway to Heaven with the nine random players, the players were teleported to the spectators’ room.

“Tian’er, I have a question. I have someone who came from another world—a place outside of the Nine Heavens. How will Stairway to Heaven work for her?”

Tian’er then spoke, “If you mean that being hidden within your shadow, she can ascend without needing to pass the trials. After all, the Stairway to Heaven is only meant for those born within the Nine Heavens. Moreover, she is already powerful enough to exist within the Supreme Heaven. It’d be pointless to make someone like her take the trials.”

“Oh? You can sense Yingzi despite her lack of presence?” Yuan commented in a somewhat surprised tone.

“Presence or not, Tian’er can sense anything and everything that exists within the Stairway to Heaven. Nothing can hide from Tian’er,” she calmly stated. “For example, Tian’er can sense the three—technically four souls resting within Master’s Dantian, as well as the three souls resting within your three Soul Weapons.”

“…” Yuan stared at Tian’er with wide eyes.

‘I know I created her, but damn… How terrifying…’ Yuan swallowed nervously as he thought to himself.

“Alright, I will begin the trial now. Also, I have two companions who will be challenging the Stairway to Heaven later. Just in case… If they fail, I can still bring them to the Fifth Heaven, right?”

Tian’er nodded, “As the master of the Stairway to Heaven, you ultimately decide who can and cannot ascend. Tian’er has said this before, but Master can also ascend without needing to take the trials.”

Yuan nodded, “Thanks. That’s all I needed to know. We can begin the trial. Ah, make sure to prevent those players in the spectators’ room from seeing my trials.”

“As you wish, Master.”

Tian’er disappeared into the void shortly later, and a staircase appeared before Yuan. Meanwhile, inside the spectators’ room, the players were puzzled by their situation.

“W-where are we? What happened to that NPC who promised to carry us?”

Due to the inherent difficulty of the trials, very few players even attempted to carry others through the Stairway to Heaven, as doing so only increased the challenge. Those who did try never reached the door, much less enter the Stairway to Heaven. In other words, aside from those previously carried by Yuan, no players were aware of the existence of the spectators’ room.

“Did we get fooled by that NPC? What if we’re trapped in here forever?” one of the players made this assumption, his face filled with fear.

“What? If that’s true, how the hell do we get out of here? This is an enclosed room with no exits!”

“What if we have to kill ourselves to leave?!”

“Fuck! I shouldn’t have trusted that damn NPC!”

Right as the players began panicking, a voice resounded, “Calm down. You will all remain here until the one challenging the Stairway to Heaven is finished with their trial.”

“Oh…”

“Wait… Whose voice was that?”

When the players looked around and saw nobody, their bodies shivered in fear.

“A ghost!”

They all gathered in one corner of the room and cowered in fear.

Meanwhile, Yuan began his first trial.

When his location transformed, Yuan found himself inside a workshop with a hammer in his grasp.

<You have started your first trial in the Stairway to Heaven> S~eaʀᴄh the NôvelFire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

<Craft a Mythic-grade treasure>

<You have 24 hours to finish the trial>

<Finish the trial in less than 12 hours an hour to receive a reward>

“I need to craft a treasure, huh? But with what materials?”

He couldn’t see any materials around, so he used divine sense to broaden his view.

Outside his cave, Yuan noticed several powerful magical beasts roaming a vast wilderness, and they had a cultivation at the peak of Divine Grandmaster.

“I see.”

Yuan wasted no time and immediately went to hunt down these magical beasts. With his current prowess, Yuan could easily kill them with less than half his strength.

After defeating his first magical beast, its corpse turned into a material.

Yuan grabbed the material before hunting the next target.

In less than half an hour, Yuan hunted fifty magical beasts and returned to the cave.

He spent a few minutes looking through the materials and several more minutes doing mental simulations.

Once he was prepared, Yuan began crafting the treasure.

Sometime later.

<You have crafted a Mystic-grade treasure and successfully cleared the first trial>

<You have cleared the trial in less than 12 hours>

A golden orb of light appeared from the treasure Yuan crafted and entered his body.

The very next moment, Yuan regained a specific memory that once belonged to Tian Qiyuan, and it was a piece of memory that he hadn’t recovered yet.

‘The location of Tian Qiyuan’s treasure vault! I remember now!’ Yuan smiled after recalling this memory.

chapters.title: Chapter 1745 The Location To Tian Qiyuan'S Secret Vault
chapters.body:
Chapter 1745 The Location To Tian Qiyuan'S Secret Vault

Long before he was recognized as the God of Creation, Tian Qiyuan maintained a secret vault to store all his most precious materials and completed creations. Within this vault lay countless Celestial-grade treasures that he had never revealed to the public, along with priceless materials so rare they could easily spark a bloody war.

During Tian Qiyuan’s era, many speculated about the existence of such a treasure vault, though none could prove it. However, just before his mysterious disappearance, Tian Qiyuan shocked the world by announcing that he had hidden his vault somewhere in the Nine Heavens. He declared that whoever found it could claim every treasure within for themselves, sparking an uproar that rippled through the entire world, igniting a fevered hunt that continued to this very day.

Countless blacksmiths and cultivators scoured every corner of the world in search of Tian Qiyuan’s treasure vault, yet none succeeded in uncovering its location.

Tian Qiyuan’s treasure vault had become such a coveted legend that top organizations in the Nine Heavens, including the Celestial Emperor, offered generous rewards for any credible hints about its location. Unbeknownst to all who sought it, the location of Tian Qiyuan’s treasure vault had finally been uncovered… by none other than Tian Qiyuan himself.

“I am still quite far away from reaching it, but I’ll definitely reclaim my treasures as soon as possible,” Yuan mumbled to himself as he proceeded to the second trial.

For the second trial, Yuan found himself standing beneath a clear night sky in the middle of what appeared to be a city. The streets were quiet, bathed in the soft glow of moonlight, with shadows stretching across the empty buildings.

<You have started your second trial in the Stairway to Heaven>

<Assassinate the Lord of this city within an hour>

<Assassinate the Lord of this city, as well as all guards, within an hour to receive a reward>

‘Assassination? This is a new type of trial…’ Yuan thought to himself as he used his divine sense to scout the entire city.

He immediately noticed many mortals living inside the buildings, but they were all trembling in a corner for some reason. Seeing this phenomenon happening throughout the entire city caused Yuan to raise an eyebrow.

Sometime later, Yuan turned his attention to the tall pagoda that stood out like a sore thumb. Even without needing to use divine sense, he could tell that his target was living inside the pagoda.

‘This place feels very nostalgic, but whose memories does it belong to?’ Yuan wondered as he approached the pagoda.

He had an hour to complete his trial, which was plenty of time. After all, his opponents were only at the peak of Divine Grandmaster, and none of them would be able to endure a single strike from him.

Instead of completing the trial as fast as possible, Yuan decided to investigate the situation instead.

He knocked on one of the doors, hoping to speak with the residents. However, there was no answer. Inside, the occupants began trembling even more at the sound of his knock, as if death itself were waiting outside their door. After trying a few more buildings without new results, Yuan decided to become more forceful.

‘This may be an illusion, but it still feels bad to do this…’ Yuan sighed inwardly as he kicked down the doors.

“Sorry to bother you, but I need—”

“Aaahhh! Please! Don’t kill me! I have a sick mother who needs to be taken care of! She’d die without me!” A young boy shouted after realizing an intruder had entered their home.

Yuan looked at this young boy with raised eyebrows and said, “Relax, kid. I am not here to claim your life.”

“Really…?” The boy slowly turned to look at him with a fearful expression.

“Yes. What would I gain from killing you?”

“Okay, I’ll believe you.”

“Then, can you tell me why everyone is hiding inside their home? I’m not from around here.”

The boy trembled just thinking about it. After a moment of silence, he finally spoke with great difficulty, “T-the Phantom Assassin—Silent Reaper—has declared that he will take the life of our lord, and anyone who dares to stand in his way will be killed as well.”

“Phantom Assassin? I have never heard of him.” Yuan shook his head.

“What?! There’s no way! The Phantom Assassin is renowned throughout the Nine Heavens! You should’ve heard of him no matter where you’re from!” The boy exclaimed, his voice filled with disbelief.

“I’ve come from a really far away place, and I lived most of my life in seclusion, so I am not privy to such information.” Yuan shrugged.

“…”

After a moment of silence, the boy explained, “The Phantom Assassin is both a judge and an executioner. Once he sentences someone to death, nobody could stop him—not even the Celestial Emperor.”

“I see. Then why did this Phantom Assassin sentence the lord of this city to death?”

“I am not too sure. They say the Phantom Assassin only executes those who deserve it, but who really knows.” The boy sighed.

“Anyway, big brother, you should leave this place as soon as possible. It’d be disastrous if you encounter the Phantom Assassin and he mistakes you as an enemy.”

Yuan scrambled his hair and teased, “Are you sure you have the luxury to worry about me? You were just shivering in a corner not too long ago.”

“T-that’s…!” The boy began blushing.

“Alright, I’ll stop bothering you now. And don’t worry. The Phantom Assassin won’t target you if you don’t block his path.”

After saying goodbye to the boy, Yuan left the house and turned to look at the pagoda in the distance.

“Phantom Assassin, huh? What a nickname.” Yuan chuckled as he stomped the ground, shooting himself off the ground and soaring toward the pagoda.

Bang!

Yuan flew directly to the top floor, smashing through the wall as he entered the pagoda. Inside, a fat, middle-aged man stood surrounded by a dozen guards, their expressions filled with shock and terror.

“Y-you must be the Phantom Assassin! Why are you trying to kill me?! What did I do wrong?!” the middle-aged man began shouting at him.

Yuan looked at him and responded, “Good question. Why don’t you tell me what you’re thinking? How did you get into this situation?”

“I really don’t know! You’re the one who sentenced me to death! How could I possibly know what you’re thinking?!” The man exclaimed.

“Is that so?”

Yuan’s arm suddenly began gushing with Chaotic Enhanced Sword Aura, and before the guards could even act, his arm flickered, disappearing for a split second.

In the very next instance, half of the guards in the room fell to the ground after being decapitated, their heads spinning in the air.

“Heavens!” The middle-aged man fell to his buttocks and soiled his pants in the process.

“One last chance. Tell me what you know, and I just might spare you.”

The middle-aged man hastily got to his knees and began begging, “I took advantage of my position as the lord of this city and abused the citizens! I was wrong! Please spare my life! I swear I will change!”

Sha!

Yuan’s arm suddenly flickered again, and the rest of the guards were killed before they even realized what had happened.

“Do you take me for a fool?” Yuan narrowed his gaze at the man as he revealed his immense killing intent.

“Ahhh!” The man cried out loud. “O-okay! I am a demon worshipper!”

‘A demon worshipper, huh? Is this life related to the Divine Paragon, or…?’ Yuan wondered inwardly.

“Tell me more,” Yuan said to him.

“Thanks to the Demon Cult’s teachings, I have acquired a technique that greatly boosts my cultivation speed and overall prowess, allowing me to fight those above my own cultivation! However, this technique requires a tremendous number of sacrifices!”

“What a vile being. While worshipping demons itself isn’t a crime, the moment you lay a finger on innocent people…”

“Please! I was tricked and brainwashed by the Demon Cult! You have to believe—”

Before the man could even finish his sentence, Yuan swung his arm.

<You have killed the lord within an hour and successfully cleared the second trial>

<You have killed the lord and all of his guards within an hour>

A golden orb of light appeared from the fat man’s corpse, entering Yuan’s body.

As Yuan absorbed the orb of light, memories began flowing into him.

‘These memories belong to… Tian Kai, the God of Music?!’ Yuan was surprised after seeing who these memories belonged to.

He couldn’t fathom how Tian Kai, initially thought to be the God of Music and the mentor to the Zither Goddess, could also be the infamous Phantom Assassin—a figure whose very name sent shockwaves of terror throughout the Nine Heavens. Unfortunately, he’d only regained a small portion of Tian Kai’s memories as the Phantom Assassin, so he still lacked the information to finish the puzzle.

‘Tian Kai… just what kind of life did he live?’

chapters.title: Chapter 1746 Arriving At The Fifth Heaven
chapters.body:
Chapter 1746 Arriving At The Fifth Heaven

<You have started your last trial in the Stairway to Heaven>

As the scenery shifted, Yuan found himself standing under a clear blue sky on a vast, empty, circular platform that resembled a stage. The area was expansive and open, with no obstacles in sight, as if it were designed specifically for a performance or a duel.

<Survive for an hour>

<Survive your opponent for an hour and kill it to receive a reward>

A red-skinned figure materialized several hundred meters away from Yuan. With a height of at least nine feet, the figure had six black horns atop its head and four muscular arms, each gripping a weapon seemingly forged from solidified blood. Its presence radiated a fierce, ominous energy that would terrify even immortals.

This entity was obviously a demon, most likely at the rank of Demon General since Yuan couldn’t see its demon crystal, and it had the impressive cultivation of Divine Lord. “A human!” the demon roared, its voice so powerful that it made the very air tremble.

“A demon, huh? Perfect. I have been wanting to test out my Demon Sealing Aura while enhanced by chaotic essence.” Yuan smiled at the sight of this demon.

However, he had to survive—keep it alive for an hour before he could kill it.

“Allow me to consume your flesh and blood!” The demon laughed out loud as it pounced at Yuan like a tiger.

“…”

Yuan didn’t even bother moving and stood in place as the demon began slashing at him with its weapons.

“Are you so terrified that you cannot even move?! Pathetic!”

The demon mocked with a devious smile, but its expression abruptly stiffened when it realized it could not scratch Yuan’s skin, let alone damage him.

“We’ve got an hour, so do your best,” Yuan said as he lay on the ground and closed his eyes to sleep. S~eaʀᴄh the Nôvelƒire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Although its skin was already red, the demon’s face seemed to flush an even deeper shade as it erupted in fury, its eyes blazing with rage.

The demon proceeded to throw its weapons away and bombarded Yuan with techniques instead.

Yet, Yuan remained still and undisturbed.

The demon could even hear Yuan snoring a bit, almost as if he were in a deep and comfortable sleep. This caused the demon to snap, forcing it to use its own life to enhance its prowess.

Yuan opened his eyes to look at the demon, whose aura suddenly changed drastically.

Although demons are essentially immortal and can live for millions of years without any external energy source due to their demon crystal, they could sacrifice the power of their demon crystal for a tremendous boost in power. Once a demon uses this forbidden technique, death is inevitable shortly afterward, which is why demons rarely resort to it, even when facing death. Additionally, the act is considered shameful, something only the lowest of low demons would even consider doing.

After destroying its own demon crystal, the demon’s cultivation soared to the peak of Divine Emperor, and its attack became capable of damaging Yuan.

However, even with its enhanced power and increased strength, the demon’s attacks could only manage to scratch the outer layer of Yuan’s skin.

“That’s not good. At this rate, you’ll die before an hour passes.”

Seeing this, Yuan stood up and extended a single finger, channeling his Demon Sealing Aura into the tip. As he touched the demon and poured Demon Sealing Aura into it, the red creature instantly turned to stone. However, since the demon was only sealed and technically still alive, the trial remained uncleared.

The demon, even in its sealed state, would still perish due to the forbidden technique it had invoked. However, the sealing slightly prolonged its inevitable death, delaying its final moments just enough for Yuan to clear the trial.

An hour later, Yuan unsealed the demon. He then infused his chaotic essence with his Demon Sealing Aura.

“W-what is that Demon Sealing Aura?! I’ve never seen or heard of anything like it!” The demon sensed a profound feeling of dread from Yuan’s upgraded Demon Sealing Aura, which sent chills through its entire being.

A terrified expression appeared on the demon’s face, and without thinking, it instinctively turned around and ran away. However, before it could get far, its body began to disintegrate, breaking apart bit by bit as the effects of the forbidden technique took their final toll.

<You have survived an hour and successfully passed the trial>

<You have survived an hour and killed the demon>

“What a pity. I didn’t even get to test out my Chaotic Demon Sealing Aura.” Yuan shook his head as he acquired some more of the Divine Paragon’s memories.

“Congratulations on clearing the Stairway to Heaven, Master.” Tian’er appeared shortly after.

Yuan then inquired, “Since I no longer have access to Rashara’s System, the other players won’t be able to ascend to the Fifth Heaven even after I cleared it, correct?”

Tian’er nodded and said, “Master is a player…but also not a player simultaneously. The Stairway to Heaven will only open to the other players if someone with Rashara’s System clears it.”

“Since there’s Rashara’s System and Shura’s System, can I assume there are other systems out there? Do you know anything about that?”

“No, Tian’er is only aware of Rashara’s System,” she said.

“I see. Thanks. Then I’ll see you later.”

Sometime later, Yuan and the nine players he carried were transported to the Fifth Heaven.

After emerging from the Stairway to Heaven, Yuan looked at the sky to see if there were any announcements.

A few moments later, when he didn’t see anything, Yuan smiled behind his mask, turned to the nine dazed players, and said, “Good luck.”

He left before any of the players could ask questions, but he didn’t go too far, as he still had to wait for Xi Meili and Lan Yingying.

“We actually got carried by an NPC and made it to the Fifth Heaven?”

“Since there’s no announcement, it’s safe to assume he wasn’t a player.”

“Hahaha! That means we’re the only players in the Fifth Heaven at this moment!”

“This is huge!”

These players logged off shortly after to share their experience on the internet with the rest of the world.

chapters.title: Chapter 1747 Second Ancient Seal
chapters.body:
Chapter 1747 Second Ancient Seal

[I was carried to the Fifth Heaven by an NPC!]

[We somehow reached the Fifth Heaven?!]

[Nine players were carried to the Fifth Heaven by a mysterious NPC!]

Such titles quickly reached the first page of the biggest forum on all Cultivation Online things related, shocking the players who saw them. Searᴄh the NôvelFire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“I didn’t know we could use NPCs in such a fashion!”

“How can we be certain that individual was an NPC and not a player?”

“If it had been a player, the Fifth Heaven would’ve been accessible to the rest of the playerbase once cleared.”

“Given how difficult the trials are, there’s no way a player could’ve carried nine people through the Stairway to Heaven!”

“Player Yuan might have the capability to do so.”

The nine players who had been carried by Yuan quickly became the center of attention, attracting a flurry of interviews and offers from top factions and influential families eager to purchase information about the Fifth Heaven.

“Looks like he successfully reached the Fifth Heaven.” Wang Xiuying smiled after seeing the news.

Meanwhile, Xi Meili was the next to challenge the Stairway to Heaven.

After training with Kelan for three years, she increased her cultivation to the peak of Divine Warrior, allowing her to breeze through the Stairway to Heaven.

“Defeat 100 opponents.” A nonchalant voice resounded as Xi Meili entered her final trial.

Soon, a hundred peak Spirit Enlightenment cultivators appeared to hunt her together.

‘Is this what it felt like being hunted by humans back in the day?’ Xi Meili wondered inwardly as she swiftly defeated the cultivators one by one.

After defeating all one hundred cultivators, Xi Meili passed the trial and ascended to the Fifth Heaven.

“Congratulations.”

Yuan appeared before her with a smile on his face shortly after she entered the Fifth Heaven.

“It was really fun and not too difficult,” she said with a prideful look on her face.

“In that case, I assume you’ll also want to climb to the other realms by yourself.”

Xi Meili nodded, “Yes, it’s a great method of training.”

They proceeded to wait for Lan Yingying.

“Defeat your opponent within thirty minutes.”

Lan Yingying had to face an opponent at the peak of Spirit Enlightenment while being at the same cultivation herself.

Not even five minutes later, Lan Yingying proceeded to the second trial. “Survive for fifteen minutes.”

The sky started raining down swords a moment later.

Sometime later, Lan Yingying also passed all of her trials and entered the Fifth Heaven.

Xi Meili and Yuan appeared to congratulate her shortly afterward.

“My trials were quite simple when compared to your trials. My expectations were affected by your experience.” Lan Yingying said to Yuan.

Unlike Yuan, who receives detailed and unique trials, everyone else faces straightforward tests that primarily assess their combat prowess and other skills relevant to the cultivation world.

“What are your plans now?” Xi Meili asked. “Straight to the Sixth Heaven?”

“We can give it a try.” Yuan nodded.

However, just as Yuan made this decision, he noticed a shift in the space around them.

“Dong Ye?” Yuan muttered. “Oh? You actually noticed me?” Dong Ye appeared the next moment with a surprised expression on his face.

After acquiring an understanding of the power of the void, Yuan could instinctively sense someone using it around him, and Dong Ye just happened to be an expert in the power of the void.

“Is this how you get around without people noticing you? Because of the power of the void?” Yuan inquired.

He nodded, “Yes, and not to brag, but not many can match my understanding of the power of the void in the Nine Heavens.”

“Is that so? Anyway, did something happen? I tried calling you before, but you didn’t respond. Now, you appear out of nowhere.” Yuan then asked.

Dong Ye rubbed his eyes and sighed, “Let’s just say that the Celestial Emperor has increased his efforts to find me. I think something has happened, but I am not sure of the details just yet.”

Hearing this, Yuan revealed, “It might have something to do with me. The Celestial Emperor is now aware of my identity.”

“What?! How did that happen?” “Are you aware of the system used by the players?”

Dong Ye nodded, “I am aware of its existence and abilities to some extent.”

“Good, then you should grasp the situation easily.”

Yuan proceeded to explain his situation—about how the Celestial Emperor removed his access to Cultivation Online to prevent him from entering the Nine Heavens.

“I see… In that case, we should also begin our arrangements…” Dong Ye muttered in a low voice after learning of the situation.

“What arrangements?” Yuan asked.

“Ah, it’s something prepared by your incarnations. I won’t go into specifics since it might affect your fate.”

Yuan had never fully understood Dong Ye’s reluctance to share details about his past lives, despite his loyalty and the fact that it had been a direct order from his incarnations. However, after learning about Fateful Reincarnations from Xu Jiaqi, everything finally made sense.

Knowing Yuan’s identity as a True Reincarnator, Dong Ye had to be exceptionally cautious with the information and assistance he provided so that he would not interfere with Yuan’s fate in unintended ways.

“Because of that, I will have to leave for a bit, so I won’t be able to watch over your friends for the time being.” Dong Ye continued.

Yuan nodded and said, “Don’t worry, my friends are currently training with someone trustworthy in the Seventh Heaven.” “Ah, but there will be someone to assist you while I am away. They were hand-picked by me after a rigorous screening,” said Dong Ye.

“Are they aware of my identity?” Yuan asked.

“They are aware of your identity as the Evil God’s reincarnation, but that’s the extent of their knowledge. Don’t worry. They’re very reliable and trustworthy.”

“I’ll take your word for it.” Yuan nodded.

“One more thing before I leave. I have found the identity of the second Ancient Seal holder. They are currently in the Fifth Heaven, and he is the Dragon King of the Shen Family.”

“Dragon King of the Shen Family?” Yuan raised an eyebrow after hearing this familiar name.

chapters.title: Chapter 1748 Shadow Army Rank Structure
chapters.body:
Chapter 1748 Shadow Army Rank Structure

“Yes, it is exactly that Dragon King.” Dong Ye confirmed, almost as if he could read Yuan’s mind.

“In order to protect your identity from the Celestial Emperor, I had his memories, as well as everyone else involved with you, erased,” he explained.

Yuan’s eyes widened.

“So they don’t remember our interactions during my previous visit?” Yuan asked for confirmation.

“No, they still remember. However, they won’t be able to remember your face and the conversations you had, much like how people only have vague memories of their dreams after waking up.” Dong Ye explained.

“However, if they see you again, there’s a chance for them to recall everything.”

“I see. In any case, I won’t be going there as Yuan, so they won’t remember.”

Dong Ye nodded.

“Then until next time, my liege.” Dong Ye bowed to Yuan before disappearing into thin air. seaʀᴄh thё ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Shortly after Dong Ye disappeared, Yuan noticed two unfamiliar presences appearing near him.

“Are you the ones sent by Dong Ye?” Yuan muttered out loud.

The next moment, two figures appeared before Yuan, one knee touching the ground and their heads already lowered. “We greet the Lord.”

“Introduce yourselves.”

The first individual, a handsome young man with a tall, robust frame, tanned skin, and striking red eyes, spoke respectfully, “This loyal subject is called Yang. Although I am only a Shadow Reaper, I will do my utmost to support the Lord’s every need.”

Then, the other individual, a beautiful young woman with a tall, slender frame, similarly tanned skin, and red eyes, spoke with a tone of reverence, “This loyal subject is called Yin, also a Shadow Reaper. I exist solely to serve the Lord and support his ambitions.”

“Yin and Yang, huh? Are you siblings?” Yuan then asked.

“Yes, we are twins.” They responded simultaneously.

“Then, can you tell me about the Shadow Army? What does it mean to be a Shadow Reaper?”

Yang responded first, “There exists five ranks within the Shadow Army, each with a unique purpose. The first and lowest rank is Shadow Scout. They specialize in scouting and information gathering. The majority of the Shadow Army are Shadow Scouts.”

Yin continued, “The second rank is Shadow Reaper. We specialize in tracking and assassination, but we are just as well-versed in information gathering as Shadow Scouts—if not better.”

Yang spoke again, “Shadow Enforcer is the next rank. They are a special unit that specializes in completing high-risk missions.”

Yin then said, “Shadow Commanders are in charge of training and overseeing the Shadows. There are only three Shadow Commanders in the Shadow Army.”

“The last rank is Shadow Sovereign, reserved for a single person—Master Dong,” finished Yang.

“How many members does the Shadow Army have?” Yuan asked a moment later.

“We apologize, but we cannot answer that question. However, it’s not because you lack the authority. We just don’t know the answer. Only Shadow Commanders and Master Dong know the exact number, so you’ll have to ask one of them.” Yin and Yang spoke perfectly in sync, almost as if they shared the same mind.

“Alright. In any case, I don’t really need any assistance for now.” Yuan said a moment later.

“If you ever need us, summon us with this.” Yang handed him a black and white jade slip shaped like a yin-yang symbol.

“We’ll always be nearby waiting for your orders,” Yin added.

“Please excuse us.”

They disappeared at the same time shortly after.

“That was something…” Xi Meili muttered with a somewhat dumbfounded look on her face.

As a princess who grew up with a small army, she couldn’t help but admire just how faithful and loyal the Shadow Army appeared.

‘Even the most loyal soldier in our family wouldn’t look at Father the way those two looked at Yuan, and they just met today,’ she sighed inwardly.

“Now then, before we head to the Shen Family to receive the second Ancient Seal, I should fill you in on the situation and my plan.”

Yuan proceeded to explain the purpose of the Ancient Seal and his reason for gathering them, detailing its importance. He then shared his plan to acquire the seal, “Fortunately, the Shen Family are dragons, so we should have an easier time acquiring their Ancient Seal with your identity.”

“My identity? Wouldn’t it be better to use your identity? When you transform, you emit a presence stronger than even my father,” she inquired with a puzzled face.

“Yes, but there exists a dragon family in the Supreme Heaven with the same surname as your Xi Family, and they have quite the authority. Who knows? There’s even a chance you’re related to them.”

“What? That’s not possible… I mean, my father had never mentioned anything about a family outside the Ancient Dragon City before. As far as I am aware, we’re the only remaining ‘Xi Family’ left.”

“I guess we’ll find out once we reach the Ninth Heaven. Anyway, let me tell you how we’ll go about it…”

Sometime later, they began making their way toward the Shen Family.

Meanwhile, at the Fairy Paradise, where Yuan once spent some time imprisoned, a beautiful woman stood before the Fairy Empress.

“How long has it been since you visited us? You should visit more often. We all miss you very much, Sister Songyun.” The Fairy Empress showed a warm smile to Tan Songyun, who had spent the last three years chasing after Yuan.

“Sorry, but I’ve been very busy in the Sixth Heaven,” Tan Songyun said.

“Are you still trying to find the bastard who broke your heart? Have you found any leads yet?”

Tan Songyun nodded and spoke with a serious expression, “Yes, I found a lead, and I believe he visited this place about three years ago.”

“What?” The Fairy Empress’ eyes widened with shock after hearing this.

“I learned that he visited my Immortal Cave in the lower heavens and came here by accident after triggering my teleport formation.”

“Three years ago…” Fairy Empress wore a perplexed expression on her face after realizing who Tan Songyun was talking about.

chapters.title: Chapter 1749 Visiting The Royal Dragon Family Again
chapters.body:
Chapter 1749 Visiting The Royal Dragon Family Again

“I remember the man who entered the Fairy Paradise a little over three years ago. However, I don’t think he’s the person you’re looking for.” Fairy Empress shook her head.

“What? Why do you say that?” Tan Songyun asked with a puzzled frown on her face.

“The man you’re looking for is a human, right?” Fairy Empress asked for confirmation.

Tan Songyun nodded with raised eyebrows.

“He is…as far as I am aware.”

“Then it’s impossible for the man who visited the Fairy Paradise three years ago to be that man. After all, the man who visited us was a Dragon Emperor,” said Fairy Empress.

“A Dragon Emperor?! Impossible!” Tan Songyun exclaimed, outright refusing to believe this information.

“He must’ve somehow tricked you! There’s no way he’s a Dragon Emperor! I am willing to bet my life on this claim! But can you describe his appearance to me, just in case?”

Fairy Empress shook her head and sighed, “Unfortunately, I don’t remember. I had my memories stolen by some fugitive, so my memories of that Dragon Emperor are gone. I only remember that he had showed up at the Fairy Paradise.”

“What? Why would anyone want to steal your memories? And how many memories did you lose?” Tan Songyun was baffled by this news.

“That’s the strange part. That fugitive only stole memories of that Dragon Emperor, such as his appearance and our conversations. Besides that, I have all of my memories. I was even interrogated by Heaven’s Mandate because of that incident.” “Heaven’s Mandate!? They only deal with the most dangerous and wanted fugitives in the Nine Heavens! I’m surprised you managed to survive an encounter with someone like that.”

“In any case, you don’t remember anything about this ‘Dragon Emperor’, right?”

Fairy Empress sighed, “Sorry. However, the Royal Dragon Family may know something about him since that’s where he went after leaving the Fairy Paradise.”

“The Royal Dragon Family…? The only Royal Dragon Family in the Fifth Heaven should be the Shen Family.” Tan Songyun muttered.

“It’s exactly that Shen Family.” Fairy Empress confirmed.

“Okay, I will give them a visit.”

“Sister Songyun, before you leave, do you think you can play a song on your zither? I have been longing to hear it again for years now.” Fairy Empress suddenly asked.

“That’s…” Tan Songyun wore a hesitant expression on her face.

“Sorry, but I am still reminded of him when I play the zither.” Tan Songyun shook her head.

Sometime later, Tan Songyun left the Fairy Paradise to make her way toward the Shen Family.

At the same time, Yuan and Xi Meili had just arrived at the Shen Family, both of them wearing masks to hide their identity.

When the guard at the gate noticed their suspicious appearance, they were immediately told to halt.

“Stop right there and identify yourselves!”

They stopped moving, and after a moment of silence, Xi Meili unleashed her aura, causing her dragon scent to spread quickly.

“I am from the Xi Family, and I am here with my mentor, who is a Dragon Emperor! We’d need to speak with the Dragon King as soon as possible!”

“The Xi Family and the Dragon Emperor?!” The guard fell on his buttocks after hearing this.

“P-please wait a moment!”

The guard hastily rushed inside to notify the Dragon King.

“What?! The Xi Family and the Dragon Emperor?! Together?! That’s impossible!” Just as the Dragon King uttered these words, an overwhelming presence suddenly enveloped the entire Shen Family, causing everyone to tremble in fear and awe.

“What an unfathomable presence! My bloodline is screaming for me to bow down! This must be the Dragon Emperor’s presence!”

Realizing this, the Dragon King wasted no time rushing outside, with the rest of the Shen Family following suit.

“The Shen Family greets the Xi Family and the Dragon Emperor!” Xi Meili stepped forward and said, “My surname is Xi. Are you the Dragon King?” she asked.

“Yes! This humble one is the Fifth Heaven’s Dragon King, Shen Guang Yao!” Xi Meili nodded and spoke, “We apologize for the sudden intrusion, but we have an urgent matter that requires your attention and cooperation.”

“Please! You’re more than welcome to intrude on us whenever you wish!” the Dragon King said, his tone with apparent reverence. “Moreover, we could never be bothered by your presence—in fact, we are humbled to be graced by such a noble presence!” He spoke as though he were nothing more than a lowly servant.

Xi Meili then said, “We would also like to apologize for not revealing our faces, as we do not wish to reveal our identity. However, we’re more than willing to prove our identities if you need.”

“No, that won’t be necessary. We already have more than enough proof…” Dragon King’s eyes shifted to Yuan as he spoke.

Even though Yuan had already withdrawn his presence, revealing it only for a split second, the Dragon King could still feel it as vividly as if it were still active and enveloping the entire place.

“Please follow me.” Dragon King said to them a moment later. Sёarch* The Novёlƒire.n(e)t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

As Yuan and Xi Meili followed the Dragon King inside, Yuan subconsciously glanced at a beautiful young lady in the crowd. She was Shen Shi, who had brought him out of the Fairy Paradise during his previous visit.

Shen Shi noticed Yuan’s gaze, but she didn’t dare to say anything and merely acknowledged him with a silent nod. Once inside the building, the Dragon King brought Yuan and Xi Meili to a private room.

“Please wait a moment for the tea—”

“It’s fine.” Xi Meili interrupted. “If you say so.” The Dragon King closed the doors and sat across from them.

“So, what brought Lady Xi and His Majesty to the Fifth Heaven?”

“I will get straight to the point. It’s about the Ancient Seal in your possession.” Xi Meili said in a solemn tone.

“A-Ancient Seal?” The Dragon King’s eyes widened.

“You don’t need to pretend. We know it’s in your possession.” Xi Meili quickly said.

“N-no… I am not pretending. I was just surprised. I see… so this is about the Ancient Seal…” he muttered in a low voice.

chapters.title: Chapter 1750 Visiting The Royal Dragon Family Again(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1750 Visiting The Royal Dragon Family Again(2)

“What’s going on? Why are the Ancient Seals an issue?” The Dragon King inquired.

Xi Meili responded, “We have credible intel of a traitor amongst the Ancient Seal holders.”

“What? A traitor?” The Dragon King’s eyes widened with shock and disbelief.

Xi Meili continued, “This traitor is trying to open the Primordial Realm for some reason. We don’t know the reason for now, but we are trying to stop it from happening.”

“D-don’t tell me… You are here today because the Xi Family thinks I— the Shen Family are traitors?!” The Dragon King stood up with a terrified look on his face.

For most dragon families in the Nine Heavens, the Xi Family’s status was as high as the heavens, owing to their direct lineage to the Dragon Ancestor. The Xi Family wielded enough power to completely erase the Shen Family from existence in the Nine Heavens, and no one would be able to stop them or hold them accountable.

“Please! There must have been a misunderstanding! Our Shen Family would never engage in such blasphemy!” The Dragon King looked as if his entire bloodline had just been sentenced to death.

“Calm down, we are not suspecting your family…yet. We came here to investigate. That’s all.”

The Dragon King released a deep sigh of relief before sitting back down.

“I apologize for that just now. However, I can confidently say that our Shen Family would never betray the Xi Family or abandon our duty to protect the Ancient Seal.”

“Words are… in the end, just words,” Yuan spoke in a calm voice, but it sounded as loud and powerful as thunder. Sёarch* The Novelƒire(.)ne*t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“How can we prove our innocence?” The Dragon King inquired with a worried frown.

“There’s really nothing for you to prove at the moment. However, we will need to confiscate your Ancient Seal until we figure out the traitor,” said Xi Meili.

“Unless… you no longer have it with you?”

“I-I have it! I don’t have it on hand since I stored it in a secret vault, but I can get it for you within two days!” “Alright. If you run away or do anything suspicious during this time, we will immediately consider you the traitor and sentence the Shen Family to execution.” Xi Meili spoke.

“I would never dare!” The Dragon King trembled in fear.

“Then I will immediately bring it back right now! If you need anything while I am away, you can speak to the elders!”

The Dragon King bowed to them before leaving the room. Before he left the Shen Family to acquire the Ancient Seal, he had to notify the entire family of the situation.

“Nobody is to leave the family’s premises until I return! If you cause any misunderstandings or offend our esteemed guests, our entire Shen Family could be executed!” The Dragon King gave his family a grim warning before leaving.

“Your presence is really effective. Even I was affected by it when you revealed it.” Xi Meili chuckled once they were alone.

Yuan sighed, “I feel bad doing this to the Shen Family, but it’s better than threatening them or causing them unnecessary harm.”

“This also makes me wonder if the other two Ancient Seal holders are dragons.”

The last two Ancient Seal holders were both of dragon bloodlines.

“I guess we’ll find out when we visit the next holder.” Xi Meili commented.

Sometime later, someone knocked on the door to Yuan and Xi Meili’s room.

“Enter.” Xi Meili said.

The door opened, and a beautiful young lady walked inside.

“My name is Shen Shi, the Dragon King’s daughter. I was just wondering if there’s anything I could do to assist you,” she said as she respectfully bowed to them.

“We don’t—”

“Why don’t you tell us a little about the Shen Family?” Yuan suddenly interrupted Xi Meili.

Shen Shi nodded, approached the couch before them, and asked, “May I?”

“Go ahead. Make yourself comfortable.” Yuan nodded.

Once she was seated, Shen Shi asked, “What would you like to know about our Shen Family?”

“You can tell us a little about your background. For example, how long have you lived in the Fifth Heaven?”

“Our family’s been living in the Fifth Heaven for as long as its history, but we’re only a branch family. The main Shen Family lives in the Supreme Heavens, but I’m sure you already know that.”

Yuan shook his head and said, “I may be the Dragon Emperor, but I don’t know every single family out there.”

“If you don’t mind me asking, what’s it like being a Dragon Emperor? I met one before, but I don’t remember our conversations.” Shen Shi then said.

“Hm? You met a Dragon Emperor before? Why wouldn’t you remember such a significant event?” Yuan tilted his head in a puzzled manner, pretending to know nothing.

“It’s a bit complicated, but my memories, as well as several other family members, had been stolen by a fugitive. I think that Dragon Emperor had something to do with it, but I cannot be sure.”

“A fugitive who steals memories, huh? I only know of one individual who is famous for that, and he’s a real nasty individual.” Yuan chuckled inwardly as he said these words.

“Anyway, life as a Dragon Emperor is pretty simple—at least for me. If I am not managing my people, I go around dealing with issues such as this one.”

“The Ancient Seals, right? I cannot fathom why anyone would want to open the Primordial Realm. There’s a good reason why it’s sealed in the first place. If the criminals inside were to be set loose, the Nine Heavens would quickly go to hell.”

“Don’t worry, we will ensure that such a thing won’t happen.” Xi Meili said.

“Thank you for your hard work, Lady Xi. I know I should be the one entertaining you, but would you mind if I asked you a few questions? I’m very interested in the Xi Family, which is said to be the closest to the Dragon Ancestor in terms of bloodline.” Shen Shi looked at her with passionate eyes.

chapters.title: Chapter 1751 Embroidery
chapters.body:
Chapter 1751 Embroidery

“You want to know about the Xi Family? It’ll have to depend on your questions since some information cannot be disclosed.” Xi Meili said.

Shen Shi nodded and asked, “Then can you tell me what life is like in the Xi Family for you?”

“Life is boring.” Xi Meili sighed. “As a princess from a royal family yourself, you should know what I mean. We spend most of our time studying and cultivating long before we even learn to run, and there’s always a certain level of expectations for our every action. There are a lot of things that I wish to do but cannot do solely because of my background. It’s a little suffocating at times if I have to be honest.” Shen Shi’s eyes flickered with emotion after hearing her own experience and feelings from Xi Meili’s mouth. “I understand!” she said, “And I’m sure it’s a lot worse for you, who is from the Supreme Heaven! I cannot even begin to fathom your hardships…”

“Well, I am used to it by now.”

“Then what do you typically do when you’re not studying or cultivating?”

“I go out to do missions such as this one for my family, which might be the only thing keeping me sane. Every time I leave home, it’s like visiting a new world.” “Do you have any hobbies that you do when you’re free?” Shen Shi continued to ask.

“Hmm… Don’t make fun of me for saying this, but I enjoy making things, such as sewing or embroidery.”

“Wow! You know embroidery?! That’s amazing!”

Xi Meili chuckled, “I can make something simple for you while we wait for your father’s return. We have plenty of time, after all.”

“Really?! Please do!” Shen Shi nodded enthusiastically.

Xi Meili proceeded to retrieve a piece of white cloth, a thread, and multiple colorful yarns. Once prepared, she began working with swift and precise movements.

Not only was Shen Shi amazed, but Yuan was also surprised by her unexpected talent.

‘I didn’t know she had such a hobby.’ Yuan smiled inwardly. He thought she was a princess who only knew how to fight, so something so delicate was truly unexpected.

Several hours later, Xi Meili handed the finished piece to Shen Shi, whose eyes flickered excitedly.

“Here you go.”

The embroidery had Shen Shi’s name, as well as Shen Shi’s beautiful face on it.

“Wow! This looks just like a painting! How did you achieve such fine details in this small piece of cloth? I watched from the beginning to end and still cannot figure it out.” “The secret lies in the materials—the yarn, specifically. See how thin they are? It will take much longer to complete the work, but it’s definitely worth it.” Xi Meili showed her the yarns.

“Want to learn? I can teach you.” Xi Meili then asked Shen Shi.

Shen Shi nodded enthusiastically, and Xi Meili began teaching Shen Shi the art of embroidery.

Sometime later, the guard outside the Shen Family noticed a lone figure wearing a veil approaching their residence.

“Halt and identity yourself!” The guard shouted at her.

“Tan Songyun from the Fairy Paradise. I would like to speak with the Dragon King.”

“What does the Fairy Paradise want from the Dragon King?”

“This is a private matter,” she said.

“Regardless, you’ll have to come back later since the Dragon King left yesterday.”

“When will he come back?” Tan Songyun asked.

“He should be back sometime today—tomorrow at the latest. However, you should return next week since we have important guests at the Shen Family, and we don’t know when they’ll be leaving.”

“Important guests?” Tan Songyun raised her eyebrows.

“Fine, I’ll be back next week. Notify the Dragon King of my presence the moment he returns.”

Tan Songyun didn’t argue with the guard and turned around.

However, just as she began leaving, her footsteps halted.

“Hm? What’s that noise?” The guard raised an eyebrow after hearing a beautiful sound coming from inside the Shen Family.

Hearing this noise, Tan Songyun turned around and approached the guard once again.

“Change of plans. I need to speak with the individual playing the zither right now,” she said.

Not only was the sound played from a zither, but Tan Songyun could sense the Soul Ensnaring Zither’s aura from those music notes.

‘The Soul Ensnaring Zither! That means that bastard is currently at the Shen Family! I have finally found you, Tian Kai!’ Tan Songyun clenched her fists in anger.

“I-I’m sorry, but that’s not possible. The Shen Family is not accepting any visitors right now, even if that individual is the Fairy Empress herself.”

“I don’t care. I will speak with the man playing that zither today, whether you let me or not.”

Tan Songyun ignored the guard and approached the gates. seaʀᴄh thё ηovёlFire .net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“If you take another step forward, I will have to stop you forcefully!” The guard raised his sword at her.

“You can try.” Tan Songyun retrieved her sword and swung it at the gate, destroying it in the blink of an eye.

The guard rushed to stop her, but he was sent flying the next moment.

“Intruder! We have an intruder!” The guard shouted, his voice resounding throughout the Shen Family’s premises.

Noticing the commotion outside, the Shen Family’s guards and elders quickly moved to assess the situation. Typically, only the guards would intervene, but with the Xi Family and the Dragon Emperor visiting, the elders felt compelled to take action as well.

“Who dares to invade the Shen Family?! Are you looking for an early death?!”

“Elders! That woman claims to be from the Fairy Paradise!” The guard said.

“What? The Fairy Paradise? Are you trying to start a war with the Royal Dragon Family?!” The guards and elders surrounded Tan Songyun.

“I have no business with the Shen Family. I only have business with whoever is playing the zither right now!” Tan Songyun said, releasing her cultivation base.

“An immortal!” The elders were shocked once they realized she was emitting Immortal Qi.

chapters.title: Chapter 1752 Vengeful Fairies
chapters.body:
Chapter 1752 Vengeful Fairies

Shortly before Tan Songyun arrived at the Shen Family, as Shen Shi became more comfortable with the art of embroidery, she turned to Yuan and asked, “Your Majesty, may I ask about your hobbies?”

“I enjoy playing music,” he said. “The zither, especially.”

“Wow! Does that mean you also know the art of sound cultivation?” “Somewhat. I can play a song if you’d like,” said Yuan.

“Please do!” Shen Shi nodded with excitement.

Yuan pulled out the Soul Ensnaring Zither without thinking too much about it and began playing a moment later.

“What a beautiful sound!”

However, shortly after Yuan began playing the zither, a sudden commotion erupted as someone outside unleashed their Immortal Qi, startling everyone in the Shen Family.

Sensing the Immortal Qi, Yuan swiftly used his divine sense to observe the situation outside.

‘A woman?’ Yuan wondered what she was doing with the Shen Family.

Due to the veil blocking her facial features, Yuan had no idea she was Tan Songyun, the Zither Goddess.

Naturally, since he was a professional, Yuan didn’t stop playing the zither despite the commotion.

“D-don’t worry, Lady Xu, Your Majesty, our Shen Family will take care of it as soon as possible…” Shen Shi spoke, but she didn’t look confident at all.

After all, their opponent was an Immortal, and there was not a single Immortal in the Fifth Heaven, much less the Shen Family.

“If you need any help, we’re more than willing to help,” said Yuan.

“If the Dragon King—my father finds out that we failed to protect you, our heads will roll.” Shen Shi politely refused their help.

“In that case, we won’t intervene,” Yuan said as he returned to focusing on his zither. However, he was still paying attention to the situation outside in case this Immortal threatened their plan.

“Senior Immortal, please retrieve your cultivation immediately. We have two guests from the Supreme Heaven in our family right now. They’re not someone even an immortal such as yourself could afford to offend!”

“What? Supreme Heaven?”

Hearing this, Tan Songyun became even more convinced that Tian Kai was inside the Shen Family.

“What are their names?” she asked a moment later.

“One is a young lady from the Xi Family. The other is a Dragon Emperor.”

“They’re not human?” Tan Songyun frowned.

“Of course not.”

“Are you sure their identities are real?” Tan Songyun inquired.

“How rude! Of course, they are real! Why would they fake their identities?!”

“Then let me confirm it,” said Tan Songyun.

“Who the hell are you to make such a demand, anyway?!”

Tan Songyun raised her arm and flipped her palm toward the sky, revealing a floating symbol.

“You’re a member of the Vengeful Fairies?” The elders of the Shen Family recognized her faction.

Though not as powerful or prestigious as the Celestial Overlords, the Vengeful Fairies are a very influential faction with countless connections throughout the Nine Heavens. This faction only accepts female members, and to join, a woman must have been wronged by a man in the past. This rule not only gives the faction a unique identity but also creates a strong bond among its members.

Furthermore, members of the Vengeful Fairies often track down the man who wronged them to exact their revenge as a ritual of sorts. This tradition is so widely known that even if a member attacked the Celestial Emperor himself, the people would only question what the Celestial Emperor had done to wrong that member, not the attack itself.

“I believe the man who wronged me is currently within the Shen Family. If you prevent me from confronting him, I will return with my fellow sisters,” Tan Songyun declared. “However, if I am mistaken, I will offer a proper apology and compensate you all for the trouble I’ve caused here.”

The elders of the Shen Family were caught in a dilemma. Members of the Vengeful Fairies were notoriously stubborn and often unreasonable when pursuing those who had wronged them. If the Shen Family prevented Tan Songyun from seeking her revenge, they would risk retaliation from the Vengeful Fairies and their powerful allies. Yet, if they allowed Tan Songyun to trouble a member of the Xi Family and the Dragon Emperor, their entire family would be in grave danger.

No matter what path the Shen Family chooses, they’d end up in danger.

‘Damn it! Why did the Dragon King have to be absent at times like this?!’ the elders cried inwardly.

“I promise that I just want to confirm the identity of the individual playing the zither. If he’s not the one I am looking for, I will quietly withdraw and never bother the Shen Family again.” Tan Songyun added a moment later.

Meanwhile, inside the building, Yuan raised an eyebrow after realizing that the Immortal was there for him.

‘She’s here for me? Could she be…?’

The image of a certain beauty suddenly appeared inside Yuan’s head.

‘The Zither Goddess? But why would she be here? And who is she looking for? Tian Kai?’

As Yuan pieced the puzzle together, the Shen Family elders were deep in their own deliberations, struggling to make a decision.

“I think we should just let her meet our guests. She said that she only wanted to confirm their identities, and the Vengeful Fairies are a righteous faction. They wouldn’t cause trouble for no reason.”

“Since she’s from the Vengeful Fairies, she’s probably here for the Dragon Emperor.”

“If the Dragon Emperor turns out to be the one she’s looking for, what then?”

“I have a feeling this is just one big misunderstanding.”

“Right? There’s no way the Dragon Emperor would wrong a human woman.”

However, before the Shen Family could reach a decision, they sensed a familiar presence approaching. When they turned, they saw Shen Shi walking toward them with their two esteemed guests. S~eaʀᴄh the Novelƒire(.)ne*t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Tan Songyun instantly focused her attention on Yuan, who was holding the zither in his grasp, almost as if he wanted her to see it.

“Your Majesty! Lady Xi! We apologize for the commotion!” The members of the Shen Family quickly lowered their heads to bow.

Yuan responded, “It’s fine. I don’t blame any of you for it.”

While Xi Meili and Shen Shi halted before reaching the destroyed gates, Yuan continued forward. Noticing his approach, the guards surrounding Tan Songyun quickly stepped aside to clear a path for him.

A few moments later, Yuan stopped just a few steps short of reaching Tan Songyun.

chapters.title: Chapter 1753 Tossed Aside Like Trash
chapters.body:
Chapter 1753 Tossed Aside Like Trash

“Do you mind giving us some space?” Yuan asked the guards surrounding them.

“B-but what if she attacks you?”

“I’ll be fine. She won’t be able to hurt me,” said Yuan, his voice filled with confidence.

The guards exchanged looks with each other before backing away.

“You’re looking for me, right? How can I help you?” Yuan started the conversation.

“I would like to verify your identity. Can you remove your mask for me?” Tan Songyun said after a moment of silence.

“Sorry, but I cannot reveal my face to a stranger. Can you introduce yourself first?” Yuan said.

Tan Songyun clenched her fists, but she played along and introduced herself, “My name is Tan Songyun, member of the Vengeful Fairies, once known as the Zither Goddess!”

‘So it really is her…’ Yuan thought to himself. He never thought he’d meet the Zither Goddess in the Fifth Heaven, and in such a weird fashion, no less.

“I see, but Lady Tan, what is my identity to you? Why do you need to confirm my identity?”

Tan Songyun suddenly removed the veil covering her beautiful features, revealing her ethereal face.

“I am looking for a man—someone who tossed me aside like trash after using me like a toy!” she declared in a cold voice.

Yuan’s eyes widened with shock after hearing this. She was obviously looking for Tian Kai, but why would she say such awful things about Tian Kai, who clearly loved her?

“I-I see… so you’re looking for someone who wronged you… but what does that have to do with me? Was the one who wronged you a Dragon Emperor? I can confidently say that I have never wronged anyone like you in my life…”

“No, the one who wronged me was a human.”

“Then—”

“However, he also has many fake identities, and I wouldn’t be surprised if a Dragon Emperor is one of those identities.”

Tan Songyun lifted her arm to point at the zither in Yuan’s grasp and continued, “There are only two individuals in this world who can play that Soul Ensnaring Zither. Me and that man.”

“This zither?” Yuan caressed his fingers across the zither, playing beautiful notes. “It’s just an instrument. How can there only be two people in the world who can play it?”

“It requires a special technique. Otherwise, it won’t make any sounds,” she explained.

“Ah, it was like that when I first found it, but after tinkering with it for a while, I managed to develop a technique for it,” Yuan chuckled.

Tan Songyun’s eyebrows twitched uncontrollably, her patience running thin.

“I left that Soul Ensnaring Zither in the Lower Heavens. Why did a Dragon Emperor from the upper heavens like yourself visit such a place?”

“Really? I found it in the upper heavens, though.”

“By the way, I never got your name,” Tan Songyun suddenly said.

“My surname is Xiao.”

Yuan was a little worried that giving a random surname might give his identity away, but when he saw no reaction from the Shen Family, he sighed in relief inwardly.

Although there are only a few Dragon Emperors in the Nine Heavens, they rarely make public appearances, and little is known about them. Even families in the upper heavens are often unaware of their true identities, much less a family in the Fifth Heaven.

“Are you sure your surname isn’t ‘Tian’?” Tan Songyun frowned.

“Tian?” The members of the Shen Family raised their eyebrows after hearing this familiar surname.

They recalled a certain incident that happened over three years ago when a Dragon Emperor visited their place. However, the Dragon Emperor disappeared like a ghost, and the Shen Family’s memories of him were stolen by a fugitive. “I’m positive my surname isn’t Tian. That’s a weird assumption to make, Lady Tan.”

“Is that so? For some reason, I am having a hard time believing you.”

“Whether you believe me or not is not my issue.”

Yuan lifted his mask just enough for Tan Songyun to glimpse his face. Since the Shen Family was behind him, they couldn’t see his face unless they used divine sense—and naturally, none of them dared risk offending a Dragon Emperor merely to satisfy their curiosity.

“Are you satisfied now, Lady Tan?” Yuan asked after wearing the mask again.

“No, not really.”

Tan Songyun retrieved a pill from her spatial ring and showed it to him.

“This pill will remove all disguises. If you consume this pill and show me your face again, I’ll be satisfied.”

“…”

Yuan looked at the pill with a stiff smile behind the mask.

“Lady Tan, I have already given you plenty of face and entertained you enough. I would appreciate it if you could stop coming after me as if I were the bad guy here. However, since you are with the Vengeful Fairies, I’ll give you some face and pretend nothing happened here. I am sorry that you had been mistreated by this man you’re looking for, but it has nothing to do with me.”

Yuan turned around and began walking away.

However, Tan Songyun stopped him again, “Wait.”

“What is it now?” Yuan asked without turning around.

“If you’re really not the person I am looking for, I apologize for everything. However, I will need that Soul Ensnaring Zither back. It was mine to begin with.”

“…”

“Of course… if you can prove that it belongs to you.”

Yuan turned to face her and said, “You said only two people in this world—excluding me—can play this zither. Well, play a song for me. I’ll also consider it as your apology.”

“I quit playing the zither long ago,” she said.

“That sounds like an excuse. I was expecting more since you were once called the Zither Goddess. Anyway, unless you play a song, I cannot give you this zither.”

Tan Songyun gritted her teeth. After a moment of silence, she approached Yuan and grabbed the Soul Ensnaring Zither out of his grasp.

Yuan didn’t resist and allowed her to take it.

With the object in her grasp, Tan Songyun placed it on her lap, hovering in the air in a seated position. S~eaʀᴄh the ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Soon, her slender fingers began dancing on the zither, and beautiful musical notes filled the air, quickly mesmerizing the audience.
