chapters.title: Chapter 1754 The Dragon King'S Return
chapters.body:
Chapter 1754 The Dragon King'S Return

As Tan Songyun’s fingers touched the zither, the melodies that flowed captivated everyone who heard them, drawing them into a trance.

When Yuan recognized the song Tan Songyun had specifically chosen, a slight smile appeared on his face. However, the song was traditionally accompanied by a voice, and since Tan Songyun played only the zither, it felt incomplete.

A minute later, Tan Songyun stopped playing the zither to look at Yuan.

“Is that enough proof?” she asked.

Yuan nodded, “Yes, that’s enough proof. Since the zither belonged to you, it’s only right to return it to its rightful owner.”

Without saying another word, Tan Songyun stored the zither inside her spatial ring.

“Can I ask you a question, Lady Tan?” Yuan suddenly spoke.

“What?”

“This man who wronged you… What are you going to do once you find him?”

“I’m going to kill him, then I am going to kill myself,” she calmly responded.

Yuan’s eyes widened after hearing this disturbing response, and he quickly said, “Don’t you think that’s a bit… too much? I don’t know what he did to you, but to take your own life over it…”

Tan Songyun gritted her teeth and said, “What do you know about my life? I pursued the zither for most of my life because of that man, only to find out that it’d been a waste!”

“Although I don’t know the details, I am willing to listen to your story,” said Yuan.

Tan Songyun narrowed her eyes at him and sneered in disdain, “Since when were Dragon Emperors so kind and compassionate? As far as I am aware, they’re all arrogant bastards who only care about themselves and their authority.”

“You speak as if you know them personally.”

“I have never met any of them, but my fellow sisters in the faction have. If you knew how many women have been wronged by these so-called Dragon Emperors, your view of them would instantly shatter—Oh, right. You’re also one, aren’t you?”

“I may be a Dragon Emperor, but that doesn’t automatically mean I am affiliated or friends with the other Dragon Emperors. As a matter of fact, I only stay within my territory and don’t interact with them.”

“Sorry, but I am not buying it.” Tan Songyun sneered.

Yuan shrugged, “You are free to believe what you want. In any case, are you satisfied with your investigation? Am I free to leave now?”

“Yes, go ahead. I won’t stop you.”

Yuan nodded, turned around, and walked back inside the Shen Family.

However, Tan Songyun remained standing outside for some reason.

“Um… Lady Tan, what are you still doing here?” one of the elders approached her and asked.

“What? I can’t be here?” she glared at him.

“N-no… but…”

“Don’t mind me. I won’t cause any more trouble for the Shen Family.”

“If you say so…”

Tan Songyun retrieved several spirit stones and handed them to the elder, “This is for the gate I damaged.”

The elder accepted the spirit stones and went back inside the building while the guards remained outside to fix the damaged gate.

However, Tan Songyun remained standing outside the Shen Family’s household even long after Yuan and the others left.

“I can’t believe she mistook you for someone else, even taking away your zither,” Xi Meili commented out loud as they returned to their room. seaʀᴄh thё Nôvel(F)ire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Yuan merely shook his head with a bitter smile on his face. He didn’t want to act like a stranger to Tan Songyun, but he had no choice. Not only would revealing his identity endanger them, but it could also endanger Tan Songyun.

‘Since she’s staying outside and refusing to leave, I can safely assume that she’s not done with me yet.’

Instead of being annoyed by Tan Songyun’s persistence, Yuan was relieved, as this gave him another opportunity to speak with her later.

‘I’ll have a proper talk with her later… when I acquire the Ancient Seal and leave the Shen Family.’

“Lady Xu, Your Majesty, we want to apologize for what—”

“Save it.” Yuan stopped Shen Shi and the elders from apologizing to him.

“This wasn’t your fault. Even if you wanted to chase her away, you didn’t have the capability since you don’t have any immortals in your family,” he elaborated.

“Still, we won’t be able to relax properly if we don’t apologize…” said Shen Shi.

“If you insist on apologizing, I won’t stop you, but let me warn you that it might offend me more than what transpired today.”

Shen Shi and the elders trembled after hearing such words, and they quickly gave up on trying to apologize.

A few hours later, the Dragon King returned to the Shen Family, and the first thing he noticed upon his return was the veiled individual standing outside their destroyed gates.

He landed before the guards and inquired about the situation.

“What the hell happened to my gates?”

“They were… destroyed by a guest…”

“What? Who dares to damage our precious gates? Not even the Fairy Empress would do something so bold!” the Dragon King exclaimed.

The guards there didn’t say anything, but their eyes were all pointing at Tan Songyun.

“What about the Dragon Emperor and Lady Xi?” he then asked.

The guards proceeded to explain the whole ordeal to him.

The Dragon King became livid after learning of the situation, and he approached Tan Songyun with heavy steps.

“Did you destroy my gates?” he questioned her.

“Yes, and I have already paid the compensation.”

“Clearly not, because you are still breathing!” the Dragon King growled, his eyes flickering with killing intent. Normally, he wouldn’t get so agitated over such an issue, but with the Dragon Emperor and the Xi Family present, things were different.

Seeing this, Tan Songyun released her Immortal Qi again.

“An Immortal?!” the Dragon King was startled by her immense aura and took steps back.

‘Those idiots forgot to mention that she was an Immortal!’ he cried inwardly.

“Although I said I won’t trouble the Shen Family again, it’s a different story if you provoke me,” she said a moment later.

chapters.title: Chapter 1755 A Fraud
chapters.body:
Chapter 1755 A Fraud

“I have already spoken and settled things with the Dragon Emperor, so why are you trying to start something you cannot end properly, Dragon King?” Tan Songyun removed her veil again, revealing her face to him.

“Y-you are!” the Dragon King swiftly recognized her beautiful face, “You’re the Zither Goddess—the one who was always beside the Fairy Empress! You left the Fairy Paradise and ascended to the Sixth Heaven just a few hundred years ago, and you’ve already managed to become an Immortal?!”

Not only was Dragon King familiar with her, he’d seen her on multiple occasions with the Fairy Empress. Although they were not friends, they were somewhat acquaintances.

“What in heaven’s name are you doing at my Shen Family, harassing the Dragon Emperor and the Xi Family?! Do you have any idea what kind of trouble you’re stirring up?! Not even the Fairy Empress could save you if you offend a Dragon Emperor, much less the Xi Family!” the Dragon King exclaimed, becoming even more aggregated after learning of her identity.

“It’s none of your business, and even if the Dragon Emperor comes for my life, I won’t ask for any help,” said Tan Songyun.

The Dragon King sucked his teeth and sneered, “I see that you’re still the same resentful individual as before, even after hundreds of years and becoming an Immortal.”

“Anyway, even if you don’t ask for help, your actions can still affect those around you. Don’t drag my Shen Family into your mess because if anything happens to us, I’ll haunt you after my death.”

Tan Songyun sneered and said, “Thanks for the warning. As a thank you, let me give you some good information. That Dragon Emperor with your Shen Family… is a fake.”

“W-what the fuck are you saying?! How can the Dragon Emperor be fake?! His presence alone caused my bloodline to tremble, and I am a Dragon King! Besides a Dragon Emperor, what could possibly cause such a reaction?!” the Dragon King refused to believe Tan Songyun.

Tan Songyun then asked, “First and foremost, why would a Dragon Emperor come down to the Fifth Heaven?”

“He’s on an important mission—and don’t even bother asking me about it. I can’t tell you.”

“And what do you even know about Dragon Emperors? Although you are considered royalty in the Fifth Heaven, you’re not even worth being servants of the Royal Families in the upper heavens.”

“Dragon Emperors are beings who spend most of their time seated on their thrones, delegating their every command to their servants. They almost never involve themselves personally unless their status is at stake. Aside from the Dragon Ancestor, not even the Royal Dragon Families can compel them to act.”

“You make it sound like you know them personally!” the Dragon King sneered, still refusing to believe Tan Songyun.

“You’re right. I don’t know any Dragon Emperors, but I know many who do. I joined a faction called the Vengeful Fairies in the upper heavens. Perhaps you have heard of them?”

“Vengeful Fairies?!” the Dragon King exclaimed. Naturally, he’d heard of them.

Tan Songyun showed the Dragon King her faction symbol to back up her claim and continued, “Many of my sisters were wronged by these Dragon Emperors. However, they are unable to get their revenge because touching a Dragon Emperor is akin to touching a dragon’s reverse scale and would spark a war between the Vengeful Fairies and the Dragon Families throughout the Nine Heavens, something nobody wants to see happen.”

A Dragon Emperor’s status is equivalent to heaven to the dragon race. To harm or kill a Dragon Emperor would be tantamount to declaring war against their entire race. The Vengeful Fairies may be powerful, but they certainly are not powerful enough to fight the entire dragon race, hence why they are unable to touch these Dragon Emperors.

It wasn’t just the Vengeful Fairies; even someone as powerful as the Celestial Emperor couldn’t lay a hand on a Dragon Emperor without a justified reason. Such an action could ignite a war between two major races.

“Also, you may not know this, but there are no Dragon Emperors with the surname Xiao in the Nine Heavens. Yet, that Dragon Emperor said his name was Xiao. How do you explain this?” Tan Songyun said.

“T-that’s impossible. No one would dare to steal a Dragon Emperor’s identity—that would provoke the wrath of the entire dragon race. And what about Lady Xi? She’s from the Xi Family,” the Dragon King said, his tone laced with growing doubt.

“I don’t know much about the Xi Family, but there’s no doubt she’s a fake too—unless she showed you undeniable proof of her identity,” Tan Songyun said firmly.

‘N-now that I think about it, we don’t have any proof of their identity, and we’re just blindly trusting them.’ The Dragon King began sweating inwardly.

Although Xi Meili was ready to provide proof of their identity, the Dragon King lacked the courage to request it, fearing it might be perceived as a sign of disrespect.

Naturally, Xi Meili and Yuan had anticipated this reaction and counted on it.

“I’ll let you in on another secret, Dragon King. That man who claims to be a Dragon Emperor is actually a fraud, a thief, and a liar. He tricked my inexperienced self, used my body, and promised we’d be together. However, he turned out to be a known playboy who targets maidens for their Yin Essence to cultivate his evil cultivation technique. I also learned that he was an assassin who has stolen countless lives.”

Yuan, who was hearing all of this from inside his room, was shocked speechless. Although Tan Songyun couldn’t detect Yuan’s divine sense, she was confident that he was watching her. Despite that, she still revealed all of this information to the Dragon King. S~eaʀᴄh the NôvelFire(.)net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“A fraud? Evil cultivation techniques?” Yuan muttered to himself in a low voice.

As unbelievable as these claims sounded, Yuan couldn’t completely dismiss them, as he lacked Tian Kai’s memories to confirm or deny their validity.

‘There’s no way, right…?’

He had lived many lives before his current one, and the possibility that one of them might have been evil couldn’t be entirely ruled out.

Of course, Yuan didn’t want to believe this, but he had no choice but to take Tan Songyun’s words as it is until he regained Tian Kai’s memories.

chapters.title: Chapter 1756 Doubting Their Identities
chapters.body:
Chapter 1756 Doubting Their Identities

“Z-Zither Goddess, I am going to ask you a question, and you’ll need to answer me as if your life depends on it.”

The Dragon King spoke with a grim expression shortly after Tan Songyun exposed the Dragon Emperor.

Tan Songyun didn’t wait for the question and responded, “That Dragon Emperor is a fake, and I am willing to stake my life on this claim.”

The Dragon King stared at her in silence for a long moment before nodding his head, “Alright. I believe you. Honestly, I have been a little suspicious of their identity since the very beginning, but it wasn’t enough to make me call them out. After all, there’s a slight chance that they were the real deal.”

“However, after hearing your words, it has become clear to me that they are likely imposters.”

Tan Songyun nodded, “Thank you for believing me.”

“That begs the question, what is their objective?” she asked the next moment.

The Dragon King hesitated for a moment before responding, “They’re here for my Ancient Seal.”

“What is that?” Tan Songyun raised her eyebrows in a puzzled manner.

The existence of Ancient Seals was a highly sensitive and closely guarded secret, with the Celestial Emperor actively suppressing their spread to minimize the risk of anyone attempting to obtain them.

Aware of how sensitive the information was, the Dragon King said, “I cannot tell you. All I can say is that it is an extremely dangerous object that must not fall into the wrong hands, or it could bring disaster to the Nine Heavens.”

‘Being disaster to the Nine Heavens…? Why would he want something dangerous like that?’ Tan Songyun pondered inwardly.

“Anyway, what are you going to do now that you know they’re imposters?”

The Dragon King fell silent again. Sometime later, he said, “I cannot outright call them frauds. It’s not that I don’t trust you, but there is still a tiny chance they could be the real deal.”

Tan Songyun couldn’t fault the Dragon King for being cautious. After all, a single mistake or offense toward a Dragon Emperor could lead to the execution of his entire Shen Family.

Moreover, although Tan Songyun was 99 percent confident that the Dragon Emperor was Tian Kai in disguise, she was not absolutely certain herself. The possibility that the Dragon Emperor was a real Dragon Emperor had crossed her mind, but she simply couldn’t believe it.

“I am going to go after the Dragon Emperor’s identity since it’d be difficult to expose the one who claims to be from the Xi Family, as I know nothing about the Xi Family myself,” said the Dragon King.

Sometime later, the Dragon King summoned everyone from the Shen Family, excluding Yuan and Xi Meili, before revealing the truth to them. Sёarᴄh the Nôvelƒire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“What?! They’re imposters?!”

“That’s ridiculous!”

“Father, why would you believe that woman who almost ruined us?!” Shen Shi exclaimed.

The Dragon King sighed, “I have known the Zither Goddess for much longer than everyone here. She may be difficult at times, but she is someone trustworthy. She also has no reason to screw us over.”

“I don’t believe they are imposters!” Shen Shi declared. “Father! Don’t do this! If you’re wrong and offend the Dragon Emperor, our entire Shen Family will be executed!”

The rest of the Shen Family agreed with Shen Shi. Nobody there wanted to confront the Dragon Emperor, even if there was a chance he could be an imposter. The risks were simply too high.

Seeing their response, Tan Songyun said, “So you’re going to continue treating him like a real Dragon Emperor despite knowing he’s an imposter? If the real Dragon Emperors from the upper heavens were to learn of this, you’d still be facing execution.” “Are you threatening us, you bitch?!” Shen Shi roared as she pointed at Tan Songyun.

“That’s quite harsh. I am merely stating the facts and possibilities. I have no intention of snitching, especially not to those Dragon Emperors,” Tan Songyun sneered.

Meanwhile, inside their room, Yuan said to Xi Meili, “They’re doubting our identity because of the Zither Goddess. There’s a good chance they’ll confront us.”

“What?! What should we do?!” Xi Meili panicked after hearing this.

“From what I’m hearing, they won’t come after you but me. I don’t know how they’ll ask me to prove my identity, but I’ll go along with it. This will be a good learning experience for the future if we need to prove our identities again,” he said.

“What if you fail to prove your identity?” Xi Meili then asked.

“Well… then we run.” Yuan shrugged, leaving her speechless.

Sometime later, as the Shen Family continued to argue back and forth about whether they should confront Yuan, their meeting was abruptly interrupted by Yuan himself.

“So you doubt my identity?” Yuan had appeared in the meeting room without alerting anyone, as they were too absorbed in their conversation.

“D-Dragon Emperor?!”

His sudden appearance shocked everyone but Tan Songyun.

Shen Shi quickly approached him and said, “T-that’s not true! I believe you, Your Majesty!”

Yuan ignored her, looked straight at the Dragon King, and said, “We offered to prove our identities when we first arrived, but you declined our offer. Now, you doubt our identities? How do you explain this, Dragon King?”

“T-that’s…” The Dragon King was speechless. He couldn’t come up with an excuse, no matter how hard he tried.

Tan Songyun then spoke, “If you’re the real deal, then you wouldn’t mind proving your identity to us, right? Of course, we won’t be relying on any of your methods since we can’t trust you.”

“What do you have in mind?” Yuan played along.

Tan Songyun turned to look at the Dragon King, who quickly responded, “I-I am a Dragon King! Regardless of my achievements and background in the Fifth Heaven, my bloodline is powerful enough to make me a Dragon King. Therefore, we will test your bloodline against mine. Do you have an issue with this, Dragon Emperor?”

Yuan shook his head and calmly responded, “Fine by me.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1757 Dragon Blood Carp
chapters.body:
Chapter 1757 Dragon Blood Carp

With Yuan’s approval, the Dragon King said, “In that case, follow me.”

The Dragon King led Yuan to an area within the Shen Family’s territory. At first glance, it appeared to be a serene garden, but scattered among the greenery were several unusual objects that seemed out of place in such a setting.

One of these objects was a stone statue carved into the shape of a dragon’s head, resting majestically atop a stone pedestal. This dragon’s head had its mouth open and tongue slightly sticking out. “This statue can determine whether someone is a dragon or not by assessing their blood. Before we begin the real assessment, we need to make sure you are a real dragon,” said the Dragon King.

If Tan Songyun was correct, there was a strong possibility that Yuan wasn’t even a dragon. In that case, this simple test could expose Yuan’s identity.

‘If he turns out to be a dragon, it won’t prove he’s a Dragon Emperor. However, if he isn’t a dragon, we won’t even need to assess his bloodline anymore.’

The Dragon King dripped his blood onto the statue’s tongue, feeding it. The moment his blood made contact, the statue’s tongue absorbed it instantly.

He then turned to look at Tan Songyun and spoke, “Can you feed it some of your blood?”

Tan Songyun nodded and approached the statue, feeding it her blood.

However, when her blood touched the statue, instead of being absorbed, it evaporated. “Dragon Emperor, it’s your turn.”

The Dragon King looked at Yuan, who calmly fed his blood to the statue. Before feeding his blood, Yuan activated his dragon transformation just in case.

Everyone watched in anticipation as Yuan’s blood touched the statue.

In the end, his blood is absorbed by the statue, confirming that he is a dragon.

Shen Shi sighed in relief after seeing this.

“Father, it’s not too late to stop this farce and apologize to the Dragon Emperor,” she said to him.

“He’s only managed to prove that he’s a dragon, that’s all,” said the Dragon King, ignoring Shen Shi’s advice. Searᴄh the ηovёlFire .net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Meanwhile, Tan Songyun was greatly surprised by the result. In fact, she was the most shocked out of everyone there.

‘He’s a dragon? That’s not possible… Unless Tian Kai was a dragon this entire time, this doesn’t make any sense!’ Tan Songyun clenched her fists anxiously. She had always believed Tian Kai to be human, and the possibility that she might have been wrong all along left her feeling deeply perplexed.

After all, if she was wrong about such a crucial detail, it raised the unsettling possibility that she might be wrong about other things as well.

‘What if he’s actually not Tian Kai but a real Dragon Emperor…?’ Tan Songyun swallowed nervously at this thought. Unfortunately, it was too late for her to do anything about it, as she’d already accused him of being an imposter.

“Follow me…” The Dragon King brought Yuan to the real assessment shortly later. Although the Dragon King seemed fine outside, he was also panicking a little inwardly.

‘Damn it… What if he really is a Dragon Emperor?’ The Dragon King felt himself on the verge of a breakdown at the mere thought of this possibility.

A few minutes later, they arrived at a large pond. Yuan used his divine sense to inspect it and discovered a single living entity within—a large red-colored fish with a fierce, almost menacing appearance.

“This is a unique creature our Shen Family has nurtured for thousands of years,” the Dragon King explained. “We call it the Dragon Blood Carp because it only consumes the blood of dragons as its food.”

“We started by feeding it low-quality blood, but once it gets a taste of higher-quality blood, it refuses to go back to the inferior kind—even if it means starving to death,” the Dragon King added.

“One day, I accidentally let the Dragon Blood Carp taste my blood. Ever since then, I’ve had to feed it my blood to keep it alive. No matter what kind of blood we offer it now, it will only accept mine,” the Dragon King continued with a resigned tone.

“Here, let me show you.”

The Dragon King glanced at Shen Shi and gestured toward her. She nodded in acknowledgment and allowed a drop of her blood to fall into the pond.

The moment the Dragon Blood Carp sensed the presence of blood in its territory, it darted toward it with eager anticipation. However, as soon as it realized the blood wasn’t the Dragon King’s, its interest vanished, and it swam away indifferently.

The Dragon King repeated Shen Shi’s actions a moment later. This time, the Dragon Blood Carp eagerly consumed his blood until nothing was left.

“This is a unique test that’s impossible to rig,” said the Dragon King a moment later.

Yuan looked at the Dragon Blood Carp and said, “Are you sure you want to use this method to prove my identity? Once the Dragon Blood Carp tastes my blood and I leave this place, it will inevitably starve to death, you know?”

The Dragon King nodded and said, “Yes, and I am prepared for it.”

The Dragon Blood Carp was a treasured creature of the Shen Family, but if sacrificing it could confirm the Dragon Emperor’s identity, the Dragon King was willing to make that trade. In his eyes, it would also serve as a fitting punishment for the Shen Family’s audacity in doubting the Dragon Emperor.

Before Yuan fed his blood to the Dragon Blood Carp, the Dragon King had several other members of the Shen Family offer their blood to the creature. Each time, the fish swam toward the blood out of instinct but quickly rejected it, proving that it would only consume the Dragon King’s blood.

“It’s your turn now, Dragon Emperor,” the Dragon King said with a solemn expression.

Yuan nodded, extending his arm over the pond before letting a single drop of his blood fall into the water.

The moment the Dragon Blood Carp sensed the blood, it darted toward it with remarkable speed. The Shen Family members watched intently, holding their breath, their faces etched with anxiety and worry, as if the very survival of their family hinged on the outcome.

chapters.title: Chapter 1758 Dragon Blood Carp(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1758 Dragon Blood Carp(2)

The instant the Dragon Blood Carp sensed Yuan’s blood entering the pond, it rushed over with the desperation of a starving creature. However, instead of consuming the blood immediately, it paused and stared at it intently, almost as if it couldn’t believe it was being offered such precious blood.

Naturally, the Dragon King wasn’t aware of this, and in his eyes, the Dragon Blood Carp had refused Yuan’s blood. Searᴄh the NôvelFire(.)net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“I knew it! Dragon Emperor, my ass! I knew you were a phony this entire time!” The Dragon King pointed at Yuan and started cursing out loud.

Shen Shi and the others only stared in shock.

“Fuck! I can’t believe I almost fell for your scam!”

Tan Songyun merely shook her head in relief, ‘I knew it…’

“Apprehend this fraud!” the Dragon King demanded.

Nearly everyone there surrounded Yuan, who remained calm despite the outcome.

He glanced at the pond and calmly said, “You might want to take another look at the Dragon Blood Carp.”

“What?” the Dragon King muttered in confusion.

Despite their skepticism, everyone instinctively followed his suggestion and turned their gaze toward the pond.

The drop of blood Yuan had dropped into the pond was gone, leaving only the Dragon Blood Carp. However, the creature was behaving strangely, its body twitching and jerking erratically for reasons unknown.

“W-what’s happening to my Dragon Blood Carp?”

Just as the Dragon King voiced his question aloud, the Dragon Blood Carp suddenly leaped out of the water, releasing a deafening roar—a sound unmistakably that of a dragon’s cry.

Instead of falling back into the water, the Dragon Blood Carp remained hovering in the air. Moments later, its body began to transform, expanding in size and length.

Within moments, the Dragon Blood Carp transformed beyond recognition. It no longer resembled a fish but had become a serpent-like entity, its body covered in shimmering, crimson scales.

“What in heaven’s name…?”

The Dragon King fell on his butt, his eyes wide with shock and disbelief.

A common legend tells of a carp that, by swimming upstream and leaping over the Dragon Gate, transforms into a dragon. While this tale has endured for billions of years, very few have ever witnessed a fish truly transform into a dragon.

A common legend tells of a carp that, by swimming upstream and leaping over the Dragon Gate, transforms into a dragon. While this tale has endured for billions of years, very few have ever witnessed a fish truly transform into a dragon.

After its transformation, the Dragon Blood Carp fixed its gaze on Yuan and lowered its head in reverence.

“Thank you for blessing this unworthy being with your precious blood. Because of your blood, I was finally able to evolve,” the Dragon Blood Carp communicated through voice transmission, its mouth remaining still.

“It wasn’t my intention, but I’m happy for you,” said Yuan.

The Dragon Blood Carp turned its gaze to the Dragon King and continued, “Though it was largely your blood that allowed me to reach this point, and I am grateful for your care in nurturing me, I am truly disappointed in you, who fails to recognize true greatness even when he stands before you.”

“What…? How are you even…?” the Dragon King remained seated on the ground with a dumbfounded face.

“Are you surprised by my ability to speak? Believe it or not, I have lived for much longer than any of you can imagine, and before being brought to the Shen Family, I have been to all kinds of places in the Nine Heavens,” said the Dragon Blood Carp.

“My consciousness and intelligence have solidified through tens of thousands of years, but without completing my evolution, I remained as a mere fish.”

The Dragon Blood Carp, who was no different than a unique-looking fish before his transformation, had suddenly become a divine beast with a cultivation that almost rivaled Tan Songyun, who was an Immortal.

“With all that said, it’s about time for me to leave,” the Dragon Blood Carp continued, “Benefactor, I will remember you. If we ever meet again, I will definitely repay you for your assistance today.”

Although Yuan was a little puzzled by the situation, he nodded.

“Ah, before you go, can you answer a simple question?”

“Of course.”

“My blood… how does it compare to the Dragon King? Do I qualify to be a Dragon Emperor?”

The Dragon King trembled after being reminded of their situation.

“Although I have never tasted the blood of a Dragon Emperor, I can assure everyone here that the Benefactor’s blood is incomparably superior to the Dragon King’s blood,” the Dragon Blood Carp replied confidently.

“Even if the Dragon King were to cultivate his bloodline for a million years, it’d never compare to the Benefactor’s bloodline.”

Yuan, satisfied with the answer, nodded with a smile, “Thank you for your honesty, Dragon Blood Carp.”

“Then until we meet next time.”

The Dragon Blood Carp proceeded to swim toward the sky, disappearing into the clouds within seconds.

Once the Dragon Blood Carp departed, an eerie silence enveloped the area, so profound that even the sound of a pin dropping miles away would have been audible.

“So…” Yuan suddenly spoke, breaking the silence. Moreover, he activated True Dragon Transformation and removed the suppression on his aura, causing his presence to envelope the entire place.

“Am I still a fraud in your eyes, Dragon King?”

After experiencing Yuan’s unrestricted presence for the first time, Tan Songyun began sweating profusely. The Dragon King dropped to his knees and kowtowed before Yuan, his voice trembling with desperation. “Please have mercy, Your Majesty! I was foolish to doubt your identity! If you must unleash your anger, do so upon me, but I beg you to spare my family!”

Seeing this, the rest of the Shen Family also fell to their knees and began begging for mercy.

Yuan ignored their pleas and instead turned his attention to Tan Songyun, whose complexion had turned as pale as a ghost.

“This all started because of you, Lady Tan. What do you suggest I do?”

Tan Songyun clenched her fists and jaw, struggling to maintain her composure. After a moment of silence, she lowered her head and said, “You’re right. This is entirely my fault. I humbly ask that you punish me alone and spare the Shen Family.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1759 Acquiring The Second Ancient Seal
chapters.body:
Chapter 1759 Acquiring The Second Ancient Seal

“Despite your earlier disrespectful behavior, I forgave you and allowed you to walk away,” Yuan said to Tan Songyun, his tone cold and unyielding. “But you chose to persist in slandering me. While I am forgiving, my patience has its limits. This time, Lady Tan, you haven’t just slandered me—you’ve also involved the Shen Family in your reckless actions.”

“I have no excuses,” Tan Songyun said, her voice weak and apologetic. “My hatred and desire for revenge clouded my judgment. If you wish to take my life for offending you, I will not protest. However, I humbly plead with you to spare the Shen Family.”

“At least you can admit when you are wrong. Unfortunately, you are not in a position to make any request at this moment.”

The Shen Family trembled at these ominous words.

“I will deal with you later.”

Yuan turned to look at the Dragon King and the rest of the Shen Family.

“Dragon King, I will give you a chance to talk before I give you my judgment.”

The Dragon King quickly responded, “I have no excuses, Your Majesty. I deserve death for doubting your identity, not to mention my unruly behavior. I know that I am in no position to request anything, but I just hope Your Majesty can forgive my family. They were against testing your identity from the very beginning, but I forced them to go along with my foolishness.”

The Dragon King did not lie. The rest of the Shen Family were still reluctant to confront Yuan despite knowing there was a good chance he was a fake.

After a moment of silence, Yuan spoke, “I don’t blame any of you for doubting my identity. As a matter of fact, I am a newly appointed Dragon Emperor.”

“A new Dragon Emperor…?” The Dragon King muttered.

There could only be five Dragon Emperors in existence, so the only way for another Dragon Emperor to be appointed was through the death or retirement of an existing Dragon Emperor.

If Yuan had been recently appointed to his position, it meant that he was replacing one who passed away.

‘The last time a Dragon Emperor passed away, we were notified even while in the Fifth Heaven, and I was ordered to personally travel to the upper heavens to attend their funeral. Why didn’t we hear anything this time?’ the Dragon King wondered, his mind racing with confusion over the lack of communication.

However, the Dragon King did not doubt Yuan’s identity despite this. He figured it was due to other issues.

Yuan continued, his tone unwavering. “I have a rule: if I am going to punish someone, I must punish everyone involved. However, there is one individual who truly does not deserve any punishment. Out of consideration for that person, I will spare the Shen Family this time.”

The Dragon King and the others felt as if a mountain had been lifted off their shoulders after hearing Yuan’s words.

“Thank you for showing us mercy, Your Majesty!” the Dragon King banged his head on the ground as he kowtowed.

Although Yuan didn’t specify who this individual was, everyone knew he was talking about Shen Shi.

However, in reality, Yuan never intended to punish them. After all, they were correct, and he was a fraud.

Yuan cleared his throat and said, “Dragon King, stand up.”

The Dragon King immediately stood up, not even bothering to wipe the blood off his forehead unless he was ordered to do so.

Yuan made a beckoning gesture, almost as if he was asking for something.

The Dragon King quickly understood what Yuan wanted and retrieved the Ancient Seal.

“I will leave this in your hands,” said the Dragon King, and he handed the Ancient Seal to Yuan.

Yuan smiled inwardly after collecting the second of four Ancient Seals out there.

“I will let the Xi Family know of your cooperation when I return.”

“There’s no need for that, Your Majesty.” The Dragon King swiftly rejected.

“Are you sure?”

“I am certain.” “Okay. Anyway, now that my business is done here, I won’t linger any longer.” Yuan proceeded to let Xi Meili know of the outcome through voice transmission.

The next moment, Xi Meili appeared before them.

“That took longer than anticipated. Was there an issue?” Xi Meili asked, pretending to have seen nothing.

Yuan shook his head and calmly said, “There’s no issue at all. We can leave now.”

However, before they left, Yuan turned to look at Tan Songyun and said, “Lady Tan, you and I are not done yet. Follow me.”

Tan Songyun silently nodded and followed.

‘Good luck, Zither Goddess.’ The Dragon King prayed for her inwardly.

As much as he wanted to help her, she was on her own.

After traveling for several days, Yuan finally stopped somewhere in a secluded area.

‘Yu Ning, can you create a concealment formation around us?’ Yuan asked her.

“Of course.”

Yu Ning took control of Yuan’s body for a moment, creating a powerful concealment formation in the blink of an eye.

‘He’s an Array Master as well?’ The Zither Goddess swallowed nervously.

Once the formation was set up and Yuan regained control over his body, he asked Xi Meili to leave them alone for a moment.

“Lady Tan, I would like to ask you a few questions. You must answer them truthfully no matter what. Consider this your punishment for slandering me.”

Tan Songyun was a little bewildered by such a punishment, but she still nodded her head in acknowledgment.

“First and foremost, can you tell me about the person you’re looking for?”

“…”

Tan Songyun answered after a moment of silence, “His name was Tian Kai, my music teacher and lover—or so I thought.”

“How long have you known this Tian Kai?” Sёarᴄh the nôᴠel Fire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“About a year before he left me.”

“What did you two do during that time?”

“He taught me the zither during the day and showed me love during the night,” she replied, her voice trembling with suppressed emotion. Despite her attempt to mask her feelings, the anguish and hatred in her tone were unmistakable.

“What went wrong?” Yuan then asked.

Hearing this question, Tan Songyun’s body trembled.

“Everything…” she sighed.

chapters.title: Chapter 1760 Existential Crisis
chapters.body:
Chapter 1760 Existential Crisis

“Can you elaborate?” Yuan asked a moment later.

Tan Songyun frowned and said, “I don’t mean to be rude, but why are you so interested in my business, Dragon Emperor? I thought dragons couldn’t care less about species that don’t have dragon blood flowing through their veins.”

“Where did you hear that? I’m actually very interested in humans, especially those who are willing to offend a Dragon Emperor for their revenge.”

Tan Songyun narrowed her eyes and said, “You want to know? Very well, I’ll tell you. After all, this might be my last time telling this story.”

“After Tian Kai disappeared, I joined a music sect to improve my zither skills and cultivation further. Once I was prepared, I ascended the Nine Heavens because Tian Kai said he was waiting for me in the upper heavens.”

“However, as I ascended and gained access to more information, I began uncovering the truth about Tian Kai,” Tan Songyun said, her voice heavy with bitterness. “According to numerous sources, including the Celestial Emperor himself, Tian Kai is none other than the infamous killer known as the Phantom Assassin—a wanted fugitive responsible for countless deaths and the destruction of many families.”

As soon as Yuan heard the name ‘Celestial Emperor’ come out of Tan Songyun’s mouth, he knew that she had been mislead by false rumors. However, he didn’t say anything and allowed her to talk without interruption.

“Not only is he an assassin, but he’s also an evil dual cultivator who targets maidens for their Yin Essence. He’s tricked thousands of maidens, promising them eternal love. I was one of these foolish maidens.”

Tan Songyun continued to recall everything she’d heard about Tian Kai, which was enough to write an entire novel, leaving Yuan speechless.

‘Celestial Emperor, that son of a bitch! How dare he spread so many false rumors! This much slander is enough to anger even Buddha!’ he cried inwardly.

“I’ve heard from another woman that Tian Kai had locked her in his basement for ten years, and he—”

“Okay, I have heard enough and get the gist of it,” Yuan eventually interrupted her.

“After hearing so many things, I have only one more question for you.”

“Lady Tan… is your faith in this Tian Kai that little?”

“What…?” Tan Songyun’s eyes widened, almost as if she wasn’t expecting such a question.

“Haaa…” Yuan decided to remove his mask, revealing his handsome face to her.

“Y-y-you are—!” Tan Songyun stammered in shock, stumbling backward before collapsing onto the ground and landing on her buttocks.

“Before you say anything, I am not Tian Kai,” Yuan said.

“…”

After a moment of silence, Tan Songyun stood up and roared, “As if I’d believe you after fooling me and the Shen Family, you trickery bastard! I knew it was you all along, Tian Kai!”

Yuan shook his head and sighed, “Once again, I am not Tian Kai. The Tian Kai you know and love is dead.”

“W-what?” Tan Songyun almost fell down again after hearing this shocking information.

“D-do you really take me for a fool?! No, even someone without a brain can tell that’s a lie! How much longer are you going to mock me, Tian Kai?! I should slay you right now!” Tan Songyun’s eyes began tearing up.

It had been tens of thousands of years since Tan Songyun last saw Tian Kai’s face. And despite the deep hatred she harbored for him, the moment she saw his face again, tears welled up in her eyes and began to fall uncontrollably.

“Please, calm down for a moment and listen to me, Lady Tan—Zither Goddess,” Yuan said gently. Sёarᴄh the Novёlƒire.n(e)t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Tian Kai is dead, and I am his reincarnation. While I don’t yet have all of Tian Kai’s memories, I do remember how we met and the year we spent together,” he explained, his tone steady but laced with sincerity.

“Reincarnation…?” Tan Songyun’s jaw dropped.

“I know this will be hard to believe, but I need you to trust me,” Yuan said, his voice calm yet earnest. “As for why Tian Kai had to leave… it was most likely because of the Celestial Emperor, who likely spread all those nasty and false rumors about him.”

“Why would the Celestial Emperor slander Tian Kai?” Tan Songyun asked with a doubtful frown.

“They—we are enemies. Although I don’t know why the Celestial Emperor decided to use such tactics, I can say with confidence that none of the rumors are true. Tian Kai truly loved you, Zither Goddess.”

“Why… Why were you pretending to be a Dragon Emperor?” she asked a moment later.

“I needed to acquire their Ancient Seal,” he didn’t hide the truth since she already knew it.

“I know, but why? According to the Dragon King, it’s a dangerous object that could endanger the Nine Heavens. Why do you need something so dangerous?”

“I need it to open the Primordial Realm to help a friend. That’s all.”

“The Primordial Realm?! Are you insane?!” Tan Songyun exclaimed, her voice rising with disbelief. “And you expect me to believe you’re not a wanted fugitive when you’re trying to open a prison that holds some of the most infamous criminals in the entire Nine Heavens?!”

“Like I said, it’s to help a friend. I have no intention of releasing the criminals inside, either,” Yuan said.

“Sorry, but everything you’ve told me so far is incredibly far-fetched and impossible to believe,” Tan Songyun sneered, her tone laced with skepticism.

“Zither Goddess, if I intended to deceive you, I wouldn’t have gone to such lengths to reveal my identity after all the effort I put into convincing you that I am a Dragon Emperor, let alone bring up Tian Kai. What could I possibly gain from doing this?” “That’s…” Tan Songyun couldn’t refute his logic.

“As a matter of fact, I am currently targeted by the Celestial Emperor, so it’s incredibly risky for me to reveal myself. However, I couldn’t bear seeing you suffer because of some false rumors. Zither Goddess, I will ask you again.”

“Is your faith in Tian Kai—in me—that little?”

Tan Songyun trembled before falling silent, her expression reflecting the turmoil of an existential crisis.

chapters.title: Chapter 1761 Tan Songyun'S Decision
chapters.body:
Chapter 1761 Tan Songyun'S Decision

Tan Songyun recalled every moment of the year she had spent with Tian Kai—the emotions that had consumed her, the warmth of his presence, and the touch of his fingers on her body.

Then, she remembered the countless rumors she had heard about Tian Kai over tens of thousands of years, along with the hatred and anger that had festered within her. While the year she had spent with Tian Kai had been filled with joy, she couldn’t simply cast aside the anguish and betrayal that had consumed her for thousands of years, as if it had never existed.

Even if the rumors were untrue and Tian Kai was innocent, the anguish Tan Songyun had endured was undeniably real and couldn’t simply be dismissed.

‘Tian Kai was innocent…? Then what have I been doing all these years? What am I doing with my life now?’

After a very long moment of silence, Tan Songyun suddenly smacked herself in the face and shook it off.

“Even after tens of thousands of years of feeling betrayed, it is undeniable that I still love Tian Kai to a certain extent, and if it turns out that all of the rumors I’ve heard about him are false, I will atone for my crimes.”

“However, there’s no way for me to confirm your words as of this moment, and I am no longer foolish enough to believe everything I hear. Therefore, I am going to follow and watch you to confirm it.”

“You’re going to follow me?” Yuan’s eyes widened at her words.

“What’s with that look? You don’t want me to? Are you afraid I might expose the real you?” Tan Songyun frowned.

“No… that’s not it. Didn’t you hear me? I am currently being hunted by the Celestial Emperor. I also have many enemies. If you stay with me, I cannot guarantee your safety.”

“So what? Even if I die in the process, I will not have any regrets—except that I won’t be able to find out the truth.”

Yuan rubbed his eyes and sighed, “I will also be traversing dangerous places.”

“This won’t be my first time visiting someplace dangerous.”

Seeing how stubborn Tan Songyun was acting, Yuan said, “Fine, you can follow me, but the moment I deem you as a burden, I’ll immediately leave you behind.”

“Like you did back then?” Tan Songyun sneered.

“…”

“Even if you follow me, how will you determine what I said is true or false?” Yuan then asked.

“That’s not something you need to know,” she said.

Yuan removed the formation shortly after and called Xi Meili back.

“Well, since you’re going to be following us, we might as well do an introduction,” said Yuan.

“You may know me as Tian Kai, but my name now is Yuan. Are you aware of the existence of players?”

“Players… you mean the Outsiders?”

Yuan nodded and said, “I am one of them, as I was reborn in that world.”

“You claim to have reincarnated, but why would you still retain Tian Kai’s memories? As far as I know, those who reincarnate lose all memories of their past lives,” Tan Songyun asked, her tone filled with doubt.

“My case is a little special. Also, not everyone forgets their memories. There are special techniques out there that help people regain their lost memories,” Yuan explained.

Xi Meili introduced herself next, “My name is Xi Meili, and I am a real dragon. My bloodline is also royalty.”

Tan Songyun’s eyes widened, and she asked, “How are you related to the Xi Family in the upper heavens?”

“I don’t know since I came from the Ancient Dragon City.”

“Ancient Dragon City…? Never heard of that place.”

“Anyway, I am Tan Songyun, and my relationship with Tian Kai here is—”

Tan Songyun gave Xi Meili a brief explanation of the situation, leaving her bewildered by the sheer strangeness of the events that unfolded.

“Um… me saying this probably won’t mean anything at this point, but there is simply no way these rumors about Yuan are true. If you spend even a little time with him, you’d understand what I mean,” said Xi Meili.

“…”

Tan Songyun narrowed her eyes at Xi Meili for a moment before asking, “What is your relationship with Tian Kai, anyway?”

“Once again, Tian Kai is dead. I am now Yuan.”

“We’re traveling companions,” said Xi Meili.

“For now, yes…”

“For now?” Tan Songyun raised an eyebrow.

“A-anyway, now that we’ve acquired the second Ancient Seal, what are we going to do next?” Xi Meili turned to look at Yuan, clearly trying to change the subject.

Yuan pondered for a moment before summoning Yin and Yang.

“Can you two figure out where the other two seals are?” Yuan asked them.

“We’ll try,” they responded simultaneously before disappearing into thin air.

Tan Songyun then asked, “Why didn’t you ask the Dragon King where the other holders are?”

“By the way, how far have you managed to ascend, Zither Goddess?” Yuan then asked her.

“Anything but Zither Goddess. I no longer qualify for such a title.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1762 Five Breakthrough Towers
chapters.body:
Chapter 1762 Five Breakthrough Towers

Sometime after explaining the situation to Tan Songyun, Yuan made his way to the Stairway to Heaven.

However, upon arriving at the Stairway to Heaven, Yuan noticed that there were only a few individuals present, and most of them weren’t participants, but spectators gathered to see the participants off.

Since Yuan had entered the Fifth Heaven alone, the rest of the player base was still stuck in the Fourth Heaven. As a result, it was essentially impossible for him to carry nine individuals through the Stairway to Heaven by himself.

Of course, Yuan could attempt to fill the required number with people from the Fifth Heaven, but he would need to find individuals willing to ascend to the Sixth Heaven. Unlike players, who could traverse between realms with ease, ascending was a significant and life-altering decision for residents of the Nine Heavens, not something they could decide on a whim.

‘Do I even need to carry people with me? I can just alter the difficulty accordingly!’ Yuan just realized this, but he wasn’t sure it’d work.

“I’ll see you on the other side,” Xi Meili said to Yuan.

“Lady Tan, do you want to come with me? Although you have already passed all trials and reached the Ninth Heaven, you can still spectate me. I’m sure you’ll learn a thing or two about Tian Kai if you do.”

Tan Songyun nodded, “That’s only natural. I was going to follow you even if you didn’t ask.”

Lan Yingying emerged shortly after to follow Xi Meili, as she also wanted to challenge the Stairway to Heaven.

“Who is this?” Tan Songyun asked with raised eyebrows. “And where did she come from?”

“My name is Lan Yingying, and I am a divine beast currently following Yuan.”

“Another divine beast?” Tan Songyun muttered with a surprised look on her face.

Having one divine beast as a follower was already rare enough, much less two.

“Why are they following you, anyways?” she then asked out of curiosity.

“They have their own reasons,” Yuan shrugged.

Yuan climbed the staircase and entered the Stairway to Heaven shortly after, followed by Tan Songyun.

“Welcome back, Master.”

“I don’t have nine people with me this time, but is it possible for me to increase the difficulty to the max?” Yuan asked her.

“If that is what you wish, Master.”

“Great. Let’s do that, then. I would also like to challenge the Stairway to Heaven now.”

Tian’er nodded and disappeared shortly later.

Meanwhile, Tan Songyun was examining the spectator’s room with great interest.

“I didn’t know there was a place like this inside the Stairway to Heaven…”

Since she did everything solo, there was simply no way for her to have known about it.

In the center of the room was a small well filled with water, and reflected on that water was Yuan, who had just begun his first trial. Sёarch* The novel(F~)ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

<You have started your first trial in the Stairway to Heaven>

<Investigate the Five Breakthrough Towers and figure out the one who needs to be punished>

<You have 365 days to complete the trial>

<Figure out the truth and punish the perpetrator within six months to receive a reward>

As Yuan stood in the middle of the bustling street, his eyes widened in surprise as he read the details of the trial’s challenge.

‘I have a whole year to complete this challenge?’ he wondered inwardly.

This officially made it his longest trial yet. Moreover, it appeared more complicated than the previous trials.

However, he wasn’t familiar with the Five Breakthrough Towers.

After standing around for a bit, Yuan decided to gather information first.

He approached someone working behind a food stall and asked, “Excuse me, can you tell me a little about the Five Breakthrough Towers?”

The middle-aged woman looked at him with raised eyebrows and spoke, “You don’t know about the Five Breakthrough Towers? You’re the first person I’ve met in the Seventh Heaven who doesn’t know them.”

“Well, I’m not from around here. I just ascended to this realm.”

“I see. I don’t mind telling you, but…”

The woman extended her hand and gestured for something.

Seeing this, Yuan retrieved some money and handed it to her.

“Thank you, Young Master.”

Satisfied by the amount of money, the woman began explaining, “The Five Breakthrough Towers are the most important organizations in the Seventh Heaven.”

“Organizations?” Yuan inquired about the specific word.

She nodded. “Yes, they are neither a family, sect, nor faction. They are an organization supported by these groups, each contributing its own unique specialty and purpose.”

“The first of the Five Breakthrough Towers is called the Alchemy Tower. They specialize in the study of alchemy and hold the vital role of developing new recipes and refining alchemy techniques,” she continued.

“The second tower is called the Array Tower. They specialize in developing new formations and enhancing existing ones.”

“The third tower is the Martial Tower. Their primary focus is on creating new martial techniques and refining older ones.”

“The fourth tower is the Cultivation Tower. They’re similar to the Martial Tower, but they only deal with cultivation techniques.”

“The final tower is called the Metal Tower. They not only create treasures, but they study smithing techniques and whatnot.”

“If you haven’t noticed yet, each of these towers was established with the purpose of advancing a specific path. However, they are entirely separate organizations with no direct association with one another. And because they are all striving for progress in their respective fields, there’s a great deal of competition among them.”

Yuan was quite amazed by this concept, and he asked, “What are their reputations like?”

“As far as I am aware, they are all very respectable and trustworthy. I don’t think I have ever heard anything bad about them,” she said.

“Not even rumors? There are always rumors.”

The woman pondered for a moment and spoke in a genuine tone, “They say the workers are overly passionate about their job, no matter which tower they belong to, but I wouldn’t consider that a bad thing.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1763 Metal Tower
chapters.body:
Chapter 1763 Metal Tower

Yuan pointed to a tall tower-like building in the distance and asked, “Is that one of these Breakthrough Towers?”

The woman nodded, “Yes, that would be the Metal Tower.”

“Where can I find the other towers?”

“They are scattered throughout the Seventh Heaven. Unfortunately, I don’t know the exact location of the other towers.”

“Is that so? Thank you for the information.”

Yuan ended their conversation and made his way toward the Metal Tower. As he approached, he noticed that the entire structure was constructed from shiny black metal, its surface gleaming under the light.

Meanwhile, Tan Songyun wore a pondering expression on her face.

“The Five Breakthrough Towers? Why are they in the Stairway to Heaven? More importantly… what the hell is going on? Did the Stairway to Heaven create such an elaborate world for his trial? I have never heard of anything like this before!”

Suddenly, she sensed a presence behind her. Turning around, she was startled to see a cute child standing there, having appeared silently, almost like a ghost.

“Who are you? And where did you come from?” Tan Songyun asked, as she was certain that nobody else had followed them into the Stairway to Heaven.

“My name is Tian’er, and I have been here since the very beginning, as I am the Stairway to Heaven’s spirit,” she responded calmly.

“What? The Stairway to Heaven has a spirit?” Tan Songyun’s eyes widened at this shocking information.

Tian’er nodded in confirmation and continued, “Master ordered me to help clear up any uncertainty you may have, so here I am.”

“Master…?”

“The individual partaking in the trial right now is my Master. He is also the creator of the Stairway to Heaven.”

“W-wait a second… Tian Kai created the Stairway to Heaven? That’s impossible. The Stairway to Heaven existed long before he even came into existence!” Tan Songyun refuted her claim, using this obvious fact as proof.

“I am not talking about Tian Kai, but an even older reincarnation. Tian Kai is merely one of Master’s multiple lives, after all.”

“What in heaven’s name…?” Tan Songyun was speechless.

However, after some pondering, Tan Songyun realized it wasn’t strange for an individual to have reincarnated multiple times. After all, humans naturally reincarnate each time they die, provided the soul remains intact.

‘Wait a minute… Can I even trust what she’s saying? Tian Kai—Yuan is her Master, so he could easily make her lie…’ Tan Songyun thought to herself.

“I heard that it was the God of Creation who created the Stairway to Heaven. Are you telling me that your Master was the God of Creation in one of his previous lives?” Tan Songyun asked.

Tian’er nodded.

“It’s not just the Stairway to Heaven. Master has created many things throughout his life, with many of them still undiscovered to this very day.”

Tan Songyun swallowed nervously at such implications.

“Then, can you tell me what’s going on with his trial? How come it’s so different? I know everyone has their own trials, but the disparity isn’t this massive.” Tan Songyun then inquired.

“For Master, the Stairway to Heaven is not merely a way to travel through the Nine Heavens,” Tian’er explained. “It also holds memories of Master’s past lives, and the trials help him recover those memories.”

“Huh? So what I am looking at right now is actually memories?” Tan Songyun looked at the vast world Yuan was in. seaʀᴄh thё NôᴠeFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“You’re half right,” she clarified. “The trials are created from his memories, but they are only fragments pieced together, like parts of a puzzle. They don’t directly mirror his actual memories.”

After taking a long moment to digest the information given, Tan Songyun asked, “Whose memories am I looking at right now?”

However, Tian’er shook her head and said, “Unfortunately, I cannot answer that.”

“Why not? Are you afraid that I might know too much?”

Tian’er shook her head once again and clarified, “No. There are just some things I am not allowed to say. If you really wish to know, you can ask Master later.”

Meanwhile, Yuan continued asking several pedestrians about the Metal Tower as he approached his destination. He was particularly curious to know if there were any negative rumors about the place. However, everyone he spoke to praised the Metal Tower, describing its reputation as sparkling clean. While that might seem reassuring on the surface, Yuan couldn’t help but become suspicious.

After all, it could all be a facade—a carefully crafted mask to conceal the true evil lurking within. Yet, Yuan had no way to know for sure without visiting the Metal Tower himself.

The Metal Tower appeared close at first glance, but it was actually several days away. Now, standing before it several days later, Yuan realized just how massive it truly was. Not only was the tower so tall that it pierced the clouds, but its interior was vast enough to house a large community of tens of thousands of people simultaneously.

The massive entrance, notably without doors, bustled with a steady stream of people entering and leaving.

After stepping inside the tower, Yuan quickly noticed that the entire first floor resembled a bustling shopping mall, filled with hundreds of stores and even personal stalls offering a wide variety of treasures.

After looking around for some time, Yuan approached one of the merchants and asked, “Excuse me, I am trying to join the Metal Tower. Where can I go to sign up?”

The best way to gain information about a place was to join them, after all.

However, the merchant merely sneered in disguise at Yuan and said, “Who do you think you are? The Metal Tower doesn’t just accept anybody.”

“I am a blacksmith, and I am very confident in my skills.”

“Hahaha! You’re truly an ignorant one, aren’t you? To make me laugh, I’ll tell you something. Talent alone won’t get you into the Metal Tower. Unless you know someone on the inside, you’ll need to increase your reputation—enough to get the Metal Tower to notice you.” The man gestured at the weapons he’d personally crafted before him.

“The fastest way to gain reputation is to showcase your weapons here, just like many of us are doing.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1764 Metal Tower(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1764 Metal Tower(2)

To capture the Metal Tower’s attention, most blacksmiths showcase their creations on the tower’s first floor, which also serves as an open marketplace. However, despite the abundance of talented blacksmiths, only about ten individuals gain the Metal Tower’s recognition every few hundred years.

“I’ve been selling my creations here for about a hundred years now,” the man said. “In all that time, I’ve only seen three people be recognized by the Metal Tower. But let me tell you, being recognized doesn’t mean you’ll be accepted—that’s an entirely different matter.”

“I see. Thank you for the information,” Yuan said to the man.

He left shortly after and began roaming the first floor, examining all of the stores available.

Now that Yuan understood how to attract the Metal Tower’s attention, he knew he needed to craft treasures that would quickly build his reputation. The simplest and most effective way to achieve this was to create something far superior to anything else being showcased around him.

After exploring every store on the first floor, Yuan observed that most of the treasures on display were only Ancient-grade, with a few Mythic-grade items scattered among them. However, even the Mythic-grade treasures were of noticeably low quality.

‘A few peak-quality Mythic-grade and Empyrean-grade treasures should be more than enough to attract the Metal Tower’s attention.’ Yuan thought to himself as he left the Metal Tower.

Thanks to the Metal Tower’s influence, the area surrounding it was teeming with smithies and smithing-related stores, including shops selling various materials and tools essential for crafting.

Yuan entered a nearby smithy and requested to use their workshop.

“Hello, I would like to borrow the workshop to create some treasures.”

The bulky man standing behind the counter looked at Yuan like he was crazy.

“You got any identification? A blacksmith identification, to be more specific.”

“Sorry, but I don’t have anything like that.”

“Then we cannot lend you our workshop, no matter how much you offer. If you wish to use our workshop, you need to be an official blacksmith.”

“How do I become an official blacksmith?” Yuan asked.

“Hahaha! You want to become an official blacksmith, but you don’t even know how? You’re an amusing one!” The bulky man laughed out loud.

He continued, “Ten blocks down, there is a place called the Blacksmith Association. In order to be recognized as an official blacksmith, you must pass several tests.”

“Thank you. Then I will be back after I acquire my identification,” Yuan said to the man.

“Hahaha! If a scrawny kid like you can pass the assessment, I’ll gladly let you use my workshop for free!”

“Scrawny?” Yuan raised an eyebrow. This was the first time he had ever been referred to with such a term.

Yuan’s physique might appear scrawny compared to the bulky man standing before him, but in reality, it was perfectly balanced and refined to an unparalleled degree of perfection, being neither too skinny nor too bulky.

Yuan left the store shortly after, but he didn’t immediately head to the Blacksmith Association. Instead, he decided to try his luck at a few more smithies. Unfortunately, all of them required him to be an official blacksmith.

‘I guess this is a process I must go through to progress the trial.’ Yuan thought to himself as he visited the Blacksmith Association.

The Blacksmith Association was an enormous building spanning several streets, its reflective surface and metallic structure giving it the appearance of a solid block of steel at first glance.

Yuan walked inside the building and approached the nearest worker.

“I’d like to take the assessment to become an official blacksmith.”

The worker, a young man, nodded, “Sure thing. May I ask how experienced you are with the art?”

“I’d say that I’m quite experienced.”

“Then what is the highest grade of treasure you’ve created?” S~eaʀᴄh the novёlF~ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Celestial-grade.” Yuan calmly responded.

The young man’s eyes immediately widened with disbelief. He didn’t respond for several moments due to being speechless.

Eventually, he said, “That’s a good joke, but I need a serious answer.”

Yuan’s expression remained calm as he responded, “I am dead serious.”

Seeing Yuan’s solemn gaze, the young man swallowed nervously and spoke in a stiff voice, “T-then will you be fine with accepting the most difficult assessment? If you pass, we’ll grant you the highest Blacksmith Rank we can possibly give you. Even if you fail, you can try again with a lower difficulty.”

Yuan nodded, “Sure, give me your most difficult assessment.”

The young man nodded and said, “Please give me a moment to notify my superiors.”

Sometime later, an old man with long, short white hair and a curly mustache approached Yuan and asked, “Are you the one who requested our most difficult assessment?”

Yuan nodded.

“Follow me.”

Yuan followed the old man to the basement, where a large workshop was available.

“Since you claim to be able to craft Celestial-grade treasures, we’ll have you craft an Empyrean-grade treasure for your assessment. If you are able to do so, the Blacksmith Association will immediately promote you to our highest blacksmith rank,” said the old man.

Yuan pondered for a moment before asking, “Does the Blacksmith Association have any connections with the Metal Tower?”

The old man quickly understood what Yuan was trying to ask and said, “While we do have some connections and can recommend you to the Metal Tower, it is up to them whether they are willing to accept you. Moreover, Divine Blacksmiths are not rare in the Metal Tower, so even if you are capable of crafting Celestial-grade treasures, it won’t guarantee you a spot in the Metal Tower.”

“I see. Thanks for the explanation.”

The old man retrieved a pile of materials a moment later and said, “You have three months to craft the treasure using the materials we provide you. Are you ready to begin?”

“I am ready.”

Yuan spent a couple of minutes examining the materials. Once he was prepared, he immediately began to work.

Less than a day later, Yuan presented his finished creation to the old man, who was utterly gobsmacked, not only by the masterpiece itself but also by the sheer brilliance of the process that had brought it to life.

chapters.title: Chapter 1765 Metal Tower(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1765 Metal Tower(3)

“What do you think? Did I pass the assessment?” Yuan asked the dumbfounded old man, who was staring at the finished treasure with wide eyes filled with disbelief.

Although smithing had advanced significantly since Tian Qiyuan’s era, with the emergence of many more Divine Blacksmiths, Tian Qiyuan’s smithing skills remained unparalleled.

“C-can I examine the treasure?” the old man asked.

Yuan nodded and handed the sword to him.

The old man held the sword in his trembling hands, feeling as though he were cradling a priceless treasure of unimaginable value.

“I have never seen an Empyrean-grade treasure of this quality before!” he muttered.

“Pass! You pass! Congratulations, you are now officially an Obisidian-rank blacksmith!” The old man handed Yuan a black badge, its surface gleaming with understated elegance. This badge held immense significance in the world of blacksmiths, representing the pinnacle of achievement. Within the Blacksmith Association, there were six ranks that could be obtained, with Obsidian—the rank represented by this badge—being the highest and most revered.

Normally, becoming an Obsidian-ranked blacksmith required passing multiple rigorous assessments. However, Yuan’s flawless performance in his first trial was so extraordinary that the remaining tests were deemed redundant and unnecessary by the old man, who was also a Divine Blacksmith.

“I haven’t introduced myself yet. I am the head of the Blacksmith Association, Lie Yan. If you ever need any assistance from the Blacksmith Association, we will always be there to help you,” said the old man.

Yuan nodded and asked, “Do you think I can borrow some materials? I would like to craft a few more treasures.”

“Of course! All official blacksmiths under the Blacksmith Association are entitled to a specific allotment of materials every ten years as a privilege,” Lie Yan explained. “As an Obsidian-ranked blacksmith, you have near unlimited access to most of our materials, provided the amount you request is reasonable.”

“I don’t need much. Just enough to craft several Mythic-grade treasures. Also, do I get to keep that sword?” Yuan pointed at the treasure he’d crafted for the assessment.

Lie Yan, seemingly reluctant to let the treasure go, said, “O-of course.”

Sometime later, Lie Yan left to acquire the materials Yuan had requested.

Once Lie Yan returned with the materials, he hesitated for a moment before asking, “May I boldly request to watch you work? Even as a Divine Blacksmith, I feel my skills improve simply by observing your craftsmanship.”

“Do as you wish,” Yuan said.

Yuan proceeded to use almost all of his materials to craft five peak-quality Mythic-grade treasures.

“Thank you for the experience.” Lie Yan bowed to Yuan.

“Then I’ll take my leave now.”

“One more thing before you leave. Although I cannot help you join the Metal Tower, I know some people there. I’ll mention your name the next time I visit them. Hopefully, that will help you a little,” said Lie Yan.

“Thanks.”

Yuan left the Blacksmith Association shortly afterward. However, instead of heading directly back to the Metal Tower, he made his way to the shop with the bulky man.

Once there, Yuan casually revealed his Obsidian badge. The man’s jaw practically hit the floor, his eyes wide with disbelief as he stared at the badge in stunned silence.

“Do I qualify to use your workshop?” he asked.

After snapping out of his daze, the man said, “O-of course! You can even have my whole shop if you want!”

Yuan merely shook his head with a slight smile on his face.

The man led Yuan to his workshop at the back of the shop a moment later.

Once inside, Yuan set to work, using the materials he had purposely saved for this occasion. Within a few hours, he had crafted a flawless Mythic-grade treasure, leaving the bulky man utterly speechless as he stared in awe at the masterpiece before him.

“Thanks for lending me the place.”

“You can come back here to borrow my workshop whenever you want, Senior! It will always be available for you!”

With his six finished treasures, Yuan returned to the Metal Tower to set up his showcase.

“Oh? You’re back already?”

The man who called Yuan ignorant was surprised to see Yuan return so soon. It’d only been a day since their conversation, after all.

“Yes. I took your advice and went to craft some treasures. Do you mind if I set up my showcase next to yours?” Yuan said.

“Hahaha! You’ve only been gone for a little over a day!” the man laughed, shaking his head. “There’s no way whatever you created in such a short time would attract the Metal Tower’s attention! Each of my treasures required months, if not years, of hard work!”

“Maybe that’s why you haven’t gotten the tower’s attention.” Yuan shrugged.

The man frowned and cursed, “You cheeky bastard!”

Yuan said nothing more and began setting up his spot. He spread a simple blanket on the floor and casually placed the six treasures he had crafted onto it, treating them as if they were mere toys rather than extraordinary creations.

The man’s eyes widened with shock after seeing the five Mythic-grade and one Empyrean-grade treasure.

“Y-you crafted that in one day?! That’s impossible! You clearly purchased these treasures!” the man screamed out loud, attracting the attention of those nearby. S~eaʀᴄh the ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“That’s quite the accusation,” Yuan said.

“Anyone with a working brain knows that isn’t possible! Even a Divine Blacksmith wouldn’t be able to craft six treasures of such quality in a day!”

“Guess I am just different.” Yuan casually shrugged.

The man gritted his teeth in frustration.

“This is blasphemy! You’re a disgrace! I bet you’re not even a blacksmith!”

“Now you’re just slandering me. Do you have proof that I didn’t craft these treasures? How do you know that I didn’t craft them prior to our last conversation?”

“T-that’s… Right! If you had these treasures from the start, you would’ve shown them during that day! Why did you leave for a day?!”

“I was hungry, so I went to eat some food,” Yuan said, leaving the man speechless.

chapters.title: Chapter 1766 Metal Tower(4)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1766 Metal Tower(4)

Noticing the commotion between Yuan and the man, the nearby blacksmiths eventually approached them and asked, “What’s going on here?”

“This bastard here is mocking us, blacksmiths! Listen to this—” the man proceeded to explain the whole situation to the others. Naturally, he spoke from his point of view, labeling Yuan as the bad guy.

“He most likely purchased those treasures from somewhere.”

The blacksmiths frowned deeply as they learned of Yuan’s actions. Most of them were Platinum-ranked blacksmiths, seasoned and proud of their craft. Yuan’s actions were enough to provoke any blacksmith, let alone ones of their caliber—if they were true.

Seeing this, Yuan retrieved his Obsidian-rank badge and shoved it before them.

“T-that’s an Obsidian badge!” the man stammered, his voice shaking with disbelief. “Only Divine Blacksmiths can qualify for that rank! Y-you’re a Divine Blacksmith?! Why didn’t you say so?!” His face, which had been full of scorn just moments ago, now bore a mix of shock and awe as he gazed at the badge that symbolized the rank he had long dreamed of achieving.

“Why didn’t I say so? Why must I disclose such information to you when I’m trying to attract the Metal Tower’s attention? Who do you think you are?” Yuan sneered.

The man’s jaw dropped, speechless.

“Now that I have cleared up the misunderstanding, why don’t we all return to our objective—acquiring the Metal Tower’s attention?” Yuan sat behind his display and closed his eyes, seemingly entering cultivation.

The man beside Yuan quietly sat down and lowered his head, doing his best to avoid drawing any attention from Yuan, as if trying to make himself invisible.

The crowd found it hard to believe that someone could craft six flawless treasures in a single day. However, the blacksmiths had already heard about Yuan’s status as an Obsidian-ranked blacksmith, so they didn’t dare to voice any doubts.

The crowd found it hard to believe that someone could craft six flawless treasures in a single day. However, the blacksmiths had already heard about Yuan’s status as an Obsidian-ranked blacksmith, so they didn’t dare to voice any doubts.

“Umm… Are any of these treasures for sale?” someone suddenly asked.

While most blacksmiths came to the tower not only to showcase their treasures but also to sell them, Yuan had no such intention. He had no need to sell his creations; his sole purpose was to use them as a means to attract the Metal Tower’s attention.

After several days of sitting idly with no noticeable change, a sudden commotion erupted on the first floor, breaking the monotony and drawing everyone’s attention.

“Someone from the Metal Tower is here, looking around! This is our chance!”

Upon hearing that someone from the tower had arrived, the blacksmiths immediately began tidying up their showcases, frantically trying to make their displays more presentable and impressive.

Meanwhile, Yuan remained seated, his eyes closed and his demeanor calm, as though the news held no significance to him.

At the same time, a handsome young man strolled through the first floor, dressed in an elegant black outfit adorned with a golden badge on his chest that read ‘Metal’.

Everywhere he walked, people around him offered respectful stares, their admiration evident. The blacksmiths, in particular, had eyes that gleamed with hope and desperation, eager to catch even a moment of his attention.

Yet, not a single blacksmith dared to approach him, almost as if he was out of their reach.

The handsome man didn’t stop walking until he was standing right before Yuan’s display.

“Are you the one who recently became an Obsidian-rank blacksmith?” the young man asked.

“And you must be Lie Yan’s friend.”

The handsome man’s eyebrows twitched slightly at Yuan’s casual response, a hint of offense flashing across his face. He glanced at the weapons on display, his gaze lingering only briefly—just a fraction longer than a passing look—before sneering, “What a waste of time. I can’t believe I came all the way down here for something so mediocre.”

The handsome man’s words shocked everyone there. Even Yuan raised an eyebrow at the man’s abrupt aggression.

“Is that what you truly think?” Yuan asked the handsome man. “Did you even look at my treasures?”

The handsome man sneered, “Who are you to question my judgment? You’re just a nobody who recently achieved the rank of Obsidian. Even the worst blacksmith in the tower can craft something a hundred times better than this crap.”

Hearing this, Yuan sighed and said calmly, “It seems I’ve wasted my time here. If the Metal Tower is run by childish individuals who lack the discernment to recognize true craftsmanship, I’d rather invest my time at another tower.”

As he spoke, Yuan packed up his treasures and prepared to leave.

The handsome man frowned, his eyes flickering with fury. “Hold it right there! Where do you think you’re going? Do you really think you can mock me and just walk away like nothing happened?” he snapped, his voice sharp with indignation.

Yuan stopped and looked at the handsome man.

“Of course, unless you plan on stopping me.”

Yuan retrieved Number One Under Heaven.

“T-that’s—!?”

The blacksmiths gasped in shock upon seeing Number One Under Heaven, Yuan’s masterpiece. Even the handsome man, despite his earlier attitude, couldn’t help but fix his gaze on it. Unbeknownst to him, his expression shifted, his face now filled with awe and admiration.

“I guess you’re not going to stop me, after all.”

A moment later, Yuan casually stowed away Number One Under Heaven, leaving the blacksmiths around him in visible despair. It was as if a cherished toy had been snatched away from a child, their excitement instantly replaced with disappointment and longing.
