chapters.title: Chapter 1767 A Chance Of Redemption
chapters.body:
Chapter 1767 A Chance Of Redemption

After putting away Number One Under Heaven, Yuan continued walking toward the exit.

Seeing this, the handsome man snapped out of his daze and shouted, “Halt!”

However, Yuan kept on walking, and he spoke as he walked, “Who do you think you are to stop me from leaving? You already said my treasures are not worth the Metal Tower’s attention, so why would I continue wasting time here? Let’s not waste each other’s time.”

The handsome man trembled with anger, but he couldn’t bring himself to utter another word. Saying anything further would be tantamount to admitting his own fault and misjudgment.

Therefore, the handsome man and the others could only watch as Yuan nonchalantly left the Metal Tower.

“He actually left…” the man who had been stationed next to Yuan muttered in a dazed tone, his face frozen in a dumbfounded expression.

A few moments after Yuan departed from the Metal Tower, before the handsome man and the others could fully process what had just occurred, another figure appeared. It was an old man with a bald head, dressed in an outfit similar to the one worn by the handsome man.

“That presence just now! Where is it?! Where did it go?!” the old man bellowed, his voice commanding and urgent, instantly grabbing everyone’s attention.

“S-Senior Liao! What brings you down here?” the handsome man exclaimed, a look of genuine surprise spreading across his face as he caught sight of the old man.

However, the old man, Senior Liao, ignored his question and asked again, “The presence that appeared just now! Where did it go?! Where is the treasure that emitted such presence?!”

“T-that’s…” the handsome man stammered, swallowing nervously as he hesitated, unsure whether to reveal the truth.

“What are you hiding from me, Apprentice Huan?” Senior Liao frowned.

The handsome man sighed and said, “I wouldn’t dare, Senior Liao. This is what happened…”

He recounted the events to Senior Liao, detailing everything that had occurred. However, he carefully omitted certain details—most notably, the reason Yuan had decided to leave.

Unfortunately for him, Senior Liao was not something easily fooled. He narrowed his eyes at Apprentice Huan and said in a cold voice, “Apprentice Huan, I accepted you because of your potential, but I am beginning to doubt my own judgment. I will give you a chance to redeem yourself. Bring that person you chased away back to the Metal Tower. If you cannot do even that, you no longer need to return to the Metal Tower.”

“S-Senior Liao?!” Apprentice Huan’s face contorted in despair, his expression twisting into something almost unrecognizable.

Having only joined the Metal Tower a decade ago, if he were kicked out over something as seemingly trivial as this, he’d become a laughingstock among his peers and ruin his reputation entirely.

“I will not repeat myself. You have one month to bring him back.” Senior Liao turned around and walked away, leaving Apprentice Huan and the others speechless.

Snapping out of his daze a moment later, Apprentice Huan turned toward the exit of the tower. Without hesitation, he rushed outside, determined to find Yuan and convince him to return to the Metal Tower.

Although it hadn’t been long since Yuan left the Metal Tower, his figure was nowhere to be seen.

“Fuck! Where did he go?! Did he really leave to another tower?!” Apprentice Huan stomped his feet in frustration. He didn’t want to travel to the other towers, but there was nothing he could do, as his future and reputation depended on it.

Meanwhile, after leaving the Metal Tower, Yuan immediately went to the nearest teleport formation.

“I’d like to visit the other Breakthrough Towers,” he said to the individual managing the teleportation formation.

“Which one?” “I will let you pick.” Sёarch* The NôvelFire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Okay…?”

Sometime later, Yuan stepped into the teleportation formation.

When he emerged on the other side, he found himself in a different city. Turning his gaze toward the skyline, he saw a massive tower in the distance. While it was just as majestic as the Metal Tower, its design and structure were distinctly different. This tower resembled a towering pagoda, exuding an air of elegance and tradition.

Yuan looked at the man managing the teleport formation and asked, “Which Breakthrough Tower is that?”

“Huh?” Although the man was puzzled by such a weird question, he answered regardless, “That would be the Martial Tower.”

“Is that so?”

Yuan approached the tower.

Unlike the bustling and open atmosphere of the Metal Tower, the Martial Tower exuded a sense of exclusivity and focus. Its entrance was closed, yet thousands of people had gathered outside, their attention fixed on five large jade tablets prominently displayed near the tower. The crowd buzzed with intensity, their gazes locked on the tablets as though they held immense significance.

Yuan picked a jade tablet at random and looked at the contents written on it.

“It’s a martial technique…” It didn’t take long for Yuan to realize what he was looking at.

Each jade tablet showcased a martial technique of a different rank engraved onto its surface. The first tablet displayed a Mortal-rank technique, the second an Earth-rank technique, and so on, progressing through the ranks until the highest, an Ancient-rank technique, was reached.

After looking through all five martial techniques, Yuan looked around until he found someone who wasn’t completely focused on the techniques and approached them.

“Excuse me, but what is the purpose of these jade tablets?”

The woman he approached looked at him with raised eyebrows and said, “If you can improve any of the martial techniques on the jade tablet and achieve a satisfactory result, the Martial Tower will accept you into their organization.”

Yuan understood the situation immediately, but he continued asking, “Why are they all of different ranks? There’s even a Mortal-rank technique, which is obviously going to be the easiest to improve. Wouldn’t it be fairer if all of the techniques were of the same rank?”

“That’s simple,” she explained. “The more difficult the challenge you take on, the higher your position within the organization will be if you succeed.”

“That makes sense. Thank you for your time and explanation.”

Yuan returned to focusing on the martial techniques shortly after.

chapters.title: Chapter 1768 Martial Tower
chapters.body:
Chapter 1768 Martial Tower

To improve a martial technique, one must first learn and master it. For Yuan, who had lived multiple lives as powerful immortals, each reigning supreme in their respective eras, this process came naturally. His vast experience with countless techniques made him exceptionally adept in this domain.

After spending a few hours carefully studying the jade tablet with the Ancient-rank technique, Yuan fully grasped it. He then spent the rest of the day with his eyes closed, mentally practicing the martial technique as if he were crafting a treasure within his mind, refining every detail and perfecting it in his imagination.

If it weren’t for a disturbance, he would’ve spent some more time inside his mind.

“You! I’ve finally found you!” Apprentice Huan shouted when he noticed Yuan amongst the crowd.

Due to Yuan’s handsome features, he stuck out like a sore thumb no matter how big the crowd around him was.

After leaving the Metal Tower, Apprentice Huan spent several hours scouring the city in search of Yuan. Eventually, he decided to check the teleportation formation, hoping for a lead. Sure enough, the attendant working the formation remembered Yuan and confirmed that he had used it recently.

Yuan frowned after seeing Apprentice Huan and said, “Don’t tell me you followed me all the way here?”

“I had no choice! You must return to the Metal Tower with me as soon as possible! Senior Liao wants to see you!” Apprentice Huan said, not even bothering to apologize.

“Senior who?” Yuan raised an eyebrow at this unfamiliar name.

“You don’t even know the Master of the Metal Tower, Liao Tian?! He once held the title of God of Creation before retiring!” Apprentice Huan exclaimed, his face filled with disbelief.

Yuan shrugged nonchalantly, “Thanks for the introduction. I definitely didn’t know him before it.”

“Y-you disrespectful son—” Apprentice Huan forcefully stopped himself from finishing the sentence.

“Now you know who’s summoned you, follow me back to the Metal Tower.”

However, Yuan sneered, “So what if he was once the God of Creation? Even if he were the current God of Creation, I don’t care! If he wants me to return to the Metal Tower, he better invite me in person!”

“You’re going too far, bastard!” Apprentice Huan roared.

“I’m going too far? You were the one who drove me away, and instead of apologizing, you’re still looking down on me. Unless this Liao Tian visits me personally, I won’t return to the Metal Tower until I visit the other four Breakthrough Towers!” Yuan declared, leaving Apprentice Huan speechless.

After a moment of silence, he trembled in anger and screamed, “Either you follow me back willingly, or I will force you to go back!”

“Oh? I’d love to see you try.”

Yuan beckoned Apprentice Huan. This was the perfect time for him to improve the Ancient-rank technique through practical use. Sёarᴄh the NôvelFire(.)net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Apprentice Huan sneered in disdain and released his cultivation base, which was at the first level of Divine Ancestor.

‘Senior Liao only mentioned bringing him back to the Metal Tower! He didn’t say in what condition! I’ll drag him back even if it’s only his corpse!’ Apprentice Huan laughed inwardly, showing a menacing expression on his face.

Seeing that two experts were about to clash, the people there immediately scrambled to safety.

Yuan retrieved his Baneful Abyss, as the Ancient-rank technique was a dagger technique.

‘Since this is the Stairway to Heaven’s trial, I don’t have to worry about killing innocent people with its powerful poison.’

Apprentice Huan trembled in fear after sensing the Baneful Abyss’ ominous aura.

“Did you create that monstrosity?” Apprentice Huan asked with a deep frown. “Where are your morals?”

“Morals? I’m surprised you can even utter such a word!” Yuan laughed out loud.

He then shouted out loud, “Martial Tower, are you watching this right now?! I will now display my improvements for the Ancient-rank martial technique!”

Yuan pointed the Banefull Abyss at Apprentice Huan and said, “Don’t worry, I won’t kill you. After all, how else will you tell Liao Tian to come find me?”

“Arrogant son of a bitch!”

Just as Apprentice Huan leaped forward, Yuan’s aura exploded after activating True Dragon Awakening.

The abrupt appearance of such powerful pressure startled Apprentice Huan, causing him to hesitate for a split second.

Yuan used this opportunity to attack.

A black streak of light appeared, piercing Apprentice Huan’s left arm faster than one could blink their eyes.

“Wha—”

Before Apprentice Huan could even process what was happening, an intense pain suddenly engulfed his entire body. It felt as though his insides were melting, the agony so overwhelming that it left him gasping for breath and unable to think clearly.

If Apprentice Huan’s cultivation weren’t at the Divine Ancestor realm, he would’ve perished the second Baneful Abyss touched him.

As Apprentice Huan rolled on the ground, screaming in pain, Yuan approached him.

Right before Apprentice Huan succumbed to the poison, Yuan removed the poison.

“Go back to Liao Tian and tell him to see me personally if he wants me to return to the Metal Tower,” he said a moment later.

“…”

A deafening silence enveloped the area as the onlookers stood frozen in disbelief. They couldn’t fathom how easily Yuan had defeated someone several cultivation realms stronger than himself, executing the feat with such effortless grace.

“Hahaha!”

An amused laugh suddenly shattered the silence.

When Yuan and the others turned toward the source of the laughter, they saw a wise-looking old man standing before them, his presence so sudden and silent it was as if he had materialized like a ghost.

“Y-you are—!” Apprentice Huan instantly recognized this old man, as did the other onlookers.

“Lord Zi!” Everyone except Yuan immediately lowered their heads in respect toward the old man, whose aura and demeanor made it clear that he was a figure of immense significance.

Without even looking at Apprentice Huan, the old man faced Yuan and spoke with a smile on his face, “That was magnificent—your improvement to that martial technique. How long did it take you to come up with something like that?”

“A little under a day. If I hadn’t been interrupted by someone, I could’ve improved it even further,” he replied.

The old man’s eyes widened.

chapters.title: Chapter 1769 Martial Tower(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1769 Martial Tower(2)

“I see… so it only took you a day to improve an Ancient-rank technique. Your wisdom and talent is very impressive. How about joining our Martial Tower? Let us usher martial techniques into a new era together!” said Lord Zi.

Yuan then said, “I can join the Martial Tower, but will I be allowed to visit the other Breakthrough Towers afterward?”

Lord Zi nodded, “Of course. While we are very competitive with each other, we all have similar goals and ambitions. We won’t bar you from joining other towers just because you are associated with another tower.”

“In that case, I’ll join the Martial Tower.”

“Hahaha! Great choice, my young friend! Follow me. I’ll give you a tour around the place.”

Yuan nodded and followed Lord Zi into the tower. Meanwhile, Apprentice Huan remained standing outside the Martial Tower with a perplexed expression on his face.

On one hand, he couldn’t return to the Metal Tower without Yuan. On the other, he couldn’t approach Yuan with Lord Zi beside him.

Lord Zi was not just a member of the Martial Tower. His status was akin to Liao Tian in the Metal Tower, with a significant amount of achievements under his belt.

Upon stepping onto the first floor of the Martial Tower, Yuan was greeted by a scene reminiscent of a bustling training ground. Numerous individuals were scattered throughout, diligently practicing martial techniques and honing their skills.

“The first ten floors of the tower are essentially the same,” Lord Zi explained. “The Martial Tower isn’t just about improving martial techniques—we also create and record them. Of course, we only have access to techniques that are entrusted to us. When we started, our resources were scarce, with barely any techniques to work with. But over time, as we proved ourselves by refining and enhancing techniques, more and more people began trusting us with their precious techniques.”

At the end of the room was a teleportation formation that provided access to the upper floors. Since the first ten floors were essentially identical, they proceeded directly to the eleventh floor. Sёarch* The NovelFire.net* website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Unlike the bustling training ground on the first floor, the eleventh floor resembled a vast library. Shelves lined the walls, filled with scrolls and books, and many individuals were seated, deeply engrossed in studying martial techniques.

“The next ten floors are like this,” Lord Zi said as they walked around the place. “We house martial techniques ranging from Mortal-rank to Celestial-rank. However, only a select few individuals are permitted to study techniques above Supreme-rank. All of the techniques on this floor are merely Mortal-rank.

“Everyone starts from Mortal-rank techniques. In order to study the next rank, they must increase their contribution, which can be gained through improving or creating martial techniques. For example, improving a Mortal-rank technique will gain one contribution point, while creating a Mortal-rank technique will grant ten points.”

“The next ten floors are like this,” Lord Zi said as they walked around the place. “We house martial techniques ranging from Mortal-rank to Celestial-rank. However, only a select few individuals are permitted to study techniques above Supreme-rank. All of the techniques on this floor are merely Mortal-rank.”

“Everyone starts from Mortal-rank techniques. In order to study the next rank, they must increase their contribution, which can be gained through improving or creating martial techniques. For example, improving a Mortal-rank technique will gain one contribution point, while creating a Mortal-rank technique will grant ten points.”

“Since you’ve already improved an Ancient-rank technique, you’ll automatically be allowed to study martial techniques up to Ancient-rank techniques.”

Next, they proceeded directly to the twenty-first floor. Unlike the previous floors, this one featured only a few rooms, each with a closed door.

“These are seclusion chambers,” Lord Zi explained. “They’re reserved for individuals working on creating new martial techniques.”

“Let me show you how they work.”

They entered an unoccupied room, and to Yuan’s surprise, the interior was completely bare, devoid of any furniture or decorations. The emptiness was stark and unexpected. However, upon closer inspection, Yuan noticed the room was actually filled with powerful formations.

“I know what you’re thinking. How can someone create martial techniques in such a boring environment?”

Creating martial techniques was a challenging and intricate process. There were several approaches to it, with the most common being drawing inspiration from one’s surroundings or observing others in combat. Some even derived martial techniques from nature—studying the movements of an insect walking, a bird flying, or the flow of water in a stream could spark the creation of a new technique.

“There are multiple formations inside this room, which can be used to do this—”

Lord Zi closed his eyes, and moments later, the empty room began to shift and change. The surroundings transformed, and they found themselves standing at the edge of a towering cliff overlooking a vast, endless ocean. The sound of crashing waves and the scent of salty air filled the space, making the illusion feel incredibly real.

The scenery shifted once more a few moments later, and they now found themselves witnessing a breathtaking battle between two powerful cultivators in the starry sky. The clash of their techniques lit up the cosmos, each strike rippling through the heavens with unimaginable force.

“The only limitation is your imagination,” said Lord Zi.

Once they left the room, Lord Zi said, “This is the furthest I can take you inside the Martial Tower. Here is your Martial Tower badge, which can be used as a storage ring and a communication jade slip. You can also see which floors you have access to inside.”

“In order to gain more access, I need to contribute to the Martial Tower, correct?” Yuan asked.

Lord Zi nodded.

“What gives the most contribution points?”

Lord Zi chuckled and said, “I like your enthusiasm, but it takes time to reach the top. However, if you can create a Mythic-rank technique, I’ll consider giving you a quick promotion to the top.”

“A Mythic-rank technique, huh?” Yuan mumbled.

Even with his vast experience throughout multiple lifetimes, it wasn’t easy to create a Mythic-rank technique on the spot. The martial techniques he had made before all required hundreds, if not thousands, of years. Of course, they were all top martial techniques amongst their ranks.

“Alright, then I will immediately start,” Yuan said a moment later.

“Sure thing. Now that you are a member of the Martial Tower, you’re free to do as you please. If you ever need help with anything, you can speak with the others.” Lord Zi said to him before leaving him alone.

Yuan returned to the room shortly after.

chapters.title: Chapter 1770 Creating A New Martial Technique
chapters.body:
Chapter 1770 Creating A New Martial Technique

After returning to the seclusion chamber, Yuan sat down and closed his eyes, entering a state of deep contemplation about the martial technique he intended to create.

Unlike crafting treasures of specific ranks, creating a martial technique was far more unpredictable and perplexing. Even if Yuan aimed to create a Mythic-rank technique, there was no guarantee it would achieve that rank. It could just as easily result in an Ancient-rank technique or even something of lower rank, depending on the execution and refinement of his concept.

However, there was a method to increase the likelihood of creating martial techniques of a specific rank— by combining two or more techniques of that rank. By merging their principles and mechanics, one could craft a new, often more powerful, technique that retained the essence of its predecessors while pushing beyond its original limitations.

For instance, Yuan could attempt to merge two Mythic-rank techniques together to create a superior technique. However, while the concept sounded simple, it was far more difficult to achieve in practice, as the techniques needed to be compatible with each other. Even a minor incompatibility could cause the fusion to fail or result in a broken technique.

After several hours of deep contemplation, Yuan decided to attempt creating a combination technique using Nine Supreme Swords and Asura’s Blade of Execution, two exceptionally powerful Mythic-rank techniques.

Not only did Yuan possess complete mastery and understanding of both techniques, but their foundations were remarkably similar, making them ideal candidates for fusion.

Nine Supreme Swords focused on overwhelming and dominating its target through relentless and precise strikes, whereas Asura’s Blade of Execution was designed purely for lethal efficiency, delivering swift and decisive killing blows.

If he successfully combined the two techniques, Yuan would create a martial technique capable of unleashing immense destruction—one that could dominate and annihilate targets with unparalleled precision and lethal force.

Using his imagination and the unique capabilities of the seclusion chamber, Yuan began visualizing the combination of the two techniques. The formations within the chamber responded to his thoughts, creating an illusion that vividly demonstrated the potential outcome of combining Nine Supreme Swords and Asura’s Blade of Execution.

However, Yuan didn’t stop there and tried several variations of the technique until he was satisfied with the results.

As Yuan became deeply engrossed in the process of creating his new technique, time seemed to blur, and before he realized it, nearly an entire month had passed in the blink of an eye.

After using the formation to demonstrate the technique one last time, Yuan left the seclusion chamber with a satisfied smile on his face.

Then, he used the Martial Tower’s badge to contact Lord Zi. Unfortunately, due to a lack of authority, Yuan could only send a message to Lord Zi and wait for a response.

While he waited for Lord Zi to respond, Yuan visited the lower floors, specifically the twenty-seventh floor, which contained mostly Ancient-rank techniques.

When Yuan arrived at the twenty-seventh floor, he noticed a stark contrast in the number of people present. Unlike the bustling twenty-first floor, which was crowded with hundreds of individuals, this floor had only about a dozen people scattered throughout.

The number of available techniques on the twenty-seventh floor was also significantly lower. While the twenty-first floor boasted tens of thousands of techniques filling tall shelves, the twenty-seventh floor had only about a hundred Ancient-rank techniques carefully arranged.

Yuan proceeded to take a look at these techniques. Naturally, he had no plans of improving them and only wanted to learn some new techniques.

‘Since this place was created by the Stairway to Heaven, these techniques are mine to begin with.’

After looking through several techniques, Yuan realized that most of them were martial techniques meant for daggers, almost as if it was implying something.

‘This trial must be related to Tian Kai…’

Although he already had such suspicions, he wasn’t certain until he saw these techniques.

Several hours later, Yuan’s Martial Tower badge trembled, indicating that someone was trying to contact him.

“Come to the tenth floor. I’ll be there in five minutes,” Lord Zi’s voice echoed.

Hearing this, Yuan promptly made his way to the tenth floor. Expecting to find a few people training, he was surprised to discover the floor completely empty, with not a single soul present besides himself.

Lord Zi showed up a few minutes later, but there was a solemn expression on his face.

“You better not be messing with me when you said that you have finished creating a new Mythic-rank martial technique. It hasn’t even been a month since our last conversation, after all.”

Yuan smiled and said, “Don’t worry, I wouldn’t dare to lie.”

Lord Zi narrowed his eyes as he studied Yuan. Having spent tens of thousands of years with the Martial Tower and personally creating dozens of Mythic-rank techniques, he fully understood the immense difficulty and time required to achieve such a feat. Yet, in all his centuries of experience, he had never encountered or even heard of anyone crafting a Mythic-rank technique in such an astoundingly short period of time.

“I will give you one chance to double-check your creation,” Lord Zi said, his expression grim and his tone severe. “If it turns out that you’ve wasted my time, I will expel you from the Martial Tower immediately. I have no tolerance for those who carelessly create techniques.”

Hearing such words, Yuan merely nodded in silence.

“Good. Then attack me with your new technique. Do not worry about hurting me.” Lord Zi unleashed his aura, revealing his cultivation to be in the Golden Immortal Realm.

Yuan immediately began demonstrating his newly created technique, and as he did so, his killing intent surged to an overwhelming level, catching Lord Zi off guard.

Suddenly, an avatar resembling God of War’s Astral Arts appeared above Lord Zi. It carried a massive sword in one hand and an equally imposing dagger in the other. The sword radiated a domineering aura, exuding an overwhelming power that seemed capable of destroying everything in its path. At the same time, the dagger emanated an intense killing intent so profound it felt as though it could annihilate all life in an instant. The sheer presence of the technique was suffocating, leaving Lord Zi in stunned silence. Searᴄh the NôvelFire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

chapters.title: Chapter 1771 God Of War’S Final Execution
chapters.body:
Chapter 1771 God Of War’S Final Execution

“W-what a terrifying technique…” Lord Zi muttered, his voice trembling as he stared at the avatar. He could feel an overwhelming sense of dread emanating from it, his entire being shuddering in a mixture of fear and awe.

Although Yuan had initially intended to combine only Nine Supreme Swords and Asura’s Blade of Execution, he found himself incorporating elements of God of War’s Astral Arts into the mix.

“I call this technique… God of War’s Final Execution.”

Yuan made a subtle gesture, and in response, the avatar above Lord Zi raised both its arms. Without hesitation, it began slashing with its sword and stabbing with its dagger in a relentless assault.

Lord Zi enveloped his body in Immortal Qi, bracing himself as he raised his arms to block the incoming strikes. While he initially managed to deflect the first few attacks with relative ease, he soon noticed that each successive strike grew more powerful and increasingly difficult to withstand.

While the Nine Supreme Swords concluded with nine devastating strikes, God of War’s Final Execution followed an entirely different principle. The technique was relentless, continuing its assault until its target was utterly obliterated.

What made it even more terrifying was its escalating power—each strike growing stronger than the last. However, this came at a significant cost, as every attack drained Yuan’s spiritual energy. Moreover, the energy expenditure increased alongside the technique’s growing strength, meaning the longer it lasted, the more difficult it became for Yuan to sustain.

At Yuan’s current cultivation level, even if he could recover his spiritual energy in the blink of an eye, it wouldn’t matter if a single strike demanded more energy than he could supply.

In the end, Yuan managed to execute God of War’s Final Execution 20 times, cutting through approximately 60 percent of Lord Zi’s Immortal Qi before exhausting the limits of his current capabilities.

However, this performance did not take into account Yuan’s Chaotic Essence, a different source of energy that, if utilized, could elevate the technique to an entirely new and far more devastating level.

Lord Zi looked at his damaged Immortal Qi with a profound expression on his face.

‘What a devastating technique. He’s only a Spirit Sovereign, but he’s managed to damage my— a Golden Immortal’s Immortal Qi to this extent. He might actually have the capability to slay a Bronze Immortal with this technique if he increased his cultivation a little more.’ Lord Zi thought to himself. Sёarch* The ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“What do you think? How would you rank this technique?” Yuan suddenly asked, his back soaked in sweat. “What do you think? How would you rank this technique?” Yuan suddenly asked, his back soaked in sweat. Lord Zi pondered for a moment before finally responding, “This technique is undeniably at least Mythic-rank. In fact, it rivals the strongest Supreme-rank martial techniques I’ve encountered, and I would rank it as such. Moreover, I believe there is potential to refine and elevate this technique to Celestial-rank in the future.”

“Supreme-rank, huh? I guess that means I qualify for a promotion, right?” Yuan said with a smile.

Lord Zi fell silent for a moment before laughing out loud, “You are correct. I will keep my word and promote you. Congratulations, you’ve become the highest-ranking member of our Martial Tower in a record-breaking time.”

“Thank you.”

“Now that you have full access to the Martial Tower, where would you like to go?” Lord Zi asked.

“What’s on the highest floor?” Yuan asked.

“That would be my private living quarters. Unfortunately, it is only accessible to me,” said Lord Zi.

“Ah, I see. Then I’ll just take a look at the other floors.”

“In that case, I will return to my own work. I can’t wait to see how you’ll contribute to the Martial Tower in the future.”

After Lord Zi left, Yuan made his way to the 28th floor of the tower, where all the Mythic-rank techniques were stored. However, upon arriving at his destination, Yuan was surprised to see not a single Mythic-rank technique available.

He went to the 29th floor next, and sure enough, there was not a single Supreme-rank technique available. However, Yuan wasn’t too shocked, only slightly disappointed.

“I bet the real Martial Tower would be filled with martial techniques on these floors,” he muttered to himself.

Despite anticipating that the 30th floor would likely be empty, Yuan decided to investigate anyway. Upon arriving and spotting a single technique available on the otherwise vacant floor, he realized his effort had been well worth it.

Yuan immediately approached the technique, which rested elegantly on a stand crafted from fine jade. With curiosity and anticipation, he began reading the technique.

Sometime later.

<Shadow Veil learned>

[Shadow Veil]

[Rank: Celestial]

[Mastery Level: 1]

[Description: Envelope yourself in a veil of shadow, completely erasing your presence. While under the shadow’s embrace, you become undetectable by divine sense and all detection techniques.]

Although Celestial Overlord Qiang had given him a technique that concealed his presence, Shadow Veil was incomparably better and could rival even Yingzi’s natural invisible presence as a Shadow Dweller.

‘Actually, this technique was most likely inspired by Shadow Dwellers and their lack of presence…’ Yuan thought to himself.

‘This technique is also perfect for this trial. With this technique, I will be able to spy on the Breakthrough Towers with more ease.’

The first thought that crossed Yuan’s mind after acquiring the concealment technique was to use it to explore the highest floor of the Martial Tower—Lord Zi’s living quarters. However, after giving it more consideration, Yuan decided to hold off until he had visited the other Breakthrough Towers. Sneaking into Lord Zi’s quarters while he was still in the tower posed unnecessary risks.

‘I should lure him out of the tower before I investigate his place,’ Yuan concluded, formulating a more cautious plan.

With that in mind, Yuan decided to visit the next Breakthrough Tower.

Meanwhile, Tan Songyun was utterly bewildered after witnessing Yuan create a Supreme-rank technique in just a single month. Adding to her astonishment were the numerous other achievements he had demonstrated during the trial.

‘Not only can he craft Empyrean-rank treasures with ease, but he’s also capable of creating powerful martial techniques in such a short time. His talent is simply outrageous…’ she thought, swallowing nervously as she recalled Yuan mentioning his previous lives.

chapters.title: Chapter 1772 Cultivation Tower
chapters.body:
Chapter 1772 Cultivation Tower

After learning the martial technique Shadow Veil, Yuan left the Martial Tower to visit another Breakthrough Tower. However, upon leaving the tower, he noticed an individual standing right outside.

This person was Apprentice Huan, who had decided to remain outside the Martial Tower to wait for Yuan. After all, he couldn’t return to the Metal Tower without Yuan.

The moment Apprentice Huan noticed Yuan coming out, he rushed over to block his path.

“You’re really persistent, I’ll give you that,” Yuan said.

“I will not return to the Metal Tower without you.”

“In that case…”

“You can kill me if you want, but I will not budge,” Apprentice Huan said, his voice unexpectedly calm. “My life will be over if I return to the Metal Tower without you, anyway.” His usual arrogant and haughty demeanor was nowhere to be seen, replaced by a quiet resignation.

“I won’t kill you, but I also won’t return to the Metal Tower with you.”

Yuan walked around Apprentice Huan and made his way to the teleportation formation.

Apprentice Huan didn’t say anything and followed him behind in silence.

Yuan pretended he wasn’t even there and continued walking.

Soon, he arrived at the teleportation formation.

When the individual managing the formation noticed the badge on Yuan’s chest, he immediately lowered his head and spoke in a respectful tone, “How can I assist you today, Senior?”

“Bring me to the Cultivation Tower,” Yuan responded.

“Right away.”

Once Yuan entered the teleportation formation and disappeared, Apprentice Huan stepped forward and said, “Take me to the Cultivation Tower as well.”

“Y-yes, Senior from the Metal Tower!”

Sometime later, Yuan arrived at the majestic Cultivation Tower, a structure similar in grandeur to the Martial Tower but exuding a vastly different atmosphere. The air around the Cultivation Tower felt serene and profound, filled with a sense of deep focus and spiritual energy.

Moreover, the Cultivation Tower was a magnet for spiritual energy, drawing it in such immense quantities that the surrounding city was shrouded in a visible fog of dense spiritual energy. When spiritual energy gathers in one place and becomes highly concentrated, it condenses into a mist-like form, making it visible to the naked eye.

Because of this immense concentration of spiritual energy, the area surrounding the Cultivation Tower became a prime location for cultivators. Only those with significant influence and power were permitted to reside close to the tower, enjoying the richer spiritual energy. Ordinary families and less influential individuals were relegated to the outer areas of the city, far from the tower’s core benefits.

As Yuan neared the Cultivation Tower, a guard noticed him and approached with a measured stride. However, the moment the guard’s eyes landed on the badge displayed on Yuan’s chest, his expression shifted to one of surprise. Without saying a word, the guard hastily turned around and walked away with even larger strides, almost as if running away from a ghost while trying not to look scared.

Outside the Cultivation Tower’s entrance, a group of cultivators stood gathered around a single tablet, their eyes fixed on its inscriptions.

Curious, Yuan approached and read the tablet’s contents. As expected, it displayed a cultivation technique. However, something about the technique felt off. Yuan couldn’t immediately identify what was wrong, but he couldn’t shake the strong sense that something wasn’t right with it.

After staring intently for a while, Yuan’s eyes lit up with a spark of realization. He muttered to himself, “Oh, I see. There’s an error in this cultivation technique. No wonder something about it felt off.”

When the other cultivators overheard Yuan’s words, they instantly snapped their heads in his direction, their eyes wide with shock and disbelief.

“Y-you… do you know where the error is?” one of them suddenly asked.

“Hm? Yes, I do. Ah, this must be the trial.” Yuan said with a smile.

While the Martial Tower required participants to improve a martial technique, the Cultivation Tower had a different challenge—identifying the faults or errors within a cultivation technique. This task demanded not only a deep understanding of cultivation principles but also keen eyes to see the flaws.

“He only just got here! How can he see through the flaws so quickly?!” someone there exclaimed.

“Look at the badge on his chest! He’s from the Martial Tower!” seaʀᴄh thё novel(F~)ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Martial techniques and cultivation techniques are completely different from each other! Just because he’s good at one does not mean he’s good at the other!”

Most of the people there have been studying the tablet for months, if not years. Although they were aware the cultivation technique had a flaw, they didn’t know where to find the error.

“I don’t believe you! If you truly believe that you have found the error, then prove it and summon the examiner!” This individual pointed at the bell located right beside the tablet.

Without hesitation, Yuan approached the bell and rang it.

Shortly after the sound of the bell ended, an individual appeared before Yuan like a ghost and asked, “Are you the one who summoned me?”

“Yes,” Yuan confirmed.

“Then tell me, where is the error in this cultivation technique? If you fail, you will not have another chance in this lifetime. Understand?”

Yuan nodded and said, “Allow me to demonstrate it.”

He sat down and began cultivating using the cultivation technique written on the tablet.

A few minutes later, Yuan recited a specific line from the technique aloud and explained, “This part is the error. At a glance, it might seem fine, but over time, it causes long-term side effects that could damage and ultimately ruin one’s spiritual veins.”

The examiner stared at Yuan in silence for a long moment before nodding his head, “Congratulations, you have managed to find the error in this cultivation technique. I see that you’re already part of the Martial Tower, but we do no discriminate. Do you wish to join our Cultivation Tower, too?”

“Yes, I would like to join the Cultivation Tower.”

“Very well. Follow me. I’ll give you a tour.”

Yuan nodded and followed the examiner inside the tower.

chapters.title: Chapter 1773 Cultivation Tower(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1773 Cultivation Tower(2)

As Yuan followed the examiner inside the Cultivation Tower, the man said, “Let me introduce myself first. My name is Ming Wu, and I have been working at the Cultivation Tower for over five thousand years.”

“My name is Yuan.”

“How long have you been with the Martial Tower?”

“Not long. About a month.”

“I see. Then, how much do you know about the Cultivation Tower?” Ming Wu suddenly asked.

“I know that they improve and create cultivation techniques, much like the Martial Tower.”

“You’re not wrong, but we are much more important than the Martial Tower, no matter how they try to argue otherwise. We are the foundation of the cultivation world. Without cultivation techniques, there would be no cultivators,” said Ming Wu.

“Well, I can’t argue with that logic.”

“At least you’re understanding. There are others from the Martial Tower who have joined our Cultivation Tower, but they are all too stubborn to admit that we are more important than them,” Ming Wu sighed.

“I won’t argue with you over that subject, if that’s what you’re trying to make me do.”

“Then let me ask you—why did you join us?”

“To learn more about cultivation techniques. Is that a bad answer?”

“Not necessarily. I joined the tower myself because I wanted a better cultivation technique. However, as I spent time in this place, I began to learn and understand the importance of cultivation techniques.”

“Anyway, the first floor isn’t used for anything, and the real tower begins on the second floor.”

When they arrived on the second floor, Yuan could see a single individual cultivating in the center of the room.

“The Cultivation Tower has the lowest number of members because we are the hardest to qualify for. After all, we don’t want anyone messing up cultivation techniques and potentially crippling people.”

“More than half of our members are dedicated to checking cultivation techniques to ensure they are safe to use. The others focus on improving existing cultivation techniques, and very few of us actually create new cultivation techniques.”

“I see… And how does one increase their rank in the Cultivation Tower?” Yuan asked a moment later.

“There are only two ranks in the Cultivation Tower,” Ming Wu explained. “The first is the Tower Master, and the rest of us share the same rank, so there’s no need to concern yourself too much with titles. What differentiates us are our jobs. Of course, seniority still plays a role in our hierarchy.”

“So, I have full access to the Cultivation Tower?” Yuan asked.

“You have full access to the resources needed for your specific role,” Ming Wu replied. “What exactly do you wish to do at our tower?”

“Am I restricted to one role? What if I wish to do everything?”

Ming Wu’s eyes widened at his words.

“While we are not restricted to a single role,” Ming Wu cautioned, “you shouldn’t bite off more than you can chew—especially since you’ve only just joined us. Here, you’re expected to contribute in all roles simultaneously.”

“I have more experience than I look,” Yuan said with a calm smile.

Ming Wu scoffed, “I believe you, but you still have no idea how much effort is required. Let me show you.”

Ming Wu brought Yuan to an empty floor and handed him two different cultivation techniques.

“This one is a Divine-rank cultivation technique with an error, and this one is a Divine-rank that can be improved. I want you to do both of them. Let’s see how long it’ll take you to accomplish this task.”

Yuan accepted the two cultivation techniques and nodded, “Alright. Any time limit?”

“No, take as long as you need. Here’s your badge. Contact me once you’re done.”

Ming Wu left Yuan alone shortly after.

‘It’ll probably take him several months to finish.’ Ming Wu thought to himself as he left.

However, to Ming Wu’s shock, Yuan contacted him before the day was even over.

“What’s wrong?” Ming Wu asked after answering Yuan’s call through the badge.

“I’m done.”

“What?” Ming Wu’s face stiffened.

Although he had a good idea of what Yuan meant, he didn’t dare to believe it.

“I’m done with both techniques,” Yuan elaborated. seaʀᴄh thё Novelƒire(.)ne*t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“There’s no way! Don’t lie to me! Although they might’ve only been Divine-rank cultivation techniques, they’re not something you can finish in less than a day!” Ming Wu said.

“I’ll only waste my breath trying to convince you with words, so I’ll show you. Come find me,” said Yuan before hanging up on Ming Wu.

Hearing this, Ming Wu dropped whatever he was doing and rushed back to Yuan’s floor.

Seeing Ming Wu before him, Yuan began his demonstration. For the cultivation technique that had an error, Yuan not only pointed out the error but also found a solution for it, something the Cultivation Tower was still working on.

As for the second cultivation technique, which had already been improved by the Cultivation Tower, Yuan managed to improve it with even better results, leaving Ming Wu speechless.

“What do you think? Do I have what it takes to do multiple roles at once?” Yuan asked him with a smile afterward.

In response, Ming Wu retrieved two more cultivation techniques and handed them to Yuan.

“Do the same thing with these two Ancient-rank cultivation techniques. If you can manage to do that, I’ll speak with the Tower Master and allow you full access to the Cultivation Tower.”

“Deal.” Yuan nodded, and he immediately began working on the techniques.

Ming Wu didn’t immediately leave this time, as he’d decided to stay behind for a few days to see Yuan’s process.

Since it had been a long time since Yuan last worked on improving techniques, he initially took some time to regain his footing. However, as he continued to work, his past life experiences began resurfacing with greater clarity. With each attempt, his ability to identify flaws and implement improvements grew significantly sharper and more profound.

Two days later, Yuan opened his eyes and turned to look at Ming Wu, who immediately swallowed nervously.

“I’m done,” he announced with a calm smile on his face.

chapters.title: Chapter 1774 Cultivation Tower(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1774 Cultivation Tower(3)

“Y-you’re done? It’s only been two days, you know? There’s no way you finished two Ancient-rank techniques in two days,” Ming Wu shook his head, refusing to believe Yuan.

“Allow me to prove it.”

However, Ming Wu raised his hand and said, “Wait! Let me contact someone first!”

“Give me some time. I’ll be back soon.”

Without offering any further explanation, Ming Wu turned and left, disappearing to an unknown destination.

A few hours later, Ming Wu returned, but he wasn’t alone. Walking beside him was a stunningly beautiful woman, her presence commanding attention with an air of elegance and grace.

“This is the Tower Master,” Ming Wu said.

“My name is Meng Xuan. Ming Wu told me that you have managed to improve an Ancient-rank technique and fix another in just two days. I never expected someone from the Martial Tower to have such talents with cultivation techniques. Can you show me how you improved the techniques?” she said.

Yuan nodded and immediately began demonstrating the improved cultivation techniques.

Meng Xuan’s eyes widened after seeing the improvements made to the techniques.

Both techniques that Ming Wu had given Yuan as a test were cultivation techniques the Cultivation Tower had planned on working on next, so there was no way Yuan could have cheated.

“Marvelous!” Meng Xuan exclaimed after seeing the improvements.

“Although they’re only Ancient-rank techniques, you’ve managed to improve them so much that they qualify for a rank promotion! This rarely happens to cultivation techniques!” she continued. Sёarᴄh the NôᴠeFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Unlike martial techniques, which can often be elevated to higher ranks through improvements due to their inherently adaptable nature, cultivation techniques are far more rigid and complex. They rarely undergo improvements so profound that they result in a promotion of the technique’s rank, as their foundational principles are much harder to alter or refine.

“And it only took you two days to do so! Such talents are rarely seen nowadays. How about this? I have been looking for a successor for some time now, but nobody in the Cultivation Tower has managed to impress me. If you can pass one more trial, I can make you the next Tower Master!”

“What?!” Ming Wu exclaimed in shock. “Tower Master, he just joined us recently! If you make him your successor, the others will not be happy about this!” “I know. That’s why I want to give him a few more tests. Also, it’s not like he’ll immediately take my position. I still need to train him,” said Meng Xuan.

“Still, that isn’t fair to the others, who have been striving to be your successor for many decades!” Ming Wu said. Hearing this, Meng Xuan sneered, “I’m surprised you can utter such words without feeling ashamed. The only thing that matters to me is your talent and ability to improve cultivation techniques. I don’t care how long you’ve been in the Cultivation Tower.”

“T-that’s…” Ming Wu lowered his head after being lectured by Meng Xuan.

“I’ll do it,” Yuan responded a moment later.

“Great.”

Meng Xuan retrieved ten techniques and said, “They are all Mythic-rank techniques, but one of them has an error. It took me nearly a hundred years to find the technique with the error and correct it. If you can do so faster, I’ll make you my successor.”

Yuan nodded, “Alright.”

Although the trial seemed simple enough, it actually required Yuan to reach a profound understanding of all ten Mythic-rank cultivation techniques and find the one with an error.

Just learning a single Mythic-rank cultivation technique would take decades for even talented cultivators, much less understanding them enough to spot errors.

“You can come directly to me at the top of the tower once you are done,” said Meng Xuan before leaving.

Ming Wu didn’t linger and followed Meng Xuan.

Once he was alone, Yuan laid out the cultivation techniques before him and began studying them.

If Yuan had been an ordinary cultivator, having access to so many Mythic-rank cultivation techniques would have been a life-altering event. However, due to his unique physique, Yuan had little need for cultivation techniques anymore. His body naturally absorbed spiritual energy at a rate far beyond what even the most powerful cultivation techniques could achieve, and he could also gather spiritual energy by consuming monster cores. For him, a cultivation technique was almost redundant.

While Yuan studied the techniques, Meng Xuan focused on creating a cultivation technique at the top of the tower. She was confident that Yuan would be able to pass her trial, but she could’ve never anticipated how fast he’d be able to do it.

Fourteen days after Meng Xuan gave Yuan the trial, she noticed someone had entered her floor.

When she opened the door and saw Yuan standing outside, her eyes widened with disbelief.

“What are you doing here so soon? Don’t tell me you’ve already finished the task…?”

Yuan nodded and said, “Yes.”

Meng Xuan narrowed her eyes and said, “Oh? Then tell me, which technique had the error?”

Yuan replied with a smile on his face, “I must admit, you fooled me for a bit. After going through all ten cultivation techniques, I couldn’t find any errors in them. I doubted myself a little, so I went through them again, hence why it took this long. There are no errors in those cultivation techniques you gave me.”

Meng Xuan silently stared at Yuan for a long moment before she burst out laughing, “Amazing! Truly amazing! I cannot fathom how you’ve managed to complete this task so soon, but here you are! Alright, I’ll make you my successor—No… I wouldn’t mind even if you took my position as Tower Master right this moment!”

“What do you even do as the Tower Master?” Yuan asked.

“Everything—from correcting cultivation techniques to improving them. Of course, you also need to know how to create them. Have you ever created a cultivation technique before?”

Yuan pondered for a moment before shaking his head, “I’ve created plenty of martial techniques before, but I’ve never created a cultivation technique before—at least, I don’t have any memories of doing so.”

“Well, there’s no need to rush. Despite being at the Cultivation Tower for tens of thousands of years, I have only created a single cultivation technique. I am in the process of creating my second one.”

Yuan nodded.

chapters.title: Chapter 1775 Successor Of The Cultivation Tower
chapters.body:
Chapter 1775 Successor Of The Cultivation Tower

“Now that you’re my successor and apprentice, I’ll have you call me Master,” said Meng Xuan with a slight smile on her face.

Yuan nodded and said, “Master, before we start training, I would like to check out the other Breakthrough Towers.”

“Why? Is the Cultivation Tower and Martial Tower not enough for you?” Meng Xuan asked with a slight frown on her face.

“You’re right. I am not satisfied with just these options,” Yuan admitted. “As a cultivator, I want to ensure my future is as bright as possible, so I want to visit the other towers and see what I can learn from them as well.”

It was an excuse Meng Xuan couldn’t argue against. She understood his perspective—after all, striving for a better future was something most people, including herself, aspired to achieve. Blaming Yuan for such a mindset would have been unreasonable.

“Fine, but try not to stay there for too long,” she said a moment later.

Yuan nodded with a smile, “Of course. Thank you for being understanding, Master.”

However, Yuan didn’t immediately leave the Cultivation Tower. He decided to explore other floors first to see if he could find anything suspicious about the tower. Unfortunately, even after searching through every floor he was permitted to visit as the Tower Master’s successor, he couldn’t find anything that could put the Cultivation Tower in a bad light.

‘This trial might be harder than it appears…’ Yuan just came to this realization.

Moreover, he still needed to visit the Alchemy Tower and Array Tower. While his experience in arrays and formations has improved significantly due to Yu Ning’s occasional teachings, he wasn’t sure it would be enough to satisfy the Array Tower’s requirements.

As for the Alchemy Tower, besides learning the basics, he’d barely touched alchemy, so there was a good chance he wouldn’t qualify to join them.

‘I guess I’ll figure it out when I get there.’

With that in mind, Yuan left the Cultivation Tower to visit the other towers.

The first thing Yuan noticed after exiting the Cultivation Tower was Apprentice Huan, who was standing outside.

Although it’d already been longer than a month—longer than the time limit Senior Liao had given him, Apprentice Huan hadn’t given up on trying to bring Yuan back to the Metal Tower.

“What will it take for you to return to the Metal Tower with me?” Apprentice Huan asked as he followed Yuan to the teleportation formation.

“I already told you. If you want me to return to the Metal Tower, you better get Liao Tian to invite me personally.”

“T-that’s…”

“What? You still think I am not worth his time? Not only have I acquired Lord Zi’s approval, but I’ve even managed to become the Cultivation Tower’s successor,” Yuan said.

“What?! You became the Cultivation Tower’s successor?!” Apprentice Huan exclaimed loudly, his face filled with shock.

Yuan showed Apprentice Huan the badge Meng Xuan gave him before he left.

“If you don’t believe me, you can ask Meng Xuan yourself.”

Apprentice Huan sighed, “Who am I to speak to the Tower Master? Senior Liao is the Metal Tower’s Tower Master, too.”

“I have said what I need to. It’s up to you whether you listen to me or not. I visited the Metal Tower first, but you drove me away, so this is a grave you dug yourself.”

Yuan stepped onto the teleportation formation and said, “Bring me to the Array Tower.”

Once Yuan disappeared, Apprentice Huan remained standing outside the teleportation formation with a pondering look on his face.

Sometime later, he stepped onto the formation and said, “The Metal Tower.”

Upon arriving at the Metal Tower, Apprentice Huan made his way to the highest floor of the tower.

“What are you doing here, Apprentice Huan?” someone asked, standing outside Liao Tian’s doors. His gaze was sharp, and his tone carried curiosity and suspicion as he inquired about Apprentice Huan’s reason for being there.

“I have an urgent matter with Senior Liao,” he said.

“You’re just a mere Apprentice. What business could you possibly have with the Tower Master that’s more important than mine?” sneered the man, who was several ranks above Apprentice Huan.

The door suddenly opened, and Liao Tian walked out, carrying a dignified aura around him.

“Tower Master—”

Just as the man opened his mouth to greet, Liao Tian interrupted, his gaze focused on Apprentice Huan.

“I was going to forgive your tardiness since you managed to bring that individual back,” Liao Tian said, his voice cold and unyielding. “But what is going on here, Apprentice Huan? Why are you alone?”

As he spoke, killing intent began to emanate from his body, causing the air around him to grow heavy with pressure.

Not just Apprentice Huan, but the other guy there trembled in fear.

“I thought I said not to return unless you have that individual with you. Are my words meaningless to you?” S~eaʀᴄh the ηovelFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“That’s not it!” Apprentice Huan exclaimed, his voice shaky as his legs trembled uncontrollably. “I found him and tried to convince him to return, but he wouldn’t listen! He even dared to say that he’ll only come back to the Metal Tower if you, Senior Liao, personally invite him back!”

His fear was evident as he struggled to steady himself under Liao Tian’s intimidating presence.

“Which bold bastards dare to utter such non—”

“Shut the fuck up!” Liao Tian roared at the man who tried to join the conversation.

Apprentice Huan continued, “That man not only managed to join the Martial Tower and received Lord Zi’s approval but he’s also managed to become the Cultivation Tower’s successor!”

Liao Tian’s eyes narrowed at this shocking information.

‘Martial Tower and Cultivation Tower, huh? Well, it doesn’t really matter who he belongs to since I am only after the treasure in his possession,’ Liao Tian thought to himself.

After pondering for a moment, Liao Tian said, “Where is he right now?”

“He went to the Array Tower before we separated!”

“Very well. If he wants me to personally see him, I shall do just that.”

As Liao Tian walked past Apprentice Huan to reach the exit, his arm flickered.

The next second, Apprentice Huan’s body exploded into a bloody mess.

“Clean up this mess, but don’t throw away the materials,” Liao Tian said to the other man without turning around.

“A-as you wish, Tower Master!”

chapters.title: Chapter 1776 Level 4 Array Master
chapters.body:
Chapter 1776 Level 4 Array Master

After arriving in the city home to the Array Tower, Yuan was immediately struck by the sheer number of formations that filled the area. The bustling streets were a showcase of intricate arrays and formations, seamlessly integrated into everyday life. From glowing defensive barriers that protected the buildings to automated market stalls and transportation platforms powered by arrays.

Compared to all the other places Yuan had visited in the Nine Heavens, this city had an almost futuristic appearance.

Moreover, the formations grew increasingly impressive as Yuan approached the Array Tower. The tower itself was a marvel of formation craftsmanship, its structure covered in profound Tier 7 and above formations that radiated an aura of power and complexity.

Out of the five Breakthrough Towers, the Array Tower was undeniably the most protected and powerful. Attacking it would be akin to trying to breach a fortress where you could be attacked in every direction, regardless of your location.

At the entrance of the Array Tower was a sign that read, “Only Level 5 Array Masters and above qualify to take the entrance exam to join the Array Tower.”

“Level 5 Array Master, huh?” Yuan was currently a Level 2 Array Master, though this was primarily because he hadn’t yet taken the assessments for the higher levels. His true knowledge and capabilities surpassed his official rank.

Occasionally, Yu Ning, with her extensive expertise, would lecture Yuan, helping to deepen his understanding of the art. While these lessons weren’t frequent, they were more than enough to help him become a Level 4 Array Master.

“I need to somehow improve my array skills to level 5 before I can even attempt to join the Array Tower…”

Yuan left the Array Tower and began wandering the city in hopes of finding something, as he couldn’t ask for Yu Ning’s help since she was sent to the spectator’s room.

However, not wanting to stress out Tan Songyun, Yuan had them sent to a second spectator’s room, so while Tan Songyun occupied a room herself, Xiao Hua, Feng Yuxiang, Yu Ning, and Yingzi also occupied their own rooms.

As for Zi Xuan, Ji Ran, and Dragon Goddess Yeyou, they remained within Yuan’s Soul Weapons. That said, Yuan wouldn’t be able to contact them until the trial ended.

Sometime later, Yuan stopped in front of a specific building.

“Array Masters’ Association.”

Such a sign was grandly displayed on this building.

“I should improve my rank first,” he muttered to himself as he entered the building. Sёarch* The NôᴠeFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

When Yuan got close enough, the young man standing by the entrance asked, “How can we help you today?”

Yuan retrieved his Level 2 Array Master badge and showed it to the young man.

“I’m here for a promotion.”

“Of course! Please, follow me.”

Yuan followed the young man into a spacious room, where three individuals were already seated behind desks. Their authoritative auras were palpable, radiating a sense of power and experience that immediately set the tone of the room. It was clear that these three were the judges responsible for overseeing his assessment. They studied Yuan with discerning eyes, their expressions neutral but their scrutiny evident.

“State your name and current rank,” said the old man sitting in the middle.

“I am a Level 2 Array Master, Yuan.”

“Then you’ll be partaking in a level 3 assessment. I want you to create a hundred level 3 array symbols within 10 seconds. You have half a minute to prepare yourself.”

Yuan then said, “I can start now.”

Without any hesitation, he began writing in the air, creating a hundred flawless level 3 array symbols in only a little over two seconds.

The judges’ eyes widened with awe and surprise after seeing his flawless array symbols. They couldn’t believe how quickly Yuan had managed to create such beautiful symbols in such a short time.

“You don’t plan on stopping after passing the level 3 assessment, correct?” one of them suddenly asked.

Yuan nodded, “I plan on taking the level 4 and level 5 assessment as well.”

Even if he wasn’t confident in passing the level 5 assessment, he still wanted to try it and see how close he was to being one.

“Very well.”

They continued the assessment shortly after.

The three judges began creating their own level 3 formations. Once they were done, they had Yuan try to replicate it to the best of his abilities.

Familiar with this process, Yuan copied them flawlessly.

“You pass.”

The judges nodded with satisfied looks on their faces.

For the final test, Yuan had to create a level 3 formation that wasn’t just used by the judges, and he had three minutes to do so.

Yuan didn’t spend much time pondering and randomly picked one—not that he knew many level 3 formations.

Sometime later, Yuan finished the formation.

“Congratulations, you have passed the assessment. Do you wish to take the level 4 assessment right away? Of course, you can return another day to take it.”

“I want to take the level four formation right now.”

The judges nodded.

“Then, for your first test, you must create forty level 4 and three hundred level 3 array symbols within 30 seconds. You have a minute to prepare, or you can start before then.”

Yuan didn’t immediately start. He took about ten seconds to mentally prepare himself before he started writing in the air.

It took him seven seconds to finish all three hundred level 3 array symbols, but the level 4 array symbols were much more complex and tricky. Although he only had to create forty of them, it took him almost as long as it took him to create the level 3 array symbols.

Next, he had to copy the judges’ level 4 formations, most of them consisting of around five hundred symbols.

Several minutes later, Yuan easily passed the second test and moved on to the final one. Sure enough, he passed it with flying colors as well, becoming a Level 4 Array Master in no time.
