chapters.title: Chapter 1777 Level 5 Array Master
chapters.body:
Chapter 1777 Level 5 Array Master

“Are you going to take the level 5 assessment immediately, too?” one of the judges asked Yuan after he passed the level 4 assessment.

“If I fail it, how long do I have to wait for a second chance?” Yuan asked, just in case.

“You will need to wait six months before you can attempt another assessment if you fail.”

“Oh… In that case, I’ll wait a little longer before I take the assessment. By the way, I can borrow manuals of level 5 array symbols from the Array Masters’ Association, correct?”

The judge sitting in the middle nodded and said, “As long as you are part of our Array Masters’ Association, we will naturally help you reach the next rank. Since you show promise, I am willing to give you personal lessons. What do you think? I am a Level 6 Array Master, so I definitely qualify to teach you.”

Yuan nodded and said, “I will humbly accept your offer, Senior.”

“My name’s Hao Chen, but you can just call me Senior Hao.”

“Yes, Senior Hao!”

“Are you busy right now?” Hao Chen then asked.

“No.”

“Good, then we’ll begin your lecture now. Follow me.”

Yuan nodded and followed the old man to a room located near the peak of the building.

“I can’t believe Senior Hao took a liking to that young man so quickly,” said one of the other two judges after they left.

“Senior Hao has a keen eye for talents. Did you not see how swiftly and perfectly that young man created the array symbols? He’ll become a formidable Array Master in the near future.”

Meanwhile, inside Hao Chen’s room, he handed Yuan several books, including one that had all level 5 array symbols. “Honestly, there really isn’t much to teach when it comes to array symbols, as it primarily depends on each individual’s memorization ability,” Hao Chen explained. “Once you’ve memorized the symbols, the rest is just practice. This is why most Array Masters don’t necessarily need a master—they only require the right resources to advance their skills.”

“That said, if you have any questions, you can ask me. I’ll be studying array symbols myself.” Hao Chen sat in his seat and began studying level 7 array symbols.

Yuan also began studying Level 5 array symbols. Thanks to his experience as an immortal, his memorization ability was extraordinarily powerful, allowing him to memorize complex symbols with ease. Additionally, his formidable soul strength significantly enhanced his capacity to learn and comprehend details more quickly, hence why soul cultivation techniques are exceptionally rare and sought after.

About a week later, Yuan memorized all level 5 array symbols, and he began practicing writing them. Hao Chen was greatly shocked by how swiftly Yuan had memorized the array symbols.

‘There’s no way he actually memorized everything from scratch in less than a week. He must’ve already memorized a portion of level 5 array symbols prior to coming here,’ Hao Chen thought to himself.

‘Still… his array symbols are too beautiful. That level of mastery requires tens of thousands of years of experience to achieve, so how can someone seemingly so young achieve it?’

Hao Chen gave Yuan three level 5 formations to study, each requiring the combination of around 500 level 5 array symbols to complete.

“Alright, I am ready to take the level 5 assessment,” Yuan said to Hao Chen a few days later.

Hao Chen stared at him with wide eyes and said, “Just how strong is your soul strength? It’s only been a little over a week, you know? I was expecting your company for at least a few months.”

Yuan shook his head with a smile, “I don’t have that much time.”

“Why the rush?”

“I need to join the Array Tower as soon as possible.”

“The Array Tower, huh? I used to work with them, but due to my lack of talents, I was forced to leave after just a hundred years. I didn’t want to admit it back then, but alas, the Array Masters there are a different breed—sort of like you.”

Sometime later, Yuan went downstairs with Hao Chen to take the level 5 assessment. Sёarch* The NovelFire.net* website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

The other two judges showed up a little later.

“You’re taking the level 5 assessment already? It’s only been a week,” one of them said to Yuan.

“I was already familiar with level 5 array symbols. I just needed a little more time to be confident in the assessment,” said Yuan.

‘I knew it…’ Hao Chen sighed inwardly after hearing this.

“Alright.”

Sometime later, Hao Chen said, “The level 5 assessment will be a little different compared to your previous trials.”

“For the first trial, you’ll need to create a level 5 defensive formation, and it must withstand the strength of a cultivator at the Divine King realm. You have five minutes to create the formation. You have a minute to prepare.”

One of the level 5 formations that Hao Chen gave to Yuan to study happened to be a defensive formation, and Yuan decided to use it for the trial.

Two minutes later, Yuan created a defensive formation in front of him and said, “It’s done.”

“Very well. I will now attack it with the strength of a Divine King.”

Hao Chen attacked the formation with a simple attack, which bounced right off the defensive formation.

“You pass. For the second trial, you must create a level 5 offensive formation, and it must have a certain level of power. You have ten minutes for this test and one minute to prepare.”

Another formation Hao Chen gave Yuan to study also happened to be an offensive formation, almost as if he was purposefully helping Yuan prepare for the level 5 assessment.

After spending six minutes creating the formation, Yuan said, “I am finished.”

Hao Chen retrieved a white talisman and tossed it inside his formation.

“Attack it.”

Yuan nodded and activated the formation. In an instant, swords composed entirely of concentrated spiritual energy materialized around the room, their edges gleaming with power. The swords moved with precision, launching toward the talisman.

A barrier appeared around the talisman in an attempt to protect it, but the barrier was instantly shattered by the swords.

“Good. Now, onto the final test,” Hao Chen said.

chapters.title: Chapter 1778 Array Tower
chapters.body:
Chapter 1778 Array Tower

“For the final test, you must activate two level 5 formations—one defense and one offensive—simultaneously and keep them activated for an hour without any external assistance, such as spirit stones. You have five minutes to prepare,” said Hao Chen.

Any Array Master could easily activate a single formation and sustain it using spirit stones. However, activating and maintaining two formations simultaneously was far more challenging. It required significantly more spiritual energy and placed a substantial burden on one’s soul strength.

Yuan spent a few minutes mentally preparing himself. Once he was ready, he recreated the offensive formation and defensive formation he showcased during the last two tests.

After activating them, he sat down and closed his eyes to focus on maintaining the formations. Normally, this test would push most Array Masters to their limits, but Yuan felt incredibly at ease.

His spiritual energy recovery was so powerful that he could easily maintain a dozen formations simultaneously, much less two level 5 formations. As for his soul strength, it had reached the point where he couldn’t even feel it depleting.

An hour passed in the blink of an eye, and Yuan passed the final trial without breaking a single drop of sweat.

“Congratulations, you are now officially a Level 5 Array Master!” Hao Chen said to him afterward.

“It was only thanks to you, Senior Hao.”

“In that case, don’t forget about me when you make it big in the Array Tower,” Hao Chen laughed merrily.

Yuan left the Array Masters’ Association shortly after and made his way back to the Array Tower.

Once he returned to the Array Tower, Yuan approached the closed entrance, where a powerful tier 5 formation kept the doors sealed.

Yuan noticed the formation before he became a Level 5 Array Master, but he did not have the means to unseal it.

He proceeded to spend several hours trying to crack the formation. Despite the formation being a level 5 formation, it could qualify as a level 6 array solely due to its complexity.

Eventually, Yuan cracked the formation, unsealing the doors.

When Yuan stepped into the Array Tower, an ordinary-looking young woman greeted him just slightly beyond the entrance.

“Congratulations, you have qualified to take the assessment to join our Array Tower. Would you like to take the assessment right now?”

As the woman spoke, her gaze landed on Yuan’s tower badges. Her eyes widened with surprise, as it was quite rare to see an individual associated with two towers simultaneously, especially the Martial Tower and Cultivation Tower.

“I would,” Yuan nodded in response.

“Very well. If you could follow me, please.”

Yuan followed the young woman to an empty room, where another individual was waiting.

This person was a middle-aged woman with dark green hair. Sёarch* The NôᴠeFire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“In order to join our Array Tower, you must defeat me using level 5 array formations only,” said the green-haired woman.

“We will both have ten minutes to prepare a defensive formation around our body. Then, we will attack using attack formations. Whoever breaks their opponent’s defensive formation wins.”

“Any questions?”

“Am I allowed to use formations lower than level 5?”

“Yes, anything below level 5 formations are allowed.”

Yuan nodded.

“Then we will begin in right now.”

Both Yuan and the green-haired woman began working on their defensive formation.

Yuan finished his level 5 defensive formation less than three minutes into the match. Seeing this, the green-haired woman sneered inwardly, ‘What a fool, rushing his defensive formation. Not only is he using one formation, but he didn’t even bother to refine it.’

A formation can be strengthened in two primary ways. The first is through refinement, where the Array Master redraws or recreates the formation, layering it over the existing one to enhance its power.

The second method involves using treasures and external resources to amplify the formation’s effects. However, for an assessment like this, relying on external aids was clearly not an option, leaving Yuan to rely solely on his skills and endurance.

The green-haired woman finished her defensive formation nine and a half minutes later. She was going to create more than one defensive formation, but seeing how Yuan was still a beginner, she didn’t bother doing so, looking down on him.

“We will begin now,” said the green-haired woman.

Yuan nodded and immediately started creating hundreds of level 1 array symbols every second.

‘This bastard, he’s using level 1 array symbols?’ The green-haired woman raised her eyebrows and thought to herself as she worked on her level 5 array symbols.

Due to how fast Yuan was creating his array symbols, he finished creating dozens of attack formations around the woman.

“Activate.”

The formations attacked the woman’s defensive formation, leaving tiny cracks on the surface every time they struck. However, these cracks quickly recovered in the blink of an eye.

“The power is quite lacking…” Yuan muttered after seeing the results.

Meanwhile, the woman was shocked that his level 1 attack formations could even damage her level 5 defensive formation.

‘His soul strength must be as high as the heavens!’ she cried inwardly, almost fumbling her attack formation from the shock.

After his initial attack, Yuan didn’t attack again, as he wanted to see how much his defensive formation could endure.

Once the woman finished her level 5 offensive formation, she immediately activated it and attacked Yuan’s defensive formation.

A strong explosion consumed Yuan’s figure and formation a moment later.

Inside the cloud of dust and smoke, Yuan could see that his defensive formation had barely been affected by the attack, with only slight cracks that healed instantly. Seeing this, he continued his assault, using level 2 attack formations.

By the time the smoke and dust settled a few moments later, Yuan had created over a dozen level 2 attack formations, using them to attack the woman, who was in disbelief and couldn’t believe that Yuan’s defensive formation had managed to survive her attack.

‘How?! His formation should’ve been heavily damaged if not outright destroyed by that attack just now!’ she cried inwardly, her eyes wide with disbelief as Yuan’s attack stuck her formation.

chapters.title: Chapter 1779 Array Tower(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1779 Array Tower(2)

When Yuan’s Level 2 attack formations struck the woman’s defensive formation, the impact was powerful enough to cause the entire thing to shake and large cracks to appear on its surface. The woman gritted her teeth as she poured her spiritual energy into the formation, quickly restoring it. However, the damage to her formation had also depleted some of her soul strength, something not easily recoverable.

‘Not good! At this rate, his level 3 attack formations will be enough to destroy my defensive formation!’

The woman started casting level 3 array symbols just like Yuan since she would be able to attack much faster.

Yuan completed his array symbols a full two seconds before the woman, and in the world of Array Masters, even a fraction of a second could determine the victor, much less two seconds.

Seeing that Yuan launched his attacks before she could, the woman swiftly transferred half of her focus from offensive to defensive, strengthening her formation with a tremendous amount of spiritual energy.

However, even with her defensive formation enhanced, Yuan’s level 3 attack formation was still strong enough to penetrate her barrier. Yet, it wasn’t enough to completely destroy the barrier, as the holes would heal faster than Yuan could damage it.

The woman had managed to survive Yuan’s onslaught, but it’d cost her dearly, and more than half of her spiritual energy was exhausted in that brief moment. Moreover, focusing on two different things took a toll on her soul strength.

Fortunately, she was able to finish creating her level 3 attack formations, and she bombarded Yuan’s barrier the very next moment.

Seeing this, Yuan decided to strengthen his own barrier with his spiritual energy.

When the woman’s attacks struck Yuan’s barrier, they bounced off effortlessly, leaving the barrier completely unscathed. Not even the faintest scratch appeared on its surface.

The unexpected result left the woman utterly speechless, her disbelief evident as she stared at the barrier in stunned silence.

At that moment, Yuan finished a level 4 attack formation, attacking her barrier with it.

Clang!

The woman’s barrier was shattered like glass after being struck by a rock despite her swift reaction and attempt to exhaust the rest of her spiritual energy to enhance her barrier. Unfortunately, even with all of her spiritual energy, she was unable to defend herself against a single level 4 attack formation from Yuan.

The woman fell to her knees and began gasping for air as her face dripped with sweat. Their battle had only lasted for a few minutes, but it was enough to completely exhaust her spiritual energy and soul strength.

“You… win,” she announced sometime later after coming back from her shock.

“That was my first battle as an Array Master. It was quite fun. Thanks for the experience,” Yuan bowed to her.

“Your first time…?” The woman stared at him with wide eyes.

She eventually shook her head, dismissing his remark as a joke, and said, “Anyway, you’ve passed the test and officially qualify to join our Array Tower.” Her tone was composed, but there was an undeniable hint of acknowledgment in her voice.

“By the way, my name is Wan Qing.”

“Yuan.”

“Here is your badge, Yuan,” Wan Qing handed him a badge that had several powerful formations hidden within.

Yuan subconsciously inspected the formations. Seeing his serious face, Wan Qing chuckled, “Relax, the formations inside that badge are there to protect you since the badge can also be used as a life-saving treasure.”

Sure enough, Yuan confirmed that the formations were all defensive formations—except one.

“Why is there a tracker in here?” Yuan asked with raised eyebrows.

“Unlike the other towers, our Array Tower frequently sends its Array Masters out to assist those in need of our expertise,” she explained. “The tracker is an additional layer of safety. In case we’re kidnapped or killed, it can detect if our life is in danger, as well as help locate us.”

“What a convenient thing,” Yuan muttered.

“By the way, what is the ranking structure like in the Array Tower?” Yuan then asked.

“It’s similar to that Array Masters’ Association, but we start from level 5—going all the way to level 9. If you want to increase your rank, you’ll also need to improve your Array Master level.”

“I see…”

Yuan immediately began pondering. There was simply no way he could become a level 9 Array Master in a few months—before the time limit for his reward expires.

‘Looks like I will have to sneak around this place, just like the Alchemy Tower.’

“I understand. Thank you.”

Wan Qing handed him a scroll and said, “This is a list of things we expect you to do while part of the Array Tower. Make sure to read it because if you fail to do even a single thing on the list, you’ll be kicked out of the tower.”

Yuan accepted the scroll and took a look at it.

The Array Tower required its Array Masters to fulfill at least one mission a year. These missions are typically business requests sent to the Array Towers. For example, a sect would like to create a powerful formation around their territory, so they contact the Array Tower. Of course, whether the Array Tower accepted the request or not was a different story. seaʀᴄh thё Nôvelƒire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

The next thing on the list required Array Masters below level 7 to reach that level within three hundred years. Failure to reach level 7 before the time limit would result in expulsion.

‘Safe to say none of these rules matter to me.’

Yuan didn’t even bother reading the rest of the list and tossed it inside his spatial ring.

“What are you going to do now that you’re part of the Array Tower?” Wan Qing suddenly asked with a curious face.

“I’m going to the Alchemy Tower,” he responded with a smile.

“…”

Wan Qing stared at him with wide eyes and muttered, “Are you trying to join every single tower out there?”

“Yes, I am.”

“Why?”

“Why not?” Yuan shrugged, leaving her speechless.

chapters.title: Chapter 1780 Alchemy Tower
chapters.body:
Chapter 1780 Alchemy Tower

“Anyway, before you leave for the Alchemy Tower, let me show you around our Array Tower,” said Wan Qing.

“Sure.”

Thus, Wan Qing began showing Yuan around the tower. The Array Tower was much less restrictive than the other towers, so they were able to visit almost every floor.

Like the other towers, the array masters in the Array Tower have different roles. Some focus on creating new formations, while others improve existing ones. Wan Qing, specifically, specialized in improving formations.

“How long have you been studying arrays?” Wan Qing asked as they walked around.

“Not long. I started learning the basics a few years ago, and I am currently learning from a Level 7 Array Master.”

“A level 7 Array Master, huh? I wonder when I’ll reach that level. I was just promoted to level 6, so it’ll probably take some time.”

After the tour, Wan Qing led Yuan back to the first floor and said, “Well, good luck at the Alchemy Tower. They have the strictest requirements out of the five Breakthrough Towers—at least according to rumors.”

“Thank you,” Yuan nodded.

After leaving the Array Tower, Yuan made his way to the Alchemy Tower.

‘I have visited four Breakthrough Towers, but none of them gave me any bad vibes… No… I have only been to the first floor of the Metal Tower, so I cannot really judge them with my current knowledge.’

Moreover, he has yet to investigate the towers properly, including the three he’d already joined.

Sometime later, Yuan arrived in the city housing the Alchemy Tower. The moment he stepped out of the teleportation formation, a potent aroma of medicinal herbs and concoctions filled the air, immediately assaulting his senses.

“Tier 5 pills! We have Tier 5 pills for sale!”

“Come get your Burning Lotus Pills here! Only available in this store!”

As Yuan made his way toward the Alchemy Tower, he noticed that nearly every street he passed featured at least one medicine store.

Yuan ignored the bustling noise and shouting around him as he followed the path leading directly to the Alchemy Tower. However, when he was almost at his destination, he suddenly came to a halt when he felt a weak tug on his robes. S~eaʀᴄh the Nôvel(F)ire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Turning around, he saw a young boy with a homeless appearance clutching the lower part of his robes, his small, dirty hand holding on tightly as he looked up at Yuan.

“What’s the matter?”

“Big brother, have you seen my mother?”

“No, I haven’t.”

“Are you sure? She left a week ago, heading toward the place you seem to be going right now,” he said.

“I’ve only just arrived at this city today, so there’s no way I could’ve seen her, who left a week ago.”

“I see… Then she must be inside that tower. Are you heading to the tower now?”

Yuan nodded.

“In that case, can you tell her to return to me if you see her? I miss her.”

“What does she look like?”

The little boy gave him a brief description.

“Alright. I’ll let your mother know if I see her inside the tower.”

“Thank you!”

The little boy turned around and ran away.

“…”

Yuan watched the little boy disappear into the alley, a strange feeling settling in his stomach. By now, he was too familiar with the workings of the Stairway to Heaven to not realize that the boy had nothing to do with the trial. However, since he lacked information, he decided to ignore it for now and continued making his way to the Alchemy Tower.

When Yuan reached the entrance of the Alchemy Tower and took in the scene before him, he was immediately reminded of the Metal Tower. Much like its counterpart, the first floor appeared to function as a bustling marketplace, with stalls and shops dedicated to selling various medical products, from herbs and pills to rare ingredients.

There was also a sign outside the entrance that read, “Pill Grandmasters must qualify to join the Alchemy Tower. Alchemy Emperors and above may join the Alchemy Tower without needing any assessment.”

“Pill Grandmasters are Tier 5 Alchemists, and Alchemy Emperors are Tier 6…” Yuan muttered to himself.

He had only managed to concoct a tier 1 pill before, and the chances of him becoming a Tier 5 Alchemist before the reward time limit were slim to none.

‘I definitely won’t have the time to become a Tier 5 Alchemist and investigate the towers. Since I have no choice, let’s just sneak around the Alchemy Tower.’

With that in mind, Yuan entered the Alchemy Tower to begin his snooping around.

‘In order to even visit the other floors, I will need a badge from someone who works in the Alchemy Tower…’

There was a minor teleportation formation that connected every floor in the tower, and only those with a badge could access the formation. However, being a Level 5 Array Master, Yuan had a feeling he could crack the formation.

After activating Shadow Veil, Yuan’s body became shrouded in a dark, ethereal veil, completely erasing his presence from the senses of those around him. Cloaked in this shadowy aura, he moved silently toward the teleportation formation on the other side of the building.

Despite walking past thousands of people, not a single individual seemed aware of his presence. Even those who looked directly at him gazed past him as if he were invisible, completely oblivious to his existence.

The area around the teleport formation was empty since it could only be accessed by the Alchemists of the Alchemy Tower. Seeing this, Yuan took his time to analyze the formation.

‘Thankfully, this formation is only level 5, even if it’s slightly more complex than usual.’

After spending many minutes analyzing the formation, Yuan managed to crack it, gaining access to it without needing a badge.

Yuan thought about going straight to the peak of the tower but decided to search from floor to floor, starting from the second floor.

Once he was prepared, he stepped into the teleport formation.

chapters.title: Chapter 1781 Alchemy Tower(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1781 Alchemy Tower(2)

Upon entering the second floor, Yuan found himself in a vast research room with hundreds of Alchemists bustling about with scrolls and medicine in their grasp.

Yuan, still in his Shadow Veil, walked around like he owned the place. Due to how busy and focused everyone there was, Yuan was able to investigate the whole floor without any issues. Hell, he could even do jumping jacks in front of someone and they still wouldn’t notice him.

It was almost as if Shadow Veil’s effect was similar to the Celestial Emperor’s curse, where it naturally made everyone oblivious to Yuan’s existence.

This level of concealment was heaven-defying, and if the world knew of its existence, there would be wars to acquire it.

Through his observations on the second floor, Yuan quickly realized that everyone there was engaged in the process of creating new pill recipes. He also noticed that the recipes being developed were relatively basic, ranging from Tier 1 to Tier 2.

The next floor was the same as the second floor, but the pills being created were much more complex, ranging from Tier 3 to Tier 4.

This went on for the next several floors until he reached the 11th floor, where there were many rooms with closed doors. Yuan used his divine sense to peek inside these rooms to find Alchemists at work inside.

Not wanting to risk detection, Yuan took only quick glances into each room as he moved steadily from floor to floor.

The moment Yuan stepped onto the 30th floor, the atmosphere shifted drastically. The pristine environment of the lower levels was replaced by a grim, unsettling corridor lined with prison cells. The air felt heavy, and the dim lighting cast eerie shadows across the cold, grimy walls, starkly contrasting the clean and orderly nature of the floors below.

As Yuan peered into the prison cells, he saw live magical beasts confined within them. Considering the crucial role magical beast materials played in alchemy, it wasn’t surprising for the Alchemy Tower to house such a facility.

From their skin to their nails, almost every part of a magical beast could be utilized in alchemy. Their blood, bones, fur, organs, and especially their monster core were valuable for crafting pills, elixirs, and other alchemical creations. Because of this, Yuan didn’t find the magical beast prison to be anything out of the ordinary. They were simply no different from livestock.

As Yuan continued climbing the tower, the magical beasts grew increasingly rare and powerful. Unfortunately for them, every prison cell was enforced with Level 7 formations. They were so powerful that even Yuan would have difficulty dealing with them.

A pungent stench of blood suddenly assaulted Yuan’s nose when he reached the 45th floor, causing him to frown. ‘This isn’t the scent of magical beasts…’ Yuan thought to himself as he used his divine sense to check the prison cells.

Sure enough, imprisoned on the 45th floor were not magical beasts but humans.

‘Using humans as resources, huh? Why am I not surprised?’ Yuan shook his head after seeing the grim situation before him.

The concept of using humans as resources for alchemy dates back to the Primordial Era, long before the emergence of demons in the world. Back then, human blood and other components were studied for their potential alchemical properties.

However, this practice was deemed highly immoral and unethical, leading to its swift prohibition. Over time, it became a forbidden art, with strict penalties for anyone who dared to pursue it, most cases being the death penalty. Despite this, whispers of its use occasionally surfaced in dark corners of the world, often associated with the most desperate or depraved Alchemists.

As Yuan continued walking down the grim corridor, his attention was drawn to one of the cells. Inside, a woman lay on the floor, seemingly asleep. Her appearance caught his eye, as her features closely matched the description the homeless little boy had given him earlier.

However, before he could even consider rescuing her, Yuan noticed another presence appearing on his floor, forcing him to hide.

Although he needed to hide, there were no suitable places to do so in the narrow corridor. Left with no other choice, he relied entirely on his Shadow Veil and remained perfectly still. Fortunately for him, the darkness of the area enhanced the effectiveness of his skill, allowing him to blend seamlessly into the shadows and remain undetected.

The other presence belonged to a middle-aged man with a hunched back. However, he didn’t appear to be affiliated with the Alchemy Tower, as he wasn’t wearing their distinctive uniform, which all the other individuals Yuan had encountered so far had worn. Moreover, his cultivation was pathetically low, being only a Spirit Warrior. Sёarᴄh the NovelFire.net* website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Yuan observed silently as the hunched man unlocked the prison cell containing the little boy’s mother and dragged her out. Then, Yuan followed closely behind the man as he made his way through the corridor and toward the teleport formation.

Despite Yuan trailing directly behind him, the man remained completely unaware of his presence.

The man was so oblivious to Yuan’s presence that Yuan managed to step into the teleportation formation alongside him, completely undetected. Moments later, they emerged on the 60th floor—the tallest floor in the Alchemy Tower.

As the hunched man approached the only door on the 60th floor and prepared to knock, Yuan quickly concealed himself behind one of the large pillars.

Moments later, the door opened, revealing an old man dressed in the distinctive uniform of the Alchemy Tower. The old man glanced at the hunched man and spoke in a commanding tone. “Bring the material inside.”

“Yes, Master.”

While the two men had their backs turned, Yuan seized the opportunity. Moving like a shadow, he slipped through the open doors and entered the room undetected. Once inside, he quickly found a dark corner to hide in, ensuring he remained out of sight as he observed the scene unfolding before him.

The first thing that caught Yuan’s attention inside the room was a large crimson cauldron filled nearly to the brim with blood. The pungent, metallic scent of human blood was so overpowering that Yuan had noticed it even before the door was opened.

However, the most horrifying sight in the room was the corpses suspended above the crimson cauldron. Their lifeless bodies dangled ominously, blood dripping steadily into the cauldron below, resembling the process of drying out slaughtered chickens.

chapters.title: Chapter 1782 Alchemy Tower(3)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1782 Alchemy Tower(3)

The gruesome sight was enough to make Yuan’s blood boil with anger, his fists clenching tightly.

However, he forced himself to remain composed, swallowing his rage as he continued to silently observe the two men, determined to uncover the full extent of their sinister actions.

Tan Songyun was covering her mouth from shock and disguise while watching this from the spectators’ room.

“I remember now… That incident at the Alchemy Tower… So this was the real cause of the Tower Master’s death?” Tan Songyun remembered that the Alchemy Tower’s Tower Master had been assassinated once before, but not because of any accusations of being an evil cultivator. Instead, the rumors claimed he was killed over a precious pill recipe he had recently created, and naturally, Yuan—Tian Kai was blamed for the assassination.

“Please spare me… I have a son… He won’t survive alone…” The woman suddenly muttered in a weak voice, “I swear I won’t tell anyone about this…”

“Hmph. Someone as worthless as you should feel honored to finally have some value being part of Master’s pill as its material,” the hunched-back man sneered, his tone dripping with contempt as he dragged the woman closer to the crimson cauldron.

‘A Spirit Warrior and… a third-level Divine Emperor, huh?’ Searᴄh the NôᴠelFirё.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

After assessing the Tower Master’s cultivation, Yuan was confident he could deal with them without too much effort.

“Master, how should I bleed this one out?” the hunched-back man asked.

The Tower Master replied, “Six-inch long cuts on the neck, wrists, and tendons.”

“As you wish, Master.”

The hunch-back man eagerly retrieved his ceremonial dagger and prepared to cut the woman.

Seeing this, Yuan decided to intervene.

He walked out of his hiding spot and spoke in a cold voice, “To practice human concoction during this day and age… how are you not ashamed of yourselves?”

“WHO’S THERE?!”

The Tower Master’s heart skipped a beat upon hearing Yuan’s unexpected voice echo through the room. Panic flashed across his face as he began looking around frantically, his eyes scanning every corner of the room. However, despite his efforts, he was unable to see or sense Yuan’s presence.

‘Shadow Veil is more effective than I thought…’ Yuan was inwardly surprised by the results. He was essentially standing right before them, but they still couldn’t see him.

Wanting to test Shadow Veil’s limits, Yuan decided to keep it active, and he continued speaking, “So, what kind of pill was enticing enough to turn you into an evil practitioner?” Yuan’s calm yet disgusted voice filled the room, cutting through the tension like a blade. “Judging by the amount of blood and your methods, it must be the Reserve Heavenly Pill, isn’t it?”

“You know about the Reverse Heavenly Pill?!” the Tower Master shouted, his voice sharp with alarm as he scanned the room more frantically than before. “Who are you?! Show yourself!”

“Honestly, I subconsciously muttered that name out loud,” Yuan admitted calmly.

As he observed the gruesome scene, fragments of Tian Kai’s memories surfaced in his mind, and with them, the name of the pill.

“Ah… I remember now… Master Tower Chang Heng,” Yuan said, his tone carrying a faint trace of nostalgia. “You were a renowned Tier 7 Alchemist, celebrated for your profound knowledge and vast experience in alchemy. You even created multiple powerful and groundbreaking recipes that helped you become the Alchemy Tower’s Tower Master.”

“Everything seemed fine for the first several centuries, but then the number of people disappearing in this city began to rise at an unnatural rate. It was alarming enough to attract my attention, so I had to investigate the cause.”

“After a thorough investigation, I uncovered the gruesome truth,” Yuan continued, his tone growing colder. “The Alchemy Tower was kidnapping homeless people and using them for experiments… and to concoct pills.”

“The once righteous Alchemist has somehow been corrupted and become an evil practitioner.”

“And for your crimes, I executed every single Alchemist who had even the slightest involvement. It caused quite the stir within the Nine Heavens back then, but in the grand scheme of things, it was merely a small ripple in a much larger wave.”

“I’ve finally found you, you sneaky little bastard!” the Tower Master suddenly interrupted, his voice filled with fury.

Without hesitation, he unleashed a powerful attack he had been secretly preparing this entire time, sending a wave of destructive energy hurtling toward Yuan’s direction.

However…

“Sorry, but I am actually over here.”

Yuan’s voice resounded behind the Tower Master.

“Wha—?”!”

Stab!

Just as the Tower Master turned his head around, Yuan stabbed him in the heart from the back with the Baneful Abyss, inflicting him with Mark of the Baneful.

The poison, having entered the Tower Master’s heart directly, worked swiftly and mercilessly. With no time to counteract or expel it, his body succumbed almost instantly. Within moments, the Tower Master collapsed, his life extinguished.

After dealing with the Tower Master, Yuan turned his gaze toward the hunched-back man. Without a word, he raised a finger and unleashed a concentrated burst of Sword Aura. The attack was swift and precise, striking the hunched-back man between the eyes before he could even react, killing him instantly.

“…”

Moments passed in complete silence, the oppressive air lingering in the room as if time had frozen in place. Yet, Yuan remained inside the Alchemy Tower.

After another moment of stillness, Yuan sighed out loud, “I knew it. Although the Alchemy Tower is guilty of practicing forbidden alchemy, this trial hasn’t ended yet. There are still others who need to be brought to justice.”

He looked at the Tower Master’s melting corpse.

“I should’ve probably asked him if he was working with someone before I killed him… Oh well. It’s not like I have no clue.”

Yuan turned his gaze toward the woman, who had fainted from exhaustion and fear. He approached her silently, gently lifting her into his arms. After activating Shadow Veil again, Yuan was pleasantly surprised to see that its effects also extended to the woman, who was in physical contact with him. However, it consumed his spiritual energy twice as fast.

Sometime later, Yuan slipped out of the Alchemy Tower as silently as he had entered. Thanks to Shadow Veil, he moved unnoticed, leaving no sign that he had ever been there.

chapters.title: Chapter 1783 Tower Master'S Visit
chapters.body:
Chapter 1783 Tower Master'S Visit

After leaving the Alchemy Tower, Yuan brought the woman back to the homeless boy, who was sitting by the street.

“M-mother!”

The boy’s dispirited expression immediately brightened upon seeing his mother. He stood up and rushed over to Yuan.

“Why is she sleeping? Is she okay?” he asked.

“Yes, she’ll be fine. She fell asleep from exhaustion, so she’ll wake up after resting a little.”

Just as he finished saying that, the woman opened her eyes.

“Where am I…?”

“Mother!!!”

“Ah! Son! Am I in heaven?!”

“Of course not!”

Yuan smiled and said, “Don’t worry, you’re alive.”

“Huh? How…? I swear I was inside the Alchemy Tower… And who are you?”

“I don’t know what happened to you, but I found you outside the Alchemy Tower,” said Yuan.

“Outside the tower…? Was that all a dream?” she muttered in a dazed voice.

“Thank you, big brother. I knew I could count on you,” said the little boy.

“No problem. Here, take this.” Yuan handed a few spirit stones to the boy despite knowing that it was a pointless gesture, as they were simply created by the Stairway to Heaven.

“I will never forget you! Let’s go, mother.”

The boy and his mother bowed to him before turning around and walking away.

“So this is how it would’ve felt if I actually saved her in time before…?” Yuan muttered with a regretful expression on his face.

In Tian Kai’s life, he’d investigated the Alchemy Tower a little too late, and that woman had already been turned into a pill. As for the homeless boy, he was heartbroken to lose his mother, and he disappeared somewhere after Tian Kai gave him some money, never to be heard from again. Searᴄh the Nôvel(F)ire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Meanwhile, on the top floor of the Array Tower, Liao Tian was conversing with the Tower Master.

“This is a rare occasion, Tower Master Liao. What brought you to my Array Tower?”

“I am looking for someone who recently joined your tower,” he said.

“I see. You’re looking for someone. But why are you looking for them?”

“He has something that belongs to me,” said Liao Tian.

The Array Tower’s Tower Master raised his eyebrows and said, “He stole something from you?”

“Well, something like that.”

The Array Tower’s Tower Master proceeded to look at the information inside his badge.

“Indeed, we had a recent join, and he was recruited by Array Master Wan Qing.”

The Array Tower’s Tower Master summoned Wan Qing to the top floor a moment later.

Once she arrived, she greeted them, “Tower Master Gao, you summoned for me?”

“Tower Master Liao here has a few questions about the Array Master who recently joined our tower,” said Tower Master Gao.

“Array Master… Yuan?”

“When you were with that person, did you see him wielding a large sword?” Liao Tian asked.

“No, I did not.”

“Is that so? Then where is he right now? I need to speak with him as soon as possible.”

“Actually, he left the tower and went to the Alchemy Tower almost as soon as he joined us,” she revealed, dumbfounding both Tower Masters.

“The Alchemy Tower? What does he intend on doing there?” Liao Tian asked.

“He’s probably going to join them too since he also has the Martial Tower and Cultivation Tower’s badges.”

“One individual joining four towers? But why would a thief even join the towers in the first place?” Tower Master Gao inquired as he looked at Liao Tian with a doubtful gaze.

“It’s complicated,” Liao Tian shrugged, refusing to elaborate further.

Seeing this, Tower Master Gao didn’t pursue further. It was none of his business, anyway.

“Since he’s at the Alchemy Tower, I’ll go ahead and take my leave now,” said Liao Tian as he suddenly stood up.

“He should be returning soon, though,” said Wan Qing.

“It’s fine. I have some business with Alchemy Tower’s Tower Master, anyway.”

Soon, Liao Tian left the Array Tower and made his way to the Alchemy Tower.

When Liao Tian arrived at the Alchemy Tower, he was warmly welcomed by the people there, who were still completely oblivious to their Tower Master’s death that had recently occurred.

“I’m here to speak with Tower Master Xu. Is he available?”

“The Tower Master is currently in seclusion. Perhaps I can be of help,” said one of the higher-ranking Alchemists there.

Liao Tian nodded and explained the situation, “I am currently looking for a certain individual. I was told he was coming to the Alchemy Tower to join you guys.”

“A new recruit?” The Alchemist looked at the log and said, “But we haven’t had any new recruits for over ten years.”

“Then he must’ve failed to join the Alchemy Tower. Did anyone named Yuan try to join recently?”

“No, we haven’t had anyone trying to join for the past month.”

‘Perhaps he hasn’t arrived yet? There’s no way that woman from the Array Tower would lie about something like this,’ Liao Tian thought to himself.

“I understand. Then I will go speak with the Tower Master in the meantime.”

“But he’s…”

“I know, but so what? Is this the first time I visited unannounced while he’s in seclusion?” Liao Tian sneered as he turned and made his way to the teleportation formation.

The Alchemist could only stand there and watch Liao Tian do as he pleased. After all, Liao Tian was a Tower Master and a good friend of their tower’s Tower Master.

Moreover, the Alchemy Tower’s Tower Master was a very understanding individual, and he wouldn’t blame them for Liao Tian’s forceful behavior.

Sometime later, Liao Tian arrived at the top of the tower.

When he stepped out of the formation, Liao Tian was assaulted by a pungent stench of blood. However, he barely reacted and continued to approach the closed doors, almost as if he was used to the scent.

He knocked on the door and shouted, “Hey, old man, it’s me. Open up.”

“…”

Silence.

Liao Tian didn’t find it weird despite the lack of response. He simply knocked on the door again a few minutes later.
