chapters.title: Chapter 1784 Chaos At The Towers
chapters.body:
Chapter 1784 Chaos At The Towers

“Open up before I take some of your materials for myself!” Liao Tian shouted again after waiting a few minutes.

Yet, there was still no response.

“Odd. He’d always fall for that threat,” he muttered with a raised eyebrow.

By now, Liao Tian began to sense that something might be amiss.

He knocked again, this time with more force.

“Open the damn door before I bust it open myself!” he roared.

No response.

Liao Tian had a strong urge to destroy the door and force himself inside to see the situation, but there was one minor problem. The door was being protected by a level 7 defensive formation, and he did not have the capability to remove it.

“Fuck…”

With no other option, Liao Tian rushed back to the Array Tower to request Tower Master Gao’s assistance.

“What? Are you out of your mind? You want me to forcefully open Tower Master Ma’s doors while he’s in seclusion—the one with a level 7 formation? Do you have any idea how much effort that will take?” Tower Master Gao exclaimed after hearing Liao Tian’s request.

“I know it sounds bad, but I think something bad happened to Tower Master Ma.”

“What could possibly happen to him while he’s inside his room? You also said he was in seclusion, so there’s a good chance he just couldn’t hear you knock. Or what? Are you saying that he, a Tier 7 Pill Ancestor, got into an accident while doing alchemy?”

“Stop asking so many damn questions and just come with me. Something bad happened. I can feel it,” said Liao Tian, clearly getting impatient.

Tower Master Gao frowned, his expression stern as he said, “That’s not the right attitude when asking for help. I may not know what you and Tower Master Ma have been working on behind the scenes, but I don’t like it.”

“Whatever. Let’s go.”

“Wait. You think I am going to remove that level 7 formation alone?”

“Didn’t you create it?”

“I did—with the help of several other level 7 Array Masters. Although I can still remove it myself, it’ll take a long time.”

“How long?”

“A few weeks.”

“We don’t have that much time.”

“Then you’ll have to wait for me to gather a few helpers.”

“I will wait for you at the Alchemy Tower,” said Liao Tian. “Come as soon as possible.”

While Liao Tian returned to the Alchemy Tower and continued knocking on the door, Tower Master Gao went to gather several high-level Array Masters.

Several hours later, Tower Master Gao visited the Alchemy Tower with these Array Masters. “How long will it take now?” Liao Tian asked.

“A few hours at least. But heavens, what is this stench? It smells like blood,” Tower Master Gao exclaimed, his face contorting in shock as the pungent metallic odor filled his senses.

“I wanted you to hurry for a reason,” Liao Tian shrugged.

“Fuck. Let’s hurry up and get to work!” Tower Master Gao and the other Array Masters began working on the formation.

Meanwhile, Yuan saw Liao Tian, Tower Master Gao, and the Array Masters enter the Alchemy Tower in haste.

After leaving the boy and his mother, Yuan actually returned to the Alchemy Tower and hid in plain sight.

‘Good thing I lingered for a little bit longer. Eight Array Masters and one blacksmith, huh? Judging by their clothes and badges, one was the Array Tower’s Tower Master, and the blacksmith was the Metal Tower’s Tower Master. They must be trying to force their way inside the room.’

‘Now that they’re outside their home and distracted by the situation at Alchemy Tower, it’s a good time to check out their towers.’

With that in mind, Yuan swiftly left the Alchemy Tower and made his way to the Array Tower.

Using Shadow Veil, Yuan traveled to the peak of the tower. However, the door was also sealed by a level 7 formation.

Since he was just promoted to level 5 as an Array Master, there was simply no way for him to unlock the level 7 formation. Fortunately, he was not out of options and had other means of opening the door.

Yuan retrieved Number One Under Heaven and activated True Dragon Awakening without hesitation.

With his full strength, he unleashed God of War’s Final Execution, directing a relentless barrage of strikes against the heavily fortified door, relying purely on brute force to force his way through. Strike after strike echoed in the air, the sheer intensity of the attacks shaking the surroundings and the tower as a whole, bewildering everyone within it.

Finally, as Yuan poured the last of his spiritual energy into one final, decisive blow, the formation protecting the door shattered under the overwhelming force.

Yuan entered the room and quickly investigated the place. After finding no hints, Yuan left the place without a second thought.

Soon after he left, the Array Masters in the Array Tower scrambled to the top floor to investigate the commotion, and the area quickly became flooded with frantic activity.

Meanwhile, Yuan was already halfway to the Metal Tower.

“S-someone broke into the Tower Master’s floor! Contact the Tower Master! Quickly!” The Array Masters tried contacting Tower Master Gao, but there was no response, as he was too busy to even notice it.

“He’s not responding! What should we do?!” seaʀᴄh thё ηovёlFire .net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“The Tower Master went to the Alchemy Tower with Tower Master Liao, and he brought along several Array Masters with him!” someone suddenly said.

“I will go to the Alchemy Tower and meet with the Tower Master myself. In the meantime, try to figure out what had happened here and if anything had been stolen!”

Sometime later, at the Metal Tower, Yuan stood before its teleportation formation.

‘This formation is virtually identical to the one at the Alchemy Tower and other towers. I guess they were all created by the Array Tower. This is great news since it saves me time trying to crack it.’

After spending just a few minutes, Yuan activated the formation and teleported himself to the top of the Metal Tower.

chapters.title: Chapter 1785 Human Remains As Materials
chapters.body:
Chapter 1785 Human Remains As Materials

Upon reaching the top floor of the Metal Tower, Yuan was met with yet another door—a heavily fortified door protected by a Level 7 formation. However, unlike the one at Array Tower, this door wasn’t only guarded by a formation; its very structure was crafted from rare and precious materials. “Looks like I will need to put in a little more effort if I want to break inside,” Yuan muttered.

He retrieved Number One Under Heaven the next moment.

However, just as he was about to bombard the door, it suddenly opened, and a young man walked out.

“Tower Master, are you back? I have a question—”

Yuan had deactivated Shadow Veil to focus on offense, so he was not concealed at this moment.

“Hm? Who are you?” The young man asked with raised eyebrows when he saw Yuan, but he quickly became distracted by the weapon in Yuan’s grasp.

“Heavens! What a masterpiece! Where in heaven’s name did you acquire it?!”

The young man approached Yuan with sparkling eyes, completely ignoring his unknown identity.

Sensing no ill intent from the young man, Yuan decided to play along and said, “I was invited here by your Tower Master. He wanted to take a look at my weapon.”

“No doubt about that! Any blacksmith would want to inspect such a beauty! Please, come inside!” The young man invited Yuan inside the room.

Yuan nodded and followed him inside.

Surprisingly, the top floor wasn’t a room but a massive smithy full of equipment and materials.

“By the way, where’s the Tower Master?” the young man suddenly asked, looking a little suspicious.

“He is still at the Alchemy Tower, but he said he’ll return as soon as possible.” Searᴄh the novёlF~ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Really? That isn’t like him at all. In fact, I’m surprised he didn’t ask for your sword on the spot.”

“I don’t know what happened at the Alchemy Tower, but it seemed very serious. Even the Array Tower’s Tower Master was there.”

“Then it must’ve been a really serious matter. Anyway, can I take a look at your sword? Of course, I just want to take a simple look. I don’t dare to touch it before the Tower Master. He’d turn me into a sword if found out.”

“Turning you into a sword? That’s quite a weird thing to say,” Yuan chuckled.

“I’m serious. It won’t be the first time he’s done it, either.”

Yuan raised an eyebrow and asked, “Are you sure you can say that out loud to a stranger like me? What if I get the wrong idea? Like mistaking him for an evil practitioner.”

The young man laughed out loud, “You must not be from around here. Practically everyone knows his obsession with the art of creation, so it’s no secret. While he is a little crazy in the head and very eccentric, he’s not evil.”

“What about using human parts for creation? Alchemists who use humans to concoct pills are all considered evil,” said Yuan.

“I know, but context is also important. For example, people donating their organs and people getting their organs forcefully harvested are two entirely different things, right? Well, most of our materials come from donations. While the process is still morally questionable, someone has to do it. Otherwise, we will never truly advance our crafts.” “Most of your materials? Then some of them were forcefully harvested?” Yuan asked.

The young man showed a stiff smile and said, “We have accidents at work.”

“I see…”

Yuan looked around the smithy and asked, “Mind if I take a look? You can look at this in the meantime.”

He placed Number One Under Heaven on the table.

The young man’s eyes lit up with a flicker of intense emotion, and his expression transformed, brimming with excitement that bordered on something almost resembling lust.

While the young man was completely engrossed in admiring his sword, Yuan took the opportunity to investigate the smithy.

As he looked around, the young man’s claims were confirmed—human organs were openly displayed, from bones to blood, scattered across workbenches and shelves. On some of the shelves that stored weapons, it was apparent that some were crafted using human materials just solely from their grotesque appearance. Yuan gazed at the weapons with disappointment etched across his face. Tian Qiyuan’s passion for creation had been immense, perhaps unparalleled in the world—a driving force that had propelled him to become the God of Creation. Yet, despite his obsession with crafting, he had never once considered using humans as materials for his treasures.

However, it wasn’t as though Yuan didn’t understand the Metal Tower. Sometimes, in the pursuit of innovation, experiments are deemed necessary, and certain moral boundaries are inevitably pushed.

‘Since most of the materials were donated, I guess I can’t really blame them. Of course, that is only if he’s telling me the truth,’ Yuan thought as he glanced at the young man.

Meanwhile, news of the Array Tower being broken into finally reached Tower Master Gao.

“What?! Someone broke into my floor?! What happened to the formation protecting it?!” Tower Master Gao had to temporarily stop working on the Alchemy Tower’s formation to inquire.

“It was forcefully destroyed.”

“You mean someone managed to destroy my level 7 formation with brute force? Why would such a monster want to break into my place? Was anything stolen?”

“We can’t say for certain yet, but at a glance, nothing appears to have been stolen,” the Array Master explained, his tone laced with confusion. “It’s almost as if the culprit broke in, looked around, and left without taking anything. Of course, the area near the door is a complete mess.”

Liao Tian had a bad feeling after hearing about this news, but he shrugged it off for now.

“I’ll return to the Array Tower once I deal with the situation here,” said Tower Master Gao.

They were halfway finished removing the formation, after all.

“Yes, Tower Master.”

“First the Alchemy Tower, now my Array Tower? What the hell is going on recently?” Tower Master Gao mumbled with a pondering expression on his face, but he didn’t linger too much on the topic and continued focusing on the formation.

chapters.title: Chapter 1786 Tower Master'S Assassination
chapters.body:
Chapter 1786 Tower Master'S Assassination

News of the break-in at the Array Tower quickly spread throughout the city, shocking everyone. Yet, it almost became nonexistent a few hours later when the incident at the Alchemy Tower began spreading a few hours later.

After spending several hours working on the level 7 formation, Tower Master Gao and his Array Masters finally deactivated it, allowing them to open the door.

However, when they finally managed to open the door and step inside, their expressions froze in shock at the gruesome scene before them. Moments later, the shock gave way to disgust as they began to grasp the horrifying reality of what had transpired in the room. The air grew heavy with a tense silence as they processed the grim sight.

A crimson cauldron, nearly overflowing with blood, stood at the center of the room. Above it, lifeless corpses hung grotesquely, their presence casting long, morbid shadows. The unmistakable signs of evil practices were laid bare for all to see, leaving no doubt about the sinister activities that had taken place there. The room reeked of death and malice, making it impossible for anyone present to deny the truth.

“Tower Master Liao, what is the meaning of this?” Tower Master Gao turned to look at Liao Tian with a grim expression on his face.

Liao Tian frowned and said, “What are you trying to say?”

“Are you telling me that you were not aware of this? Is this not why you urgently wanted us to access his room?” seaʀᴄh thё novёlF~ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Are you insane? How could I have possibly known about this? I came to you for help because I sensed something was amiss.” Liao Tian sneered, denying all allegations.

“Also, are you trying to say that I had killed him? Why would I bring so many witnesses with me if that was the case?”

“No, I don’t think you killed him. However, I think you knew of the Alchemy Tower’s forbidden practices. There’s even a good chance you were working together. After all, it’s public knowledge that you use human remains to craft treasures.”

“Spare me the allegations. While I work with human remains, they were all donations.”

“What of those you personally killed? You think I don’t know about that?”

“They all made mistakes deserving of death. It’s as simple as that,” Liao Tian said, not denying it.

“Anyway, why don’t we stop this pointless talk and figure out who killed Tower Master Ma?”

Tower Master Gao sneered, looked at his Array Masters, and said, “Go contact the other Tower Master and let them know of the situation here.”

“Yes, Tower Master.”

Soon, news of Tower Master Ma’s assassination spread like wildfire throughout the Alchemy Tower, leaving everyone in shock and disbelief. However, the revelation of the tower’s forbidden practices was deliberately kept under wraps for the time being. The matter was deemed too sensitive and required further investigation before being exposed, as its implications could have far-reaching consequences for the tower, which could affect not just the Seventh Heaven but the Nine Heavens as a whole.

“Although Tower Master Ma was stabbed in the heart, what truly caused his death was poison—an extremely deadly one that surpasses my knowledge,” revealed the alchemist who examined his body.

“Even if his guard was down, it shouldn’t be easy to kill Tower Master Ma. Yet, all evidence shows that he was killed effortlessly,” said Liao Tian.

“This seemingly flawless assassination… reminds me of a certain individual,” said Meng Xuan.

The entire floor suddenly turned dead silent.

“Y-you mean the Phantom Assassin? The Grim Reaper? Why would he kill—” Tower Master Gao halted his sentence midway.

There was no doubt that Tower Master Ma was killed for his crimes.

Realizing this, Tower Master Gao looked at Liao Tian and said, “You should probably watch your back.”

“You’re really begging for a beating, huh?” Liao Tian frowned.

“Enough, you two,” said Lord Zi. “What should we do about the Alchemy Tower?”

“What is there for us to do, anyway? We’re not responsible for the Alchemy Tower. Even if it were to collapse overnight, it’s none of our concern,” Liao Tian remarked with a casual shrug.

Tower Master Gao added, “I don’t want to admit it, but he’s right. We may be known as the Five Breakthrough Towers, but we’re unrelated. Moreover, I doubt the Alchemy Tower will collapse over this incident. They’ll just have to figure things out themselves.”

Knowing there was a strong possibility that the infamous Phantom Assassin was involved, nobody present showed any inclination to involve themselves in the Alchemy Tower’s affairs. The matter was far too dangerous and delicate to meddle with, and it was true that the towers were not obligated to take responsibility for one another, as they were all separate organizations.

“Since there’s nothing else for us to do here, I’ll return to my tower now,” Liao Tian said a moment later.

“Why were you looking for Tower Master Ma, anyway?” Lord Zi suddenly questioned.

“I came to the Alchemy Tower for a different reason.”

“You mean the individual who stole your treasure?” Tower Master Gao said.

“Someone stole your treasure?” Lord Zi and Meng Xuan raised their eyebrows.

Liao Tian looked at them and suddenly smiled, “That’s right. In fact, he joined the Martial Tower and Cultivation Tower recently.”

“Recently?”

The two of them frowned, as there was only one individual who joined both towers recently.

“You mean Yuan? You’re saying he stole your treasure? I don’t believe it,” Lord Zi was the first to doubt his claim.

“Believe what you want,” Liao Tian shrugged as he left.

“I have to return to my tower as soon as possible. Someone broke into my place,” said Tower Master Gao.

“Do you think it’s related to this incident?” Meng Xuan asked.

“I hope not.”

“What are you going to do? Tower Master Meng?” Lord Zi asked her once they were alone.

“I’ll stay here to assist the Alchemy Tower,” she said.

“I understand. Good luck.” Lord Zi left shortly after.

Sometime later, Liao Tian returned to the Metal Tower. However, just as he approached the entrance, he stopped abruptly, his senses picking up on a powerful presence emanating from the peak of the tower.

“This presence! There’s no doubt—it’s that treasure!” Liao Tian exclaimed, his face lighting up with excitement. Without a second thought about the peculiar circumstances, Liao Tian hurried upstairs, driven by his excitement and curiosity, like a moth drawn to the flames.

chapters.title: Chapter 1787 Fighting Liao Tian
chapters.body:
Chapter 1787 Fighting Liao Tian

“The treasure! Where is the treasure?!” Liao Tian bellowed as he flung open the large metal doors to his smithy with a resounding clang.

Inside, the young man was still fixated on Number One Under Heaven, his gaze locked onto the weapon as if bewitched. He had been inspecting it for hours, his expression a mixture of awe and obsession, utterly consumed by the masterpiece before him.

When Liao Tian’s eyes fell upon Number One Under Heaven, his expression mirrored the young man’s—filled with awe and fascination. He hurried over to the weapon, his hands trembling slightly as he hovered them above it, almost as if he yearned to touch it but didn’t dare to. The sheer presence of the treasure commanded reverence, leaving him entranced.

“This! This is it!” Liao Tian exclaimed, his voice trembling with excitement. “This is what I’ve been searching for my entire life—the perfect weapon! The materials, the craftsmanship—it’s beyond anything I’ve ever seen despite being a God of Creation at one point!”

Just as Liao Tian reached to touch the sword, it suddenly moved on its own, flying away.

“What the fu—” Liao Tian’s gaze followed the sword until it landed in Yuan’s grasp.

“Who are you?! How dare you touch my treasure?!” Liao Tian roared after seeing the treasure being touched by someone who wasn’t him.

“Your treasure? That’s funny, considering I was the one who brought it here,” Yuan responded with a smile.

“You… You must be Yuan.” Liao Tian narrowed his eyes.

“And you must be Liao Tian. What happened to that man you sent after me?”

“I killed him for failing to correct his mistake,” he said.

“Is that so?”

“At least he’ll be more useful as a weapon.”

Hearing this, Yuan shrugged, “I don’t understand your obsession with human weapons. Not only do they lack elegance, but they’re nothing special.”

“Who the hell are you to question my creations? You’re just a nobody who somehow acquired a masterpiece. I was once the God of Creation!”

Yuan laughed out loud, “The God of Creation? With your level of skills? I doubt it.” S~eaʀᴄh the nôvel_Fire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Liao Tian gritted his teeth and shouted, “Hand over the treasure! You do not even qualify to touch it!”

“If I, its master and creator, do not qualify to touch this weapon, then nobody in this world would qualify,” Yuan’s voice echoed calmly through the room as he revealed this information.

“You created that treasure? That’s a sick joke,” Liao Tian sneered, not believing him even for a split second.

“Even if it was a joke, it’s not as sick as your hobby and evil practices.”

“Evil? Just because I use unorthodox materials does not make me evil.”

“You think I am only talking about your methods? I was told that most of your human materials were donated, but after inspecting the materials myself, I confirmed that most of them are actually forcefully harvested.”

“That’s slander and a baseless accusation. Where is your evidence?”

“My evidence, huh?” Yuan said with a calm yet chilling tone. “Well, I’ve killed enough people to know the difference between someone who died a peaceful, natural death and someone who met a forceful, violent end.”

“Moreover, the weapons you created are all screaming in agony—though I doubt you can hear it.”

Liao Tian sneered, his expression filled with disdain. “What kind of evidence is that? None of it is substantial or verifiable.”

Yuan smiled and said, “You came from the Alchemy Tower, right? I’m sure you saw what happened to their Tower Master.”

Liao Tian instantly frowned.

“You… Don’t tell me you were responsible for Tower Master Ma’s death?”

“And what if I was? Are you going to avenge him?”

Liao Tian retrieved a large blade and brandished it with a cold expression. The weapon’s appearance was grotesque and unsettling—its surface was textured like raw flesh, clearly crafted from human remains. It also radiated a sinister aura that caused the temperature in the room to drop significantly.

“Why would I bother avenging him? I was going to kill you anyway, regardless of whether you killed Tower Master Ma or not.”

“Are you even capable of doing such a thing?” Yuan mocked.

“You’ll find out soon enough!”

Liao Tian swung his blade with a fierce motion, and in an instant, the weapon expanded dramatically in size and length.

Although slightly surprised by the blade’s sudden transformation, Yuan blocked the attack with relative ease. As soon as it was deflected, the grotesque weapon retracted, shrinking back to its original size. Its movements were unnervingly fluid, almost as if the blade itself were alive.

“Unlike ordinary weapons, weapons made from humans can grow stronger without needing refinement,” Liao Tian said with a sinister grin, “Not only that, they’re truly alive! I don’t know if you can hear it, but my weapon has a heartbeat!”

“If you’re so satisfied with your weapon, why covet mine?” Yuan asked.

“…”

“You think I want it because I want to replace my sword? No! I am going to feed it to my creation so it can become truly flawless!” Liao Tian laughed out loud.

Yuan shook his head, “I knew it was going to be stupid, but I didn’t think it would be this dumb.”

“Say whatever you want! That sword will be mine, anyway!”

Having had enough of the farce, Yuan decided it was time to end things. Activating True Dragon Awakening, his aura surged, filling the room with an overwhelming presence. The battle afterward was swift and decisive, and within just a few minutes, Yuan completely overpowered Liao Tian.

As Liao Tian lay on the ground, barely clinging to life, Yuan calmly approached the grotesque weapon.

Looking down at it with a mixture of pity and disdain, he said, “I’ll release you all now.”

“No!”

Liao Tian used what little strength he had left and uttered, but Yuan ignored him and destroyed the weapon.

As Yuan destroyed the weapon, a faint, eerie noise echoed from the weapon, resembling a distant, anguished scream. The blade shattered into countless fragments, scattering across the floor.

Liao Tian, witnessing the destruction of his prized creation, stared in disbelief. The shock was too much for his already weakened body, and with a final, shuddering breath, he succumbed to death.

chapters.title: Chapter 1788 Phantom Assassin
chapters.body:
Chapter 1788 Phantom Assassin

After killing Liao Tian, Yuan turned to look at the young man, who had been shivering in a corner since the beginning of their fight.

“W-who are you?” He asked Yuan in a fearful voice.

“I’m nobody. Sorry you had to witness that. Unfortunately, your Tower Master was corrupt, so I had to get rid of him.”

“…”

After a moment of silence, the young man asked an unexpected question, “Is it true? That you crafted that sword?”

“Yes.”

The young man stopped trembling and nodded, “If the creator of such a pure and beautiful masterpiece says so, who am I to question you? If you say the heavens are corrupt, then the heavens are corrupt.”

Yuan could only chuckle at the young man’s eccentric character.

“Then I’ll take my leave now.”

“What should I do now?” the young man asked.

“Whatever you want,” Yuan said as he activated Shadow Veil, disappearing from the Metal Tower.

The young man stood there in a daze. He silently stared at Liao Tian’s corpse for a moment before sighing and walking away. Soon, the top floor flooded with blacksmiths from the Metal Tower.

News of Liao Tian’s death quickly spread, shocking the world again.

“Tower Master Ma was assassinated recently, and now Tower Master Liao suffered the same fate? What in heaven’s name is going on?”

“More importantly, who is capable of killing two Tower Masters in such a short time? And why? They were both reputable and highly respected men.”

“I wonder what the other Tower Masters are thinking right now.”

“They are probably thinking if they’ll be next.”

Sure enough, after they learned of Liao Tian’s death, the remaining three Tower Masters gathered for a second time to discuss the situation.

“I had a feeling he’d be next, but why am I still shocked that it actually happened?” Tower Master Gao sighed out loud.

“According to the witness who saw the whole incident, the killer was a young man,” said Lord Zi.

“However, when questioned about the killer’s identity, the witness refused to explain further. It’s truly bizarre.”

“Why would the killer spare the witness, anyway? How would that benefit him?” Tower Master Gao asked.

“Maybe he doesn’t care about being caught,” Meng Xuan said, her tone laced with unease. “This is the Phantom Assassin, after all. He wants us to know he’s coming for us—to make us live in constant fear, cowering and dreading every second of our lives.”

Her words sent a chill through the room, causing Tower Master Gao and Lord Zi to tremble.

“Do you really think this is the work of the Phantom Assassin—that he’s really coming after our lives?” Lord Zi asked Meng Xuan, who nodded with a serious expression.

“Yes, I am almost certain it’s him.”

Lord Zi sighed, “Then there’s nothing we can do but enjoy what little time we have left.”

“What? You’re not even going to fight back?” Tower Master Gao frowned at such a response.

“Fight back? Do you even know who the Phantom Assassin is? Nobody has ever managed to escape once they enter his hit list. He’s killed true immortals before, much less weaklings like us.”

“I don’t care who he is! I have done nothing to deserve this! I will not lie down and let him take my life!” Tower Master Gao roared.

“If you have truly done nothing wrong, there’s nothing to worry about,” a fourth, unfamiliar voice suddenly echoed through the room.

The unexpected intrusion startled the three Tower Masters, their heads snapping in every direction to locate the source. None of them could sense any other presence besides their own, even now, which made the situation all the more terrifying. It made them feel vulnerable and exposed. “Who’s there?!” Lord Zi shouted.

After a few moments of tense silence, a shadowy figure began to materialize before them, emerging from the dimness as if he had been there all along.

Yuan deactivated Shadow Veil at that point, revealing his identity to them.

“Y-you’re! Yuan!” Lord Zi exclaimed.

“My successor?!” Meng Xuan followed.

“Who?” Tower Master Gao raised his eyebrows in a puzzled manner.

“Wait… Yuan? You mean the one who stole Tower Master Liao’s treasure?”

Yuan looked at him and smiled, “That’s what he told you? Well, it’s a lie.”

“What are you doing here, Yuan? How did you manage to get in here?” Meng Xuan asked the next moment.

“And what you said just now… You seem to know the situation…” Lord Zi added.

“Oh, I followed you guys here, so I have been here since the very beginning,” said Yuan. He continued, “I also know the situation. After all, I am the one responsible for the two Tower Masters’ deaths.”

“You what?!” They exclaimed simultaneously.

“T-there’s no way you could’ve killed those two… Stop joking and tell us the truth.” Meng Xuan said with a stiff smile.

“I am telling you the truth. I killed the Alchemy Tower’s Tower Master by stabbing him in the heart from the back, and Tower Master Liao died after I destroyed his treasure.”

The room fell into a heavy silence as Yuan casually revealed details about the deaths of the Tower Masters—details that had not been made public.

“Why did you kill them?” Tower Master Gao suddenly asked.

“Are you asking me that because you really don’t know, or are you trying to confirm something? You should’ve seen the scene yourself. They committed crimes against humanity, so I punished them.”

“Even if what they did was wrong… who are you to punish them? Are you trying to be a hero or something?”

“A hero? No, I killed them for selfish reasons. I didn’t like what they were doing, so I killed them. Or what? Are you telling me that I shouldn’t have punished them?”

“That’s…” Tower Master Gao became speechless. S~eaʀᴄh the Nôvelƒire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Yuan, you haven’t answered one of my questions. Why are you here? Are you going to kill us, too?” Meng Xuan asked a moment later.

“Who knows?” Yuan shrugged.

chapters.title: Chapter 1789 Exposing The Third
chapters.body:
Chapter 1789 Exposing The Third

“Who knows…? This isn’t the time for snarky remarks, Yuan. You’ve already killed two Tower Masters,” Meng Xuan frowned.

“Yes, and unfortunately, there will be at least another death.”

Tower Mater Gao suddenly realized something and uttered, “Wait… Does this mean you suspect one of us is an evil practitioner like Tower Master Ma and Tower Master Liao?”

Yuan shook his head and said, “There’s no need for suspicions because I am already certain that is the case, and there could be more than one of you.”

“That’s outrageous! Do you have evidence?!” Lord Zi exclaimed.

“Evidence, huh? I don’t have any. However, I am suspicious of the Array Tower.”

“What?” Tower Master Gao’s eyes widened at his words. “Wait a second! You must be the one who broke into my place!”

Yuan nodded, “I needed to make sure you were not involved with Alchemy Tower. After all, you assisted them with all sorts of formations, including the ones used to imprison humans.” Searᴄh the Novёlƒire.n(e)t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Naturally, the Array Tower provided its expertise not just to the Alchemy Tower but to the other towers as well. Moreover, the formations in question had been established long before any of the current Tower Masters came into existence.

However, as formations required periodic maintenance and repairs, there was a possibility that the Array Tower had uncovered or at least become aware of the Alchemy Tower’s misdeeds during these interventions.

“I know it looks bad on the surface, but I really had no idea about the Alchemy Tower’s practices until recently! I mean, I knew they were doing something behind the scenes, but I never imagined it was something so evil!”

“As for the formations used to imprison humans—we thought they were using it for magical beasts! At least that’s what we intended them to do! They always empty the cells when we show up to do maintenance on them, too!”

Yuan stared at Tower Master Gao as he explained himself but didn’t say anything. This made Tower Master Gao extremely anxious. Unbeknownst to the Tower Masters, Yuan had long ceased suspecting the Array Tower. After breaking into Tower Master Gao’s residence, he had found no evidence tying them to any wrongdoing. Everything he said up until now was just an act solely to lower the others’ guards and observe their reactions. After all, he hadn’t investigated the Martial Tower and Cultivation Tower. However, even without investigating these two towers, Yuan already knew which of the two he needed to kill.

After a long moment of silence, Yuan smiled at Tower Master Gao and said, “I trust you.”

“R-really? You’re not pulling my leg?” Tower Master Gao swallowed nervously.

Despite being a Tower Master and Divine Sovereign, he felt powerless before Yuan, who was only a mere Spirit Sovereign.

Yuan nodded before turning to look at Lord Zi and Meng Xuan.

“Although I haven’t investigated your towers properly, I doubt there will be any evidence. Fortunately for me, I already have the evidence I need.”

Yuan retrieved Number One Under Heaven and pointed it at one of them.

“I didn’t notice it at first because you hid it really well, but that’s no longer the case, and I can sense it as clear as day. Did killing those two really rile you this much? Tower Master Meng?”

“What…?”

Tower Master Gao and Lord Zi followed the direction of Yuan’s blade, which was pointed straight at Meng Xuan.

“Y-you think I am an evil practitioner? You cannot be serious!” Meng Xuan stammered, her voice unsteady as she visibly trembled.

“Evil practitioner? No, I think you’re worse than that, you Demon Worshipper!” Yuan said, his voice filled with disgust.

“What?! Demon Worshipper?!” Both Tower Master Gao and Lord Zi nearly jumped from shock.

In the world of cultivation, two paths were universally condemned and labeled as evil by society. The first was the path of evil practitioners, individuals who defied human morals and engaged in heinous acts such as forbidden alchemy or cruel experiments. The second, even more despised, was the path of demon worshippers, those who betrayed humanity by aligning themselves with demons, forsaking their own kind for power.

“It’s subtle, but I can sense the power of demons within your Dantian. You hid it well, but your emotions betrayed you. Now the question is—how are you related to the Alchemy Tower and Metal Tower? Since those two weren’t demon worshippers but evil practitioners, I have a feeling you were the one who enticed them. That’s what demon worshippers are good at, after all.”

Meng Xuan clenched her fists and trembled in anger, “I have had enough of your farce, Yuan! Tower Master Gao! Tower Master Zi! Are you really going to believe him?! Why would I need the power of demons when I am already the Tower Master of the Cultivation Tower?! That’s outrageous!”

Tower Master Gao and Lord Zi exchanged glances with each other. Neither of them wanted to say anything, but they were both aware of Meng Xuan’s obsession with cultivation and immortality.

They had known Meng Xuan long before she ascended to the position of Tower Master. Initially, she lacked the qualifications and talent. However, her cultivation suddenly soared at an unprecedented rate, almost as if her latent talents had awakened overnight.

This sudden and meteoric rise had surprised everyone, but no one questioned it, chalking it up to a rare stroke of fortune or an undiscovered potential finally blooming. Now, under Yuan’s scrutiny, that sudden rise seemed far more suspicious.

“When it comes to enticing humans, demons typically promise unmatched power and immortality. While it’s true that they can boost your cultivation and extend your longevity, there is always a drawback—a flaw.”

Yuan suddenly pointed two fingers at Meng Xuan and muttered, “Reveal your true self, hateful demon.”

A golden light suddenly appeared from Yuan’s fingers, shooting toward Meng Xuan with blinding speed. It pierced through her chest and entered her heart, but it did not harm her. Instead, it roused the demonic energy hidden within her Dantian.

Meng Xuan’s body instantly began to heat up, an uncontrollable sensation spreading through her entire being.

Panic flashed across her face as she roared, “W-what did you do to me?!”

Yuan, remaining calm, responded in a nonchalant tone, “Just speeding up the inevitability.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1790 Spirit Enlightenment
chapters.body:
Chapter 1790 Spirit Enlightenment

After being struck by Yuan’s golden light, Meng Xuan felt her blood begin to boil, an intense heat coursing through her veins. Her muscles twitched and squirmed uncontrollably, as though they were trying to break free from her skin.

“That power you accepted from the demon may have increased your cultivation and longevity, but it comes with a price. It will strip away your humanity. While you won’t fully become a demon, you’ll turn into something in-between—something neither human nor demon.”

As Yuan spoke, Meng Xuan’s body began to contort unnaturally, her form twisting and shifting. Her white skin darkened in patches, and her beautiful features took on a grotesque, unrecognizable appearance.

Regret flickered in Meng Xuan’s eyes, a fleeting moment of clarity amidst the chaos consuming her. However, it didn’t last long. As her body continued to transform, her emotions and sanity were stripped away, leaving behind a hollow shell of what she once was. Her gaze turned vacant, and a primal, unrecognizable presence replaced her former self.

Soon, her eyes became filled with rage and bloodlust.

“What a pity.”

Not waiting for Meng Xuan to go on a rampage, Yuan activated True Dragon Awakening and immediately began attacking her with Demon Sealing Aura.

Several strikes later, Meng Xuan was completely sealed, turning into a stone statue.

“I can’t believe it… To think Tower Master Meng had been a demon worshipper this entire time,” Tower Master Gao muttered, his eyes wide with disbelief.

Sometime later, Yuan finished off the job.

After killing Meng Xuan, she unexpectedly dropped a demon core. Normally, this wouldn’t be possible since only full-fledged demons have demon cores, but this was a trial created by the Stairway to Heaven.

Yuan picked up the demon core, its dark and ominous energy radiating in his hand. He stared at it for a moment, his expression contemplative. Then, without hesitation, he tossed it into his mouth and consumed it. Sёarᴄh the NôvelFire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

<You have reached Second Level Spirit Sovereign>

<You have reached Third Level Spirit Sovereign>

<…>

<You have reached Ninth Level Spirit Sovereign>

<You have reached First Level Spirit Enlightenment>

After consuming a demon core with energy equivalent to a Divine Ancestor, Yuan’s cultivation soared an entire realm, entering the first level of Spirit Enlightenment.

Moreover, all of the chaotic essence he’d consumed was replenished.

“As I thought… the chaotic energy within demons can be used to replenish my chaotic essence…” Yuan muttered with a satisfied smile.

By consuming the demon core, Yuan confirmed his suspicions— that it replenished his Chaotic Essence, a rare and invaluable energy source within the Nine Heavens. This realization meant that as long as he had access to demon cores, he could not only restore his Chaotic Essence but also develop his Chaotic Heart even further.

<You have cleared the trial within six months>

<Congratulations, you have successfully passed all trials in the Stairway to Heaven!>

<You have gained access to the Sixth Heaven>

“Oh? I only needed to pass a single trial this time?”

“Congratulations, Master. Here is your reward,” Tian’er appeared to personally hand him the reward for the first time.

In her grasp was a long wooden box. Yuan accepted the box without hesitation and opened it immediately. Inside, nestled securely, were five distinct items.

The first item was a golden pill. [True Heavenly Pill] [Tier 7] [Purity: 95%]

[Effects: Greatly increases one’s innate talents and cultivation speed. Its effects will only work once.]

The second item inside the box was a silver ore. It was clearly a material for crafting treasures.

The third and fourth items were intricately rolled scrolls.

Yuan picked them up one at a time, examining the titles inscribed on their surfaces. The first scroll was titled “Shadow Puppet,” while the second bore the name “Shadow Extraction.”

“A martial technique and a cultivation technique, huh?”

The fifth and final item was a thin, unassuming book with the title “A World of Darkness” ominously etched on its cover. After reading the first page, which was filled with array symbols, it became clear that it was a powerful formation.

However, despite his knowledge, Yuan was unable to accurately determine its exact level. What he could deduce with certainty, though, was that it was at least a Level 7 formation.

“By the way, your Divine Beasts friends—the dragon and serpent have already passed their trials. They’re waiting for you inside the Stairway to Heaven,” Tian’er suddenly revealed.

Yuan nodded and said, “Thank you.”

“Should I take you to them now?”

“Give me a moment.”

Yuan used this moment to consume the True Heavenly Pill and study the Shadow Puppet martial technique.

‘I don’t need the cultivation technique, but Yingzi might be able to use it to start her second cultivation.’

Although magical beasts typically avoided using human cultivation techniques due to their inherent incompatibility, it was not entirely unheard of. There were rare cases where particularly intelligent or adaptable beasts had managed to modify and adapt human techniques to suit their unique physiology.

A few hours later.

<Shadow Puppet learned>

[Shadow Puppet]

[Rank: Supreme]

[Mastery Level: 1]

[Description: This ability grants the user control over shadows, enabling them to manipulate and shape the darkness around them into powerful weapons. The shadows become an extension of the user’s will, offering versatility in both offense and defense.]

“Okay, I am ready,” Yuan said sometime later.

Tian’er teleported Yuan to the waiting room, where Lan Yingying and Xi Meili had been waiting since they finished their trials.

“Sorry for taking so long. I didn’t think the trial would require so much time,” he said to them.

“It’s fine. I needed some time to digest what I learned from my trial, anyway,” Xi Meili said.

Sometime later, Tian’er brought Yuan to the others.

“So, what did you think?” Yuan asked Tan Songyun, who was speechless at first.

“I don’t know what to think. How much of what you showed me is real, anyway?”

“While the trial isn’t a perfect replay of history, it’s true that those Tower Masters were all corrupted and the reason Tian Kai ended their lives.”

“I have a lot of questions, but they can wait. I am still trying to process what I witnessed,” she sighed.

Yuan smiled.

“Take your time.”

chapters.title: Chapter 1791 Sixth Heaven
chapters.body:
Chapter 1791 Sixth Heaven

After his conversation with Tan Songyun, Yuan was reunited with the others.

“Congratulations on your breakthrough to Spirit Enlightenment, Young Master!” Feng Yuxiang said after seeing his growth.

Yuan smiled and said, “You’ve also grown much stronger yourself, Feng Feng.”

In just a little over two months, Feng Yuxiang’s cultivation had soared to the peak of Spirit Enlightenment. Naturally, this wasn’t the result of any training but just her sealed cultivation returning over time.

“Yingzi, here you go.” Yuan handed the cultivation technique Shadow Extraction to Yingzi after seeing her.

“What is this?” she asked with a nonchalant expression on her face.

“It’s a cultivation technique meant for humans, but given your nature, there’s a small chance you might be compatible with it. If you can cultivate it, you’ll be able to create a Dantian and have a second cultivation like myself.”

“A second cultivation… I will try. Thank you.” Yingzi accepted the cultivation technique.

“Now then, let’s head to the Sixth Heaven.” Yuan declared shortly later.

“Wait a minute.”

Tian’er suddenly stopped them.

“Master, before you head to the Sixth Heaven, there’s something you should know. While you were in the middle of your trial, the Celestial Emperor sealed the entrance to the Seventh Heaven.” Tian’er revealed this unexpected news to him.

“What?” Yuan frowned, wondering if the Celestial Emperor had somehow learned about his existence.

“Does this mean I won’t be able to enter the Seventh Heaven until he removes this seal?” “As the Master of the Stairway to Heaven, you can naturally remove the seal and enter the Seventh Heaven. However, that would alert the Celestial Emperor.”

“So I will have to either wait for the Celestial Emperor to remove the seal himself or find another way to reach the Seventh Heaven, huh? No… I have to enter the Seventh Heaven through the Stairway to Heaven because I have to do the trials… How troublesome.”

“Master can still take the trials even if you already ascended through another method,” she revealed.

“I see. What about those who went to the lower heavens? How will they get back to the upper heavens?” Yuan looked at Tan Songyun as he asked.

“The seal only affects those who wish to ascend, so it won’t affect those who came from the Seventh Heaven.”

“This isn’t the first time it’s happened, so it’s not too weird.” Tan Songyun suddenly joined the conversation.

“Huh? What do you mean?” Yuan inquired.

“How much do you even know about the Nine Heavens?”

“Honestly, probably not as much as I should.”

She sighed and said, “The Nine Heavens is split into three levels—lower, middle, and upper. The First Heaven to the Third Heaven is considered ‘lower,’ and the Fourth Heaven to the Sixth Heaven is considered ‘middle’. While the first six heavens are relatively similar to each other, everything is vastly different in the Seventh Heaven and above.”

“Many people consider the ‘upper’ heavens to be the true cultivation world—the Immortal Realm—while anything below that is the Mortal Realm, and in order to not overpopulate the Seventh Heaven, the Celestial Emperor often places restrictions on it. The seal is one such restriction.”

“How long do these restrictions typically last?” Yuan then asked.

“It varies. Some last only a few years, while others have lasted for decades—even centuries. The situation in the upper heavens is also an important factor.”

“Anyway, one primary reason the Celestial Emperor enforces these restrictions is because of resources. To ensure resources remain abundant, he limits the number of people who can access them. After all, what truly differentiates the upper heavens from the lower heavens is the quality and quantity of their resources.”

“I see…”

“Are you in a hurry to reach the Seventh Heaven?” Tan Songyun asked.

Yuan paused for a moment, contemplating her question before responding. “While it won’t kill me to delay my journey there, it’ll definitely become more risky the longer I wait.”

“In any case, even if the Seventh Heaven wasn’t sealed, I still need to take a look around the Sixth Heaven before I ascend. After all, there’s a good chance I might find something related to my past lives there. I can figure things out when I intend to ascend. Since you came from the upper heavens, do you have any information on the Sixth Heaven? Any big events happening soon?”

“Big events, huh?” Tan Songyun began pondering seriously.

A few moments later, she spoke with a serious face, “Actually, there is one thing. Before I descended to the lower heavens to find you, rumors of a Hidden Domain being discovered in the Sixth Heaven were spreading. The rumors also say this Hidden Domain is very ancient and likely dates back to the Primordial Era.”

“Hidden Domain?” Yuan repeated in a mumble.

Hidden Domains are essentially secret realms, concealed for a multitude of reasons. Unlike Legacy Tombs, which are intentionally left behind to be discovered, Hidden Domains were never meant to be found. They are often remnants of real places that once existed in the Nine Heavens but were deliberately hidden away, sealed, or forgotten for reasons unknown.

“Alright, then it’s decided. I will take a look at this Hidden Domain,” he declared a moment later.

Tan Songyun had no objections, as she was also interested in the Hidden Domain and would’ve likely visited it if she hadn’t had to find Yuan.

“Good luck, Master.” Tian’er bid them farewell before teleporting them out of the Stairway to Heaven.

Once they entered the Sixth Heaven, Yuan and his group made their way to the nearest city to gather more information on the Hidden Domain. Searᴄh the nôᴠel Fire.nёt website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

It didn’t take long for them to gather information about the Hidden Domain, as it was the talk of the streets. Nearly every passerby and group they overheard were discussing it with excitement, speculation, and even caution. “Did you hear? The recently discovered Hidden Domain turned out to be a sect from the Primordial Era.”

“What was the name again?”

“The Immortal Monastery!” “What? Immortal Monastery?” Yuan’s eyes widened with disbelief after hearing this familiar name.

chapters.title: Chapter 1792 Hidden Domain
chapters.body:
Chapter 1792 Hidden Domain

“Did they just say Immortal Monastery? Or was I just hearing things?” Yuan turned to look at Tan Songyun and asked for confirmation.

“They definitely said Immortal Monastery. Why? Do you recognize the place?” Tan Songyun asked.

Although the Immortal Monastery was once one of the most renowned sects in existence, it has not been seen since the Primordial Era. Its disappearance over countless millennia caused its name to fade from memory, making it unsurprising that most people in the present day were unaware of its legacy or even its existence.

“Yes, I recognize it… After all, I used to be a disciple there.”

‘Young Master.” Yu Ning suddenly called out to him. “In case you don’t remember, Ji Ran used to be a Sect Leader at the Immortal Monastery.’

‘I remember,’ he said.

He recalled Ji Ran mentioning it during their first encounter in the Shadow Realm.

“Fate is really scary…” Yuan muttered with a bittersweet smile on his face.

Sometime later, Yuan approached some of the pedestrians to learn more about the Hidden Domain.

After speaking to a dozen people, he learned that the Hidden Domain was discovered around four years ago, but it wasn’t opened until very recently due to the powerful formations protecting it.

Once the Hidden Domain was opened, several scouts were sent inside to investigate. According to their reports, the domain housed none other than the Immortal Monastery, one of the most ancient and powerful sects in history. The discovery sent ripples throughout the Nine Heavens, as the Immortal Monastery was a legendary sect that had vanished since the Primordial Era. Its reappearance promised untold treasures, profound techniques, and secrets long thought to be lost to time.

Once news of the Immortal Monastery reached the Upper Heavens, it sparked a frenzy. Influential backgrounds wasted no time mobilizing their experts, sending them to the Sixth Heaven in droves. Prestigious sects, prominent families, powerful factions, and even hidden backgrounds emerged from the shadows, all eager to explore the legendary Immortal Monastery.

“According to the scouts who entered the Hidden Domain, the place hasn’t fully opened yet, and they were only able to reach the sect’s entrance before being blocked by a barrier.”

After gathering enough information, Yuan made his way to a private room in a nearby restaurant. The room was quiet and secluded, offering a perfect space for discussion while they relaxed.

Yuan retrieved Baneful Abyss so he could speak with Ji Ran. S~eaʀᴄh the Nôvel(F)ire.ηet website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Ji Ran, you were the Sect Leader of the Immortal Monastery at one point, right?”

“Yes, I was the fourth Sect Leader in history.”

“Did you know of a disciple named Tian Yang?”

Ji Ran pondered for a moment before responding, “No, I am not familiar with that name. However, it’s been countless years, so my memories may not be reliable.”

“Then do you know what happened to the Immortal Monastery or how it became a Hidden Domain?”

Ji Ran sighed and said, “The Immortal Monastery was one of the top sects during the Primordial Era. While the amount of sects back then wouldn’t hold a candle to the sects available today, we were much more competitive.”

“We participated in the Heavenly War of Immortals and Gods and went against the Celestial Emperor, which was the beginning of our downfall. According to what I was told by those who survived the war, the Immortal Monastery went into hiding after I was banished to the Shadow Realm, and as far as I was aware, they never emerged again.”

“What are the chances the Immortal Monastery survived to this day?” Yuan wondered out loud.

“Zero. After all, they disappeared during the Primordial Era. How many years do you think have passed since then? A sect needs to continuously recruit disciples and earn resources to survive. Even if they tried to do it in secrecy, there’s no way they managed to survive this long without exposing themselves.”

To secretly recruit disciples and earn resources for hundreds of millions of years without exposing themselves was statistically impossible. “Moreover, they were already on the verge of collapse before going into hiding,” Ji Ran added.

“So what you’re saying is… the Immortal Monastery should still have their treasures and techniques?” Tan Songyun said.

Ji Ran nodded, “Young Master Yuan, if it’s the sect’s techniques you want, I have most of them in my head, including the most valuable ones. I can give them all to you if you want.”

Yuan shook his head and said, “I don’t really care about the techniques or treasures. One of my past lives was a disciple of the Immortal Monastery, and I believe I’ll be able to learn more about him if I go there.”

“What? You used to be a disciple at my sect?” Ji Ran’s eyes widened with shock after hearing this news.

“Yes. Although I don’t have much of his memories, I know that he’d spent most of his earlier years there.”

“Do you remember the name of the Sect Leader when you were a disciple?” Ji Ran asked.

“No, I do not.”

“That’s a pity.”

Yuan turned to look at Tan Songyun and asked, “What is the accessibility like for Hidden Domains nowadays? Can anyone just enter them?”

“Of course not. Hidden Domains are owned by those who discovered them. In this instance, it was found by someone from the Heavenly Beast Sect, so they get to decide who enters and who doesn’t.”

“So I need to give this Heavenly Beast Sect a visit first, huh? Do you have any information on them?”

“They’re one of the top sects in the Sixth Heaven, and they specialize in taming magical beasts,” she said. “But that’s the extent of my knowledge of them.”

“It’s fine. We can learn more about them when we visit them. Let’s finish eating first.”

After finishing their meals, Yuan and his group departed for the Heavenly Beast Sect, situated at the border between a vast, empty plain and the towering mountain ranges.

The travel took several days, and when they finally arrived at the Heavenly Beast Sect, they were met with the sight of a massive sea of people gathered outside the sect.

chapters.title: Chapter 1793 Limited Slots
chapters.body:
Chapter 1793 Limited Slots

“There are so many people here…” Xi Meili swallowed nervously.

What was truly awe-inspiring about the crowd wasn’t its sheer size, but the overwhelming number of experts it contained. The majority of those present hailed from powerful sects and prestigious families, each sending their most accomplished members to represent them. Not wanting to show any disrespect to the Heavenly Beast Sect, these factions had ensured that only their best and brightest were in attendance.

As for those from the upper heavens, most of them were directly sent inside the sect without needing to wait around.

“How does one receive permission to enter the Hidden Domain?” Xi Meili then asked.

“In most cases, you pay the party with ownership,” said Tan Songyun. “I wonder how much it’ll cost. Seeing how many are interested, I’m willing to bet it’ll be expensive,” Xi Meili sighed.

“Let’s enter the line first,” Yuan said a moment later.

Soon, they entered the massive queue.

After waiting in line for about an hour, a young woman wearing a unique uniform approached them.

“Hello, can you tell me your background? I am from the Heavenly Beast Sect,” said the young woman.

“We’re a group. My name is Xiao Yang, and I am from the Celestial Overlords.”

Yuan decided to use this as his primary identity for the time being.

“C-Celestial Overlords?!” The young woman exclaimed with a startled face.

When those around them heard the woman’s voice, they all turned to look at Yuan, who was still wearing a mask.

Even Tan Songyun was staring at him with wide eyes.

‘What are you doing?! To impersonate someone from the Celestial Overlords, are you seeking death?!’ Tan Songyun shouted at him through voice transmission. Yuan ignored her and showed the young woman his faction symbol.

“Please follow me, esteemed guests!” The young woman bowed to them.

“…” Tan Songyun wore a dumbfounded look on her face, as she didn’t expect Yuan actually to be part of such a famous faction.

Yuan looked at Tan Songyun and smiled. “Is it worse than impersonating a Dragon Emperor?” he asked her through voice transmissions.

Tan Songyun could only remain speechless.

Sometime later, the female disciple brought them to a luxurious building and said, “Please wait here for a moment. One of our sect elders will come to speak with you as soon as possible.”

After leaving Yuan’s group, the female disciple immediately went to notify the sect elder responsible for receiving guests from the upper heavens.

“What? Someone from the Celestial Overlords is here? That’s impossible,” said the sect elder.

“Why is it impossible? That person showed me the Celestial Overlords’ faction symbol,” explained the disciple.

“It’s impossible because people from the Celestial Overlords already visited us, and there were four of them. Why would the Celestial Overlords send two groups separately?”

“That’s…” the disciple fell silent.

“Whatever. I will see them myself. If he turns out to be an imposter, I’ll notify the real member.”

The sect elder didn’t wait around and immediately left.

Sometime later, the sect elder arrived at Yuan’s guest house and knocked on the door.

“You must be the sect elder.” Xi Meili opened the door.

“Yes, I am Elder Pan. Are you the guest from the Celestial Overlords?” the sect elder asked in a respectful tone.

Although he was suspicious of their identity, he had to remain cautious just in case it was a misunderstanding.

Xi Meili shook her head and said, “No, he’s inside. I am his traveling companion.”

The sect elder entered the building and followed Xi Meili to the living room, where Yuan and Tan Songyun were sipping tea.

“Welcome to our humble Heavenly Beast Sect, esteemed guests. I heard from my disciple that you’re from the Celestial Overlords. I apologize in advance if this is a misunderstanding, but a group of individuals from the Celestial Overlords has already visited our sect.”

“Is that so? Although I am from the Celestial Overlords, I am visiting for personal reasons, so it’s not like I was sent by my faction.”

Yuan showed him his faction symbol and rank, “I was recently recruited by Celestial Overlord Qiang, so my rank is lacking.”

“You were personally recruited by a Celestial Overlord?!” The sect elder swallowed nervously after hearing this.

“If you don’t believe me, I can contact her for you,” Yuan said.

‘And he even has direct access to her?!’ the sect elder cried inwardly.

“N-no, it’s fine. I believe you. I apologize for doubting your identity.”

“Don’t worry, I understand.”

‘The Celestial Overlords have sent four people to explore the Hidden Domain, huh? They must’ve sent one from each faction,’ Yuan thought to himself.

“Anyway, I am here to request permission to enter the Hidden Domain. How do I acquire it?”

“About that… the Hidden Domain has limited spots—20,000 spots to be precise, so there’s fierce competition for them.”

“Limited spots? This is news to me.”

“We just found out ourselves. Moreover, this is a ‘rule’ within the Hidden Domain, so it’s not something we made up to drive up the price. I apologize for any inconvenience.”

“I see. And how many slots have been taken so far?”

“A little over 19,000.”

“So there are less than a thousand spots left, huh? So, how much does the Heavenly Beast Sect want for an entry?”

Although entry to the Hidden Domain would likely come at an exorbitant cost, Yuan wasn’t overly concerned about being unable to afford it.

“Unfortunately, we are unable to directly sell you a slot right now. However, we will take your offer into consideration and give you an answer next month.” Sёarch* The novёlF~ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“So the highest bidder wins,” Yuan mumbled.

He began counting the number of slots he needed.

‘Xiao Hua, Feng Feng, Yingzi, Meili, Yingying, Yu Ning… will those living within my Soul Weapons need a slot?’

He turned to look at Tan Songyun and asked, “Do you also want to enter?”

“Of course, but you don’t need to pay for my slot. I’ll handle it myself,” she said.

“Alright.”
