chapters.title: Chapter 1794 Offers For The Slots
chapters.body:
Chapter 1794 Offers For The Slots

Yuan looked at Elder Pan and asked, “I happen to wield a Soul Weapon that contains a spirit. Will that count as a slot?”

“Only those who will be participating will take up a slot, so unless the spirit within your Soul Weapon wishes to participate, they won’t take up a slot. This is true for everyone else, too. Also, you won’t be able to use any treasures as a participant, including your Soul Weapons.”

“Can you elaborate? What is considered participation?”

Elder Pan proceeded to explain, “According to the ‘rules’ of the Hidden Domain, those who will become ‘disciples’ are considered a participant. We don’t have much information about the details since nobody has entered the Hidden Domain yet, but we believe the main theme of this Hidden Domain is ‘roleplay’, and we’ll be roleplaying as disciples of the Immortal Monastery.”

Some Hidden Domains are structured around a specific theme, much like the trials Yuan had encountered within the Stairway to Heaven. These themes often dictate the nature of the challenges within the domain, such as tests of strength, wisdom, or even teamwork.

“I see… In that case…” Yuan mumbled.

‘Feng Feng, do you wish to participate?’ he asked her.

‘Although it sounds fun, I will remain a spectator this time,’ she said, not wanting to waste a precious slot.

‘What about you, Yingzi?’

‘I will participate.’

‘Yingying?’

‘I would also like to participate.’

“Do you want to participate as well?” Yuan asked Xi Meili.

She looked at Elder Pan and asked, “Is the Hidden Domain dangerous?” “I’m sorry, but I genuinely do not know.” Elder Pan shook his head.

Some Hidden Domains are rife with peril, filled with deadly traps, fierce beasts, and trials that test one’s very limits. Others, however, are serene and peaceful, offering a haven for cultivation or exploration. Unfortunately, there is no way to truly discern the nature of a Hidden Domain until one steps inside.

While the Heavenly Beast Sect had managed to access the Hidden Domain, their exploration had only reached the entrance. The true nature and contents of the domain remained shrouded in mystery.

“It’s better to assume that the place will be dangerous,” said Yuan.

Xi Meili pondered for a moment before nodding, “Alright, I’ll participate.”

‘What about you, Yu Ning?’ Yuan asked her next.

‘I’m okay being a spectator,’ she said.

Yuan retrieved Baneful Abyss and asked Ji Ran next, ‘Since you were once the Sect Leader of the Immortal Monastery, I assume you want to participate?’

‘If you don’t mind letting me participate, I would love to do so,’ he said.

‘Sure.’

After counting, Yuan said, “I will need five slots. As for my offer…”

He began pondering what he should offer the Heavenly Beast Sect for these slots.

“How about this?”

He retrieved a handful of leaves and even some liquid from the Golden Celestial Tree.

“These belong to a Golden Celestial Tree that is several million years old.”

Elder Pan’s eyes widened with excitement after seeing this.

‘Young Master, in order to secure our spot, how about giving him some of my Phoenix blood?’ Feng Yuxiang suddenly said.

‘Are you sure?’

‘Of course. Now that my bloodline is no longer cursed, I essentially have an unlimited supply of Phoenix blood. Giving them a few drops won’t hurt me in the slightest,’ she said.

‘Alright. Thank you.’

Yuan then said, “I will also offer a few drops of Phoenix blood from a royal bloodline.”

“Phoenix blood from a royal bloodline?!” Elder Pan stood up from shock, his eyes wide with disbelief. “Are you serious?!”

“If you have a method to examine the blood, I wouldn’t mind letting you examine it.” Yuan nodded.

Although Feng Yuxiang never claimed to possess a royal bloodline, it was almost certain that her lineage surpassed even the most exalted royal bloodlines. As a direct descendant of the Primordial Phoenix, she carried the purest and most ancient Phoenix bloodline.

“Although we do not have something like that, we have something that can evaluate a bloodline’s grade,” said Elder Pan. Searᴄh the novel(F~)ire.net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“In that case, I would also like to offer some of my blood,” Xi Meili suddenly added. “I’m actually a dragon with a royal bloodline, and I’m willing to add some of my blood to the offer.”

Xi Meili had hidden her horns and tail to appear less conspicuous, so Elder Pan did not know she was a dragon until it was mentioned.

“Y-you’re a dragon?!” Elder Pan fell back down from shock.

To prove her identity, Xi Meili partially transformed, revealing her horns, tail, and scales.

“P-please wait a minute! I will contact our Sect Leader right now! He will be able to guarantee you those slots!”

“Take your time.” Yuan nodded with a smile.

Elder Pan left the guest house and rushed to the Sect Leader’s side to notify him of Yuan’s offer.

“In addition to leaves and liquid from a Golden Celestial Tree that is several million years old, he’s also offering the blood of a dragon and phoenix—ones with a royal bloodline?!” The Sect Leader of the Heavenly Beast Sect almost couldn’t believe his ears after hearing such a generous offer.

“Are you sure you heard him properly?! I swear I’ll skin you alive if it turns out to be a misunderstanding!”

“I swear on my life!” Elder Pan reassured.

“Bring me to him! I must speak with this individual myself!”

Elder Pan hurriedly returned to Yuan’s guest house with the Sect Leader following behind.

“My name is Bao Shuya, and I am the current Sect Leader of the Heavenly Beast Sect. It’s a pleasure to meet you, esteemed guests. Regarding your offer, once we confirm the Phoenix blood and Dragon blood’s quality, we will immediately give you five slots for the Hidden Domain.”

“Sure thing.” Yuan nodded.

“Please follow me.”

Sect Leader Bao guided Yuan and his group to another building. Inside, the room was filled with intricate devices specifically designed to evaluate the grade and purity of beast bloodlines.

“I’ll go first,” Xi Meili said.

chapters.title: Chapter 1795 Heavenly Beast Sect
chapters.body:
Chapter 1795 Heavenly Beast Sect

“Please place your hands inside the device,” said Sect Leader Bao, his hands gesturing at the device before him.

Xi Meili approached the device and placed her hands on it. Soon, her body became enveloped by a soft, red glow.

“Imperial-grade! Your bloodline is Imperial-grade, which is definitely classified as a royal bloodline!” Sect Leader Bao exclaimed in excitement.

He turned to look at Yuan and asked, “Where is the Phoenix blood? If the Phoenix is not here, we will have to use another method to evaluate the blood.”

The next moment, Feng Yuxiang emerged from Yuan’s Dantian, startling Sect Leader Bao.

“D-Did she just come out of your Dantian?!” Sect Leader Bao exclaimed, his voice filled with shock and disbelief. “That’s only possible if the two of you are in a contract!”

His wide eyes darted between Yuan and Feng Yuxiang. The idea of a human forming a contract with a Phoenix, especially one with a royal bloodline, was almost unimaginable. Yet, it appeared to be a reality right before him.

Feng Yuxiang ignored Sect Leader Bao’s shocked state and walked to the device to grade her bloodline.

A few moments later, her body was enveloped by a golden glow.

“Divine-grade!” Sect Leader Bao gasped, his eyes and jaw wide with shock.

However, Feng Yuxiang didn’t appear to be happy about the results.

‘Is it because my bloodline is still recovering? As a Primordial Phoenix, my bloodline should be Supreme-grade,’ she wondered inwardly.

After snapping out of his daze, Sect Leader Bao swallowed nervously before asking, “Um… How many drops of blood will you be offering for the slots?”

Feng Yuxiang narrowed her eyes at Sect Leader Bao, her gaze sharp and calculating. After a moment of tense silence, she raised her hand, holding up two fingers.

Xi Meili then spoke, “I will offer three drops.”

Sect Leader Bao pondered with a serious expression on his face before responding, “I would like to request another drop of your Phoenix blood. In exchange, I will no longer ask for the leaves and liquid from the Golden Celestial Tree.”

Feng Yuxiang’s blood was far more valuable and beneficial to the Heavenly Beast Sect than the Golden Celestial Tree, so Sect Leader Bao’s request was very natural.

“What do you think, Young Master?” Feng Yuxiang asked for Yuan’s advice.

“I’ll let you decide. It’s your blood, after all.”

“Then I’ll accept your offer.” Feng Yuxiang retrieved an empty vial and filled it with three drops of her blood. S~eaʀᴄh the NôvelFire(.)net website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

Xi Meili did the same.

“Thank you very much, esteemed guests. This is your proof of entry to the Hidden Domain. Please do not lose it because we won’t be able to replace it.”

Sect Leader Bao handed Yuan five wooden badges with the words ‘Immortal Monastery’ engraved onto them, as well as a communication jade slip. Read exclusive chapters at empire

“They were found by the entrance to the Immortal Monastery, hence how we figured out there would only be 20,000 available slots,” he added.

“Thanks.”

Yuan accepted the badges and tossed them into his spatial ring.

“When will we be able to enter the Hidden Domain?” Yuan then asked.

“Once we fill up the slots by the end of the month, we will notify you through the communication jade slip. Currently, we intend to enter the Hidden Domain within three months.”

“Three months, huh? That’s quite long.”

“If you’d like, you can stay in the Heavenly Beast Sect as our guest in the meantime.”

Yuan nodded, “That would be great since we don’t really have a place to stay at this moment.”

“Then it’s settled. Just a heads up, there are other participants living in this area, and we do not tolerate any fights between participants.”

“You don’t need to worry. We don’t know anyone, so we have no reason to cause any trouble.”

Sect Leader Bao nodded. He proceeded to guide them back to their living quarters.

“Then I’ll leave you alone for now. If you need anything, we have servants on standby outside.”

He gestured toward the individuals wearing a specific uniform walking around the place.

“Wait, I also want to give you an offer for a slot.” Tan Songyun suddenly stopped him.

“What?” Sect Leader Bao looked at her with raised eyebrows and a puzzled expression.

“Although we’re traveling together, I am going to pay for my own slot,” she explained.

“Is that so…? Since I am already here, I will listen to your offer.”

Tan Songyun proceeded to retrieve a scroll from her spatial ring and handed it to him.

Sect Leader Bao didn’t think much of it and opened it. However, when he read the contents of the scroll, his eyes widened with shock and disbelief.

“T-this is—! Is this real?!”

Tan Songyun remained calm and said, “What would I gain from lying to you?”

She showed her faction symbol, revealing her background.

“Vengeful Fairies!”

He began contemplating shortly later. After a moment of heavy silence, he finally made his decision. Reaching into his robe, he retrieved another wooden badge and extended it toward Tan Songyun.

“I’ll accept your offer.”

“Thank you.” Tan Songyun casually accepted the badge.

Sect Leader Bao shook his head. “No, I should be thanking you.”

He quickly stored the scroll inside his spatial ring, even looking around to make sure nobody else saw it.

After he left, Yuan asked Tan Songyun in a curious tone, “What did you offer him?”

“Let’s just say it will benefit the Heavenly Beast Sect greatly if they are able to acquire it.”

Yuan raised an eyebrow and guessed, “A treasure map?”

“Good guess, but you’re wrong.”

“Are you really going to keep it a secret?”

“Does it annoy you?”

“I can’t say that I am not curious, but I won’t lose any sleep not knowing.”

“Is that so?” Tan Songyun didn’t say anything else and returned to the building.

Yuan could only shake his head inwardly and follow her inside.

“Three months, huh? What should we do in the meantime?” Xi Meili asked once they were inside.

chapters.title: Chapter 1796 Heavenly Beast Sect(2)
chapters.body:
Chapter 1796 Heavenly Beast Sect(2)

“You guys can do whatever. I’m going to cultivate,” said Tan Songyun.

She pointed to a certain room and continued, “I’ll be staying in there. Don’t bother me unless it’s an emergency.”

After saying that, Tan Songyun entered the room she picked.

“It’s not easy getting along with her,” Xi Meili sighed.

“I’m sure she’ll open her heart more as she comes to trust us,” Yuan said with a calm and reassuring tone. “Anyway, it’s only three months. I don’t really have anything in mind, so I’ll probably just cultivate.”

When Yuan mentioned he would cultivate, he wasn’t referring to increasing his cultivation but rather to strengthening his Soul Strength. While his soul cultivation technique passively enhanced his Soul Strength over time, actively cultivating it allowed for much greater and faster progress.

“In that case, I’ll take a look around the Heavenly Beast Sect. This is my first time visiting a human sect, much less one that focuses on taming magical beasts,” said Xi Meili.

“Have fun, and try not to cause any trouble.”

“I’ll go with her.” Feng Yuxiang suddenly emerged from his Dantian and said.

“Alright.”

“Are we going now?” Feng Yuxiang asked Xi Meili a moment later.

“Yes.”

“Wait, I also want to come.” Lan Yingying suddenly appeared and said.

“The more the merrier.” Xi Meili chuckled.

Thus, Feng Yuxiang, Lan Yingying, and Xi Meili left the building to explore the Heavenly Beast Sect.

Once they left, Yingzi appeared and said, “I am going to try to cultivate with the cultivation technique you gave me.”

“Sure. If there is anything you don’t understand about the technique, let me know.”

Yingzi nodded, and she sat on the ground with the cultivation technique in her grasp.

While Yuan cultivated his soul strength and Yingzi started learning the cultivation technique, Xi Meili and her group approached one of the Heavenly Beast Sect’s servants.

“Excuse me, we would like to take a look around the Heavenly Beast Sect. Would that be fine?” Xi Meili asked the young servant.

“Of course. However, you will need a sect elder to accompany you at all times. Do you see that tall building there? You can find someone to accompany you there.” The servant pointed at the pagoda not too far in the distance.

“I see it. Thank you.”

Sometime later, Xi Meili and the others entered the pagoda. Inside, several individuals with a powerful presence were present, and they were all speaking with a sect elder of the Heavenly Beast Sect.

Noticing Xi Meili’s group, a female sect elder approached them and asked, “Hello, are you here for a guide?”

“Yes, we’d like to take a look around the sect.”

“I understand. Please, follow me.”

The sect elder brought them to a nearby empty desk.

“Before we assign you with someone, is there any specific place within the sect you would like to visit?”

“Not really. What about you guys?” Xi Meili asked the other two.

“I don’t really care, either.” Feng Yuxiang said.

Lan Yingying then said, “I would like to learn more about taming in general. How do normal cultivators and their contracted beasts typically act?”

“Oh? Are you looking to become a beast tamer yourself?” the sect elder asked, misunderstanding Lan Yingying’s intentions.

“Something like that, I guess.” Lan Yingying decided to go along with the sect elder.

“In that case, we can give you a full tour around the Heavenly Beast Sect. Please give me a moment to see if any guide is available…”

A few moments later, she spoke, “I’m sorry, but all of our guides are currently attending to other guests. I can put you on a waiting list for now, and we’ll contact you as soon as the next guide becomes available.”

“Sure.” Xi Meili nodded. Searᴄh the Novelƒire(.)ne*t website on Google to access chapters of novels early and in the highest quality.

“Are you currently residing within the sect?” the sect elder then asked.

“Yes, we’re staying at building 909.”

“In that case, we will send the guide straight to your building whenever we have one.”

“Okay. Thank you.”

Xi Meili and the others returned to their living quarters and waited for their guide to show up.

A few hours after they returned home, someone knocked on their door.

“Excuse me, I am here as your guide.” A woman’s voice loudly resounded outside.

“Already?” Xi Meili was pleasantly surprised by how fast the guide showed up.

When she opened the door, a woman with red and yellowish hair greeted her.

“My name is Hong Ling, and I will be your guide.”

Xi Meili and the others gathered outside.

“Um…”

When Hong Ling saw Feng Yuxiang, she hastily approached her and said, “Um… It’s an honor to meet you, Senior Phoenix!”

Feng Yuxiang raised an eyebrow and asked, “Do I know you?”

“No, but the Sect Leader—my master—told me about you!” Hong Ling explained, her tone filled with a mix of excitement and awe. “I am actually a beast with the Crimson Raven bloodline,” she added.

Explore more stories with empire

After hearing about Feng Yuxiang, Hong Ling wanted to meet her immediately. However, Hong Ling didn’t want to appear nosy, so she spent a good amount of time wondering how she should approach Feng Yuxiang.

After learning about their situation, Hong Ling immediately volunteered to be their guide. No, she demanded to be their guide.

“I see. Nice to meet you.”

Hong Ling’s eyes sparkled with a mix of excitement and reverence, her demeanor resembling that of a fangirl standing before her idol.

Crimson Ravens were powerful magical beasts, highly respected for their mastery of fire. Though they were not true phoenixes, their abilities and lineage placed them in close relation, often considered distant cousins of phoenixes.

Hong Ling retrieved a flying treasure that was an enormous crimson feather with a flourish, her excitement palpable as she exclaimed, “I heard you wanted to learn more about beast taming! Don’t worry—I’ll make sure you know everything there is to know about our path before this tour ends!”

“We’ll be in your care.” Feng Yuxiang nodded as she stepped onto the flying treasure.

Xi Meili and Lan Yingying followed.
